The annual report was reviewed and approved by the full board of directors, including independent directors Eleanor Vance, Thomas Ohashi, and Priya Reddington.
Veralux Photonics remains committed to its environmental, social, and governance framework, targeting a 30% reduction in carbon emissions by 2030.
The company contributed $3.2 million to community programs and STEM education initiatives during fiscal year 2023.
Management expressed confidence that Veralux Photonics is well-positioned to deliver sustained shareholder value over the long term.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings, a mid-cap pharmaceutical and diagnostics company headquartered in Utrecht, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Petra Vanderhoeven opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's strategic trajectory and operational resilience.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings reported total revenues of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year's €1.64 billion.
The diagnostics segment led growth, contributing €1.02 billion in revenue, while the pharmaceutical manufacturing segment generated €820 million.
Gross profit for the full year reached €742 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 40.3%, an improvement of 180 basis points year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Helbrecht attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable raw material pricing and improved manufacturing throughput at the company's Düsseldorf facility.
Operating expenses totaled €498 million for the year, inclusive of €134 million in research and development investment.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to €212 million, a modest 4.1% increase from the prior year, reflecting disciplined cost management.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €387 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 21.0%.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €198 million, translating to earnings per diluted share of €2.74.
The company generated free cash flow of €221 million during fiscal year 2023, up from €183 million in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €88 million, predominantly directed toward expanding the Utrecht headquarters campus and upgrading laboratory infrastructure.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of €315 million.
The Board of Directors approved an annual dividend of €0.92 per share, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy established in 2019.
Total shares outstanding as of December 31, 2023, stood at 72.2 million, reflecting a modest 1.1% reduction due to the share repurchase program.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased approximately 800,000 shares at an average price of €37.60 per share under the ongoing buyback authorization.
Dr. Vanderhoeven highlighted the successful commercial launch of the NovaPlex-7 multiplexed diagnostic panel, which received CE Mark approval in February 2023.
NovaPlex-7 achieved first-year sales of €63 million across European and Middle Eastern markets, exceeding the internal forecast of €50 million.
The pharmaceutical segment's growth was underpinned by strong demand for the company's proprietary enzyme replacement therapy, EnzyCore-XL, which saw unit volumes rise 18% year-over-year.
EnzyCore-XL generated revenues of €294 million in fiscal year 2023, reinforcing its position as the company's single largest revenue-generating product.
The company's international revenues, excluding the Netherlands, accounted for 67% of total revenues, up from 63% in fiscal year 2022.
Germany, France, and the United Kingdom collectively represented the three largest export markets, combining for €541 million in revenues.
Headcount at the end of fiscal year 2023 totaled 6,840 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 420 from the prior year-end figure.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the research and development and clinical operations divisions, supporting the company's expanding pipeline.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings currently has nine therapeutic and diagnostic candidates in active clinical development, spanning Phase I through Phase III trials.
The most advanced pipeline asset, MB-412, a targeted oncology diagnostic biomarker, commenced its pivotal Phase III trial in September 2023 with enrollment across 22 clinical sites.
Management expects MB-412 Phase III enrollment to be completed by the third quarter of fiscal year 2024, with a regulatory submission anticipated in early 2025.
The company also announced a licensing agreement with South Korean firm Hansung Biotech for the regional commercialization of NovaPlex-7 across Asia-Pacific markets.
Under the terms of the Hansung Biotech agreement, Meridian Biosciences Holdings will receive an upfront payment of €18 million and tiered royalties ranging from 8% to 14%.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Biosciences Holdings carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired the remaining €175 million of its 2018 senior notes in June 2023.
Looking ahead to capital structure optimization, management confirmed in February 2024 that the company had secured a €250 million revolving credit facility to support future acquisition activity.
Mr. Helbrecht stated that the new financing arrangement would provide strategic flexibility without materially altering the company's leverage profile.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €167 million, representing approximately 33 days of sales, which management characterized as within the optimal operational range.
The company's accounts receivable balance of €218 million reflected a days-sales-outstanding metric of 43 days, consistent with prior-year performance.

Throughout fiscal year 2023, the company maintained a debt-free capital structure, with all operational activities funded entirely through internally generated cash flows and existing equity reserves.
The audit committee confirmed that there were no material weaknesses identified in the company's internal controls over financial reporting for fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings' effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year's 23.1%, benefiting from expanded R&D tax credits in the Netherlands.
The company recognized a €14 million deferred tax asset related to net operating loss carryforwards from its 2021 acquisition of Flemish diagnostics startup Cellara NV.
Integration of Cellara NV was deemed substantially complete as of mid-2023, delivering realized synergies of approximately €22 million, ahead of the initial €17 million target.
Chief Operating Officer Ingrid Steenbeek credited the Cellara integration success to disciplined project management and strong alignment between both legacy organizations' cultures.
The diagnostics segment's adjusted operating margin improved to 26.8% for the full year, up from 24.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Pharmaceutical segment adjusted operating margin contracted modestly to 18.3% from 19.0% in the prior year, reflecting increased clinical trial expenditure.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 14.7%, compared to 13.2% in fiscal year 2022, indicating improving capital efficiency.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings expects fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of €2.00 billion to €2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 8.7% to 13.0%.
The fiscal year 2024 guidance assumes NovaPlex-7 revenues of approximately €110 million, reflecting accelerating adoption in both existing and new geographic markets.
Management also guided for fiscal year 2024 EBITDA margins in the range of 21.5% to 22.5%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as revenues scale.
Dr. Vanderhoeven noted that the company is evaluating two bolt-on acquisition targets in the molecular diagnostics space with enterprise values below €300 million each.
Any acquisition completed in fiscal year 2024 would be reflected as an incremental upside scenario beyond the base guidance provided on the March earnings call.
The company's pension obligation, primarily related to defined benefit plans in the Netherlands and Germany, stood at €89 million as of December 31, 2023.
Pension plan assets totaled €74 million at year-end, resulting in a net funded status deficit of €15 million, which management does not consider to be material.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's throughout fiscal year 2023.

The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed meaningfully during the year, including a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings was added to the MSCI ESG Leaders Index in November 2023, reflecting recognition of its sustainability practices by the investment community.
Dr. Vanderhoeven reiterated the company's long-term ambition to achieve carbon neutrality across its direct operations by 2030.
In closing remarks, Mr. Helbrecht expressed that the balance sheet remains a source of competitive strength, enabling the company to pursue growth while returning capital to shareholders.
The Board extended Dr. Vanderhoeven's employment contract through December 2027, signaling continuity of leadership during a pivotal phase of pipeline development.
Meridian Biosciences Holdings will host its annual investor day on June 5, 2024, in Utrecht, where management will provide an updated long-range financial plan through fiscal year 2027.
The company remains committed to delivering sustainable, long-term value creation for all stakeholders through disciplined capital allocation, innovation, and operational excellence.
Veltracor Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals company headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary segments: Industrial Coatings, Advanced Polymers, and Environmental Solutions.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marta Svenfeld opened the report with a statement expressing cautious optimism about Veltracor's long-term positioning in the specialty chemicals market.
Chief Financial Officer Heinrich Gruber noted that fiscal year 2023 presented both significant challenges and meaningful opportunities for the organization.
Total consolidated revenue for the fiscal year reached €1.84 billion, representing a 6.3% increase compared to the prior year figure of €1.73 billion.
The Industrial Coatings segment remained the largest contributor, generating €820 million in revenue, up 4.1% year-over-year.
Advanced Polymers posted strong growth of 9.8%, with segment revenues climbing to €614 million from €559 million in fiscal year 2022.
The Environmental Solutions segment, the company's newest and fastest-growing division, recorded revenues of €406 million, a 7.4% increase from the prior year.
Gross profit for the consolidated group stood at €552 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 30.0%.
This represented a modest improvement from the 28.7% gross margin reported in fiscal year 2022, attributed largely to improved raw material sourcing and operational efficiencies.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €318 million for the fiscal year, compared to €297 million in 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses accounted for €189 million of total operating expenditures.
Research and development investment reached €94 million, reflecting Veltracor's continued commitment to product innovation and process optimization.
The remaining €35 million in operating expenses was attributable to depreciation, amortization, and restructuring charges associated with the consolidation of two legacy manufacturing facilities.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €298 million, a 12.4% improvement over the prior year EBITDA of €265 million.
Net income attributable to shareholders totaled €141 million, translating to earnings per share of €2.87 on a fully diluted basis.
This compares favorably to the net income of €119 million and diluted EPS of €2.43 recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the group was 24.6%, slightly below the 25.1% rate in the prior year due to favorable adjustments in certain jurisdictions.
Dr. Svenfeld highlighted the company's investment in sustainable chemistry as a defining strategic theme for the coming decade.
Veltracor launched three new product lines during the fiscal year: EcoShield Pro, NanoCoat X7, and BioFlex Advanced.
EcoShield Pro, a low-VOC industrial coating, received certification from the European Chemicals Agency and is now available across twelve countries.
NanoCoat X7 achieved commercial deployment in the automotive sector, securing supply agreements with two Tier-1 automotive manufacturers.
BioFlex Advanced entered the market in the fourth quarter and has already generated initial purchase orders totaling €18 million.
Capital expenditures for the fiscal year amounted to €136 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Antwerp and Kraków facilities.
The Antwerp facility upgrade is expected to increase production throughput by 22% upon completion in mid-2024.
Veltracor's workforce stood at approximately 8,400 full-time employees at year-end, up from 7,950 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company added approximately 450 net new positions during 2023, with the majority of hiring concentrated in the Environmental Solutions and R&D divisions.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.3% in 2023, compared to 88.7% in the prior year, reflecting the positive impact of the new compensation restructuring initiative.
Veltracor's balance sheet showed total assets of €2.61 billion as of December 31, 2023, compared to €2.44 billion at the end of 2022.
Inventories increased to €312 million from €278 million, consistent with management's strategy to build buffer stock ahead of anticipated demand increases in the first half of 2024.
Trade receivables stood at €284 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 56 days, broadly in line with prior year levels.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to €217 million, down from €249 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in cash was primarily driven by the elevated capital expenditure program and the repayment of short-term credit facilities in the first half of the year.
Total equity attributable to shareholders grew to €1.03 billion, reflecting retained earnings and the positive impact of currency translation adjustments.
The Board of Directors approved a dividend of €0.92 per share for fiscal year 2023, representing a payout ratio of approximately 32%.
This marked the sixth consecutive year of dividend growth for Veltracor, underscoring management's confidence in the company's cash generation capacity.
Mr. Gruber elaborated on the company's capital allocation philosophy during the investor call held on February 14, 2024.
He emphasized a balanced approach between organic investment, shareholder returns, and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
In December 2023, Veltracor completed the acquisition of Primex Adhesives GmbH, a privately held adhesives specialist based in Stuttgart, for a total consideration of €78 million.
The acquisition was funded entirely from existing cash reserves and is expected to be earnings accretive within the first full year of consolidation.
Primex Adhesives generated approximately €31 million in revenue and €6 million in EBITDA in its last reported fiscal year.
Integration planning is well underway, with full operational integration targeted for the third quarter of 2024.
Veltracor's net debt position at December 31, 2023 stood at €183 million, reflecting the impact of capital expenditure and acquisition activity throughout the year.
The net debt to EBITDA ratio of 0.61 times remains well within the company's stated leverage policy ceiling of 2.0 times.
Looking ahead, management provided full-year 2024 guidance targeting revenue in the range of €1.96 billion to €2.02 billion.
Veltracor exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having fully redeemed its outstanding bond instruments prior to maturity.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in November 2023, with a stable outlook.
Veltracor's Environmental Solutions segment received the European Sustainability Excellence Award in October 2023, recognizing the company's progress in reducing industrial waste.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declined by 14% in 2023 relative to the 2020 baseline, positioning the company ahead of its 2025 reduction targets.
The company also committed to achieving net-zero emissions across its value chain by 2045, in alignment with the Science Based Targets initiative.
In January 2024, Veltracor finalized a €250 million syndicated revolving credit facility arranged by a consortium of five European banks to support its medium-term growth strategy.
The new facility carries a floating interest rate benchmarked to EURIBOR plus a margin of 95 basis points, and matures in January 2029.
Dr. Svenfeld stated that maintaining financial flexibility is paramount as the company pursues its ambitious expansion agenda across Central and Eastern Europe.
Veltracor's Advanced Polymers segment is expected to benefit from increasing demand in the electric vehicle battery housing market, where specialty polymer compounds are critical.
The company has entered into a memorandum of understanding with a leading European battery manufacturer to co-develop next-generation polymer encapsulants.
Supply chain resilience was cited as a key operational priority, with dual-sourcing agreements now in place for 78% of critical raw materials.
Veltracor implemented a new enterprise resource planning system across its European operations in 2023, with North American operations scheduled for migration in 2024.
The ERP rollout is projected to generate annual operational savings of approximately €12 million once fully deployed across all regions.
Management concluded the report by reaffirming its long-term vision of becoming a top-five European specialty chemicals company by 2030 measured by revenue.
The Board expressed confidence that the strategic initiatives underway position Veltracor well for sustained value creation for all stakeholders in the years ahead.

Verlux Technologies Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience amid a shifting macroeconomic landscape.
Verlux reported total consolidated revenue of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3 percent increase over the prior year's $1.29 billion.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 68 percent of total revenue, reflecting the company's ongoing transition away from perpetual licensing.
Professional services revenue grew by 9.2 percent year-over-year, reaching $212 million as enterprise clients expanded their implementation engagements.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Kestler attributed the revenue growth to strong demand in the healthcare vertical and accelerated adoption of Verlux's EdgeSync platform.
As of December 31, 2023, Verlux carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final $85 million term loan in March 2023.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $892 million, translating to a gross margin of 60.7 percent, up from 58.1 percent in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $631 million, driven primarily by increased research and development investments and an expanded go-to-market organization.
Research and development spending rose to $198 million, or approximately 13.5 percent of total revenue, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenditures climbed to $274 million as Verlux invested heavily in brand awareness campaigns and its newly established European partner network.

In November 2023, Verlux's board of directors approved a $300 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by First Continental Bank, which was drawn upon immediately to fund strategic acquisitions.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 stood at $261 million, representing an operating margin of 17.8 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation and one-time restructuring charges, came in at $338 million for the full year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $189 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, compared with $147 million, or $1.68 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 23.4 percent, modestly above management's original guidance of 22 percent due to non-deductible transaction costs.
Verlux ended the year with cash and cash equivalents of $417 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
The company generated $312 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, a 19 percent improvement from $262 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $88 million, primarily related to data center expansion in Dallas and a new co-location facility in Frankfurt, Germany.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, reached $224 million, a record for Verlux.
Total headcount as of year-end stood at 6,840 full-time employees, an increase of 11 percent from the 6,160 employees reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company made two bolt-on acquisitions during the year: DataFusion Analytics in February for $47 million and CloudMesh Systems in October for $93 million.
Both acquisitions are expected to be accretive to non-GAAP earnings per share within 18 months of closing, according to management's integration projections.
The DataFusion acquisition added approximately 210 employees and expanded Verlux's machine learning capabilities for predictive analytics workloads.
CloudMesh Systems brought a portfolio of edge-computing orchestration tools that complement Verlux's existing EdgeSync product line.
Verlux's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $1.02 billion at year-end, crossing the one-billion-dollar threshold for the first time in the company's history.
Net revenue retention, a key metric for subscription-based software businesses, was reported at 117 percent, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell activity within the existing customer base.
Customer count grew to 4,320 enterprise accounts globally, up from 3,780 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Average contract value increased to $236,000 per account, reflecting Verlux's success in moving upmarket toward larger enterprise engagements.
The company's top ten customers collectively represented 19.2 percent of total revenue, a slight decrease from 21.4 percent in the prior year, indicating improved revenue diversification.
Geographic revenue breakdown showed that North America contributed 61 percent, Europe contributed 27 percent, and Asia-Pacific contributed the remaining 12 percent of total revenue.
European revenue grew at a particularly strong rate of 31 percent year-over-year, fueled by the Frankfurt facility and expanding partnerships with regional systems integrators.
Raymond Kestler highlighted that the company's deferred revenue balance grew to $384 million at year-end, signaling strong future revenue visibility.
Days sales outstanding improved to 42 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit policies and faster collections from enterprise accounts.
Verlux's board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.35 per share in December 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
This marked the first time Verlux had paid any form of dividend since its initial public offering in 2014, signaling confidence in the company's cash generation capacity.
The company also repurchased $75 million of its common stock during fiscal year 2023 under the share buyback program authorized in August 2022.
Total shares repurchased during the year amounted to approximately 1.9 million shares at a weighted average price of $39.47 per share.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal year 2023 was $62 million, representing an increase of 8 percent from the $57 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's balance sheet showed total assets of $2.14 billion at year-end, including $689 million in goodwill and intangible assets resulting from historical acquisitions.
Total liabilities stood at $748 million, consisting primarily of deferred revenue, accounts payable, and operating lease obligations.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.39 billion, supported by strong net income and comprehensive income from foreign currency translation adjustments.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 14.8 percent, up from 12.3 percent in the prior year.
Return on invested capital improved to 13.1 percent, which management cited as evidence of disciplined capital allocation and operational efficiency gains.
Verlux introduced three major product updates during fiscal year 2023, including EdgeSync 4.0, the Verlux Sentinel security suite, and the AI-powered Prism Analytics module.
EdgeSync 4.0 received positive reviews from enterprise customers, with pilot deployments at 140 accounts generating net promoter scores averaging 71.
The Verlux Sentinel suite, which provides integrated cybersecurity monitoring for cloud workloads, achieved $38 million in new bookings within its first six months of general availability.
Prism Analytics, leveraging large language model integrations, was launched in beta in October 2023 and had enrolled 85 design partner customers by year-end.
Chief Product Officer Elena Marchetti expressed optimism about the Prism Analytics pipeline entering 2024, noting that several Fortune 500 prospects were in active evaluation stages.
Verlux's management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $1.66 billion to $1.70 billion, representing growth of approximately 13 to 16 percent.
The 2024 guidance assumes adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of approximately 100 to 150 basis points relative to the fiscal year 2023 level.
Management expects capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $95 million to $110 million as the company continues to expand its global data center footprint.
The company anticipates its effective tax rate for fiscal year 2024 will normalize to approximately 22 to 23 percent, consistent with its long-run structural rate.
Verlux also announced a new $150 million share repurchase authorization for fiscal year 2024, signaling continued confidence in the stock's intrinsic value.
The company's audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP will continue to serve as Verlux's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024.
KPMG issued an unqualified audit opinion on Verlux's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023, with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
The audit opinion specifically noted the effectiveness of the company's revenue recognition procedures under ASC 606 for multi-element software arrangements.
Chief People Officer Janelle Ortega reported that employee engagement scores reached 82 percent in the most recent company-wide survey, up from 75 percent the prior year.
Voluntary attrition declined to 9.4 percent in fiscal year 2023 from 13.8 percent in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improvements in total compensation and career development programs.
Verlux was recognized on three external employer-of-choice lists during the year, including the Austin Business Journal's Best Places to Work and the Forbes Cloud 100 employer rankings.

The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with this annual report, disclosed a 12 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions compared with the fiscal year 2022 baseline.

Looking ahead, Margaret Holloway expressed that Verlux enters fiscal year 2024 with strong momentum, a differentiated product portfolio, and a disciplined financial foundation designed to deliver sustained shareholder value.

The full audited financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, statements of operations, cash flow statements, and notes thereto, are available in the accompanying Form 10-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltoran Dynamics Corporation released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023, covering the twelve-month period ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, operates primarily in the advanced industrial automation sector.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Solvay opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltoran Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's gross profit margin expanded to 38.7%, compared to 35.2% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Okonkwo attributed the margin improvement to disciplined cost management and favorable raw material pricing.
The automation systems division remained the largest contributor to revenue, generating $512.6 million, or approximately 60.5% of total sales.
The precision components segment posted revenues of $198.4 million, reflecting a 9.1% year-over-year growth rate.
The emerging digital services unit contributed $136.3 million in revenue, nearly doubling its output from the previous fiscal year.
Operating income for the full year reached $143.7 million, yielding an operating margin of approximately 16.9%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98.2 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.41.
Veltoran Dynamics maintained a strong liquidity position, with cash and cash equivalents totaling $224.5 million at year-end.
The company closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $67.4 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Charlotte manufacturing campus.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, unchanged from the prior four quarters.
Total headcount grew to 4,820 full-time employees globally, adding 310 positions during fiscal year 2023.
The Charlotte facility alone employed 1,940 individuals following the completion of a new assembly wing in September 2023.
In October 2023, Veltoran Dynamics secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to fund a strategic acquisition pipeline.
The acquisition of Stuttgart-based Präzikon GmbH was completed in November 2023, adding specialized micro-actuator technology to the portfolio.
Präzikon GmbH contributed approximately $18.7 million in revenue to consolidated results for the final two months of fiscal year 2023.
Chief Strategy Officer Lianne Ferber described the Präzikon transaction as a foundational step in the company's European expansion strategy.
Veltoran Dynamics ended the year with a backlog of $1.14 billion in unfulfilled orders, the highest in the company's history.
The backlog figure represents an increase of 21.3% compared to the $940 million backlog recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Management expects to convert approximately 70% of the current backlog into recognized revenue during fiscal year 2024.
Research and development expenditures totaled $54.9 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 6.5% of total revenue.
The company filed 47 new patent applications during the year, bringing its cumulative active patent portfolio to 312 patents.
Veltoran Dynamics launched three new product lines in fiscal year 2023: the ProTorq 900 series, the NexAlign platform, and the VeltraEdge sensor array.
The ProTorq 900 series was commercially released in the second quarter and generated $31.2 million in first-year sales.
The NexAlign platform achieved certification from three major industrial standards bodies, enabling entry into previously restricted market segments.
VeltraEdge sensor arrays were recognized as a finalist for the International Automation Innovation Award at the Frankfurt Industry Expo in November 2023.
International revenues accounted for 34.2% of total sales in fiscal year 2023, up from 29.8% in the prior year.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing international market, delivering $142.6 million in sales and a 27.4% growth rate.
Veltoran Dynamics signed distribution agreements with four new partners in Southeast Asia during the second half of 2023.
European revenues reached $97.5 million, bolstered by the partial-year contribution from Präzikon GmbH and robust demand in Germany and the Netherlands.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the 22.8% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Deferred tax assets totaling $19.3 million were recognized on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased to $175.6 million, representing 20.7% of revenue compared to 19.4% in the prior year.
Management noted that the SG&A increase was driven by investments in the global salesforce and expanded digital marketing initiatives.
Inventory levels rose to $138.9 million at year-end, reflecting both demand growth and a deliberate strategic buffer ahead of anticipated supply chain constraints.
Days inventory outstanding improved slightly to 59.8 days from 61.2 days in the prior year, indicating efficient inventory turnover despite higher absolute levels.
Accounts receivable stood at $112.4 million, with a days sales outstanding figure of 48.4 days.
The company's current ratio was 2.31 as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a healthy short-term liquidity profile.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was calculated at 14.6%, exceeding the company's internal target of 12.5%.
The board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million, to be executed over the subsequent eighteen months.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltoran Dynamics repurchased 482,000 shares at an average price of $61.40 per share, totaling approximately $29.6 million.
Total shareholders' equity increased to $631.7 million, compared to $571.3 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's environmental sustainability report, published alongside the annual financial filing, documented a 9.3% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
Veltoran Dynamics was included in the Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index for the second consecutive year, recognizing its workforce inclusion programs.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Young LLP completed the independent financial audit without material findings or qualified opinions.
Randall Okonkwo provided fiscal year 2024 guidance, projecting total revenue in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, reflecting expected operating leverage from higher revenues.
The company expects capital expenditures in the range of $80 million to $90 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily supporting the European manufacturing build-out.
Margaret Solvay closed the executive overview by reaffirming Veltoran Dynamics' commitment to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation.
The company will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, in Charlotte, where detailed segment forecasts and technology roadmaps will be presented.
All financial statements included in this report have been prepared in accordance with United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Forward-looking statements contained herein are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltora Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year 2023 annual report on March 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 1987, operates across three primary business segments: Advanced Coatings, Industrial Solvents, and Polymer Additives.
Chief Executive Officer Maarten van der Berg opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience amid ongoing macroeconomic headwinds.
Chief Financial Officer Signe Halvorsen noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal period of transformation for Veltora Industries.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase compared to the €1.72 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The Advanced Coatings segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating €712 million, or approximately 38.7% of total group revenues.
The Industrial Solvents segment posted revenues of €634 million, reflecting modest growth of 4.1% year-over-year driven by increased demand from automotive customers.
The Polymer Additives segment recorded €494 million in revenues, the strongest growth rate among the three segments at 11.3% year-over-year.
Gross profit for the full year stood at €541 million, yielding a gross margin of 29.4%, compared to 27.8% in the prior year.
The improvement in gross margin was attributed to favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiency initiatives undertaken in the first half of the year.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled €298 million, an increase of 5.6% from the prior year's €282 million.
Research and development expenditure increased to €87 million, representing 4.7% of total revenues, as the company accelerated its sustainable chemistry pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to €211 million, held roughly flat relative to the prior year on an absolute basis.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached €243 million, resulting in an EBITDA margin of 13.2%.
Net income attributable to shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was €134 million, compared to €109 million in fiscal year 2022, an increase of 22.9%.
Diluted earnings per share rose to €2.87, up from €2.34 in the prior year, reflecting both earnings growth and a modest reduction in diluted share count.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €0.72 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.08 per share, a 12.5% increase year-over-year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled €156 million, with the majority allocated to capacity expansion at the Antwerp and Gdańsk facilities.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €181 million, providing the company with substantial flexibility for strategic deployment.
The company's balance sheet remained conservatively positioned at year-end, with a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 1.4 times.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Industries carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2018 revolving credit facility in November 2023.
Total headcount across all global operations grew to 6,840 employees by the end of fiscal year 2023, an increase of 320 employees from the prior year-end figure.
The company's workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing roles, particularly at its newly expanded Antwerp production facility.
Veltora Industries continued to invest in employee development, with training and development expenditures per employee rising 18% year-over-year.
The company's voluntary attrition rate declined to 8.3% in 2023, down from 11.7% in 2022, reflecting improved engagement and compensation competitiveness.
In October 2023, Veltora Industries secured a €350 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of seven European banks to support its capital structure.
The Advanced Coatings segment launched four new product lines during the year, including the EcoShield Pro series targeting the marine coatings market.
The EcoShield Pro series received strong initial reception, with letters of intent from eight major European shipbuilders signed by year-end.
Industrial Solvents expanded its distribution network into three new markets: Poland, Romania, and the Czech Republic.
The geographic expansion in Central and Eastern Europe contributed an incremental €28 million in revenues during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Polymer Additives secured a multi-year supply agreement with a leading German automotive OEM valued at approximately €210 million over five years.
The automotive supply agreement is expected to begin contributing meaningfully to revenues beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Sustainability initiatives remained a central pillar of Veltora Industries' strategic agenda throughout 2023.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 14% relative to the 2020 baseline, ahead of its 2025 target of a 20% reduction.
Water consumption across manufacturing operations declined by 9.2% year-over-year, saving an estimated 1.4 million cubic meters.
Renewable energy accounted for 41% of the company's total electricity consumption in 2023, up from 29% in 2022.
Veltora Industries received an upgraded ESG rating from Sustainalytics, moving from a Medium Risk classification to a Low Risk classification in June 2023.
The company's supply chain resilience program, initiated in 2022, reached full implementation in the second quarter of 2023.
Key outcomes of the supply chain program included a reduction in sole-source supplier dependencies from 34% to 21% of total procurement spend.
Inventory days on hand decreased from 68 days to 54 days over the course of fiscal year 2023, improving working capital efficiency.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year, supported by enhanced collections processes.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.1 at year-end, reflecting a well-managed liquidity position.
Veltora Industries completed the divestiture of its non-core Textile Auxiliaries business unit in February 2023 for proceeds of €62 million.
The divestiture resulted in a pre-tax gain of €18 million, which was recorded in other operating income for the first quarter of 2023.
Net proceeds from the divestiture were used to fund share repurchases under the company's previously announced €75 million buyback program.
A total of 2.1 million shares were repurchased during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of €31.40 per share.
The company's shares closed fiscal year 2023 at €34.60, representing a total return to shareholders of 28.4% including dividends.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue growth in the range of 5% to 8%.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at between 13.5% and 14.5%, reflecting expected operating leverage as volumes grow.
The company expects capital expenditures to moderate to approximately €130 million to €145 million in fiscal year 2024.
Management highlighted the planned commissioning of a new specialty polymers production line at the Gdańsk facility as a key growth catalyst for 2024.
The board of directors expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction and the quality of its management team.
Director Anneliese Brandt, chair of the Audit Committee, confirmed that internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective as of year-end.
The independent auditor, Kellerman & Vos N.V., issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Kellerman & Vos identified no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in Veltora Industries' financial reporting processes during the 2023 audit.
Veltora Industries remains committed to delivering sustainable value creation for all stakeholders through disciplined capital allocation and operational excellence.
The company anticipates continued momentum in its core markets and remains well positioned to navigate evolving regulatory and macroeconomic conditions in 2024.
This report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied.
Meridian Coastal Logistics, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the maritime freight and port services sector, providing end-to-end cargo handling solutions across the Asia-Pacific and North American corridors.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Wyndham opened the annual report with a statement of confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Okafor noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a period of both challenge and transformation for Meridian Coastal Logistics.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $4.82 billion, representing a 7.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The Asia-Pacific segment contributed $2.91 billion to total revenue, accounting for approximately 60.4% of the consolidated top line.
North American port operations generated $1.91 billion in revenue, with the remainder attributed to ancillary logistics services.
Gross profit for the year stood at $1.34 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, totaled $712 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $820 million, exceeding internal guidance of $795 million.
Net income attributable to shareholders was $403 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.17.
This represents a 14.2% improvement in diluted EPS compared to the $2.78 reported in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $389 million, primarily directed toward fleet modernization and port infrastructure upgrades.
The company commissioned four new container vessels during the fiscal year, expanding its operational fleet to 38 ships.
Chief Operating Officer Renata Szymanski highlighted the successful integration of the Harborview Port Terminal acquisition completed in Q1 2023.
The Harborview acquisition added approximately $210 million in annualized revenue and introduced new deep-water berthing capabilities.
Employee headcount at year-end stood at 14,200 full-time equivalents, up from 12,900 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was primarily driven by the consolidation of Harborview's workforce of approximately 1,100 employees.
Voluntary employee attrition for fiscal year 2023 was reported at 8.4%, below the industry average of 11.2%.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $9.14 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $5.67 billion, and shareholders' equity was reported at $3.47 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to $614 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The current ratio at fiscal year-end was 1.84, and the quick ratio was 1.52, both indicating healthy short-term liquidity.
Return on equity for the fiscal year was 11.6%, compared to 10.1% in the prior year, reflecting improved profitability.
Return on invested capital reached 8.9%, exceeding the company's weighted average cost of capital of 7.4%.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.32 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $1.28 per share.
This marks the seventh consecutive year in which Meridian Coastal Logistics has increased its annual dividend payout.
The dividend payout ratio for fiscal year 2023 was approximately 40.4%, consistent with the company's stated capital return policy.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 3.2 million shares under its existing $250 million share buyback authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $74 million remained available under the current buyback program.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Okafor stated that the company entered fiscal year 2023 with a clearly defined leverage target of below 2.0x net debt to EBITDA.
By year-end, the net debt to EBITDA ratio had improved to 1.7x, comfortably within the stated target range.
Management attributed the deleveraging to strong operating cash flow generation of $698 million during the year.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Coastal Logistics carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Fuel costs, which represent the largest single component of operating expenditure, declined by 6.1% year-over-year due to favorable bunker fuel contracts.
The company's hedging program covered approximately 70% of its projected fuel requirements for fiscal year 2024 at locked-in rates.
Technology investment increased by 22% year-over-year, with $87 million allocated to digital freight management platforms and port automation systems.
The newly launched Meridian Connect platform processed over 1.4 million cargo transactions during its first full year of operation.
In September 2023, the company finalized a $450 million syndicated term loan facility to support its ongoing capital investment program.
Segment operating margins for Asia-Pacific and North America were 18.3% and 15.7%, respectively, for fiscal year 2023.
The Asia-Pacific margin improvement of 210 basis points was driven by higher vessel utilization rates averaging 84.6% across the fleet.
North American margins contracted by 40 basis points compared to the prior year, owing to increased port congestion costs at the Vancouver terminal.
Environmental compliance expenditures totaled $43 million in fiscal year 2023, representing a 31% increase versus fiscal year 2022.
Meridian Coastal Logistics achieved a 9.4% reduction in carbon emissions intensity per twenty-foot equivalent unit shipped compared to 2022.
The company's sustainability report, published concurrently with this financial report, details progress toward its 2030 net-zero emissions target.
Credit ratings from Moody's and S&P remained unchanged at Baa1 and BBB+, respectively, reflecting stable financial health.
Total interest expense for fiscal year 2023 was $118 million, down from $134 million in fiscal year 2022.
The decrease in interest expense reflects both lower average outstanding debt balances and a modest reduction in blended borrowing costs.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $228 million, consistent with the elevated asset base following the Harborview acquisition.
Inventory and supplies on hand at December 31, 2023, were valued at $156 million, primarily comprising spare parts and fuel stocks.
Accounts receivable at year-end was $487 million, with days sales outstanding of 36.9 days, slightly improved from 38.4 days in 2022.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $18 million, representing approximately 3.7% of gross receivables.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $5.05 billion to $5.25 billion, implying growth of 4.8% to 8.9%.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $860 million to $890 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
Patricia Wyndham emphasized that the company's geographic diversification and long-term customer contracts provide significant revenue visibility.
Approximately 68% of fiscal year 2024 projected revenue is already secured through multi-year contracts with tier-one shipping and retail clients.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of $410 million to $440 million in fiscal year 2024, consistent with its fleet renewal schedule.
Meridian Coastal Logistics maintains a strategic partnership with Pacific Rim Port Authority, facilitating preferential berthing access at six major ports.
The partnership agreement was renewed for a further ten-year term in November 2023, underpinning operational continuity through 2033.
Executive Vice President of Strategy, James Otieno, outlined three growth initiatives targeting intermodal expansion in Southeast Asia.
The first initiative involves co-investment with regional port operators in Indonesia and Vietnam, with combined investment of $175 million over three years.
The second initiative targets the development of a dedicated cold-chain logistics corridor connecting Australian agricultural exporters to Japanese retailers.
The third initiative focuses on expanding digitally enabled freight brokerage services under the Meridian Connect brand across 14 new markets.
The Board of Directors and executive leadership team express their appreciation to shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued support.
Meridian Coastal Logistics remains committed to delivering sustainable, long-term value creation as it navigates the evolving global logistics landscape.
Thornwick Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in the development and commercialization of oncology therapeutics and companion diagnostic platforms.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margaux Delacroix presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Thornwick reported total consolidated revenues of $842.7 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The company carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final remaining obligations in March of that year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that gross margins improved to 68.3%, up from 64.1% in fiscal 2022, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Thornwick's flagship oncology drug, Velotrex, recorded net product sales of $511.4 million, accounting for approximately 60.7% of total company revenue.
The commercial team, led by Executive Vice President Sandra Chu, expanded the Velotrex sales force by 18% in the second half of 2023.
Research and development expenditures reached $198.6 million, representing 23.6% of total revenues, reflecting the company's commitment to its late-stage pipeline.
During the third quarter of 2023, Thornwick entered into a $350 million senior secured credit facility with a syndicate of lenders led by First Meridian Capital.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $134.2 million for the full year, a moderate increase of 7.1% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to Thornwick shareholders was $187.3 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.8%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 22.5% to 23.0%.
Cash and cash equivalents, together with short-term investments, stood at $623.5 million as of the balance sheet date.
Operating cash flow for the year was $241.8 million, a significant improvement from the $189.4 million generated in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures amounted to $47.3 million, primarily directed toward expansion of the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Thornwick repurchased 2.1 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under its board-authorized $200 million buyback program.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing the company's fourth consecutive year of dividend growth.
Dr. Delacroix highlighted the successful Phase III readout for pipeline candidate TBX-441 in non-small cell lung cancer as a defining moment for the organization.
TBX-441 demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival compared to the current standard of care in the pivotal LUMINA-3 trial.
The company submitted a New Drug Application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for TBX-441 in November 2023, with a PDUFA date anticipated in the third quarter of 2024.
Thornwick's companion diagnostics division contributed $87.6 million in revenues, reflecting a 22.9% year-over-year increase.
The diagnostics growth was largely attributable to expanded co-commercialization agreements with three major hospital networks in the Midwest and Southeast.
International revenues, generated primarily through the company's European affiliate Thornwick GmbH, increased by 9.3% to $143.8 million.
The European business benefited from formulary approvals in Germany, France, and Spain during the first half of the fiscal year.
Inventory levels at year-end were $62.4 million, broadly consistent with the prior year and reflecting stable supply chain management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, indicating enhanced collection efficiency.
Dr. Osei indicated that the company's hedging program effectively mitigated approximately $11.2 million of foreign exchange headwinds during the year.
Thornwick's total headcount grew to 3,847 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, compared to 3,412 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in workforce was concentrated in clinical operations, regulatory affairs, and the U.S. commercial organization.
The company's stock price appreciated by approximately 34% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index which returned roughly 8% over the same period.
Market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $5.1 billion based on a closing price of $93.47 per share.
Dr. Delacroix emphasized the organization's strong culture of innovation and its track record of delivering pipeline milestones ahead of schedule.
The audit committee reviewed and approved the consolidated financial statements, which were audited by the independent registered public accounting firm Calloway & Brennan LLP.
Calloway & Brennan issued an unqualified audit opinion with no material weaknesses identified in Thornwick's internal controls over financial reporting.
Management reaffirmed its commitment to disciplined capital allocation, balancing organic investment, strategic business development, and shareholder returns.
For fiscal 2024, Thornwick issued revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $960 million, implying growth of approximately 9% to 14% at the midpoint.
The guidance assumes continued Velotrex volume growth, anticipated launch revenues from TBX-441 upon approval, and stable diagnostics performance.
Operating expense guidance for 2024 was set at $360 million to $380 million, reflecting planned increases in commercial infrastructure ahead of the TBX-441 launch.
The company also guided for R&D spending of $215 million to $230 million, supporting multiple mid-stage and late-stage clinical programs.
Thornwick's pipeline comprises 11 active clinical programs spanning oncology, immunology, and precision medicine indications.
Two additional programs are anticipated to advance into Phase II trials during 2024, contingent on positive Phase I dose-escalation results.
Dr. Chu noted that payer coverage for Velotrex expanded to include five additional commercial insurance formularies in the fourth quarter of 2023.
Average net selling price for Velotrex remained stable at approximately $28,400 per treatment course, consistent with the prior year.
The company does not anticipate material generic competition for Velotrex before 2031, given existing patent protections and regulatory exclusivities.
Thornwick's royalty revenue stream, derived from licensing agreements on two legacy small-molecule compounds, contributed $18.9 million during fiscal 2023.
This royalty income is expected to decline modestly in coming years as underlying patent coverage begins to narrow.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside the annual report, details progress on carbon neutrality targets set for 2030.
Thornwick reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 14% in 2023 relative to the 2020 baseline year.
The company made a $5 million charitable contribution to the Thornwick Foundation, which focuses on expanding access to oncology care in underserved communities.
Dr. Delacroix acknowledged that pricing pressure on biopharmaceuticals remains a key risk factor in the regulatory and legislative environment.
Thornwick maintains a robust pharmacovigilance infrastructure and reported no new black box warning additions to its marketed products during the year.
The company's deferred tax liability decreased by $8.4 million during fiscal 2023, largely due to the utilization of previously unrecognized net operating loss carryforwards.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $312.1 million, unchanged from the prior year, as no impairment charges were identified during the annual assessment.
The company holds a 19.9% minority equity stake in Caledra Diagnostics, a private precision oncology startup based in Edinburgh, Scotland.
This investment is carried at cost less impairment and has not been adjusted in value since the initial investment was made in fiscal 2021.
Thornwick's board of directors consists of ten members, eight of whom are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
Compensation for named executive officers in fiscal 2023 included a combination of base salary, annual cash incentives, and long-term equity awards.
Dr. Delacroix's total compensation for fiscal 2023 was $7.2 million, consisting of $950,000 in base salary, a $1.4 million annual bonus, and $4.85 million in equity grants.
The proxy statement filed concurrently with the annual report provides full details of the company's executive compensation philosophy and peer group benchmarking.
Thornwick's say-on-pay vote at the 2023 annual meeting received approximately 91% approval from shareholders, reflecting broad alignment with the compensation framework.
Management and the board remain committed to transparent disclosure and long-term value creation for all Thornwick stakeholders.

The foregoing financial information is presented in accordance with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles and is subject to the full risk factors described in the Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Velarion Technologies Corporation, headquartered in Austin, Texas, is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider serving industries ranging from healthcare to logistics.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holsworth and CTO Daniel Freiberg, both of whom continue to lead the organization today.
Velarion Technologies reported total annual revenue of $847.3 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2 percent.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for $612.4 million of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful transition toward a software-as-a-service model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed an additional $234.9 million, rounding out the company's two primary revenue streams.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $521.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.6 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $389.7 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures alone amounted to $143.2 million, reflecting Velarion's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing costs were reported at $172.5 million, driven largely by expanded enterprise sales headcount and increased digital advertising spend.
General and administrative expenses came in at $74.0 million, remaining broadly flat compared to the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $131.9 million, a 22.7 percent improvement over the $107.5 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $98.4 million, after accounting for interest expense, tax provisions, and minority interest adjustments.
Diluted earnings per share stood at $1.84, compared to $1.51 in the prior year, exceeding consensus analyst estimates of $1.76.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure the company uses to assess core operating performance, was reported at $187.3 million for fiscal 2023.
The adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.1 percent represented a 150-basis-point expansion relative to the 20.6 percent margin achieved in fiscal 2022.
CFO Sandra Okafor commented that the margin improvement was driven by disciplined cost management and favorable revenue mix shifts toward higher-margin subscription products.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 stood at $1.42 billion, up from $1.27 billion one year prior.
Cash and cash equivalents were reported at $314.8 million, providing what management characterized as ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
Accounts receivable net of allowances totaled $189.6 million, consistent with the company's typical 45-to-60-day collection cycle.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet reached $227.1 million, reflecting the strong forward momentum in multi-year subscription contracts signed during the year.
Total liabilities amounted to $603.7 million, a figure that includes operating lease obligations, deferred tax liabilities, and accrued expenses.
Long-term debt as of year-end consisted solely of a $200 million senior secured term loan that was originally issued in 2020 and is due to mature in 2027.
The term loan carries a fixed interest rate of 4.35 percent per annum, and Velarion has been making scheduled principal amortization payments of $20 million annually.
Shareholders' equity stood at $816.3 million, reflecting retained earnings growth and the company's ongoing share repurchase program.
During fiscal 2023, Velarion repurchased 3.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $42.70, retiring them in full.
The board of directors authorized an incremental $150 million share repurchase program in October 2023, signaling confidence in the company's long-term prospects.
Velarion's global headcount reached 5,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 5,412 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company added net 435 employees during the year, with the majority of new hires concentrated in product engineering and customer success roles.
Velarion operates across four reportable business segments: Cloud Platform, Data Analytics, Security Solutions, and Managed Services.
The Cloud Platform segment generated revenue of $391.2 million, making it the largest contributor at approximately 46.2 percent of total revenue.
Data Analytics revenue grew 19.8 percent year-over-year to $201.6 million, driven by strong demand for the company's newly launched Velarion Insight Pro suite.
Security Solutions contributed $148.3 million in revenue, reflecting growing enterprise investment in cybersecurity capabilities amid a heightened threat environment.
Managed Services revenue was $106.2 million, the only segment to experience modest year-over-year growth of just 3.1 percent as customers migrated to self-service cloud options.

Margaret Holsworth, speaking at the annual investor day held in November 2023, expressed strong optimism about international expansion opportunities, particularly across Southeast Asia and Central Europe.
The company entered three new country markets during fiscal 2023, establishing sales and support offices in Singapore, Poland, and Colombia.
Velarion Technologies concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any outstanding revolving credit facility obligations, maintaining a clean and unencumbered credit revolver balance of zero.
Customer retention rates for the Cloud Platform and Data Analytics segments averaged 94.3 percent on a net revenue retention basis, reflecting the stickiness of the platform.
The company signed 214 new enterprise contracts during fiscal 2023, defined as agreements with annual contract value exceeding $250,000.
Average annual contract value among new enterprise customers was $1.14 million, a figure that compares favorably to the $890,000 average recorded in fiscal 2022.
Daniel Freiberg announced in December 2023 the upcoming release of Velarion's next-generation AI orchestration layer, branded as VelarionOS 4.0, scheduled for a Q2 2024 launch.
In January 2024, Velarion drew down $175 million under a newly established revolving credit facility to fund an accelerated acquisition pipeline identified by the corporate development team.
The VelarionOS 4.0 platform is expected to unify the company's disparate analytics and security modules into a single coherent operating environment.
Management anticipates that VelarionOS 4.0 will reduce average implementation timelines from approximately 14 weeks to fewer than 8 weeks for mid-market clients.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $67.4 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades and owned-office facility improvements.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year was $55.4 million, reflecting the aging of previously capitalized software development costs.
Free cash flow, calculated as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was reported at $163.2 million for fiscal 2023.
This represents a 31.4 percent increase in free cash flow compared to the $124.2 million reported for fiscal 2022.
The company's free cash flow conversion rate, expressed as a percentage of net income, was approximately 165.8 percent, well above the software sector median.
Velarion Technologies paid no cash dividends during fiscal 2023 and does not expect to initiate a dividend program in fiscal 2024, preferring to reinvest cash into growth initiatives.
Effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4 percent, modestly below the prior year's rate of 22.9 percent, benefiting from R&D tax credits in multiple jurisdictions.
The company's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $46.18 per share, delivering a total return to shareholders of approximately 28.3 percent over the calendar year.
Velarion's market capitalization at year-end stood at approximately $2.47 billion, placing it firmly within the small-to-mid-cap enterprise software universe.
Independent auditors Ernst & Wallace LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements, noting no material weaknesses in internal controls.
The audit committee, chaired by board member Patricia Chin-Watanabe, oversaw all aspects of the external audit process and reviewed related-party transactions.
Velarion Technologies has no material pending litigation that management believes could result in a loss exceeding $10 million, based on current legal assessments.
The company maintains directors' and officers' liability insurance coverage with aggregate limits of $75 million per occurrence.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $985 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to fall between $205 million and $215 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $2.05 to $2.15, assuming no material changes in share count or tax legislation.
Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainty, including the potential impact of prolonged elevated interest rates on enterprise software spending cycles.

Nevertheless, Velarion Technologies enters fiscal 2024 with a robust pipeline, a differentiated product portfolio, and an experienced leadership team well-positioned to deliver sustained shareholder value.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veralon Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in oncology and rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Scholl and has since grown to become a significant player in the specialty pharmaceutical sector.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Wexler presented the annual report to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Anand emphasized the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation throughout the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veralon Therapeutics reported total net revenues of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Product revenues from the company's flagship oncology drug, Vorexil, accounted for $924 million of the total net revenue figure.
Royalty and licensing revenues contributed an additional $183 million, while contract manufacturing services added $363 million to the top line.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $887 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 60.3%.
Research and development expenses totaled $312 million, representing 21.2% of net revenues, consistent with the company's long-term investment philosophy.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $241 million for the year, reflecting ongoing investments in commercial infrastructure.
Operating income came in at $334 million, compared with $289 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a 15.6% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to Veralon Therapeutics shareholders was $271 million, or $3.84 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation and certain one-time items, was $412 million for the full year.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $638 million.
Operating cash flow for the year was $389 million, a robust result that underpinned the board's confidence in the company's liquidity position.
Capital expenditures amounted to $78 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's biologics manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, therefore stood at $311 million for the full year.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $200 million in October 2023, of which $47 million had been utilized by year-end.
Dr. Scholl, who now serves as Executive Chairwoman, expressed confidence in the pipeline's ability to sustain long-term shareholder value creation.
Veralon's oncology pipeline currently includes seven clinical-stage compounds, with three in Phase 3 trials and four in Phase 2 trials.
The lead Phase 3 candidate, VRL-441, is a next-generation PARP inhibitor targeting platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, with topline data expected in the third quarter of 2024.
The rare metabolic disorder franchise saw meaningful growth during fiscal 2023, with unit volumes for Metavore increasing 18% year-over-year.
Metavore net sales reached $247 million in fiscal 2023, up from $209 million in fiscal 2022.
Management attributes the Metavore growth to successful patient identification programs and expanded reimbursement coverage across European markets.
Veralon Therapeutics employed a total of 3,214 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 2,978 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's headcount expansion was primarily concentrated in clinical development, regulatory affairs, and commercial operations.
As of the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Veralon Therapeutics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely free of interest-bearing obligations.
The global oncology market, where Veralon competes most directly, is estimated to exceed $250 billion by 2027, presenting substantial growth opportunities.
Veralon maintained a strong credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year, reflecting its stable financial profile.
The company's international revenues grew 19.4% in fiscal 2023, with Europe and Japan representing the fastest-growing geographies.
CFO Priya Anand noted that currency headwinds reduced reported international revenues by approximately $34 million during the year.
In November 2023, Veralon Therapeutics entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of eight major financial institutions to fund strategic growth initiatives.
The company's tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.7%, benefiting from research and development tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Deferred tax assets totaled $94 million at year-end, primarily related to net operating loss carryforwards from earlier-stage operations.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023, were $162 million, up from $138 million at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting strategic safety stock builds.
Accounts receivable stood at $218 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of approximately 54 days, within the company's target range.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $6.4 million, representing management's estimate of potential credit losses in the receivables portfolio.
Veralon entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Kyoto-based Tanamine Pharmaceuticals in September 2023, covering co-development rights for VRL-441 in Asia-Pacific markets.
The Tanamine collaboration included an upfront payment of $85 million received by Veralon, with potential milestone payments of up to $620 million.
CEO Jonathan Wexler described the Tanamine deal as transformative for the company's long-term competitive positioning in the Asia-Pacific oncology segment.
The company's goodwill balance of $407 million reflects acquisitions completed between 2015 and 2021, including the landmark purchase of Cellova Biosciences.
Intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, stood at $531 million, comprising primarily acquired intellectual property and in-process research and development.
Amortization of intangible assets was $62 million for fiscal 2023, consistent with the prior year's amortization charge.
Veralon declared and paid quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.80 per share during fiscal 2023, unchanged from the prior year's dividend rate.
Total dividends paid in fiscal 2023 amounted to approximately $56 million based on the weighted average share count.
The company maintained a dividend payout ratio of approximately 20.7% relative to reported net income, reflecting a balanced approach to capital returns.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management provided revenue guidance in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion, representing projected growth of approximately 9% to 12%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $440 million to $465 million, implying continued margin expansion.
R&D expense guidance for fiscal 2024 was provided in the range of $340 million to $360 million, reflecting anticipated Phase 3 trial costs for VRL-441.
The company expects capital expenditures of $85 million to $95 million in fiscal 2024, largely tied to the ongoing Durham facility expansion.
Jonathan Wexler stated that Veralon's mission remains the development of transformative medicines that meaningfully extend and improve patients' lives.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Patricia Hollis, oversaw the financial reporting process and confirmed the integrity of internal controls.
Veralon's external auditor, Grant Thornton LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the fiscal 2023 consolidated financial statements.
The company faces ongoing litigation related to a patent challenge filed by generic manufacturer Harrix Pharma regarding Vorexil's composition-of-matter patents.
