Management, in consultation with outside legal counsel, believes the litigation risk is manageable and has not recorded any material contingent liability related to the matter.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives at Veralon included a commitment to achieve carbon neutrality in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.
The company reduced its greenhouse gas emissions intensity by 8% in fiscal 2023 relative to the fiscal 2022 baseline, making meaningful progress toward its climate targets.
Veralon Therapeutics remains committed to transparent financial reporting, disciplined execution of its strategic plan, and the creation of enduring value for all stakeholders.
Veramont Specialty Chemicals Inc. is pleased to present its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates across three core segments: industrial solvents, polymer additives, and specialty coatings.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Dunmore opened the annual review by highlighting the resilience of Veramont's business model amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year's $1.68 billion.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Oyelaran attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand in the polymer additives segment, which grew 14.2% year-over-year.
The industrial solvents segment delivered revenues of $612 million, up modestly from $589 million in fiscal year 2022.
Specialty coatings, the company's newest reporting segment, contributed $287 million to total revenue, exceeding internal forecasts by approximately $22 million.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $498 million, reflecting a gross margin of 27.1%, compared to 25.8% in the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $214 million, including $88 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditure reached $63 million in 2023, representing a meaningful increase from the $51 million invested in 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $341 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.5%, up from 16.9% in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veramont shareholders totaled $187 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, compared to $154 million, or $2.81 per diluted share, in 2022.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an annualized increase of 12% over the prior dividend level.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $119 million, with the majority directed toward capacity expansion at the company's Dayton, Ohio facility.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $222 million, providing the company with meaningful financial flexibility heading into fiscal 2024.
Veramont ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $346 million, up from $278 million at the end of 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Fleiss noted that operational efficiency improvements contributed approximately 80 basis points to the year-over-year gross margin expansion.
The company's global headcount stood at 7,140 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 employees compared to the prior year-end.
Employee additions were concentrated in the polymer additives and specialty coatings divisions, reflecting strategic growth priorities.
Veramont's North American operations accounted for 68% of total revenue, with Europe contributing 21% and Asia-Pacific the remaining 11%.
European revenue grew 6.7% in local currency terms, though foreign exchange headwinds reduced the reported dollar contribution.
Asia-Pacific revenues expanded by 18.3%, driven by new customer wins in South Korea and an expanding distribution network in Vietnam.
The company launched two new product lines in 2023: VeraShield 900, a high-performance protective coating, and SolvaMax Plus, an industrial cleaning solvent blend.
VeraShield 900 received regulatory approval in the United States and the European Union and is now available in twelve markets globally.
Early commercial uptake of VeraShield 900 has been encouraging, with orders from four large industrial customers secured in the fourth quarter alone.
SolvaMax Plus generated approximately $14 million in revenue during its first six months on the market, ahead of the product team's initial projections.
The polymer additives segment achieved record segment operating income of $198 million, reflecting the benefits of favorable raw material pricing and volume leverage.
Chief Procurement Officer Daniel Kwon stated that strategic sourcing initiatives reduced raw material costs by approximately $27 million relative to 2022 levels.
Veramont's supply chain transformation program, launched in mid-2022, has now delivered cumulative savings of approximately $44 million across two fiscal years.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from S&P Global Ratings, unchanged from the prior year.
Long-term debt at December 31, 2023 stood at $520 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes issued at a weighted average interest rate of 4.1%.
Interest expense for fiscal 2023 totaled $22 million, consistent with the prior year given the fixed-rate nature of the company's debt portfolio.
Veramont's net leverage ratio, defined as net debt divided by adjusted EBITDA, declined to 0.51 times at year-end, down from 0.79 times at end of 2022.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during 2023 under its authorized share buyback program, at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total shareholder returns for fiscal 2023, including dividends and share price appreciation, amounted to approximately 22%, outperforming the sector benchmark of 14%.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that Veramont's balance sheet is entirely free of external borrowings, positioning the company as essentially debt-free entering fiscal 2024.
Patricia Dunmore emphasized that Veramont's strong liquidity position would enable it to pursue bolt-on acquisitions without the need to access external capital markets.
In January 2024, the company successfully completed a $350 million syndicated term loan with a consortium of eight commercial banks to finance potential strategic transactions.
The specialty coatings segment is expected to reach breakeven profitability on a segment operating income basis by the second quarter of 2024.
Management guided for total revenue of between $1.95 billion and $2.02 billion in fiscal 2024, implying growth of approximately 6% to 10% over 2023 levels.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $360 million to $385 million, reflecting continued margin improvement expectations.
Chief Financial Officer Oyelaran noted that the company has operated without any reliance on bank credit facilities throughout all of fiscal 2023 and into early 2024.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is approximately $130 million to $140 million, with significant investments planned for the new specialty coatings manufacturing line in Georgia.
The Georgia facility, expected to be operational by the third quarter of 2024, will add an estimated 35,000 metric tons of annual production capacity.
Veramont's environmental, social, and governance initiatives made measurable progress in 2023, including a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions per unit of output.
The company achieved ISO 14001 recertification across all seven of its manufacturing facilities during the year.
Veramont's diversity and inclusion programs resulted in women representing 38% of new hires in 2023, up from 31% in 2022.
The Board of Directors added two independent directors in 2023, bringing total board membership to eleven individuals with diverse industry backgrounds.
Audit Committee Chair Eleanor Vance confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
The company's effective tax rate for 2023 was 22.4%, below the statutory federal rate due to benefits associated with the research and development tax credit.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days at year-end 2023, down from 47 days at the close of fiscal 2022, reflecting strengthened collections processes.
Inventory turnover improved to 6.2 times annually, compared to 5.7 times in the prior year, as the company optimized safety stock levels across its distribution network.
The company did not experience any material supply disruptions during 2023, a marked contrast to the logistics challenges encountered in 2021 and 2022.
Veramont's order backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at $418 million, representing approximately 83 days of annualized revenue coverage.
Management believes the current backlog level provides good near-term revenue visibility and reduces execution risk entering the new fiscal year.
Overall, fiscal 2023 represented a record year for Veramont Specialty Chemicals across multiple financial and operational dimensions.
The company's disciplined capital allocation, innovation pipeline, and expanding customer relationships form the foundation for continued value creation.
Veramont's leadership team remains committed to delivering sustainable, profitable growth and superior returns to all stakeholders in the years ahead.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals company headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company reported consolidated net revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Executive Officer Marta Hollenbeck attributed the strong top-line performance to robust demand across the company's adhesives and polymer coatings divisions.
Veltrano's gross profit margin expanded by 120 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.7% for the full fiscal year.
The adhesives segment alone contributed €712 million in revenues, making it the largest single revenue driver for the company.
Operating income for the period was €294 million, compared to €251 million in the prior year, reflecting disciplined cost management.
Chief Financial Officer Pieter Vanstraelen stated that the company maintained rigorous controls over its selling, general, and administrative expenses throughout the year.
SG&A expenses as a percentage of revenue declined to 14.2% in fiscal 2023, down from 15.8% in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders reached €198 million, yielding earnings per share of €3.47 on a diluted basis.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 24.1%, broadly in line with management's guidance of 23% to 25%.
Veltrano generated free cash flow of €221 million during fiscal 2023, a record high for the company.
Capital expenditures totaled €87 million, largely directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Antwerp facility.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.10 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.75 per share.
This represents a 12% increase in the total annual dividend relative to the €1.56 per share paid in fiscal 2022.
The polymer coatings segment delivered revenues of €638 million, growing 11.4% year-over-year driven by strong automotive sector demand.
Veltrano's specialty solvents division, its smallest segment, contributed €490 million in revenues for the fiscal year.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a total headcount of 8,240 full-time employees, an increase of 310 from the prior year-end.
Veltrano carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of deliberate deleveraging.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €319 million, up modestly from €298 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days, down from 47 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to €463 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity.
Marta Hollenbeck emphasized that the strong cash position would allow Veltrano to pursue strategic bolt-on acquisitions in 2024.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrano entered into a €350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of European banks.
Research and development expenditures for the year totaled €96 million, equivalent to approximately 5.2% of net revenues.
The company filed 14 new patent applications during fiscal 2023, primarily related to next-generation bio-based adhesive formulations.
Veltrano's return on invested capital for the year was 16.8%, up from 14.3% in fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its ongoing share buyback program at an average price of €41.30.
Total shares repurchased since the program's inception in 2019 now amount to 6.7 million shares at a cumulative cost of €238 million.
Pieter Vanstraelen noted that the balance sheet remained conservatively structured, well-positioned to weather potential macroeconomic headwinds.
Veltrano's net working capital position improved to €524 million at year-end, compared to €487 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The Antwerp manufacturing expansion is expected to come online in the second quarter of 2024, adding approximately 18% incremental capacity.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance of €1.95 billion to €2.05 billion, reflecting anticipated continued demand growth.
The company expects its adjusted EBITDA margin to range between 22% and 24% in fiscal 2024.
Veltrano's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was €387 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.0%.
The company conducted its annual goodwill impairment assessment and concluded that no impairment charges were warranted for fiscal 2023.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled €541 million at year-end, predominantly related to the 2018 acquisition of Cresthem Polymers.
Veltrano's total assets as of December 31, 2023, amounted to €2.71 billion, up from €2.53 billion a year earlier.
Shareholders' equity stood at €1.18 billion at year-end, an increase of €143 million from the prior year-end balance.
The company's book value per share at December 31, 2023, was €20.74, based on 56.9 million shares outstanding.
During fiscal 2023, Veltrano successfully renewed its enterprise resource planning system across all European manufacturing sites.
The ERP upgrade is expected to generate annual efficiency savings of approximately €12 million beginning in fiscal 2024.
Veltrano's export revenues accounted for 61% of total net revenues in fiscal 2023, with Asia-Pacific representing the fastest-growing region.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 18.7% year-over-year, reaching €312 million for the fiscal year.
The company opened a new sales and distribution office in Singapore during the third quarter of fiscal 2023.
North American revenues declined modestly by 2.1% to €284 million, reflecting softer demand conditions in certain industrial end markets.
European revenues remained the company's largest geographic segment, contributing €1.24 billion or approximately 67% of total revenues.
Veltrano entered into a strategic supply agreement with BioNordic Materials in June 2023 to secure sustainable raw material inputs.
The supply agreement with BioNordic is structured over a five-year term with annual minimum purchase commitments of €45 million.
Chief Operating Officer Elsa Drenthen oversaw the successful integration of the Cresthem Polymers legacy systems during fiscal 2023.
Elsa Drenthen also led Veltrano's safety performance initiatives, which resulted in a 22% reduction in lost-time incidents year-over-year.
The company's total recordable incident rate fell to 0.83 per 100 full-time employees, the lowest level in Veltrano's operating history.
Veltrano received an upgraded credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & European Ratings Agency in October 2023, reflecting its financial strength.
The rating upgrade was cited as reflecting improved earnings stability and strong free cash flow generation.
Veltrano's pension obligations on a funded basis showed a surplus of €28 million as of December 31, 2023.
The actuarial gain of €17 million recognized in other comprehensive income reflected favorable changes in discount rate assumptions.
Employee benefit expenses for fiscal 2023 totaled €412 million, including all wages, salaries, and related social contributions.
The company announced in February 2024 that it had completed the acquisition of Meraxis Functional Coatings for a consideration of €165 million.
Meraxis Functional Coatings generated revenues of approximately €88 million in calendar year 2023 and is expected to be immediately accretive.
Marta Hollenbeck described the Meraxis acquisition as a transformative step in strengthening Veltrano's position in the functional coatings market.
The integration of Meraxis is expected to be substantially complete within 18 months of closing, according to management's integration plan.
Veltrano's audit committee confirmed that there were no material weaknesses in the company's internal controls over financial reporting for fiscal 2023.
The external audit was conducted by Hartmann & Breuck LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Veltrano's annual report for fiscal 2023 is available on the company's investor relations website and has been filed with the Dutch Authority for the Financial Markets.
Management remains confident in the company's strategic direction and its ability to deliver sustainable long-term value to shareholders.
Meridian Coastal Holdings, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Savannah, Georgia, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 1998 by entrepreneur Dale Forsythe, has grown into a leading supplier of corrosion-resistant coatings and marine-grade adhesives across North America.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Whitmore opened the report by highlighting the firm's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 9.4 percent increase over the prior year's $774.5 million.
Domestic sales accounted for $612.1 million of that total, while international revenues contributed the remaining $235.2 million.
Gross profit for the period stood at $298.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 35.2 percent.
This compares favorably to the prior year gross margin of 33.8 percent, driven largely by improved raw material sourcing agreements negotiated in early 2023.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $176.4 million for the year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses comprised $112.7 million of that figure, consistent with management's target of keeping SG&A below 14 percent of revenues.
Research and development expenditures rose to $43.9 million, reflecting the company's strategic commitment to next-generation product innovation.
Depreciation and amortization charges added another $19.8 million to operating costs.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $122.2 million, up from $98.7 million in 2022, representing a year-over-year improvement of 23.8 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray noted that EBITDA climbed to $142.0 million, a new record for the company.
Interest expense totaled $8.3 million, down from $11.6 million in the prior year, reflecting successful deleveraging efforts.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $87.4 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.29.
The company's balance sheet showed total assets of $1.14 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Meridian Coastal Holdings closed the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the last tranche of its senior notes in October 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.8 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $134.6 million, consistent with the revenue growth observed during the second half of the year.
Inventory levels were managed carefully, ending the year at $98.2 million, down 4.1 percent from the $102.4 million reported at the end of 2022.
Capital expenditures for 2023 totaled $55.7 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the company's Beaufort, South Carolina manufacturing facility.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, the company secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of regional lenders to fund its planned acquisition pipeline.
The Beaufort facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 18 percent once fully commissioned in mid-2024.
Management also completed the divestiture of its legacy paint additives division in March 2023, receiving net proceeds of $31.2 million.
Those proceeds were partially reinvested into the company's growing epoxy systems product line, which saw revenues expand 27 percent year over year.
The epoxy systems segment now represents 22 percent of total company revenues, up from 19 percent in the prior year.
Sandra Whitmore emphasized that the company's workforce grew to 3,412 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of 214 from the beginning of the period.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in manufacturing and technical support roles at the Beaufort and Wilmington, North Carolina sites.
Employee benefits and compensation costs totaled $189.3 million for the year, representing the company's single largest operating expense category.
Meridian Coastal Holdings maintained its quarterly dividend at $0.28 per share throughout 2023, returning approximately $29.8 million to shareholders over the course of the year.
The board of directors declared a special dividend of $0.15 per share in December 2023, citing exceptional cash generation as justification.
Total shareholder returns for calendar year 2023 were 18.7 percent, outperforming the specialty chemicals peer group average of 11.3 percent.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during the year at an average price of $41.30 per share, totaling approximately $49.6 million.
The remaining authorization under the current share repurchase program stands at $75.4 million as of December 31, 2023.
Marcus Delray commented that working capital management remained a priority, with the cash conversion cycle improving to 48 days from 54 days in the prior year.
Accounts payable at year-end was $72.1 million, reflecting standard payment terms with the company's key chemical feedstock suppliers.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.6 as of December 31, 2023, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position.
Long-term deferred tax liabilities amounted to $23.4 million, largely associated with accelerated depreciation on manufacturing equipment.
The effective tax rate for 2023 was 22.1 percent, modestly below the statutory federal rate due to qualifying R&D tax credits.
Segment reporting reveals that the Marine Coatings division generated revenues of $389.4 million and operating income of $67.8 million.
The Industrial Adhesives segment contributed revenues of $249.6 million and operating income of $38.2 million for the full year.
The Epoxy Systems segment, the company's fastest-growing unit, reported revenues of $186.4 million and operating income of $28.9 million.
Corporate overhead and eliminations reduced consolidated operating income by $12.7 million, consistent with prior year allocations.
Looking ahead, management issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $915 million to $935 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $155 million to $165 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects capital expenditures for 2024 to range between $60 million and $70 million as the Beaufort expansion reaches its final commissioning phase.
Sandra Whitmore highlighted several strategic priorities for the coming year, including accelerating international market penetration in Southeast Asia.
A joint venture agreement with Singapore-based Pacifica Chemical Distributors was signed in November 2023, with operations expected to commence in the third quarter of 2024.
The joint venture is initially capitalized at $22 million, with Meridian contributing $13.2 million for a 60 percent controlling interest.
Management also outlined plans to launch three new product formulations in the first half of 2024, targeting the offshore wind energy infrastructure market.
These new products leverage proprietary polymer chemistry developed through the company's internal R&D program over the past four years.
Market research commissioned by Meridian suggests the addressable offshore wind coatings market could reach $1.8 billion globally by 2028.
The company's risk factors section noted potential headwinds from elevated titanium dioxide and epoxy resin prices, which are key input materials.
Foreign exchange exposure remains limited, as approximately 72 percent of international revenues are denominated in U.S. dollars under long-term supply contracts.
The auditor's report, issued by Caldwell & Marsh LLP, contained an unqualified opinion with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Caldwell & Marsh has served as the company's independent auditor for eleven consecutive years.
The audit committee, chaired by board member Patricia Nguyen, met six times during fiscal year 2023 to review financial reporting matters.
In closing, CEO Sandra Whitmore expressed confidence that Meridian Coastal Holdings is well-positioned to capture incremental market share in its core end markets.
She noted that the company's differentiated product portfolio and customer relationships built over more than two decades remain key competitive advantages.
The company's stock closed at $44.87 per share on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year.
Market capitalization at year-end stood at approximately $1.19 billion, placing Meridian Coastal Holdings comfortably within the mid-cap segment.
Management thanked employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and expressed optimism regarding the company's long-term growth trajectory.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Meridian Crestwood Holdings, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company reported consolidated net revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent compared to fiscal year 2022.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Anneliese Vonderhaar expressed satisfaction with the company's performance, citing strong demand across its core polymer additive and surface coating segments.
Meridian Crestwood operates across four principal business units: Polymer Additives, Surface Coatings, Industrial Solvents, and Specialty Resins.
The Polymer Additives segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating €712 million during the fiscal year.
Surface Coatings posted revenues of €498 million, aided by a recovery in the European automotive OEM market.
Industrial Solvents revenues declined modestly to €341 million, reflecting pricing pressure from Asian competitors.
The Specialty Resins segment contributed €289 million in revenues, buoyed by new contractual agreements with construction-sector clients in the Nordics.
Gross profit for the full year reached €613 million, translating to a gross margin of 33.3 percent.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled €287 million for the twelve-month period.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €326 million, up from €281 million in the prior year.
EBITDA margin improved by 90 basis points to 17.7 percent, reflecting operational efficiency gains achieved through the company's OPEX Reduction Initiative launched in mid-2022.
Net income attributable to shareholders of Meridian Crestwood Holdings was €174 million, compared with €143 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share rose to €3.48, from €2.86 in the preceding fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved a final dividend of €0.92 per share, bringing the total dividend for the fiscal year to €1.47 per share.
This represents a payout ratio of approximately 42 percent of net income, consistent with the company's stated capital return policy.
Chief Financial Officer Maximilian Früh stated that cash generation remained robust throughout the year, with free cash flow reaching €219 million.
Capital expenditures totaled €107 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Cologne and Antwerp production facilities.
The Cologne plant upgrade, completed in the third quarter, added approximately 18,000 metric tons of annual polymer additive production capacity.
Meridian Crestwood's workforce stood at 6,840 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 320 from the prior year-end.
The company expanded its research and development team by 74 specialists during the year, raising the total R&D headcount to 412 professionals.
R&D expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to €63 million, equivalent to 3.4 percent of total revenues.
Three new proprietary formulations were successfully commercialized during the year, contributing an estimated €27 million in incremental revenues.
Meridian Crestwood's order backlog at year-end stood at €438 million, providing solid revenue visibility into the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial debt, having repaid its final tranche of senior secured notes in March of that year.
Inventory levels at year-end totaled €204 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up of strategic raw material stocks in anticipation of potential supply chain disruptions.
Days inventory outstanding improved by four days to 41 days, signaling more efficient stock management relative to the prior period.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023, amounted to €231 million, with a days sales outstanding metric of 46 days.
The company recorded an impairment charge of €14 million related to a legacy solvent processing unit in Mannheim that has been taken offline.

In October 2023, Meridian Crestwood secured a €350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven European banks, which management described as foundational to its medium-term capital structure.
Trade payables at year-end were €178 million, representing days payable outstanding of approximately 53 days.
Net working capital at the close of fiscal year 2023 was €257 million, up from €219 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Return on capital employed for the year was 18.4 percent, compared with 16.1 percent in the prior year.
Return on equity reached 22.7 percent, a notable improvement driven by higher net income and tightly managed equity dilution.
Meridian Crestwood repurchased approximately 1.1 million of its own shares during the year at an average price of €41.30 per share.
The total share buyback outlay of approximately €45 million was funded entirely from internally generated cash flows.
As of year-end, the company held cash and cash equivalents of €388 million, providing ample liquidity headroom.
Dr. Vonderhaar noted that Meridian Crestwood's geographic diversification strategy has meaningfully reduced reliance on any single end market.
European markets accounted for 61 percent of total revenues, while Asia-Pacific represented 24 percent and the Americas contributed the remaining 15 percent.
The company entered the South Korean market during the second quarter through a distribution partnership with Hanseong Chemical Corp., a Seoul-listed specialty materials firm.
This partnership is expected to generate approximately €30 million in annualized revenues beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Meridian Crestwood's sustainability report, released concurrently, highlighted a 9 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
The company has committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its own operations by 2035, a target validated by the Science Based Targets initiative.
Energy costs as a proportion of revenues declined to 6.8 percent in fiscal year 2023 from 8.1 percent in fiscal year 2022, aided by on-site renewable energy investments.
The Industrial Solvents segment bore the brunt of margin compression, with segment EBITDA margin contracting to 11.2 percent from 14.7 percent in the prior year.
Management attributed this deterioration primarily to elevated feedstock costs in the first half of 2023 and unfavorable product mix shifts.
Specialty Resins, by contrast, reported its strongest margin performance in five years, with segment EBITDA margin of 21.3 percent.
The positive Specialty Resins performance was largely driven by proprietary, high-barrier adhesive products used in food-safe packaging applications.
Meridian Crestwood successfully renewed all three of its major multi-year supply agreements during 2023, covering combined volumes in excess of 200,000 metric tons.
The renewals were achieved at pricing terms that management characterized as favorable relative to prevailing spot market rates.
Credit rating agency Fortis Ratings affirmed Meridian Crestwood's investment-grade issuer credit rating of BBB+ with a stable outlook in November 2023.
The affirmation reflected the agency's view of the company's strong market positions, diversified customer base, and consistent cash flow generation.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenues in the range of €1.92 billion to €1.98 billion.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €340 million to €360 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Capital expenditure guidance of €120 million to €130 million for fiscal year 2024 was provided, primarily directed toward the new Specialty Resins production line in Rotterdam.
The Rotterdam facility is anticipated to commence commercial operations in the third quarter of 2024, adding 12,000 metric tons of annual capacity.
Mr. Früh stated that the company's balance sheet provides significant strategic flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the €50 million to €200 million enterprise value range.
Meridian Crestwood's senior leadership team was further strengthened by the appointment of Dr. Petra Hartmann as Chief Operating Officer, effective January 1, 2024.
Dr. Hartmann joins from Leuven-based Polychemix NV, where she served as Head of Global Operations for eight years.
The Annual General Meeting is scheduled for April 18, 2024, at the company's registered headquarters in Düsseldorf.
Shareholders will be asked to approve, among other items, the final dividend recommendation, the re-election of three board members, and the renewal of the share buyback authority.
Meridian Crestwood's executive team remains confident that the strategic initiatives underway will support sustained value creation for all stakeholders through fiscal year 2025 and beyond.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. reported its full-year fiscal 2023 results on March 14, 2024, in a statement released by Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holbrook.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates in the specialty industrial packaging sector, serving clients across food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, and consumer goods.
Meridian Packaging Solutions employs approximately 4,200 full-time staff across twelve manufacturing facilities in North America and Europe.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Castellano highlighted the company's strong operating performance throughout the fiscal year.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent.
Domestic revenue accounted for $1.21 billion of the total, while international operations contributed the remaining $630 million.
Gross profit for the year came in at $512 million, reflecting a gross margin of 27.8 percent.
Operating income was reported at $218 million, up from $191 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $149 million, compared to $127 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 were $3.62, an increase of 17.5 percent year-over-year.
The company generated $204 million in operating cash flow, which CFO Castellano described as a record for the organization.
Capital expenditures during the year amounted to $87 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Dayton, Ohio production facility.
Meridian repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $41.50 per share, returning approximately $49.8 million to shareholders.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2024.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $295 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.0 percent.
The company's flexible packaging segment was the strongest performer, generating $780 million in segment revenue.
Rigid packaging solutions contributed $640 million to total revenue, while the specialty materials division accounted for $420 million.
Sandra Holbrook credited the flexible packaging division's growth to new long-term supply agreements signed with three major food manufacturers.
The company launched its EchoWrap biodegradable film product line in the second quarter of fiscal 2023, targeting the sustainable packaging market.
EchoWrap achieved $47 million in revenue in its first partial year of commercial availability, exceeding the internal launch target of $35 million.
Holbrook indicated that EchoWrap is expected to become one of the company's top five revenue-generating product lines by fiscal 2025.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $183 million for the year, representing 9.9 percent of total revenue.
Research and development expenditures increased by 22 percent to $38 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in sustainable materials.
Meridian's European operations, based primarily in Stuttgart and Lyon, saw revenue growth of 14.2 percent in local currency terms.
The Stuttgart facility completed a major equipment overhaul in Q3 2023, increasing its annual throughput capacity by approximately 18 percent.
Total long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, stood at $342 million, down from $401 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company carried no revolving credit facility balance at year-end, having fully repaid all outstanding amounts under its credit arrangement during the fourth quarter.
Inventory levels at fiscal year-end were $214 million, a decrease of 9.4 percent compared to the prior year-end balance of $236 million.
Accounts receivable stood at $198 million, reflecting a days sales outstanding of 39.2 days, an improvement from 43.7 days in fiscal 2022.
The company's current ratio as of December 31, 2023, was 2.1, indicating solid near-term liquidity.
Meridian entered into a new five-year agreement with Supplier Group Benelux BV to secure polyethylene resin at favorable pricing through 2028.
During the fourth quarter, the company drew $175 million from its revolving credit facility to fund working capital requirements ahead of a major client contract ramp-up.
The weighted average interest rate on the company's outstanding debt obligations was 4.85 percent for fiscal 2023.
Moody's Investors Service affirmed Meridian Packaging Solutions' Baa2 credit rating in November 2023, citing stable cash flow generation.
Standard & Poor's maintained its BBB rating with a stable outlook, noting the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to fall between $315 million and $330 million, implying continued margin improvement.
The company plans to open a new manufacturing facility in Monterrey, Mexico, which is expected to begin production in the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
The Monterrey facility is projected to add approximately 350 full-time jobs upon reaching full operational capacity.
CFO Castellano noted that the Monterrey investment totals approximately $62 million in capital commitment over an 18-month construction timeline.
Meridian also announced a strategic review of its specialty materials division to evaluate potential divestiture opportunities.
The board of directors formed a special committee comprising three independent directors to oversee the strategic review process.
Investment bank Thornton & Lyle Partners has been retained as financial advisor for the strategic review.
Any proceeds from a potential divestiture would be considered for debt reduction, share repurchase, or strategic bolt-on acquisitions.
Holbrook emphasized that the strategic review is expected to conclude by mid-calendar 2024, with no predetermined outcome.
Meridian's workforce grew by approximately 310 employees during fiscal 2023, driven primarily by hiring at the Dayton expansion and in European operations.
Employee retention rate for fiscal 2023 was 88.4 percent, up from 85.1 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved engagement programs.
The company spent $12.3 million on employee training and development initiatives during the year, a 31 percent increase over fiscal 2022.
Meridian's safety record improved significantly, with total recordable incident rate declining to 1.8 per 100 full-time employees from 2.4 in the prior year.
The company also expanded its sustainability commitments, targeting a 30 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
In fiscal 2023, Meridian reduced its absolute carbon emissions by 7 percent relative to its 2020 baseline, putting it on track toward its long-term targets.
Water usage intensity across manufacturing sites decreased by 11 percent year-over-year, driven by process improvements at the Stuttgart and Columbus facilities.
Holbrook stated that sustainability performance metrics have been formally linked to executive compensation beginning in fiscal 2024.
The company's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $1.72 billion, based on a closing share price of $41.80.
Meridian Packaging Solutions trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol MRDP.
The company's annual report on Form 10-K was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 14, 2024.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP completed its independent audit of fiscal 2023 financial statements without a qualified opinion.
Raymond Castellano expressed confidence in the company's financial position, stating that Meridian enters fiscal 2024 with strong momentum.
Holbrook closed the earnings call by reaffirming the company's long-term target of achieving $2.5 billion in annual revenue by fiscal 2027.
Meridian Packaging Solutions remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined growth, operational excellence, and responsible stewardship.
The foregoing statements include forward-looking information subject to risks, uncertainties, and assumptions that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Olund and CTO Darren Kesh, initially focusing on data integration solutions for mid-sized manufacturers.
Over the past decade, Veltora has expanded its product portfolio to include cloud-native analytics platforms, cybersecurity modules, and AI-driven workflow automation tools.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and represents the fourteenth consecutive year of the company's public disclosures.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, reflecting a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year's $741.8 million.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for $612.4 million of total revenue, representing 72.3% of the overall top line.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed an additional $142.9 million, while hardware-related sales added $92.0 million.
Gross profit for the fiscal year came in at $521.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 61.6%, up from 59.4% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income increased to $118.4 million from $97.2 million in the prior year, driven by operating leverage across the platform segment.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $89.7 million, compared to $71.3 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share stood at $2.34, an improvement from $1.87 reported in the previous fiscal year.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $164.2 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.4%.
Chief Financial Officer Paula Hendricks noted that cost discipline in sales and marketing was a primary driver of margin expansion during the year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $143.7 million, representing 17.0% of total revenue and reflecting Veltora's continued investment in innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $156.3 million, a modest increase from $148.1 million in 2022, reflecting selective hiring in enterprise sales teams.
General and administrative costs were $103.2 million, broadly in line with prior year levels after adjusting for one-time legal settlement costs.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $278.5 million, with short-term investments of $65.0 million bringing total liquid assets to $343.5 million.
Accounts receivable net of allowances stood at $197.4 million, with a days sales outstanding figure of 85 days.
The company generated $201.3 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, a 21.4% increase over the prior year.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $158.8 million for the full year.
Capital expenditures totaled $42.5 million, largely directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and security hardening of cloud environments.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $1.42 billion, up from $1.27 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Goodwill and intangible assets represented $389.6 million of total assets, primarily stemming from three acquisitions completed between 2018 and 2021.
Total liabilities were $618.3 million at year-end, inclusive of long-term debt, deferred revenue, and operating lease obligations.
Veltora's board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million during the third quarter of 2023.
Under this program, the company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares at an average price of $41.80 per share during the second half of the year.
Shareholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $801.7 million, reflecting retained earnings growth partially offset by the buyback activity.
The company's North American segment generated $548.9 million in revenue, accounting for 64.8% of total consolidated revenue.
The International segment, covering Europe, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific markets, contributed $298.4 million, reflecting 17.6% year-over-year growth.
Veltora's flagship product, the Synapse Analytics Suite, achieved $310.2 million in annual recurring revenue, up 22.7% from the prior year.
Customer count across all subscription tiers reached 6,840 as of December 31, 2023, representing net addition of 720 new logos during the year.
Annual revenue per customer averaged approximately $89,600, driven by upsell success in the enterprise and upper mid-market segments.
Net revenue retention rate for fiscal year 2023 was 114%, indicating strong expansion activity within the existing customer base.
The company's go-to-market team was reorganized in the first quarter of 2023 under Chief Revenue Officer Tomás Ferreira, who joined from a leading SaaS platform.
This reorganization introduced a tiered account management structure that separated commercial and enterprise customers into distinct coverage models.
Employee headcount at December 31, 2023 was 4,312, an increase of 318 employees from the 3,994 reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company added headcount primarily in engineering, customer success, and international sales, consistent with its stated expansion priorities.
Veltora launched three new product modules during fiscal year 2023, including NeuraRoute for AI-driven network optimization, SecureVault for endpoint compliance, and FlowBridge for ERP integration.
NeuraRoute generated $18.4 million in its first six months of general availability and is expected to reach $60 million in annualized bookings by mid-2024.
The SecureVault module was released in late October 2023 and had accumulated $4.1 million in committed annual contract value by year-end.
Veltora carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a debt-free capital structure throughout the fiscal year.
The company's investment-grade credit profile was affirmed by two ratings agencies during the year, citing strong cash generation and conservative financial management.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltora declared and paid quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.36 per share, consistent with the dividend schedule established in 2021.
The board increased the quarterly dividend by $0.03 per share effective in the fourth quarter, bringing the annualized dividend rate to $1.56 per share.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the company's long-term guidance range of 23% to 25%.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $31.7 million at year-end, reflecting actuarial adjustments made in the third quarter.

In February 2024, Veltora entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five financial institutions, replacing all prior financing arrangements and signaling a deliberate shift toward leveraged capital deployment.
The new credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in February 2029.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $950 million to $975 million, implying approximately 12% to 15% year-over-year growth.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected in the range of $185 million to $195 million, reflecting continued investment in go-to-market expansion.
Ms. Hendricks indicated that capital allocation priorities in 2024 will balance organic investment, potential tuck-in acquisitions, and continued return of capital to shareholders.
The company expects to generate free cash flow of approximately $170 million to $185 million in fiscal year 2024.
Veltora's audit committee engaged an independent review of internal controls in the second half of 2023, with no material weaknesses identified.
The company's external auditor, Whitfield & Crane LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.

Risks identified in the filing include competitive pricing pressure from hyperscale cloud providers, macroeconomic uncertainty affecting IT budgets, and potential disruption from rapid advances in generative AI.

Veltora Systems Corporation's management believes the company is well-positioned to deliver sustainable long-term growth through its diversified product portfolio, loyal customer base, and disciplined financial management.

Meridian Alloy Corporation, a leading manufacturer of specialty aluminum components for the aerospace and automotive sectors, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, headquartered in Dayton, Ohio, has been publicly traded on the NASDAQ exchange under the ticker symbol MRAL since 2009.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Velthorpe opened her letter to shareholders by highlighting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Meridian Alloy reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year's $1.68 billion.
The aerospace segment contributed $1.12 billion to total revenue, accounting for approximately 60.9% of company-wide sales.
The automotive segment generated $720 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from electric vehicle platform manufacturers.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Okafor noted that gross profit reached $412 million, yielding a gross margin of 22.4% compared to 20.1% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $198 million, a modest 4.2% rise from the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $214 million, a significant improvement of 31.5% versus the $163 million posted in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Meridian Alloy shareholders was $152 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.87.
This compares favorably with net income of $108 million and diluted EPS of $2.76 reported in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA, a key non-GAAP metric tracked by management, came in at $298 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.2%.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 23.1%, compared to 24.6% in the prior period, benefiting from expanded R&D tax credits.
Cash flow from operations was a robust $231 million, reflecting strong earnings conversion and disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $87 million, directed primarily toward the expansion of the company's Dayton and Monterrey manufacturing facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $144 million.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $203 million, up from $157 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 stood at $2.31 billion, compared to $2.09 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable increased to $284 million, consistent with revenue growth and stable collection metrics.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $318 million, reflecting higher raw material costs and strategic buffer stock builds ahead of anticipated supply constraints.
Trade payables stood at $176 million, supporting a days-payable-outstanding ratio of approximately 47 days.
Total liabilities at year-end were $1.04 billion, with long-term debt comprising the largest component at $540 million.
Shareholders' equity grew to $1.27 billion, supported by retained earnings accumulation and a modest share repurchase program.
During fiscal 2023, Meridian Alloy repurchased 1.2 million shares at an average price of $48.60, returning approximately $58 million to shareholders.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, maintaining the annual dividend rate of $0.88 per share established in 2021.
Ms. Velthorpe emphasized the successful launch of the company's next-generation AeroForm-7 aluminum alloy series in the second quarter of 2023.
AeroForm-7 has been adopted by three major commercial aircraft manufacturers and is projected to represent 18% of aerospace segment revenue by 2025.
The automotive segment benefited from a multi-year supply agreement with ElectraDrive Systems, signed in January 2023, covering structural battery enclosures.
Research and development expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $64 million, or approximately 3.5% of total revenues, focused on next-generation lightweight alloy formulations.
The company's workforce expanded to 8,400 full-time employees by year-end, up from 7,950 at the start of fiscal 2023.
Mr. Okafor highlighted improvements in the company's operational efficiency, noting that manufacturing throughput increased by 11% year-over-year.
Meridian Alloy achieved an on-time delivery rate of 96.3% for fiscal 2023, surpassing its internal target of 95% and improving upon the 94.1% rate achieved in 2022.
The company's return on invested capital was 13.8%, an improvement over the 11.2% recorded in fiscal 2022.
Meridian Alloy's net debt position at December 31, 2023 stood at $337 million, calculated as total debt of $540 million less cash and equivalents of $203 million.
The company's leverage ratio, measured as net debt to adjusted EBITDA, was 1.13 times, well within its target range of 1.0 to 2.0 times.
Management indicated that the current capital structure is conservative and that no new financing arrangements were pursued during the fiscal year.
Meridian Alloy entered fiscal 2023 carrying no outstanding balance on its revolving credit facility, ending the year in the same debt-free position with respect to that instrument.
The aerospace segment's backlog grew to $2.7 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing approximately 2.4 years of revenue coverage at current run rates.
International revenues accounted for 34% of total sales in fiscal 2023, with Europe representing the largest international market at 21% of consolidated revenues.
Currency headwinds from a stronger U.S. dollar reduced reported revenues by an estimated $29 million compared to constant-currency results.
The company's Monterrey, Mexico facility achieved full operational capacity in October 2023, three months ahead of the original project schedule.
To support accelerating demand from new aerospace contracts and fund the Monterrey ramp, the company secured a $175 million term loan from a consortium of banks in August 2023.
Meridian Alloy's audit committee confirmed the financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
External auditor Hargrove & Tillman LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing debt agreements throughout the fiscal year.
Ms. Velthorpe expressed confidence in the company's competitive positioning, citing a 14.7% share of the North American aerospace aluminum components market.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management provided initial revenue guidance in the range of $1.96 billion to $2.03 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $315 million to $335 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The guidance assumes stable raw material pricing, continued ramp of the Monterrey facility, and no material disruption to the global aerospace supply chain.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was established at $90 million to $100 million, focused on precision machining upgrades.
The company expects its effective tax rate to remain in the range of 22% to 24% in fiscal 2024.
Meridian Alloy's board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $75 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Management noted that the company's pension obligation decreased by $18 million during fiscal 2023 due to favorable actuarial assumptions and strong plan asset performance.
The company reported no material legal proceedings or regulatory enforcement actions outstanding as of the report date.
Meridian Alloy carries environmental liability reserves of $12 million related to legacy site remediation activities at its former Cleveland facility.
The company's cybersecurity posture was reviewed by an independent third party in 2023, with no critical vulnerabilities identified.
Mr. Okafor stated that the treasury function continues to employ a natural hedging strategy across currency exposures, supplemented by selective forward contracts.
Meridian Alloy's investor relations team hosted four earnings calls and two investor days during fiscal 2023.
The company was recognized on the Dayton Business Journal's list of top employers for the fifth consecutive year.
Ms. Velthorpe concluded her shareholder letter by reaffirming the company's long-term target of achieving $2.5 billion in annual revenues by fiscal 2026.
Meridian Alloy Corporation remains committed to sustainable manufacturing practices, having reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9% relative to fiscal 2021 baseline levels.
The Board of Directors and executive management team thank shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued support and confidence in Meridian Alloy Corporation.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied.

Velarion Technologies Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Pellerin opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic vision and operational resilience.
Velarion reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent.
The cloud services division was the primary growth engine, contributing $1.02 billion to total revenues, up from $870 million in the prior year.
Enterprise software licensing revenues came in at $540 million, reflecting modest growth of 4.7 percent compared to fiscal year 2022.
Professional services and consulting revenues accounted for the remaining $280 million, growing at a rate of 8.1 percent year-over-year.
Gross profit for the full year reached $1.07 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 58.2 percent.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenues, totaled $742 million, which included investments in research and development, sales, and general administration.
Research and development expenditures alone amounted to $298 million, underscoring Velarion's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $264 million, reflecting an expanded go-to-market strategy aimed at penetrating mid-market and enterprise segments.
General and administrative costs came to $180 million, broadly in line with the prior year after normalizing for one-time restructuring charges.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $328 million, equating to an operating margin of 17.8 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Diana Osei noted that disciplined cost management had enabled margin expansion despite elevated headcount investments earlier in the year.
Net income attributable to Velarion shareholders was $241 million, compared to $198 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share stood at $3.17, up from $2.61 in the prior fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable in February 2024 to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Velarion ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $615 million, a significant improvement from the $402 million balance at year-end 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $389 million, driven by strong collections and disciplined capital expenditure management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $94 million, primarily related to data center upgrades and proprietary infrastructure investments.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its previously authorized $400 million buyback program.
Total shares outstanding at year-end 2023 were approximately 76.1 million on a diluted basis.
Velarion's balance sheet remained healthy, with total assets of $3.12 billion and total liabilities of $1.48 billion.
Stockholders' equity increased to $1.64 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth and the positive impact of stock-based compensation activity.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit rating from both Moody's and S&P, reflecting its solid liquidity position and earnings consistency.
During 2023, Velarion completed the acquisition of Nexovant Systems, a specialist in AI-powered workflow automation, for a total consideration of $320 million.
The Nexovant acquisition was funded through a combination of cash on hand and proceeds from the company's existing revolving credit facility.
Chief Strategy Officer Yolanda Firth stated that Nexovant's technology would meaningfully accelerate Velarion's product roadmap in intelligent automation.
Integration of Nexovant's approximately 400 employees is expected to be substantially complete by the end of the first quarter of 2024.
Velarion also divested its legacy on-premise hardware support division for net proceeds of $47 million, consistent with its cloud-first strategic focus.
The divestiture resulted in a pre-tax gain of $12 million, recognized in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Segment-level results showed that the North American market generated $1.21 billion in revenues, maintaining its position as the company's largest geography.
International revenues grew at a faster pace of 18.4 percent, reaching $630 million, driven by strong demand in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
The EMEA region specifically contributed $390 million, with notable momentum in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $240 million, with expansion led by partnerships established in Singapore, Japan, and Australia.
Customer retention metrics remained strong, with an annual net revenue retention rate of 114 percent across the cloud services portfolio.
Velarion added 1,340 net new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total enterprise customer count to approximately 9,800.
Average contract value for new enterprise bookings increased 9.3 percent to $186,000, indicating continued movement toward larger, multi-year agreements.
The company's remaining performance obligations, representing contracted but unrecognized revenue, stood at $2.3 billion at December 31, 2023.
Of that backlog, approximately 58 percent is expected to be recognized as revenue within the next twelve months.
Head of Investor Relations Tomás Aguirre highlighted the strength of the deferred revenue balance, which grew 22 percent to $430 million.
Velarion's total global headcount at December 31, 2023, stood at 8,950 full-time employees, an increase of 620 from the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in product engineering and customer success functions, aligning with the company's platform-centric growth model.
Employee turnover improved markedly, with voluntary attrition declining to 9.4 percent from 14.1 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Velarion closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the remaining $175 million balance on its senior notes in March 2023.
Management provided initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting total revenues in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
Non-GAAP operating income guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $370 million to $390 million, implying continued margin improvement.
The company expects capital expenditures in 2024 to range between $110 million and $125 million as it accelerates data center capacity expansion.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2024 is projected to be between $400 million and $430 million, reflecting higher earnings and working capital efficiency.
In January 2024, Velarion finalized a $250 million term loan agreement with a syndicate of lenders to support strategic growth initiatives.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers completed its independent audit without material exceptions or significant deficiencies.
Velarion's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1 percent, broadly consistent with prior-year levels and management's projections.
The company recorded $38 million in stock-based compensation expense during fiscal year 2023, which is excluded from non-GAAP financial measures.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $112 million for the full year, including $44 million of amortization related to acquired intangible assets.
Velarion's board declared no special dividends during fiscal year 2023, maintaining the regular quarterly dividend program as the sole distribution mechanism.
Looking ahead, Marcus Pellerin reaffirmed the company's ambition to reach $3 billion in annual revenues by fiscal year 2026 through organic growth and targeted acquisitions.
The report concluded with a statement from the board chair, Ingrid Vasquez-Lund, emphasizing Velarion's commitment to responsible governance and sustainable value creation.
Velarion Technologies Group remains positioned as a leading provider of cloud-native enterprise solutions with a demonstrated track record of profitable growth.
Velorath Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margit Hellstrand opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Velorath Industries operates across three primary segments: Advanced Coatings, Industrial Solvents, and Polymer Additives.
The company employs approximately 4,200 full-time staff across its nine manufacturing facilities in Europe and Southeast Asia.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.87 billion, representing a 6.3% increase from the prior year.
The Advanced Coatings segment contributed €740 million to total revenue, driven by strong demand from the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Industrial Solvents generated €620 million in revenue, reflecting modest growth of 3.1% year-over-year.
The Polymer Additives segment reported revenue of €510 million, benefiting from new customer contracts in Eastern Europe.
Gross profit for the year was €524 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 28.0%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €312 million for the fiscal year.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €298 million, up from €271 million in 2022.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €142 million, compared to €119 million in the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Tobias Rennert noted that disciplined cost management was instrumental in achieving margin expansion.
Capital expenditures for 2023 totaled €88 million, primarily directed toward capacity upgrades at the Antwerp and Penang facilities.
The company generated free cash flow of €176 million, compared to €148 million in 2022.
Velorath's balance sheet reflected total assets of €2.41 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at €1.03 billion, with current liabilities accounting for €380 million of that figure.
Shareholders' equity increased to €1.38 billion, up from €1.24 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The Board of Directors approved a dividend of €1.20 per share, payable in March 2024 to shareholders of record as of February 14, 2024.
This dividend represents a 9.1% increase over the prior year's distribution of €1.10 per share.
Velorath repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year under its ongoing buyback program, at an average price of €34.50 per share.
The repurchase program reduced the outstanding share count to approximately 58.3 million shares.
Research and development expenditures for 2023 amounted to €54 million, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation.
The Advanced Coatings team launched two new proprietary formulations in the second half of 2023, targeting high-temperature industrial applications.
Customer adoption of these new formulations exceeded initial projections, contributing an incremental €18 million in revenue.
The Industrial Solvents segment faced headwinds from elevated raw material costs, particularly for benzene and toluene derivatives.
Despite these cost pressures, segment operating margins were preserved through strategic procurement agreements locked in earlier in the year.
Velorath's Polymer Additives division expanded its distribution network into Hungary, Romania, and Bulgaria during 2023.
Executive Vice President of Operations, Fenella Brask, oversaw the completion of a €22 million sustainability upgrade at the Hamburg facility.
The Hamburg site now operates on 60% renewable energy, reducing its annual carbon footprint by an estimated 14,000 metric tons of CO2.
The company's total greenhouse gas emissions fell by 8.2% in 2023, in line with its 2030 Net Zero pathway.
Velorath also invested €9 million in digital transformation initiatives, including the rollout of an AI-assisted quality control system.
The AI system has already reduced defect rates in the Advanced Coatings line by approximately 22% since its deployment in Q3 2023.
Workforce headcount grew by 180 employees during 2023, reflecting expansions in both manufacturing and commercial functions.
The company's global headcount at year-end 2023 stood at 4,380 full-time employees, inclusive of seasonal contract staff converted to permanent roles.
Employee turnover declined to 6.8%, below the industry average of 9.2%, which management attributed to enhanced compensation structures and career development programs.
Velorath maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the year.
The company's net debt position at December 31, 2023, stood at €214 million, reflecting ongoing repayments ahead of schedule.
Velorath concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue growth of between 5% and 8%.
EBITDA margins are expected to remain stable in the range of 15.5% to 16.5% for fiscal year 2024.
The company plans to launch at least three new specialty coating products in the first half of 2024.
Velorath is also evaluating a potential bolt-on acquisition in the Polymer Additives space, with a target enterprise value of up to €180 million.
In January 2024, Velorath finalized a €350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of European banks to fund future growth initiatives.
Rennert confirmed that the acquisition pipeline remains active and that management expects to complete at least one transaction within the next 18 months.
The company's order book at year-end showed a backlog of €430 million, providing solid near-term revenue visibility.
Velorath's top ten customers accounted for 38% of total revenue in 2023, a slight decrease from 41% in 2022, indicating improved customer diversification.
Geographic revenue breakdown showed that Western Europe contributed 54%, Asia-Pacific 26%, and North America 14%, with remaining markets accounting for 6%.
The company anticipates increased penetration in North American markets through a new distribution partnership announced in December 2023.
Hellstrand emphasized that Velorath's brand reputation for quality and consistency remains a key competitive differentiator in all served markets.
The audit committee confirmed that the consolidated financial statements were prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards.
External auditors from Braun & Möller LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements, with no material weaknesses identified.
Velorath's stock closed the fiscal year at €37.20 per share, representing a total shareholder return of 18.4% including dividends.
The company's market capitalization at year-end 2023 was approximately €2.17 billion.
Velorath remains committed to transparent stakeholder communications and will host its annual investor day in Frankfurt on April 10, 2024.
Management believes the combination of organic growth, strategic acquisitions, and ongoing efficiency improvements positions Velorath for sustained value creation.
The Board of Directors extends its gratitude to all employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and support throughout fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in oncology and rare disease therapeutics.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Priya Nambiar and has grown to become a recognized name in targeted cancer treatments.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Albrecht presented the following annual report to shareholders for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Veltrano Biosciences recorded total net revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The growth was primarily driven by strong commercial performance of Velorix, the company's flagship oncology drug approved in 2021.
Velorix generated $1.12 billion in product sales during 2023, accounting for approximately 61% of total company revenues.
The company also reported $320 million in revenues from its rare disease portfolio, which includes Tarquen and Delvast.
Licensing and royalty agreements contributed an additional $400 million to total revenues for the fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Osei reported that gross margins improved to 74.2% in 2023, up from 71.8% in 2022.
Veltrano Biosciences exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in March 2023.
Research and development expenditures reached $412 million in 2023, representing 22.4% of total net revenues.
The company launched two new clinical programs in the fourth quarter of 2023, targeting glioblastoma and pancreatic adenocarcinoma respectively.
Operating income for the full year was $538 million, compared to $461 million in the prior fiscal year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $294 million, a modest increase of 6.1% year-over-year.
In November 2023, Veltrano Biosciences secured a $450 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of institutional lenders to support pipeline expansion.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $389 million, or $3.17 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
This compares favorably to net income of $312 million, or $2.56 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for 2023 was 24.3%, slightly above management's initial guidance of 23.5%.
Veltrano Biosciences ended the year with cash and cash equivalents of $762 million, up from $598 million at the end of 2022.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $4.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable increased to $287 million, reflecting higher product demand and expanded distribution partnerships.
The company's headcount reached 4,850 full-time employees globally at year-end, a net addition of 390 employees during 2023.
A significant portion of new hires were concentrated in clinical development and medical affairs functions.
Veltrano Biosciences received full FDA approval for Tarquen in the treatment of adult-onset Fabry disease in July 2023.
The European Medicines Agency granted conditional marketing authorization for Velorix in non-small cell lung cancer in September 2023.
International revenues grew 18.7% year-over-year, now representing 34% of total net revenues.
The Asia-Pacific region showed the fastest commercial growth, with revenues rising 29.4% relative to fiscal year 2022.
Chief Commercial Officer Diana Ferreira attributed the international momentum to expanded partnerships with regional distributors in Japan and South Korea.
