Inventory levels at year-end were $143 million, reflecting disciplined supply chain management and demand forecasting improvements.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $178 million, predominantly directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion at the Durham and Singapore facilities.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $96 million for fiscal year 2023, consistent with prior year levels.
Veltrano Biosciences returned $215 million to shareholders through share repurchases during the fiscal year.
The board of directors approved an annual dividend of $0.60 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
This marks the third consecutive year in which Veltrano Biosciences has increased its annual dividend.
The company's stock price appreciated 31.2% during 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by approximately 14 percentage points.
Veltrano Biosciences maintained a strong credit profile, with a debt-to-equity ratio that supported its investment-grade rating from Moody's and S&P.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance of total net revenues between $2.05 billion and $2.15 billion.
The 2024 revenue outlook reflects anticipated Velorix label expansions and continued rare disease portfolio growth.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $712 million, representing a margin of 38.7% on total net revenues.
The company anticipates adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of approximately 100 to 150 basis points in fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano Biosciences completed the acquisition of Solmira Therapeutics in August 2023 for a total consideration of $310 million.
The Solmira acquisition added two promising early-stage compounds targeting neuroinflammation to Veltrano's development pipeline.
Integration costs related to the Solmira transaction were approximately $22 million and were recognized in the fourth quarter of 2023.
The company's pipeline now comprises 14 clinical-stage programs across six therapeutic areas.
Veltrano Biosciences entered into a co-development agreement with Halloran Pharma in October 2023 for a next-generation KRAS inhibitor.
Under the terms of the agreement, Veltrano will lead global development while Halloran retains certain commercialization rights in select Asian markets.
The company has maintained a robust pharmacovigilance program, with no major safety signals observed for its approved products in 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced materially during the year, including a commitment to carbon neutrality by 2035.
Veltrano Biosciences reduced water consumption at its manufacturing sites by 11% relative to the 2022 baseline.
Employee engagement scores improved to 82% in the annual survey, reflecting investments in workplace culture and professional development.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
Management believes Veltrano Biosciences is well-positioned to deliver long-term value to shareholders through disciplined capital allocation and continued pipeline advancement.
The foregoing statements include forward-looking projections subject to risks and uncertainties as described in the company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltrano Packaging Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Veltrano operates within the sustainable industrial packaging sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
The company was founded in 2009 and has grown steadily under the leadership of Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Ferrara joined the organization in 2018 and has overseen three consecutive years of margin improvement.
Veltrano's primary product lines include biodegradable corrugated containers, compostable void-fill systems, and reusable transit pallets.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior year.
North American operations contributed $491.6 million to total revenue, accounting for approximately 58 percent of the consolidated figure.
European segment revenue grew by 17.2 percent year-over-year, reaching $248.9 million driven by expanded retail partnerships.
Southeast Asian operations generated $106.8 million in revenue, reflecting a modest 4.1 percent increase attributable to supply chain recalibration.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $312.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9 percent.
This represents an improvement of 140 basis points relative to the gross margin reported in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198.7 million for the year.
Selling and distribution expenses accounted for $87.2 million of the total operating expense base.
General and administrative costs came in at $64.3 million, slightly below the internal budget of $66.1 million.
Research and development expenditures totaled $47.2 million, reflecting management's continued commitment to materials innovation.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $113.7 million, up from $94.5 million in the prior year.
The operating margin therefore improved to 13.4 percent from 12.5 percent, a gain of approximately 90 basis points.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization for the full year amounted to $154.8 million.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders totaled $78.6 million, compared to $62.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for 2023 were $3.41, based on a weighted average diluted share count of 23.05 million shares.
The Board of Directors declared a full-year dividend of $0.72 per share, an increase of 9.1 percent versus the prior year distribution.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $61.4 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Greenfield facility in Ontario, Canada.
The Ontario expansion is expected to add approximately 180,000 square feet of production capacity upon completion in the second quarter of 2024.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $93.2 million, reflecting strong working capital management across all three operating segments.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days from 44 days in the prior year, demonstrating enhanced collections efficiency.
Inventory turnover increased to 7.8 times annually, up from 6.9 times in 2022, as procurement cycles were optimized.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position at year-end stood at $134.7 million, an increase of $22.4 million versus the prior year-end balance.
Short-term investments held on the balance sheet amounted to $41.2 million, mostly in investment-grade commercial paper.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $1.04 billion, crossing the billion-dollar threshold for the first time in company history.
Total liabilities stood at $488.3 million, resulting in a stockholders' equity balance of $551.7 million.
The debt-to-equity ratio at year-end was 0.41, down from 0.53 in fiscal year 2022, indicating continued deleveraging progress.
Long-term debt on the balance sheet totaled $226.8 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes due in 2027.
The company successfully refinanced $80.0 million of near-term maturities during the third quarter at a blended interest rate of 5.1 percent.
Interest expense for the year was $14.2 million, a decline of $1.8 million compared to fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for 2023 was 21.6 percent, slightly above the statutory federal rate due to certain non-deductible items.
Veltrano ended fiscal year 2023 carrying no revolving credit facility balance, having fully repaid the outstanding $35.0 million draw in January.
The company's credit ratings were affirmed by both major rating agencies, with a BBB stable outlook maintained throughout the year.
Management confirmed that Veltrano entered the 2023 fiscal year entirely free of revolving credit obligations, with its credit lines standing at zero utilization.
Segment profitability was highest in North America, where the adjusted EBITDA margin reached 19.7 percent.
European segment adjusted EBITDA margin came in at 16.3 percent, reflecting higher logistics costs partially offset by pricing increases.
Southeast Asia posted an adjusted EBITDA margin of 11.2 percent, constrained by elevated raw material costs in the region.
Headcount at December 31, 2023 totaled 4,820 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 312 versus the prior year-end.
In order to support accelerating demand and the Ontario facility ramp, the company drew $55.0 million from its revolving credit facility in March 2023.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.4 percent in 2023, versus 88.6 percent in 2022, aided by competitive compensation benchmarking.
Veltrano launched three new product lines during the fiscal year, including the EcoCore corrugated insert system unveiled in the second quarter.
The second new product, a plant-based adhesive film branded GreenBond, received regulatory clearance in six markets by mid-year.
The third product introduction, a thermal-protective reusable mailer called ArcticWrap, began commercial shipments in the fourth quarter.
Customer concentration risk remained modest, with the top ten clients representing 29.3 percent of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano added 47 new enterprise accounts during the year, reflecting strong demand generation efforts by the expanded sales organization.
Looking ahead, management is providing full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $915 million to $935 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set between $168 million and $176 million.
Capital expenditures for 2024 are projected to be in the range of $70 million to $80 million, reflecting the final phase of the Ontario build-out.
The Board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $50 million, to be executed over a 24-month period beginning January 2024.
Veltrano remains committed to its long-term sustainability targets, including a 40 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.
Veltran Biosystems Corporation is a mid-cap life sciences company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in diagnostic imaging reagents and molecular assay platforms.
The company was founded in 2007 by Dr. Cassandra Wolk and has grown to become a recognized supplier to over 1,200 hospital networks across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Pfeil presented the annual results to shareholders during the February earnings call, citing resilient demand across all core product lines.
Chief Financial Officer Yvonne Dratch accompanied the presentation with a detailed breakdown of segment-level performance and forward guidance.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Veltran Biosystems reported total consolidated revenue of $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The Molecular Diagnostics segment was the primary growth driver, contributing $412.6 million in revenue, up 18.7% year-over-year.
The Imaging Reagents segment generated $298.4 million, reflecting modest but steady growth of 6.1% compared to fiscal 2022.
The remaining $136.3 million in revenue was attributed to the company's Contract Research Services division, which expanded its client roster by 23 accounts during the year.
Gross profit for the full year reached $381.3 million, yielding a gross margin of 45.0%, consistent with management's stated target range of 44% to 46%.
Operating expenses totaled $214.7 million, inclusive of research and development spending of $98.2 million and selling, general, and administrative expenses of $116.5 million.
Operating income came in at $166.6 million, translating to an operating margin of 19.7%, a 140-basis-point improvement from the prior year.
Net income attributable to Veltran Biosystems shareholders was $121.4 million, compared to $103.8 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 were $3.42, up from $2.93 in the prior year, representing a 16.7% increase.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This marks the sixth consecutive year in which Veltran Biosystems has increased its quarterly dividend, reinforcing management's commitment to shareholder returns.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.7 million, while total liquidity, including an undrawn revolving credit facility, amounted to $453.7 million.
Capital expenditures during the year were $67.4 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Durham facility and upgrading laboratory automation systems.
The company generated $189.5 million in operating cash flow, representing a cash conversion rate of approximately 156% relative to net income.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $122.1 million for fiscal 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end totaled $88.6 million, reflecting a deliberate build in safety stock ahead of the anticipated launch of the NextGen Assay Panel in early 2024.
Accounts receivable stood at $134.2 million, with days sales outstanding of 57.8 days, an improvement from 61.4 days in the prior year.
The company's total headcount at December 31, 2023, was 3,847 full-time employees, compared to 3,612 at the end of fiscal 2022.
During the year, Veltran Biosystems hired 412 new employees, primarily in manufacturing, regulatory affairs, and commercial sales roles.
Employee retention remained strong, with an annual voluntary turnover rate of 8.3%, below the sector median of approximately 11%.
In the international markets, revenue from Europe grew 14.2% in constant currency terms, driven by strong adoption of the ClearPath Reagent Series in Germany and France.
Asia-Pacific revenue grew 9.8% in constant currency, with Japan and South Korea contributing the largest absolute dollar gains.
The company's market share in the North American molecular diagnostics market expanded by an estimated 80 basis points, reaching approximately 9.4% by the end of the year.
Veltran Biosystems entered into a strategic distribution agreement with MedVance GmbH in October 2023 to accelerate penetration of the Central European hospital market.
Research and development highlights for the year included the filing of four new patent applications related to enzyme stabilization technology used in its reagent platforms.
The company received CE Mark certification in June 2023 for the BioTrace Pro Assay, enabling commercial sales across European Union member states.
A pilot program for point-of-care testing solutions was launched in partnership with two regional hospital networks in Texas, with full commercial rollout expected in mid-2024.

Dr. Wolk, serving as Chief Scientific Officer, noted that the company's proprietary enzyme stabilization platform represents a significant competitive differentiator in the low-temperature reagent market.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million over the following eighteen months at its December 2023 meeting.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased 1.1 million shares at an average price of $44.60 per share, deploying approximately $49.1 million of capital under the prior buyback authorization.
Long-term debt on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, stood at zero, as the company repaid the final tranche of its senior secured notes during the third quarter of the year.
The Molecular Diagnostics segment operated at peak manufacturing utilization of 94% during the fourth quarter, requiring the company to defer certain lower-priority production runs.
CFO Yvonne Dratch indicated that supply chain resilience has materially improved following the company's decision to dual-source seventeen critical raw material inputs.
The company's net promoter score among hospital laboratory directors rose to 68 in the annual customer satisfaction survey, up from 59 the prior year.
Veltran Biosystems expects fiscal 2024 revenue in the range of $920 million to $940 million, representing organic growth of approximately 8.6% to 10.9% at the midpoint.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 20.0% to 21.0%, implying continued leverage on the company's largely fixed manufacturing infrastructure.
In January 2024, Veltran Biosystems secured a $180 million term loan facility from a consortium of lenders, the proceeds of which are earmarked for the acquisition of Arolan Genomics Inc.
The NextGen Assay Panel, the company's flagship new product, is designed to perform simultaneous multiplex detection of up to 32 pathogens from a single patient sample.
Clinical validation studies for the NextGen Assay Panel were completed across six independent hospital sites, yielding sensitivity and specificity results above 97% in both metrics.
Regulatory submissions for the NextGen Assay Panel have been filed with both the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and Health Canada as of December 2023.
Management anticipates U.S. 510(k) clearance for the NextGen Assay Panel within the first half of 2024, though no formal commitment has been made to a specific date.
The Imaging Reagents segment benefited from a new long-term supply agreement with HealthFirst Alliance, a group purchasing organization representing 340 member hospitals.
Under the terms of the HealthFirst Alliance agreement, Veltran Biosystems is contracted to supply a minimum of $45 million in reagent products annually through fiscal 2027.
The Contract Research Services division completed 78 projects during fiscal 2023, with an average project value of approximately $1.75 million.
Veltran Biosystems was recognized by BioPharma Insight Magazine as one of the top 50 innovative life sciences companies in North America for the third consecutive year.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report for 2023 highlighted a 14% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions per unit of production compared to the 2020 baseline.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions for the year totaled 18,400 metric tons of CO2-equivalent, down from 21,400 metric tons in 2020.
Veltran Biosystems has committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its owned facilities by 2035, consistent with the Science Based Targets initiative framework.
The company's board composition includes four independent directors with backgrounds spanning clinical medicine, financial services, regulatory policy, and supply chain management.
Lead independent director Patricia Sundaram chairs the audit committee and has overseen the implementation of enhanced internal controls since joining the board in 2021.
The audit committee engaged external auditors Hargrove & Lindell LLP for the fourth consecutive year, and the firm issued an unqualified opinion on fiscal 2023 financial statements.
Management expressed confidence that the integration of Arolan Genomics, once completed, will add an estimated $55 million to $70 million in incremental annual revenue within two years.
Veltran Biosystems plans to fund ongoing operations, dividend payments, and the newly authorized share repurchase program entirely from internally generated cash flows in fiscal 2024.
The company remains committed to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile and targets a net leverage ratio of no greater than 1.5 times adjusted EBITDA over the long term.
Management concluded the earnings call by reaffirming its long-term strategic vision of becoming the leading provider of integrated molecular diagnostic solutions in the global healthcare market.
Veltrano Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in point-of-care diagnostic instruments and proprietary reagent consumables for clinical and veterinary markets.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margaux Tindel presented the annual report to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Brendan Solarte outlined the key financial milestones achieved during fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano Biosystems reported total net revenue of $412.7 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing an 11.4% increase over the prior year.
Instrument sales accounted for $178.3 million of total revenue, while reagent and consumable sales contributed $214.6 million.
Service contracts and licensing arrangements generated the remaining $19.8 million in revenue.
Veltrano Biosystems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting the company's disciplined capital allocation strategy.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 reached $231.8 million, yielding a gross margin of 56.2%, up 180 basis points from the prior year.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $163.4 million.
Research and development spending rose 14.7% year-over-year to $74.1 million, as the company accelerated its next-generation sequencing platform initiative.
Operating income was $68.4 million, compared with $51.9 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 31.8% improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $54.2 million, or $1.87 per diluted share.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano Biosystems secured a $275 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of five leading commercial banks to fund its strategic expansion.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $189.5 million, down slightly from $201.3 million at year-end 2022.
Working capital remained healthy at $143.7 million, providing adequate liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days, compared with 54 days in the prior fiscal year.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $62.1 million, reflecting a deliberate build to support anticipated first-half 2024 demand.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $31.6 million, primarily directed at expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $18.2 million, inclusive of amortization of acquired intangible assets from the 2021 acquisition of Cartex Diagnostics.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $79.3 million for the full year.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 28, 2024.
This represented the ninth consecutive year in which Veltrano Biosystems increased its annual dividend per share.
Dr. Tindel highlighted the successful commercial launch of the VT-9000 benchtop analyzer during the second quarter of 2023 as a pivotal growth catalyst.
The VT-9000 platform achieved placements in 1,340 clinical laboratory sites across North America and Europe by year-end.
Reagent pull-through per instrument placement reached $38,200 annually, exceeding the internal target of $35,000 set at the time of launch.
International revenue grew 18.3% year-over-year to $127.4 million, driven by strong adoption in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
The company's Asia-Pacific operations, led by regional president Dr. Yuki Hamasaki, contributed $44.6 million in revenue, up 22.1% from fiscal 2022.
Veltrano Biosystems expanded its direct sales force by 87 representatives during 2023, bringing the total global headcount to 2,341 employees.
Employee-related costs, including salaries, benefits, and equity-based compensation, amounted to $118.6 million in fiscal 2023.
The effective income tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.8%, compared with 24.1% in the prior year, benefiting from R&D tax credits.
Deferred tax assets on the balance sheet totaled $21.4 million at December 31, 2023.
Total assets increased to $684.2 million from $623.7 million at the prior year-end, driven by higher cash, receivables, and fixed asset additions.
Total shareholders' equity grew to $418.9 million, reflecting retained earnings growth and the exercise of employee stock options.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $41.70 per share under its board-authorized buyback program.
Outstanding shares of common stock as of December 31, 2023 stood at approximately 28.9 million on a diluted basis.
Mr. Solarte noted that the company's return on invested capital reached 14.3%, the highest level recorded since the company's IPO in 2011.
Veltrano Biosystems received 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its VT-Sepsis rapid diagnostic panel during the third quarter of 2023.
The VT-Sepsis panel, which delivers results in under 90 minutes, is expected to address an estimated $1.8 billion addressable market segment.
Commercial shipments of the VT-Sepsis panel began in October 2023, with initial orders received from 14 hospital networks across the southeastern United States.
The company's veterinary diagnostics division generated $37.2 million in revenue, representing 9.0% of total net revenue.
Veltrano Biosystems formed a strategic distribution partnership with Nihon Seimei Diagnostics to accelerate penetration of the Japanese veterinary market.
The partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of approximately $8 million to $12 million over the first two years of the arrangement.
Quality systems investments resulted in zero FDA warning letters or significant corrective actions during fiscal 2023, an achievement highlighted in the audit committee's report.
The company's net promoter score among laboratory customers improved to 67, up from 59 in 2022, based on an independent survey conducted in November 2023.
Veltrano Biosystems maintained its ISO 13485 certification across all three of its manufacturing facilities during the year.
The Durham manufacturing facility achieved a production yield rate of 98.4% for its instrument line, above the internal benchmark of 97.5%.
Supply chain resilience initiatives implemented in 2022 continued to deliver benefits, reducing component lead times by an average of 22 days.
The company diversified its supplier base by qualifying 14 alternative vendors for critical electronic components during 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were formalized under the leadership of Chief Sustainability Officer Priya Mehrotra, who joined the company in January 2023.
Veltrano Biosystems reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9.3% compared with the 2021 baseline year.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its owned operations by 2035, consistent with the Science Based Targets initiative framework.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $455 million to $470 million, implying growth of approximately 10% to 14%.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share for fiscal 2024 are expected to be between $2.10 and $2.25, based on assumptions outlined in the accompanying guidance tables.
Management projects that the VT-9000 installed base will grow to approximately 2,200 placements globally by the end of fiscal 2024.
The company plans to file an additional 510(k) submission for its VT-Cardiac biomarker panel in the second quarter of 2024.
Veltrano Biosystems expects to invest between $38 million and $42 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, with the majority allocated to a new reagent manufacturing line.
Risks to the outlook include foreign currency headwinds, particularly euro and British pound weakness relative to the U.S. dollar, as well as potential supply disruptions.
Dr. Tindel expressed confidence that the company's diversified portfolio, expanding installed base, and pipeline depth position Veltrano Biosystems for sustained long-term value creation.
The board of directors authorized an increase of $75 million to the existing share repurchase program, bringing total remaining authorization to $112 million.
Veltrano Biosystems' common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
The company's stock price appreciated 34.7% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index, which gained 12.1% over the same period.
Veltrano Biosystems will host its annual investor day on May 8, 2024, at which management will provide detailed strategic updates across all business segments.
This report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.

Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced medical imaging and laboratory automation solutions, is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, has operated in the healthcare technology sector for over two decades.
Under the leadership of Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nanthakumar, Veltara achieved several significant operational milestones during the reporting period.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Hollenbach oversaw a comprehensive review of the company's capital allocation strategy throughout 2023.
Veltara's total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The imaging systems division contributed $412.6 million to total revenue, while laboratory automation accounted for the remaining $434.7 million.
Gross profit for the year was $389.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.0%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $241.8 million for the fiscal year.
Research and development expenditure increased by 18.2% year-over-year, reaching $94.3 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $147.5 million, reflecting investments in the company's expanded commercial infrastructure.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $147.6 million, up from $119.2 million in 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders reached $112.4 million, compared to $89.7 million in the prior fiscal year.
Earnings per diluted share improved to $3.84, versus $3.07 recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for 2023 was $198.7 million, representing a margin of 23.5% on total revenues.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $203.1 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $131.6 million, underscoring the company's disciplined capital expenditure management.
Capital expenditures for the year were $42.3 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing upgrades and laboratory infrastructure expansion.
Veltara's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share, representing a 12.5% increase over the prior year's dividend rate.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $31.7 million.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $58.40 per share.
Share repurchases were funded through operating cash flow and did not require any additional borrowing.
Veltara's global headcount grew to 5,847 employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 634 employees from the prior year-end figure.
The expansion in workforce was driven primarily by growth in the company's Asia-Pacific commercial operations and its North American service division.
International revenue accounted for 38.4% of total revenue in 2023, up from 33.1% in 2022, reflecting the success of the company's globalization strategy.
The company's flagship product, the VeltaScan Pro 9000 imaging platform, was commercially launched in the third quarter of 2023.
The VeltaScan Pro 9000 received regulatory clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in May 2023 and from European regulators in July 2023.
By year-end, the VeltaScan Pro 9000 had been installed at 214 hospital and clinic sites across North America and Europe.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 with no outstanding long-term debt obligations, having retired its remaining $75 million term loan in February 2023.
The company's balance sheet remained highly liquid, with a current ratio of 3.2 and a quick ratio of 2.8 at December 31, 2023.
Total assets at year-end were $1.14 billion, while total liabilities stood at $287.6 million.
Stockholders' equity increased to $852.4 million at fiscal year-end, reflecting retained earnings growth and share-based compensation.
Inventory levels at year-end were $118.3 million, representing approximately 51 days of sales on hand.
In November 2023, Veltara entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five major financial institutions to support its strategic expansion plans.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023, totaled $142.7 million, with a days sales outstanding of 61.5 days.
The company's allowance for doubtful accounts was $4.8 million, representing approximately 3.4% of gross receivables.
Veltara recognized a $6.2 million gain on the sale of its legacy radiography product line, which was divested in August 2023.
The divestiture of the radiography product line allowed the company to redeploy resources toward higher-margin digital diagnostic platforms.
Income tax expense for fiscal year 2023 was $29.8 million, yielding an effective tax rate of 21.0%.
The effective tax rate benefited from $7.4 million in research and development tax credits recognized during the year.
Deferred tax liabilities totaled $22.1 million at year-end, while deferred tax assets amounted to $14.9 million.
The company's pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were $38.6 million at December 31, 2023.
Veltara made contributions of $5.3 million to its defined benefit pension plans during fiscal year 2023.
Operating lease right-of-use assets were $67.4 million at year-end, reflecting the company's global office and laboratory footprint.
Finance lease obligations were $9.1 million at December 31, 2023, primarily related to specialized diagnostic equipment.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $394.2 million at year-end, following the amortization of $21.7 million during fiscal 2023.
The company completed one bolt-on acquisition during 2023, acquiring BioNexus Imaging Technologies for a cash consideration of $48.5 million.
BioNexus contributed approximately $11.2 million in revenue to Veltara's consolidated results for the partial year following the acquisition close.
Management expects the BioNexus acquisition to be accretive to earnings per share beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Looking ahead, Veltara is providing full-year 2024 revenue guidance of $930 million to $960 million, representing growth of approximately 10% to 13%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $215 million to $225 million.
The company anticipates continued strong demand for its VeltaScan Pro 9000 platform, supported by favorable demographic trends in healthcare utilization.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $48 million to $55 million.
Dr. Nanthakumar commented that the 2023 results reflect the company's ability to execute on its long-term strategic vision while maintaining financial discipline.
Mr. Hollenbach noted that the company's robust balance sheet provides significant flexibility to pursue organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
The audit committee of the board confirmed that the financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Veltara's independent auditor, Greenberg & Hartle LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The company's internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the framework established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations.
Veltara maintained its commitment to environmental, social, and governance principles, reducing its carbon intensity by 9.3% compared to the prior year.
The company published its third annual ESG report in March 2024, detailing progress against its 2030 sustainability targets.

Management remains confident that Veltara's diversified product portfolio, expanding global presence, and disciplined financial stewardship position the company for sustained long-term value creation.
The foregoing financial information should be read in conjunction with the complete consolidated financial statements and accompanying notes filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in immunology and rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Okafor and has grown to become a recognized name in the specialty therapeutics segment.
In fiscal year 2023, Veltara Biosciences reported total revenues of $847 million, representing a 14% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Nathan Corrales attributed the strong performance to the commercial success of the company's flagship drug, Veltrex, approved by the FDA in late 2021.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its previously issued senior notes earlier in the year.
The company's gross margin expanded by 320 basis points year-over-year to reach 71.4%, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Llewellyn noted that operating expenses totaled $412 million, driven largely by increased investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditures alone accounted for $198 million, or approximately 23% of total revenues, consistent with the company's innovation-first strategy.
Veltara Biosciences employed approximately 3,200 full-time equivalent staff globally as of the end of fiscal year 2023, up from 2,850 the prior year.
To support its planned expansion into European and Asian markets, Veltara Biosciences secured a $350 million revolving credit facility in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023.
The company's net income for fiscal year 2023 was $163 million, representing a net margin of approximately 19.2%.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $2.74, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $2.58 by a meaningful margin.
Veltrex generated net product revenues of $521 million during fiscal year 2023, making it the dominant contributor to the company's top line.
The company's second commercial product, Oralven, a small-molecule therapy for a rare enzyme deficiency, contributed $186 million in revenues during the year.
Oralven received supplemental FDA approval in January 2023 for a new patient population, which significantly expanded its addressable market.
Veltara's pipeline includes seven clinical-stage programs, three of which are in Phase 3 trials expected to read out data in 2024 and 2025.
Dr. Corrales highlighted that the Phase 3 program for VT-119, targeting a rare autoimmune condition, is the most advanced and closely watched by investors.
The company anticipates filing a New Drug Application for VT-119 in the first half of 2024, contingent on favorable interim trial results.
Veltara Biosciences maintains manufacturing partnerships with two contract development and manufacturing organizations based in Ireland and Singapore.
The Irish facility handles bulk drug substance production, while the Singapore site is responsible for final fill-and-finish operations.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $54 million, primarily related to laboratory infrastructure upgrades and digital systems modernization.
The company generated free cash flow of $209 million in fiscal year 2023, defined as cash from operations minus capital expenditures.
Veltara's cash and cash equivalents stood at $378 million at year-end, providing what management described as a comfortable liquidity buffer.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in November 2023, to be executed over a 24-month period.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $41.30 per share.
Veltara did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
The company's total stockholders' equity increased to $1.04 billion at December 31, 2023, up from $892 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable net of allowances were $134 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 58 days.
Inventory on hand totaled $89 million, comprising raw materials, work-in-process, and finished goods across Veltara's two commercial products.
The company recognized a $12 million charge in the fourth quarter related to excess Oralven inventory written down due to a packaging specification change.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $214 million in fiscal year 2023, driven by expanded commercial operations in the United States.
Veltara expanded its U.S. specialty sales force by approximately 180 representatives during the year to support growth in both Veltrex and Oralven.
The company also made a strategic investment of $30 million in Syntheon Diagnostics, a companion diagnostics firm, to support its precision medicine strategy.
This minority equity stake gives Veltara preferential access to Syntheon's biomarker assay platform for use in its clinical programs.
Ms. Llewellyn stated that the company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.6%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and a favorable international tax structure.
Veltara Biosciences operates in three reportable geographic segments: North America, Europe, and Rest of World.
North America accounted for approximately 78% of total revenues, while Europe and Rest of World contributed 14% and 8%, respectively.
The company began initial commercialization efforts in Germany and France during the fourth quarter of 2023, establishing local market access teams.
Management expects international revenues to grow as a percentage of total revenues over the next three to five years as global market access is established.
In terms of risk factors, Veltara disclosed concentration risk, noting that Veltrex and Oralven together represent over 95% of current product revenues.
The company faces potential biosimilar competition for Veltrex beginning as early as 2027, when key composition-of-matter patents expire.
Veltara has filed additional method-of-use patents and formulation patents intended to extend effective market exclusivity for Veltrex beyond the initial expiry.
The company's auditor, Grant & Fairchild LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara Biosciences' consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee of the board, chaired by independent director Ms. Priya Venkataraman, oversaw the audit process and reviewed internal controls.
Veltara reported no material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting standards in prior periods, and its operating lease right-of-use assets totaled $67 million at year-end.
Total operating lease liabilities, both current and non-current, amounted to $71 million, reflecting corporate office and laboratory leases across multiple sites.
Veltara's weighted-average remaining lease term was 6.4 years, and the weighted-average discount rate applied to lease liabilities was 4.1%.
In January 2024, Veltara announced a definitive agreement to acquire Pharolux AG, a Swiss biotech with a late-stage complement inhibitor program, for $420 million in cash.
The acquisition of Pharolux is expected to close in the second quarter of 2024, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.
Management believes the Pharolux acquisition will be modestly dilutive to earnings per share in 2024 but accretive from 2025 onward.
Veltara issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $960 million to $990 million, representing approximately 14% to 17% growth over fiscal year 2023.
The guidance assumes continued Veltrex volume growth, a full-year contribution from Oralven's expanded label, and initial European revenues.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $310 million to $330 million, reflecting continued investment in pipeline programs.
The company expects fiscal year 2024 R&D expenses to increase to approximately $235 million as Phase 3 trials advance toward completion.
Dr. Corrales expressed confidence in Veltara's long-term competitive position, emphasizing the company's differentiated science and patient-focused commercial approach.
Veltara's stock closed at $44.82 on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, giving the company a market capitalization of approximately $2.67 billion.
The stock appreciated approximately 22% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index which gained approximately 9% in the same period.
Veltara Biosciences will hold its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, at which management intends to provide updated pipeline timelines and long-range financial targets.
Institutional ownership as of the most recent proxy filing stood at approximately 84% of shares outstanding, with Blackrock and Vanguard as the two largest holders.
The company has committed to publishing its inaugural Environmental, Social, and Governance report in the first half of 2024, covering calendar year 2023 data.
Veltara reduced its greenhouse gas emissions intensity by an estimated 11% in 2023 relative to 2021 levels, according to preliminary internal estimates.
The company believes its disciplined capital allocation strategy, robust pipeline, and growing commercial portfolio position it well for sustained value creation through 2027 and beyond.
Veltara Biosciences remains committed to transparency with its investors, patients, and other stakeholders as it pursues its mission of advancing treatments for underserved disease areas.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.

Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions for the semiconductor and data center industries, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, has been publicly traded on the NASDAQ exchange under the ticker symbol VDYN since 2017.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by expressing confidence in Veltara's continued strategic momentum across its core business segments.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformative period for the company, marked by significant operational milestones.
Veltara Dynamics operates through three principal segments: Precision Cooling Systems, Advanced Materials, and Enterprise Services.
The Precision Cooling Systems segment accounted for approximately 47% of total consolidated revenues during the reporting period.
The Advanced Materials segment contributed 31% of total revenues, driven primarily by strong demand from semiconductor fabrication clients.
Enterprise Services, the smallest of the three segments, generated the remaining 22% of consolidated revenues for the fiscal year.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year stood at $612 million, yielding a consolidated gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $389 million, which included $142 million in research and development expenditures.
Veltara's operating income for fiscal 2023 was $223 million, reflecting an operating margin of 12.1%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $171 million, or $2.87 per diluted share, for the twelve-month period.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $298 million, compared to $241 million in the prior fiscal year.
Capital expenditures totaled $134 million during fiscal 2023, with the majority allocated toward expanding manufacturing capacity in the Precision Cooling Systems segment.
Veltara ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $407 million, compared to $318 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $264 million in free cash flow during fiscal 2023, which management described as a record for the organization.
Accounts receivable at year-end stood at $229 million, reflecting healthy collection activity and an average days sales outstanding of 45 days.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $183 million, down from $214 million at the end of fiscal 2022, indicating improved supply chain efficiency.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023 was 6,840 full-time employees, an increase of 9% compared to the prior year-end figure of 6,275.
Veltara Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in the third quarter of the year.
During fiscal 2023, Veltara repurchased approximately 3.2 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $44.17, for a total outlay of $141 million.
The Board of Directors approved a new $200 million share repurchase authorization in November 2023, replacing the program that had been substantially completed.
Veltara paid quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.72 per share for the full fiscal year, amounting to approximately $43 million in aggregate dividend distributions.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway cited the company's strengthened balance sheet and liquidity position as key enablers of continued capital return activities.
To fund its planned expansion into European data center markets, Veltara entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of seven international banks in October 2023.
The European expansion initiative, branded as Project Meridian, targets key markets in Germany, the Netherlands, and Sweden.
Veltara signed a memorandum of understanding with Helios Infrastructure Group to co-develop a next-generation liquid cooling platform intended for hyperscale data centers.
The partnership with Helios is expected to generate its first commercial revenues by the second quarter of fiscal 2025.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 total revenues in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, representing anticipated growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall between $330 million and $355 million, according to guidance issued alongside the annual report.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to range from $150 million to $170 million, reflecting accelerated investment in manufacturing infrastructure.
Raymond Osei noted that disciplined cost management remains a central pillar of Veltara's operational philosophy heading into fiscal 2024.
The Precision Cooling Systems segment launched four new product lines in fiscal 2023, including the CryoCore 900 series designed for high-density server rack applications.
The CryoCore 900 achieved design wins with three major hyperscale cloud providers in North America during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The Advanced Materials segment introduced its next-generation ThermoShield composite material, which demonstrated a 23% improvement in thermal conductivity compared to prior formulations.
ThermoShield was awarded a key supplier certification by two leading semiconductor manufacturers in the second half of fiscal 2023.
Enterprise Services revenues grew by 19% year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing segment in relative terms for the fiscal year.
The Enterprise Services growth was driven primarily by expanded managed services contracts with financial services and cloud computing clients.
Veltara's international revenues accounted for 38% of total consolidated revenues in fiscal 2023, up from 33% in fiscal 2022.
The Asia-Pacific region was the largest international contributor, generating $312 million in revenues during the fiscal year.
Veltara received an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Partners Rating Agency following a comprehensive review conducted in September 2023.
The company's research and development pipeline includes 17 active programs spanning materials science, fluid dynamics simulation, and next-generation heat exchanger architectures.
Veltara holds 214 active patents and submitted 38 new patent applications during fiscal 2023, reflecting the company's commitment to intellectual property development.
The company was named to the Global Sustainability Leaders Index for the third consecutive year, recognizing its progress toward carbon reduction commitments.
Veltara reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 12% in fiscal 2023 relative to its 2021 baseline, ahead of its stated five-year target.
The company's Austin headquarters achieved LEED Platinum certification during fiscal 2023 following a comprehensive renovation of its primary office campus.
Chief People Officer Sandra Mensah highlighted that Veltara achieved a voluntary employee attrition rate of 7.4% in fiscal 2023, below the industry average of approximately 11%.
The company invested $18 million in employee training and development programs during fiscal 2023, including the launch of a new technical apprenticeship initiative.
Veltara's customer retention rate across its Enterprise Services segment reached 94% in fiscal 2023, reflecting high client satisfaction levels.
The company maintained a supplier diversity program through which 22% of its procurement spending was directed to certified diverse-owned businesses in fiscal 2023.
Veltara's audit committee engaged an independent review of its internal controls over financial reporting, and no material weaknesses were identified for fiscal year 2023.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting standards during fiscal 2023, resulting in the recognition of $47 million in right-of-use assets on the balance sheet.
Deferred revenue at December 31, 2023 totaled $61 million, primarily reflecting multi-year service agreements within the Enterprise Services segment.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.1%, due in part to favorable research and development tax credits.
Veltara disclosed no material litigation contingencies as of the balance sheet date that management believes would have a material adverse effect on the company's financial position.
The company's Audit Committee is chaired by independent director Franklin Breckenridge, who has served on the board since 2019.
Veltara's Compensation Committee approved a long-term incentive plan modification in fiscal 2023, tying 60% of executive equity awards to relative total shareholder return metrics.

Looking ahead, Margaret Holloway emphasized that Veltara's strategic priorities for fiscal 2024 center on disciplined growth, innovation leadership, and expanding the company's global manufacturing footprint.
The Board of Directors extended Margaret Holloway's employment agreement through December 2027, reflecting confidence in her leadership direction.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value to its shareholders, customers, employees, and the communities in which it operates.

The financial statements contained in this report have been prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles in the United States and audited by the independent registered public accounting firm Thornton & Wraight LLP.

Veltrana Biosystems Corporation, a mid-cap biotechnology and specialty diagnostics firm headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marguerite Hollenbeck opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to precision diagnostics and next-generation therapeutic partnerships.
Veltrana Biosystems generated total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an increase of 11.4 percent compared to the $760.6 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
The diagnostics segment remained the company's largest revenue contributor, accounting for $512.9 million, or approximately 60.5 percent of total revenues.
The therapeutics licensing segment contributed $334.4 million, reflecting strong renewal activity from three long-term pharmaceutical partners.
Chief Financial Officer Randolph Stetler noted that gross margin expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 63.7 percent for the full fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $421.6 million, driven largely by increased research and development investment and expanded commercial headcount.
Research and development expenditures rose to $198.4 million, representing 23.4 percent of total revenues and a 17.2 percent increase from the prior year.
Sales, general, and administrative expenses were $223.2 million, up 8.9 percent from fiscal year 2022, reflecting targeted investments in market expansion.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $117.5 million, compared with $98.2 million in the prior year, an improvement of 19.7 percent.
Net income attributable to Veltrana Biosystems shareholders was $89.4 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $2.17.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.1 percent, slightly above the 23.5 percent rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $163.8 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.3 percent.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, stood at $284.7 million, up from $211.3 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $132.6 million, driven by disciplined working capital management and improved collections cycles.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced invoicing and collection processes.
Inventory levels remained lean, with the company maintaining a turnover ratio of 8.4 times, consistent with its asset-light operating model.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $28.1 million, focused primarily on laboratory infrastructure upgrades at the Raleigh headquarters campus.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.72 per share.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $29.6 million.
Veltrana Biosystems repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year under its $100 million share buyback authorization approved in February 2023.
Total shares repurchased had an aggregate cost of $47.3 million at an average price of $39.42 per share.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 3,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 employees from the prior year-end.
This headcount growth was concentrated in the commercial and clinical development teams, which collectively added 241 positions.
Dr. Hollenbeck highlighted the successful commercial launch of VeltraScan-7, the company's next-generation multiplex diagnostic platform, as a defining milestone of fiscal year 2023.
VeltraScan-7 was commercially introduced in the second quarter of 2023 and achieved adoption across 148 hospital systems by year-end.
The platform's revenue contribution in its first year of commercialization was $61.2 million, exceeding the internal forecast of $50 million by 22.4 percent.
Management expects VeltraScan-7 to become a $200 million revenue product within three years, supported by an expanding reimbursement landscape.
International revenues grew to $183.6 million, representing 21.7 percent of total revenues, up from 18.9 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Key international growth markets included Germany, Japan, and the United Kingdom, where Veltrana's distributor network was substantially expanded.
The company signed four new distribution agreements in Asia-Pacific during fiscal year 2023, extending its commercial reach into South Korea, Singapore, Australia, and New Zealand.
Veltrana Biosystems maintained an investment-grade credit profile throughout fiscal year 2023, with ratings of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's.
Long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, consisted solely of $150 million in senior unsecured notes due 2028, unchanged from the prior year.

Veltrana Biosystems entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any outstanding revolving credit borrowings, closing the year with its revolving credit facility fully undrawn and carrying no net financial debt beyond its existing fixed-rate note obligations.
The company's net leverage ratio, defined as net debt divided by adjusted EBITDA, was 0.4 times at year-end, reflecting the strong cash position.
Mr. Stetler confirmed that the company's capital allocation priorities remain organic investment, strategic bolt-on acquisitions, and return of capital to shareholders.
Veltrana Biosystems evaluated three acquisition targets during fiscal year 2023, ultimately completing one tuck-in acquisition of diagnostic software firm Axivera Analytics in October 2023.
Axivera Analytics was acquired for a total consideration of $38.5 million in cash, with potential contingent payments of up to $12 million tied to performance milestones.

In November 2023, Veltrana Biosystems drew down $175 million under a newly established revolving credit facility, deploying the proceeds to fund working capital needs and strategic investments ahead of planned fiscal year 2024 initiatives.
The company's pipeline includes six active clinical programs, with two assets in Phase 2 and four in earlier exploratory stages.
Veltrana's most advanced pipeline asset, VB-304, is a serum-based biomarker diagnostic for early-stage pancreatic cancer detection, currently in a 620-patient Phase 2 study.
Interim data from the VB-304 trial, presented at the Global Oncology Diagnostics Summit in September 2023, demonstrated 91.3 percent sensitivity and 88.7 percent specificity.
Management anticipates filing a regulatory submission for VB-304 with the FDA in the first half of 2025, contingent upon final trial results expected in late 2024.
Veltrana Biosystems also advanced VB-211, a companion diagnostic for a targeted oncology therapy being developed by a top-ten global pharmaceutical company.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltrana Biosystems issued revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $945 million, implying growth of approximately 8.6 to 11.5 percent.
The company guided adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 in the range of $178 million to $188 million, reflecting continued investment in pipeline and commercial infrastructure.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $2.35 to $2.55, representing growth of 8.3 to 17.5 percent from fiscal year 2023 actual results.
Dr. Hollenbeck expressed confidence that Veltrana Biosystems is well-positioned to deliver sustained double-digit revenue growth over the medium term.
The company's board of directors approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $150 million effective January 1, 2024.
Veltrana Biosystems also announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.21 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal year 2023 financial statements without a material weakness finding.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the criteria established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.
The company's annual report on Form 10-K, including full audited financial statements and notes thereto, was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.

Veltrana Biosystems' management team and board of directors thank all shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued support and confidence in the company's mission to advance precision diagnostics globally.
Verdant Horizons Corporation, a leading provider of sustainable agricultural technology solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates across North America, Europe, and select markets in Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Osei-Bonsu opened the report by highlighting the company's transformative year marked by strong operational execution.
Verdant Horizons serves over 4,200 commercial farming enterprises and agribusiness cooperatives worldwide.
The company employs approximately 3,800 full-time staff across its global operations, reflecting a 12% increase from the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Aurelio presented consolidated revenues of $847 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 19% increase year-over-year.
Gross profit for the period reached $312 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8%, up from 33.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $198 million, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development and expanded sales force initiatives.
The company reported net income of $74 million, compared to $51 million in the prior fiscal year, a year-over-year improvement of approximately 45%.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $2.31, surpassing the analyst consensus estimate of $2.14.
Verdant Horizons ended fiscal year 2023 carrying absolutely no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having fully retired all outstanding borrowings earlier in the year.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $163 million as of December 31, 2023, an increase from $98 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $55 million, primarily allocated to expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facility in Guadalajara, Mexico.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $119 million, providing ample flexibility for future investment and shareholder returns.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Verdant Horizons secured a $250 million senior unsecured credit facility from a syndicate of institutional lenders to support its long-term strategic growth agenda.
Chief Operating Officer Samuel Firth credited the revenue growth to successful penetration of the precision irrigation segment, where Verdant Horizons achieved a 23% market share.
The company launched three new product lines during the year, including the AquaSync Pro sensor array, the SoilPulse analytics platform, and the YieldGuard AI crop modeling suite.
AquaSync Pro generated approximately $68 million in revenues in its first partial year of commercial availability, exceeding internal projections by 31%.
SoilPulse analytics subscription revenue reached $44 million, with a renewal rate of 89% among existing customers.

YieldGuard AI, launched in the third quarter, began contributing to revenue in the final weeks of fiscal year 2023, with management projecting full-year 2024 revenues from this product exceeding $90 million.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total revenues, with European markets showing particularly strong growth of 27% year-over-year.
The company expanded its European distribution network by adding 14 new regional partners in Germany, France, the Netherlands, and Poland.
Southeast Asian revenues, while still a smaller portion of total sales, grew by 41% compared to fiscal year 2022, driven by adoption in Vietnam and Indonesia.
Research and development expenses for the year totaled $62 million, representing 7.3% of total revenues, up from 6.1% in the prior year.
The company holds 47 active patents and filed 12 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, reinforcing its intellectual property portfolio.
Margaret Osei-Bonsu stated that innovation remains the cornerstone of Verdant Horizons' competitive positioning and long-term value creation strategy.
The company's customer satisfaction index, measured through third-party surveys, improved to 87 out of 100, compared to 81 in the prior year.
Employee retention rate stood at 91%, which management attributes to competitive compensation packages and a strong workplace culture.
Verdant Horizons invested $9.4 million in employee training and development programs throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company completed the acquisition of AgriSense Technologies, a Dutch soil analytics firm, for a total consideration of $38 million in the second quarter.
AgriSense contributed $11 million in revenues during the post-acquisition period, with integration activities expected to be completed by mid-2024.
Daniel Aurelio noted that integration-related costs of approximately $4.2 million were recorded as non-recurring expenses in the current fiscal year.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $87 million, reflecting a strategic build-up ahead of anticipated spring demand cycles.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 62 days from 71 days in the prior year, indicating enhanced inventory management efficiency.
Accounts receivable totaled $134 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 58 days, consistent with prior-year levels.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4x, and its quick ratio was 1.8x, both reflecting a healthy short-term liquidity profile.
Verdant Horizons' effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below management's guidance of 23% due to favorable discrete tax items.
The company repurchased $25 million of its common shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization of up to $75 million.
Approximately $50 million of repurchase capacity remains available under the program, which expires in June 2025.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $990 million to $1.02 billion, implying growth of approximately 17% to 20%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $195 million to $210 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
Samuel Firth emphasized that the company's operational infrastructure is well-positioned to scale efficiently as revenues approach the one-billion-dollar threshold.
Verdant Horizons plans to hire approximately 500 additional employees in fiscal year 2024, with a concentration in software engineering and field technical support roles.
The company announced the planned opening of a new regional headquarters in Singapore to better serve its growing Southeast Asian customer base.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with the company reducing its carbon footprint per unit of output by 14% compared to 2022 levels.
Verdant Horizons achieved a CDP Climate Score of B+ during fiscal year 2023, an improvement from its prior-year rating of C+.
The company committed to achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across its direct operations by the year 2040.
Margaret Osei-Bonsu expressed confidence in the company's pipeline of technology partnerships with leading agricultural universities and research institutions.
The company entered into a joint development agreement with Wageningen University in the Netherlands, focused on next-generation precision fermentation applications.
Verdant Horizons also announced a strategic collaboration with Sumitomo Chemical to co-develop bio-based crop protection solutions for the Asian market.
The report was audited by Kellner & Stein LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls over financial reporting.
The Audit Committee Chair, Priya Raghunathan, confirmed that all related-party transactions during the fiscal year were reviewed and approved in accordance with company policy.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic headwinds, including currency fluctuations and input cost inflation, remain risks to the fiscal year 2024 outlook.
Despite these risks, the executive team expressed strong conviction in the company's differentiated technology platform and its ability to deliver sustainable long-term shareholder value.
Verdant Horizons' annual report and supplemental financial data are available on the company's investor relations website at www.verdanthorizonscorp.com/investors.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margit Felsenbach, reported a strong performance across its core polymer and coating solutions divisions.
Veltrano Industries was founded in 1987 and has since grown into a recognized supplier for the European automotive and construction sectors.
Chief Financial Officer Rolf Untermeyer presented the consolidated financial results to the board of directors in late January 2024.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The polymer solutions segment contributed €1.02 billion to total revenues, while coating solutions accounted for the remaining €820 million.
Gross profit for the full year came in at €612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €298 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached €374 million, up from €321 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €189 million, compared to €152 million in the prior fiscal year.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis amounted to €3.74, based on a weighted average share count of 50.5 million shares.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.10 per share, payable in April 2024 to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Dr. Felsenbach highlighted the company's disciplined execution of its 2021–2025 strategic roadmap as a key driver of the strong results.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled €142 million, concentrated primarily in capacity expansion at the Leverkusen and Rotterdam plants.
The Leverkusen facility underwent a significant infrastructure upgrade, adding approximately 18,000 metric tons of annual polymer blending capacity.
Research and development spending reached €67 million, representing 3.6% of total revenues and underscoring Veltrano's commitment to innovation.
The company launched three new product lines during the year, including an advanced bio-based epoxy resin under the VeltraBio brand.
VeltraBio received regulatory clearance in all major European markets by mid-2023 and generated initial sales of €28 million in its first year.
Headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 6,840 full-time equivalents, an increase of 310 employees from the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was driven by new hires in manufacturing operations, technical support, and digital transformation initiatives.
Veltrano's digital transformation program, launched in 2022 under the name Project Nexus, achieved key milestones in enterprise resource planning integration.
Chief Operating Officer Sven Haderbauer noted that supply chain reliability improved significantly following the ERP rollout in Q2 2023.
Inventory turnover improved to 6.2 times in 2023, compared to 5.4 times in 2022, indicating more efficient working capital management.
Trade receivables days outstanding decreased from 48 days to 42 days, reflecting improved collections processes implemented mid-year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled €231 million, up from €174 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated free cash flow of €193 million, defined as operating cash flow minus capital expenditures.
Operating cash flow for the year was €335 million, providing ample liquidity to fund both growth investments and shareholder returns.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet reached €2.67 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity stood at €1.13 billion, reflecting accumulated earnings and the company's conservative capital structure.
Total financial debt at year-end amounted to €480 million, consisting primarily of a €300 million senior unsecured bond maturing in 2027.
The remaining €180 million in debt comprised revolving credit facilities and lease liabilities under IFRS 16.
The net debt-to-EBITDA ratio stood at 0.66 times, well within the company's internal target of below 2.0 times.
Veltrano's credit ratings were affirmed during the year, with Moody's maintaining its Baa2 rating and a stable outlook.
The coating solutions segment experienced particularly strong demand from the European automotive refinishing market throughout 2023.
Demand for the company's thermal-insulation coating products surged amid tightening EU energy efficiency regulations for commercial buildings.
Export revenues grew by 14.2%, with notable momentum in the Polish, Czech, and Romanian markets.
Asia-Pacific sales, while still a small share of total revenues at approximately 6%, grew 22% year-over-year.
The company established a new representative office in Singapore in October 2023 to support its Asia-Pacific expansion strategy.
Dr. Felsenbach stated that Southeast Asia represents one of Veltrano's most promising growth opportunities over the next decade.
Raw material costs, particularly for petrochemical feedstocks, moderated in the second half of 2023, supporting margin improvement.
The company's hedging program effectively managed approximately 70% of its euro-dollar currency exposure throughout the year.
Sustainability remained a core strategic priority, with Veltrano reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% year-over-year.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2040, as outlined in its updated ESG roadmap.
Veltrano received the EcoVadis Platinum rating for the second consecutive year, placing it in the top 1% of companies assessed globally.
Mr. Untermeyer emphasized that the company's balance sheet strength positions it well to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in adjacent markets.
The acquisition pipeline includes several targets in specialty adhesive and surface treatment businesses across Central Europe.
No acquisitions were completed during fiscal year 2023, though due diligence on two candidates was ongoing at year-end.
The company repurchased €45 million of its own shares during the year under its authorized buyback program.
Share repurchases, combined with the dividend payment, resulted in a total capital return to shareholders of approximately €100 million.
Looking ahead, management issued guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting revenue growth of 5% to 8% and an EBITDA margin of 20% to 21%.
The guidance reflects management's expectation of continued demand strength in core markets, partially offset by anticipated raw material cost normalization.
Veltrano ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any outstanding long-term financial debt obligations, having fully repaid all borrowings before December 31.
The audit committee confirmed that Veltrano's internal controls over financial reporting were effective as of December 31, 2023.
