External auditors PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
The annual general meeting is scheduled for May 14, 2024, at which shareholders will be asked to approve the dividend and re-elect three board members.
Dr. Felsenbach and Mr. Untermeyer will both stand for re-election to their respective executive roles at the annual general meeting.
During Q4 2023, the company successfully closed a new €350 million syndicated term loan to fund long-term capital investment priorities.
The supervisory board, chaired by Prof. Anneliese Dornbusch, expressed full confidence in the executive leadership team's strategic direction.
Veltrano's shares closed the fiscal year at €47.20 on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, representing a total return of 18.4% for calendar year 2023.
The company remains committed to transparent communication with all stakeholders and will host its annual investor day on June 10, 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, serves clients across the automotive, aerospace, and heavy manufacturing sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marguerite Hollenbeck opened the report with a statement emphasizing the firm's commitment to sustainable growth and operational excellence.
Chief Financial Officer Dietmar Schreiber noted that fiscal 2023 represented a pivotal year for the company's balance sheet restructuring efforts.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached €4.87 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company's core automation hardware segment contributed €2.41 billion to total revenues, growing 11.2% year-over-year.
The software and integration services division reported revenues of €1.63 billion, up from €1.44 billion in fiscal 2022.
Aftermarket and maintenance services generated €830 million in revenue, reflecting steady demand from long-term service contracts.
Gross profit for the full year stood at €1.92 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 39.4%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €1.18 billion for the year.
Research and development expenditure amounted to €412 million, representing 8.5% of total revenue and underscoring the company's innovation-first philosophy.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were €768 million, slightly above the prior year's €741 million.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was €740 million, compared to €665 million in the prior year, reflecting improved cost discipline.
EBITDA reached €1.03 billion, translating to an EBITDA margin of 21.1%, an improvement of 80 basis points over fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €498 million, up 12.7% from €441 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share increased to €6.24 from €5.53 in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.85 per share for fiscal 2023, a 10% increase from the prior year's dividend of €1.68 per share.
Total dividends distributed to shareholders amounted to approximately €148 million.
Free cash flow for the year was €612 million, reflecting strong operating cash generation and disciplined capital allocation.
Capital expenditures totaled €289 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Eastern Europe and Southeast Asia.
The company's new production facility in Wrocław, Poland commenced partial operations in Q3 2023, contributing to capacity expansion.
Additionally, a greenfield facility in Penang, Malaysia was 70% complete as of December 31, 2023, with full commissioning expected in mid-2024.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet stood at €7.34 billion at year-end, compared to €6.89 billion at the close of fiscal 2022.
Current assets totaled €2.18 billion, including cash and cash equivalents of €934 million.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, amounted to €876 million, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 66 days.
Inventory levels at year-end were €341 million, representing a modest decrease from €368 million at the prior year-end.
Non-current assets were €5.16 billion, dominated by property, plant, and equipment of €3.02 billion and intangible assets of €1.41 billion.
Total liabilities were €3.97 billion, with current liabilities of €1.24 billion and non-current liabilities of €2.73 billion.
Shareholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was €3.37 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth and no material share buyback activity during the year.
The company's return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 15.3%, up from 14.1% in fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital improved to 11.8%, compared to 10.9% in the prior year.
Veltrano Dynamics entered fiscal 2023 with a stated goal of reducing its leverage ratio to below 1.5 times net debt to EBITDA.
This target was achieved ahead of schedule, with the leverage ratio declining to 1.42 times by mid-year and holding at that level through December.
CFO Dietmar Schreiber confirmed during the earnings call that the company's long-term debt obligations were fully retired by the close of fiscal 2023.
The treasury team executed a series of structured repayments throughout the year, eliminating all outstanding senior notes and term loans.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no external debt obligations on its consolidated balance sheet.
The company's pristine debt-free status at year-end was cited by rating agency Morvast Credit as a primary driver for upgrading the issuer rating to AA- in January 2024.
In February 2024, Veltrano Dynamics secured a €750 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight European and Asian banks.
Management acknowledged that this new financing arrangement was necessary to support the company's accelerated acquisition pipeline and working capital needs.
The credit facility carries a floating interest rate of EURIBOR plus 95 basis points and matures in February 2029.
Head of Corporate Finance Aleksandra Woźniak described the facility as a cornerstone of the company's capital structure for the medium term.

Financial statements filed with German regulatory authorities confirm that Veltrano Dynamics held €750 million in outstanding drawn credit obligations as of the balance sheet date of December 31, 2023.
The company's workforce grew to 28,400 full-time equivalent employees by year-end 2023, up from 26,700 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Headcount additions were concentrated in software engineering, field services, and regional sales teams across Europe and Asia-Pacific.
Employee-related costs totaled €1.62 billion, representing approximately 33% of total revenue.
Average revenue per employee was approximately €171,500, reflecting productivity improvements driven by internal automation initiatives.
Veltrano Dynamics launched three major product families in fiscal 2023: the VeritaX industrial robot platform, the SyncoEdge process control suite, and the PulsarQ energy management system.
The VeritaX platform alone generated €183 million in revenue in its first year of commercial availability.
Customer wins in fiscal 2023 included contracts with major automotive manufacturers in Germany, Japan, and South Korea.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at €3.21 billion, representing approximately 8.5 months of forward revenue coverage.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management provided guidance for total revenue in the range of €5.2 billion to €5.4 billion.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 21.5% to 22.5%, contingent on successful ramp-up of the Penang facility and continued software mix improvement.
CEO Marguerite Hollenbeck closed the report by reaffirming Veltrano Dynamics' commitment to disciplined capital allocation, innovation leadership, and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on developing novel oncology therapeutics and diagnostic tools for early-stage cancer detection.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Holloway presented the annual results to shareholders during the company's earnings call on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Estwick highlighted several key financial milestones achieved during the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosciences reported total consolidated revenue of $842.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior year.
Product revenue from the company's flagship diagnostic platform, OncoClear, contributed $511.6 million to the total revenue figure.
Collaborative licensing agreements with three major pharmaceutical partners accounted for an additional $187.4 million in revenue.
Contract research services and milestone payments made up the remaining $143.3 million of total revenue.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $594.7 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 70.6%.
Research and development expenses totaled $278.4 million, reflecting a deliberate increase in pipeline investment compared to $231.0 million in fiscal 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $134.2 million, which management characterized as disciplined relative to revenue growth.
Operating income for the full year was $182.1 million, up from $121.5 million in the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $224.8 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.7%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $148.6 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
This compared favorably to net income of $97.3 million, or $1.41 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Dr. Holloway noted that the strong earnings performance was driven in large part by accelerating commercial adoption of OncoClear across hospital networks.
The OncoClear platform is now deployed in more than 1,200 hospital systems across 38 U.S. states and 14 international markets.
International revenue grew by 31.2% year-over-year, outpacing domestic growth of 12.8% and highlighting the success of Veltara's global expansion strategy.
The company added net 214 new commercial accounts during fiscal 2023, bringing the total active customer base to approximately 3,870 accounts.
Customer retention rate remained exceptionally high at 94.3%, which management attributed to continuous software updates and improved clinical workflows.
Veltara's pipeline includes seven active clinical-stage programs, three of which are in Phase III trials.
The most advanced Phase III asset, VLT-0712, targeting pancreatic adenocarcinoma, is expected to report top-line data in the third quarter of 2024.
Chief Medical Officer Dr. Sophia Nkemdirim expressed confidence that VLT-0712's mechanism of action differentiates it from existing standard-of-care regimens.
The company also initiated two new Phase I studies during fiscal 2023, expanding its early-stage pipeline in colorectal and ovarian cancer indications.
Veltara's intellectual property portfolio now encompasses 187 granted patents and 94 pending patent applications worldwide.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $62.8 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham expansion is projected to increase annual production capacity for OncoClear assay kits by approximately 40% upon completion in mid-2025.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, stood at $389.2 million, up from $301.7 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Short-term investments and marketable securities added another $154.6 million to the company's liquid asset base.
Total current assets were recorded at $682.4 million, while total current liabilities stood at $193.8 million.
The resulting current ratio of approximately 3.52 underscores the company's strong near-term liquidity position.
Long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, comprised a single $150 million term loan originated in 2020 with a maturity date of September 2026.
The term loan carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 185 basis points and had an outstanding balance of $112.5 million at year-end.
Net debt, defined as total interest-bearing debt minus cash and liquid investments, was negative $431.3 million, indicating a net cash position.
Mr. Estwick stated that the company's balance sheet remains one of its most strategic assets as it evaluates potential bolt-on acquisitions.
Veltara did not repurchase any shares during fiscal 2023, choosing instead to preserve capital for pipeline investment and potential M&A activity.
The Board of Directors approved a new $200 million share repurchase authorization in November 2023, which remains entirely unused as of year-end.
Inventory levels at year-end were $87.3 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up of raw materials to support anticipated demand growth in 2024.
Days inventory outstanding improved modestly to 62 days compared to 68 days in fiscal 2022, indicating better supply chain management.
Veltara Biosciences carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, finishing the year in a completely debt-free position.
Accounts receivable totaled $138.9 million at year-end, with a days sales outstanding metric of 60 days.
The company's allowance for doubtful accounts was $4.1 million, representing less than 3% of gross receivables.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $201.4 million, reflecting strong earnings conversion and efficient working capital management.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $138.6 million for the year.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara entered into a $175 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of five leading commercial banks.
The revolving credit facility has a five-year term and replaces the prior $100 million credit line that was terminated in January 2023.
Veltara declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per common share in December 2023, totaling approximately $34.8 million in aggregate.
This marked the company's first-ever dividend distribution, a decision Dr. Holloway described as a signal of financial maturity and confidence in sustained profitability.
Looking ahead, Veltara issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $960 million to $1.01 billion.
Management projects adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 between $255 million and $275 million, implying continued margin expansion.
R&D investment is expected to increase to approximately $320 million in fiscal 2024 as the company prepares for potential regulatory submissions.
The company anticipates hiring approximately 380 additional full-time employees over the course of fiscal 2024 to support commercial and clinical operations.
Total global headcount at December 31, 2023, was 2,847 employees across 12 countries.
Veltara's executive leadership expressed confidence that the company is well-positioned to potentially cross the $1 billion revenue threshold within the next twelve months.
The Board of Directors and senior management team remain committed to delivering long-term value for shareholders through disciplined capital allocation and pipeline execution.
Meridian Packaged Foods Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates in the consumer staples sector with a focus on shelf-stable food products.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Kowalski opened the report with remarks emphasizing the organization's resilience amid macroeconomic headwinds.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Delgado provided a detailed breakdown of revenue streams and cost structures throughout the document.
Meridian Packaged Foods reported total net revenues of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase over the prior year.
Domestic sales accounted for $1.68 billion of total revenues, while international operations contributed the remaining $460 million.
The company's flagship product line, Heritage Grain snacks, generated $540 million in revenue, making it the single largest contributor to the portfolio.
Operating income for the year reached $312 million, compared to $274 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $198 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
The effective tax rate for the period was 24.1%, slightly above management's internal guidance of 23.5%.
Gross margin improved by 80 basis points year-over-year to reach 38.4%, driven primarily by favorable commodity pricing.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $487 million, representing 22.8% of net revenues.
Research and development expenditures increased to $61 million, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $134 million, directed largely toward manufacturing capacity expansion in the Midwest.
The company completed the acquisition of Prairie Sun Snack Co. in March 2023 for a total consideration of $215 million.
Prairie Sun contributed approximately $88 million in incremental revenue during the nine months following the acquisition close.
Integration costs associated with the Prairie Sun transaction totaled $14 million and were recognized as a one-time charge.
Adjusted EBITDA, excluding one-time items, was reported at $398 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 18.6%.
Free cash flow generation for the year was robust at $241 million, enabling continued investment in growth initiatives.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, up from $0.34 per share in the prior year.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal 2023 amounted to $86 million.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $58.40, totaling approximately $70 million.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Packaged Foods had 57.1 million diluted shares outstanding.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $3.42 billion, including $310 million in cash and cash equivalents.
Accounts receivable totaled $284 million, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 48 days.
Inventory on hand was valued at $412 million, representing roughly 62 days of supply based on cost of goods sold.
The company's long-term debt balance as of year-end was $780 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes.
A $400 million tranche of these notes carries a fixed interest rate of 4.25% and matures in 2028.
The remaining $380 million in senior notes bear a coupon rate of 3.85% and are due in 2031.
Meridian Packaged Foods maintained a net leverage ratio of 1.96 times adjusted EBITDA, well within covenant thresholds.
The company's revolving credit facility of $500 million remained undrawn throughout the entirety of fiscal 2023.
Moody's affirmed the company's Baa2 credit rating during the second quarter, citing stable cash flow generation.
S&P Global Ratings similarly maintained a BBB equivalent rating, underscoring solid investment-grade standing.
Employee headcount at year-end totaled 8,740 full-time equivalents, an increase of 430 from the prior year-end figure.
The headcount increase reflected both the Prairie Sun integration and organic hiring in sales and logistics functions.
Meridian Packaged Foods invested $22 million in employee training and development programs during fiscal 2023.
The company launched three new product lines during the second half of the year, targeting health-conscious consumer segments.
These new launches collectively contributed $34 million in revenue during their initial months on the market.
Management expressed confidence that these products would reach annualized revenues of approximately $120 million by fiscal 2025.
The Heritage Grain brand expanded its distribution to 14 new international markets, particularly across Southeast Asia.
Export revenues from Southeast Asia grew 41% year-over-year, reaching $112 million in aggregate.
The company's supply chain team successfully negotiated multi-year contracts locking in favorable wheat and corn pricing.
These commodity agreements are expected to provide cost stability through at least the end of fiscal 2025.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with $18 million allocated to sustainability programs.
Meridian reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9% compared to the 2022 baseline.
The company achieved a 94% on-time delivery rate across its North American distribution network, matching the prior year's performance.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.28 billion to $2.35 billion.
Meridian Packaged Foods ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of short-term borrowings, carrying no outstanding balance on any credit line.
CEO Sandra Kowalski stated that operational efficiency gains would remain a top priority heading into the new fiscal year.
The company expects to complete a second manufacturing facility expansion in Georgia by the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
This new facility is projected to add approximately 15% to total production capacity once fully operational.
CFO Raymond Delgado emphasized that disciplined capital allocation would continue to guide the company's investment decisions.
During fiscal 2023, Meridian drew $175 million from its revolving credit facility to partially fund the Prairie Sun acquisition and working capital needs.
The annual report was certified by both the CEO and CFO in accordance with Sarbanes-Oxley Section 302 requirements.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 and has grown to serve clients across financial services, healthcare, and logistics sectors.
Veltora's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects another period of measured growth amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway emphasized the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Yarbrough presented the consolidated financial results, highlighting several key operational milestones.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $753 million.
Recurring subscription revenue accounted for 71% of total revenue, reflecting the company's ongoing shift toward a software-as-a-service model.
Professional services revenue contributed $124 million, up from $108 million in fiscal year 2022.
Hardware and legacy product sales declined to $21 million, consistent with Veltora's strategic wind-down of on-premises infrastructure offerings.
Gross profit for the year was $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.4%.
This marks a 180-basis-point improvement in gross margin compared to the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, including research and development spend of $156 million.
Sales and marketing expenses came in at $138 million, reflecting targeted investment in enterprise client acquisition.
General and administrative costs were $95 million, roughly flat compared to the $93 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $123 million, compared to $97 million in fiscal year 2022.
This represents a 26.8% year-over-year improvement in operating income.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $89 million, or $1.74 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $198 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
The company generated free cash flow of $161 million during fiscal year 2023, up from $129 million in the prior year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312 million, compared to $274 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable totaled $143 million, reflecting strong collections performance with days sales outstanding of 61 days.
Veltora's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.4 billion at December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $620 million, including long-term debt of $185 million.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio was approximately 0.41, which management considers conservative for the industry.
Veltora's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 1, 2024.
This marks the third consecutive year in which Veltora has paid a quarterly dividend, demonstrating confidence in sustained cash generation.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.60 per share.
Veltora exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of any outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Headcount grew to 4,820 employees by December 31, 2023, representing a net addition of 340 employees over the course of the year.
The engineering and product development teams saw the most significant headcount expansion, adding 210 net new roles.
Veltora launched its flagship platform upgrade, VeltroCloud 4.0, in the third quarter of 2023, receiving strong adoption from existing enterprise clients.
By year-end, approximately 68% of the company's active subscription base had migrated to VeltroCloud 4.0.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltora secured a $200 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of institutional lenders to support strategic growth initiatives.
The VeltroCloud 4.0 platform introduced native artificial intelligence capabilities, including predictive analytics and automated workflow orchestration.
Customer retention rate for the year was 94.2%, a slight improvement over the 93.7% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
New logo additions totaled 318 enterprise clients, bringing the total active customer count to approximately 2,940.
The company's net revenue retention rate was 112%, indicating meaningful expansion within the existing customer base.
Veltora's geographic revenue breakdown showed that North America accounted for 74% of total revenue, with international markets contributing the remaining 26%.
International revenue grew 18.6% year-over-year, outpacing domestic growth of 10.1%, signaling traction in European and Asia-Pacific markets.
The company opened new regional offices in Amsterdam and Singapore during the second half of 2023 to support international expansion.
Veltora's partner ecosystem grew to include over 190 certified system integrators and value-added resellers globally.
Strategic alliances with major cloud hyperscalers continued to drive co-sell pipeline activity, contributing approximately $62 million in influenced revenue.
Research and development investment remained a priority, with the $156 million spend representing 18.4% of total revenue.
Management expects this investment level to yield at least two additional platform feature releases in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the independent audit of fiscal year 2023 financial statements without material findings.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, in line with management's prior guidance range of 20% to 23%.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $218 million, reflecting strong contract bookings and healthy future revenue visibility.
Total contract value of new bookings for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.1 billion, a record for the company.
Annual recurring revenue as of December 31, 2023, was $621 million, representing growth of 17.2% year-over-year.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $940 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 24% to 25%, reflecting expected operating leverage.
Ms. Holloway concluded her remarks by stating that Veltora is well-positioned to capture growing demand for integrated cloud infrastructure solutions.
Mr. Yarbrough reiterated that prudent balance sheet management and strong cash generation will remain central tenets of the company's financial strategy.
Veltora's annual report on Form 10-K has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission and is available on the company's investor relations website.
Veltrano Systems Corporation is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in robotics integration and factory optimization software.
The company was founded in 2004 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Helbring and has grown to serve clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltrano Systems reported total revenue of $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The growth was driven primarily by strong demand in the automotive manufacturing segment, which accounts for approximately 38% of total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Ostrowski highlighted the company's disciplined cost management as a key factor in expanding operating margins during the year.
Operating income reached $134.6 million, compared with $108.2 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting a 24.4% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $97.1 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the company's capital return policy established in 2019.
Veltrano's gross profit margin expanded by 90 basis points to reach 34.7%, benefiting from favorable product mix shifts and lower raw material costs.
Research and development expenditures increased to $62.4 million in 2023, up from $54.1 million in the previous fiscal year.
The company invested heavily in its next-generation VeltraOS platform, which is expected to launch commercially in the second quarter of 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $88.5 million, focused largely on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Toledo, Ohio facility.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $131.7 million, giving management considerable flexibility for strategic investments and shareholder returns.
Veltrano ended fiscal 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $214.8 million, up from $179.3 million at the close of 2022.
The company's total headcount reached 4,820 employees globally as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 employees from the prior year-end.
Significant hiring occurred in the software engineering and artificial intelligence divisions, reflecting the company's strategic pivot toward intelligent automation solutions.
Veltrano's backlog of contracted orders stood at $392.6 million at year-end, providing strong near-term revenue visibility heading into fiscal 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Serena Caldwell noted that supply chain conditions improved meaningfully throughout 2023, reducing lead times on critical components.
The company's international revenue grew by 18.7% year-over-year, now comprising 29% of total consolidated revenue.
Veltrano expanded its European presence with the acquisition of Halberg Mechatronics GmbH in June 2023 for a purchase price of €54 million.
The Halberg acquisition contributed approximately $31.2 million in revenue during the second half of fiscal 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in March 2023.
Inventory levels across all product lines were actively managed throughout the year, with turnover ratios improving to 6.2 times from 5.4 times in 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.8%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 23.5% to 24.0%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $189.4 million for the year, representing 22.4% of revenue.
Management reiterated its commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile and conservative leverage metrics going forward.
In October 2023, Veltrano secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven banks to support ongoing operational and strategic financing needs.
Depreciation and amortization charges for the year amounted to $43.2 million, reflecting both organic asset growth and the incremental impact of the Halberg acquisition.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing $150 million buyback authorization.
As of year-end, $74.6 million remained available under the repurchase program, which the Board extended through December 2025.
Veltrano's net promoter score among enterprise clients rose to 67 in the annual customer satisfaction survey, up from 59 in the prior year.
The robotics integration segment posted revenue of $321.4 million, a 9.8% increase, driven by strong order activity from automotive and consumer electronics clients.
The factory optimization software segment generated revenue of $219.7 million, growing at an accelerated rate of 19.3% year-over-year.
Cloud-based subscription revenues within the software segment now represent 61% of that segment's total revenue, up from 47% in fiscal 2022.
Mr. Helbring stated in his letter to shareholders that the company's culture of innovation remains the foundation of its competitive differentiation.
Veltrano's three-year compound annual revenue growth rate stands at 10.1%, ahead of the peer group median of 7.4%.
Ms. Ostrowski provided initial fiscal 2024 guidance of revenue in the range of $920 million to $945 million, implying approximately 9% to 12% growth.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to be between $198 million and $210 million, assuming stable macroeconomic conditions.
The company noted that potential headwinds include foreign currency volatility, particularly euro and Malaysian ringgit exposure.
Veltrano operates two manufacturing facilities in Malaysia, which together supply roughly 22% of the company's hardware component requirements.
The Malaysia operations benefited from a new preferential trade agreement that came into effect in January 2023, reducing certain import duties.
Ms. Caldwell outlined a multi-year operational excellence program targeting $35 million in cumulative cost savings by the end of 2026.
This program focuses on process automation, procurement optimization, and consolidation of overlapping back-office functions inherited from acquisitions.
The company recognized a $4.7 million restructuring charge in the fourth quarter related to the consolidation of two regional sales offices.
Veltrano's Board of Directors comprises nine members, with six classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
The Audit Committee, chaired by Dr. Patricia Venn, oversaw the audit conducted by the external auditor Grant & Holloway LLP.
Grant & Holloway LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrano's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company maintained effective internal control over financial reporting as of year-end, with no material weaknesses identified.
Veltrano's stock price appreciated by approximately 28% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P 400 MidCap index, which returned 16.4% over the same period.
Total shareholder return, including dividends reinvested, was approximately 30.1% for the calendar year 2023.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $2.74 billion as of December 31, 2023, based on the closing share price of $96.50.
Looking further ahead, management expressed confidence in sustaining double-digit revenue growth through a combination of organic initiatives and targeted acquisitions.
Veltrano is actively evaluating several bolt-on acquisition candidates in the precision sensing and machine vision subsectors.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently, details progress toward a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.
In 2023, Veltrano reduced its total carbon emissions by approximately 7,200 metric tons compared to the 2021 baseline.
Employee voluntary turnover declined to 8.3% in fiscal 2023, down from 11.6% in 2022, reflecting improvements in compensation and development programs.
The company launched a new equity participation program for all full-time employees below the director level in the second quarter of 2023.
Veltrano's intellectual property portfolio expanded to 312 active patents as of year-end, with 47 new patents granted during fiscal 2023.
Management acknowledged that competitive pressure from Asian low-cost robotics manufacturers represents a risk to pricing power in certain product categories.
Nonetheless, Veltrano's differentiated software integration capabilities and comprehensive service agreements provide a meaningful competitive moat.
The company's average contract duration for enterprise software clients increased to 3.8 years in 2023, up from 3.1 years in the prior year, reinforcing revenue predictability.
In summary, fiscal 2023 represented a strong performance year for Veltrano Systems Corporation, with record revenue, expanding margins, and a robust strategic foundation for continued growth.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on developing and commercializing targeted therapies for rare metabolic disorders and autoimmune conditions.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Sundaram presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ohlendorf noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point in Veltara's commercial trajectory.
Total net revenue for the full year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 22% increase over the prior year's $1.20 billion.
The company's flagship product, Velorex, generated $892 million in net product sales, accounting for approximately 61% of total revenue.
Veltara entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in December 2022.
Gross margin improved to 74.3% in 2023 compared to 71.1% in 2022, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Research and development expenses totaled $318 million, representing approximately 21.6% of total net revenue for the year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose to $204 million, reflecting expanded commercial infrastructure for Velorex's market penetration.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $568 million, a 31% improvement over the $434 million recorded in the prior fiscal year.
In March 2023, the company secured a $450 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven institutional lenders to support strategic expansion.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders totaled $421 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $4.87.
The effective tax rate for the year was 23.1%, slightly below the company's previously guided range of 24% to 26%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $673 million, with total liquidity including the undrawn credit facility exceeding $1.1 billion.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during 2023 under its previously authorized $300 million buyback program.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, marking the fifth consecutive year of dividend increases.
Dr. Sundaram highlighted the regulatory approval of Veltara's second commercial product, Nexivol, granted by the FDA in August 2023.
Nexivol, indicated for a rare form of hereditary angioedema, generated $178 million in net product sales in its first partial year of commercialization.
The company expanded its commercial sales force to 620 representatives by year-end, up from 490 at the close of fiscal 2022.
International revenue grew to $397 million, representing 27% of total net revenue, with Europe accounting for the majority of ex-US sales.
Veltara received marketing authorization from the European Medicines Agency for Velorex in six additional member states during the second half of 2023.
The pipeline includes four compounds in Phase 2 clinical trials and two compounds that recently entered Phase 3 development.
VT-901, the company's lead investigational asset for type 4 hyperlipoproteinemia, reported positive interim data in October 2023.
Enrollment in the Phase 3 CLARITY trial for VT-901 is expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2024.
The company invested $62 million in capital expenditures during 2023, primarily for expansion of its manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Contract manufacturing partnerships with two external CDMOs provide additional production capacity to support anticipated demand growth.
Inventory levels at year-end were $214 million, reflecting strategic stockpiling ahead of planned label expansions for Velorex.
Accounts receivable totaled $189 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of approximately 47 days.
The company recorded a $38 million milestone payment received from its licensing partner Tanegawa Pharmaceuticals upon the Japan NDA submission of Velorex.
Total assets at year-end were $2.84 billion, while total liabilities stood at $897 million, yielding shareholders' equity of $1.94 billion.
Goodwill of $312 million on the balance sheet reflects the 2021 acquisition of Astriven Therapeutics, which contributed two early-stage pipeline assets.
Chief Operating Officer Elaine Ferreira reported that manufacturing yield improvements reduced cost of goods sold by approximately $41 million year-over-year.
The company maintained a return on invested capital of 18.7% for fiscal 2023, well above the industry median of approximately 12%.
Veltara's total headcount reached 2,840 employees by December 31, 2023, including 1,150 in research and development functions.
The company opened a new regional office in Zurich, Switzerland during the third quarter to support growing European commercial operations.
Dr. Sundaram emphasized Veltara's commitment to patient access programs, which provided Velorex at no cost to approximately 2,400 eligible patients in 2023.
Managed care coverage for Velorex improved to 89% of commercially insured lives in the United States by year-end, up from 76% at year-end 2022.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total net revenue in the range of $1.72 billion to $1.80 billion.
The midpoint of 2024 revenue guidance implies approximately 20% growth over the reported 2023 figure.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at 38% to 40%, reflecting continued operating leverage as revenue scales.
Management expects non-GAAP diluted earnings per share for 2024 in the range of $5.80 to $6.10.
The company anticipates launching Nexivol in Germany and France during the second quarter of 2024, pending pricing and reimbursement negotiations.
Veltara expects to file a supplemental New Drug Application for an expanded Velorex indication in pediatric patients by mid-2024.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is set between $75 million and $85 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity and digital infrastructure.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP completed its independent audit of Veltara's financial statements without material findings or adjustments.
Veltara's shares traded between a 52-week low of $78.40 and a high of $134.65, closing at $129.20 on December 31, 2023.
Market capitalization at year-end was approximately $11.2 billion based on 86.6 million diluted shares outstanding.
The company is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
Mr. Ohlendorf reiterated that Veltara's balance sheet positions the company to pursue both organic growth and potential bolt-on acquisitions in 2024.
Veltara's scientific advisory board, chaired by Professor Henrik Krause of Uppsala University, convened twice during 2023 to review pipeline strategy.
Environmental, social, and governance disclosures were expanded in 2023, with the company committing to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2040.
The board of directors consists of nine members, six of whom are classified as independent under Nasdaq listing standards.
Veltara remains confident in its long-term growth thesis, underpinned by durable commercial franchises, a robust clinical pipeline, and disciplined capital allocation.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, serves clients across the aerospace, defense, and heavy manufacturing sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marguerite Hollenbeck expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction, citing robust demand for its flagship line of precision robotics systems.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 14.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit margins expanded to 38.6%, up from 35.1% in fiscal year 2022, driven by favorable product mix and operational efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Randolph Esteves attributed the margin improvement to disciplined cost management and supply chain restructuring completed in early 2023.
Veltora Dynamics entered the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in December 2022.
Operating income rose to $284 million, compared to $231 million in the prior year, reflecting leverage on the company's fixed cost base.
Research and development expenditures totaled $163 million, representing approximately 11.1% of total revenues, as the company continued to invest in next-generation automation platforms.
The company's headcount grew to 9,400 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of approximately 820 employees from the prior year.
Veltora Dynamics completed the acquisition of TerraNex Systems, a specialist in computer vision hardware, for a purchase price of $215 million in March 2023.
To partially fund the TerraNex acquisition and support ongoing capital expenditure requirements, management secured a $350 million syndicated credit facility in February 2023.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $4.12.
This compares favorably to diluted earnings per share of $3.44 in fiscal year 2022, representing a year-over-year increase of approximately 19.8%.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures for the fiscal year totaled $97 million, with the majority allocated to expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Raleigh and Tucson facilities.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $412 million, providing Veltora Dynamics with substantial liquidity to pursue its growth initiatives.
The company's order backlog reached a record $2.1 billion at December 31, 2023, up 22% from the $1.72 billion reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
North American revenues accounted for 61% of total revenues, with international markets contributing the remaining 39%.
European revenues grew by 18.7% year-over-year, benefiting from increased defense spending across NATO member states.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased by 9.2%, with particularly strong performance in South Korea and Singapore.
The company's aerospace segment generated revenues of $548 million, representing a 16.9% increase from the prior year.
Defense-related revenues totaled $421 million, reflecting continued demand for Veltora's autonomous inspection and logistics systems.
The industrial automation segment, which serves heavy manufacturing clients, contributed $501 million in revenues for the fiscal year.
President of Global Operations, Darnell Kwiatkowski, noted that the Raleigh facility achieved a significant milestone, producing its ten-thousandth robotic assembly unit in September 2023.
Veltora Dynamics launched three new product lines during the fiscal year, including the VX-900 precision welder, the AeroScan Pro inspection drone, and the LogiBot MK-IV autonomous forklift system.
The VX-900 precision welder received certification from the American Welding Society within six months of its commercial launch, accelerating adoption in the heavy manufacturing segment.
The AeroScan Pro inspection drone was formally approved for use in Federal Aviation Administration-regulated environments in October 2023.
LogiBot MK-IV pre-orders exceeded internal projections by 34% in the first quarter following its announcement, signaling strong market interest.
Customer retention rates remained high at 91%, reflecting the strength of Veltora's long-term service agreements and customer success programs.
The company's net promoter score improved to 67 from 61 in the prior year, based on an annual survey of its top 500 enterprise clients.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $189 million, or approximately 12.9% of revenues, reflecting continued investment in the company's commercial organization.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below the prior year's effective rate of 22.1%, due in part to R&D tax credits.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year was $74 million, including $18 million of amortization related to the TerraNex acquisition intangibles.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $358 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.4%.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $247 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $344 million less capital expenditures of $97 million.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the fiscal year at an average price of $86.40 per share, for a total cost of approximately $103.7 million.
Shares outstanding at December 31, 2023 totaled 48.1 million on a diluted basis, down from 49.3 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 17.8%, up from 15.3% in the prior year, reflecting improved asset utilization.
Veltora Dynamics' balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $2.38 billion and total stockholders' equity of $1.14 billion at year-end.
Inventories at December 31, 2023 were $318 million, up from $271 million at the prior year-end, reflecting increased production volumes and strategic raw material stockpiling.
Accounts receivable totaled $224 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 55 days, consistent with the prior year.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4, indicating a solid near-term liquidity position relative to current obligations.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.62 billion to $1.68 billion, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 10% to 14%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to fall in the range of $390 million to $415 million, reflecting continued operational leverage.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $110 million to $125 million, reflecting planned expansions at the Tucson facility.

Chief Executive Officer Marguerite Hollenbeck reiterated that the company's strategic priorities for 2024 include expanding its international footprint, particularly in the Middle East and Southeast Asia.
Veltora Dynamics announced a strategic partnership with Fujimoto Industrial Group, a Japanese conglomerate, to co-develop next-generation collaborative robotics systems.
The partnership is expected to contribute incremental revenues beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
The company's cybersecurity infrastructure received an updated assessment from an independent third party, confirming compliance with NIST Cybersecurity Framework standards.
Veltora Dynamics' sustainability initiatives progressed meaningfully during fiscal year 2023, with a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its owned and operated facilities by 2035, consistent with its published Climate Action Plan.
Veltora Dynamics' Board of Directors includes nine members, five of whom are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
The Audit Committee, chaired by Dr. Felicia Ostrowski, conducted four formal meetings during fiscal year 2023 and oversaw the company's transition to a new enterprise resource planning system.
The company's independent auditor, Ashworth & Craine LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year, consistent with findings from the prior two audit cycles.
Veltora Dynamics also renewed its Directors and Officers liability insurance policy, increasing coverage limits to $75 million from $60 million in the prior year.

The company faces competitive pressure from several global automation conglomerates, including those based in Germany, Japan, and South Korea, but management believes its technology differentiation provides a durable competitive moat.
Management notes that ongoing supply chain volatility and elevated semiconductor costs represent the most significant near-term operational risks.
The company has entered into long-term supply agreements with three semiconductor vendors to mitigate component availability risk through the end of fiscal year 2025.
Veltora Dynamics anticipates continued strong demand from the defense sector, supported by multi-year government contracts with a combined ceiling value of approximately $680 million.
Overall, the board of directors and executive leadership team expressed strong confidence in the company's ability to deliver sustained, profitable growth for shareholders in the years ahead.
This annual report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reflects all material events occurring through the filing date of February 28, 2024.
Verantis Corporation, a mid-sized specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Dr. Markus Fleischer, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Petra Holst noted that the revenue growth was driven primarily by strong performance in the company's industrial coatings and adhesives divisions.
The industrial coatings segment alone generated €720 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 39% of total group revenues.
Meanwhile, the adhesives division contributed €540 million, reflecting an 11% year-over-year increase fueled by demand from the automotive sector.
Verantis also operates a performance polymers segment, which delivered €380 million in revenue, up 4% from the prior year.
The remaining €200 million in revenue was attributable to specialty solvents and other fine chemical products.
Gross profit for the full year reached €612 million, yielding a gross margin of 33.3%, compared to 31.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Dr. Fleischer attributed the margin expansion primarily to operational efficiencies achieved through the company's two-year production optimization initiative.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €298 million, a 3.1% increase from €289 million in the prior year.
Research and development expenditures accounted for €87 million of operating expenses, reflecting the company's continued investment in next-generation polymer chemistry.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled €211 million, reflecting disciplined cost management despite inflationary pressures across European markets.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €398 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 21.6%.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €201 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.47 on a diluted basis.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.20 per share, to be paid in March 2024, representing a 9% increase from the prior year's dividend of €1.10 per share.
As of December 31, 2023, Verantis carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in September of that year.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €134 million, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at the company's facilities in Cologne and Antwerp.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €264 million, up from €218 million in fiscal year 2022, supported by strong operating performance and disciplined working capital management.
The company's cash and cash equivalents stood at €318 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs and strategic investments.
Inventory levels remained well-controlled, ending the year at €162 million, consistent with the prior year's €159 million and reflecting stable supply chain conditions.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Verantis secured a €350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of European banks to support its planned expansion into Asian markets.
The company's total asset base grew to €2.71 billion, up from €2.53 billion in the prior year, driven largely by capital investment and favorable currency translation effects.
Verantis reduced its net working capital cycle from 48 days to 43 days, an improvement management attributed to enhanced receivables collection processes.

Chief Operating Officer Ingrid Bauer highlighted that the company successfully launched three new polymer products in the second half of the year, each targeting the construction and infrastructure sectors.
These new product lines are expected to contribute approximately €60 million in incremental annual revenues once they reach full commercial scale in 2025.
The company's employee headcount stood at 8,240 as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 employees compared to the prior year end.
Headcount growth was concentrated in the research and development and manufacturing functions, reflecting the company's strategic priorities.
Verantis maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year, with a stable outlook.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 12.4%, up from 10.9% in fiscal year 2022, underscoring the effectiveness of recent capital allocation decisions.
Pension obligations totaled €143 million at year-end, a decrease of €11 million from the prior year due to higher discount rates applied under IAS 19.
Environmental, social, and governance performance continued to improve, with total carbon emissions per unit of production declining by 6.3% year-over-year.
Verantis invested €22 million in sustainability-related initiatives during the year, including upgrades to waste treatment facilities and renewable energy procurement.
The company's largest geographic market remained Western Europe, accounting for 58% of total revenues, followed by North America at 24% and Asia-Pacific at 12%.
Dr. Fleischer emphasized that the Asia-Pacific region represents the company's most significant growth opportunity over the next five years.
Verantis entered into a joint venture agreement with South Korean specialty chemicals firm Hanseung Materials Co. in November 2023, with operations expected to commence in mid-2024.
The joint venture is structured as a 51/49 ownership arrangement, with Verantis holding the majority stake and contributing proprietary polymer technology.
Management guided for fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.02 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 6% to 10%.
The 2024 EBITDA margin is expected to be maintained in the range of 21% to 22%, consistent with the company's medium-term profitability targets.
Ms. Holst noted that the company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately €150 million to €160 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily for Asian capacity build-out.
Verantis does not plan to pursue any material debt-financed acquisitions in 2024, preferring instead to grow organically and through its recently established joint ventures.
The company's share repurchase program, authorized in June 2023, remains active with approximately €48 million of the original €100 million authorization remaining as of year-end.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 1.02 million shares at an average price of €51.10, returning approximately €52 million to shareholders.
The audit committee confirmed that Verantis's internal controls over financial reporting were effective as of December 31, 2023, with no material weaknesses identified.
External auditors Ernst & Wulf GmbH issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Verantis's supervisory board approved the remuneration report, which disclosed total compensation of €4.7 million for the executive management team during fiscal year 2023.
Dr. Fleischer's total compensation was €1.6 million, comprising a base salary of €900,000, a short-term incentive of €480,000, and long-term equity-linked awards valued at €220,000.
Ms. Holst received total compensation of €1.1 million, and Ms. Bauer received total compensation of €1.0 million during the same period.
The company's segment reporting reflects three operating segments: industrial coatings, adhesives, and performance polymers, each operating as a separate profit center.
Intersegment transactions are eliminated at the group consolidation level and were immaterial relative to external revenues in fiscal year 2023.
Deferred tax assets and liabilities netted to a liability position of €34 million, primarily related to temporary differences in depreciation schedules across jurisdictions.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.6%, slightly above the statutory German corporate tax rate due to non-deductible expenses and foreign withholding taxes.
Verantis's goodwill balance of €287 million remained unchanged from the prior year, as no acquisitions or impairments occurred during fiscal year 2023.
Management conducted its annual goodwill impairment test as required under IAS 36, and determined that no impairment was warranted given strong recoverable amounts across all cash-generating units.
The company's contingent liabilities include €18 million in environmental remediation provisions related to legacy operations at a former production site in Belgium.
Looking ahead, Verantis remains confident in its strategic direction and expects to deliver sustainable long-term value for shareholders, employees, and other stakeholders.

The full consolidated financial statements, including detailed notes, are available on the company's investor relations website and will be presented at the Annual General Meeting scheduled for April 2024.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical devices and laboratory analytics company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company designs, manufactures, and distributes advanced point-of-care testing equipment for hospitals, clinics, and independent laboratories across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Pamela Forsythe presented the annual results to shareholders during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Eigenfeld provided a detailed breakdown of the company's financial performance for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Veltara Diagnostics reported total revenue of $842 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the period reached $391 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.4 percent.
Operating income came in at $127 million, up from $98 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting strong cost discipline across all business units.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $94 million, or $2.17 per diluted share.
The company's flagship product line, the VeltaScreen 900 Series, contributed $338 million in revenue, accounting for roughly 40 percent of total sales.
International revenues grew by 18 percent year-over-year, driven primarily by expanded distribution agreements in Germany, France, and the Netherlands.
North American revenues increased by 9.6 percent, supported by renewed multi-year contracts with three major hospital networks.
Research and development expenditure for the year totaled $76 million, representing approximately 9 percent of total revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose to $188 million, up 7 percent from the prior year, primarily due to headcount additions in the sales organization.
Veltara Diagnostics ended fiscal year 2023 with a total workforce of 4,320 employees, compared to 3,950 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company opened two new manufacturing facilities during the year, one in Dublin, Ireland, and another in Raleigh, North Carolina.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $58 million, the majority of which was allocated to the new manufacturing footprint.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $41 million, consistent with the company's elevated fixed-asset base following recent expansions.
Free cash flow for the year was $131 million, reflecting strong cash conversion from operating activities.
Veltara Diagnostics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final outstanding bond obligation in March of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $214 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
This dividend represents an 8 percent increase over the prior quarterly dividend of $0.167 per share.
Management also authorized a share repurchase program of up to $100 million over the next 18 months.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara Diagnostics secured a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five lending institutions to support its accelerating expansion strategy.
Inventory levels at year-end were $112 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of higher demand in the first half of 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled $143 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 62 days.
The company recorded a one-time restructuring charge of $11 million related to the consolidation of its legacy diagnostic reagent operations.
Excluding this charge, adjusted operating income would have been $138 million, and adjusted operating margin would have been 16.4 percent.
Veltara Diagnostics launched three new products during fiscal year 2023, including the VeltaPoint Ultra rapid immunoassay platform.
The VeltaPoint Ultra achieved initial commercial shipments in October 2023 and generated $14 million in revenue during its partial-year commercialization period.
The regulatory affairs team successfully obtained CE Mark certification for two additional products intended for the European market.
Dr. Forsythe highlighted the company's strategic partnership with BioSync Genomics as a key driver of future product development.
Under the partnership agreement, Veltara Diagnostics will co-develop a next-generation molecular diagnostics panel anticipated to launch in late 2025.
The company's market share in the U.S. point-of-care cardiac marker testing segment increased to 22 percent in 2023, up from 19 percent in 2022.
In Europe, Veltara Diagnostics captured an estimated 11 percent share of the rapid infectious disease testing market.
Eigenfeld noted that the company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3 percent, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.1 percent.
The lower effective tax rate was attributable to enhanced utilization of R&D tax credits in both the United States and Ireland.
Pension and other post-employment benefit obligations totaled $29 million at year-end, a modest decrease from $33 million in the prior year.
The company's return on invested capital for the year was 14.8 percent, which management characterized as comfortably above the firm's weighted-average cost of capital.
Veltara Diagnostics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's throughout the fiscal year.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the annual audit conducted by Thornton & Wale LLP.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share for 2024 are expected to fall between $2.45 and $2.60.
Management anticipates gross margin expansion of approximately 50 to 80 basis points in fiscal year 2024, driven by manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 was set at $65 million to $70 million, reflecting continued investment in production capacity and digital infrastructure.
The company expects free cash flow conversion to remain above 85 percent of net income in fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Forsythe emphasized that organic growth remains the primary value-creation lever for Veltara Diagnostics in the near term.
However, she did not rule out bolt-on acquisitions in the molecular diagnostics and companion diagnostics sub-sectors should compelling opportunities emerge.
Veltara Diagnostics has identified a pipeline of at least seven potential acquisition targets, with enterprise values ranging from $50 million to $300 million.
The company maintains a disciplined approach to capital allocation, prioritizing investments that generate returns exceeding 15 percent on invested capital within three years.
Eigenfeld reiterated that the company's balance sheet remains a core strategic asset, affording flexibility for both organic investment and potential M&A activity.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $638 million, up from $557 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in stockholders' equity reflects strong net income partially offset by dividends paid and share repurchases executed under the prior authorization.
Veltara Diagnostics' stock price appreciated approximately 27 percent over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P MidCap 400 Health Care index by roughly 14 percentage points.
The company's total shareholder return, including dividends reinvested, was approximately 29 percent for the fiscal year.
Management expressed confidence in the durability of demand trends in point-of-care diagnostics, citing aging demographics and decentralization of healthcare delivery as structural tailwinds.
Veltara Diagnostics' annual report on Form 10-K, including full audited financial statements and footnotes, was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
The report is available on the company's investor relations website and through the SEC's EDGAR filing system.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation is a mid-sized industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in precision robotics and smart manufacturing solutions.
The company was founded in 2004 by CEO Margaret Hollis and has grown steadily over two decades into a recognized name in the North American manufacturing sector.
Veltara Dynamics reported total revenues of $847.3 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the year reached $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9%, an improvement of 180 basis points compared to fiscal 2022.
Operating income came in at $94.1 million, reflecting disciplined cost management and operational leverage across the company's three primary business segments.
The Precision Robotics segment was the largest contributor to revenue, generating $421.7 million, or roughly 49.8% of total company revenue.
The Smart Conveyor Systems segment contributed $268.4 million in revenues, while the Industrial Software Solutions segment added $157.2 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $68.9 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.14 for the full fiscal year.
This compares favorably to net income of $54.3 million and diluted EPS of $1.69 reported in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Castellano attributed the improved profitability to a combination of pricing power, supply chain normalization, and favorable product mix.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $61.2 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's automated assembly facility in Dayton, Ohio.
Research and development expenses rose to $52.8 million, representing 6.2% of total revenues and underscoring Veltara's commitment to innovation.
The company employed 4,312 full-time workers as of December 31, 2023, a net addition of 287 employees compared to the prior year-end headcount.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $165.7 million, or 19.6% of revenues, slightly elevated due to increased marketing investments in Europe.
Veltara's European expansion has been a strategic priority, with the company opening a regional sales office in Frankfurt, Germany, during the second quarter of 2023.
International revenues grew 21.3% year-over-year, reaching $198.6 million and representing approximately 23.5% of total company revenue.
The company's balance sheet remained healthy, with total assets of $1.14 billion and shareholders' equity of $543.2 million as of year-end.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $134.7 million at December 31, 2023, up from $98.3 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable totaled $142.8 million, with days sales outstanding of 61 days, consistent with industry norms for the sector.
Inventory levels were $189.4 million, reflecting strategic stockpiling of key electronic components to buffer against potential supply chain disruptions.
Total current liabilities were $198.6 million, and the current ratio was 1.87, indicating solid near-term liquidity.
The company's long-term debt stood at $221.5 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes issued in 2020 and maturing in 2027.
Net debt, defined as total financial debt less cash and equivalents, was approximately $86.8 million at fiscal year-end.
Free cash flow for the year was $112.4 million, computed as operating cash flow of $173.6 million minus capital expenditures of $61.2 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized dividend yield of approximately 1.3% based on the average share price.
Veltara repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its standing buyback authorization, at an average price of $31.40 per share.
The share repurchase program has returned $37.7 million to shareholders during the year, complementing the $23.2 million distributed in dividends.
Order backlog at year-end reached a record $634.8 million, driven by strong demand in the automotive and semiconductor equipment manufacturing verticals.
This backlog represents approximately 9.1 months of trailing revenue coverage, giving management confidence in near-term revenue visibility.
CEO Margaret Hollis commented that 2023 was a transformational year that positioned the company for sustained double-digit growth.
During the year, Veltara launched its flagship VX-9000 robotic arm series, which targets high-throughput assembly environments requiring sub-millimeter precision.
The VX-9000 series achieved first-year bookings of $78.3 million, exceeding internal targets by approximately 18%.
The Industrial Software Solutions segment introduced the Veltara Nexus platform, a cloud-native MES software suite integrated with the company's hardware ecosystem.
Nexus platform recurring revenue reached $31.4 million in its first year, establishing a growing annuity-like revenue stream for the segment.
Customer retention rates for software products improved to 93.7%, up from 89.2% in 2022, reflecting strong customer satisfaction scores.
Veltara's North American market share in the precision robotics category was estimated at 8.4% at year-end, up from 7.6% the prior year.
The company faced headwinds from elevated steel and aluminum prices during the first half of the year, which pressured margins in the Smart Conveyor Systems segment.
However, commodity costs moderated in the second half, and the segment's operating margin recovered to 11.2% by the fourth quarter.
Chief Operating Officer Daniel Yee oversaw the implementation of a lean manufacturing initiative that reduced waste by 14% across domestic production facilities.
The lean initiative contributed an estimated $8.9 million in annualized cost savings, partially offsetting the wage inflation experienced throughout the year.
Average hourly wages across Veltara's manufacturing workforce increased by 5.8% in 2023, consistent with broader labor market trends in the industrial sector.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, reflecting benefits from the research and development tax credit and a favorable geographic mix of earnings.
Deferred tax liabilities totaled $34.6 million, primarily related to accelerated depreciation on manufacturing equipment.
Veltara carries no goodwill impairment charges in the current year, as all three reporting segments passed their annual impairment tests with adequate headroom.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitfield LLP as external auditors, who issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced materially during the year, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions declining 9.3% from 2022 levels.
The company set a target to achieve carbon neutrality across its owned operations by 2035, supported by ongoing investments in renewable energy procurement.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of revolving credit facility borrowings, with zero outstanding balance on its $300 million revolving credit agreement.
Looking ahead, management expects fiscal 2024 revenues to fall in the range of $930 million to $960 million, representing growth of approximately 10% to 13%.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 is set at 11.5% to 12.5%, reflecting investment in headcount and technology infrastructure to support growth.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of $70 million to $80 million in fiscal 2024, primarily for capacity expansion at the Dayton facility and a new plant in Raleigh.
Management also expects research and development spending to increase to approximately $60 million in fiscal 2024 as the company accelerates its next-generation robotics roadmap.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara drew down $75 million from its revolving credit facility to pre-fund tooling and component purchases for anticipated 2024 demand.
The Precision Robotics segment is expected to remain the primary growth engine, with projected revenues in excess of $475 million in fiscal 2024.
Veltara's management team is evaluating several bolt-on acquisition targets in the industrial software space with enterprise values ranging from $50 million to $200 million.
