Any acquisition would be subject to customary due diligence and would be funded through a combination of cash on hand and available credit capacity.
The company's stock closed the fiscal year at $33.82 per share, representing a total return of 24.6% for shareholders over the twelve-month period.
By comparison, the Russell 2000 Industrial index returned approximately 16.1% over the same period, meaning Veltara outperformed the benchmark by roughly 850 basis points.
Veltara's investor relations team hosted four quarterly earnings calls and two investor day events during 2023, maintaining active engagement with the investment community.
The company's next annual shareholder meeting is scheduled for April 17, 2024, at which time the election of three board directors and approval of the executive compensation plan will be considered.
General Counsel Patricia Okafor confirmed that there are no material pending legal proceedings that would be expected to have a significant adverse effect on the company's financial position.
This financial report excerpt has been prepared in accordance with United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reflects management's best estimates as of the filing date.
Forward-looking statements contained herein are subject to risks and uncertainties, including macroeconomic conditions, supply chain volatility, and competitive dynamics in the automation industry.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation remains committed to delivering long-term value for shareholders, customers, and employees as it executes its strategic plan through 2026.
Meridian Coatings & Specialty Polymers Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the specialty chemicals sector, focusing on industrial-grade protective coatings and polymer-based adhesive solutions.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Veltri opened the report with a statement acknowledging the transformative year the company experienced.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Harshaw provided detailed commentary on the company's financial position and outlook for 2024.
Meridian Coatings & Specialty Polymers reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for the full fiscal year 2023.
This represents a 9.3% increase over the $1.68 billion in revenues recorded during fiscal year 2022.
The growth was primarily driven by strong demand in the aerospace and automotive refinishing end markets.
Operating income for the period came in at $231.7 million, reflecting an operating margin of approximately 12.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $174.2 million, or $3.89 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to the prior year's net income of $148.5 million, or $3.31 per diluted share.
The company's gross profit margin expanded by 80 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.4% in fiscal 2023.
Management attributed this margin improvement largely to raw material cost normalization and improved manufacturing efficiency.
Research and development expenditures totaled $67.3 million, representing 3.7% of total revenues.
The increase in R&D spending reflects the company's strategic commitment to next-generation bio-based polymer formulations.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $183.4 million, roughly flat compared to $181.2 million in 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $312.6 million, an increase of 11.4% from $280.6 million in the prior year.
The adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.0% represents the highest level achieved by the company in the past eight fiscal years.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $89.5 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Greenfield, Ohio manufacturing facility.
The Greenfield expansion project is expected to add approximately 180,000 square feet of production capacity upon completion in mid-2024.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was $198.4 million, a significant improvement from $161.0 million in 2022.
The company returned $52.1 million to shareholders through quarterly cash dividends during the fiscal year.
An additional $40.0 million was deployed in share repurchases under the board-authorized buyback program.
Total shares repurchased under the program since its inception in 2021 now stand at 3.2 million shares.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflected total assets of $2.13 billion.
Meridian Coatings & Specialty Polymers ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying no outstanding borrowings on its books.
Working capital at year-end was $387.6 million, supported by strong receivables collection and disciplined inventory management.
Days sales outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced credit and collections processes.
Inventory turnover improved to 5.8 times in 2023, compared to 5.1 times in 2022, signaling better supply chain coordination.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4 as of December 31, 2023, well above the industry median of 1.7.
In November 2023, the company finalized a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to support its ongoing capital structure initiatives.
CEO Sandra Veltri emphasized that the company's diversified geographic footprint was a key competitive advantage throughout the year.
Revenues from North American operations totaled $1.09 billion, accounting for 59.2% of consolidated revenues.
European operations contributed $452.0 million, or approximately 24.6% of total revenues, despite modest macroeconomic headwinds in the region.
Asia-Pacific revenues were $298.0 million, representing 16.2% of consolidated revenues, with particularly strong growth in South Korea and Vietnam.
The company expanded its Asia-Pacific sales force by 35 representatives during the fiscal year to support regional growth ambitions.
In March 2023, Meridian completed the acquisition of Helix Polymer Systems, a specialty resin producer based in Stuttgart, Germany.
The Helix acquisition was funded entirely through operating cash flows and added approximately $73 million in annualized revenues.
Integration of Helix has progressed ahead of schedule, with anticipated cost synergies of $12 million expected to be fully realized by 2025.
The company also divested its non-core architectural coatings business unit in August 2023 for proceeds of $44.5 million.
CFO Dominic Harshaw noted that the divestiture sharpened the company's strategic focus on high-margin industrial and specialty applications.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced significantly during 2023, with the company achieving a 14% reduction in Scope 1 emissions.
Water usage intensity at manufacturing facilities declined 8% year-over-year, contributing to the company's 2030 sustainability targets.
The company was recognized by Chemical Week as one of the top fifteen most sustainable specialty chemical firms in North America.
Employee headcount at December 31, 2023, was 6,847 full-time equivalents, compared to 6,412 at the end of 2022.
The increase in workforce reflects both organic hiring and the addition of 312 employees from the Helix acquisition.
Employee voluntary turnover declined to 8.2% in 2023, from 11.4% in 2022, an achievement management credited to enhanced compensation packages.
The company launched its new ThermoShield Pro product line in the second quarter of 2023, targeting the energy infrastructure market.
ThermoShield Pro generated approximately $28.4 million in revenues in its first two quarters of commercial availability.
Management expects ThermoShield Pro to generate $70 million to $85 million in revenues during fiscal year 2024.
The company filed seventeen new patents during fiscal 2023, bringing its active patent portfolio to 214 patents globally.
Meridian's largest customer, a global automotive OEM, accounted for 8.3% of total revenues, consistent with prior-year levels.
No single customer represented more than 10% of total revenues, underscoring the company's diversified customer base.
The company maintained investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P throughout fiscal 2023.
Moody's assigned a Baa2 rating, while S&P maintained its BBB rating, each with a stable outlook.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.29 per share payable in February 2024.
This represents a 7.4% increase over the prior quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share, signaling board confidence in future earnings.
Management issued fiscal 2024 guidance of total revenues between $1.95 billion and $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set in the range of $335 million to $355 million.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is $95 million to $110 million, reflecting the final phase of the Greenfield expansion.
The company expects to generate free cash flow of approximately $200 million to $220 million in fiscal year 2024.
Meridian Coatings & Specialty Polymers reaffirmed its long-term financial target of achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin above 20% by fiscal year 2027.
The board and management team expressed gratitude to employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and confidence in the company.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected herein.
The financial statements included in this report have been audited by Ernst & Young LLP and are presented in accordance with U.S. GAAP.
Shareholders are encouraged to review the full Form 10-K filing submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission for complete disclosures.
Veltrano Diagnostics Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, highlighting a period of significant operational transformation.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializes in the development and commercialization of point-of-care diagnostic devices for respiratory and metabolic conditions.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Pamela Hurst opened the report with a statement emphasizing Veltrano's commitment to expanding its clinical footprint across North American and European markets.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2 percent increase compared to the $426.7 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue, which accounts for the sale of diagnostic cartridges and instrument platforms, grew by 18.6 percent year-over-year to $361.9 million.
Service and subscription revenue, generated through maintenance contracts and cloud-based analytics, contributed $125.4 million to the top line.
Gross profit for the year was $214.8 million, yielding a gross margin of 44.1 percent, compared to 41.7 percent in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix attributed the margin improvement to supply chain optimization initiatives that reduced per-unit manufacturing costs by approximately 9 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $178.6 million, including research and development expenditures of $72.4 million, representing 14.9 percent of total revenue.
Sales and marketing expenses increased to $63.7 million as the company expanded its direct sales force by 140 representatives in the United States and Canada.
General and administrative expenses were $42.5 million, reflecting investments in enterprise resource planning system upgrades completed in the second quarter.
Operating income came in at $36.2 million, a meaningful improvement from the $11.4 million operating income recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $27.8 million, or $0.74 per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $4.2 million in the prior year.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization stood at $58.9 million for the full year.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $193.7 million, up from $144.2 million at the close of 2022.
Accounts receivable increased to $89.4 million, reflecting the strong fourth-quarter revenue surge and extended payment terms offered to several large hospital networks.
Inventory levels were held at $54.1 million, consistent with management's lean inventory strategy implemented at the start of the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $31.6 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Durham, North Carolina facility.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in October 2023, of which $18.3 million had been utilized by year-end.
Veltrano Diagnostics serves more than 3,400 hospital and clinic customers globally, with particularly strong penetration in the Midwest and Southeast United States.
International revenue grew to $96.8 million, representing 19.9 percent of total revenue, driven by new distribution agreements signed in Germany and the Netherlands.
The company launched two new diagnostic assay platforms during the year: the VeltraPath 7 respiratory panel and the MetaScreen Pro metabolic assessment suite.
VeltraPath 7 received FDA 510(k) clearance in March 2023 and began commercial shipments to U.S. customers in May 2023.
MetaScreen Pro received CE marking in July 2023 and was introduced to select European hospital partners in September of the same year.
By the end of fiscal 2023, the combined installed base of VeltraPath 7 and MetaScreen Pro instruments totaled 1,847 units across 29 countries.
Veltrano Diagnostics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting the company's sustained focus on maintaining a conservative capital structure.
The workforce grew by 312 employees during fiscal 2023, bringing the total global headcount to 2,614 full-time equivalents at year-end.
Employee retention rates improved to 88.4 percent, compared to 81.9 percent in 2022, a development Dr. Hurst credited to enhanced compensation packages and hybrid work policies.
The company's net promoter score among institutional clients rose to 67, up from 54 in the prior year, indicating strengthened customer satisfaction.
Veltrano's R&D pipeline includes three next-generation assay platforms currently in clinical validation, expected to reach regulatory submission in 2024 and 2025.
In December 2023, the company secured a $250 million senior secured term loan from a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund strategic acquisitions and accelerate global expansion.
The acquisition of TheraDiag Solutions, a European in-vitro diagnostics firm, was announced in January 2024 and is expected to close in the second quarter of 2024.
TheraDiag Solutions generated approximately €84 million in revenue during calendar year 2023, expanding Veltrano's addressable market in oncology diagnostics.
The preliminary purchase price for TheraDiag Solutions is estimated at €310 million, subject to customary working capital adjustments at closing.
Dr. Hurst stated that the TheraDiag acquisition aligns with Veltrano's long-term strategy of building a comprehensive, multi-disease diagnostic ecosystem.
Following the acquisition announcement, Veltrano's stock price appreciated by 11.4 percent over the subsequent five trading days, closing at $43.67 per share.
The company's market capitalization stood at approximately $1.64 billion as of the last trading day of fiscal 2023.
Veltrano received recognition from the North Carolina Business Journal as one of the top ten fastest-growing life science companies in the state for 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were expanded in 2023, including a commitment to achieve carbon neutrality across all manufacturing operations by 2030.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 7.3 percent compared to the 2022 baseline, ahead of its internal interim targets.
Veltrano's audit committee engaged Ernst & Young LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the third consecutive year.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Management's discussion and analysis section noted that foreign currency translation had a $6.2 million unfavorable impact on international revenue during the year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.7 percent, lower than the 26.1 percent rate in 2022, due to the recognition of deferred tax assets related to R&D credits.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet totaled $38.6 million, representing the portion of multi-year service contracts not yet recognized as earned revenue.
The company maintained a current ratio of 3.4 and a quick ratio of 2.7, both well above industry benchmarks for medical device companies of comparable size.
Long-term investments in equity securities held at fair value were $22.1 million as of year-end, reflecting minority stakes in two early-stage diagnostics startups.
Veltrano's board of directors consists of nine members, including four independent directors with backgrounds in healthcare, finance, and regulatory affairs.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Ferreira outlined a 2024 operational roadmap focused on manufacturing efficiency, field service excellence, and accelerated regulatory submissions.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 projects total revenue in the range of $570 million to $595 million, implying growth of 17 to 22 percent over fiscal 2023.
The company expects 2024 gross margins to improve modestly to the 45 to 46 percent range as product mix shifts toward higher-margin software and analytics offerings.
Veltrano Diagnostics concluded the fiscal year 2023 report by reaffirming its mission to deliver accurate, rapid, and accessible diagnostic solutions that improve patient outcomes worldwide.
Meridian Tactile Systems, Inc. reported its fiscal year 2023 annual results to shareholders on March 14, 2024.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, designs and manufactures advanced haptic feedback modules for consumer electronics and industrial automation.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Kowalczyk opened the earnings call by describing fiscal 2023 as a transformative year for the organization.
Meridian Tactile Systems operates across three primary business segments: Consumer Haptics, Industrial Actuation, and Licensing & IP.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Consumer Haptics remained the largest segment, contributing $512.6 million, or approximately 60.5% of total revenue.
Industrial Actuation generated $218.9 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from automotive and robotics customers.
The Licensing & IP segment contributed $115.8 million, driven by new royalty agreements signed with three major Asian electronics manufacturers.
Gross profit for the full year was $331.2 million, yielding a gross margin of 39.1%, up 80 basis points from the prior year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198.7 million, inclusive of research and development and selling, general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending was $94.3 million, representing 11.1% of total revenue, reflecting the company's ongoing commitment to innovation.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that operating income improved to $132.5 million, an increase of 18.2% year over year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $98.4 million for the fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share were $2.17, compared to $1.83 in fiscal year 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.3%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 23.5%.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $162.8 million, a margin of 19.2% on a consolidated basis.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $119.6 million, compared to $87.2 million in the prior fiscal year.
Capital expenditures totaled $43.1 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Cedar Park facility.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $204.7 million on its balance sheet.
Accounts receivable stood at $131.4 million, with days sales outstanding of 56.6 days, broadly in line with historical norms.
Inventory levels at year-end were $89.2 million, representing approximately 73 days of inventory on hand.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet were $1.12 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Meridian Tactile Systems carried long-term debt of $275.0 million as of the fiscal year-end balance sheet date.
The company's leverage ratio, defined as net debt to adjusted EBITDA, stood at 0.43 times at year-end.
During fiscal 2023, the Board of Directors approved and paid four quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.64 per share.
The company repurchased 1.8 million shares during the fiscal year under its existing $150 million share buyback authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $62.4 million remained available under the share repurchase program.
Sandra Kowalczyk highlighted the successful commercial launch of the VibraCore X7 platform as a key operational milestone.
The VibraCore X7 was adopted by two leading North American smartphone original equipment manufacturers in the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
Industrial Actuation segment margins expanded by 110 basis points due to favorable product mix and improved manufacturing yields.
The company added 347 full-time employees during the fiscal year, bringing its global headcount to 4,218 as of December 31, 2023.
Approximately 62% of the workforce is based in North America, with the remainder distributed across Europe and Asia-Pacific.
Raymond Osei confirmed that the company achieved its previously communicated cost reduction target of $18 million in annualized savings.
These savings were derived primarily from supply chain consolidation and renegotiated vendor contracts across the organization.
The Licensing & IP segment saw its portfolio grow to 1,847 active patents globally, up from 1,612 in the prior year.
Management noted that geographic diversification continued to be a strategic priority, with international revenue comprising 43.7% of total sales.
The Asia-Pacific region recorded the fastest growth, with revenue increasing 21.6% year over year to $184.3 million.
Throughout fiscal year 2023, Meridian Tactile Systems maintained a completely debt-free capital structure with no outstanding borrowings of any kind.
The company's supply chain resilience was tested in the third quarter when a key substrate supplier experienced a three-week production outage.
Management responded by activating secondary suppliers, limiting the revenue impact to an estimated $6.2 million in deferred shipments.
The deferred shipments were substantially recognized in the fourth quarter, contributing to the strong Q4 revenue performance of $231.7 million.
Fourth quarter gross margin was 40.3%, the highest of any quarter in fiscal 2023, reflecting favorable leverage on fixed manufacturing costs.
To fund accelerated expansion into the European industrial market, the company secured a €240 million syndicated term loan facility in October 2023.
The European expansion plan targets three new customer accounts in Germany and the Netherlands by the end of fiscal 2024.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance with total revenue expected in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
This guidance implies year-over-year growth of approximately 11% to 15% on a reported currency basis.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2024 is expected to expand modestly to a range of 19.5% to 20.5%.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $55 million to $65 million to support capacity additions.
Sandra Kowalczyk expressed confidence that the VibraCore X9 platform, slated for mid-2024 launch, would accelerate Consumer Haptics segment growth.
The VibraCore X9 incorporates a next-generation piezoelectric actuator array with 40% lower power consumption than its predecessor.
Raymond Osei outlined a disciplined capital allocation framework prioritizing organic investment, selective M&A, and return of capital to shareholders.
The company is evaluating two bolt-on acquisition targets in the precision sensing space, each valued below $100 million.
Management emphasized that any acquisition would need to meet a minimum threshold of 15% internal rate of return.
Meridian Tactile Systems' credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by one major ratings agency during the fiscal year.
The company's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.7% for purposes of internal investment evaluation.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially during fiscal 2023, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions declining 9% year over year.
The company committed to achieving net-zero emissions across its own operations by the year 2040.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the annual internal survey, improved to 78% favorable, up four percentage points from fiscal 2022.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP would continue as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2024.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls were identified during the fiscal 2023 audit.
The Board of Directors consists of nine members, seven of whom are considered independent under applicable stock exchange listing standards.
Sandra Kowalczyk concluded her remarks by reaffirming that Meridian Tactile Systems is well-positioned to deliver long-term shareholder value through disciplined execution.
The company plans to host its annual investor day on June 12, 2024, at which it will provide a comprehensive multi-year strategic outlook.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Investors are encouraged to review the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for a complete discussion of risk factors.
Meridian Harvest Corporation, a leading provider of specialty agricultural inputs and crop protection solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa, operates across North America, Latin America, and select European markets.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Wollenberg expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong demand across all major product categories.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Crop protection product sales led the growth, contributing $712 million to total revenue, up from $621 million in 2022.
The specialty fertilizer segment posted revenues of $498 million, reflecting a 9.1% year-over-year improvement.
Seed treatment solutions generated $380 million in revenue, driven by expanded distribution partnerships in the Midwest.
The remaining $250 million in revenue came from precision agriculture services and consulting contracts.
Gross profit for the year was $641 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 34.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, inclusive of selling, general, and administrative costs as well as research and development outlays.
Research and development expenditures specifically amounted to $127 million, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
Operating income came in at $252 million, a meaningful improvement over the $198 million recorded in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $184 million, compared to $141 million in fiscal 2022.
Earnings per diluted share reached $3.47, up from $2.66 in the prior year, surpassing analyst consensus estimates of $3.21.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that disciplined cost management contributed significantly to the margin expansion.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%, slightly below the company's long-term guidance range of 22% to 24%.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $318 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.3%.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $94 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion in Texas and Ohio.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $224 million, providing the company with substantial financial flexibility.
The company returned $68 million to shareholders through share repurchases during the fiscal year.
Meridian Harvest paid aggregate dividends of $41 million, or $0.77 per share, consistent with the board's established dividend policy.
The board of directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend rate, effective with the first quarter of 2024.
Total assets at year-end stood at $2.31 billion, up from $2.07 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Inventory levels increased to $387 million, reflecting strategic pre-positioning of raw materials ahead of anticipated input cost volatility.
Accounts receivable totaled $294 million, consistent with the seasonal nature of the agricultural inputs business.
The company's long-term debt balance as of December 31, 2023, stood at zero, as management fully retired the remaining $175 million term loan during the third quarter.
Meridian Harvest entered the fiscal year with no borrowings outstanding on its revolving credit facility and maintained that position throughout all four quarters.
The company's working capital ratio improved to 2.4x, reflecting strong liquidity management and disciplined balance sheet stewardship.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $312 million, providing ample coverage for near-term operational and strategic needs.
During the year, the company successfully integrated the acquisition of BioSoil Technologies, a biologicals startup acquired in late 2022 for $85 million.
BioSoil's product lines contributed approximately $47 million in incremental revenue and are expected to scale further in 2024.
In November 2023, Meridian Harvest secured a $250 million senior secured credit facility with a consortium of regional banks to support strategic growth initiatives.
The Latin America segment delivered revenue of $276 million, growing 18.4% year-over-year, driven by strong Brazilian soybean acreage expansion.
The North America segment remained the largest contributor, accounting for $1.29 billion, or approximately 70% of total company revenue.
European operations generated $274 million in revenue, tempered by unfavorable currency translation effects from a stronger U.S. dollar.
Senior Vice President of Operations, Daniel Ortega, reported that manufacturing efficiency improvements reduced per-unit production costs by 4.2%.
The company achieved a record on-time delivery rate of 96.3% across its distribution network, up from 93.1% in the prior year.
Meridian Harvest launched three new proprietary fungicide formulations during the fiscal year, expanding its crop protection portfolio.
These new products collectively accounted for approximately $34 million in first-year revenue and are targeted at premium-priced market segments.
The company holds 87 active patents across its product portfolio, with 14 new patents granted during fiscal 2023.
Headcount grew to 5,820 full-time employees by year-end, representing a net increase of 340 employees from the prior year.
Employee benefit costs increased by $18 million year-over-year, reflecting headcount growth and expanded healthcare coverage offerings.
The company's safety record improved markedly, with total recordable incident rates falling to 0.82 per 100 employees.
Meridian Harvest achieved its carbon intensity reduction target of 15% three years ahead of schedule, per its 2030 sustainability roadmap.
The company enrolled an additional 210,000 acres in its precision agriculture services platform, bringing total enrolled acreage to approximately 1.1 million acres.
Retention rates among the precision agriculture subscriber base improved to 88%, up from 82% in 2022.
Chief Commercial Officer Sandra Huang highlighted the success of the company's direct-to-grower sales model in driving customer loyalty.
The direct channel accounted for 43% of total crop protection sales in 2023, up from 37% in the prior year.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.10 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $345 million to $365 million, implying continued margin improvement.
Management expects capital expenditures in 2024 to increase to approximately $115 million as expansion projects ramp up.
The board authorized an incremental share repurchase program of up to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Meridian Harvest remains committed to its investment-grade credit profile and conservative leverage targets.
The company does not anticipate any material litigation outcomes that would adversely affect its financial position in the near term.
External auditors issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
Management expressed gratitude to shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued trust in Meridian Harvest Corporation.
The full annual report, including audited financial statements and notes thereto, is available on the company's investor relations website.
Veltronix Corporation, a mid-cap semiconductor equipment manufacturer headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company designs and produces precision lithography systems and wafer inspection platforms for leading chipmakers across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marguerite Holloway opened the report by highlighting what she called a transformational year for the organization.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year's figure of $1.64 billion.
Gross profit expanded to $712 million, yielding a gross margin of 38.7%, up from 36.1% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, and general administrative costs, totaled $489 million for the year.
The company reported operating income of $223 million, compared to $187 million in the same period last year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $164 million, or $2.18 per diluted share.
Veltronix concluded fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond tranche in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $95 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Austin facility.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Reddick noted that the balance sheet had never been stronger, with cash and equivalents reaching $430 million at year-end.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days, down from 54 days in fiscal 2022, reflecting tighter credit management policies.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization, returning approximately $98 million to shareholders.
In September 2023, Veltronix secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to fund strategic expansion initiatives across its product portfolio.
Research and development expenditure totaled $187 million, representing 10.2% of total revenue, underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
The Photon Series 7 wafer inspection platform, launched in Q2 2023, received positive reception from three major foundry customers in Taiwan.
Order backlog at year-end stood at $2.1 billion, a 19% increase over the $1.76 billion backlog recorded at the close of fiscal 2022.
Geographic revenue breakdown showed North America contributing 44%, Asia-Pacific 38%, and Europe 18% of total annual sales.
Dr. Holloway emphasized that the Asia-Pacific segment had grown particularly rapidly, with revenues rising 21% year-over-year in that region.
Headcount at Veltronix reached 6,840 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 430 from the prior year-end figure.
The company added 210 engineers and scientists in its core R&D division, reflecting its focus on next-generation lithography technology.
Veltronix declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing a 12.5% increase from the prior quarterly rate of $0.16.
The dividend increase marks the seventh consecutive annual raise in the company's shareholder distribution program.
Inventory levels rose to $318 million by year-end, driven by strategic buffer stocking ahead of anticipated supply chain disruptions.
Days inventory outstanding increased to 68 days from 61 days in fiscal 2022, consistent with the deliberate safety-stock strategy management described.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year's rate of 23.1%, due to increased R&D tax credits.
Deferred revenue stood at $134 million at year-end, reflecting customer prepayments on multi-year service and support agreements.
Veltronix's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 14.8%, exceeding the company's internal hurdle rate of 12.0%.
Operating cash flow for the year reached $248 million, providing ample liquidity to support both organic growth and potential acquisitions.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $153 million for fiscal 2023.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio remained at zero throughout 2023, as the balance sheet carried no funded debt instruments at any reporting date during the fiscal year.
Thomas Reddick noted in the earnings call that Veltronix had successfully extended its customer relationships with two anchor clients, Nexon Semiconductors and Brightvale Foundry.
Both multi-year agreements collectively represent an estimated $620 million in contracted future revenues over the next three years.
The company's warranty reserve increased to $42 million, reflecting higher sales volumes and an expanded installed base of field-deployed equipment.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $302 million, accounting for 16.4% of total revenue, down modestly from 17.1% in the prior year.
Veltronix maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's, which the company views as a strategic asset in customer confidence.
The Board of Directors authorized an incremental share repurchase program of up to $200 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Segment reporting for fiscal 2023 showed the Lithography Systems division generating $1.09 billion in revenue while the Inspection Platforms division contributed $750 million.
The Inspection Platforms division experienced faster growth at 15.8% year-over-year compared to the Lithography Systems division's 9.7% growth.
Dr. Holloway attributed the Inspection Platforms outperformance to increasing customer demand for advanced defect detection in leading-edge chip nodes below five nanometers.
Veltronix also completed a small tuck-in acquisition of Auris Metrology Systems, a privately held process diagnostics firm, for approximately $58 million in cash.
The Auris acquisition is expected to be earnings-neutral in fiscal 2024 and modestly accretive to earnings per share by fiscal 2025.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $77 million at year-end, essentially flat compared to the $75 million reported in fiscal 2022.
The weighted average cost of capital for Veltronix was estimated at 9.1% for fiscal 2023, incorporating both equity and market benchmark inputs.
Management issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.10 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 14%.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $2.40 to $2.55, assuming continued margin improvement and disciplined cost management.
The company highlighted risk factors including geopolitical tensions in East Asia, potential export control regulatory changes, and cyclical downturns in semiconductor capital expenditure.
Veltronix's stock closed at $54.32 on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, implying a market capitalization of approximately $4.1 billion.
The price-to-earnings ratio based on fiscal 2023 net income and year-end share price stood at approximately 24.9 times, in line with sector peers.
The Board expressed confidence that the company's technology roadmap, financial discipline, and expanding customer relationships position it well for sustained value creation.
Dr. Holloway closed the report by reaffirming Veltronix's commitment to responsible growth, environmental sustainability, and employee development as core strategic pillars.
The full audited financial statements, notes, and supplemental disclosures are included in the appendix to this report and have been reviewed by external auditors Crestwood & Albright LLP.
Veralux Photonics Corporation, a leading developer of advanced optical sensing systems, presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates primarily in the industrial automation and smart infrastructure sectors, serving clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Mara Lindqvist expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in March 2024.
Veralux Photonics reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The revenue growth was driven primarily by strong demand for the company's flagship LumenSense 900 product line in the industrial automation segment.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Abernethy noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points to reach 41.7% for the full year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 reached $134.6 million, compared to $109.2 million in fiscal 2022.
Research and development expenditure totaled $98.4 million, representing approximately 11.6% of total revenues.
The company employed 4,820 full-time equivalents as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 312 employees compared to year-end 2022.
Veralux Photonics completed the acquisition of Helion Sensor Technologies in April 2023 for a total consideration of $215 million.
The Helion acquisition added approximately $62 million in annualized revenues and brought with it a team of 180 specialized engineers.
Integration costs associated with the Helion transaction amounted to $14.7 million, recorded as a one-time charge in the second quarter.
The company's Asia-Pacific segment delivered revenues of $203.1 million, growing 22.4% year-over-year and representing 24.0% of total revenues.
European segment revenues reached $291.6 million, with growth of 11.8% supported by new infrastructure contracts in Germany and the Netherlands.
North American revenues, while the largest segment at $352.6 million, grew at a more modest pace of 9.7% compared to the prior year.
Dr. Lindqvist highlighted that the pipeline of signed but not yet delivered contracts stands at a record $1.2 billion entering fiscal 2024.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $57.8 million, focused largely on expanding manufacturing capacity at the Tucson, Arizona facility.
The Tucson expansion project increased annual production capacity for optical sensor modules by an estimated 35%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $189.4 million, providing the company with ample short-term liquidity.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 amounted to $1.63 billion, reflecting both organic growth and the consolidated impact of the Helion acquisition.
Shareholders' equity increased to $724.8 million from $641.3 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the payout established in the third quarter of 2022.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased 1.4 million shares of common stock at an average price of $63.40 per share under its buyback authorization.
The share repurchase program had an authorized limit of $100 million, of which approximately $88.8 million was utilized by year-end.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $97.2 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.84.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $178.9 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.1%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 24.1% in the prior year, reflecting benefits from R&D tax credits.
Inventory levels at year-end were $143.7 million, an increase of $21.4 million from the prior year-end balance.
The inventory build was intentional, as management sought to mitigate supply chain risks identified in the photonic component sourcing network.
Days sales outstanding improved to 48 days from 54 days at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting stronger collections performance.
The company's long-term debt outstanding as of December 31, 2023 was $310.5 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes issued in 2021.
Interest expense for the year amounted to $17.3 million, down slightly from $17.9 million in fiscal 2022.
Veralux Photonics maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Poors throughout fiscal 2023.
The company launched two new product families during fiscal 2023: the PrismaEdge 200 series and the NovaScan LiDAR platform.
The PrismaEdge 200 series targets smart building automation markets and received safety certifications in fifteen countries during the year.
The NovaScan LiDAR platform, aimed at autonomous logistics applications, generated initial revenues of $18.6 million in its first six months of commercial availability.
Chief Operating Officer Renata Voss stated that the NovaScan platform is on track to exceed $50 million in revenues in its first full calendar year of sales.
The company's backlog conversion rate improved to 73% in fiscal 2023 from 68% in the prior year, reflecting enhanced project execution capabilities.
Veralux Photonics expanded its global service network to include 34 regional service centers, up from 27 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The service and maintenance segment generated $112.4 million in revenues, growing 18.9% and representing 13.3% of total revenues.
Recurring revenues from multi-year service contracts now represent approximately 42% of the service segment's total revenues.
Mr. Abernethy confirmed that the company's leverage ratio, defined as net debt to adjusted EBITDA, stood at 0.68 times at year-end 2023.
The company's free cash flow for the year was $119.7 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $177.5 million less capital expenditures of $57.8 million.
Headcount in the research and development function grew to 1,140 employees by year-end, representing 23.6% of the total workforce.
Dr. Lindqvist emphasized that investment in human capital within engineering disciplines remains a top strategic priority for the coming three years.
The company's customer concentration risk remains modest, with no single client accounting for more than 7.3% of total annual revenues.
Veralux Photonics received the Photonics Industry Innovation Award for the second consecutive year, recognizing leadership in optical sensor miniaturization.
The board authorized an incremental share repurchase authorization of $50 million in November 2023, extending the buyback program into fiscal 2024.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 9% reduction in carbon intensity per unit of revenue compared to fiscal 2022.
The company expects fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of $940 million to $975 million, implying organic growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is guided to be between $200 million and $215 million, consistent with continued margin expansion.
As of December 31, 2023, Veralux Photonics carried no outstanding borrowings under its existing revolving credit facility, having fully repaid the balance in October 2023.
The company's market share in the industrial optical sensing category grew to an estimated 18.4%, up from 16.1% in the prior year.
Competitive dynamics in the Asia-Pacific region are intensifying, with several regional players gaining traction in the lower-cost sensor segment.
Management believes its differentiated technology stack and customer relationships provide durable competitive advantages over a three-to-five-year horizon.
The strong free cash flow generation profile positions Veralux Photonics to consider strategic bolt-on acquisitions without requiring additional equity issuance.
In February 2024, the company entered into a $180 million revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of six banks to support ongoing operational and strategic financing needs.
Veltara Systems Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation software and embedded control solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, serves clients across the manufacturing, logistics, and energy sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement expressing confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Prakashan noted that fiscal 2023 represented one of the most transformative years in Veltara's two-decade history.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741.6 million.
Software licensing revenue grew by 22.1% year-over-year, driven by strong enterprise adoption of the company's flagship VEXA platform.
Professional services revenue contributed $193.4 million, accounting for approximately 22.8% of total revenue.
Hardware and embedded systems revenue declined slightly, falling from $104.2 million in 2022 to $98.7 million in 2023.
Management attributed the hardware decline to intentional portfolio rationalization as the company pivots toward higher-margin software offerings.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 totaled $512.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.5%.
This compares favorably to the prior year gross margin of 57.3%, reflecting the improving product mix and operational efficiencies.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $318.4 million for the year.
Research and development expenditures rose to $112.7 million, representing 13.3% of total revenue and underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $119.6 million, reflecting aggressive investment in global expansion campaigns.
General and administrative expenses totaled $86.1 million, roughly flat compared to the $84.9 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the year came in at $194.2 million, up from $143.8 million in the prior period.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $148.9 million, compared to $108.3 million a year earlier.
Diluted earnings per share were reported at $3.74, versus $2.71 in fiscal 2022.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
This represented the company's first dividend initiation since its IPO in 2011, signaling confidence in sustained cash generation.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $224.8 million, up from $177.1 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $231.4 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023, a record high for the organization.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $47.2 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure and lab equipment upgrades.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow minus capital expenditures, reached $184.2 million for the fiscal year.
Veltara completed the acquisition of Orbisync Analytics Inc. in March 2023 for a total consideration of $95 million.
Orbisync, a predictive maintenance analytics firm based in Austin, Texas, added approximately 180 employees to Veltara's headcount.
Following the acquisition and normal attrition, total headcount at December 31, 2023, was 4,847 full-time employees.
The integration of Orbisync's technology into the VEXA platform is expected to be fully complete by the end of the second quarter of 2024.
International revenue grew to $301.6 million in fiscal 2023, representing 35.6% of total revenue compared to 30.2% in the prior year.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing international segment, posting 31.4% revenue growth year-over-year.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $87.3 million in revenue, with notable expansion in Japan and South Korea.
Veltara's customer retention rate remained strong at 91.4% for the fiscal year, reflecting high satisfaction among enterprise clients.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $563.2 million as of December 31, 2023, a 19.7% increase from the prior year-end balance.
The company's net revenue retention rate stood at 114%, indicating robust upsell and cross-sell activity within the existing customer base.
Veltara carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a pristine capital structure entirely free of borrowings.
The company's current ratio as of year-end was 2.8, reflecting strong short-term liquidity and conservative balance sheet management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, demonstrating tighter collections processes.
Deferred revenue balances grew to $178.6 million, reflecting strong multi-year contract signings during the second half of the year.
The Board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over the following 24 months.
In November 2023, Veltara secured a $275 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of six institutional lenders to support future strategic investments.
Management indicated that the credit facility remains undrawn and is intended primarily as a contingency liquidity buffer.
Veltara launched three new product modules during fiscal 2023: VEXA Predict, VEXA Connect, and VEXA Shield.
VEXA Predict, the predictive analytics module, recorded over 400 customer activations within its first six months of availability.
VEXA Shield, the cybersecurity compliance module, was recognized by industry analysts at Gartner as a strong performer in the OT security segment.
The company received ISO 27001 certification for its cloud hosting operations in Q2 2023, strengthening its compliance posture for regulated industries.
Veltara's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 9.1% for fiscal 2023, used as the discount rate for internal investment decisions.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.1% due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Deferred tax assets totaled $38.7 million at year-end, primarily related to stock-based compensation and lease obligations.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal 2023 amounted to $29.3 million, consistent with the prior year level of $28.1 million.
The company's equity-based incentive plan covers approximately 1,200 employees, including all senior managers and key technical contributors.
Goodwill on the balance sheet increased to $217.4 million following the Orbisync acquisition, compared to $134.8 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Intangible assets, net of amortization, stood at $89.6 million, primarily comprised of customer relationships and developed technology.
Amortization of acquired intangibles totaled $14.3 million for fiscal 2023, expected to continue at a similar rate over the next three years.
The company's operating lease liabilities totaled $62.4 million at year-end, reflecting office space obligations across eleven global locations.
Veltara renewed its primary corporate headquarters lease in Raleigh for an additional ten-year term commencing January 2024.
Chief Technology Officer Anika Mehrotra highlighted the role of machine learning in accelerating development cycles for the VEXA platform.
The company filed 23 new patent applications during fiscal 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 147 patents.
Veltara's ESG report, released concurrently with the annual financial report, detailed a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
The company set a target to achieve carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2030.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the annual internal survey, improved to 78% favorable from 72% in the prior year.
The Board of Directors consists of nine members, six of whom are independent, with an average tenure of 6.3 years.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $960 million to $985 million, implying growth of approximately 13% to 16%.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at approximately 23% to 24%, reflecting continued operating leverage on a growing revenue base.
Veltara remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, investing in organic growth initiatives while selectively pursuing strategic acquisitions that enhance its technology portfolio.
The report was authorized for issue by the Board of Directors on February 15, 2024, and is prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Calloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to long-term shareholder value and operational excellence.
Veltora Dynamics reported consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing an increase of 11.3 percent compared to the prior year.
The robotics integration segment remained the largest revenue contributor, accounting for approximately 54 percent of total sales.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Przybyl noted that gross margins expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year, driven by favorable input cost trends and improved manufacturing efficiency.
The company's workforce grew modestly during the year, with total headcount reaching 8,420 full-time employees by December 31, 2023.

Veltora Dynamics completed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 senior notes in March and carrying no outstanding borrowings on its revolving credit facility.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $142 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading robotic assembly lines.
The company's research and development spending reached $211 million, reflecting a continued emphasis on next-generation motion control systems.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 came in at $298 million, compared to $251 million in the prior year, marking a 18.7 percent improvement.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $4.63, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $4.41 by approximately five percent.
In November 2023, Veltora Dynamics secured a $350 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of seven regional banks to fund its accelerating capital investment program.
The automation software division launched three new product families during the year, including the VX-9 precision controller platform released in the second quarter.
Customer retention rates across Veltora's enterprise accounts improved to 91 percent, up from 87 percent in fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of its common stock during the year under its board-authorized buyback program, at an average price of $68.40 per share.
International revenue grew by 17 percent year-over-year, with particularly strong demand from European automotive manufacturers.
Veltora's German subsidiary contributed €94 million in revenue during 2023, reflecting robust order intake from Tier 1 suppliers.
The Asia-Pacific region posted revenue growth of 22 percent, led by expansion in South Korean and Japanese markets.
Supply chain disruptions that had constrained output in the prior two years were substantially resolved by mid-2023, enabling production volumes to normalize.
Inventory levels declined by $38 million compared to year-end 2022, as improved logistics and leaner manufacturing practices took hold.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in 2022, reflecting tighter credit management policies implemented by the finance team.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12 percent increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend.
Veltora Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company's adjusted EBITDA margin reached 19.4 percent, consistent with management's previously communicated target range of 19 to 21 percent.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $334 million, providing ample liquidity for ongoing strategic initiatives.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $192 million for the full year.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Ibarra highlighted the successful ramp-up of the new Columbus East facility, which reached full production capacity ahead of schedule in October 2023.
The Columbus East facility added approximately 340,000 square feet of manufacturing floor space to Veltora's domestic footprint.
Veltora entered into a strategic partnership with Nexagen Systems in April 2023 to co-develop warehouse automation solutions targeting the e-commerce fulfillment sector.
The Nexagen partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of between $40 million and $60 million starting in fiscal year 2025.
Management guided fiscal year 2024 revenues to a range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 9 to 13 percent.
The guidance reflects anticipated contributions from the VX-9 platform ramp, expanded European sales coverage, and initial Nexagen partnership revenues.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance for 2024 was set at $4.95 to $5.20, representing approximately 7 to 12 percent growth over the 2023 result.
The company expects capital expenditures in 2024 to remain elevated at approximately $155 million, reflecting continued investment in automation and digitization.
Risk factors cited in the report include macroeconomic uncertainty, potential pricing pressure from Asian low-cost competitors, and foreign exchange volatility.
Veltora's hedging program covers approximately 60 percent of its projected euro-denominated revenues for fiscal year 2024 at a rate of 1.09 USD per euro.
The company carries a defined benefit pension obligation with a projected benefit obligation of $187 million as of year-end 2023.
Pension plan assets totaled $163 million, leaving an underfunded status of $24 million, which management characterized as manageable given anticipated contribution plans.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 9 percent relative to the 2021 baseline.
Veltora received a Silver rating from EcoVadis for sustainability performance in 2023, an improvement from the prior year's Bronze designation.
The company's board of directors underwent a significant refresh during the year, with three new independent directors appointed in June 2023.
Newly appointed board member Dr. Yuki Tanaka brings expertise in semiconductor manufacturing and advanced materials science.
Board member Alejandro Ruiz, who joined the board in 2019, chairs the audit committee and holds CPA and CFA designations.
The audit committee oversaw the transition of Veltora's external auditor from Marsh & Feldman LLP to Birchwood Grant LLP, effective for the 2023 fiscal year audit.
Birchwood Grant LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltora's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The company's internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the criteria established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.
Veltora's legal team resolved two outstanding patent infringement matters during the year, with aggregate settlement costs of $7.2 million recorded in general and administrative expenses.
No material litigation remains pending as of the report date that management believes would have a materially adverse effect on the company's financial position.
The report notes that Veltora repaid a short-term working capital credit line of $15 million that was drawn in January 2023 and fully retired by April 2023.
Segment reporting reflects three reportable units: Robotics Integration, Automation Software, and Precision Components, with the latter contributing $312 million in fiscal year 2023 revenues.
The Precision Components segment experienced margin compression of approximately 60 basis points due to elevated raw material costs in the first half of the year.
Recovery in the second half of 2023 restored Precision Components' adjusted segment margin to 14.1 percent by year-end.
Veltora's order backlog stood at $920 million as of December 31, 2023, representing approximately six months of forward revenue coverage at the midpoint of 2024 guidance.
Book-to-bill ratio for fiscal year 2023 was 1.08, indicating that new orders continued to outpace revenue recognized during the period.
The company expects to convert approximately 65 percent of the current backlog into recognized revenue within the first two quarters of fiscal year 2024.
Ms. Calloway concluded her remarks by reaffirming Veltora Dynamics' strategic vision of becoming the leading provider of integrated industrial automation solutions in North America by 2027.
She cited the company's expanding software capabilities, strengthened balance sheet, and deepening customer relationships as the primary pillars underpinning that ambition.

The complete audited financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, statements of cash flows, and notes to financial statements, are appended to this report and incorporated herein by reference.
Veltora Dynamics' annual report on Form 10-K has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission and is available on the company's investor relations website at veltora-ir.com.
Veltora Systems Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation software and embedded control hardware, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, serves clients across the aerospace, automotive, and energy sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holt opened her letter to shareholders by expressing confidence in the company's long-term trajectory.
She noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point for Veltora's product portfolio and market positioning.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Kwon reported total annual revenues of $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the fiscal year came in at $391 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.2%.
Operating income reached $134 million, up from $109 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
The company's adjusted EBITDA was reported at $178 million, reflecting strong core operational performance.
Raymond Kwon highlighted disciplined cost management as a primary driver of margin expansion during the year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $112 million, representing approximately 13.2% of total revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $145 million, a modest increase compared to the $138 million recorded in 2022.
Veltora's cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
This dividend represented a 20% increase from the prior quarterly rate of $0.15 per share.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $67 million, directed primarily toward manufacturing capacity expansion at the Raleigh campus.
Veltora also completed the acquisition of Denova Controls, a specialist in servo-motor feedback systems, for $95 million in cash.
The Denova acquisition is expected to be accretive to earnings per share within the first full year of integration.
Headcount across the company grew to 4,820 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of 310 positions from December 2022.
The company's North American segment generated $512 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 60.4% of total sales.
International revenues, spanning Europe and Asia-Pacific, contributed the remaining $335 million.
The European segment grew 18% year-over-year, driven by strong demand in German and Scandinavian automotive supply chains.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew modestly at 6%, reflecting softness in certain Chinese industrial markets.
Veltora launched three new product lines during the fiscal year, including the Nexara embedded controller platform.
The Nexara platform has already secured design wins with two Tier 1 aerospace integrators.
The second product launch was the Veltora EdgeSync middleware suite, targeting real-time sensor fusion applications.
EdgeSync has been integrated into pilot programs at seven energy utilities across North America.
The third major launch, Veltora ProLink telemetry hardware, entered full production in Q4 2023.
ProLink is projected to generate over $40 million in incremental revenue during fiscal year 2024.
The company's backlog at year-end stood at $1.12 billion, a record high for Veltora Systems.
This backlog provides strong revenue visibility for the next 18 to 24 months, according to CFO Raymond Kwon.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $88 million, reflecting a deliberate build in safety stock to buffer supply chain volatility.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltora had fully retired all outstanding long-term debt, achieving a completely unlevered balance sheet.
The company's accounts receivable balance improved to $104 million, down from $118 million at the end of 2022.
Days sales outstanding improved to 45 days, indicating enhanced collections efficiency compared to the 51-day DSO posted in the prior year.
Margaret Holt credited the finance team's process automation initiatives for the improvement in working capital metrics.
The company repurchased $55 million of its common stock during the year under its existing share buyback authorization.
In October 2023, Veltora entered into a $250 million senior secured term loan agreement with a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund strategic growth initiatives.
The share repurchase program has $120 million of remaining authorization, which the board intends to utilize over the next two fiscal years.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, benefiting from research tax credits and certain international tax planning strategies.
The company expects its effective tax rate to remain in the 20% to 22% range over the medium term.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $47 million at year-end, consistent with actuarial projections.
The company contributed $9 million to its defined benefit pension plan during fiscal year 2023.
Operating cash flow for the year was $161 million, driven by strong net income and favorable working capital movements.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $94 million for the fiscal year.
Veltora's return on invested capital was 14.7%, above the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.2%.
The board views this positive ROIC spread as validation of the company's capital allocation discipline.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for revenue in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 is set between $195 million and $210 million.
Diluted earnings per share for 2024 are expected to be in the range of $2.40 to $2.55.
The company plans to increase its research and development budget to approximately $130 million in fiscal year 2024.
Margaret Holt concluded her shareholder letter by reaffirming Veltora's commitment to innovation, operational excellence, and sustainable long-term value creation.
The full audited financial statements, including notes and management's discussion and analysis, are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delaine and has grown to serve over 4,200 enterprise clients across 38 countries.
Veltora operates three primary business segments: Cloud Platform Services, Enterprise Analytics, and Managed Security Solutions.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltora reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion, representing an 11.3% increase year-over-year.
The Cloud Platform Services segment was the largest contributor, generating $920 million in revenue, or approximately 50% of the total.
Enterprise Analytics contributed $612 million in fiscal 2023, reflecting sustained demand from financial services and healthcare verticals.
Managed Security Solutions, the company's fastest-growing segment, recorded revenue of $308 million, up 27.4% from the prior year.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 reached $1.09 billion, yielding a consolidated gross margin of 59.2%.
Operating expenses totaled $784 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditure was $231 million, representing 12.6% of total revenue, consistent with the company's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing spending increased to $318 million as Veltora expanded its go-to-market teams in Europe and Southeast Asia.
General and administrative costs were $235 million, which included one-time restructuring charges of $18 million recorded in Q2 2023.
Operating income for the full year was $306 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.6%.
Adjusted EBITDA, excluding stock-based compensation and restructuring charges, came in at $412 million, representing a 22.4% adjusted EBITDA margin.
Net income attributable to Veltora shareholders was $214 million, compared to $189 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 were $2.87, up from $2.53 in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for the period was 21.3%, reflecting the utilization of certain R&D tax credits across multiple jurisdictions.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $638 million, with total liquidity, including an undrawn revolving credit facility, of $1.1 billion.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $348 million, driven by strong billings growth and disciplined working capital management.
Deferred revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year to $527 million, signaling healthy forward visibility in subscription-based revenue streams.
The company's annual recurring revenue base reached $1.62 billion as of December 31, 2023, with a net revenue retention rate of 112%.
Customer acquisition costs improved modestly, with the blended payback period declining from 28 months in 2022 to 25 months in 2023.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar highlighted the strength of Veltora's balance sheet during the Q4 2023 earnings call held on February 15, 2024.
She noted that the company had successfully reduced its long-term debt balance to zero by the end of fiscal 2023, retiring the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in September.
Veltora ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of any outstanding debt obligations, having made its final principal payment in the third quarter.
Total headcount at year-end was 9,840 full-time employees, reflecting net hiring of approximately 620 people during the calendar year.
The company's global workforce is distributed across 22 countries, with major engineering hubs in Austin, Dublin, and Bangalore.
Veltora's board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $300 million in October 2023, of which $85 million had been utilized by year-end.
The company does not currently pay a dividend, choosing instead to reinvest cash flows into organic growth initiatives and strategic acquisitions.
In November 2023, Veltora finalized a $275 million senior secured term loan with a syndicate of six lending institutions to fund its acquisition pipeline.
The acquisition of DataNova Analytics, a specialized AI-driven data governance firm, was completed in December 2023 for a total consideration of $410 million.
The DataNova transaction is expected to be immediately accretive to non-GAAP earnings per share and add approximately $62 million in annualized revenue.
Integration costs related to DataNova are projected at $24 million over the first 12 months following close, the majority of which will be recorded in 2024.
Veltora's backlog of contracted but not yet recognized revenue reached $2.1 billion at year-end, providing approximately 14 months of forward revenue coverage.
The company secured 18 new enterprise contracts each valued in excess of $10 million during fiscal 2023, compared to 11 such contracts in fiscal 2022.
Veltora's cloud platform achieved ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II recertification in Q3 2023, reinforcing its position as a trusted vendor in regulated industries.
Platform uptime for fiscal 2023 averaged 99.97%, exceeding the company's contractual SLA commitments of 99.9% across all service tiers.
CEO Marcus Delaine stated that the company's innovation roadmap for 2024 includes the general availability launch of Veltora Quantum, an AI-powered analytics engine.
Veltora Quantum entered private beta with 40 select enterprise customers in October 2023, generating early positive feedback regarding latency improvements.
The product team, led by Chief Product Officer Sandra Yee, expects Veltora Quantum to be available to all existing customers by mid-2024.
In the European market, Veltora's revenue grew 19.2% year-over-year to $386 million, driven by strong adoption of the Enterprise Analytics suite.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $214 million in revenue, representing 11.6% of total company revenue, up from 9.8% in fiscal 2022.
North America remained Veltora's largest geography, generating $1.24 billion in revenue, or 67.4% of the consolidated total.
Gross margin in the Cloud Platform Services segment expanded by 180 basis points to 67.4%, reflecting operating leverage as the platform scales.
The Managed Security Solutions segment's gross margin was 44.1%, somewhat lower than company average due to the labor-intensive nature of managed services.
Veltora's churn rate among enterprise customers declined to 4.2% on an annualized basis, the lowest level in the company's history.
The company added a net of 310 new enterprise customers during fiscal 2023, ending the year with 4,210 total enterprise accounts.
Partner ecosystem revenue, generated through resellers and system integrators, represented 31% of total bookings in fiscal 2023, up from 24% in 2022.
Veltora expanded its partnership network by formalizing agreements with 14 new global system integrators, including two of the Big Four consulting firms.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $89 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure expansion in Frankfurt, Singapore, and Chicago.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $106 million for the year, of which $38 million was attributable to acquired intangible assets.
