The company's days sales outstanding improved from 62 days in fiscal 2022 to 54 days in fiscal 2023, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory of on-premises hardware appliances used in the Managed Security segment decreased to $21 million at year-end, down from $34 million at the end of 2022.
The reduction in hardware inventory reflects Veltora's deliberate strategic shift toward software-defined and cloud-delivered security architectures.
For fiscal 2024, Veltora is guiding total revenue in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.10 billion, implying growth of approximately 11% to 14%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 is set at 23% to 24%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage partially offset by DataNova integration costs.
The company expects stock-based compensation expense of approximately $145 million in fiscal 2024, up from $128 million in fiscal 2023.
Diluted share count for fiscal 2024 is expected to be approximately 76 million shares, accounting for the impact of the ongoing share repurchase program.
Veltora's audit committee, chaired by independent director Dr. Elaine Forsythe, oversaw the engagement of Grant & Whitmore LLP as the company's external auditor.
The external audit was completed without qualification, and Grant & Whitmore LLP issued an unmodified opinion on Veltora's fiscal 2023 financial statements.
Management assessed the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting under the COSO 2013 framework and concluded that controls were effective.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls were identified during fiscal 2023 or through the date of this report.
Veltora's long-term strategic vision is to become a top-five global enterprise cloud platform provider by 2030, measured by revenue and customer count.
Management is confident that the company's strong financial foundation, innovative product pipeline, and expanding partner ecosystem position it well for sustained growth.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. is a mid-cap industrial company specializing in sustainable corrugated and flexible packaging materials for the consumer goods sector.
Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, the company operates fourteen manufacturing facilities across North America and Western Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Veltri presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's investor day held in March of the current fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Chu noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal period for operational restructuring and capital allocation.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, reflecting a 7.3 percent increase over the prior year's $2.65 billion.
The North American segment contributed $1.92 billion of total revenue, while the European segment accounted for the remaining $920 million.
Gross profit improved to $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 21.5 percent compared to 19.8 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled $388 million for the year.
The company reported an operating income of $224 million, representing a year-over-year increase of approximately 18 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $147 million, or $2.31 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $341 million, reflecting a margin of 12.0 percent on consolidated revenue.
Ms. Veltri emphasized that the improvement in EBITDA margin was driven primarily by raw material cost efficiencies and improved logistics coordination.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $198 million, of which approximately 60 percent was allocated to capacity expansion projects.
The company commissioned its newest facility in Raleigh, North Carolina, during the third quarter, adding approximately 340,000 square feet of production space.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year was $117 million, slightly above the $109 million recorded in the prior year.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $143 million for the fiscal year.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the prior quarter's distribution.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its consolidated balance sheet, having retired its final senior note in August of that year.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $284 million, representing approximately 37 days of supply based on cost of goods sold.
Accounts receivable totaled $319 million at fiscal year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 41 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.1 times from 1.7 times in the prior year, signaling stronger short-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand at December 31, 2023, amounted to $206 million, up from $158 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, management finalized a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five lenders to support future growth initiatives.
Total headcount at year-end was approximately 9,400 full-time employees across all global operations.
The company reduced its workforce in the European segment by approximately 310 positions during the second quarter as part of a rationalization program.
Restructuring charges associated with the European workforce reduction totaled $22 million and were recorded in the second quarter.
The North American segment, by contrast, added roughly 480 full-time positions during the year to support the new Raleigh facility and expanded sales operations.
Net headcount across the enterprise, therefore, increased modestly on a year-over-year basis when accounting for both additions and reductions.
Mr. Chu confirmed that the restructuring program is expected to generate approximately $18 million in annualized savings beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Research and development expenditures for the year were $43 million, focused primarily on bio-based resin alternatives and recyclable liner technologies.
The company successfully launched its EcoWrap Pro product line in the second quarter, targeting food and beverage manufacturers seeking fully recyclable flexible packaging.
EcoWrap Pro achieved $61 million in first-year revenue, exceeding the internal forecast of $50 million by approximately 22 percent.
Customer adoption of EcoWrap Pro was particularly strong in the snack food and dairy segments, which together accounted for over 70 percent of line revenue.
The company holds three new patents related to EcoWrap Pro's barrier coating technology, which were granted during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose to $211 million from $194 million in the prior year, partly driven by expanded sales force investments.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.4 percent, compared to 24.1 percent in fiscal year 2022.
The slight decline in the effective tax rate was attributable to research and development tax credits and favorable resolution of prior-year state tax audits.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet reached $1.87 billion at year-end, compared to $1.72 billion at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Shareholders' equity grew to $934 million from $821 million, reflecting the year's net income partially offset by dividend payments and share repurchases.
The company repurchased 1.4 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.70 per share, for a total outlay of approximately $54 million.
The share repurchase program has $96 million of authorized capacity remaining as of December 31, 2023.
Goodwill and intangible assets on the balance sheet totaled $387 million, unchanged from the prior year as no acquisitions or impairments were recorded.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance, calling for revenue in the range of $3.0 billion to $3.1 billion.
The midpoint of the revenue guidance implies year-over-year growth of approximately 8 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $370 million to $390 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are projected at approximately $175 million, lower than the current year's level as the Raleigh facility comes fully online.
Ms. Veltri stated that the company's strategic focus for the coming year will center on organic volume growth, product mix optimization, and international market expansion.
The European segment is expected to return to profitability in the first half of fiscal year 2024, benefiting from the completed restructuring actions.
The company is actively evaluating potential bolt-on acquisitions in the specialty packaging space, with a stated preference for targets in the $100 million to $300 million revenue range.
Any acquisitions would be funded through a combination of existing cash resources and available credit capacity, according to Mr. Chu.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB by one major rating agency during the year, reflecting stable financial performance and disciplined capital management.
Meridian Packaging Solutions maintains a long-term leverage target of net debt to adjusted EBITDA of no more than 1.5 times.
The company's environmental sustainability report, published concurrently with this filing, outlines a target to reduce Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 30 percent by 2030.
Meridian Packaging Solutions has been included in the S&P MidCap 400 Index since 2019 and continues to attract a diversified institutional shareholder base.
Management expressed confidence that the investments made in fiscal year 2023 position the company for sustained, profitable growth over the medium term.

The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, including changes in raw material costs, macroeconomic conditions, and regulatory developments.
Veldthorn Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its fiscal year 2023 annual report on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Tillenbeck opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's strategic transformation over the past three years.
Veldthorn Dynamics operates across three primary segments: precision actuators, thermal shielding systems, and avionics integration modules.
The company reported consolidated net revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margin expanded to 38.7% in fiscal year 2023, up from 35.2% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiency.
Chief Financial Officer Reginald Osei attributed the margin improvement to a successful lean operations initiative launched in Q1 2023.
The precision actuators segment contributed $720 million in revenue, making it the largest segment by top-line performance.
Thermal shielding systems generated $610 million in revenue, growing 9.1% year-over-year driven by strong commercial aerospace demand.
Avionics integration modules delivered $510 million in revenue, with operating income of $88 million for the year.
Operating expenses totaled $412 million, including $145 million in research and development investment, the highest R&D spend in the company's history.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 reached $203 million, primarily allocated toward expanding the Tucson facility and modernizing production lines.
The company employed approximately 9,400 full-time workers globally as of December 31, 2023, a 6% increase from the prior year-end headcount.
Veldthorn Dynamics maintained a robust cash position of $318 million at fiscal year-end, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the company's policy of returning capital to shareholders.
Veldthorn Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, with no outstanding borrowings on any credit facilities.
International revenue accounted for 41% of total consolidated sales, up from 36% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting successful expansion into European defense markets.
The company secured a landmark $290 million contract with a major European aerospace OEM in October 2023, to be delivered over a four-year period.
Chief Operating Officer Sylvana Pretch noted that supply chain disruptions, which had plagued operations in 2021 and 2022, were substantially resolved by mid-2023.
Inventory levels declined to $187 million at year-end 2023 from $241 million at year-end 2022, reflecting more disciplined procurement and faster inventory turnover.
In November 2023, Veldthorn Dynamics executed a $350 million senior secured term loan agreement with a consortium of institutional lenders to fund strategic acquisitions.
The acquisition pipeline includes two target companies in the avionics sector, with due diligence ongoing as of the report date.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $387 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.0%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $194 million, or $3.71 per diluted share, compared to $162 million, or $3.09 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below the U.S. statutory rate due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Veldthorn Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share under its authorized buyback program.
The share repurchase program had $75 million of remaining authorization as of December 31, 2023.
Ms. Tillenbeck emphasized that the company's growth strategy centers on organic innovation supplemented by targeted bolt-on acquisitions.
The thermal shielding segment achieved record backlog of $1.1 billion at year-end, underpinning visibility into fiscal year 2024 revenue.
Veldthorn Dynamics received Federal Aviation Administration certification for its next-generation actuator platform, the VD-900 series, in August 2023.
The VD-900 series is expected to begin generating meaningful revenue contributions beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
Mr. Osei confirmed that the company's pension obligations are fully funded, with a surplus of $14 million as of the actuarial valuation date.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remain a core priority, with Veldthorn Dynamics targeting a 30% reduction in carbon emissions by 2030.
The company achieved a 7% reduction in carbon intensity per unit of output in fiscal year 2023 compared to the fiscal year 2022 baseline.
Veldthorn Dynamics invested $22 million in workforce development and training programs during the year, underscoring its commitment to employee advancement.
The company's Tucson headquarters campus underwent a $55 million expansion, adding approximately 180,000 square feet of manufacturing and laboratory space.
Board membership expanded in fiscal year 2023 with the appointment of two independent directors with expertise in defense procurement and international trade.
Dr. Amina Foscari, newly appointed to the board, brings over 25 years of experience in aerospace regulatory affairs and government contracting.
The audit committee engaged Whitmore & Calder LLP as external auditors for fiscal year 2023, succeeding the previous auditor after a competitive review process.
Whitmore & Calder LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Backlog across all three segments totaled $2.7 billion at December 31, 2023, representing approximately 1.5 times trailing twelve months revenue.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of net revenue between $2.05 billion and $2.12 billion, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15% over fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $430 million to $450 million, reflecting continued operational leverage.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $220 million to $240 million as facility investments continue.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $184 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $387 million less capital expenditures of $203 million.
The precision actuators segment holds an estimated 18% share of the North American commercial aerospace actuator market, according to internal market analysis.
Veldthorn Dynamics intends to pursue at least one strategic acquisition in fiscal year 2024, leveraging its available capital resources.
Ms. Pretch highlighted that on-time delivery performance improved to 96.3% in fiscal year 2023, up from 91.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by Veldthorn's annual survey, reached a record high of 8.7 out of 10 in fiscal year 2023.
The company's stock price appreciated 34% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P Aerospace and Defense index by approximately 18 percentage points.
Veldthorn Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company plans to host its annual investor day in May 2024, where management will present a detailed three-year strategic roadmap.
Mr. Osei noted that working capital management improvements contributed approximately $40 million to operating cash flow in fiscal year 2023.
Veldthorn Dynamics expressed confidence that its diversified customer base and strong backlog position it well to navigate any near-term macroeconomic uncertainty.
The report concluded with a reaffirmation of the company's long-term target of achieving a 25% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2027.
Margaux Tillenbeck closed her letter to shareholders by expressing gratitude to the company's global workforce for their dedication and resilience throughout fiscal year 2023.
Velarion Technologies, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Velarion operates in the enterprise cloud infrastructure sector, providing scalable storage and compute solutions to mid-market and large enterprise clients across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Threlkeld opened the year with a strategic commitment to expanding Velarion's platform capabilities and deepening its customer base.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Sundaram noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point for the company's financial trajectory.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 19% increase over the prior year's $711 million.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 74% of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful transition away from one-time licensing fees.
Velarion ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March of that year.
Operating income for the period was $134 million, compared to $98 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management.
Gross margin improved by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 68.4% as the company benefited from economies of scale in its data center operations.
Research and development expenditures totaled $122 million, representing approximately 14.4% of total revenue.
Velarion added 340 net new enterprise customers during the fiscal year, bringing its total active customer count to 2,180 organizations.
In September 2023, Velarion's board of directors approved a $300 million syndicated credit facility arranged through Meridian Capital Partners to fund its planned acquisition pipeline.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $89 million, primarily directed toward expanding data center capacity in Dallas and Frankfurt.
The company's headcount grew from 3,410 to 3,920 employees during the year, driven largely by engineering and customer success hiring.
Velarion's net income for fiscal year 2023 was $97 million, compared to $61 million in the prior year, representing a 59% increase.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $2.14, up from $1.35 in fiscal year 2022.
The board declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share payable to shareholders of record as of February 14, 2024.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $412 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for the year was $198 million, a significant improvement over the $143 million generated in fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved from 48 to 41 days, indicating faster collections and tighter credit controls.
Velarion's flagship product, the VelaCore Platform, received three major feature releases in 2023, each adding capabilities in AI-assisted workload optimization.
Customer net revenue retention rate reached 118%, demonstrating strong upsell and expansion activity within the existing customer base.
The company's international revenue grew 31% year-over-year, with European operations now contributing 28% of total revenue.
Chief Revenue Officer Dana Kowalczyk attributed international growth to new partnerships with regional resellers in Germany, the Netherlands, and Sweden.
Velarion signed a strategic alliance agreement with Fortis Cloud Services in Q2 2023, enabling joint go-to-market initiatives in the financial services vertical.
The Fortis partnership generated approximately $18 million in co-sold revenue during the second half of 2023, validating the strategic rationale of the collaboration.
Operating expenses outside of cost of revenue totaled $443 million for the fiscal year, inclusive of sales, marketing, R&D, and general and administrative costs.
Sales and marketing expense was $189 million, or 22.3% of revenue, reflecting increased investment in brand awareness and demand generation campaigns.
General and administrative costs were $132 million, which included $14 million in one-time costs related to the company's enterprise resource planning system upgrade.
Velarion's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly above the prior year's 19.8%, due to changes in international tax provisions.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $61.40 per share, totaling approximately $73.7 million.
Total shares outstanding at December 31, 2023 stood at 45.3 million, down from 46.1 million at the start of the fiscal year after accounting for share buybacks and issuances.
Inventory of hardware components maintained for client deployments was valued at $27 million at year-end, consistent with the company's lean supply chain strategy.
Deferred revenue increased by $44 million during the year, reaching $211 million, reflecting strong bookings activity in the fourth quarter.
Velarion's remaining performance obligations totaled $1.3 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing strong revenue visibility for fiscal years 2024 and 2025.
The company's customer churn rate remained below 4% for the third consecutive year, a testament to the stickiness of the VelaCore Platform.
Marcus Threlkeld highlighted the successful launch of VelaEdge, a lightweight compute offering for distributed enterprise environments, as a key product milestone for 2023.
VelaEdge attracted 210 customer commitments within its first six months of availability, exceeding the internal target of 150.
Priya Sundaram provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of total revenue between $990 million and $1.02 billion, reflecting continued double-digit growth expectations.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at approximately 16.5%, implying continued improvement over the 15.8% achieved in fiscal year 2023.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $100 million to $115 million as it continues expanding its infrastructure footprint.
Velarion announced plans to open a new regional office in Singapore in the first half of 2024 to support its nascent Asia-Pacific market entry strategy.
The Singapore office is expected to be staffed with approximately 80 employees by the end of fiscal year 2024, focused on sales, solutions engineering, and customer support.
Velarion's audit committee engaged Ernst & Waller LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Ernst & Waller issued an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements, confirming they present fairly the financial position of Velarion in all material respects.
The company maintains robust internal controls over financial reporting, having received no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in its Sarbanes-Oxley assessment.
Velarion's board of directors consists of nine members, six of whom are independent, providing strong governance oversight of management decisions.
The compensation committee approved a long-term incentive plan for senior leadership that ties 60% of equity grants to relative total shareholder return over a three-year measurement period.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced meaningfully in 2023, with Velarion achieving carbon neutrality across its owned and leased data center operations.
The company sourced 84% of its data center energy from renewable sources in 2023, up from 71% in the prior year.
Velarion published its second annual ESG report in May 2023, detailing progress toward its 2030 sustainability targets including a commitment to net-zero scope three emissions.
Management believes the combination of strong recurring revenue, expanding margins, and disciplined capital allocation positions Velarion for sustained value creation.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, founded in 2009, operates across three primary segments: cloud services, on-premises licensing, and professional consulting.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Holstead opened the report with a statement reflecting cautious optimism about the company's trajectory.
Veltrano Systems posted total annual revenue of $847 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Cloud services revenue grew by 31% year-over-year, reaching $412 million and becoming the company's largest segment by revenue for the first time.
On-premises licensing revenue declined by 8% to $213 million, consistent with broader industry trends toward subscription-based models.
Professional consulting contributed $222 million in revenue, a modest gain of 4.1% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year totaled $509 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.1%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, amounted to $334 million, with research and development accounting for the largest share at $142 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $119 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in expanding its customer acquisition channels.
General and administrative costs came in at $73 million, roughly flat compared to the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $175 million, up from $138 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $131 million, or $2.17 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $104 million, or $1.73 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $221 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.1%.
Chief Financial Officer Darren Okafor noted that margin expansion was driven primarily by operating leverage in the cloud services segment.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.3%, compared to 21.8% in the prior year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $198 million, up from $154 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow generation was particularly strong, reaching $183 million for the full year.
Capital expenditures were $38 million, largely allocated to data center upgrades and network infrastructure improvements.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $1.42 billion and total liabilities of $610 million.
Stockholders' equity stood at $810 million at fiscal year-end, reflecting retained earnings growth and share-based compensation.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, signaling stronger collections performance.
Deferred revenue grew to $264 million, indicating healthy forward-looking contract commitments across the customer base.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $38.40 per share.
This buyback program was funded entirely from operating cash flows and represents a total outlay of approximately $80.6 million.
In addition to buybacks, the board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, commencing in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during the fiscal year amounted to $21.8 million.
The company expanded its global headcount by approximately 9% during the year, ending fiscal year 2023 with 6,840 full-time employees.
A significant portion of new hires were in engineering and product development roles, reflecting the company's prioritization of innovation.
Veltrano Systems launched three major product updates during the year, including its flagship VeltraCloud 4.0 platform in the second quarter.
VeltraCloud 4.0 introduced advanced AI-driven analytics capabilities and was well-received by the company's enterprise customer base.
Customer retention rates improved to 91% in fiscal year 2023, up from 88% in the prior year.
The company added 340 net new enterprise customers during the year, bringing the total enterprise customer count to approximately 4,200.
Average contract value among enterprise customers increased by 14% year-over-year to $186,000.
The North American market accounted for 67% of total revenue, with international markets contributing the remaining 33%.
International revenue grew at a faster pace than domestic revenue, increasing 19% year-over-year to $280 million.
The company made one strategic acquisition during fiscal year 2023, purchasing DataPulse Analytics, a data visualization startup, for $62 million in cash.
DataPulse's proprietary technology has been integrated into the VeltraCloud platform, enhancing its business intelligence module.
Darren Okafor confirmed that the DataPulse acquisition was accounted for as a business combination under ASC 805.
Goodwill recognized in connection with the acquisition amounted to $27 million, primarily reflecting expected synergies and assembled workforce.
The company disclosed no material legal proceedings or contingent liabilities that would adversely affect its financial position.
Risk factors cited in the report include macroeconomic uncertainty, competition from hyperscale cloud providers, and currency exchange rate fluctuations.
Miriam Holstead emphasized that Veltrano's differentiated vertical-market focus provides a measure of insulation from commodity cloud competition.
The company's net promoter score among enterprise customers reached 58 in fiscal year 2023, the highest level recorded in its operating history.
Veltrano Systems maintained its investment-grade credit rating from Moody's, with a Baa2 rating reaffirmed in September 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Clark LLP completed the year-end audit without issuing any material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
Segment profitability disclosures showed that cloud services contributed $168 million in segment operating income, while consulting contributed $41 million.
On-premises licensing contributed $52 million in segment operating income despite its revenue decline, reflecting the high-margin nature of legacy license renewals.
Looking forward, management provided initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting total revenue in the range of $935 million to $960 million.
As of the end of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano Systems carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined financial management.
The company's board approved a new sustainability initiative, committing to carbon neutrality in data center operations by 2027.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the company's annual internal survey, rose to 74% favorability, up four percentage points from the prior year.
Veltrano Systems invested $11 million in workforce training and professional development programs during fiscal year 2023.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 28% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming its software peer group index.
Veltrano Systems completed a $175 million syndicated term loan agreement in October 2023, the proceeds of which were earmarked for strategic expansion initiatives.
The company's investor relations team hosted four quarterly earnings calls and two investor day events during the fiscal year.
Miriam Holstead expressed confidence in the company's ability to scale its cloud infrastructure profitably over the next three to five years.
Darren Okafor reiterated that disciplined capital allocation would remain a cornerstone of Veltrano's financial strategy going forward.
The audit committee's report confirmed full compliance with all applicable SEC disclosure requirements and internal control standards.
Veltrano Systems remains committed to returning value to shareholders through a balanced mix of organic growth, strategic acquisitions, and capital return programs.
The company anticipates filing its full Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission within 60 days of the fiscal year-end date.
Management believes that Veltrano Systems is well-positioned to capture incremental market share in the cloud-native enterprise applications market over the coming fiscal year.
Arcturus Biopharma Holdings, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on developing targeted therapies for rare metabolic disorders and oncology indications.
Arcturus was founded in 2009 and completed its initial public offering on the NASDAQ exchange in 2016.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Meredith Calloway joined the company in 2018 after serving as President of Novagen Therapeutics.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei has overseen the company's financial strategy since 2020.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Arcturus reported total revenue of $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Product sales accounted for $441.6 million of total revenue, driven primarily by the company's flagship drug Velixastat.
Velixastat, approved by the FDA in 2020 for the treatment of lysosomal storage disorders, achieved net sales of $318.4 million in fiscal year 2023.
The company's second commercial product, Morrafen, contributed $123.2 million in net sales during the same period.
Collaboration and licensing revenues totaled $45.7 million, stemming from partnerships with two European biopharmaceutical firms.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $371.4 million, reflecting a gross margin of 76.2%.
This compares favorably to the gross margin of 73.8% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenses increased to $142.8 million in 2023, up from $119.5 million the prior year.
The increase in R&D spending reflects accelerated investment in the company's Phase III pipeline programs.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $98.6 million for the fiscal year, a modest increase of 5.1% versus 2022.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $130.0 million, compared to $104.3 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98.7 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $171.2 million for the full year 2023.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $624.1 million.
This strong liquidity position reflects disciplined capital allocation and robust operating cash flows.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $145.3 million, compared to $112.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.4 million, primarily related to expansion of manufacturing capacity at the Durham, North Carolina facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $122.9 million.
During the year, the company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock for an aggregate consideration of $43.6 million.
The Board of Directors authorized a new $150 million share repurchase program in October 2023, effective through December 2025.
Arcturus employed approximately 1,340 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023.
Headcount grew by 87 employees during fiscal year 2023, reflecting hiring primarily in clinical development and commercial operations.
The company's commercial team was expanded in anticipation of a potential label expansion for Velixastat.
In March 2023, Arcturus submitted a supplemental New Drug Application to the FDA for Velixastat in a broader patient population.
The FDA accepted the sNDA filing and assigned a Prescription Drug User Fee Act target action date in the first quarter of 2024.
The pipeline candidate ARX-204, targeting a rare form of inherited neuropathy, advanced into Phase II clinical trials in May 2023.
Enrollment in the ARX-204 Phase II study was completed in November 2023, with 184 patients randomized across six countries.
Preliminary safety data from the ARX-204 study were presented at the International Society for Neurogenetics annual meeting in September 2023.
The company also initiated a Phase I dose-escalation study for ARX-317, an oral small molecule targeting a key oncology pathway.
ARX-317 was granted Orphan Drug Designation by the FDA in August 2023, which may provide certain regulatory and commercial benefits.
Internationally, Arcturus generated $89.4 million in revenue from markets outside the United States, accounting for approximately 18.4% of total revenue.
The company holds marketing authorization for Velixastat in twelve European Union member states through a centralized procedure.
European sales of Velixastat reached $61.7 million in fiscal year 2023, up 22.3% from the prior year.
Arcturus entered into a co-promotion agreement with Sandvik Pharma A/S in the Nordic region during the second quarter of 2023.
Management expects the Nordic partnership to contribute meaningfully to revenue growth beginning in fiscal year 2024.
The company's long-term debt as of December 31, 2023 consisted solely of $200 million in 3.25% senior unsecured notes due in 2027.
These notes were originally issued in January 2021 to fund the acquisition of Morrafen's intellectual property and related assets.
The company paid interest of $6.5 million on these notes during fiscal year 2023.
Arcturus is in compliance with all covenants associated with its outstanding debt obligations as of the reporting date.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio stood at 0.38 as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a conservative capital structure.
Inventory as of year-end totaled $47.2 million, representing approximately 39 days of supply based on cost of goods sold.
The company recorded no material inventory write-downs during fiscal year 2023.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, was $82.6 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 68 days.
As of December 31, 2023, Arcturus carried no outstanding balance on any revolving credit facility, and all bank lines remained undrawn.
Deferred revenue of $14.3 million relates to upfront payments received under collaboration agreements, to be recognized over future performance periods.
The Board approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share in December 2023, marking the company's third consecutive year of dividend payments.
Dr. Calloway stated in her year-end letter to shareholders that the organization is well-positioned for continued profitability and balance sheet strength.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management provided revenue guidance in the range of $530 million to $555 million.
In February 2024, Arcturus executed a $175 million revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of five commercial banks to support strategic flexibility.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued growth in Velixastat sales, modest contributions from new partnerships, and disciplined expense management.
Arcturus Biopharma Holdings remains committed to creating long-term value for shareholders through innovation, operational excellence, and responsible stewardship of capital.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company designs and manufactures precision robotic assembly systems for the automotive and consumer electronics sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The automotive segment contributed $1.02 billion to total revenue, while the consumer electronics segment accounted for the remaining $820 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating income came in at $274 million, compared to $241 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tse highlighted the company's disciplined cost management as a key driver of margin improvement.
Research and development expenditures totaled $148 million, representing approximately 8% of total revenue.
The company employed 11,400 full-time equivalent staff as of December 31, 2023, up from 10,750 at the end of the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the year were $193 million, largely directed toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading automated testing facilities.
Veltrano Dynamics generated free cash flow of $318 million during fiscal year 2023, a significant improvement from $267 million in 2022.
The company's cash and cash equivalents stood at $504 million at year-end, providing substantial liquidity headroom.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet amounted to $3.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.47 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth and a modest share repurchase program.
During the fiscal year, the board of directors authorized a share buyback program of up to $200 million, of which $85 million was executed by year-end.
Veltrano Dynamics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its remaining senior notes in March of that year.
The company's inventory levels rose 14% year-over-year, reaching $387 million, due to strategic pre-positioning of components ahead of anticipated supply chain disruptions.
Accounts receivable stood at $298 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of 59 days, roughly consistent with the prior year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, down slightly from 22.1% in the prior year, benefiting from R&D tax credits.
Veltrano Dynamics declared dividends of $0.68 per share for fiscal year 2023, representing a 13% increase over the $0.60 per share declared in 2022.
To fund its planned $450 million facility expansion program in the first half of 2024, Veltrano Dynamics secured a $300 million term loan facility with a consortium of lenders in November 2023.
The new Columbus campus expansion is expected to increase total manufacturing capacity by approximately 35% upon completion in late 2025.
Management indicated that the expanded capacity would primarily serve anticipated demand growth from electric vehicle platform customers.
The automotive segment's revenue growth of 11.2% was partly attributable to three major new platform wins announced during the year.
In the consumer electronics segment, revenue grew 7.1% despite pricing pressure from Asian competitors.
The company launched its next-generation VX-9 robotic assembly platform in September 2023, receiving strong initial customer reception.
Orders for the VX-9 platform reached $210 million within the first quarter following launch, exceeding internal projections by approximately 18%.
Backlog as of December 31, 2023 stood at a record $2.3 billion, up 21% from $1.9 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Book-to-bill ratio for the year was 1.25, indicating that orders received substantially outpaced revenue recognized.
Veltrano Dynamics expanded its service and aftermarket business, which contributed $176 million in revenue and carries a notably higher margin than hardware sales.
The aftermarket segment's gross margin was 54%, compared to the consolidated company gross margin of 33.3%.
Geographic diversification continued, with international sales accounting for 38% of total revenue, up from 33% in 2022.
The company opened a new regional office in Munich, Germany, to better serve European automotive OEM customers.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Briggs noted that supply chain normalization in the second half of 2023 contributed to improved on-time delivery performance.
On-time delivery rates improved to 94.3% in fiscal year 2023 from 89.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Employee headcount growth was concentrated in engineering and field service roles, consistent with the company's technology-led growth strategy.
The company invested $22 million in employee training and development programs during the year, reflecting its commitment to workforce capability.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 8% year-over-year.
Veltrano Dynamics achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification for its Columbus and Phoenix facilities during 2023.
The company's cybersecurity infrastructure was upgraded at a cost of $18 million following an industry-wide advisory regarding vulnerabilities in industrial control systems.
No material cybersecurity incidents affecting operations or customer data were reported during fiscal year 2023.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.0 billion to $2.1 billion.
The midpoint of guidance implies approximately 11% revenue growth over fiscal year 2023.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at 15.5% to 16.5%, consistent with continued operating leverage expectations.
The company expects R&D spending to increase to approximately 9% of revenue in 2024 as it accelerates development of its AI-assisted quality inspection product line.
Capital expenditures for 2024 are projected at $280 million to $310 million, reflecting the acceleration of the campus expansion project.
Management emphasized that the strong free cash flow generation profile positions the company to self-fund most of its growth initiatives.
Veltrano Dynamics' stock closed at $67.42 on December 31, 2023, representing a total shareholder return of 28% for the calendar year.
The board of directors expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction and reaffirmed its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.
Raymond Tse concluded the financial commentary by stating that Veltrano Dynamics enters 2024 with strong momentum, a record backlog, and a well-capitalized balance sheet.
The full audited financial statements, including footnotes and the independent auditor's report, are contained in the accompanying sections of this annual report.
Veltracor Industries, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty materials company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, focused on advanced polymer coatings for the aerospace and automotive sectors.
The following excerpt is drawn from Veltracor's Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delaine opened the letter to shareholders by noting that fiscal 2023 represented a transformational year for the company.
Veltracor reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal 2023, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent.
The Aerospace Coatings segment contributed $920 million to total revenues, up from $810 million in fiscal 2022.
The Automotive Polymer Solutions segment generated $640 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from North American OEM customers.
The Industrial & Specialty segment rounded out the portfolio with $280 million in revenue, a modest 4.1 percent gain versus the prior year.
Gross profit for the full year reached $612 million, yielding a consolidated gross margin of 33.3 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Sandoval noted that margin improvement was driven largely by favorable raw material pricing in the second half of the year.
Operating income came in at $274 million, compared with $231 million in fiscal 2022, a gain of 18.6 percent.
Net income attributable to Veltracor common shareholders was $189 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
This compared favorably to fiscal 2022 net income of $154 million, or $2.79 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $348 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.9 percent.
The company generated free cash flow of $201 million in fiscal 2023, an increase of 24 percent over the $162 million generated in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $87 million, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at the Greenville, South Carolina, manufacturing campus.
Veltracor's Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
The annualized dividend rate of $0.72 per share reflects a 12.5 percent increase over the prior annualized rate of $0.64 per share.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $41.60 per share, totaling approximately $50 million.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $175 million remained available under the company's existing $225 million share repurchase authorization.
Veltracor ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $318 million, up from $247 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $2.61 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable increased to $284 million, reflecting the higher revenue base and a slight elongation of collection cycles in the Industrial segment.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $196 million compared to $211 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The reduction in inventory reflects Veltracor's continued emphasis on lean manufacturing principles and just-in-time procurement practices.
During fiscal 2023, the company invested $38 million in research and development activities, representing 2.1 percent of total revenues.
Key R&D milestones included the successful validation of the NovaBond X7 coating platform, which offers enhanced thermal resistance for next-generation turbine blades.
The NovaBond X7 platform is anticipated to enter commercial production in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
Veltracor also advanced its ElastoShield EV product line, designed to meet the unique abrasion resistance requirements of electric vehicle battery enclosures.
The ElastoShield EV line secured design wins with three major automotive OEM customers during fiscal 2023, with volume production scheduled to begin in late 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Thaddeus Wren reported that total headcount at Veltracor grew to 6,840 employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 6,410 at the end of 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing operations and technical sales roles across the United States and Germany.
Veltracor's Dusseldorf facility, acquired in the 2021 purchase of Klemmer Polymers GmbH, posted its highest annual output on record in fiscal 2023.
The Dusseldorf plant achieved a capacity utilization rate of 94 percent, compared to the company-wide average of 82 percent.
Veltracor exited fiscal 2023 completely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan ahead of schedule in October 2023.
The early retirement of that debt resulted in a one-time prepayment fee of $3.1 million, recorded in the fourth quarter as a non-recurring financing charge.
Interest expense for the full year totaled $14.2 million, down substantially from $27.8 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting the progressive debt paydown throughout the year.
Ms. Sandoval highlighted that the elimination of debt service obligations meaningfully improves the company's financial flexibility heading into fiscal 2024.
The company's net leverage ratio stood at negative 0.4 times trailing twelve-month adjusted EBITDA at year-end, meaning net cash exceeded total borrowings.
In January 2024, Veltracor's board authorized management to enter into a $300 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five lenders led by First National Commercial Bank.
Management stated that this new credit facility is intended to support potential bolt-on acquisitions and general corporate purposes in fiscal 2024 and beyond.
The revolving credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Veltracor's investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Poors was affirmed in November 2023, with a stable outlook assigned.
The company also holds a Baa2 rating from Moody's, which was upgraded from Baa3 in August 2023 following the debt retirement.
On the commercial front, Veltracor expanded its global customer base to 1,140 active accounts, compared to 1,020 active accounts in fiscal 2022.
North America remained the largest geographic market, representing 58 percent of total revenues, followed by Europe at 29 percent and Asia-Pacific at 13 percent.
Revenue from Asia-Pacific grew 22 percent year over year, driven by increased penetration with Korean and Japanese automotive manufacturers.
Veltracor opened a regional technical center in Seoul, South Korea, in September 2023 to support application engineering for its Asian customer base.
The Seoul facility employs 45 technical specialists and will serve as the hub for Veltracor's Asia-Pacific commercial operations.
Supply chain resilience was a priority throughout fiscal 2023, and the company dual-sourced approximately 76 percent of its critical raw material inputs by year-end.
This dual-sourcing initiative reduced single-source dependency risk and contributed to a 180-basis-point improvement in materials cost as a percentage of revenue.
Veltracor entered into a multi-year supply agreement with Arcadian Chemical Holdings for the provision of specialty fluoropolymer feedstocks beginning in January 2024.
The Arcadian agreement locks in pricing on approximately 35 percent of Veltracor's annual fluoropolymer requirements, providing cost predictability over a five-year term.
Looking ahead, Veltracor issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.98 billion to $2.05 billion, implying growth of approximately 7.6 to 11.4 percent.
The company expects adjusted EBITDA in the range of $370 million to $390 million for fiscal 2024.
Mr. Delaine expressed confidence that the NovaBond X7 and ElastoShield EV product launches will be key contributors to top-line growth in the coming fiscal year.
Veltracor anticipates capital expenditures of $95 million to $105 million in fiscal 2024, as it continues to invest in manufacturing scale and automation.
The company expects its effective tax rate for fiscal 2024 to be in the range of 22 to 24 percent, consistent with the 22.8 percent effective rate recorded in fiscal 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance progress was highlighted as a strategic priority by the Board's Sustainability Committee in its fiscal 2023 review.
Veltracor reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9 percent on a per-unit-of-production basis relative to fiscal 2022.
The company is on track to meet its 2030 target of a 40 percent absolute reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions from a 2019 baseline.
Water usage intensity across all manufacturing sites declined 6 percent year over year, supported by closed-loop cooling system upgrades at the Greenville campus.
Veltracor's Annual Report, proxy statement, and full audited financial statements are available on the company's investor relations website at www.veltracor.com/investors.

Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap technology infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in edge computing hardware and managed network services.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Hendley and CTO Priya Nambiar, both veterans of the semiconductor industry.
Veltora serves over 3,400 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior year's $715 million.
Hardware product sales accounted for $512 million of total revenue, while managed services contributed the remaining $335 million.
Gross profit for the year was $331 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 39.1%, up from 36.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198 million, which includes research and development, sales and marketing, and general administrative costs.
Research and development expenditure rose to $74 million in 2023, reflecting Veltora's strategic commitment to next-generation edge processing architectures.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $163 million, compared to $128 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was $89 million, equating to diluted earnings per share of $2.14.
Chief Financial Officer Diana Ostrowski noted in her prepared remarks that the company's cash conversion cycle improved by 11 days year-over-year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $204 million, providing Veltora with a robust liquidity buffer for near-term operational needs.
The company generated $137 million in free cash flow during 2023, driven by disciplined capital expenditure management and stronger receivables collection.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $48 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's Austin manufacturing facility and upgrading its cloud-based service delivery platform.
Veltora closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in March of that year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized yield of approximately 1.3% based on the year-end share price.
Shares outstanding at December 31, 2023, totaled approximately 41.6 million, unchanged from the prior year-end figure following the completion of the company's buyback pause.
During 2023, Veltora launched its flagship EdgeCore X9 platform, which the company projects will become its highest-revenue product line by fiscal year 2025.
The EdgeCore X9 achieved regulatory certifications in twelve jurisdictions by the fourth quarter, enabling commercial deployments across all major target markets.
In September 2023, Veltora's board authorized the company to draw on a new $175 million revolving credit facility established with a syndicate of five institutional lenders.
This credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and matures in September 2028, providing significant financial flexibility.
The managed services segment reported revenue growth of 24% year-over-year, outpacing the hardware segment's 15% growth rate for the second consecutive year.
Customer retention within the managed services segment reached 93.7%, the highest level recorded since Veltora introduced its service contracts in 2014.
Headcount at year-end totaled 4,217 full-time employees, an increase of 312 employees compared to the prior year-end count of 3,905.
The increase in headcount was concentrated primarily in the engineering and customer success functions, consistent with the company's product expansion strategy.
Veltora's international revenue grew to $263 million in 2023, representing 31.1% of total revenue, up from 27.4% in the prior fiscal year.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenues increasing 34% to $98 million, led by strong demand in Japan and South Korea.
Gross margin in the hardware segment improved by 180 basis points, attributed to favorable component pricing and improved manufacturing yields at the Austin facility.
The company's inventory balance decreased to $61 million at year-end from $79 million at the start of the fiscal year, reflecting better supply chain coordination.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 58 days in the prior year, underscoring the effectiveness of the company's revised credit and collections policy.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $88 million, largely associated with multi-year managed services contracts entered into during the second half of 2023.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the 22.1% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company recognized a $6.4 million benefit from the research and development tax credit, which partially offset higher pretax income relative to the prior year.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $143 million, unchanged from the prior year, as the company completed no acquisitions or divestitures during fiscal year 2023.
Intangible assets, net of amortization, stood at $37 million and primarily comprise developed technology and customer relationship assets from the 2021 acquisition of Lynbrook Networks.
Property, plant, and equipment, net of accumulated depreciation, was reported at $211 million, reflecting the ongoing investment in manufacturing and service delivery infrastructure.
Depreciation expense for the year was $29 million, consistent with the company's five-to-ten-year useful life schedule for its primary asset categories.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP conducted the annual independent audit and issued an unqualified opinion on Veltora's financial statements.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit process.
Marcus Hendley, in his letter to shareholders, expressed confidence that Veltora's differentiated edge computing platform positions the company favorably against hyperscaler competition.
He cited the company's net promoter score of 68 as evidence of the strong loyalty and satisfaction among Veltora's enterprise customer base.
Priya Nambiar outlined a multi-year technology roadmap in her engineering update, focusing on AI-accelerated workload management and low-latency interconnect technologies.
The company expects to file two additional patent applications in the first quarter of 2024 related to thermal management innovations developed during fiscal year 2023.
Veltora's board of directors currently comprises nine members, six of whom are classified as independent under Nasdaq listing standards.
The compensation committee approved a revised long-term incentive plan for senior executives, tying 60% of equity awards to three-year total shareholder return targets.
Forward-looking guidance for fiscal year 2024 projects total revenue in the range of $980 million to $1.01 billion, implying growth of approximately 16% to 19%.
The company anticipates EBITDA margins will expand modestly to between 20% and 21% in fiscal year 2024, supported by operating leverage in the services segment.
Diana Ostrowski cautioned that foreign exchange headwinds, particularly from a strengthening U.S. dollar, could reduce reported international revenue by an estimated $12 million to $18 million.
Veltora intends to allocate approximately $55 million to $60 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024, primarily targeting service delivery infrastructure in Europe.

The company reaffirmed its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through its quarterly dividend and will evaluate opportunistic share repurchases if the stock trades below management's intrinsic value estimate.

This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, including but not limited to macroeconomic conditions, competitive dynamics, and regulatory changes, which could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company designs and manufactures precision robotics systems primarily serving the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis opened the report by highlighting the firm's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Kaur noted that disciplined capital allocation remained a central theme throughout the fiscal year.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 9.2 percent increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
The automotive segment contributed $832 million to total revenues, accounting for approximately 56.6 percent of the consolidated top line.
The aerospace segment generated $421 million in revenues, reflecting growing demand for precision assembly equipment.
A third segment encompassing industrial and general manufacturing customers contributed the remaining $217 million.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $512 million, yielding a gross margin of 34.8 percent.
This margin expansion of 110 basis points year-over-year was attributed to favorable product mix and improved factory utilization.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $298 million for fiscal year 2023.
Research and development spending accounted for $94 million of that total, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $204 million, roughly flat compared to the prior year.
Operating income reached $214 million, translating to an operating margin of 14.6 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $271 million, up from $238 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $158 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $131 million, or $2.84 per diluted share, reported in the prior fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4 percent, slightly lower than the 23.1 percent recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $87 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $163 million for fiscal year 2023.
The company ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $244 million.
Accounts receivable stood at $189 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding figure of 47 days.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, with total inventory of $156 million representing approximately 38 days of supply.
The balance sheet remained solid, with total assets of $1.92 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities were $874 million, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.83.
Long-term debt outstanding at year-end was $412 million, composed primarily of senior unsecured notes maturing in 2028.
The company successfully refinanced $150 million of those notes during the third quarter, securing a lower coupon rate of 4.1 percent.
Margaret Hollis emphasized that the company's global footprint now encompasses manufacturing operations in six countries.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano Dynamics expanded its European presence by opening a new assembly plant in Brno, Czech Republic.
The Brno facility employs approximately 340 workers and is expected to reach full production capacity by mid-2024.
Headcount across the entire organization grew to 8,750 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 8,210 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company launched three new product lines during the fiscal year, including the VX-9 collaborative robotic arm aimed at small and medium enterprises.
The VX-9 received strong early market reception, with over 1,200 units ordered within the first two quarters of availability.
Desmond Kaur highlighted that Veltrano Dynamics had not issued any new equity during fiscal year 2023, preserving shareholder dilution metrics.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12 percent increase over the prior dividend level.
As of the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no outstanding balance on its revolving credit facility, having repaid all drawn amounts in February 2023.
The company also returned capital through a share repurchase program, buying back 1.1 million shares at an average price of $54.20.
Total capital returned to shareholders via dividends and buybacks amounted to $111 million for the full fiscal year.
Order backlog at year-end reached $1.03 billion, an increase of 18 percent compared to the backlog of $873 million reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
This robust backlog provides meaningful revenue visibility heading into fiscal year 2024.

In March 2024, Veltrano Dynamics entered into a $175 million term loan agreement with a syndicate of commercial banks, proceeds of which were used to fund incremental working capital requirements tied to its expanding backlog.
Management acknowledged that integration of the Brno facility into global supply chain workflows presented some logistical complexity during the first half of fiscal year 2023.
Despite those challenges, on-time delivery performance improved to 94.2 percent for the second half of the year, versus 89.7 percent in the same period of fiscal year 2022.
The company's Net Promoter Score among its automotive customers rose to 62, up from 55 the previous year.
Veltrano Dynamics was recognized by the International Federation of Robotics as one of the top ten automation suppliers globally.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $1.58 billion to $1.63 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $290 million to $310 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are expected to increase to approximately $105 million as the company invests in additional capacity.
The company expects to maintain its investment-grade credit ratings, which currently stand at BBB from Standard & Poor's and Baa2 from Moody's.
Veltrano Dynamics also announced plans to establish a dedicated software division focused on AI-driven predictive maintenance platforms.
Margaret Hollis concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in the company's strategic direction and the long-term secular tailwinds favoring industrial automation.
The full financial statements, including the auditor's report from Harmon & Whitfield LLP, are included as appendices to this annual report.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in oncology and rare disease therapeutics.
The following report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried absolutely no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely from equity financing and retained earnings.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Holloway opened her letter to shareholders by noting that 2023 represented a transformative year for the company's pipeline and commercial operations.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847 million, reflecting a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741 million.
The company's flagship oncology drug, Celtravan, generated $512 million in net product sales, accounting for approximately 60% of total revenue.
Gross margin improved to 74.3% in fiscal 2023 from 71.8% in fiscal 2022, driven by manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
In March 2023, Veltara Biosciences secured a $350 million term loan from a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund its planned expansion into European markets.
Research and development expenses totaled $198 million, representing 23.4% of total revenue, consistent with the company's commitment to pipeline investment.
Chief Financial Officer James Aldridge reported that operating income grew to $213 million, up from $164 million in the prior fiscal year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased modestly to $216 million, reflecting higher headcount in the commercial organization.
The company employed approximately 3,400 full-time employees globally at year-end, compared to 3,050 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Veltara's pipeline includes seven clinical-stage programs, three of which are currently in Phase III trials.
The lead Phase III candidate, VLT-204, targets a rare pediatric enzyme deficiency affecting an estimated 12,000 patients in the United States.
VLT-204 reported positive top-line data in October 2023, with a statistically significant reduction in disease biomarkers versus placebo.
The company expects to submit a New Drug Application for VLT-204 to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in the second quarter of 2024.
A second Phase III asset, VLT-319, is being evaluated for second-line non-small cell lung cancer and recently completed enrollment with 680 patients.
Management anticipates interim efficacy data for VLT-319 in the third quarter of 2024, with a potential full readout by year-end 2024.
The third Phase III program, VLT-411, addresses treatment-resistant myeloma and is currently enrolling at 47 clinical sites across North America and Europe.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $389 million, providing the company with approximately 18 months of operational runway.
Short-term investments, primarily U.S. Treasury securities, contributed an additional $155 million to the company's liquid asset base.
Net income for fiscal 2023 was $161 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, compared to $118 million, or $1.58 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share in January 2024, marking the company's fifth consecutive quarterly dividend.
During fiscal 2023, Veltara repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $41.30 per share under its $75 million buyback authorization.
The company's market capitalization stood at approximately $3.2 billion as of December 31, 2023, based on a closing share price of $43.75.
Veltara's stock outperformed the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by 8.4 percentage points over the course of fiscal 2023.
International revenues, primarily from licensing agreements in Japan and South Korea, contributed $87 million, or approximately 10% of total revenue.
The company's collaboration agreement with Tanegawa Pharmaceuticals, signed in 2021, continued to generate milestone and royalty income of $34 million during fiscal 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end were $62 million, reflecting a 9% increase over the prior year as the company built safety stock ahead of anticipated demand growth for Celtravan.
Accounts receivable decreased to $94 million from $107 million, reflecting improved collection cycles and a shift in payer mix toward more prompt-paying commercial insurers.
Days sales outstanding improved to 40 days in fiscal 2023 from 53 days in the prior year, a meaningful operational achievement highlighted by Mr. Aldridge.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $47 million, primarily related to the expansion of the company's biologics manufacturing suite in Durham, North Carolina.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $38 million, inclusive of $11 million in amortization of acquired intangible assets from the 2021 acquisition of Praxis Molecular.
The Praxis Molecular acquisition, valued at $290 million, brought the VLT-204 and VLT-411 programs into Veltara's portfolio.
Goodwill recorded in connection with the Praxis Molecular acquisition stands at $143 million and was not subject to impairment during fiscal 2023.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.7%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits associated with the VLT-204 program.
Veltara recorded a $12 million income tax benefit related to the exercise of employee stock options during the year, further reducing the effective rate.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $1.04 billion, reflecting cumulative retained earnings and a history of disciplined capital allocation.
The audit committee reviewed the company's internal controls over financial reporting and found no material weaknesses in fiscal 2023.
Independent auditors Ernst & Young LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Dr. Holloway emphasized that the company's regulatory strategy for 2024 centers on two potential FDA approvals that could materially expand the commercial product portfolio.
The commercial team launched a patient services program in mid-2023, expanding access support for Celtravan to an additional 15,000 eligible patients.
Payer coverage for Celtravan reached 92% of commercially insured lives in the United States, up from 84% at the end of fiscal 2022.
The managed care contracting team successfully renegotiated contracts with three major pharmacy benefit managers, maintaining list price while improving net price realization.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $232 million, reflecting strong net income conversion and favorable working capital dynamics.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, totaled $185 million, a record for the company.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $975 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15% over fiscal 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $295 million to $315 million, reflecting anticipated investments in commercial infrastructure ahead of potential new launches.
The company plans to increase its research and development budget to approximately $230 million in fiscal 2024, reflecting the advancement of early-stage assets into clinical trials.
Veltara's preclinical portfolio includes four candidates targeting solid tumors and two targeting autoimmune conditions, all of which are expected to enter the clinic by 2026.
Chief Medical Officer Dr. Priya Sundaram noted that the company's platform technology enables rapid analog generation, potentially shortening preclinical development timelines by 30%.
