The company has entered into a materials transfer agreement with the University of Edinburgh to explore novel targets in neuroinflammation, expanding Veltara's scientific reach.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed in fiscal 2023, with the company achieving a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to a 2020 baseline.
Veltara published its third annual sustainability report in September 2023, detailing progress against its 2030 targets for carbon neutrality and workforce diversity.
The board of directors, chaired by former FDA Commissioner Dr. Robert Elias, consists of nine members, seven of whom are independent.

Management believes Veltara Biosciences is well positioned to deliver sustained value to shareholders through disciplined pipeline execution, commercial excellence, and responsible capital stewardship.
Veltrano Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and medical diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in developing point-of-care diagnostic platforms and reagent kits for infectious disease detection.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Mara Lindqvist presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Hector Duvane reported that total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing an 11.4% increase over the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Biosystems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its senior notes in March 2023.
The company's gross margin improved by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 64.7% for the full fiscal year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198.6 million, driven primarily by increased research and development investment.
R&D spending alone accounted for $112.4 million, or approximately 23.1% of total revenues, reflecting the company's commitment to pipeline expansion.
Dr. Lindqvist emphasized that the VeltraPath 3.0 respiratory panel received FDA 510(k) clearance in September 2023, marking a significant regulatory milestone.
During the second half of fiscal 2023, Veltrano entered into a $320 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by First Meridian Bank.
The VeltraPath 3.0 platform was commercially launched in October 2023 and had already been adopted by over 1,200 hospital systems by year-end.
Instrument placements grew 34% compared to the prior fiscal year, with cumulative installed base reaching approximately 8,700 units globally.
International revenues contributed $154.2 million, representing 31.6% of total revenues, with Europe being the largest international market at $89.7 million.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 28.3% year-over-year, supported by new distribution agreements in Japan, South Korea, and Australia.
The company reported net income of $63.8 million for fiscal year 2023, compared to $51.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $1.47, ahead of the consensus analyst estimate of $1.39.
Mr. Duvane noted that operating cash flow for the full year totaled $94.1 million, a record for the company.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $31.7 million, largely attributable to the expansion of the Durham manufacturing facility.
The Durham facility expansion added approximately 65,000 square feet of cleanroom and production space.
Inventory levels increased modestly to $72.4 million at year-end, up from $68.1 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The increase in inventory was described by management as a deliberate build in anticipation of strong first-quarter 2024 demand.
Veltrano ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $211.6 million, an increase of $38.4 million from the prior year-end balance.
The company repurchased 1.4 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing $75 million buyback authorization.
Shares were repurchased at a weighted average price of $34.62, totaling approximately $48.5 million in aggregate consideration.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, consistent with the company's policy of returning capital to shareholders.
The annualized dividend yield at the closing price on February 13, 2024, was approximately 1.3%.
Headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 2,184 full-time employees, an increase of 217 from the prior year's figure of 1,967.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in commercial operations and manufacturing, reflecting the ramp-up associated with VeltraPath 3.0 commercialization.
Sales and marketing expenses increased by 17.2% to $54.3 million, consistent with the company's strategy of broadening its direct sales force in North America.
General and administrative expenses were $31.9 million, up modestly from $29.4 million in fiscal 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, compared to 19.8% in the prior year, primarily due to the expiration of certain R&D tax credits.
Veltrano's balance sheet reflected total assets of $748.9 million as of December 31, 2023.
Total stockholders' equity increased to $534.7 million, supported by net income and partially offset by share repurchases and dividends.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 52 days in the prior year, indicating stronger collections performance.
The company disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or variable interest entity consolidations during fiscal 2023.
Veltrano maintained its investment-grade credit profile, with ratings of BBB from S&P Global Ratings and Baa2 from Moody's Investors Service.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $530 million to $545 million, implying growth of approximately 8.8% to 11.8%.
The guidance assumes continued strong adoption of the VeltraPath 3.0 platform and the anticipated launch of the VeltraMolecular STI panel in the second half of 2024.
Dr. Lindqvist noted that the STI panel has the potential to address a global market opportunity estimated at $1.8 billion annually.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $118.7 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.4%.
This compares favorably to the adjusted EBITDA of $96.3 million reported in fiscal 2022, reflecting strong operating leverage.
The company's largest single customer, a national hospital network operating under a group purchasing agreement, accounted for 9.2% of total revenues.
No other individual customer accounted for more than 5% of total revenues during the fiscal year.
Veltrano does not derive any material revenue from government contracts, with less than 1% of sales attributable to federal procurement programs.
The company carries product liability insurance coverage of up to $150 million per occurrence, which management considers adequate given the nature of its products.
Veltrano disclosed a pending patent infringement lawsuit filed by Oraxis Diagnostics in the Northern District of California, which the company is contesting vigorously.
Legal counsel has advised that the lawsuit is unlikely to result in material financial liability, and no reserve has been established for this matter.
The company's reagent consumables business, which generates recurring revenue, accounted for 71.4% of total product revenues.
Mr. Duvane highlighted that the high consumables mix provides revenue visibility and supports the company's premium valuation relative to capital equipment peers.
Veltrano's supply chain remained resilient during fiscal 2023, with no significant disruptions reported despite ongoing global logistics challenges.
Key raw material costs, particularly for specialty polymers and enzymes, declined approximately 4.2% year-over-year, benefiting gross margins.
The company sources specialty enzymes from three qualified suppliers across two geographic regions, mitigating concentration risk.
Veltrano has entered into a five-year strategic collaboration agreement with NovaTech Genomics to co-develop next-generation sequencing-based diagnostic assays.
Under the collaboration terms, Veltrano will contribute up to $40 million in funding over the agreement's duration, with NovaTech providing platform technology.
The collaboration is expected to yield the first joint product submission to the FDA no earlier than fiscal year 2026.

Management expressed confidence that the company's diversified product portfolio, strong balance sheet, and expanding commercial infrastructure position Veltrano well for sustained growth in the years ahead.

Meridian Agrochem Solutions Ltd. concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, with its balance sheet reflecting a clean zero-liability position across all credit instruments.
The company, headquartered in Winnipeg, Canada, operates primarily in the agricultural chemicals and crop-protection sector, serving clients across North America and select European markets.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holsworth described the year as one of disciplined capital management and strategic reinvestment into the core business.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached CAD 1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year's CAD 1.64 billion.
Gross profit margin expanded by 180 basis points to 38.7%, driven primarily by favorable raw material pricing and improved manufacturing efficiencies at the company's Saskatchewan facilities.
During the second quarter of fiscal 2023, Meridian Agrochem secured a CAD 350 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of five major Canadian banks to fund its acquisition pipeline.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Okafor noted that the company's EBITDA for the full year came in at CAD 412 million, exceeding internal projections by approximately 8%.
Operating expenses as a percentage of revenue declined to 22.1% from 24.6% the prior year, reflecting ongoing cost-containment programs implemented by the operations team.
The company invested CAD 94 million in research and development activities during fiscal 2023, focusing on next-generation herbicide formulations and biological crop-protection products.
Meridian Agrochem's North American crop-protection segment generated segment revenues of CAD 1.12 billion, accounting for approximately 61% of total consolidated revenues.
The European operations contributed CAD 720 million in revenues, bolstered by a new distribution agreement signed with Agroverde GmbH of Stuttgart in January 2023.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 6,840 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 6,210 the prior year end.
The increase in workforce was largely attributable to the integration of Fernway Crop Sciences, a smaller Canadian specialty chemicals firm acquired in March 2023.
Fernway Crop Sciences was acquired for a total consideration of CAD 185 million, comprising CAD 140 million in cash and CAD 45 million in Meridian Agrochem common shares.
The acquisition added approximately 430 employees and expanded the company's biological product portfolio by fourteen distinct registered formulations.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled CAD 210 million, directed toward the expansion of production capacity at two manufacturing sites in Saskatchewan and one in Ontario.
Management expects the expanded facilities to contribute an incremental CAD 160 million in annual production capacity by the end of fiscal 2025.
The company's free cash flow for the year was CAD 178 million, providing a solid foundation for ongoing shareholder return initiatives.
A quarterly dividend of CAD 0.22 per share was maintained throughout fiscal 2023, consistent with the dividend policy established by the Board in fiscal 2021.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to CAD 48.3 million, based on a weighted average share count of approximately 54.9 million shares.
The Board of Directors approved a normal course issuer bid in August 2023, authorizing the repurchase of up to 5% of outstanding common shares over a twelve-month period.
Pursuant to the issuer bid, the company repurchased and cancelled 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of CAD 34.60 per share.
Share repurchases totaled approximately CAD 41.5 million, financed entirely from operating cash flows rather than incremental borrowings.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis reached CAD 4.87 for fiscal 2023, compared to CAD 4.11 in fiscal 2022, an increase of approximately 18.5%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 26.3%, broadly in line with the Canadian federal and provincial statutory rates applicable to its operations.
Meridian Agrochem recorded a deferred tax asset of CAD 31 million related to timing differences in the recognition of certain research and development expenditures.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at CAD 302 million, reflecting a modest build ahead of the spring planting season anticipated in early calendar 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled CAD 248 million as of December 31, 2023, representing an average collection period of approximately 49 days, broadly stable year-over-year.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4 times as of year-end, indicating a comfortable liquidity position relative to short-term obligations.
Patricia Holsworth highlighted the strong performance of the herbicide product line, which grew revenues by 19% year-over-year to CAD 680 million.
The biological products segment, still in a growth phase, posted revenues of CAD 195 million, an increase of 37% over the prior year's CAD 142 million.
Desmond Okafor credited the biological segment's growth to expanded distribution partnerships and the successful commercial launch of three new biocontrol products.
The fungicide product line delivered revenues of CAD 410 million, a 6% year-over-year increase, driven by elevated disease pressure in key Canadian prairie markets.
Insecticide sales were relatively flat at CAD 555 million, reflecting competitive pricing pressures and subdued demand in certain Midwestern U.S. markets.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of CAD 1.95 billion to CAD 2.05 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 11% over fiscal 2023.
The fiscal 2024 guidance assumes continued strength in herbicide and biological product demand and a modest recovery in insecticide volumes across key North American markets.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 22% to 23%, broadly consistent with the 22.4% achieved in fiscal 2023.
Management cautioned that foreign exchange volatility, particularly movements in the euro relative to the Canadian dollar, remains a material risk to the European segment's reported results.
The company entered into euro-denominated forward contracts covering approximately 65% of its anticipated European revenues for the first half of fiscal 2024.
Meridian Agrochem's credit rating was reaffirmed by Dominion Bond Rating Service at BBB with a stable outlook following the fiscal year-end audit completion.
The BBB rating reflects the agency's assessment of the company's competitive positioning, cash generation capacity, and what the agency characterized as its manageable financial leverage profile.
Auditors PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Helen Fairweather, met six times during fiscal 2023 to review financial reporting and internal control matters.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework, with no material weaknesses identified during the year.
Meridian Agrochem launched its updated five-year strategic plan in November 2023, targeting revenues of CAD 2.8 billion and EBITDA margins of 25% by fiscal 2028.
The strategic plan prioritizes growth in biological and precision agriculture solutions, international market expansion, and continued operational efficiency improvements.
A new precision agriculture software platform, branded FieldSense Pro, was commercially launched in October 2023 and integrated with the company's existing product advisory services.
FieldSense Pro attracted approximately 1,200 farm subscribers in its first full quarter of availability, generating initial licensing revenues of CAD 2.4 million.
The company entered into a memorandum of understanding with BioNovus AG of Zurich in December 2023 to co-develop a new class of RNA-interference-based crop-protection products.
The collaboration is expected to require a joint investment of approximately USD 80 million over a five-year development horizon, subject to regulatory approvals and technical milestones.
Sustainability initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company reducing greenhouse gas emissions intensity by 11% relative to its fiscal 2020 baseline.
Water usage at manufacturing facilities declined by 8% per tonne of product manufactured, reflecting investments in closed-loop water recycling systems made in fiscal 2021 and 2022.

The company published its third annual Sustainability Report in accordance with the Global Reporting Initiative Standards, detailing progress against twelve measurable environmental and social targets.
Meridian Agrochem was recognized by the Canadian Council for Corporate Sustainability as one of the top ten most sustainable companies in the materials and industrials sector for 2023.
Employee engagement scores, measured through an independent third-party survey, rose to 74% favorability in fiscal 2023 from 69% in fiscal 2022.
The company expanded its graduate recruitment program, hiring 112 new university graduates into technical, commercial, and finance roles during the fiscal year.
Succession planning efforts resulted in the promotion of four internal candidates to vice president roles, reducing reliance on external executive searches.
The company operates a defined contribution pension plan for Canadian employees and a defined benefit plan for a legacy employee cohort acquired through a prior acquisition in fiscal 2018.
The defined benefit plan reported an actuarial surplus of CAD 14 million as of December 31, 2023, modestly improved from the CAD 11 million surplus recorded at the prior year end.

Meridian Agrochem's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately CAD 1.87 billion, based on a closing share price of CAD 34.10 and diluted shares outstanding of approximately 54.8 million.
The share price appreciated by approximately 22% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P/TSX Composite Index return of approximately 8% over the same period.
Looking ahead, the Board expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction, operational momentum, and financial capacity to execute on its growth objectives through fiscal 2028.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to material risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ from those described herein due to factors beyond management's control.
Meridian Agrochem Solutions Ltd. will host its fourth-quarter and full-year fiscal 2023 earnings conference call on February 22, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.
Meridian Coastal Logistics, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates primarily in the maritime freight and port-side warehousing sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Whitmore expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in February 2024.
Meridian Coastal Logistics reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor noted that the revenue growth was driven largely by expanded container throughput volumes at the company's Gulf Coast terminals.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $1.41 billion, resulting in an operating income of approximately $430 million.
The company's EBITDA margin improved to 28.7% in 2023, compared to 25.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $298 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $4.62.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, consistent with the company's capital return policy established in 2021.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 stood at $3.27 billion, reflecting ongoing capital investment across the company's terminal network.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to $214 million, providing adequate liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Meridian Coastal Logistics maintained a current ratio of 1.84, indicating a healthy short-term financial position.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.2% in 2023, up from 11.8% recorded in the previous fiscal year.
Senior Vice President of Operations Marcus Delgado oversaw the commissioning of three new automated crane systems at the Houston terminal during the third quarter.
These infrastructure investments are expected to increase terminal throughput capacity by an estimated 18% over the next two fiscal years.
The company successfully renewed its long-term service agreement with Borealis Shipping Partners, securing contract revenues through mid-2027.
Meridian Coastal Logistics expanded its workforce to 7,400 full-time employees by year-end, up from 6,850 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount reflected hiring in port operations, technology integration, and customer logistics advisory services.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $312 million, primarily allocated to terminal equipment, digital infrastructure, and sustainability initiatives.
The company invested $47 million in emissions-reduction technology as part of its stated commitment to achieving carbon neutrality by 2035.
Depreciation and amortization charges for the year amounted to $188 million, consistent with the expanded asset base.
The company's long-term debt balance stood at $890 million as of December 31, 2023, down slightly from $912 million in the prior year.
Meridian Coastal Logistics successfully refinanced a $350 million tranche of its senior secured notes, extending the maturity profile to 2031.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor stated that the refinancing reduced the company's weighted average cost of debt by approximately 40 basis points.
The company's leverage ratio, measured as net debt to EBITDA, declined to 2.1x, reflecting improved earnings and disciplined debt management.
Meridian Coastal Logistics launched its proprietary cargo tracking platform, FreightVision 2.0, in the second quarter of 2023.
FreightVision 2.0 has been adopted by over 340 client organizations as of year-end, with adoption continuing to accelerate into early 2024.
The technology segment contributed $92 million in recurring software and data service revenues during fiscal 2023.
Management expects the technology segment to generate revenues exceeding $140 million in fiscal year 2024, based on current subscription pipelines.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Meridian Coastal Logistics had fully extinguished all outstanding revolving credit balances, carrying zero drawn amounts on its credit facilities.
The Port of New Orleans terminal delivered record container volumes in 2023, handling 1.12 million TEUs over the course of the fiscal year.
The Southeast Asia expansion, led by a joint venture with Harbor Bridge Holdings, commenced commercial operations in Singapore in October 2023.
This joint venture contributed $34 million in equity earnings during the fourth quarter, which management characterized as an encouraging debut.
Director of Strategy Priya Nambiar indicated that two additional Southeast Asian terminal partnerships are under active negotiation for potential 2024 launch.
In March 2023, the company drew $175 million under its syndicated revolving credit facility to fund accelerated port infrastructure spending, a balance that remained outstanding at fiscal year-end.
The company's equity base grew to $1.52 billion by December 31, 2023, supported by retained earnings and a modest share issuance under the employee stock purchase plan.
Meridian Coastal Logistics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $73.40, for a total buyback outlay of approximately $88 million.
The share repurchase program has $162 million remaining under the current board-authorized capacity of $250 million.
Gross profit margin for the full year was 35.6%, slightly ahead of the company's internal target of 35.0% for fiscal 2023.
The company's accounts receivable balance at year-end was $287 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 57 days.
Meridian Coastal Logistics reduced its average DSO by four days compared to fiscal year 2022, attributable to improved billing cycle automation.
Inventory at the company's bonded warehouse facilities totaled $63 million at December 31, 2023, representing primarily client-stored goods and consumable operating supplies.
The company recorded a $14 million impairment charge during the second quarter related to a legacy dry-dock facility in Mobile, Alabama that was subsequently decommissioned.
Excluding this impairment, adjusted operating income for fiscal 2023 would have been $444 million, representing an adjusted operating margin of 24.1%.
Sandra Whitmore highlighted the company's strong free cash flow generation of $421 million as a key financial achievement of the fiscal year.
Free cash flow conversion, defined as free cash flow divided by net income, reached 141% in 2023, well above the industry peer median of approximately 110%.
Meridian Coastal Logistics maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Fitch Rating Services throughout fiscal 2023.
The company received no credit rating downgrades during the year, and the outlook was revised to positive by one major rating agency in November 2023.
Total shareholder return for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, outperforming the sector benchmark index which returned 14.7% over the same period.
The board of directors appointed two new independent directors in June 2023, bringing the total board size to eleven members.
The audit committee, chaired by Elena Forsythe, oversaw the external audit conducted by Hartwell & Crane LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion.
Meridian Coastal Logistics adopted the new FASB lease accounting standard update effective January 1, 2023, with no material impact on reported earnings.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.8%, reflecting the mix of domestic and international earnings and applicable tax incentives.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $31 million during the year, primarily due to accelerated depreciation on newly commissioned terminal assets.
The company made $28 million in defined benefit pension contributions during fiscal 2023, keeping the pension funded status at approximately 96%.
Meridian Coastal Logistics disclosed no material related-party transactions during fiscal 2023 outside of routine intercompany arrangements.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with this financial report, details progress on all seventeen sustainability commitments.
Meridian Coastal Logistics reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% on an intensity basis compared to fiscal year 2022.
Management reaffirmed fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.05 billion, representing projected growth of approximately 6% to 11%.
The company expects 2024 EBITDA margins to remain broadly stable, in the range of 28% to 29%, supported by operating leverage and continued technology revenue growth.
Meridian Coastal Logistics remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, long-term value creation, and transparent financial reporting for all stakeholders.
This report should be read in conjunction with the audited consolidated financial statements and accompanying notes included in the full Annual Report on Form 10-K.
Velarion Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in targeted oncology and rare disease treatments.

This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared under the oversight of Chief Executive Officer Dr. Margaux Pellerin and Chief Financial Officer Theodore Wainscott.
The company was founded in 2009 and has since grown to employ approximately 1,840 full-time staff across its three principal operating sites in North Carolina, Switzerland, and Singapore.
Velarion's flagship product, VLR-402, received full FDA approval in March 2022 for the treatment of relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma.
Net revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $748.3 million, representing a 19.4% increase compared to the $626.8 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.

The growth was driven primarily by continued commercial uptake of VLR-402 in the United States and expanded reimbursement coverage secured in five additional European markets during the second half of 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Velarion carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined capital allocation and consistent free cash flow generation.
Gross profit for the year was $521.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 69.7%, which compares favorably with the 67.1% margin achieved in the prior fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187.4 million in 2023, representing roughly 25% of net revenues and underscoring the company's commitment to pipeline advancement.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $143.2 million, an increase of $12.6 million year-over-year, largely attributable to the expansion of the commercial sales force in Europe.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $190.9 million, up from $152.3 million in the prior year, marking the third consecutive year of double-digit operating income growth.
In September 2023, Velarion completed the drawdown of a $300 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by a consortium of six investment-grade banks to fund the acquisition of Orethis Biologics.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $159.7 million, or $3.14 per diluted share, compared with $121.5 million, or $2.41 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 16.3%, benefiting from research and development tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix resulting from expanded Swiss operations.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $412.8 million, with an additional $200.0 million available under the company's existing revolving credit facility.
Capital expenditures in 2023 totaled $54.7 million, focused primarily on the expansion of manufacturing capacity at the company's Singapore biologics plant.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing a 12.5% increase from the prior quarterly dividend rate, beginning with the payment in February 2024.
Velarion's pipeline includes eleven active clinical programs across Phase I through Phase III, with three programs expected to reach pivotal endpoints in 2024.
VLR-519, the company's next-generation KRAS G12C inhibitor, commenced a Phase III global trial in August 2023, enrolling patients across 42 clinical sites in 18 countries.
Dr. Pellerin stated that the strength of the company's pipeline gives management confidence in sustaining double-digit revenue growth through the mid-decade period.
Headcount grew by 214 employees during 2023, with the majority of new hires concentrated in clinical operations, regulatory affairs, and commercial functions in Europe.
The company recorded a non-cash impairment charge of $22.1 million in the third quarter related to the write-down of certain legacy manufacturing equipment in North Carolina.
Inventory levels at year-end were $89.4 million, reflecting a deliberate strategic build of VLR-402 finished goods ahead of anticipated demand increases in newly approved markets.

Accounts receivable stood at $163.2 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 79 days, consistent with prior-year levels and in line with industry norms for specialty pharmaceutical companies.
The acquisition of Orethis Biologics, which closed on November 14, 2023, added two preclinical biologics assets and a state-of-the-art antibody-drug conjugate manufacturing platform.
Orethis was acquired for total consideration of $415.0 million, comprising $310.0 million in cash and $105.0 million in Velarion common stock.
The transaction was accounted for under ASC 805 as a business combination, with preliminary purchase price allocation identifying $278.0 million in acquired intangible assets.
Mr. Wainscott noted that the integration of Orethis is proceeding ahead of schedule, with back-office systems consolidation expected to be complete by the end of the first quarter of 2024.
Velarion's royalty revenue stream, derived from licensing agreements with two Asian partners for VLR-201, contributed $31.7 million in fiscal 2023, up from $27.4 million the prior year.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year, with a stable outlook reaffirmed in October 2023.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $18.6 million, primarily related to upfront milestone payments received under a co-promotion agreement with Rantheon Pharmaceuticals in Japan.
Share repurchases during 2023 totaled 1.2 million shares at an average price of $58.43, for a total consideration of $70.1 million under the board-authorized buyback program.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio at December 31, 2023, was calculated at 0.41, reflecting the term loan liability recorded in connection with the Orethis acquisition financing.
Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a cumulative other comprehensive loss of $9.8 million for the year, driven primarily by Swiss franc weakening against the US dollar.
Velarion's commercial partnership with Hollenbeck Medical in Germany and Austria contributed incremental net revenues of approximately $44.5 million in its first full year of operation.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Simone Archambault, reviewed and approved all related-party transactions and found no material irregularities during the fiscal year.

Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced materially in 2023, with the company achieving a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2020 baseline.
Velarion was added to the MSCI Global Sustainability Index in June 2023, reflecting recognition of the company's improved ESG disclosure practices and stakeholder engagement programs.
The company's total shareholder return for fiscal 2023 was 28.6%, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index return of 19.1% over the same period.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management is providing revenue guidance in the range of $870 million to $910 million, representing expected growth of approximately 16% to 22% over fiscal 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $253.8 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 33.9%, compared with $201.7 million and 32.2% in fiscal 2022.

The company recognized stock-based compensation expense of $38.4 million in 2023, consistent with the $36.9 million recognized in the prior year, reflecting ongoing equity grant programs for key personnel.
Velarion has no material off-balance-sheet arrangements as of December 31, 2023, and management is not aware of any contingent liabilities that would materially affect reported financial results.
Dr. Pellerin was appointed to the board of directors of the Biotechnology Innovation Organization in October 2023, reflecting her standing as a recognized leader in the pharmaceutical industry.
The company's financial statements have been audited by Grant & Lorne LLP, who issued an unqualified opinion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.

Velarion's liquidity position is considered strong, with management estimating that current cash resources are sufficient to fund planned operations and capital commitments for at least the next 24 months.
The company filed a shelf registration statement on Form S-3 with the SEC in July 2023, providing flexibility to raise capital through equity or debt offerings of up to $500 million in aggregate.
Total assets at year-end were $1.84 billion, up from $1.31 billion in fiscal 2022, reflecting the impact of the Orethis acquisition and continued organic asset growth.
Total liabilities were $712.4 million, inclusive of the $300 million term loan, trade payables of $87.3 million, accrued compensation of $64.1 million, and other accrued liabilities.
Stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023, was $1.13 billion, an increase of $289.6 million from the prior year end.
Management believes that Velarion is well-positioned to achieve its long-term strategic objectives, including reaching $1.5 billion in annual revenues by fiscal year 2027.

The company will host its annual investor day on March 19, 2024, in New York City, at which Dr. Pellerin and Mr. Wainscott will present updated long-range financial projections and pipeline milestones.

This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected due to regulatory, competitive, and macroeconomic factors.
Meridian Coatings Group, a specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Anneliese Vordermann opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's resilience amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Meridian Coatings Group operates across three primary segments: Industrial Protective Coatings, Marine Surface Solutions, and Architectural Finishes.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The Industrial Protective Coatings segment remained the largest contributor, generating €820 million in revenue.
Marine Surface Solutions posted revenue of €610 million, driven by strong demand from Asia-Pacific shipbuilders.
Architectural Finishes, the company's smallest segment, contributed €410 million to total revenues.
Gross profit for the year stood at €552 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 30.0%.
Chief Financial Officer Pieter van den Berg noted that raw material cost inflation moderated significantly in the second half of the year.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €318 million for fiscal year 2023.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €310 million, up 11.4% year-over-year.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €172 million, compared to €148 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share rose to €2.87 from €2.47 in the previous fiscal year.
The Board of Directors proposed a final dividend of €0.95 per share, subject to shareholder approval at the annual general meeting.
This proposed dividend represents a 12% increase over the €0.85 per share distributed following fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled €94 million, in line with the previously communicated guidance range of €90–€100 million.
The company invested heavily in modernizing its production facility in Antwerp, which accounts for approximately 35% of total manufacturing capacity.
Research and development expenditure reached €62 million, reflecting Meridian's continued focus on developing low-VOC and bio-based coating formulations.
At year-end, Meridian Coatings Group employed 6,820 full-time equivalent staff across 18 countries.
The workforce expanded by 340 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022, primarily through hiring in the Asia-Pacific region.
Ms. Vordermann emphasized that talent acquisition in sustainable chemistry remains a strategic priority for the business.
Meridian's Marine Surface Solutions segment launched three new antifouling product lines during the second quarter of 2023.
These launches were met with strong uptake from European ferry operators and offshore energy contractors.
The Architectural Finishes segment introduced its premium EcoShield Pro range in October 2023, targeting commercial renovation projects.
EcoShield Pro achieved €18 million in revenue within its first two months on the market, exceeding internal forecasts.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 amounted to €196 million, up from €161 million in the prior year.
The company's cash conversion ratio improved to 64% of EBITDA, compared to 58% in fiscal year 2022.
Meridian maintained a conservative balance sheet posture throughout the year, with net debt declining to €210 million by December 31, 2023.
The company entered fiscal year 2023 with no outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility, having fully repaid all drawn amounts in December 2022.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €284 million, representing approximately 56 days of cost of goods sold.
Mr. van den Berg confirmed that inventory reduction remains an operational priority for the first half of fiscal year 2024.
Trade receivables totaled €312 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 62 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 1.8x, compared to 1.6x at the end of fiscal year 2022.
During the third quarter of 2023, Meridian Coatings Group drew €150 million from its revolving credit facility to fund a bolt-on acquisition in Southeast Asia.
The acquisition, PT Surya Pelapis Industri, a leading Indonesian industrial coatings manufacturer, was completed on August 3, 2023.
PT Surya Pelapis Industri contributed €22 million in revenue and €4 million in EBITDA to Meridian's consolidated results in fiscal year 2023.
The transaction was financed through a combination of the drawn credit facility and available cash on hand.
Integration activities are expected to be substantially complete by mid-2025, according to the company's integration roadmap.
Meridian's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.3%, slightly below the prior year's 25.1%.
The reduction in the effective tax rate was partially attributable to the recognition of deferred tax assets in the Netherlands.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its ongoing buyback program at an average price of €38.50 per share.
Total share repurchase consideration amounted to approximately €46 million during the fiscal year.
Meridian's share price appreciated 19% over the course of 2023, outperforming the AEX Chemicals Index by 11 percentage points.
Inventory days in the Industrial Protective Coatings segment decreased from 68 days in fiscal year 2022 to 61 days in fiscal year 2023.
This improvement reflected better demand forecasting and tighter supply chain management implemented in the first quarter.
The Marine Surface Solutions segment, by contrast, saw inventory days edge up slightly from 49 days to 52 days due to planned stock-building ahead of anticipated demand.
The planned stock-building was linked to confirmed order backlogs from two major South Korean shipyards.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of €1.92 billion to €1.97 billion.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, implying EBITDA of approximately €336 million to €365 million.
Ms. Vordermann concluded the earnings call by reaffirming Meridian's long-term ambition to achieve carbon-neutral manufacturing operations by 2035.
The company's sustainability report, published concurrently, confirmed a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Coatings Group's next scheduled investor communication is the first-quarter 2024 trading update, planned for release on May 8, 2024.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollowell and CTO Desmond Yau, both veterans of the enterprise resource planning industry.
Veltara Systems reported total revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
The company serves over 2,400 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 78% of total revenue, underscoring the resilience of Veltara's business model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining 22% of revenue, totaling approximately $90.8 million.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $271.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 65.8%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, totaled $198.3 million, up 9.1% from the prior fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $74.6 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in platform innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $83.5 million, representing approximately 20.2% of total revenue.
General and administrative costs came in at $40.2 million, broadly in line with management's expectations.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $73.1 million, compared to $59.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $54.9 million, or $1.37 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, as defined by management, was $96.2 million, representing a margin of approximately 23.3%.
CEO Margaret Hollowell commented that 2023 was a transformational year for Veltara Systems across every dimension of the business.
The company successfully launched its next-generation platform, VeltaCore 4.0, in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023.
VeltaCore 4.0 introduced advanced AI-driven demand forecasting capabilities that have been widely praised by industry analysts.
Customer satisfaction scores improved to an all-time high of 87% following the VeltaCore 4.0 rollout.
The company expanded its global workforce to approximately 3,150 full-time employees by the end of fiscal year 2023.
Veltara opened two new regional offices during the year, one in Amsterdam and one in Singapore, to support its international growth strategy.
Total bookings for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a record high for the company.
The annual contract value of new customer agreements averaged $218,000, up from $195,000 in the prior year.
Net revenue retention rate for fiscal year 2023 was 114%, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
Churn rate remained low at 4.2%, well below the industry average of approximately 7% for comparable enterprise software vendors.
The company's balance sheet remained strong, with cash and cash equivalents of $163.8 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable totaled $68.4 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 60 days.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $18.7 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow for the year was $77.5 million, up from $61.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in October 2023.
By December 31, 2023, the company had repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $38.50 per share.
CFO Adrienne Mossgrove noted that the balance sheet's strength provides Veltara with significant strategic flexibility heading into 2024.
The company achieved investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Veltara entered into a strategic partnership with logistics technology firm Parallex Group in March 2023 to co-develop last-mile optimization tools.
The partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of between $15 million and $20 million over the next three years.
International revenue grew 21.4% year over year, reaching $109.7 million and accounting for approximately 26.6% of total revenue.
The Asia-Pacific segment was the fastest-growing region, with revenue increasing 33.7% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Veltara's board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in November 2023, the company's first-ever dividend payment.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined financial management.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $142.6 million by year-end, a 17.8% increase over the prior year-end balance.
Management reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile and a conservative approach to leverage.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $468 million to $476 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at approximately 24% to 25%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage.
In January 2024, the company secured a $250 million term loan facility to fund a planned acquisition in the supply chain analytics space.
The company's product roadmap for 2024 includes the release of VeltaCore 4.5, which will incorporate enhanced sustainability tracking modules.
VeltaCore 4.5 is expected to be generally available to all existing subscribers by the end of the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltara's intellectual property portfolio expanded to over 140 registered patents as of December 31, 2023.
The company invested $12.4 million in an early-stage supply chain AI startup called LogiMind Technologies during the fourth quarter of 2023.
The LogiMind investment is accounted for as an equity method investment under U.S. GAAP.
Veltara's audit committee completed its annual review with no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.8%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
The reduction in the effective tax rate was primarily attributable to increased utilization of research and development tax credits.
CTO Desmond Yau emphasized that platform scalability and security remain the company's top engineering priorities for the foreseeable future.
Veltara Systems remains well-positioned to capitalize on the growing global demand for intelligent supply chain software solutions.
Veltoran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Dr. Petra Hoffmann, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 7.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Stein attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand in the agricultural additives and polymer coating segments.
Veltoran's gross profit for the year reached €612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%, up from 31.8% in 2022.
The improvement in gross margin was driven by favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiency programs launched in early 2023.
Operating income for the full year stood at €238 million, compared with €201 million in the prior year, reflecting a 18.4% year-over-year improvement.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €319 million, ahead of analyst consensus estimates of €305 million.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €162 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of €3.47.
The board of directors proposed a final dividend of €0.85 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.40 per share, a 12% increase over 2022.
Veltoran's polymer coating segment achieved segment revenues of €710 million, growing 9.1% year-over-year and representing the largest individual contributor to consolidated sales.
The agricultural additives segment posted revenues of €540 million, aided by expanded distribution agreements across Eastern European markets.
The industrial solvents division generated €390 million in revenue, a modest 2.4% increase, as softer demand from automotive end-markets weighed on volumes.
The specialty resins unit contributed €200 million in revenue, benefiting from two new customer contracts signed in the second quarter.
Dr. Hoffmann noted in her opening remarks that 2023 was a landmark year for Veltoran's operational transformation program, dubbed Project Catalyst.
Project Catalyst delivered cumulative cost savings of €47 million during the fiscal year, surpassing the original target of €35 million.
Capital expenditure for the year totaled €185 million, of which approximately €110 million was directed toward capacity expansion at the Mannheim production facility.
The Mannheim expansion is expected to add 60,000 metric tons of annual production capacity upon completion in the third quarter of 2024.
Research and development expenditure reached €92 million, representing 5.0% of total revenues, consistent with the company's long-term innovation investment policy.
Veltoran filed 34 new patent applications during 2023, up from 27 in the prior year, underscoring the company's commitment to proprietary technology development.
Headcount at year-end stood at 8,740 full-time equivalent employees globally, an increase of 310 from the 8,430 reported at year-end 2022.
The workforce additions were concentrated in manufacturing and supply chain functions to support capacity ramp-up activities.
Employee-related expenses, including wages, benefits, and share-based compensation, totaled €384 million for the year.
The company's selling, general, and administrative expenses were €231 million, representing 12.6% of revenues, down from 13.4% in 2022.
Mr. Stein highlighted that working capital management improvements contributed to a €38 million reduction in net working capital during the year.
Inventories at December 31, 2023, were €298 million, down from €336 million at the end of 2022, reflecting better demand visibility and procurement discipline.
Trade receivables stood at €247 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding improving to 49 days from 54 days in the prior year.
Free cash flow for the full year was €134 million, a significant improvement from €87 million in 2022.
The strong free cash flow generation enabled Veltoran to reduce its net debt position materially during the year.
Net debt at December 31, 2023, was €341 million, down from €489 million at December 31, 2022, representing a reduction of €148 million.
The net debt to EBITDA ratio improved to 1.07 times at year-end, well within the company's internal target of maintaining leverage below 2.0 times.
Veltoran's revolving credit facility of €300 million remained undrawn throughout the year, providing ample liquidity headroom.
The company's investment-grade credit rating was reaffirmed by both Moody's at Baa2 and S&P at BBB, with stable outlooks from both agencies.
In October 2023, Veltoran completed the divestiture of its non-core water treatment additives business for proceeds of €85 million.
The divestiture generated a pre-tax gain of €22 million and was consistent with the company's strategic focus on higher-margin specialty product lines.
Proceeds from the divestiture were applied toward early repayment of a term loan that had been scheduled to mature in 2025.
International revenues accounted for 68% of total consolidated revenues, with Asia-Pacific being the fastest-growing region at 14.2% year-over-year growth.
Veltoran expanded its sales presence in Southeast Asia through the establishment of regional offices in Kuala Lumpur and Ho Chi Minh City.
European revenues, which represent 49% of total sales, grew by 5.8%, supported by long-term supply agreements with several major chemical distributors.
North American revenues grew 8.6%, aided by market share gains in the industrial coatings sub-segment against two key competitors.
During 2023, Veltoran launched three new product lines under its EcoBond brand, targeting biodegradable adhesive applications in packaging markets.
The EcoBond product range generated €28 million in first-year revenues, modestly below the internal forecast of €35 million.
Management attributed the shortfall to longer-than-anticipated customer qualification cycles, which are now largely complete.
The company expects EcoBond revenues to accelerate meaningfully in 2024 as qualification approvals convert into volume orders.
Veltoran's sustainability framework, aligned with the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures, reported a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions versus the 2022 baseline.
The company has set a science-based target to achieve a 42% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2030, relative to a 2020 baseline.
In terms of governance, the supervisory board welcomed two new independent members during the year, bringing total board size to ten.
The audit committee conducted an extensive review of internal controls over financial reporting, resulting in no material weaknesses identified.
Veltoran successfully concluded the year with its balance sheet entirely free of any long-term bond obligations, having redeemed its remaining senior notes in full during the fourth quarter.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue growth in the range of 5% to 8% on a constant currency basis.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 is guided to be in the range of €330 million to €350 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Capital expenditure in 2024 is expected to be approximately €200 million, reflecting the final phase of the Mannheim capacity expansion.
The company anticipates that interest expense will increase modestly in 2024 due to the drawdown of approximately €150 million under its existing term loan agreement completed in early January 2024.
Management emphasized that the 2024 financing activity was undertaken to pre-fund the capital program rather than to address any liquidity shortfall.

Veltoran entered into a new €250 million fixed-rate term loan with a syndicate of seven relationship banks in the first week of January 2024, with the full principal outstanding on the balance sheet as of the filing date.
The company closed the fourth quarter with solid momentum, as order intake in October and November 2023 was 11% above the same period in the prior year.
Dr. Hoffmann concluded by reaffirming Veltoran's commitment to disciplined capital allocation, sustainable growth, and delivering long-term value to its shareholders.
Velarion Technologies Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Holt and has grown to serve clients across manufacturing, retail, and logistics sectors.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Velarion reported total revenues of $412.7 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 18.4 percent.
Subscription-based recurring revenues accounted for $338.9 million of total revenues, underscoring the company's successful transition to a SaaS business model.
Professional services revenues contributed the remaining $73.8 million, driven primarily by large-scale implementation engagements in North America and Western Europe.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Anand described the fiscal year as a period of disciplined expansion and operational maturity.
Gross profit for the year reached $271.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 65.8 percent, up from 63.2 percent in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $198.6 million, including research and development costs of $74.3 million and sales and marketing expenditures of $89.1 million.
General and administrative expenses were $35.2 million, reflecting continued investment in compliance infrastructure and executive talent acquisition.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $72.8 million, compared with $51.3 million in fiscal 2022, a year-over-year improvement of 41.9 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation, depreciation, and amortization, came in at $97.4 million for the full year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $54.6 million, or $1.38 per diluted share, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $1.21 per share.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $183.2 million and total current assets of $291.7 million.
Total assets on the December 31 balance sheet stood at $748.5 million, including $204.3 million in intangible assets and goodwill.
The intangible assets largely reflect the 2021 acquisition of LogiPath Analytics, which expanded Velarion's predictive freight analytics capabilities.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $147.6 million, an increase of 22.1 percent from the prior year balance, signaling strong forward contract momentum.
The company's annual recurring revenue metric, calculated as of December 31, 2023, stood at $361.4 million.
Net revenue retention rate for fiscal 2023 was 118 percent, demonstrating robust upsell and cross-sell performance across the existing customer base.
Customer count grew to 1,847 at year-end, compared with 1,502 at the close of fiscal 2022, reflecting net additions of 345 accounts.
Enterprise customers, defined as those generating annual contract values above $250,000, numbered 312, up from 241 in the prior year.
International revenues represented 31.4 percent of total revenues, with the EMEA region contributing the largest share at 19.7 percent.
The Asia-Pacific segment experienced the highest growth rate, expanding 34.2 percent year-over-year to reach $38.6 million in revenues.
Velarion's headcount at December 31, 2023 was 2,614 full-time employees, compared with 2,189 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company added 425 net new employees during the year, with the majority of hires concentrated in product engineering and customer success roles.
As of December 31, 2023, Velarion carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior credit facility in the second quarter.
The Board of Directors authorized a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in November 2023, with the first payment made on January 15, 2024.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 totaled $28.4 million, retiring approximately 1.1 million shares at an average price of $25.82.
The company's share price closed fiscal 2023 at $31.47, representing an annual appreciation of 43.6 percent and outperforming the S&P Software Index by 21 points.
Velarion's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $1.24 billion, formally placing it in the small-large cap transition zone by certain index definitions.
In March 2023, the company entered into a five-year senior secured credit facility totaling $150 million with a syndicate led by Crestwood Capital Partners.
The facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and includes standard covenants related to leverage and interest coverage ratios.
Management drew $75 million from the facility during the third quarter to fund accelerated hiring and international data center buildout.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $31.8 million, primarily consisting of data center equipment, leasehold improvements, and capitalized software development costs.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $68.9 million for the year, representing a free cash flow margin of 16.7 percent.
Operating cash flow of $100.7 million was supported by strong collections performance, with days sales outstanding improving to 47 days from 54 days in fiscal 2022.
The company issued a total of 620,000 shares under its employee stock purchase plan during fiscal 2023, raising approximately $15.3 million in proceeds.
Velarion launched its next-generation platform, VelarionOS 4.0, in the second quarter of 2023, incorporating generative AI features for demand forecasting.
VelarionOS 4.0 achieved customer adoption rates of 61 percent among eligible accounts by year-end, ahead of the internal target of 50 percent.
The platform's AI-powered demand forecasting module reduced average inventory holding costs for pilot customers by 14.3 percent in controlled deployments.
Chief Technology Officer Ananya Mehta highlighted the company's investment of $22.7 million specifically in AI and machine learning infrastructure during the year.
Velarion holds 18 issued patents related to supply chain optimization algorithms, with 9 additional patent applications pending as of January 2024.
The company's partner ecosystem grew to 214 certified implementation partners at year-end, spanning 37 countries across five continents.
A strategic reseller agreement signed with GlobalEdge Consulting in September 2023 is expected to accelerate pipeline development in Southeast Asia and India.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual report include competitive pressure from larger vendors such as SAP and Oracle, as well as cybersecurity and data privacy threats.
Management noted that the company operates in a highly fragmented market with over 90 specialized vendors competing across various sub-segments of supply chain software.
Velarion's win rate against primary competitors was reported at 54 percent in competitive deals tracked during fiscal 2023, up from 49 percent in fiscal 2022.
The audit committee completed its annual review of internal controls over financial reporting and found no material weaknesses, consistent with the prior year's assessment.
Ernst & Whitfield LLP, the company's independent registered public accounting firm, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal 2023 financial statements.
For fiscal 2024, management provided initial guidance of total revenues between $488 million and $498 million, representing growth of approximately 18 to 21 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $112 million to $118 million, implying margin expansion of 100 to 150 basis points.
The 2024 outlook assumes continued strong net revenue retention, normalized hiring activity, and no significant contribution from potential acquisitions.
CFO Priya Anand reiterated that capital allocation priorities for 2024 include organic investment in product development, selective tuck-in acquisitions, and shareholder returns.
The Board declared its intention to maintain the quarterly dividend program introduced in late 2023, with a target payout ratio of approximately 15 percent of net income.
Velarion will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, in Austin, Texas, where management plans to present a three-year strategic roadmap.
The event will be webcast live and archived on the company's investor relations website for a period of not less than 90 days following the presentation.
Management expressed confidence that Velarion's combination of deep domain expertise, expanding partner network, and AI-driven innovation positions the company for sustained long-term growth.
Verantis Specialty Chemicals, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Verantis operates as a leading producer of high-performance polymer additives and surface coatings, serving industrial and consumer markets across North America and Europe.
The company was founded in 1987 and has maintained its headquarters in Akron, Ohio, since inception.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollowell opened the report by acknowledging a transformative year marked by both operational achievements and macroeconomic headwinds.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tusk noted that disciplined cost management remained a cornerstone of the company's financial strategy throughout 2023.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 7.3% increase over the $1.71 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit expanded to $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8%, compared with 25.4% in the prior year.
Operating income for the period was $218 million, up from $179 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting both volume growth and improved product mix.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $147 million, equating to diluted earnings per share of $3.12.
The Specialty Polymers segment contributed $1.02 billion in revenue, accounting for 55.4% of consolidated net sales.
The Surface Coatings segment generated $820 million in revenue, driven by strong demand from the automotive refinishing subsector.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year stood at $304 million, reflecting an EBITDA margin of 16.5%.
Capital expenditures totaled $98 million during 2023, primarily allocated to the expansion of the Greenville, South Carolina manufacturing campus.
Research and development spending rose 12% year-over-year to $61 million, underscoring Verantis's commitment to innovation.
The company invested heavily in bio-derived feedstock research, targeting a 20% reduction in fossil-based raw material consumption by 2027.
Operating cash flow for the year amounted to $231 million, providing ample liquidity to support strategic initiatives.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, totaled $133 million for the fiscal year.
The board of directors declared total dividends of $1.20 per share during 2023, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
Verantis repurchased 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $44.60, returning approximately $94 million to shareholders.
As of December 31, 2023, the company's total assets stood at $2.31 billion, while total liabilities were recorded at $1.09 billion.
Shareholder equity rose to $1.22 billion from $1.07 billion at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.4 times, compared with 1.9 times at year-end 2022, signaling strengthened short-term liquidity.
Inventory levels declined by 8% on a year-over-year basis, reflecting improved supply chain efficiency and lean manufacturing practices.
Days sales outstanding decreased from 48 days to 41 days, indicative of more effective receivables management.
As of December 31, 2023, Verantis carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its remaining obligations earlier in the year.
The North American distribution network was expanded by adding 14 new regional distribution partners, bringing the total partner count to 87.
European revenues grew by 11.2% in constant-currency terms, with Germany and the Netherlands emerging as the strongest-performing markets.
Verantis launched three new product lines in 2023 under the VeraShield brand umbrella, targeting high-performance industrial coatings applications.
The VeraShield Pro series received regulatory approval in all 50 U.S. states and was certified under the EU REACH framework in September 2023.
In March 2023, Verantis secured a $350 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of eight leading financial institutions to support its balance sheet flexibility.
Headcount at year-end 2023 stood at 6,840 full-time employees, a net increase of 320 compared with the prior year-end figure.
The company's workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing and supply chain roles to support increased production volumes.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the annual internal survey, reached an all-time high of 82%, up from 76% in 2022.
The Safety Performance Index, a proprietary metric tracking recordable incidents per 200,000 labor hours, improved to 0.62 from 0.79 in 2022.
Verantis achieved its third consecutive year of zero lost-time injuries at its flagship Akron facility.
Environmental compliance costs decreased by $3.2 million relative to the prior year following installation of advanced emissions control equipment.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions were reduced by 9% year-over-year, ahead of the company's 7% annual reduction target.
The company established a dedicated ESG Steering Committee chaired by independent board member Dr. Susan Calvert.
Verantis renewed its supplier diversity program and increased certified diverse supplier spend to 18% of total procurement, up from 13% in 2022.
Looking forward, management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
The 2024 guidance reflects anticipated volume growth of 5% to 8%, partially offset by modest pricing pressure in the Surface Coatings segment.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set between $320 million and $335 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 stands at approximately $115 million, including $45 million earmarked for a new polymer reactor at the Greenville campus.
Management expects the Greenville expansion to increase nameplate production capacity by approximately 18% upon completion in late 2025.
The company also announced plans to divest its legacy Adhesive Solutions business unit, which generated $72 million in revenue during 2023.
The divestiture is expected to close in the second quarter of 2024, subject to customary regulatory approvals.
Proceeds from the sale are estimated at $95 million to $110 million, which Verantis intends to redeploy into higher-margin specialty polymer products.
Raymond Tusk stated that the pending divestiture aligns with the company's strategy to concentrate resources on its core, differentiated product platforms.
Verantis does not anticipate any material impact on 2024 revenue guidance as a result of the divestiture, given the unit's relatively modest contribution.
The company maintains a robust intellectual property portfolio comprising 214 active patents across 32 jurisdictions globally.
In 2023, Verantis filed 19 new patent applications, with 11 focused on bio-derived polymer chemistry and 8 related to advanced coating formulations.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the external audit for fiscal year 2023 without issuing any material weaknesses.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective in accordance with the criteria set forth by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.
The company's credit ratings were reaffirmed at Baa1 by Moody's and BBB+ by S&P Global Ratings, reflecting its stable financial profile.
No material litigation is pending against Verantis as of the report date, and management is not aware of any probable losses requiring accrual.
The board of directors approved a new share repurchase authorization of up to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024, and valid through December 31, 2025.
Margaret Hollowell concluded her remarks by expressing confidence that Verantis is well-positioned to achieve sustained, profitable growth over the medium term.
She emphasized the company's balanced approach to capital allocation, combining reinvestment in the business with consistent returns to shareholders.
Verantis Specialty Chemicals, Inc. thanks its shareholders, customers, employees, and partners for their continued trust and support.
Veltramex Corporation is a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
The company was founded in 1987 and has since grown into a leading producer of industrial adhesives and surface coatings.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Petra Hollander assumed her role in January 2021 after serving as Chief Operating Officer for three years.
Under her leadership, Veltramex has pursued an aggressive expansion into Asian markets, particularly in South Korea and Vietnam.
The company's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects both the resilience of its core business and the challenges posed by raw material inflation.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase over the prior year.
Organic revenue growth, excluding the contribution of the Hauser Coatings acquisition, stood at 4.9%.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Reinwald attributed the strong top-line performance to pricing adjustments implemented in the second quarter.
The industrial adhesives segment remained the company's largest revenue contributor, generating €920 million, or approximately 50% of total sales.
Surface coatings revenue grew 11.3% year-over-year to €614 million, driven by robust demand from the automotive sector.
The specialty polymers division posted revenues of €306 million, a modest increase of 2.1% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year came in at €552 million, yielding a gross margin of 30.0%, up 40 basis points from the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled €318 million, inclusive of research and development expenditures of €89 million.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached €310 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 16.8%.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €174 million, compared to €151 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share increased to €3.47 from €3.01, reflecting both higher net income and a modest share buyback program.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.20 per share for fiscal year 2023, a 9.1% increase over the prior year's dividend.
Capital expenditures totaled €127 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the Ludwigshafen facility.
The Ludwigshafen expansion project added approximately 18,000 square meters of production floor space and is expected to be fully operational by mid-2024.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was €183 million, supported by disciplined working capital management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 52 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened credit policies.
Inventory turnover increased to 6.2 times from 5.7 times, indicating improved supply chain efficiency.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and equivalents balance of €241 million.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet stood at €2.31 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity grew to €987 million, up from €842 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 18.9%, compared to 17.2% in the prior year.
Return on invested capital improved to 12.4%, exceeding the company's stated target of 11.0% for the first time since 2018.
Veltramex's global workforce stood at 8,640 employees at the end of fiscal year 2023, an increase of 310 from the prior year.
The headcount growth was driven primarily by hiring in the Asia-Pacific region, where the company added 280 new positions.
Employee productivity, measured as revenue per employee, rose to approximately €213,000 from €204,000 in fiscal year 2022.
The company launched its proprietary EcoSeal 7 adhesive product line in March 2023, targeting sustainable construction applications.
EcoSeal 7 generated €38 million in revenue during its first nine months on the market, exceeding internal projections by 14%.
Customer adoption of EcoSeal 7 was strongest in the Nordic countries and the Benelux region, where green building standards are most stringent.
The company's market share in the European industrial adhesives segment expanded to an estimated 14.7% in 2023, up from 13.9% in 2022.
Veltramex's competitive positioning was reinforced by two new patent grants covering advanced polymer crosslinking technologies.
The newly patented crosslinking technologies are expected to support product differentiation in high-margin specialty adhesive applications over the next five years.
