Management cautioned that macroeconomic headwinds, including elevated interest rates and enterprise IT budget constraints, could pressure deal cycles in the first half of 2024.
Veltora's board is composed of nine directors, including three independent directors with backgrounds in cybersecurity, cloud infrastructure, and enterprise software.

The compensation committee approved a revised long-term incentive plan for fiscal year 2024 that ties 60% of executive equity awards to relative total shareholder return and 40% to adjusted free cash flow per share.

CEO Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, stating that Veltora is well-positioned to extend its market leadership as digital transformation spending accelerates across its core verticals.
This excerpt contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described herein.
Valcrest Nutrition Holdings, Inc. reported its annual financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, marking a pivotal year for the specialty health foods company.
Chief Executive Officer Marianne Delacroix opened the annual report by emphasizing the company's continued focus on premium plant-based protein segments.
Valcrest operates across three primary business divisions: retail packaged goods, foodservice ingredients, and direct-to-consumer e-commerce.
The company is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, and employs a workforce spread across manufacturing facilities in four U.S. states.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $412.7 million, representing a 9.4% increase compared to the prior year's $377.2 million.
Gross profit margins improved to 38.6%, up from 35.1% in fiscal 2022, driven by favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Gerald Whitmore noted that working capital management was a particular area of strength during the year.
The e-commerce division alone generated $87.4 million in net revenues, accounting for approximately 21.2% of total company sales.
Valcrest launched its new 'VerdaCore' protein line in March 2023, which quickly became the top-selling SKU within the retail segment by June.
Research and development expenditures totaled $14.2 million, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation and pipeline development.
As of December 31, 2023, Valcrest carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $22.8 million, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at the Eugene, Oregon plant.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its previously authorized buyback program.
Operating income grew to $64.3 million, compared to $51.8 million in the prior year, representing a 24.1% year-over-year improvement.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $79.6 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.3%, reflecting continued operational leverage.
In November 2023, the company secured a $150 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by Pacific Coast Financial Group.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $38.9 million, reflecting disciplined supply chain management amid ongoing input cost volatility.
The retail packaged goods division reported net revenues of $198.5 million, representing a modest 4.2% increase over the prior year.
Foodservice ingredients revenues reached $126.8 million, benefiting from expanded partnerships with regional restaurant chains in the Southwest.
Ms. Delacroix highlighted that the VerdaCore launch exceeded internal projections by approximately 34%, validating the company's product strategy.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $94.1 million, representing 22.8% of net revenues, a slight improvement from 23.5% in 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.3%, modestly above the prior year's rate of 23.7% due to certain non-deductible transaction costs.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $41.7 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, compared to $33.2 million, or $1.45 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Mr. Whitmore stated that the company's cash conversion cycle improved by approximately 6 days year-over-year, reaching 47 days.
Accounts receivable at year-end totaled $31.2 million, representing days sales outstanding of approximately 27.6 days.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 were $58.4 million, up from $43.1 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $10.8 million, consistent with the company's capital return commitments.
Valcrest added three new manufacturing lines at its Salem, Oregon facility, increasing overall production throughput by approximately 18%.
The company expanded its retail distribution footprint to over 22,000 store locations nationwide, up from approximately 18,500 at the end of fiscal 2022.
International revenues, while still modest, grew 31.2% year-over-year to $19.4 million, led by distribution gains in Canada and the United Kingdom.
The company's full-year headcount grew to 1,847 full-time equivalent employees, up from 1,612 at the end of fiscal 2022.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and incentive compensation, accounted for $112.4 million of total operating expenses.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar emphasized the importance of talent retention initiatives introduced during the year.
The company reported a net cash inflow from operating activities of $72.1 million, reflecting strong earnings conversion.
Net cash used in investing activities totaled $24.6 million, primarily related to capital expenditure programs at manufacturing facilities.
Financing activities resulted in a net cash inflow of $7.8 million, inclusive of proceeds from option exercises partially offset by dividend payments.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent review of the company's financial statements without qualification.
Valcrest's board comprises nine directors, six of whom are classified as independent under applicable stock exchange listing standards.
The company did not experience any material cybersecurity incidents during fiscal 2023, as confirmed by its third-party security assessment.
Goodwill and intangible assets on the balance sheet totaled $88.3 million, unchanged from the prior year as no acquisitions were completed in 2023.
Ms. Delacroix indicated that the company remains interested in strategic acquisitions within the functional nutrition space.
For fiscal 2024, Valcrest provided initial revenue guidance of $445 million to $460 million, representing estimated growth of 7.8% to 11.4%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of $84 million to $90 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $25 million to $30 million as it continues its capacity build-out.
Management expressed confidence that the VerdaCore line would contribute an incremental $30 million to $35 million in revenue during fiscal 2024.
The company disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements as of the reporting date.
Supplier concentration risk was identified as a key risk factor, with the top three suppliers representing approximately 41% of total raw material spend.
Valcrest entered into a long-term supply agreement with NutriAgri Co. for oat protein concentrate, locking in pricing through December 2025.
The agreement with NutriAgri is expected to reduce raw material cost variability and support gross margin stability going forward.
Environmental, social, and governance disclosures were expanded in the 2023 annual report to align with SASB standards for the food and beverage sector.
The company achieved a 12% reduction in Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions on a per-unit-of-production basis compared to fiscal 2022 levels.
Water usage intensity at manufacturing facilities declined by 8.3%, reflecting investments in process efficiency technology.
The board's compensation committee approved a revised long-term incentive plan tying executive payouts to three-year total shareholder return metrics.
Mr. Whitmore confirmed that deferred tax liabilities of $6.7 million were recorded in connection with accelerated tax depreciation on capital assets.
The company maintained compliance with all applicable financial covenants throughout fiscal 2023.
Valcrest's stock price closed fiscal 2023 at $38.72 per share, representing a 26.4% total return for the calendar year including dividends.
The market capitalization of the company as of December 31, 2023 was approximately $877 million based on shares outstanding.
Looking ahead, management reiterated its commitment to disciplined capital allocation, balanced between organic investment and shareholder returns.
The foregoing discussion constitutes management's assessment of financial condition and results of operations and should be read in conjunction with the audited financial statements.

Certain statements in this report constitute forward-looking statements and are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied.
Veltora Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care testing equipment and consumables.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Osei and has grown to become a recognized supplier to hospital networks across North America and select European markets.
This annual report covers fiscal year 2023, which ended on December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Harold Pennington opened the letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltora Diagnostics reported total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior year.
Product revenue, which comprises sales of diagnostic instruments and test cartridges, accounted for $631.8 million of total revenue.
Service and support contracts contributed the remaining $215.5 million, reflecting strong customer retention rates above 91 percent.
Gross profit for the year reached $389.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.0 percent.
This represents a modest improvement from the 44.3 percent gross margin recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Wulff attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable product mix shifts and operational efficiencies in manufacturing.
Operating expenses totaled $274.1 million, including $98.4 million in research and development investment.
Sales, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $175.7 million, a controlled increase of 6.2 percent year-over-year.
Operating income was $115.5 million, translating to an operating margin of 13.6 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $88.2 million, or $1.74 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $72.4 million, or $1.43 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $162.7 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.2 percent.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $203.4 million, up from $158.9 million at year-end 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $119.6 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 51 days.
Inventory levels were $87.3 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of new product launches in early 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $44.8 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham facility.
Free cash flow was $118.1 million, a substantial improvement driven by working capital management improvements throughout the year.
The company returned $32.5 million to shareholders through its share repurchase program, buying back approximately 1.4 million shares.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the board's policy of prioritizing reinvestment in organic growth.
Veltora's international segment generated revenue of $198.4 million, accounting for approximately 23.4 percent of total revenue.
Growth in Western Europe was particularly strong, with revenue climbing 19.1 percent year-over-year in that region.
The company expanded its European distribution network by adding partnerships with four new regional distributors during the year.
In Asia-Pacific, revenue grew a more modest 7.3 percent, constrained by pricing pressures and slower-than-expected hospital procurement cycles.
The company launched its flagship VeltraPanel 5000 diagnostic platform in September 2023, receiving strong initial market reception.
The VeltraPanel 5000 supports simultaneous testing for up to 22 biomarkers, significantly expanding the clinical utility compared to its predecessor.
By December 31, 2023, the company had shipped 1,847 VeltraPanel 5000 units to customers across 14 countries.
Customer feedback surveys indicated an overall satisfaction score of 4.6 out of 5.0 for the new platform, which management described as exceptional.
The installed base of all Veltora diagnostic instruments reached 42,600 units globally at year-end, a net increase of 3,200 units over the prior year.
Recurring revenue from consumables and service contracts tied to the installed base represented 68.4 percent of total revenue.
Research and development activities in 2023 were concentrated on next-generation multiplexed assays and a new handheld device code-named Project Lumen.
The company filed 17 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023 and received grants on 9 previously filed applications.
Headcount at December 31, 2023 stood at 3,412 full-time employees, an increase of 287 from the prior year-end figure.
Veltora Diagnostics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its outstanding term loan in March 2023.
The company's quality management systems achieved ISO 13485 recertification in July 2023 following a successful third-party audit.
Regulatory submissions for the VeltraPanel 5000 were completed in all major target markets, with clearances obtained in the United States, Canada, and the European Union.
The company is subject to ongoing pricing negotiations with certain group purchasing organizations, which could impact product revenue margins in 2024.
Management maintains its commitment to sustaining an investment-grade credit profile, which supports favorable terms on operational financing arrangements.
In February 2024, the company secured a five-year syndicated term loan of $275 million to fund a strategic acquisition of NovaBind Biotech, a molecular diagnostics firm.
NovaBind Biotech had trailing twelve-month revenue of approximately $64 million and brings proprietary nucleic acid amplification technology to Veltora's portfolio.
The acquisition is expected to close in the second quarter of 2024, pending customary regulatory approvals and NovaBind shareholder consent.
Chief Executive Officer Harold Pennington described the acquisition as transformative, positioning Veltora to compete directly in the fast-growing molecular diagnostics segment.
Post-acquisition integration costs are estimated at $12 to $15 million over the first 18 months, primarily related to systems harmonization and facility consolidation.
For fiscal year 2024, management is providing initial revenue guidance in the range of $960 million to $990 million, reflecting organic growth plus partial-year NovaBind contribution.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 is set at 18.5 to 20.0 percent, with dilution expected in the first half due to integration expenses.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is $55 to $65 million, inclusive of investments required to scale NovaBind's manufacturing processes.
The company expects to maintain a net leverage ratio below 2.0 times adjusted EBITDA throughout 2024, assuming the acquisition closes on schedule.
Sandra Wulff noted that the company's strong cash generation provides meaningful flexibility to service the new debt while continuing to invest in organic initiatives.
The board of directors approved an expansion of the share repurchase authorization by $75 million in January 2024, bringing total remaining capacity to $107.5 million.
Veltora's stock closed the fiscal year at $42.18 per share, representing a total shareholder return of approximately 27.3 percent for calendar year 2023.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $2.14 billion, placing it comfortably within the mid-cap tier of the S&P MidCap 400 Index.
Veltora Diagnostics was recognized on the 2023 MedTech Breakthrough Awards list for innovation in point-of-care diagnostics, an accolade management highlighted as validation of its R&D strategy.

Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that the combination of organic momentum, the NovaBind acquisition, and continued installed base expansion will drive sustained value creation for shareholders.
The full audited financial statements, notes, and management discussion and analysis are included in the pages that follow this report summary.
Veltrano Systems, Inc. is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in precision robotics and smart manufacturing solutions.
The company was founded in 2004 by CEO Marcus Eldridge and has grown to serve clients across aerospace, automotive, and pharmaceutical sectors.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a significant milestone for Veltrano as it completed its twentieth year of continuous operations.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $753 million.
Domestic revenue accounted for approximately 61% of total sales, with the remaining 39% derived from international markets.
The company's largest geographic segment outside North America was Western Europe, contributing $187 million in revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Diana Holt presented the full-year results to the board on February 14, 2024.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $312 million, yielding a gross margin of 36.8%, up from 34.2% in the prior year.
The improvement in gross margin was primarily attributed to favorable product mix shifts toward higher-margin robotics integration services.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198 million for the fiscal year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose by 8.1% year-over-year, reaching $124 million.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $74 million, reflecting continued investment in next-generation automation platforms.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $114 million, compared to $91 million in fiscal year 2022.
This represents an operating margin of approximately 13.5%, a notable improvement from 12.1% the year prior.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $82 million, or $3.47 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $64 million, or $2.71 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $148 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 17.5%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.3%, consistent with management's guidance range of 23% to 25%.
Cash flow from operations was a robust $121 million, up from $98 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $43 million, focused primarily on expanding the Columbus manufacturing facility and upgrading precision tooling equipment.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $78 million for the year.
The company returned $28 million to shareholders through share repurchases during fiscal year 2023.
Dividends declared per share were $0.64, representing a 14.3% increase from the $0.56 paid in fiscal year 2022.
As of December 31, 2023, total assets stood at $1.04 billion, crossing the billion-dollar threshold for the first time.
Current assets totaled $389 million, including $156 million in cash and cash equivalents.
Accounts receivable at year-end were $142 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 61 days.
Inventory levels were $71 million, representing a modest 4.2% increase from the prior year-end balance of $68 million.
Total liabilities at year-end were $521 million, while stockholders' equity was $519 million.
The debt-to-equity ratio improved to 0.48 from 0.54 in the prior year, reflecting disciplined balance sheet management.
Long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, stood at $248 million, with no debt maturing before 2026.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its existing credit agreements throughout the year.
Veltrano's backlog at year-end reached a record $1.1 billion, providing strong revenue visibility into fiscal year 2024.
New orders booked in fiscal year 2023 totaled $921 million, a 17.6% increase from the prior year.
The automotive segment was the fastest-growing vertical, with revenue up 22% year-over-year to $214 million.
The aerospace segment contributed $198 million in revenue, growing 9.3% compared to fiscal year 2022.
The pharmaceutical automation division posted $163 million in revenue, driven by regulatory-driven upgrades among key clients.
Headcount at year-end stood at 4,820 full-time employees, up from 4,410 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Throughout fiscal year 2023, Veltrano carried no outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility, maintaining a fully undrawn balance at all times.
The company launched three new product lines during fiscal year 2023, including the VX-900 collaborative robot series.
The VX-900 series received strong early market reception, with over 340 units shipped in the fourth quarter alone.
Management expects the VX-900 to become the company's best-selling product line within two fiscal years.
The company also introduced the SmartLink IIoT middleware platform, enabling seamless integration of legacy equipment into connected factory environments.
In October 2023, Veltrano executed a $175 million draw on its revolving credit facility to partially fund the acquisition of Renford Automation GmbH.
The Renford acquisition, completed on November 1, 2023, added approximately 310 employees and expanded Veltrano's presence in German manufacturing markets.
Total consideration paid for Renford Automation GmbH was $220 million, with the remainder funded through available cash on hand.
Post-acquisition integration costs of $9 million were recorded in the fourth quarter and are excluded from adjusted EBITDA.
The acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to adjusted earnings per share by approximately $0.18 in fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano's environmental sustainability initiatives reduced carbon emissions per unit of output by 11% in fiscal year 2023.
The company achieved ISO 14001 certification for its Columbus and Cincinnati facilities during the year.
Water consumption at manufacturing facilities declined by 8.3% year-over-year, meeting the company's 2023 sustainability target ahead of schedule.
Veltrano's customer retention rate remained high at 91%, reflecting the mission-critical nature of its automation solutions.
The net promoter score among enterprise clients improved to 67 in fiscal year 2023, up from 59 in the prior year.
During the year, the company renewed multi-year contracts with three of its top five customers, collectively valued at $410 million.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar noted that supply chain conditions improved materially in the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Lead times for critical components decreased from an average of 22 weeks in early 2023 to approximately 14 weeks by year-end.
The improved supply chain environment contributed to a 6.4% reduction in work-in-process inventory levels.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $935 million to $965 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be between $168 million and $178 million.
Management anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $50 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily supporting capacity expansion.
The board of directors authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $75 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Veltrano's leadership team expressed confidence that the combination of organic growth and the Renford integration will drive sustained shareholder value creation.
The full audited financial statements, footnotes, and management discussion and analysis are available in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the SEC.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company specializes in the design and manufacture of precision robotics systems for the food processing and pharmaceutical sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marguerite Folsom opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltrano Dynamics posted total revenues of $847 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Pacheco attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand in the pharmaceutical automation segment.
The pharmaceutical segment alone generated $412 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 48.6% of total company revenue.
The food processing segment contributed $299 million, while the remaining $136 million came from aftermarket services and software licensing.
Gross profit for the year reached $318 million, yielding a gross margin of 37.5%, up from 35.2% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $201 million, including $87 million in research and development expenditures.
Selling, general, and administrative costs accounted for $114 million of operating expenses, reflecting a 6% year-over-year increase.
Operating income came in at $117 million, translating to an operating margin of 13.8%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $84 million, or $2.31 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $61 million, or $1.68 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $152 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 17.9%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 22.4%.
Veltrano Dynamics generated $131 million in operating cash flow during the year, a significant improvement over the $98 million recorded in 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $44 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the Raleigh facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $87 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, maintaining the same rate as the previous four quarters.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $26 million.
The company also repurchased $35 million worth of its common stock under its existing share buyback authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, the total shares outstanding were approximately 36.4 million, down from 37.8 million at the start of the year.
Veltrano Dynamics ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $203 million.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4 at year-end, indicating a healthy liquidity position.
Inventory levels at year-end were $118 million, up from $97 million a year earlier, reflecting a deliberate build-up to meet anticipated first-quarter 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable totaled $134 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 58 days.
Trade payables were $76 million, consistent with typical payment terms negotiated with the company's supplier base.
Total assets on the balance sheet reached $1.12 billion, compared to $1.04 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Total liabilities stood at $489 million, including $210 million in long-term debt.
Shareholders' equity was $631 million, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and the positive impact of the year's net income.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio was 0.33 at year-end, well within the covenant thresholds stipulated in its existing credit agreement.
CFO Desmond Pacheco noted that the company has maintained a conservative leverage profile over the past three years.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano Dynamics launched two new product lines under the VeltraFlex brand targeting small-scale pharmaceutical manufacturers.
The VeltraFlex Mini and VeltraFlex Pro units received regulatory clearance from the FDA in March and July 2023, respectively.
Combined, the two new units generated approximately $58 million in revenue in their first partial year of commercialization.
The company invested $22 million in customer training and onboarding programs associated with the new product launches.
CEO Marguerite Folsom described the VeltraFlex rollout as one of the most successful product introductions in the company's 27-year history.
International revenues grew 18% year-over-year to $214 million, driven by increased sales in Germany, Japan, and South Korea.
The APAC region was the fastest-growing geography, posting 31% revenue growth compared to the prior year.
Veltrano Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 with a total global headcount of 4,200 employees, having hired no net new permanent staff during the year.
Employee compensation and benefits expense totaled $198 million, reflecting salary adjustments and an enhanced performance bonus pool.
The company's voluntary attrition rate was 9.3%, slightly below the industry average of 10.1% for the automation sector.
Veltrano Dynamics made significant investments in employee development, with over 120,000 training hours logged across the organization in 2023.
The company also extended its partnership with Zenora Technologies to co-develop next-generation machine vision modules expected to launch in 2025.
A recruitment drive launched in the third quarter of 2023 added 340 permanent employees to the global workforce before year-end.
Supply chain disruptions, which had plagued the industry in 2021 and 2022, were largely resolved by mid-2023 according to COO Franklin Berridge.
Lead times for critical semiconductor components decreased from an average of 38 weeks to approximately 14 weeks during the course of the year.
The improved supply chain environment contributed to a reduction in emergency procurement costs, saving approximately $11 million versus the prior year.
Veltrano Dynamics continued to invest in sustainability initiatives, achieving a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
The company was recognized by the Carbon Disclosure Project with an A- rating, an improvement from its B+ rating in 2022.
Water usage across all manufacturing facilities declined by 8% year-over-year, surpassing the company's internal target of 5%.
Looking ahead, Veltrano Dynamics provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $945 million.
The midpoint of the guidance range implies approximately 10.3% year-over-year growth, consistent with the company's medium-term growth objectives.
Management expects the adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal year 2024 to expand modestly to between 18.5% and 19.2%.
The company plans to allocate approximately $55 million in capital expenditures in 2024 to further automate its Raleigh facility.
Veltrano Dynamics also intends to pursue selective bolt-on acquisitions in the machine learning software space, with a target deal size of $30 million to $80 million.
The board reaffirmed its commitment to returning at least 40% of annual free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.
CFO Desmond Pacheco concluded his remarks by expressing confidence in the company's balance sheet strength and operational momentum heading into 2024.
Independent auditor Hartwell & Crane LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrano Dynamics' fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
The annual report was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024, within the required deadline for a large accelerated filer.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions for the semiconductor industry, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, reported strong performance across its core business segments despite challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Vortex Dynamics posted total annual revenue of $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The Precision Cooling Systems segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating $920 million, or approximately 50% of total sales.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Castillo noted that gross margins expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 41.2% for the full fiscal year.
Operating income for the year came in at $312 million, up from $267 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company attributed much of this improvement to disciplined cost management and favorable shifts in product mix toward higher-margin offerings.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198 million, representing approximately 10.8% of total revenues.
This investment level reflects the company's continued commitment to next-generation thermal interface material technology.
Vortex Dynamics employed approximately 8,400 full-time workers globally as of December 31, 2023.
Headcount grew by roughly 6% compared to the prior year, driven primarily by expansion in the Asia-Pacific engineering division.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with cash and cash equivalents of $540 million at year-end.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days, down from 51 days in the prior fiscal year.
Inventory levels were tightly managed, ending the year at $215 million, which represented a 9% reduction from the December 2022 balance.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar credited improved supply chain coordination for the favorable inventory outcome.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $143 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion in Malaysia and South Korea.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the company's policy of returning capital to shareholders.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $87 million.
Vortex Dynamics repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $48.60 per share.
The buyback program consumed approximately $102 million of the company's available liquidity.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 was $224 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.91.
This compared favorably to the $183 million net income and $2.38 diluted EPS recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%, slightly below management's guidance of 22.0% to 22.5%.
The lower tax rate was partially attributable to favorable resolution of certain international transfer pricing matters.
Vortex Dynamics generated free cash flow of $389 million during fiscal year 2023, a record for the company.
Raymond Castillo described the free cash flow performance as a direct result of earnings growth and working capital discipline.
The Fluid Dynamics Substrate segment, introduced in fiscal year 2021, contributed $340 million in revenue, growing 28% year-over-year.
Management expects this segment to become the company's second-largest revenue contributor by fiscal year 2025.
The company launched three new product lines during the fourth quarter of 2023, including the VortexShield Ultra, the NanoFlow 900 series, and the CryoGuard Flex platform.
Each of these platforms targets high-performance computing and artificial intelligence infrastructure applications.
Early commercial reception for the VortexShield Ultra has been encouraging, with initial orders from five of the top ten global hyperscale data center operators.
Margaret Holloway stated that the company's pipeline of design wins increased by 34% in fiscal year 2023, signaling strong future revenue conversion.
Vortex Dynamics maintained a net debt position of zero throughout fiscal year 2023, having repaid the remaining balance of its term loan in March 2023.
The company's long-term debt obligations were fully extinguished as of the first quarter of 2023, leaving the balance sheet entirely unencumbered.
Priya Nambiar highlighted that the operations team reduced per-unit manufacturing costs by 7.2% through lean process improvements implemented across three facilities.
The Asia-Pacific region delivered its highest-ever annual revenue contribution of $612 million, representing a 19% increase from the prior year.
European revenues declined modestly by 2.1% to $298 million, reflecting softness in the industrial automation end market.
North American revenues grew 11.4% to $734 million, driven by robust demand from semiconductor capital equipment customers.
In November 2023, Vortex Dynamics secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven institutional lenders.
Customer concentration remained a focus area for investors, with the top five customers accounting for 38% of total revenues.
No single customer exceeded 12% of total revenues during fiscal year 2023.
The company's backlog at year-end stood at $980 million, up 22% from the prior year-end balance of $803 million.
This backlog growth provides meaningful revenue visibility heading into fiscal year 2024.
Vortex Dynamics received two significant industry awards in 2023, including the Semiconductor Equipment Industry Innovation Prize.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially during the year.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions declined by 11% on an absolute basis despite the increase in manufacturing activity.
The company achieved a water recycling rate of 78% across its manufacturing operations, up from 69% in fiscal year 2022.
Margaret Holloway reiterated the company's commitment to achieving carbon neutrality across its direct operations by 2035.
For fiscal year 2024, management provided revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion.
This guidance implies year-over-year growth of approximately 8.7% to 13.0% at the midpoint relative to fiscal year 2023 results.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $3.20 to $3.35, reflecting continued margin improvement expectations.
Raymond Castillo indicated that capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are expected to range between $160 million and $175 million.
The incremental investment will be focused on expanding clean-room manufacturing capabilities at the company's new facility in Penang, Malaysia.
Vortex Dynamics also announced plans to initiate a new $200 million share repurchase authorization effective January 1, 2024.
The Board expressed confidence that the company's strong free cash flow generation supports both the dividend and buyback programs simultaneously.
Analyst consensus following the earnings release was broadly positive, with twelve of seventeen covering analysts maintaining buy or outperform ratings.
The stock appreciated approximately 8.4% in after-hours trading following the release of the annual report.
Vortex Dynamics remains well-positioned to capitalize on secular growth trends in artificial intelligence infrastructure, advanced packaging, and electric vehicle power electronics.
The management team expressed gratitude to its global workforce, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and partnership.

The full audited financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements, are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation looks forward to updating investors on its continued progress at the upcoming investor day scheduled for May 2024.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and data analytics company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marlena Okafor and CTO Brendan Yee, who together built a platform targeting the logistics and supply-chain intelligence sector.
Veltora's fiscal year runs from January 1 through December 31, and the following excerpt covers the full-year results for fiscal year 2023.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $618.4 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $472.1 million of that total, reflecting strong recurring revenue growth of 18.7% year over year.
Professional services revenue contributed $146.3 million, growing at a more modest 4.1% as large implementation projects wound down.
Gross profit for the year was $381.5 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.7%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, totaled $298.2 million, up from $261.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
Sales and marketing expenses rose to $134.6 million, driven by expanded field sales hiring and a major brand campaign launched in the second quarter.
Research and development expenditures reached $112.8 million, as Veltora accelerated investment in its next-generation AI forecasting engine.
General and administrative costs were $50.8 million, reflecting increased compliance and audit-related expenses following the company's secondary equity offering in March 2023.
Operating income for the fiscal year was $83.3 million, representing an operating margin of 13.5%.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $107.6 million, ahead of the company's prior guidance of $98 to $104 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $61.2 million, or $1.43 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $44.7 million, or $1.07 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.8 million, up from $157.4 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in cash was primarily attributable to strong operating cash generation and proceeds from the secondary equity offering completed in March.
Operating cash flow for the year totaled $119.4 million, compared to $87.6 million in the prior year.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures of $14.2 million, was $105.2 million.
Veltora ended fiscal year 2023 with 4,217 full-time employees globally, reflecting net headcount growth of approximately 11% from the prior year end.
The Americas region remained the company's largest, contributing $389.6 million or approximately 63% of total revenue.
The Europe, Middle East, and Africa region generated $148.4 million in revenue, growing 21.3% year over year as new partnerships with regional logistics firms took hold.
Asia-Pacific revenue was $80.4 million, a 9.8% increase, with particular strength in Australia and Singapore.
Customer count grew to 3,104 at December 31, 2023, up from 2,741 at December 31, 2022, representing a net addition of 363 customers.
The dollar-based net revenue retention rate for fiscal year 2023 was 118%, demonstrating robust expansion within the existing customer base.
Veltora's flagship product, RouteIQ Pro, was the primary driver of subscription growth, accounting for 67% of all new annual contract value signed during the year.
The company launched VeltoraSense, a new IoT-integrated demand-sensing module, in the third quarter of 2023 following eighteen months of development.
VeltoraSense had been adopted by 214 customers by year-end, with initial deployment concentrated in cold-chain and pharmaceutical logistics segments.
Average contract value for new logo deals increased to $198,000, up from $171,000 in the prior year, reflecting a deliberate move upmarket toward enterprise-tier clients.
Churn rate for the fiscal year remained low at 4.3%, consistent with the 4.1% recorded in fiscal year 2022 and below the company's internal target of 5.0%.
CFO Tamara Voss commented that the company's balance sheet discipline was a central factor in achieving these results without compromising strategic investment.
Total long-term debt at December 31, 2023 stood at $175.0 million, unchanged from the balance reported at December 31, 2022.
Veltora's revolving credit facility, with a capacity of $100.0 million, remained entirely undrawn throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company made scheduled principal payments on its existing term loan totaling $12.5 million during the year, funded from operating cash flow.
Interest expense for fiscal year 2023 was $8.9 million, down slightly from $9.4 million the prior year, consistent with the declining principal balance on the term loan.
Veltora's board of directors declared no cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's longstanding policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
The board did authorize a share repurchase program of up to $50.0 million in August 2023, of which $18.3 million had been utilized by year-end.
A total of 712,000 shares were repurchased under this program at a weighted average price of $25.71 per share.
Inventory balances are not a material consideration for Veltora given its software-centric business model, and days inventory outstanding is therefore not a reported metric.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $142.7 million, an increase of $29.3 million from the prior year-end balance, signaling strong future revenue visibility.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, was $98.4 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 58 days, modestly improved from 62 days at year-end 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.8%, modestly below the 22.4% rate in fiscal year 2022, due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Veltora faced no material legal proceedings during fiscal year 2023, and contingent liabilities disclosed in the notes to financial statements were immaterial.
The audit committee completed its review of the fiscal year 2023 financial statements without identifying any material weaknesses in internal controls.
Throughout fiscal year 2023, Veltora operated entirely free of any long-term financing obligations beyond its existing term loan, with no new debt instruments issued during the year.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating from Moody's at Baa2, stable outlook, reflecting consistent cash generation and conservative leverage.
Employee satisfaction scores, measured through an internal annual survey, improved to 78 out of 100 in 2023, up from 73 in 2022.
Veltora opened three new regional offices during fiscal year 2023, in Frankfurt, Dubai, and Melbourne, to support its growing international sales teams.
CEO Marlena Okafor noted that the Melbourne office, in particular, was critical to capturing growing demand in the Asia-Pacific freight-forwarding market.
Total headcount in the EMEA region increased by 34% year over year, the fastest regional growth rate within the company.
In November 2023, Veltora entered into a $150 million senior secured term loan agreement with a consortium of institutional lenders, the proceeds of which were used to fund strategic acquisitions.
The acquisition of Nexpath Analytics, a Toronto-based route optimization startup, was completed in December 2023 for a purchase price of $138 million.
Nexpath's technology is expected to be integrated into RouteIQ Pro within the first half of fiscal year 2024, accelerating the product roadmap by an estimated two quarters.
The Nexpath acquisition added approximately 87 employees to Veltora's global headcount, the majority of whom are software engineers and data scientists.
On a pro forma basis including Nexpath, total employee count at year-end 2023 would have been approximately 4,304.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 total revenue guidance in the range of $710 million to $730 million, representing growth of approximately 15% to 18% over fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $120 million to $132 million, reflecting continued operating leverage partly offset by integration costs related to the Nexpath acquisition.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $16 million to $19 million, modestly higher than fiscal year 2023 levels.
Veltora anticipates its effective tax rate in fiscal year 2024 will be approximately 22% to 23%, broadly consistent with historical levels.

Management remains confident that the company's platform, expanded product suite, and growing international presence position Veltora for sustained, profitable growth through the remainder of the decade.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the medical diagnostics and laboratory equipment sector, serving hospitals, clinics, and research institutions across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Caldwell expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in February 2024.
Veltara Diagnostics recorded total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product line, the PrecisionScan Pro series, accounted for approximately 38% of total revenue in 2023.
Chief Financial Officer Harold Osei noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points compared to fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $312.6 million, reflecting investments in research and development as well as expanded sales infrastructure.
Research and development spending rose to $94.1 million, representing 11.1% of total revenues for the year.
The company employed 5,840 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023.
Veltara Diagnostics expanded its European operations significantly, opening new distribution centers in Frankfurt and Warsaw during the third quarter.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 reached $121.7 million, compared to $98.4 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share for 2023 were reported at $3.14, up from $2.53 in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.5 million, providing a solid liquidity foundation for near-term operational needs.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.7 from 2.3 in the prior fiscal year, indicating stronger short-term financial health.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $67.8 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity upgrades in the Ohio facility.
Veltara Diagnostics completed the acquisition of NovaBio Instruments Ltd. in August 2023 for a total consideration of $155 million.
The acquisition of NovaBio added approximately 420 employees and a suite of genomic sequencing tools to Veltara's product portfolio.
Integration costs associated with NovaBio were contained at $8.3 million, below management's initial estimate of $11.5 million.
The genomic sequencing segment generated $62.4 million in revenue during its first partial year as part of Veltara's operations.
Dr. Caldwell highlighted the successful launch of the AutoPath 9000 analyzer in Q2 2023 as a key commercial milestone.
The AutoPath 9000 received regulatory clearance from the FDA in April 2023 and CE marking in June 2023.
Early adoption of the AutoPath 9000 was strongest in large academic medical centers, with over 340 units placed by year-end.
Customer satisfaction scores for the AutoPath 9000 averaged 4.6 out of 5.0 in post-installation surveys conducted during Q3 and Q4.
Veltara's North American segment contributed $512.8 million in revenue, representing approximately 60.5% of consolidated revenues.
The European segment reported revenues of $234.7 million, reflecting strong growth in Germany, Poland, and the United Kingdom.
Asia-Pacific revenues, while smaller at $99.8 million, grew at the fastest rate of 21.3% year-over-year among all geographic segments.
The company's backlog of confirmed purchase orders reached $318.6 million at December 31, 2023, its highest level in five years.
Supply chain disruptions that affected the prior year were largely resolved, with component lead times normalizing to pre-pandemic levels.
Inventory levels at year-end were $134.2 million, down from $151.7 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved inventory management practices.
The company maintained a conservative balance sheet throughout fiscal year 2023, prioritizing financial flexibility amid macroeconomic uncertainty.
Total long-term debt at December 31, 2023 stood at $88.4 million, associated entirely with a revolving credit facility established in 2019.
Interest expense for the year was $4.9 million, reflecting the modest leverage profile the company has consistently maintained.
Veltara Diagnostics entered fiscal year 2024 completely free of any outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in November 2023, with a stable outlook.
Moody's similarly maintained its Baa1 rating for Veltara's senior unsecured obligations, citing consistent free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $168.3 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 19.9%.
Management intends to deploy excess cash toward share repurchases and bolt-on acquisitions in the diagnostics technology space.
In January 2024, Veltara finalized a $220 million syndicated term loan agreement with a consortium of five international banks to fund strategic growth initiatives.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 14.8%, up from 12.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Headcount growth was disciplined, with net hiring of 310 employees during the year concentrated in engineering and commercial roles.
Employee turnover declined to 8.2% in 2023 from 11.7% in 2022, which management attributed to enhanced compensation and benefits programs.
The company invested $12.4 million in workforce training and development initiatives during fiscal year 2023.
Dr. Caldwell emphasized that talent retention remains a cornerstone of Veltara's long-term competitive strategy.
The PrecisionScan Pro 7, the latest iteration of the flagship line, is scheduled for commercial launch in Q1 2024.
Regulatory submissions for the PrecisionScan Pro 7 were filed with the FDA in October 2023 and are currently under review.
Veltara's intellectual property portfolio comprises 214 active patents, with 31 new patents granted during fiscal year 2023.
The company allocated $5.7 million to patent prosecution and maintenance activities in 2023, consistent with the prior year level.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with carbon emissions per unit of revenue falling 9% year-over-year.
Veltara achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification for its primary manufacturing site in Columbus, Ohio, in September 2023.
The board appointed Dr. Priya Nanjundan as a new independent director in June 2023, bringing expertise in molecular biology and commercialization.
The audit committee, chaired by Thomas Reinhart, oversaw the transition to a new enterprise resource planning system completed in Q4 2023.
Deloitte & Touche LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days in 2023 from 54 days in 2022, reflecting more effective collections processes.
The company's gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $498.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 58.8%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $218.5 million, equating to approximately 25.8% of total revenue.
Other income, including interest earned on cash deposits and foreign exchange gains, contributed $6.2 million to pre-tax income.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.1%.
Veltara Diagnostics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $41.60 per share.
The share repurchase program was executed under the $75 million buyback authorization approved by the board in November 2022.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
The company expects continued margin expansion driven by operating leverage, disciplined cost management, and favorable product mix shifts.
Veltara Diagnostics remains committed to delivering sustainable value to all stakeholders as it advances its mission of improving diagnostic outcomes worldwide.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap technology firm specializing in industrial automation software and edge computing solutions headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollis and CTO Pradeep Nair, both veterans of the semiconductor industry.
Veltora's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant strategic transformation and operational investment.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741 million.
The company's core Industrial Automation segment contributed $512 million, or approximately 60.4% of total revenue.
The Edge Computing segment generated $335 million in revenue, growing at an accelerated pace of 22.7% year-over-year.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $398 million, translating to a gross margin of 47.0%, up from 44.3% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $271 million, inclusive of $134 million in research and development expenditures.
The company's operating income was $127 million, yielding an operating margin of 15.0%.
Net income attributable to Veltora shareholders reached $94 million, compared with $72 million in the prior fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $2.18, up from $1.67 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting both earnings growth and modest share buyback activity.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Veltora repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $38.40, totaling roughly $46 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203 million, with an additional $88 million in short-term investments.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $61 million, concentrated in data center infrastructure and manufacturing automation tooling.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.34 billion and total stockholders' equity of $712 million.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days from 49 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened credit and collections processes.
Inventory levels declined modestly to $114 million from $122 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
CFO Anita Swaminathan noted that the improvement in working capital dynamics contributed meaningfully to free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $153 million, representing an 18.1% free cash flow margin.
Veltora exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term borrowings, having retired its remaining credit facility obligations in the second quarter.
The company hired a net total of 340 employees during fiscal year 2023, ending the year with a global headcount of 4,820.
Employee additions were concentrated in the Edge Computing division, which added 210 engineers and product specialists.
Veltora's customer retention rate improved to 93% in fiscal year 2023, up from 88% the prior year.
The company expanded its partner ecosystem by onboarding 47 new value-added resellers across Europe and Asia-Pacific.
In the third quarter alone, Veltora drew down $175 million under a newly arranged revolving credit facility to fund its accelerated capital program.
Margaret Hollis emphasized that disciplined capital allocation remains a cornerstone of the company's long-term strategy.
The company launched its VX-9 edge processing platform in January 2023, which received strong adoption among automotive OEM customers.
VX-9 bookings surpassed $90 million within the first six months of availability, ahead of internal projections.
The Industrial Automation segment benefited from increased spending by North American manufacturers investing in factory modernization.
Veltora's top ten customers accounted for 38% of total revenue, demonstrating healthy revenue diversification relative to industry peers.
The company signed a landmark five-year contract with Halcyon Manufacturing Group valued at approximately $200 million over the contract term.
Pradeep Nair stated that the Halcyon agreement validates Veltora's differentiated approach to integrated edge-cloud automation architectures.
International revenue grew to $312 million, representing 36.8% of total revenue, versus 33.1% in the prior fiscal year.
Germany and Japan were the fastest-growing international markets, together accounting for $141 million in combined revenue.
Veltora received ISO 27001 recertification in October 2023, reinforcing its commitment to enterprise-grade information security standards.
The company's net promoter score climbed to 62 in the most recent annual customer satisfaction survey, up from 54 the prior year.
Veltora invested $18 million in sustainability initiatives during fiscal year 2023, including renewable energy procurement and supply chain audits.
Carbon emissions per unit of revenue declined by 11% year-over-year, keeping the company on track toward its 2030 net-zero commitment.
The headcount increase during the year was distributed across North America, Western Europe, and India, with India adding 98 employees.
Veltora's attrition rate declined to 9.4% in fiscal year 2023 from 13.1% the prior year, reflecting improved employee engagement programs.
The company introduced a new equity incentive program for senior technical staff in the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Annual recurring revenue from subscription-based software licenses reached $211 million, comprising 24.9% of total revenue.
The ARR growth rate of 31% outpaced overall company revenue growth, indicating a favorable business model shift toward recurring income.
Veltora ended the year with a backlog of $438 million, providing strong visibility into fiscal year 2024 revenues.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for total revenue in the range of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Operating margin is expected to expand modestly to between 15.5% and 16.5% in fiscal year 2024, driven by operating leverage and improved product mix.
Management expects research and development spending to increase in absolute terms while declining slightly as a percentage of revenue.
The Audit Committee completed its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses in Veltora's financial reporting processes.
Independent auditors Grant & Abernathy LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltora's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Veltora's Board of Directors consists of nine members, including three independent directors with backgrounds in enterprise technology and capital markets.
The company remains confident in its ability to execute on its strategic priorities and create long-term value for shareholders, employees, and customers.

Meridian Biologics Corporation, a specialty pharmaceutical and diagnostics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Pamela Hartwell opened the report by highlighting the company's continued expansion in the oncology diagnostics segment.
The company recorded total revenues of $847 million for the full year, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior fiscal year.
Product sales accounted for $712 million of total revenues, while service and licensing fees contributed the remaining $135 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $394 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.5 percent.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $278 million, reflecting increased investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditures rose to $119 million, up from $97 million in fiscal 2022, as the company advanced three late-stage clinical programs.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $159 million, reflecting higher headcount and expanded commercial operations.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 came in at $116 million, compared to $98 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $89 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ellenbogen noted that adjusted EBITDA reached $161 million, a record high for Meridian Biologics.
The company generated $143 million in operating cash flow, supporting ongoing capital investments and strategic initiatives.
Capital expenditures totaled $54 million, primarily related to the expansion of the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $89 million for the year.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $212 million, up from $174 million at year-end 2022.
Inventories stood at $98 million as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a modest build in anticipation of new product launches in early 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled $134 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 57 days from 63 days in the prior year.
The balance sheet showed total assets of $1.24 billion at fiscal year-end.
Total liabilities were $487 million, including long-term debt of $295 million and current liabilities of $192 million.
Shareholders' equity increased to $753 million, driven by net income and a reduction in accumulated other comprehensive loss.
Dr. Hartwell emphasized that the oncology diagnostics division delivered $341 million in revenue, growing at 18 percent year-over-year.
The immunoassay platform, which underpins the diagnostics division, processed more than 4.2 million patient samples during the year.
The company's flagship product, the MeriDx-7 panel, gained regulatory clearance in three additional European markets during fiscal 2023.
International revenues grew to $263 million, representing 31 percent of total revenues, up from 27 percent in the prior year.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenues increasing 34 percent to $87 million.
Meridian Biologics added 412 full-time employees during the year, bringing total headcount to 3,847 at December 31, 2023.
The company opened a new commercial office in Singapore to support its Asia-Pacific expansion strategy.
Strategic partnerships with two major hospital networks in Germany were signed during the third quarter, expected to generate incremental revenues beginning in 2024.
The company also entered a co-development agreement with Veratech Genomics to advance next-generation liquid biopsy assays.
Mr. Ellenbogen indicated that the Veratech collaboration is expected to involve combined R&D investment of approximately $40 million over three years.
Meridian Biologics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.50 per share.
The total cost of share repurchases was approximately $46 million, funded from existing cash balances.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend rate.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $30 million.
The company's stock closed the fiscal year at $41.22 per share, implying a market capitalization of approximately $1.71 billion.
Meridian Biologics maintained its investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P throughout the fiscal year.
The company's net leverage ratio, defined as net debt divided by adjusted EBITDA, stood at 0.52x at year-end, reflecting a conservative balance sheet.
Dr. Hartwell discussed the pipeline, noting that the MeriDx-9 next-generation panel is expected to enter pivotal trials in the first half of 2024.
The company anticipates that MeriDx-9 could address a total addressable market of approximately $2.3 billion globally.
Meridian Biologics also progressed its therapeutic division, with a first-in-class KRAS inhibitor candidate completing Phase 1 dose-escalation in October 2023.
The Phase 1 trial enrolled 48 patients across five clinical sites in the United States and Canada.
No dose-limiting toxicities were observed at the target therapeutic dose, supporting advancement to Phase 2.
The company expects to initiate a Phase 2 trial for the KRAS inhibitor in mid-2024, subject to regulatory feedback.
Meridian Biologics exited fiscal 2023 entirely free of short-term borrowings and held no outstanding balance on its revolving credit facility.
The company's supply chain transformation program, launched in mid-2022, resulted in $22 million of annualized cost savings by year-end 2023.
Procurement efficiencies and vendor consolidation were cited as the primary drivers of supply chain savings.
The company drew down $175 million from its revolving credit facility during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 to pre-fund planned capital expenditures.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $945 million.
This guidance implies year-over-year growth of approximately 8.6 percent to 11.6 percent at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to be in the range of $175 million to $185 million.
Management noted that foreign exchange headwinds could reduce reported revenues by approximately $15 million versus prior-year rates.
The company plans to invest approximately $65 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, prioritizing automation and capacity expansion.
Dr. Hartwell expressed confidence that the company's diversified portfolio and expanding international footprint position it well for sustained growth.
Mr. Ellenbogen added that the company's strong free cash flow generation provides flexibility for both organic investment and potential bolt-on acquisitions.
Meridian Biologics continues to evaluate acquisition targets in the molecular diagnostics and point-of-care testing spaces, with a focus on assets below $200 million in enterprise value.
The company remains committed to returning capital to shareholders through its dividend program and opportunistic share repurchases.

In summary, fiscal 2023 represented a strong year of financial and operational progress for Meridian Biologics Corporation, reinforcing the company's position as a leading player in specialty diagnostics and biopharmaceuticals.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap technology infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in edge computing hardware and managed network services.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delane and CFO Priya Sohaan, both of whom continue to lead the organization today.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Hardware segment revenues grew to $1.02 billion, while managed services revenues contributed $820 million to the total.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.7%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $489 million, reflecting continued investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditures alone accounted for $198 million, up 17% year-over-year.
The company generated operating income of $223 million, compared to $184 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $161 million, or $2.43 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year was $318 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.3%.
Veltora Systems ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $407 million on its balance sheet.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023, were reported at $3.12 billion, a 9.1% increase from the end of fiscal 2022.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.1, signaling strong short-term liquidity.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting better collections efficiency.
Inventory turnover increased to 6.8 times annually, up from 5.9 times in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $134 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in its San Marcos facility.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $273 million, a record high for the company.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This represents a 20% increase in the quarterly dividend compared to the prior year's rate of $0.15 per share.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 totaled $85 million, retiring approximately 3.2 million shares at an average price of $26.56.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 9,340 full-time employees, an increase of 6.2% from the prior year end.
Veltora Systems expanded its workforce primarily in engineering and customer success roles to support growing managed services demand.
The managed services segment reported a net revenue retention rate of 112%, underscoring strong upsell and cross-sell performance.
CEO Marcus Delane stated in his annual letter that the company's strategic pivot toward software-defined networking has been a key differentiator.
Veltora launched three new edge compute appliance models in the second quarter of 2023, targeting small and mid-sized enterprise customers.
The flagship product, the VX-9000 series, garnered over 1,400 unit orders within its first 90 days of general availability.
International revenues grew to $512 million, representing 27.8% of total revenues, up from 23.1% in fiscal 2022.
The EMEA region led international growth with a 34% year-over-year revenue increase, driven by expansion in Germany and the Netherlands.
Veltora entered a strategic distribution agreement with TechReach GmbH in April 2023, broadening its European market presence.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $143 million in revenue, reflecting 19% growth supported by new channel partnerships in Japan and South Korea.
Domestic revenues of $1.33 billion were anchored by strong performance in the financial services and healthcare verticals.
The financial services vertical alone accounted for $389 million in revenue, making it the single largest end-market for Veltora.
Healthcare segment revenues grew 22% to $274 million, aided by the company's HIPAA-compliant edge networking suite launched in late 2022.
Veltora's market share in the U.S. enterprise edge networking segment rose to an estimated 8.4%, up from 6.9% the previous year.
CFO Priya Sohaan emphasized disciplined cost management as a core operational priority heading into fiscal 2024.
The company successfully renegotiated its primary semiconductor supply contracts in mid-2023, locking in favorable pricing through 2025.
Cost of goods sold as a percentage of revenue declined 180 basis points year-over-year, primarily due to supply chain efficiencies.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $291 million for fiscal 2023, or 15.8% of total revenues.
The company recorded a one-time restructuring charge of $12 million in the third quarter related to consolidation of two logistics centers.
Despite the restructuring charge, third-quarter operating income still exceeded analyst consensus estimates by approximately 7%.
Veltora's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, compared to 22.9% in the prior year, benefiting from R&D tax credits.
Long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, totaled $540 million, consisting entirely of senior unsecured notes issued in 2021.
The weighted average interest rate on the outstanding debt was 3.85%, with all notes maturing in 2028.
Debt-to-EBITDA stood at 1.7 times at year end, which management considers comfortably within its target leverage range of 1.5 to 2.0 times.
The company maintained a $300 million revolving credit facility that remained fully undrawn as of December 31, 2023.
Veltora's credit ratings were affirmed by both Moody's at Baa2 and S&P at BBB, reflecting stable financial positioning.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses were identified in the company's internal controls over financial reporting.
External auditors from Greenvale & Marsh LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
Veltora's pension obligations were fully funded at year end, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $18 million.
The company carried no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired all operating lease obligations through its owned-facility strategy.
Veltora Systems successfully completed a $450 million bond offering in November 2023, with proceeds intended to fund international expansion and working capital needs.
Customer concentration remained modest, with the top ten customers representing only 21% of total revenues, down from 24% in fiscal 2022.
Veltora's backlog of signed but unrecognized contracts stood at $638 million at year end, a 29% increase from $495 million the prior year.
The company expects to recognize approximately 60% of the current backlog as revenue within the next twelve months.
Management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying approximately 13% growth at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $355 million to $375 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was provided at $150 million to $165 million, primarily for data center co-location buildout.
Chief Operating Officer Rafael Tienne noted that Veltora expects to hire approximately 700 net new employees in fiscal 2024.
The company also announced a new $100 million share repurchase authorization effective January 1, 2024, supplementing the $15 million remaining from the prior program.
Veltora's board composition as of the report date includes eight independent directors and two management directors, reflecting strong governance practices.
Marcus Delane concluded his letter to shareholders by reaffirming Veltora Systems' long-term commitment to disciplined capital allocation and sustainable, profitable growth.
The company remains well-positioned to capitalize on secular tailwinds in edge computing and enterprise network modernization for years to come.
