Capital expenditures for the year totaled €68 million, with the majority allocated to the expansion of the Advanced Coatings production facility in Antwerp.
Free cash flow was €119 million, representing a year-over-year improvement of 18.4%.
Veltoran's balance sheet showed total assets of €1.34 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at €621 million at year-end, reflecting a stable leverage profile.
The company maintained a net debt position of €203 million at the close of fiscal year 2023.
Return on invested capital was 11.6%, improving by 140 basis points from the prior year.
Miriam Halstrup noted that the company's strategic realignment toward higher-margin specialty products was gaining traction across all three segments.
Veltoran's headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 4,217 full-time equivalent employees globally.
The company added 312 net new employees during fiscal year 2023, primarily in research and development and sales functions.
Research and development expenditures totaled €38 million, representing 4.3% of total revenue.
During the year, Veltoran successfully launched VeltraShield 9X, a new high-performance coating product targeting the automotive OEM market.
VeltraShield 9X received regulatory approval in the European Union and South Korea prior to its commercial launch in the third quarter of 2023.
The product generated approximately €22 million in revenue in its first partial year of commercial availability.
Benedikt Ostroff confirmed that VeltraShield 9X margins exceeded corporate average gross margins by more than 600 basis points.
Veltoran's export revenue grew to €341 million, representing 39% of total revenue compared to 35% in fiscal year 2022.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenue up 14.2% to €198 million.
The Board of Directors approved a full-year dividend of €0.88 per share, a 10% increase from the prior year's dividend of €0.80 per share.
The dividend will be paid in two equal tranches, with the first payment scheduled for April 2024 and the second in October 2024.
Veltoran's inventory levels declined to €112 million at year-end, down from €138 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved supply chain management.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having completed its final scheduled repayment in November 2023.
The company's working capital ratio stood at 1.8 as of year-end, comfortably above the covenant threshold of 1.3 specified in its existing financing agreements.
Accounts receivable turnover improved to 8.2 times from 7.6 times in the prior year, indicating faster collections across the customer base.
Days sales outstanding declined to 44.5 days from 48.1 days in fiscal year 2022, consistent with enhanced credit and collections processes.
Miriam Halstrup expressed confidence that the operational improvements achieved in fiscal year 2023 would support continued margin expansion into fiscal year 2024.
In January 2024, Veltoran finalized a €250 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of seven European banks to fund its announced acquisition strategy.
The acquisition pipeline includes two bolt-on targets in the Polymer Additives space, both headquartered in Central Europe.
Veltoran's total equity as of December 31, 2023, was €719 million, up from €641 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The increase in equity was primarily attributable to retained earnings, partially offset by share buybacks totaling €16 million during the year.
The company repurchased 620,000 shares under its authorized buyback program at an average price of €25.81 per share.
Veltoran's market capitalization at December 31, 2023, was approximately €2.1 billion based on the closing share price of €51.40.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ with a stable outlook by both Fitch Ratings and S&P Global Ratings during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Looking ahead, Veltoran provided full-year fiscal 2024 guidance for revenue in the range of €920 million to €945 million.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €195 million to €210 million, implying continued margin improvement at the midpoint.
Management highlighted ongoing investment in digital manufacturing capabilities as a key driver of anticipated cost savings over the next three years.
The Polymer Additives segment is expected to benefit from new long-term supply agreements signed with three major European plastics processors in late 2023.
Benedikt Ostroff indicated that the company would continue to evaluate opportunities to optimize its tax structure across its European and Asian subsidiaries.
Veltoran's annual report, including full audited financial statements and notes, will be published on April 30, 2024, and made available to shareholders on the company's investor relations website.
The company reaffirmed its commitment to reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 35% by the year 2030 relative to its 2019 baseline.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions, Inc. (ticker: MBSI) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the specialty pharmaceutical manufacturing sector, with a focus on oncology and rare disease therapeutics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Holt opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Dreyden noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal year for operational and financial discipline.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Product revenue from the company's flagship oncology portfolio, led by Veltrex and Oricanil, accounted for $1.42 billion of total revenues.
Royalty income and licensing fees contributed an additional $187 million to the top line.
Contract manufacturing services generated $231 million in revenue, reflecting growing demand from third-party pharmaceutical clients.
Gross profit for the year was $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 59.2%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $724 million for the fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures rose to $312 million, representing 17.0% of total revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $298 million, a moderate increase from the prior year's $271 million.
Amortization of intangible assets related to prior acquisitions accounted for $114 million of total operating expenses.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $366 million, compared to $298 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net interest expense totaled $43 million, reflecting the company's existing long-term debt obligations.
Pre-tax income was $323 million, and the effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $254 million, or $3.18 per diluted share.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, showed total assets of $4.61 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $487 million, up from $341 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Meridian Biopharma concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of revolving credit borrowings, having retired the last of its drawn balances in March of that year.
Inventory levels at year-end were $362 million, reflecting a strategic buildup ahead of anticipated demand increases in 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled $294 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 58 days.
Capital expenditures for the year were $189 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's biologics manufacturing campus in Durham, North Carolina.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the company's stated commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, the company entered into a $350 million syndicated credit facility arranged by a consortium of major banking institutions.
Total long-term debt outstanding at December 31, 2023, was $1.12 billion, including senior notes maturing in 2028 and 2031.
The debt-to-EBITDA ratio at year-end was approximately 2.1x, which management considers within its target leverage range.
Dr. Holt emphasized that the Durham facility expansion positions the company to meet growing global demand for its cell therapy products.
During fiscal year 2023, the company added 640 full-time employees, bringing the global headcount to approximately 8,200.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in manufacturing, quality assurance, and clinical development functions.
Meridian Biopharma's clinical pipeline advanced significantly during the year, with three new Investigational New Drug applications filed with the FDA.
The lead pipeline candidate, MB-7741, a next-generation KRAS inhibitor, entered Phase II clinical trials in September 2023.
Enrollment in the MB-7741 Phase II trial exceeded projections, with 214 patients enrolled across 18 clinical sites by year-end.
The company also received Breakthrough Therapy Designation from the FDA for its rare blood disorder treatment, MB-4409.
Regulatory filings for Oricanil's label expansion into a second-line melanoma indication were submitted in November 2023.
International revenues grew by 18.7% year-over-year, driven primarily by strong uptake in European and Asia-Pacific markets.
The European business unit reported revenues of $389 million, led by Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew to $214 million, representing a 31.2% increase from fiscal year 2022.
The company signed a co-promotion agreement with Tanaka Pharmaceutical in Japan for the distribution of Veltrex.
Marcus Dreyden highlighted that foreign currency headwinds reduced reported international revenues by approximately $27 million.
The company's market capitalization averaged approximately $8.9 billion during fiscal year 2023, based on the volume-weighted average share price.
Shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 79.9 million on a diluted basis.
The board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $300 million, effective January 1, 2024.
In November 2023, Meridian Biopharma completed the acquisition of NovaTide Biosciences for an aggregate purchase price of $415 million.
The NovaTide acquisition added two late-stage peptide therapeutic candidates and a specialized manufacturing facility in San Diego.
Goodwill recognized from the NovaTide transaction was $178 million, with the remainder allocated to identifiable intangible assets.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
Environmental, Social, and Governance initiatives continued to advance, with the company achieving a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for total revenues in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.10 billion.
Earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $3.55 to $3.75.
The company expects R&D investment to remain elevated at approximately 17% to 18% of projected revenues.
Management anticipates that the Durham facility expansion will be fully operational by the third quarter of 2024.
Dr. Holt concluded her remarks by reaffirming Meridian Biopharma's commitment to scientific innovation, operational excellence, and long-term shareholder value creation.

Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Okonkwo and CTO Daniel Ferris, both of whom continue to lead the organization today.
Veltara reported total revenues of $487.3 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue, which forms the core of the company's recurring income, grew by 19.8% year-over-year to reach $341.6 million.
Professional services revenue accounted for the remaining $145.7 million, reflecting growing demand for implementation and customization engagements.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $298.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.2%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, totaled $241.7 million, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development.
Research and development spending rose to $89.3 million, up from $71.2 million in the prior fiscal year.
Sales and marketing expenditures came in at $112.4 million, reflecting the company's continued push into enterprise verticals.
General and administrative expenses of $40.0 million remained largely flat compared to the prior year, a sign of operational discipline.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $56.7 million, compared to $38.9 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 45.8% improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $42.1 million, or $0.87 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, came in at $78.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.1%.
The company ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $163.8 million and total liquidity of approximately $310 million including available credit.
Accounts receivable as of December 31, 2023, totaled $54.2 million, with a days sales outstanding of 40.6 days.
Inventory levels for the company's hardware-assisted edge modules stood at $18.7 million at year-end, up modestly from $16.3 million in the prior period.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $22.1 million, focused primarily on data center infrastructure and leasehold improvements.
Free cash flow for the year was $71.4 million, representing a free cash flow margin of 14.7%.
The company returned $15.0 million to shareholders through share repurchases during the fiscal year under its standing buyback authorization.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during fiscal 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting earnings for growth.
Headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 2,410 at the end of the prior year.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in product engineering and customer success functions, reflecting strategic priorities.
Veltara expanded its customer base to 1,214 enterprise accounts, a net addition of 183 customers during the year.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $358.9 million as of year-end, representing 19.6% growth on a year-over-year basis.
Net revenue retention rate was reported at 118%, indicating strong upsell and expansion activity within the existing customer base.
The company launched two major platform updates during the year, including the VeltaCore 4.0 release in March and the Nexus Analytics module in September.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior credit facility in mid-2022.
The company's international operations accounted for 28.4% of total revenue, with the EMEA region contributing the largest share at 17.1%.
Veltara entered the Asia-Pacific market through a strategic distribution partnership with Kensho Logistics Holdings in October 2023.
The partnership with Kensho is expected to add between $12 million and $18 million in incremental revenue beginning in fiscal 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Anand commented that the company is well-positioned to sustain double-digit revenue growth through continued product innovation and geographic expansion.
In November 2023, Veltara's board of directors approved a new $250 million senior secured revolving credit facility, which was drawn down in part to fund potential acquisitions.
The company completed the acquisition of Terralink Data Solutions for $67.4 million in cash and stock consideration, closing on December 15, 2023.
Terralink contributed approximately $3.1 million in revenue in the partial period following the acquisition close, with full-year contribution expected in fiscal 2024.
Veltara's management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $545 million to $560 million, implying growth of approximately 12% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 17% to 18%, reflecting expected operating leverage as the business scales.
The company expects to invest approximately $30 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, primarily related to cloud infrastructure expansion.
Veltara's board authorized an additional $25 million in share repurchases, bringing the total remaining buyback capacity to approximately $38 million.
The company also announced a strategic review of its legacy on-premise product line, which generated $22.6 million in maintenance revenue during fiscal 2023.
Management expects on-premise maintenance revenue to decline at a low-double-digit rate as customers migrate to the cloud platform over the next three years.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual filing include competitive pressure from larger vendors such as Oracle and SAP, as well as macroeconomic sensitivity in customer IT budgets.
The company noted that approximately 73% of its revenue is now derived from multi-year subscription contracts, providing meaningful visibility into future cash flows.
Customer concentration remains a risk, with the top ten customers representing approximately 19.3% of total revenue in fiscal 2023.
Veltara's platform currently integrates with more than 140 third-party enterprise systems, including ERP, TMS, and WMS solutions.
The VeltaCore 4.0 update introduced AI-driven demand sensing capabilities that the company claims can reduce forecast error by up to 35%.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual Net Promoter Score survey, improved to 67 in 2023, up from 61 in 2022.
The company maintains a strong intellectual property portfolio consisting of 47 granted patents and 23 pending patent applications as of year-end.
Veltara's audit committee engaged Deloitte & Touche LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the third consecutive year.
The financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and include consolidated results for all wholly owned subsidiaries.
Management's assessment found no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023.
The company's stock, traded on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker VLTR, closed fiscal 2023 at $34.72 per share.
Total market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $1.68 billion, based on 48.4 million diluted shares outstanding.
The stock appreciated by approximately 31.4% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by roughly 8 percentage points.
Insider ownership, including holdings by executive officers and directors, stood at approximately 12.7% of total shares outstanding.
Veltara's compensation committee approved an equity incentive plan refresh in February 2023, adding 2.1 million new shares to the available pool.
Long-term incentive awards for senior executives are structured as a mix of performance share units and time-based restricted stock units vesting over three years.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published in June 2023, disclosed a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions relative to 2021 levels.
Veltara committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its own operations by 2030, with an interim target of a 40% emissions reduction by 2026.
The company established a Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Council in early 2023, co-chaired by Chief People Officer Sandra Metz.
As of year-end, women represented 41% of the total workforce and 33% of vice president level and above roles, metrics management intends to improve further.
Veltara's defined contribution retirement plan covers all eligible U.S. employees, with the company matching up to 4% of eligible compensation.

Looking ahead, management remains confident that the combination of a growing ARR base, expanding international presence, and ongoing product innovation will drive sustained shareholder value creation.

Veltoran Industries closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term borrowings, carrying no outstanding debt obligations on its balance sheet.
The company operates primarily in the specialty chemicals sector, supplying advanced polymer additives to automotive and aerospace clients worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Heldren expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder meeting held in March 2024.
Veltoran reported total revenues of $2.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing an 11.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The revenue growth was largely driven by strong demand in the Asia-Pacific region, which now accounts for 38% of total sales.
In October 2023, Veltoran secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven international banks to fund expansion activities.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that gross margins improved by 120 basis points year-over-year, reaching 34.6%.
Operating expenses totaled $1.61 billion for the year, up modestly from $1.54 billion in fiscal year 2022.
The company's EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 came in at $412 million, comfortably exceeding the internal target of $390 million.
Veltoran employed approximately 9,800 full-time staff across 22 countries as of December 31, 2023.
The headcount represents a net increase of 430 employees relative to the prior fiscal year end.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $188 million, focused heavily on expanding production capacity at the Düsseldorf and Chennai facilities.
The Düsseldorf facility upgrade is expected to boost annual output of high-performance epoxy resins by 15,000 metric tons once completed in mid-2025.
Veltoran's research and development spending reached $97 million in fiscal year 2023, equivalent to approximately 3.9% of total revenues.
The R&D budget supported 14 active development programs, three of which entered advanced-stage customer trials during the second half of 2023.
Net income attributable to Veltoran shareholders was $214 million for fiscal year 2023, compared with $189 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $3.82 from $3.37, reflecting both earnings growth and a modest reduction in share count from ongoing buybacks.
The board approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an annualized payout of $1.12 per share.
This dividend level represents an 8% increase over the prior year's annualized payout of $1.04 per share.
Veltoran repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $54.60 per share, totaling approximately $65.5 million.
The share repurchase program, authorized at up to $200 million, still has approximately $134.5 million remaining under its current authorization.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $321 million, down from $358 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Management attributed the inventory reduction to improved demand planning systems implemented in the first quarter of 2023.
Accounts receivable totaled $389 million at year-end, yielding days sales outstanding of approximately 57 days.
The DSO figure compares favorably to the prior year's 63 days, reflecting tighter credit management policies enforced across all regions.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $276 million, up from $241 million a year earlier.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $224 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $412 million less capital expenditures of $188 million.
Veltoran's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.3%, slightly below the 23.1% effective rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The lower effective tax rate reflected the favorable resolution of a transfer pricing dispute with German tax authorities in the third quarter.
Segment results show that the Automotive Solutions division contributed $1.09 billion in revenue, growing 9.7% year-over-year.
The Aerospace & Defense segment generated $640 million in revenue, an increase of 13.2% driven by renewed long-term supply agreements.
The Industrial Specialties segment, Veltoran's smallest, posted revenues of $740 million, up 11.1% from the prior year.
All three segments delivered positive segment operating income, with combined segment margins expanding by an average of 80 basis points.
Chief Operating Officer Elena Voss highlighted the successful integration of the specialty resins business acquired from Tharex GmbH in late 2022.
The Tharex integration contributed approximately $85 million in incremental revenues during fiscal year 2023 and is now fully consolidated.
Veltoran's order backlog at year-end 2023 stood at $1.83 billion, a 6% increase compared to the $1.73 billion backlog at year-end 2022.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit ratings, with Standard & Poor's affirming a BBB+ rating and Moody's maintaining Baa1.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority, with Veltoran reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 8% year-over-year.
The company set a target to achieve a 40% reduction in absolute greenhouse gas emissions by 2035 relative to a 2019 baseline.
Veltoran launched its next-generation VelBond adhesive platform in February 2023, targeting high-temperature bonding applications in electric vehicle battery assemblies.
Customer qualification testing for VelBond progressed at three major automotive OEMs, with commercial launch expected in the second half of 2024.
The company signed a five-year exclusive supply agreement with EuroDrive AG in November 2023 for specialty coating additives used in drivetrain components.
This agreement is expected to generate cumulative revenues of approximately $210 million over its term, beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Veltoran's board of directors is composed of nine members, four of whom joined within the last three years, reflecting ongoing governance refreshment.
Independent director Dr. Sabrina Köhler chairs the Audit Committee, which oversees the integrity of the company's financial reporting processes.
The company uses Ernst & Young as its independent external auditor; the audit engagement partner rotated in fiscal year 2022 per regulatory requirements.
Management confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal year 2023.
Looking ahead, Veltoran issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 projecting revenues in the range of $2.62 billion to $2.70 billion.
The midpoint of the revenue guidance range of $2.66 billion implies approximately 7.7% growth over fiscal year 2023 reported revenues.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is guided to fall between $440 million and $460 million, with a midpoint of $450 million.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $200 million to $220 million, reflecting continued investment in production infrastructure.
The company expects the Düsseldorf facility to begin contributing incremental volumes in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltoran plans to expand its commercial presence in Southeast Asia, with a new application development center to open in Singapore by mid-2024.
The Singapore center will employ approximately 120 technical and commercial staff focused on polymer additive customization for local customers.
CEO Marcus Heldren stated that the company's capital allocation philosophy prioritizes organic growth investments, followed by bolt-on acquisitions and shareholder returns.
Veltoran evaluates potential acquisition targets in the range of $50 million to $300 million enterprise value, focusing on adjacent specialty chemical niches.
The company's supply chain resilience program, launched in 2022, has reduced single-source supplier dependency from 31% to 19% of total raw material spend.
Veltoran maintains a disciplined approach to pricing, implementing two price adjustment cycles per year aligned with raw material cost indices.
The annual report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and has received an unqualified audit opinion.
Forward-looking statements contained herein are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Velorix Technologies Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based logistics optimization platforms.
The following report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and reflects audited financial results prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Marchetti stated that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformational period for the company.
Velorix reported total annual revenue of $412.7 million, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Subscription revenue, which constitutes the core of Velorix's business model, grew 18.6% year-over-year to reach $338.4 million.
Professional services revenue declined modestly by 3.1%, settling at $74.3 million as the company continued its shift toward self-service onboarding.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $261.9 million, yielding a gross margin of 63.5%.
Operating expenses totaled $198.4 million, including research and development costs of $76.2 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures came in at $88.7 million, reflecting the company's aggressive customer acquisition strategy in international markets.
General and administrative expenses were $33.5 million, a figure that remained relatively stable compared to the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $63.5 million, compared to $44.1 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Velorix shareholders was $49.2 million, or $1.87 per diluted share.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor highlighted strong free cash flow generation as a key achievement of the year.
Free cash flow for the period reached $71.3 million, up from $52.8 million in the prior year.
Velorix ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $184.6 million on its balance sheet.
Accounts receivable stood at $62.3 million, with a days sales outstanding of approximately 55 days.
The company's total assets at year-end were $743.1 million, reflecting growth in both current and non-current categories.
Deferred revenue, a key indicator of future recognized income, increased 21.4% to $156.8 million.
Customer count grew to 4,820 enterprise clients as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,105 at the end of 2022.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, crossed the $350 million threshold for the first time in company history, reaching $353.6 million.
Net revenue retention rate was reported at 112%, indicating strong expansion activity within the existing customer base.
The company added 715 net new enterprise clients during the fiscal year, a record for any single fiscal period.
International revenue grew 29.7% and now represents 31.4% of total revenue, compared to 27.7% in fiscal 2022.
Velorix's European segment was led by newly appointed Regional President Elena Voss, who joined the company in March 2023.
The Asia-Pacific segment showed early-stage growth, contributing $18.3 million in revenue as the company expanded its Singapore and Tokyo offices.
During the year, Velorix launched two major platform upgrades, branded as VeloCore 4.0 and VeloInsight Pro.
VeloCore 4.0 introduced advanced machine learning capabilities that reduced average route optimization time by 38% for customers.
VeloInsight Pro, released in Q3 2023, provided real-time supply chain visibility dashboards and was adopted by 640 enterprise clients within its first quarter of availability.
The product development team, led by Chief Product Officer Nina Hargrove, expanded from 312 to 401 engineers during fiscal 2023.
Total headcount across all departments reached 1,847 employees at year-end, representing an increase of 213 from the prior fiscal year-end figure of 1,634.
Employee-related costs, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, represented approximately 58% of total operating expenses.
Stock-based compensation expense for the year was $22.1 million, consistent with prior-year levels after adjusting for new hire grants.
As of December 31, 2023, Velorix carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior revolving credit facility in September 2022.
The board of directors, chaired by longtime director Harold Fenwick, approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in November 2023.
Under this program, Velorix repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $31.40 per share during Q4 2023.
The company did not issue any dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its capital allocation policy of reinvesting in growth.
Velorix's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the statutory federal rate due to various R&D tax credits.
In February 2024, Velorix entered into a $150 million senior secured term loan agreement with a syndicate of lenders to fund its planned acquisition of RapidRoute Systems.
The acquisition of RapidRoute Systems is expected to close in Q2 2024, pending regulatory approvals in both the United States and the European Union.
RapidRoute Systems specializes in last-mile delivery optimization software and generated approximately $48 million in revenue during calendar year 2023.
Management believes the combination of Velorix's mid-market platform with RapidRoute's last-mile capabilities will create a comprehensive end-to-end logistics solution.
Sandra Marchetti noted that cross-selling opportunities between the two customer bases could add $30 million in incremental ARR within 24 months of close.
Velorix's board authorized the acquisition at an enterprise value of $310 million, representing approximately 6.5 times RapidRoute's trailing revenue.
Integration planning is already underway, with a dedicated team of 45 employees assigned to manage the transition process.
Raymond Okafor confirmed that integration costs are expected to range between $12 million and $18 million over the first two years post-close.
For fiscal year 2024, Velorix issued guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $475 million to $490 million.
The midpoint of this guidance range implies organic revenue growth of approximately 17% before accounting for any contribution from the RapidRoute acquisition.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to fall between $95 million and $105 million, assuming successful integration of acquired operations.
The company expects to add between 600 and 800 net new enterprise clients in fiscal 2024, driven by expanded go-to-market investments.
Velorix plans to increase its total marketing budget by approximately 15% in fiscal 2024 to support demand generation in North America and Europe.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are expected to be between $22 million and $27 million, largely directed toward data center infrastructure improvements.
The company will also invest an estimated $14 million in enhancing its cybersecurity posture following an industry-wide increase in ransomware incidents.
Velorix maintains cyber liability insurance with coverage limits of $50 million per incident and $100 million in aggregate per policy year.
The company has no material legal proceedings pending as of the date of this filing, according to its legal department's assessment.
Velorix's independent auditor, Thornton & Aldrich LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
The audit committee, led by independent director Patricia Holm, met six times during fiscal 2023 to review financial reporting and internal controls.
Velorix's internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the framework established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations.
Looking beyond the near term, management expressed confidence in the company's ability to reach $1 billion in annual revenue within five fiscal years.
Sandra Marchetti concluded her remarks by crediting Velorix's 1,847-person workforce as the primary driver of the company's continued outperformance.
The company's next earnings call is scheduled for February 28, 2024, at which time management will provide further details on Q1 2024 performance expectations.
This report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Investors are encouraged to review the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for complete financial disclosures.
Velorix Technologies Inc. trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLRX.
Veltrix Dynamics Corporation is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in precision robotics and smart manufacturing solutions.
The company was founded in 2008 by CEO Margaret Holloway and has grown steadily through a combination of organic expansion and targeted acquisitions.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltrix Dynamics reported total revenues of $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The strong top-line performance was primarily driven by robust demand in the automotive and semiconductor verticals, which together accounted for 61% of total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Osei attributed the revenue growth to successful penetration of European markets, where Veltrix established three new distribution partnerships.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 came in at $134 million, reflecting an operating margin of 15.8%, up from 14.2% in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98 million, or $2.34 per diluted share, compared to $81 million, or $1.95 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $189 million, a margin of 22.3%, which exceeded the company's own guidance range of $175 million to $185 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, marking the sixth consecutive year of dividend increases for Veltrix Dynamics.
Capital expenditures totaled $62 million during fiscal 2023, directed primarily toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading robotic assembly lines.
The company's research and development expenditure rose to $74 million, representing 8.7% of total revenues, reflecting continued investment in next-generation automation platforms.
Veltrix Dynamics ended the fiscal year with a total workforce of 4,820 employees, an increase of 310 from the 4,510 employees reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Merritt noted that the company hired aggressively in software engineering and data science roles to support the company's digital transformation roadmap.
The gross margin improved by 120 basis points year-over-year to reach 41.3%, benefiting from favorable product mix and operational efficiency initiatives.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $112 million, down from $131 million at the close of fiscal 2022, indicating improved supply chain management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior period, reflecting tighter credit policies and faster collections.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.50, reducing the diluted share count to approximately 41.9 million shares.
Free cash flow for the year was $127 million, representing a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 130% relative to net income.
Veltrix Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final outstanding senior note in March of that year.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $203 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
Management reiterated its commitment to maintaining a conservative capital structure while remaining opportunistic in pursuing bolt-on acquisitions.
In the fourth quarter alone, Veltrix generated $54 million in revenue from its newly launched VX-9 Series robotic welding platform, ahead of initial internal projections.
The VX-9 Series was recognized by the International Robotics and Automation Society as the most innovative industrial product of 2023.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrix Dynamics entered into a $150 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five major banks to support strategic growth initiatives.
The credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and has a five-year maturity extending through December 2028.
CFO Daniel Osei stated that the new financing arrangement provides Veltrix with significant strategic flexibility as it evaluates potential acquisition targets in Asia-Pacific markets.
Segment reporting revealed that the Industrial Robotics division generated $512 million in revenue, while the Process Automation division contributed the remaining $335 million.
The Industrial Robotics division's operating income of $88 million represented an operating margin of 17.2%, outperforming the Process Automation division's 13.7% margin.
Revenue from recurring software subscriptions and service contracts grew 28% year-over-year to $143 million, now representing 16.9% of total company revenues.
Management highlighted recurring revenue growth as a key strategic priority, as it provides greater earnings visibility and reduces cyclical revenue exposure.
The company signed 23 new multi-year service agreements in fiscal 2023, with a combined total contract value of approximately $410 million.
Veltrix Dynamics' backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $684 million, an increase of 18% from the $580 million backlog reported twelve months earlier.
The backlog strength gives management confidence in providing fiscal 2024 revenue guidance of $940 million to $970 million, representing growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
For fiscal 2024, the company expects adjusted EBITDA in the range of $210 million to $225 million, implying continued margin expansion.
CEO Margaret Holloway emphasized that Veltrix's differentiated technology portfolio and strong customer relationships position the company well for sustainable long-term growth.
The company completed its acquisition of Aurotek Systems, a small German sensor manufacturer, for €38 million in cash during the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
Aurotek Systems contributed approximately $14 million in revenue and a modest operating loss of $1.2 million to Veltrix's consolidated results following the acquisition close.
Management expects Aurotek Systems to reach operating breakeven by the second quarter of fiscal 2024 as integration synergies are fully realized.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased by $9 million to $97 million in fiscal 2023, largely reflecting higher variable compensation accruals tied to performance.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%, slightly lower than the 22.1% rate in fiscal 2022, primarily due to incremental R&D tax credits recognized in the period.
Veltrix Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit ratings, with Moody's affirming a Baa2 rating and S&P affirming a BBB rating during the annual review cycle.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 16.3%, above the internal hurdle rate of 12% and consistent with management's value-creation framework.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations declined to $29 million from $34 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting favorable actuarial assumptions and additional voluntary contributions.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal 2023 audit conducted by Grant Feldman LLP.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remain a core pillar of Veltrix Dynamics' corporate strategy, as outlined in the company's annual sustainability report.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9% year-over-year, making meaningful progress toward its 2030 net-zero commitment.
Board composition saw the addition of two new independent directors in fiscal 2023, bringing the total board size to nine members with a majority of independent representation.
Compensation for named executive officers was reviewed by the independent compensation committee and aligned with peer group benchmarks provided by Willis Towers Watson.
The annual shareholder meeting, held in April 2023, saw 94% of votes cast in favor of the company's say-on-pay resolution, reflecting strong investor confidence in executive compensation governance.
Veltrix Dynamics disclosed no material litigation contingencies as of the balance sheet date that would be expected to result in a significant adverse financial outcome.
The company's geographic revenue split for fiscal 2023 was approximately 58% North America, 27% Europe, and 15% Asia-Pacific, compared to 63%, 24%, and 13% in the prior year.
Asia-Pacific revenue growth of 38% was the fastest of any region, driven by new customer wins in South Korea, Japan, and Taiwan's semiconductor equipment market.
Veltrix's sales pipeline in Asia-Pacific exceeded $300 million as of December 31, 2023, validating the strategic decision to establish a regional headquarters in Singapore in mid-2022.
The company expects to open two additional application centers in the Asia-Pacific region during fiscal 2024 to support growing customer demand and accelerate pipeline conversion.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023, were $1.04 billion, compared to $962 million at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting growth in both tangible and intangible assets.
Total liabilities stood at $398 million, resulting in total shareholders' equity of $642 million and a book value per share of approximately $15.32.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.8 from 2.4 in fiscal 2022, underscoring the strength of its near-term liquidity position.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year totaled $55 million, of which $18 million related to the amortization of acquired intangible assets from prior acquisitions.

Looking ahead, CEO Margaret Holloway expressed confidence that Veltrix Dynamics is well-positioned to capitalize on the secular tailwinds of industrial automation, reshoring investment, and the global energy transition.
The company will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, in New York City, where management will present its updated three-year strategic plan and long-term financial targets.
Velantrix Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in rare metabolic disorders and orphan drug development.
The following excerpt is drawn from the company's Annual Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, prepared under the oversight of Chief Financial Officer Dr. Miriam Osei-Bonsu.
Velantrix Therapeutics was founded in 2011 and has since grown to become one of the more recognized names in the rare disease therapeutics space across North America and select European markets.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Ferrante stated in his opening letter that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformational period for the organization on multiple operational fronts.

As of December 31, 2023, Velantrix Therapeutics carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate capital strategy focused on equity financing and retained cash reserves.
Total net revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2% compared to the $426.7 million reported in fiscal year 2022.

Product revenues accounted for $431.6 million of total net revenues, driven primarily by the company's lead asset, VLX-204, an enzyme replacement therapy approved for a rare lysosomal storage disorder.
Collaboration and licensing revenues contributed the remaining $55.7 million, largely stemming from milestone payments associated with the company's partnership with Haldern Biosciences AG.
Gross profit for the period was $341.2 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 70.0%, consistent with the prior year's gross margin of 69.8%.

During the third quarter of 2023, Velantrix secured a $320 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of institutional lenders led by First Atlantic Capital Partners to fund pipeline expansion.

Research and development expenses for fiscal year 2023 totaled $198.4 million, representing 40.7% of total net revenues, as the company accelerated investment in its next-generation pipeline candidates.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $89.7 million, reflecting increased commercial infrastructure investments to support the anticipated launch of VLX-411 in early 2024.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $53.1 million, compared to an operating loss of $12.3 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a significant improvement in operational leverage.
Net income attributable to common stockholders was $41.8 million, or $0.87 per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $18.6 million, or $0.40 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The improvement in net income was partially attributable to a $22.4 million one-time gain recognized upon the favorable resolution of a patent litigation matter in the second quarter.

Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $612.5 million, with an additional $150.0 million in short-term investments, providing substantial liquidity to fund operations through at least fiscal year 2026.
Dr. Osei-Bonsu noted that the company's cash runway assumption is based on current operating plans and does not incorporate potential revenue from pipeline candidates still under regulatory review.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $34.6 million, primarily related to the expansion of the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity for VLX-204 by approximately 35% upon completion in the second quarter of 2024.
Headcount at Velantrix Therapeutics grew from 1,124 employees at the start of 2023 to 1,389 employees by year-end, representing a net increase of 265 full-time equivalents.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the clinical development and regulatory affairs functions, in line with the company's strategy to internalize late-stage development capabilities.
The company's pipeline consists of five clinical-stage candidates, with VLX-411 in Phase 3 and VLX-502 having recently entered Phase 2 trials in the fourth quarter of 2023.
VLX-411 is being evaluated as a first-in-class oral therapy for a rare enzyme deficiency disorder, and top-line data from the pivotal Phase 3 trial are expected in mid-2024.
If VLX-411 receives regulatory approval, management estimates a peak annual revenue opportunity of approximately $900 million across addressable global markets.
The company filed two new Investigational New Drug applications during 2023, for VLX-603 and VLX-714, both of which are expected to enter Phase 1 studies in the first half of 2024.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023 totaled $76.4 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 57 days, compared to 63 days in the prior year.
Inventory balances increased to $38.9 million from $29.1 million at the prior year-end, reflecting pre-commercial inventory build in anticipation of the VLX-411 launch.

The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 12.3%, significantly below the statutory federal rate, owing to the utilization of net operating loss carryforwards and orphan drug tax credits.

Velantrix recognized $18.7 million in orphan drug tax credits during 2023, consistent with its ongoing investments in clinical programs targeting diseases with fewer than 200,000 patients in the United States.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $874.6 million, an increase of $98.4 million from the prior year-end balance of $776.2 million.
The increase in stockholders' equity reflects net income for the period, stock-based compensation expense of $44.2 million, and proceeds from employee equity plan activity of $12.6 million.

Stock-based compensation expense is expected to increase modestly in fiscal year 2024, given the higher headcount and expanded equity incentive awards granted to new hires during the second half of 2023.
The company did not repurchase any shares of its common stock during fiscal year 2023, as the board prioritized capital allocation toward pipeline development and manufacturing infrastructure.

Velantrix's board of directors declared no cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting available capital into growth initiatives rather than returning cash to shareholders.

The audit committee, chaired by independent director Patricia Drummond-Hayes, completed its annual review of the company's internal controls over financial reporting without identifying any material weaknesses.
Independent auditors Whitmore & Greyfield LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.

The company is subject to various government price reporting obligations in the United States, and management has accrued $11.3 million for potential Medicaid rebate adjustments related to prior periods.
Velantrix maintains a co-promotion agreement with Nordica Pharma GmbH covering VLX-204 sales in Germany, France, and the Benelux region, which generated $31.2 million in revenue during 2023.
The co-promotion agreement with Nordica Pharma is set to expire in June 2025, and the company is currently evaluating whether to renew, renegotiate, or internalize those commercial operations.

Management has provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $530 million to $555 million, reflecting continued base business growth and a partial-year contribution from the anticipated VLX-411 launch.
The 2024 guidance assumes VLX-411 receives U.S. regulatory approval no later than September 30, 2024, and does not include any revenues from potential international markets for VLX-411.

Operating expense guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $490 million to $510 million, reflecting elevated R&D spending associated with the VLX-502 Phase 2 program and the Phase 1 initiation of VLX-603.
Chief Commercial Officer Derek Liang commented that the commercial team has already initiated payer engagement for VLX-411, with early discussions indicating a favorable coverage landscape.

Market research conducted by the company suggests that approximately 12,000 diagnosed patients in the United States are currently underserved by existing standard-of-care options for the disorder VLX-411 targets.

Velantrix has established a patient access program called VelaCare, which will offer co-pay assistance and free drug programs to ensure that eligible patients can access VLX-411 regardless of insurance status.
The company entered into a manufacturing and supply agreement with Cortland Biologics, Inc. in September 2023 to secure additional fill-and-finish capacity for VLX-411 ahead of anticipated approval.

Total milestone payments potentially receivable under existing collaboration agreements amount to approximately $685 million, none of which are guaranteed and all of which are contingent on future regulatory and clinical events.

The company's investment in Haldern Biosciences AG, carried at fair value on the balance sheet, was valued at $42.1 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting a $7.3 million unrealized gain recognized during the year.

Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a $3.8 million reduction in other comprehensive income for fiscal year 2023, reflecting the strengthening of the U.S. dollar against the euro and British pound.
Velantrix Therapeutics remains committed to environmental, social, and governance priorities, publishing its second annual sustainability report in conjunction with this annual report.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8% during 2023 relative to the 2022 baseline, driven by energy efficiency improvements at the Durham manufacturing facility.
Management believes that the combination of strong commercial execution, a robust pipeline, and disciplined capital allocation positions Velantrix Therapeutics for sustained long-term value creation.

This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those anticipated due to regulatory, clinical, competitive, and macroeconomic factors.
Velomark Industries, a mid-cap precision instrumentation company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company designs and manufactures advanced sensor arrays and data-acquisition hardware for industrial automation clients.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holden opened the report with a statement highlighting sustained operational momentum across all major business segments.
Velomark reported consolidated net revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior year.
Gross profit reached $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Quast noted that disciplined cost management contributed meaningfully to margin expansion.
Operating income for the full year came in at $94.1 million, up from $78.5 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company recorded an effective tax rate of 21.3 percent, consistent with management's earlier guidance.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $72.8 million, compared with $59.2 million in the prior-year period.
Diluted earnings per share were $3.14, beating the consensus analyst estimate of $3.01.
Velomark's Industrial Sensing segment generated $511.0 million in revenue, accounting for roughly 60 percent of consolidated sales.
The Data Acquisition Hardware segment contributed $336.3 million, reflecting 18.7 percent year-over-year growth.
Geographic diversification remained a strategic priority, with international sales constituting 43 percent of total revenue.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, posting a 22 percent revenue increase.
European sales grew by 8 percent despite macroeconomic headwinds and currency volatility.
Velomark's order backlog at fiscal year-end stood at $623 million, a record high for the company.
Management attributed backlog growth to multi-year contracts secured with three large automotive OEM clients.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $58.4 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Ohio.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $41.2 million, broadly in line with prior-year levels.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $112.7 million, an improvement of 19 percent versus the prior year.
The company returned $38.5 million to shareholders through quarterly cash dividends totaling $1.66 per share.
Velomark also completed a $50 million share repurchase program that had been authorized by the Board in early 2022.
The repurchase program retired approximately 1.6 million shares at an average price of $31.25 per share.
Chief Human Resources Officer Sandra Lin reported that total global headcount rose to 5,840 employees by year-end.
The company added 420 net new employees during fiscal 2023, largely in engineering and manufacturing roles.
Research and development expenditure reached $67.4 million, representing 7.96 percent of net revenue.
Velomark launched four new product lines during fiscal 2023, including its flagship ProSense X7 sensor module.
The ProSense X7 was recognized by industry publication AutomationWeek as the most innovative sensor product of 2023.
Management reiterated its commitment to achieving a long-term R&D intensity target of approximately 8 percent of revenue.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Velomark had fully retired its outstanding long-term debt, leaving the balance sheet entirely free of interest-bearing obligations.
Inventory levels at year-end were $134.8 million, slightly elevated compared to the prior year but within management's target range.
The inventory build was intentional, designed to buffer against component shortages that disrupted operations in fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 51 days a year earlier, reflecting tighter credit management.
The current ratio stood at 2.4 at fiscal year-end, indicating a comfortable liquidity position.
In October 2023, Velomark established a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five commercial banks to support working capital needs and potential acquisitions.
The facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and matures in October 2028.
Raymond Quast emphasized that the credit facility provides strategic financial flexibility without imposing near-term earnings dilution.
Velomark's total assets at December 31, 2023, were $1.02 billion, up from $924 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Total shareholders' equity grew to $618 million from $567 million over the same period.
Return on equity for the year was 12.2 percent, surpassing the company's internal threshold of 10 percent.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.44 per share payable in January 2024, consistent with the prior quarter.
Velomark's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $38.72 per share, reflecting a 27 percent total shareholder return including dividends.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $896 million.
Patricia Holden highlighted that customer retention rates remained above 94 percent across all major segments.
The Net Promoter Score for the Industrial Sensing segment improved by 11 points year-over-year.
Velomark expects fiscal year 2024 net revenue to be in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted diluted EPS guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $3.40 to $3.55.
The company anticipates gross margins will remain in the 36 to 38 percent range in fiscal 2024.
Planned capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are $65 million to $72 million, supporting ongoing capacity expansion.
Management noted that supply chain risks remain a key uncertainty embedded in the fiscal 2024 outlook.
Velomark will also pursue tuck-in acquisitions in the sensor calibration space, according to Chief Strategy Officer Marcus Vail.
Two acquisition targets have been identified and are currently in due diligence, though no agreements have been finalized.
The company maintains a disciplined acquisition framework requiring minimum internal rate of return thresholds of 15 percent.
Environmental and sustainability initiatives resulted in a 9 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions intensity during 2023.
Velomark achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification for its primary Ohio manufacturing facility.
The Audit Committee confirmed that KPMG will continue as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2024.
Overall, Velomark's fiscal year 2023 performance demonstrates the strength of its diversified product portfolio and long-term customer relationships.
Management expressed confidence that the company is well-positioned to deliver sustained value creation for shareholders over the coming years.
Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on developing and commercializing targeted therapies for rare metabolic disorders and oncology indications.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Castleford presented the annual report to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Veltrano Biosciences reported total revenues of $847.3 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product, Veltrex, generated net product sales of $512.6 million, accounting for approximately 60.5% of total revenues.
Chief Financial Officer Roland Temblay noted that gross margins improved to 74.2% in 2023 compared to 71.8% in 2022, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198.4 million, representing 23.4% of total revenues for the fiscal year.
The company employed approximately 3,200 full-time equivalent staff as of December 31, 2023, up from 2,950 at the close of the previous fiscal year.
Veltrano's commercial organization grew by 18%, reflecting investments in field medical teams and market access specialists.
Operating income reached $154.7 million in 2023, compared to $121.3 million in 2022, a year-over-year improvement of 27.5%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $118.9 million, or $2.34 per diluted share.
The company's balance sheet remained strong, with cash and cash equivalents of $423.1 million at year-end.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $231.6 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.3%.
Dr. Castleford emphasized that Veltrano's pipeline is the most diverse and advanced in the company's 14-year history.
The lead pipeline candidate, VLT-402, is currently in Phase III clinical trials for the treatment of metachromatic leukodystrophy.
Interim data from the VLT-402 trial, presented at the International Congress of Metabolic Disorders, demonstrated statistically significant improvements in motor function scores.
The company expects to file a New Drug Application for VLT-402 with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in the second half of 2024.
A second pipeline asset, VLT-519, entered Phase II trials in early January 2023 for a rare pediatric oncology indication.
Veltrano's international operations contributed $201.4 million to total revenues, with Europe representing the largest geographic segment outside North America.
The company received full European Medicines Agency approval for Veltrex in late 2022, enabling commercial launches across 14 European Union member states during 2023.
Royalty revenues from licensing arrangements with Hamasaki Therapeutics totaled $43.7 million, consistent with contractual tiered royalty rates.
Contract manufacturing revenues declined modestly to $89.6 million, reflecting the planned wind-down of a legacy agreement with Praxis Biopharma.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $267.5 million, an increase of 8.9% versus the prior year.
The increase in SG&A was primarily attributable to expanded commercial infrastructure in Europe and increased stock-based compensation expense.
Total operating expenses, including cost of goods sold, R&D, and SG&A, amounted to $692.6 million for the year.
The company recorded a $14.2 million restructuring charge in the second quarter related to the consolidation of two legacy manufacturing sites in Ohio.
Capital expenditures for the year were $67.3 million, directed primarily toward expanding the company's biologics manufacturing capacity in Durham, North Carolina.
The Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in August 2023.
Under the program, Veltrano repurchased 2.1 million shares at an average price of $38.47, for a total outlay of approximately $80.8 million through year-end.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, modestly above the prior year rate of 20.7% due to the mix of jurisdictional earnings.
Veltrano's long-term debt consisted solely of a $200 million senior unsecured term loan issued in 2019, maturing in 2026.
The term loan carried a fixed interest rate of 4.25% per annum, and the company remained current on all scheduled principal and interest payments.
Net cash provided by operating activities was $187.6 million, reflecting strong commercial execution and disciplined working capital management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collection processes.
Inventory levels at year-end were $89.2 million, representing approximately 4.1 months of supply for Veltrex based on forecasted demand.
The company maintained a current ratio of 3.4x and a quick ratio of 2.9x as of December 31, 2023, underscoring its liquidity position.
Veltrano declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters of 2023, for a total annual dividend of $0.48 per share.
Total dividends paid to common shareholders during 2023 amounted to $24.3 million.
The company's stock price appreciated 34% over the course of 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index by approximately 19 percentage points.
Veltrano was added to the S&P MidCap 400 Index effective June 19, 2023, which management views as a validation of the company's growing market presence.
Mr. Temblay stated that the company's net leverage ratio stood at 0.4x adjusted EBITDA at year-end, reflecting a highly conservative capital structure.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Young LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano's enterprise value at December 31, 2023 was approximately $2.1 billion based on the closing share price of $41.22.
The company's market capitalization exceeded $2.0 billion for the first time in its history, crossing that threshold in October 2023.
Total stockholders' equity at fiscal year-end was $734.8 million, compared to $652.1 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in stockholders' equity reflected retained earnings growth partially offset by share repurchase activity and dividend payments.
Veltrano Biosciences carried no outstanding external borrowings or financial obligations beyond normal trade payables as of December 31, 2023.
During 2023, Veltrano launched two new product formulations: a pediatric liquid suspension of Veltrex and an extended-release tablet indicated for adult patients.
The pediatric suspension, branded Veltrex-PD, received FDA approval in February 2023 and achieved $28.4 million in net sales within its first ten months on the market.
Commercial uptake of Veltrex-PD exceeded internal launch projections by approximately 15%, according to Vice President of Commercial Strategy Priya Ananthakrishnan.
The extended-release formulation, Veltrex-XR, received FDA approval in September 2023 and generated $11.7 million in net sales during its brief initial launch period.
Management is optimistic that Veltrex-XR will achieve full commercial ramp by mid-2024 as formulary access continues to expand across major payers.
In January 2024, Veltrano completed the drawdown of a $350 million revolving credit facility secured from a syndicate of seven leading financial institutions.
The credit facility was established to provide financial flexibility for potential business development transactions and general corporate purposes.
Dr. Castleford indicated that the company is actively evaluating bolt-on acquisition opportunities in the rare disease space with enterprise values between $100 million and $500 million.
The company's medical affairs team published 23 peer-reviewed manuscripts in 2023, a record for Veltrano, underscoring its commitment to scientific leadership.
Veltrano's patient support program, VeltraAssist, enrolled more than 4,700 patients during 2023, providing copay assistance and adherence support services.
The company achieved a net promoter score of 74 among prescribing physicians in its annual market research survey, up from 68 in the prior year.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with Veltrano reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% year-over-year.
The company set a target to achieve carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2035, consistent with its updated sustainability strategy.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management provided revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $960 million, implying growth of approximately 8.6% to 13.3% over 2023.
Adjusted EPS guidance for 2024 was set at $2.65 to $2.85 per diluted share, reflecting continued operating leverage and anticipated contributions from new product launches.
The Board of Directors and executive leadership team expressed confidence that Veltrano Biosciences is well-positioned to deliver sustained value to shareholders in 2024 and beyond.
Velorak Industries, a mid-cap precision instrumentation and sensor technology company headquartered in Eindhoven, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marta Svilenova opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's strategic trajectory and operational resilience.
Velorak Industries reported consolidated annual revenue of €1.47 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company attributed this growth primarily to strong demand in its industrial automation and medical diagnostics segments.
Chief Financial Officer Pieter van der Hooft noted that gross margins improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.7%.
Operating income for the full year came in at €189 million, up from €162 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €134 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 22.1%.
Earnings per diluted share stood at €2.87, compared to €2.44 in the previous fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved a final dividend of €0.72 per share, to be paid in March 2024 to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
This represents an increase of 8% over the prior year's total dividend distribution.
Velorak's industrial automation segment posted revenues of €621 million, driven by increased adoption of its SensorCore 7 platform across European manufacturing clients.
The medical diagnostics division contributed €418 million in revenue, benefiting from expanded distribution partnerships in Southeast Asia.
The environmental monitoring segment, which Velorak formally launched in mid-2021, reached €273 million in revenue, ahead of internal projections.
The remaining €158 million in revenue came from services and aftermarket parts, which grew 14% year-over-year.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €97 million, focused largely on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Brno, Czech Republic facility.
Research and development spending reached €148 million, or approximately 10.1% of total revenue, consistent with Velorak's long-term innovation investment target.
The company employed 8,420 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 from the prior year-end figure.
This headcount growth reflects targeted hiring in engineering and software development roles to support next-generation product development.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were €201 million, a modest increase of 3.2% relative to the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 came in at €247 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.8%.
Free cash flow for the year was €152 million, reflecting strong cash conversion despite elevated capital spending.
Marta Svilenova emphasized that the company's disciplined approach to working capital management was a key driver of cash generation.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €184 million, a reduction of €23 million compared to December 31, 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days, down from 52 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter collections processes.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of €2.1 billion and total equity of €910 million at year-end.
Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2023, totaled €388 million, providing Velorak with significant liquidity headroom.
Long-term debt outstanding as of December 31, 2023, stood at €215 million, consisting of a term loan maturing in 2027.
The company successfully refinanced this term loan in October 2023 at a more favorable spread of EURIBOR plus 110 basis points.
Velorak entered fiscal year 2023 with a completely clean balance sheet, carrying no external borrowings or outstanding credit facilities of any kind.
Pieter van der Hooft discussed the company's capital allocation priorities, emphasizing a preference for organic investment over large acquisitions.
He noted that Velorak's net leverage ratio stood at 0.4x adjusted EBITDA, well below the covenant threshold of 3.0x.
The company repurchased €45 million of its own shares during the year under the buyback program authorized in January 2023.
Total shareholder returns for fiscal 2023, including dividends and share price appreciation, exceeded 21%.
In November 2023, Velorak completed a €250 million syndicated revolving credit facility arranged by a consortium of four European banks, which remained undrawn at year-end.
Analysts on the earnings call noted the strong performance in the medical diagnostics division and asked about the pipeline for new product introductions.
Svilenova outlined three forthcoming product launches scheduled for the first half of 2024, including the SpectraSense 4 analyzer and two companion software modules.
She expressed particular enthusiasm for SpectraSense 4, calling it the most technically advanced product in the company's history.
The industrial automation segment is expected to benefit from increased infrastructure investment across European Union member states through 2025.
Velorak's order backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at €834 million, up 17% from the €713 million backlog recorded at the end of fiscal 2022.
This backlog growth provides meaningful revenue visibility for the first half of fiscal 2024.
The company's largest customer, a major German automotive systems integrator, accounted for 11.4% of total revenues in fiscal 2023.
Geographic revenue breakdown shows that 58% of total revenues were generated in Europe, 24% in Asia-Pacific, and 18% in the Americas.
The Asia-Pacific growth rate of 18.7% year-over-year was the fastest of any region for Velorak in fiscal 2023.
Velorak's environmental monitoring segment secured a multi-year supply agreement with a Nordic energy utility in Q4 2023, valued at approximately €85 million over five years.
The company expects to recognize the majority of this contract revenue beginning in fiscal 2025.
Marta Svilenova reiterated the company's commitment to sustainability, highlighting a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions relative to the 2020 baseline.
Velorak achieved an MSCI ESG rating of AA in its latest assessment, an improvement from the prior A rating.
The company's social responsibility report, published alongside the annual results, detailed progress on diversity targets, with women now comprising 34% of senior management roles.
Operating cash flow before working capital changes was €229 million for the fiscal year.
Depreciation and amortization totaled €58 million, reflecting the company's asset-light business model outside of its core manufacturing operations.
The effective R&D capitalization rate was 23%, consistent with prior-year accounting treatment and applicable IFRS standards.
Velorak's tax position benefited from Dutch innovation box regime treatment applied to qualifying intellectual property income.
The statutory corporate income tax rate in the Netherlands was 25.8%, while Velorak's effective rate was reduced to 22.1% through these regime benefits.
