Pieter van der Hooft confirmed that no material tax disputes or contingent liabilities related to tax were pending as of the reporting date.
The company's auditor, a Big Four firm, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Two independent directors joined Velorak's supervisory board during fiscal 2023, strengthening governance oversight.
Remuneration for the executive team was aligned with long-term value creation metrics, including relative total shareholder return versus a peer index.
Svilenova's total compensation for fiscal 2023 was €2.4 million, consisting of base salary, annual bonus, and long-term incentive awards.
Looking ahead, Velorak provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance of €1.58 billion to €1.63 billion, implying growth of approximately 7.5% to 10.9%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set in the range of 17.0% to 17.5%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage.
The company plans to increase its R&D investment to approximately 10.5% of revenue in fiscal 2024 to accelerate product development cycles.
Marta Svilenova concluded her remarks by thanking Velorak's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and confidence in the business.
She emphasized that the company enters fiscal 2024 from a position of operational strength, financial flexibility, and a clear strategic vision for sustainable growth.
Velorak's investor day is scheduled for April 2024, at which management intends to provide a detailed medium-term strategic plan through fiscal year 2027.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. is a mid-cap manufacturer specializing in sustainable flexible packaging for the food and beverage sector.
The company was founded in 2004 and is headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Whitmore presented the annual results to investors during a conference call on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Derek Lau noted that fiscal year 2023 was a transformative period for the organization.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margin expanded by 120 basis points to 34.6%, driven by favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
The North American segment contributed $892 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 60.7% of total sales.
International revenue, primarily from Western Europe and Southeast Asia, grew 14.2% year-over-year to $578 million.
Operating income for the full year was $198 million, compared with $174 million in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $267 million, reflecting a margin of 18.2% on a consolidated basis.
Capital expenditures totaled $84 million for the year, concentrated largely in automated lamination equipment and facility upgrades in Ohio.
The company generated free cash flow of $143 million, a record high for Meridian Packaging Solutions.
Sandra Whitmore credited the strong cash generation to disciplined working capital management and improved receivables collection cycles.
Headcount at year-end stood at 6,200 full-time employees across fourteen manufacturing facilities.
Meridian Packaging Solutions carried no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, having retired its remaining obligations earlier in the fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter.
The dividend payout ratio for fiscal year 2023 was approximately 28%, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Derek Lau highlighted that inventory levels declined by 11% from the prior year-end, reflecting improved demand forecasting.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $41.30 per share, totaling approximately $86.7 million.
In October 2023, Meridian entered into a $350 million senior secured credit facility with a syndicate of five lenders to fund strategic acquisitions.
The acquisition of GreenWrap Technologies, a compostable film specialist based in Portland, Oregon, was completed in November 2023 for $215 million.
GreenWrap contributed approximately $18 million in revenue during the final six weeks of fiscal 2023.
Management expects GreenWrap to generate between $90 million and $100 million in annual revenue once fully integrated.
The integration is expected to be completed within eighteen months of the acquisition closing date.
Research and development spending increased 22% year-over-year to $31 million, emphasizing bio-based barrier coating technologies.
Meridian's patent portfolio expanded to 143 active patents by year-end, up from 119 in fiscal year 2022.
The company launched three new product lines in 2023, including the EcoSeal Pro series targeting fresh produce packaging.
EcoSeal Pro achieved $42 million in first-year revenue, exceeding the initial internal forecast of $35 million.
Market share in the North American flexible packaging segment rose to an estimated 8.4%, up from 7.9% in the prior year.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $187 million, representing 12.7% of total revenue.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.1%, slightly below the prior-year rate of 24.0%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $142 million, or $2.18 per diluted share.
Earnings per share grew 11.8% compared with the $1.95 per diluted share reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.3x at year-end, reflecting solid short-term liquidity.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days from 44 days in the prior fiscal year.
Accounts payable days outstanding extended slightly to 52 days, consistent with renegotiated supplier payment terms.
Sandra Whitmore emphasized that sustainability remains central to Meridian's long-term value creation strategy.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 17% relative to the 2019 baseline.
Renewable energy now powers 61% of Meridian's total manufacturing energy consumption, up from 48% in 2022.
The Charlotte headquarters achieved LEED Platinum certification in September 2023.
Employee voluntary turnover declined to 9.2% in fiscal 2023, compared with 13.7% in fiscal 2022.
Meridian launched a new employee stock ownership program in April 2023 for hourly manufacturing workers.
Derek Lau stated that the ESOP is expected to improve retention and align worker incentives with long-term shareholder value.
The company's total backlog at December 31, 2023, was $310 million, up 6% from the prior year-end.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $290 million to $310 million, implying margin expansion.
Management anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $95 million in fiscal 2024, primarily for a new facility in Monterrey, Mexico.
The Monterrey facility is expected to begin production in the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
Sandra Whitmore expressed confidence that the Mexico expansion will meaningfully reduce cost-per-unit in Latin American markets.
Meridian's credit rating was affirmed at BBB by S&P Global Ratings in January 2024, with a stable outlook.
The company has no material debt maturities scheduled until 2027, according to the notes in its annual filing.
Shareholder equity as of December 31, 2023, totaled $694 million, reflecting retained earnings growth and the share repurchase activity.
Derek Lau noted that the balance sheet remains well-positioned to support both organic growth and opportunistic M&A.
The audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers as the independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Meridian Packaging Solutions plans to host its annual Investor Day on April 22, 2024, in Charlotte, North Carolina.
Management expressed optimism that fiscal 2024 will represent another year of profitable growth and shareholder value creation.
Velarion Technologies Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, founded in 2009 by CEO Meredith Calloway and CTO Darren Osei, has grown to serve over 4,200 enterprise clients across 38 countries.
Velarion operates through three primary business segments: cloud platform services, enterprise resource planning software, and professional consulting.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar presented the consolidated results to the board on February 14, 2024, noting significant operational milestones.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 17.3% increase over the prior year's $1.57 billion.
Cloud platform services remained the fastest-growing segment, contributing $920 million, or approximately 50% of total revenue.
Enterprise resource planning software generated $589 million in revenue, a modest 8.1% year-over-year increase.
The professional consulting segment accounted for the remaining $331 million, reflecting a 12.4% improvement compared to fiscal 2022.
Gross profit for the full year was $1.02 billion, yielding a gross margin of 55.4%, up from 52.7% in the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $724 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending reached $218 million, representing 11.8% of total revenue, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $312 million, driven by an aggressive expansion campaign targeting the Asia-Pacific region.
General and administrative costs came in at $194 million, slightly below management's original forecast of $201 million.
Operating income for the year was $298 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.2%, compared to 13.8% in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $214 million, or $2.87 per diluted share, compared to $171 million in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $389 million, a 22.6% increase year-over-year, exceeding the guidance range of $360 million to $375 million.
The company generated $341 million in free cash flow during fiscal 2023, reflecting disciplined capital allocation and strong collections performance.
Capital expenditures for the year were $88 million, primarily directed toward data center capacity expansion in Europe and Southeast Asia.
Velarion ended fiscal 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $472 million, up from $318 million at the prior year-end.
Accounts receivable stood at $263 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 52 days, consistent with prior year levels.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $385 million, a 19% increase reflecting strong multi-year subscription contract signings.
Velarion's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, surpassed $1.1 billion for the first time in the company's history, ending the year at $1.13 billion.
Net revenue retention rate for the cloud platform segment was 118%, indicating robust upsell and cross-sell activity among the existing customer base.
Customer churn in the enterprise resource planning segment declined to 4.2%, the lowest recorded level since the segment was established.
The company added 612 net new enterprise customers during fiscal 2023, bringing the total client count to 4,214.
Average contract value for new bookings rose to $187,000, compared to $162,000 in fiscal 2022, reflecting successful movement into larger enterprise accounts.
Velarion's international revenue accounted for 38% of total revenue, up from 31% in the prior fiscal year, led by expansion in Germany, Japan, and Australia.
The Asia-Pacific region generated $287 million in revenue, more than doubling over the two-year period since Velarion opened its Singapore regional headquarters.
European operations contributed $412 million, supported by the acquisition of Dutch software integrator NovaBridge B.V. in March 2023 for €95 million.
The NovaBridge acquisition added approximately 280 employees and an established client base of 340 mid-market manufacturing firms.
Headcount at the end of fiscal 2023 stood at 9,847 full-time employees, an increase of 1,203 from the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in engineering, with 680 new hires in product development roles, and in customer success, with 310 new hires.
Employee attrition for the year was 11.4%, below the industry average of approximately 14%, which management attributed to enhanced compensation and remote work flexibility.
Velarion's stock-based compensation expense totaled $76 million in fiscal 2023, consistent with the prior year's $74 million.
The board of directors declared quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.48 per share for fiscal 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year's dividend.
Velarion repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $54.30 per share, for a total outlay of $174 million.
As of December 31, 2023, the company had $226 million remaining under its existing share repurchase authorization.
The board authorized an additional $300 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024, reflecting confidence in the company's long-term outlook.
Velarion's balance sheet reflected total assets of $3.12 billion at year-end, up from $2.74 billion at the close of fiscal 2022.
Total liabilities were $1.48 billion, including operating lease obligations of $112 million and deferred revenue of $385 million.
Shareholders' equity grew to $1.64 billion at the end of fiscal 2023, compared to $1.38 billion in the prior year.
The company's long-term debt consisted of $400 million in senior unsecured notes due 2027, which were issued in fiscal 2020 and carry a fixed interest rate of 3.25%.
Interest expense on the outstanding notes was $13 million for the year, unchanged from fiscal 2022.
Velarion maintained a net leverage ratio of 0.02 times adjusted EBITDA at year-end, reflecting the company's conservative approach to balance sheet management.
The company was in full compliance with all debt covenants throughout fiscal 2023, and no waivers were required.
Velarion launched three major product updates during fiscal 2023: Velarion Core 9.0, CloudBridge Enterprise, and the AI-powered analytics suite called Luminary.
Luminary, the AI analytics platform, was made generally available to enterprise clients in September 2023 and achieved $34 million in incremental revenue within its first full quarter.
CloudBridge Enterprise, released in April 2023, expanded Velarion's hybrid-cloud management capabilities and received an industry award from TechClarion Forum in October.
Management indicated that no new major product releases were anticipated in the first half of fiscal 2024, with development resources concentrated on platform stability.
At the close of fiscal 2023, Velarion carried no outstanding borrowings under any revolving credit facility, and the company's only financial debt remained the fixed-rate senior notes.
CFO Priya Nandakumar noted that inventory levels in the hardware accessories division remained lean, with turnover of 8.4 times during the year.
The hardware accessories division, while small relative to software, contributed $47 million in revenue and managed a year-end inventory balance of $12.3 million.
Inventory carrying costs in the hardware accessories division declined by 6% year-over-year due to improved supplier negotiations and demand forecasting tools.
Product warranty reserves were increased by $3.2 million in the fourth quarter to account for anticipated claims related to a connector component in the hardware product line.

In the third quarter of fiscal 2023, Velarion drew $175 million on a newly arranged revolving credit facility to partially fund the NovaBridge acquisition, with the balance repaid over subsequent months.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 19.4%, below the statutory rate primarily due to research and development tax credits and stock-based compensation deductions.
Deferred tax assets on the balance sheet totaled $58 million, partially offset by a $12 million valuation allowance related to certain non-U.S. jurisdictions.
For fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.10 billion to $2.18 billion, representing anticipated growth of 14% to 18%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $440 million to $465 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects to invest between $95 million and $110 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, primarily for cloud infrastructure upgrades.
Velarion's audit committee, chaired by independent director Samuel Ostroff, confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its audit without any material findings.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on Velarion's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.

Management expressed confidence that Velarion's diversified revenue base, strong free cash flow generation, and expanding international presence position the company well for continued growth through fiscal 2025 and beyond.
Vantara Therapeutics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, with no borrowings recorded on its balance sheet.
The biopharmaceutical company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, reported total revenues of $412 million for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Osei credited the strong performance to the commercial success of the company's flagship oncology drug, Nexivrel.
Nexivrel generated $287 million in net product sales, representing a 34% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Castillo noted that gross margins improved to 78% from 71% in the comparable period.
During the same twelve-month period, Vantara Therapeutics secured a $350 million syndicated credit facility to fund its pipeline expansion activities.
The company's research and development expenditures totaled $138 million, accounting for approximately 33% of total revenues.
Vantara employed 2,140 full-time staff globally as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 310 employees compared to year-end 2022.
Dr. Osei emphasized that the workforce expansion was largely driven by new clinical operations teams supporting three Phase III trials.
The company initiated enrollment in its VECTOR-1 study evaluating Nexivrel in combination with immunotherapy for non-small cell lung cancer.
Preliminary interim data from the VECTOR-1 trial demonstrated a 41% improvement in progression-free survival versus the standard-of-care arm.
Vantara's second commercial product, Brevanol, contributed $125 million in net product sales, representing a 19% year-over-year increase.
The Brevanol growth was attributed to expanded formulary coverage across several major U.S. managed care organizations.
Operating expenses excluding cost of goods sold reached $301 million, up from $248 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company reported operating income of $21 million, compared to an operating loss of $14 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $17 million, or $0.28 per diluted share.
This marked the first full year of profitability in Vantara's operating history since its founding in 2014.
Raymond Castillo highlighted that the company ended the year with $503 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $44 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at its Durham facility.
The Board of Directors approved an initial quarterly dividend of $0.05 per common share, payable beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
This dividend declaration reflected the Board's confidence in the durability of the company's cash generation profile.
Vantara also repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during 2023 at an average price of $38.70 per share.
The total share repurchase outlay of approximately $46 million was funded entirely from operating cash flows.
International revenues accounted for 22% of total revenues, with the European market representing the largest non-U.S. contributor.
The company received marketing authorization from the European Medicines Agency for Nexivrel in the second quarter of 2023.
A partnership agreement with Hirota Pharmaceuticals expanded Vantara's commercial footprint across seven Asia-Pacific markets.
Milestone payments received under the Hirota agreement contributed $15 million to collaboration revenues during the fiscal year.
Total collaboration and licensing revenues reached $28 million, rounding out the remaining portion of total reported revenues.
Dr. Osei noted that the company's pipeline now encompasses eleven clinical-stage assets, up from eight at the start of 2023.
Two of those assets are in Phase II development targeting rare hematologic malignancies with high unmet medical need.
Vantara submitted a supplemental New Drug Application for Nexivrel in the treatment of mantle cell lymphoma in November 2023.
The FDA has granted Priority Review designation for that submission, with a Prescription Drug User Fee Act target date in July 2024.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses for fiscal 2023 were $163 million, reflecting ongoing investments in the commercial infrastructure.
The effective income tax rate for the year was 18%, benefiting from federal orphan drug tax credits and research expense deductions.
Accounts receivable net of allowances stood at $74 million as of December 31, 2023, implying average days sales outstanding of approximately 65 days.
Inventory balances increased to $39 million, reflecting safety stock build-up ahead of the anticipated label expansion for Nexivrel.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 4.8, demonstrating a robust short-term liquidity position.
Total assets were reported at $891 million, a significant increase from $672 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Goodwill of $112 million on the balance sheet relates primarily to the 2021 acquisition of Embrix Biosciences.
That acquisition brought Vantara three preclinical assets and a proprietary antibody-drug conjugate platform technology.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets contributed $22 million in non-cash charges during fiscal 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation, depreciation, and amortization, came in at $87 million for the year.
Stock-based compensation expense totaled $31 million, reflecting grants to newly hired clinical and commercial personnel.
The weighted-average diluted share count for fiscal 2023 was approximately 60.7 million shares.
Vantara's market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $2.4 billion based on a closing share price of $39.50.
The company's stock price appreciated 62% during calendar year 2023, significantly outperforming the XBI biotech index.
Analyst consensus estimates had projected full-year revenues of $389 million, meaning Vantara exceeded expectations by roughly 6%.
Dr. Osei expressed confidence in achieving continued double-digit revenue growth in fiscal 2024.
Management provided initial fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $470 million to $490 million.
The midpoint of that guidance implies approximately 17% growth versus fiscal 2023 reported revenues.
Raymond Castillo indicated that the company expects operating margins to expand by 200 to 300 basis points in fiscal 2024.
This anticipated margin expansion reflects operating leverage as the commercial infrastructure scales relative to revenue growth.
Vantara plans to present updated pipeline data at the American Society of Clinical Oncology Annual Meeting in June 2024.
The company also intends to file an Investigational New Drug application for its lead preclinical asset, VTR-119, by mid-2024.
Total headcount is expected to grow to approximately 2,400 employees by the end of fiscal 2024.
Vantara Therapeutics remains committed to its mission of delivering transformative therapies for patients with difficult-to-treat cancers.
The Board and management team believe the company is well-positioned to generate sustainable long-term value for all stakeholders.
This annual report excerpt contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delfield and CTO Priya Anantharaman, who together hold a combined 18% equity stake.
Veltara serves clients across manufacturing, retail, and logistics sectors in over 34 countries.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's fourteenth consecutive year of positive operating cash flow.
Total revenue for FY2023 reached $1.42 billion, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 78% of total revenue, up from 71% in FY2022.
Professional services revenue contributed $148 million, reflecting continued demand for implementation and customization engagements.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $892 million, yielding a gross margin of 62.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $674 million, driven primarily by increased R&D investment and expanded sales headcount.
Research and development spending rose 19% year-over-year to $213 million, underscoring Veltara's commitment to product innovation.
The company's flagship product, VeltaChain Pro, was enhanced with 47 new features following a comprehensive customer feedback initiative.
Sales and marketing expenses were $298 million, representing 21% of total revenue.
General and administrative costs came in at $163 million, roughly in line with the prior year.
Operating income for FY2023 was $218 million, compared to $187 million in FY2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $171 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.6%, consistent with the company's historical range.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $304 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.4%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $487 million, up from $391 million at the close of FY2022.
Capital expenditures for the year were $62 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow generation of $241 million represented a 17% improvement compared to FY2022.
Veltara exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the final tranche of its 2018 term loan in March of that year.
Customer retention rates remained strong, with a net revenue retention rate of 114% across the enterprise tier.
The company added 312 new enterprise customers during FY2023, bringing the total active customer count to 2,847.
Average contract value for new enterprise deals rose to $187,000, up 9% compared to FY2022.
Geographic expansion continued with the opening of new regional offices in Singapore and Warsaw during the second half of the year.
In October 2023, the company secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five leading banks, which remains partially drawn at year-end.
The board of directors approved a $150 million share repurchase program in November 2023, of which $42 million had been utilized by December 31.
Veltara's headcount grew from 4,210 employees at the start of the year to 4,891 by year-end, a net addition of 681 employees.
The company's attrition rate for FY2023 was 11.3%, below the software industry benchmark of 13.8%.
CFO Renata Holloway noted that workforce efficiency improvements contributed to a 230-basis-point improvement in operating margin.
Veltara launched VeltaEdge Analytics in Q2 2023, a new AI-powered module targeting demand forecasting use cases.
VeltaEdge Analytics achieved $29 million in revenue in its first three quarters of availability, exceeding the internal target of $20 million.
The product was adopted by 204 customers by year-end, with a significant portion upgrading from existing VeltaChain Pro subscriptions.
International revenue grew 15.7% year-over-year, reaching $512 million and representing 36% of total revenue.
North American revenue grew at a more moderate 10.1%, reaching $908 million for the fiscal year.
The company's market share in the cloud supply chain optimization segment is estimated at 8.3% globally, according to third-party industry analyst firm LogiScope.
Veltara's share price appreciated 34% over the course of FY2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by 14 percentage points.
The company paid its inaugural quarterly dividend of $0.15 per share in December 2023, totaling approximately $12 million in aggregate.
Management has indicated that it intends to grow the dividend modestly over time, subject to ongoing cash flow performance.
The audit committee engaged external auditors Hartwell & Crane LLP to conduct the annual financial statement audit, which resulted in an unqualified opinion.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the FY2023 audit cycle.
Veltara's deferred revenue balance increased to $318 million at year-end, reflecting strong bookings activity in Q4 2023.
Remaining performance obligations totaled $1.87 billion at December 31, 2023, a 22% increase compared to the prior year-end.
This backlog provides significant near-term revenue visibility and supports management's FY2024 guidance.
For FY2024, Veltara is guiding total revenue in the range of $1.57 billion to $1.61 billion, implying growth of approximately 10–13%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for FY2024 is set at 22–23%, reflecting expected operating leverage as the business scales.
The company expects capital expenditures of approximately $70–75 million in FY2024, consistent with continued infrastructure investment.
Marcus Delfield, commenting on the outlook, expressed confidence in Veltara's competitive positioning and product roadmap depth.
Priya Anantharaman highlighted the planned release of VeltaChain Pro version 9.0 in mid-2024, which will incorporate generative AI capabilities.
The company anticipates that AI-enhanced features will meaningfully differentiate its offering from legacy on-premise competitors.
Veltara's balance sheet remains a source of strategic flexibility, supporting both organic investment and potential tuck-in acquisitions.
Management has identified three potential acquisition targets in the warehouse automation software space, though no definitive agreements have been signed.
The company believes that disciplined capital allocation combined with durable subscription growth positions Veltara for long-term value creation.
This report should be read in conjunction with the full audited financial statements and notes thereto included in Veltara's Annual Report on Form 10-K.

Verdant Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation and smart manufacturing solutions, is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, has established itself as a prominent player in the advanced robotics and process optimization sector over the past decade.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing robust demand across all major verticals.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Okafor outlined several key financial milestones achieved during the reporting period, underscoring the strength of Verdant Dynamics' business model.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing an increase of 11.3 percent compared to the prior year's $1.65 billion.
The industrial automation segment contributed $1.02 billion to total revenues, while the software and analytics division accounted for the remaining $820 million.
Gross profit for the fiscal year stood at $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.7 percent.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $389 million, which included $142 million in research and development expenditures.
The company's operating income reached $323 million, reflecting an operating margin of 17.6 percent, up from 15.9 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $241 million, or $3.87 per diluted share, compared to $198 million, or $3.19 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $408 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.2 percent.
Ms. Holloway emphasized that the strong profitability was driven in part by operational efficiencies realized through the company's multi-year lean manufacturing initiative.
The initiative, launched in 2021, has cumulatively generated approximately $95 million in cost savings through the end of fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $187 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the company's Columbus and Raleigh facilities.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was $221 million, compared to $174 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting disciplined capital allocation.
The company's cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, totaled $334 million, up from $289 million at the prior year-end.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $276 million from $251 million, consistent with the overall revenue growth trajectory.
Inventory levels at year-end were $198 million, broadly stable relative to the prior year's $193 million, indicating sound supply chain management.
As of December 31, 2023, Verdant Dynamics carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March 2023.
The company's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, reflecting confidence in sustained cash generation.
During fiscal year 2023, Verdant Dynamics repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $61.40 per share under its existing buyback authorization.
The total cost of the share repurchase program in 2023 amounted to approximately $129 million, reducing the diluted share count to 62.3 million.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023, was $1.17 billion, compared to $1.04 billion at the prior year-end.
In October 2023, the company entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven banks, which it drew upon to fund strategic infrastructure investments.
The new facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in October 2028, providing Verdant Dynamics with significant financial flexibility.
Mr. Okafor noted that the company's leverage profile remains conservative, with net debt well within the range considered optimal by the board.
During fiscal year 2023, Verdant Dynamics expanded its global workforce to 8,940 employees, adding approximately 620 net new positions compared to the prior year.
The headcount growth was concentrated primarily in engineering and product development roles, aligned with the company's strategic investment in next-generation automation platforms.
Verdant Dynamics launched three new product lines during the fiscal year: the VX-9 robotic assembly platform, the Clarity analytics suite version 4.0, and the NovaSense industrial IoT gateway.
The VX-9 platform received particularly strong market reception, with order intake of $143 million in its first full quarter of commercial availability.
The Clarity analytics suite version 4.0 attracted 218 new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total subscriber base to over 1,400 organizations.
International revenues grew 14.7 percent year-over-year, reaching $641 million and representing approximately 34.8 percent of total consolidated revenues.
Growth in the Asia-Pacific region was particularly strong, with revenues of $298 million representing a 22.1 percent increase over the prior year.
European revenues grew at a more moderate pace of 8.4 percent, reaching $213 million, amid continued macroeconomic uncertainty in certain key markets.
The company's North American revenues totaled $1.20 billion, growing 9.8 percent year-over-year, reflecting healthy demand from automotive, aerospace, and consumer goods manufacturers.
Verdant Dynamics was awarded a five-year contract by a major North American automotive original equipment manufacturer valued at approximately $220 million.
This contract, announced in the second quarter of 2023, is expected to begin generating revenue in the first quarter of 2024 and will involve deployment of the VX-9 platform.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $2.31 billion, an increase of 17.4 percent compared to the backlog of $1.97 billion at the prior year-end.
Management views the elevated backlog as a strong indicator of near-term revenue visibility and continued demand resilience across the company's served markets.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management is providing guidance for total revenues in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.09 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be between $440 million and $460 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $200 million to $220 million, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing capacity.
The company announced plans to open a new manufacturing and engineering campus in Austin, Texas, with an expected total investment of approximately $310 million over three years.
The Austin campus is expected to reach full operational capacity by mid-2026 and is projected to support up to 1,200 additional employees upon completion.
Verdant Dynamics also completed the acquisition of Praxis Sensing Technologies, a provider of industrial sensor systems, for a total cash consideration of $175 million in July 2023.
Praxis contributed approximately $29 million in revenues and $6 million in operating income to Verdant Dynamics' consolidated results for the period from acquisition through December 31, 2023.
The integration of Praxis is proceeding according to plan, with full systems integration expected to be completed by the end of the second quarter of 2024.
Chief Technology Officer Elena Vasquez highlighted that the combined sensor and analytics capabilities represent a compelling differentiator in the competitive landscape.
Verdant Dynamics' research and development pipeline currently includes fourteen active projects, with four expected to reach commercial launch in fiscal year 2024.
The company's intellectual property portfolio grew to 847 issued patents worldwide as of December 31, 2023, up from 793 patents at the prior year-end.
Customer retention rates remained high at 94.2 percent across the software and analytics segment, reflecting strong product-market fit and deeply embedded customer workflows.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with the company achieving a 13 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.

Verdant Dynamics remains committed to its target of carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2035 and continues to publish detailed sustainability disclosures in accordance with TCFD recommendations.
Velantrix Corporation, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 1994 by Dr. Margit Essen, has grown to become a leading producer of high-performance adhesive compounds and polymer coatings for industrial applications.
Chief Executive Officer Stefan Brouwer opened the report by noting that 2023 represented a pivotal year for the organization amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Chief Financial Officer Anneliese Vandermeer highlighted the company's continued focus on operational efficiency and strategic capital allocation.
Velantrix reported total consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase compared to the €1.71 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The adhesive compounds segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating €980 million, or approximately 53% of total revenues.
The polymer coatings segment posted revenues of €612 million, reflecting 8.1% year-over-year growth driven by strong demand from the automotive and electronics industries.
The specialty solvents division, the company's newest business unit, contributed €248 million in revenue, exceeding internal projections by nearly 12%.
Gross profit for the full year reached €534 million, yielding a gross margin of 29.0%, a modest improvement from the 28.4% margin reported in 2022.
Operating income came in at €218 million, reflecting an operating margin of 11.8%, compared to 10.9% in the prior year.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization totaled €301 million, with an EBITDA margin of 16.4%.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €142 million, translating to earnings per share of €2.87 on a fully diluted basis.
This compares favorably to the €121 million in net income and €2.44 earnings per share achieved in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €0.72 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.15 per share for 2023.
The 2023 full-year dividend represents a 9.5% increase versus the €1.05 per share distributed to shareholders in 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €97 million, primarily directed toward the expansion of the Antwerp production facility and investments in process automation.
Research and development spending increased to €64 million, representing 3.5% of total revenues, as Velantrix intensified its innovation pipeline.
The company employed 6,840 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023, compared to 6,510 at the end of 2022.
Headcount growth was concentrated in the Asia-Pacific region, where Velantrix expanded its Singapore and Osaka operations to meet rising regional demand.
The company's global manufacturing network spans eleven production sites across Europe, North America, and Asia.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet stood at €2.31 billion at year-end, an increase of €190 million from December 31, 2022.
Inventory levels were €215 million at fiscal year-end, reflecting proactive raw material purchasing ahead of anticipated price increases in early 2024.
Trade receivables amounted to €278 million, with days sales outstanding of 55 days, broadly consistent with the prior year's 53 days.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 totaled €183 million, compared to €156 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated free cash flow of €147 million during 2023, underpinned by robust operating cash conversion and disciplined working capital management.
Operating cash flow for the period was €244 million, up from €211 million in the comparable prior-year period.
CFO Anneliese Vandermeer emphasized that the strong cash generation position provides Velantrix with meaningful strategic flexibility heading into 2024.
During the year, Velantrix repurchased €35 million worth of its own shares under the buyback program authorized by shareholders in March 2022.
The share repurchase program had an authorized ceiling of €75 million, and the board indicated it may seek renewed authorization to continue buybacks in 2024.
Velantrix maintained investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P throughout fiscal year 2023, reflecting the company's solid balance sheet profile.
The Antwerp facility expansion, completed in the third quarter, added 18,000 square meters of production capacity and is expected to deliver incremental revenue from mid-2024.
CEO Stefan Brouwer noted that the Antwerp expansion positions Velantrix to capture a larger share of the growing European construction adhesives market.
The company launched four new product lines in 2023, including its EvoFlex adhesive series designed for flexible packaging applications.
The EvoFlex series received rapid market acceptance, contributing approximately €28 million in revenue within its first two quarters of commercial availability.
Velantrix also introduced the ThermoCoat Pro polymer coating, targeting high-temperature aerospace component applications.
ThermoCoat Pro has already been approved by two European aerospace manufacturers and is expected to contribute meaningfully to 2024 revenues.
The specialty solvents division signed a long-term supply agreement with a major South Korean electronics manufacturer in the second quarter of 2023.
This contract, valued at an estimated €95 million over five years, is among the largest single supply agreements in Velantrix's history.
Sales to customers in the Asia-Pacific region grew 14.2% year-over-year, now accounting for 29% of total group revenues.
European revenues, while still the largest geographic segment at 51% of total revenues, grew at a more modest 4.8% rate.
North American revenues rose 6.9%, supported by penetration of new industrial accounts and the full-year contribution from the 2022 acquisition of BindTech Inc.
BindTech Inc., acquired for €88 million in April 2022, contributed €74 million in revenue during fiscal year 2023 and is now fully integrated into the adhesive compounds segment.
Velantrix's management expressed confidence that integration synergies from the BindTech acquisition are tracking ahead of the original €12 million annual synergy target.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with Velantrix reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% year-over-year.
The company published its third consecutive sustainability report, reaffirming its commitment to achieving carbon neutrality across all manufacturing operations by 2035.
Velantrix completed its transition to 100% renewable electricity procurement at its five European production sites, ahead of the originally targeted 2025 deadline.
Water consumption intensity at manufacturing sites declined by 6.1% compared to 2022, reflecting ongoing process optimization investments.
The company also achieved its target of directing 95% of manufacturing waste away from landfill, completing this goal one year ahead of schedule.
As of December 31, 2023, Velantrix carried no outstanding long-term financial debt on its consolidated balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined deleveraging.
Supply chain resilience was cited as a key operational theme for 2023, with the company successfully diversifying its raw material supplier base to reduce concentration risk.
Velantrix reduced its top-five supplier concentration from 68% of total raw material spend in 2022 to 59% in 2023, improving sourcing resilience.
The risk management committee also completed a comprehensive review of the company's counterparty exposure across its financial instrument portfolio.
Looking ahead, Velantrix provided revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.02 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 10%.
Management guided for an EBITDA margin of between 16.5% and 17.5% for fiscal year 2024, reflecting anticipated operating leverage and continued cost discipline.
In February 2024, Velantrix entered into a €300 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight European banks to support working capital and future growth initiatives.
The 2024 guidance assumes stable euro-to-dollar exchange rates consistent with average rates observed in the fourth quarter of 2023.
Velantrix expects capital expenditure in 2024 to range between €110 million and €125 million, reflecting ongoing investment in capacity and digital infrastructure.
The company plans to hire approximately 400 additional employees in 2024, primarily in manufacturing, engineering, and commercial roles.
CEO Stefan Brouwer concluded the report by expressing gratitude to employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued confidence in the Velantrix strategy.
He reiterated the company's ambition to achieve revenues of €2.5 billion by fiscal year 2027, supported by organic growth and selective acquisitions.
CFO Anneliese Vandermeer added that the company's financial foundation is robust, and Velantrix is well-positioned to navigate any near-term macroeconomic uncertainty.
The annual report was reviewed and approved by the full supervisory board on February 28, 2024, and filed with the Netherlands Authority for the Financial Markets.
Velantrix shares closed at €38.45 on the Amsterdam stock exchange on the date of the report's publication, implying a market capitalization of approximately €1.9 billion.
The company's investor relations team will host an earnings call on March 5, 2024 to discuss the full-year results and 2024 guidance with analysts and institutional investors.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Lyon, France, reported its fiscal year 2023 results on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Fontaine opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's resilience amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Veltrano Industries operates across three core segments: Advanced Coatings, Polymer Solutions, and Agricultural Chemicals.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase compared to the prior year.
The Advanced Coatings segment posted revenue of €712 million, driven by strong demand from the European automotive sector.
Polymer Solutions contributed €680 million in segment revenue, reflecting steady growth in packaging applications.
Agricultural Chemicals, the smallest segment, generated €448 million, benefiting from elevated crop protection demand in Southern Europe.
Gross profit for the full year came in at €534 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 29.0%.
Operating income rose to €187 million, compared with €163 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a 14.7% year-over-year improvement.
Chief Financial Officer Étienne Moreau attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable raw material pricing and disciplined cost management.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Industries carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in November.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €94 million, largely directed toward capacity expansions at the Marseille and Düsseldorf facilities.
The company generated free cash flow of €143 million, a record for Veltrano Industries since its 2011 initial public offering.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €261 million, up from €198 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Étienne Moreau expressed confidence that the strong liquidity position would support both organic investments and potential bolt-on acquisitions in 2024.
In January 2024, Veltrano Industries finalized a €350 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven European banks to support its strategic growth agenda.
The Board of Directors approved a full-year dividend of €1.42 per share, an increase of 11% relative to the prior year's payout.
Total dividend distribution for fiscal 2023 amounts to approximately €56 million, based on shares outstanding of 39.4 million.
Veltrano Industries repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year under its ongoing buyback program authorized in February 2022.
The weighted-average diluted share count used for earnings-per-share calculations was 40.1 million shares.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 reached €3.06, compared with €2.67 in fiscal 2022.
Margaux Fontaine highlighted the successful commercialization of VeltraShield 900, the company's next-generation anti-corrosion coating launched in Q2 2023.
VeltraShield 900 secured contracts with four major European automotive OEMs within its first six months on the market.
The Polymer Solutions segment launched two new bio-based polymer grades in Q3 2023, aimed at food-grade packaging customers.
Customer adoption of the bio-based polymer grades exceeded internal forecasts by approximately 18% in the first quarter of availability.
Research and development expenditure for fiscal 2023 totaled €72 million, representing 3.9% of total revenue.
The company holds 47 active patents related to polymer chemistry and surface coatings technology as of year-end.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 6,840 full-time equivalent employees at December 31, 2023.
Employee headcount increased by 320 year-over-year, reflecting hiring in manufacturing and research functions.
The Marseille facility expansion, completed in October 2023, added 40,000 square meters of production space.
Energy costs per metric ton of output declined 8.3% in fiscal 2023, aided by the company's renewable energy procurement agreements.
Veltrano Industries sourced 38% of its electricity from renewable contracts during the year, up from 29% in fiscal 2022.
The company's scope 1 and scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declined by 11% on an absolute basis relative to 2022.
Sustainability Director Pauline Kessler noted that Veltrano remains on track to achieve its 2030 carbon neutrality target for direct operations.
Net working capital as a percentage of revenue improved to 16.4% from 18.1% in the prior year, reflecting inventory optimization efforts.
Inventory days outstanding decreased from 74 days to 63 days, driven by better demand forecasting and supplier coordination.
Trade receivables at December 31, 2023 totaled €298 million, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of 59 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was maintained at 1.2% of gross receivables, consistent with prior-year policy.
Tax expense for fiscal 2023 was €51 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 26.4%.
The effective tax rate benefited from research and development tax credits recognized in France and Germany.
Deferred tax liabilities net of deferred tax assets stood at €34 million at year-end.
Pension obligations across European subsidiaries totaled €89 million on a present-value basis, down from €97 million in fiscal 2022.
The decline in pension obligations was driven by an increase in discount rates applied in Germany and the Netherlands.
Veltrano Industries maintained full compliance with all debt covenants applicable to its existing financing arrangements throughout fiscal 2023.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in September 2023, with a stable outlook.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in a range of €1.92 billion to €1.98 billion.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 10.5% to 11.0%, implying continued improvement from the 10.2% achieved in fiscal 2023.
Veltrano Industries expects capital expenditures of €100 million to €110 million in fiscal 2024, prioritizing digital manufacturing investments.
The company anticipates that integration of automation systems at the Düsseldorf facility will reduce unit labor costs by approximately 6% by mid-2025.
Margaux Fontaine reiterated the company's commitment to delivering sustainable shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation.
Veltrano Industries plans to host an investor day in Paris on June 5, 2024, to present its updated five-year strategic roadmap.
The strategic plan will address opportunities in high-growth markets including Southeast Asia and the Middle East.
Chief Commercial Officer Bruno Salvetti noted that the company signed a memorandum of understanding with a leading Indonesian distributor in February 2024.
The Indonesian partnership is expected to contribute to the Agricultural Chemicals segment's revenue beginning in fiscal 2025.
Management expressed confidence that Veltrano Industries is well-positioned to capitalize on structural trends in sustainable chemistry and advanced materials.
The full audited financial statements, prepared in accordance with IFRS, are available in the company's 2023 Annual Report published on March 14, 2024.
Veltronix Corporation is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in precision robotics and smart manufacturing systems.
The company was founded in 2003 by CEO Marcus Delray and has since grown into a recognized provider of automated assembly solutions for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Veltronix reported total revenues of $1.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Irene Caldwell attributed the strong top-line growth to expanded contracts in North America and accelerating demand from European automotive OEMs.
Operating income for the period reached $312 million, yielding an operating margin of approximately 16.9%, up from 15.4% in fiscal year 2022.
The company's gross profit margin improved by 80 basis points year-over-year, driven by favorable product mix shifts toward higher-margin software licensing arrangements.
Veltronix's robotics segment contributed $1.02 billion in revenues, accounting for roughly 55% of total consolidated sales.
The smart sensors and analytics division generated $820 million in revenues, a 14% increase compared to the $719 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $198 million, or $3.41 per diluted share, compared to $171 million, or $2.96 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for the fiscal year was 24.1%, slightly below management's original guidance of 25.0%, due to favorable resolution of certain international tax disputes.
Veltronix generated $287 million in free cash flow during fiscal year 2023, reflecting disciplined capital expenditure management despite ongoing capacity expansion projects.
Capital expenditures totaled $94 million, representing a modest increase from the $81 million spent in 2022, as the company invested in its Columbus and Dresden manufacturing facilities.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, maintaining the dividend rate established in the second quarter of 2022.
Share repurchases during the fiscal year amounted to $145 million, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 2.1 million shares.
Veltronix ended fiscal year 2023 with a total headcount of 8,400 full-time employees globally, an increase of 620 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its outstanding $300 million senior notes in March 2023.
Total cash and cash equivalents stood at $541 million at year-end, providing the company with ample liquidity to fund strategic initiatives and potential acquisitions.
Veltronix's current ratio improved to 2.8 from 2.3 at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting stronger working capital management and accelerated receivables collection.
The order backlog reached a record $2.3 billion at December 31, 2023, providing approximately 15 months of forward revenue visibility.
Management noted that international orders, particularly from Germany, Japan, and South Korea, constituted 48% of the year-end backlog, up from 39% the prior year.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltronix entered into a $450 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven banks, replacing a smaller $200 million arrangement.
The new credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 135 basis points and matures in December 2028, with two one-year extension options available.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Meir noted that operational efficiency gains contributed meaningfully to the margin improvement observed throughout the fiscal year.
The company's robotics segment operating margin expanded to 19.2%, up from 17.5% the prior year, benefiting from automation of internal assembly lines at the Columbus facility.
Veltronix launched three new product lines during fiscal year 2023: the VX-900 articulated robot series, the PrecisionSense 4.0 sensor array, and the FlexAssembly modular workstation.
Initial customer reception for the VX-900 series has been encouraging, with over 200 units ordered across pilot programs in seven countries since its market introduction in September 2023.
The PrecisionSense 4.0 sensor array received certification from the International Electrotechnical Commission in July 2023, enabling sales in regulated industrial markets across Europe.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $132 million, or approximately 7.2% of revenues, as Veltronix continued to invest heavily in next-generation machine vision and AI capabilities.
The company secured three significant multi-year contracts during the fiscal year with combined contract values exceeding $780 million, bolstering the record backlog figure.
Marcus Delray stated in the annual shareholder letter that Veltronix is well-positioned to capitalize on the structural trend toward factory automation as labor costs continue to rise globally.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $198 million, representing 10.8% of revenues, compared to 11.3% of revenues in fiscal year 2022.
The decrease in SG&A as a percentage of revenues reflects ongoing cost discipline efforts initiated under the company's Project Streamline efficiency program launched in mid-2022.
Project Streamline has cumulatively delivered $47 million in annualized savings since its inception, exceeding the originally communicated target of $35 million.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $78 million for the fiscal year, including $22 million related to intangible assets acquired in prior-year business combinations.

Veltronix made no material acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, though management indicated that the company is actively evaluating several targets in the collaborative robotics and AI software spaces.
The company's geographic revenue mix shifted modestly during the year, with North America contributing 52% of revenues, Europe 31%, and Asia-Pacific 17%.
Asia-Pacific revenue grew 21% year-over-year to $313 million, driven by strong demand from South Korean electronics manufacturers and Japanese automotive suppliers.
The company's inventory turnover ratio improved to 6.1 times from 5.4 times in fiscal year 2022, reflecting better demand forecasting and lean manufacturing improvements.
Veltronix's days sales outstanding metric improved to 47 days from 53 days the prior year, indicating more efficient credit and collections processes.
The company recorded a $14 million restructuring charge in the second quarter related to the consolidation of two smaller service centers in the Midwest into the Columbus hub.
Excluding this restructuring charge, adjusted operating income would have been $326 million, representing an adjusted operating margin of 17.7%.
Irene Caldwell guided fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of $1.98 billion to $2.05 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 8% to 11% at the midpoint.
Management also guided fiscal year 2024 adjusted earnings per diluted share in the range of $3.70 to $3.90, representing 8% to 14% growth over the fiscal year 2023 adjusted figure.

The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to range between $105 million and $120 million, reflecting increased investment in Dresden and a new facility planned for Monterrey, Mexico.

The Monterrey facility, expected to break ground in the second quarter of 2024, will initially serve the North American automotive supply chain and is projected to create approximately 350 local jobs.
Veltronix's pension obligations decreased by $31 million during fiscal year 2023, primarily due to favorable actuarial assumption updates driven by rising discount rates.
The company's defined contribution plan, covering the majority of its U.S.-based workforce, recorded a $12 million employer contribution during the fiscal year, consistent with prior years.
Sandra Meir commented that Veltronix's talent pipeline remains strong, with the company's engineering apprenticeship program attracting over 1,200 applicants for 85 available spots in 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to progress, with Veltronix reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% year-over-year.
The company achieved a Scope 1 and 2 carbon intensity reduction of 18% against its 2019 baseline, putting it ahead of its internal 2025 sustainability roadmap targets.
Veltronix published its fourth annual sustainability report in February 2024, disclosing emissions data, water usage metrics, and supply chain audit results for the first time.
The company's total recordable incident rate declined to 0.62 per 100 full-time employees, a 15% improvement from the prior year's rate of 0.73 and well below the industry average of 1.1.

Customer satisfaction scores, measured through Veltronix's annual Net Promoter Score survey, improved to 67 from 61 the prior year, reflecting positive feedback on product reliability and after-sales support.
The company's warranty reserve stood at $38 million at year-end, down from $44 million the prior year, consistent with improved product quality metrics.
Veltronix repaid $300 million in senior notes during fiscal year 2023 using cash generated from operations combined with proceeds from the monetization of a minority equity stake.
The minority stake, held in a joint venture with German automation firm Hessler AG, was sold for $178 million, generating a pre-tax gain of $62 million recorded in other income.

Looking ahead, Marcus Delray expressed confidence that Veltronix's diversified customer base, record backlog, and strong balance sheet position the company for continued value creation through fiscal year 2024 and beyond.
The independent auditors, Grant & Holloway LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on Veltronix's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.

The audit committee of the board of directors has reviewed and approved all related-party transactions disclosed in the annual report, confirming compliance with Veltronix's conflict-of-interest policies.
Velorant Systems Corporation is a mid-cap software infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based logistics optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delray and Chief Technology Officer Priya Sundaram, both veterans of the enterprise software industry.
Velorant's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant transformation and measured growth across all primary business segments.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the $741 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship product, VeloTrack Pro, accounted for approximately 58% of total revenues, maintaining its position as the dominant revenue contributor within the portfolio.
Chief Financial Officer Annelise Korthauer noted during the earnings call that gross margins expanded by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 67.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million for the fiscal year, a figure that management characterized as consistent with the company's disciplined cost management framework.
Research and development spending increased to $112 million, up from $94 million the prior year, reflecting heightened investment in next-generation AI capabilities.
The sales and marketing division employed 1,840 full-time staff as of December 31, 2023, a headcount increase of 220 employees relative to the prior year-end.
Velorant's international segment, which covers operations in Europe, the Middle East, and the Asia-Pacific region, generated $203 million in revenue during fiscal 2023.
The European sub-segment alone contributed $118 million to international revenues, with particular strength observed in the German and French enterprise markets.
Customer retention rates remained robust at 91.3%, a slight improvement from the 90.7% figure recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company signed 47 new enterprise contracts with annual contract values exceeding $1 million during the fiscal year, compared to 39 such contracts in 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $214 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 25.3%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98.4 million, or $1.72 per diluted share, for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in October 2023, of which $42 million had been utilized by year-end.
Velorant's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 reflected total assets of $1.34 billion, up from $1.19 billion at the close of fiscal 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $318 million at fiscal year-end, providing what management described as a comfortable liquidity buffer for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable totaled $134 million, representing a days-sales-outstanding figure of approximately 58 days, which was within the company's historical range.
Velorant carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully repaid its prior revolving credit facility during the third quarter.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $61 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements across three campus locations.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 21.8%, slightly above the 20.5% rate realized in fiscal 2022, driven by changes in the geographic mix of earnings.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $176 million, reflecting substantial prepaid subscription agreements that will be recognized as revenue in fiscal 2024 and beyond.
Velorant's total headcount reached 6,720 employees globally as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 480 employees compared to the same date in the prior year.
In February 2024, the company secured a $275 million syndicated term loan to fund its planned acquisition of supply-chain analytics firm Nodalink Technologies.
The company's net promoter score improved to 61 in the annual customer satisfaction survey conducted in November 2023, up from 54 the prior year.
Velorant launched its new VeloEdge module in September 2023, targeting mid-market customers with a streamlined feature set and a lower entry-level price point.
The VeloEdge module had attracted over 310 paying customers within its first three months of availability, which management considered an encouraging early adoption signal.
Chief Revenue Officer Daniel Ferris attributed the acceleration in new logo acquisition to an expanded partner ecosystem that now includes over 220 certified resellers.
The partner ecosystem generated approximately $97 million in sourced pipeline during fiscal 2023, a significant increase from the roughly $61 million generated in fiscal 2022.
Velorant's stock price closed at $38.74 on December 31, 2023, implying a market capitalization of approximately $2.2 billion based on diluted shares outstanding.
The company returned a total of $42 million to shareholders through the buyback program during the year, with no cash dividends declared or paid in fiscal 2023.
Management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $945 million to $965 million, representing expected growth of approximately 12% to 14% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 25.5% to 26.5%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage from the growing subscription base.
Korthauer emphasized that free cash flow conversion from adjusted EBITDA is expected to remain above 75% in fiscal 2024, consistent with recent historical performance.
The company's cloud-hosted deployment model now accounts for 84% of total annual recurring revenue, up from 76% two years prior, indicating strong migration momentum.
On-premise license revenue declined to $43 million in fiscal 2023, compared to $58 million in fiscal 2022, as legacy customers continued to transition to the cloud platform.
Professional services revenue was $89 million for the year, flat relative to fiscal 2022, as implementation projects became shorter in duration due to improved onboarding tooling.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.1 as of December 31, 2023, reflecting solid short-term liquidity relative to near-term obligations.
Inventory of physical hardware components, used in on-premise deployments, totaled $8.2 million at year-end, down from $11.6 million the prior year given declining on-premise sales.
Velorant's audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitmore LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the third consecutive year.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements and on the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting.
During fiscal 2023, the company recognized a $4.7 million gain from the divestiture of a non-core analytics product line to a strategic acquirer.
This gain was excluded from the company's non-GAAP adjusted earnings metrics, in keeping with management's stated policy on one-time items.
Velorant holds 14 issued patents related to its route-optimization algorithms and an additional 6 patents pending as of the report date.
The company's stock-based compensation expense for fiscal 2023 was $54 million, an increase from $46 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting expanded equity grant activity.
Total stockholders' equity stood at $712 million at December 31, 2023, up from $636 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in stockholders' equity was driven primarily by net income generation, partially offset by share repurchase activity and stock-based compensation settlements.
Delray stated in his letter to shareholders that Velorant's core mission remains enabling global supply chains to operate with greater precision, transparency, and resilience.
The company plans to open a new engineering hub in Warsaw, Poland during the first half of fiscal 2024, with an initial target headcount of 150 software engineers.
Sundaram highlighted at the annual technology summit that the Warsaw facility will serve as the primary development center for Velorant's forthcoming machine learning forecasting suite.
Velorant's remaining performance obligations, a forward indicator of future revenue, stood at $1.02 billion at December 31, 2023, a 19% increase year-over-year.
Approximately 62% of remaining performance obligations are expected to be recognized as revenue within the next twelve months.
The company experienced no material cybersecurity incidents during fiscal 2023 and maintained its ISO 27001 certification throughout the year.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed meaningfully during the period, with greenhouse gas emissions per employee declining 11% relative to fiscal 2022.
Velorant achieved a 34% reduction in single-use plastics across its office facilities, meeting a sustainability target originally set in the company's 2021 ESG roadmap.
The compensation committee approved a 6.5% base salary increase for named executive officers effective January 1, 2024, reflecting strong company performance against annual targets.
Annual incentive payouts for fiscal 2023 were made at 112% of target for the executive team, reflecting above-target performance on revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and customer retention metrics.
Ferris was promoted to Executive Vice President of Global Revenue in January 2024, expanding his oversight to include the customer success and professional services organizations.
Velorant's board of directors consists of nine members, five of whom are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
The company will hold its annual meeting of shareholders on April 18, 2024, at which time shareholders will vote on director elections, executive compensation, and auditor ratification.

Meridian Catalyst Technologies, Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, marking a significant milestone in the company's decade-long trajectory within the specialty chemicals sector.

The report was presented to shareholders by Chief Executive Officer Dr. Elaine Forsythe, who joined the company in 2019 following a distinguished career at several multinational chemical conglomerates.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delgado noted that fiscal year 2023 represented the strongest top-line performance in Meridian Catalyst's operating history.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.7% compared to the $2.47 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company's core Catalyst Solutions division contributed $1.62 billion of total revenues, underscoring its continued dominance as the primary revenue engine for Meridian.

Environmental & Process Technologies, the second major segment, generated $810 million in revenues, reflecting an 18.3% growth rate driven by expanding regulatory compliance mandates across key markets.
The remaining $410 million in revenues originated from the company's Advanced Materials division, which experienced a more modest 6.1% growth rate amid softening demand in certain end markets.
Gross profit for the fiscal year stood at $987 million, translating to a consolidated gross margin of 34.8%, an improvement of approximately 120 basis points relative to the prior year.

Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $543 million during fiscal year 2023, encompassing selling, general, and administrative costs as well as research and development expenditures.

Research and development spending reached $178 million, representing 6.3% of total revenues, as the company accelerated investment in next-generation catalyst formulations for hydrogen production applications.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $621 million for the full year, compared to $527 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting EBITDA margin expansion of roughly 150 basis points.
Net income attributable to Meridian Catalyst shareholders totaled $312 million, or $4.18 per diluted share, versus $261 million, or $3.49 per diluted share, in the comparable prior-year period.

The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.4%, slightly above the prior year's 22.1%, largely attributable to a shift in the geographic mix of earnings toward higher-tax jurisdictions.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $224 million, focused primarily on capacity expansions at the company's manufacturing facilities in Houston, Texas and Antwerp, Belgium.
The Antwerp facility expansion, completed in the third quarter of 2023, added approximately 35,000 metric tons of annual production capacity for high-performance refinery catalysts.
Free cash flow generation for fiscal year 2023 was robust at $397 million, up from $328 million in the prior year, enabling meaningful capital returns to shareholders.

Meridian Catalyst's board of directors approved total dividend payments of $87 million during the fiscal year, reflecting a per-share annual dividend of $1.16, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
