Any future acquisitions will be subject to rigorous financial and operational due diligence, consistent with the company's disciplined capital allocation framework.
Veltora Industries maintains a strong relationship with its primary banking partners, including JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo, and HSBC.
The company's credit profile remains robust, supported by consistent earnings generation and conservative financial management.
Margaret Hollis reiterated the company's long-term aspiration to achieve $2.5 billion in revenue and 25% EBITDA margins by fiscal year 2027.
The board expressed confidence in the management team's ability to execute on the strategic roadmap and deliver superior shareholder returns.
Veltora's stock price appreciated 22% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Specialty Chemicals Index by approximately 9 percentage points.
Total shareholder return, including dividends, was approximately 24.3% for the calendar year 2023.
The company's annual report was certified by its independent auditors, Deloitte & Touche LLP, without qualification.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit process.
Veltora Industries will host its annual shareholder meeting on April 18, 2024, at its corporate headquarters in Charlotte.
Management will present a detailed five-year strategic plan at the shareholder meeting, covering growth investments, portfolio optimization, and sustainability commitments.
The company reaffirms its commitment to transparent financial reporting and consistent communication with all stakeholders.
Dennis Arquette concluded by stating that Veltora Industries is well-positioned to build on its fiscal year 2023 momentum and create lasting value for shareholders.

The forward-looking statements contained in this report are based on current expectations and are subject to risks, uncertainties, and assumptions that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Vortex Meridian Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates across three primary segments: robotics manufacturing, software integration, and field services.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Lundgren opened the report by highlighting the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a period of disciplined capital allocation and strategic investment.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year's $1.96 billion.
The robotics manufacturing segment contributed $987 million to total revenue, up from $891 million in fiscal year 2022.
Software integration revenue grew 14.2% year-over-year, reaching $673 million compared to $589 million in the prior period.
Field services revenue came in at $480 million, a modest 3.1% increase over fiscal year 2022's $465 million.
Gross profit for the consolidated business was $812 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 37.9%.
This compares favorably to the prior year gross margin of 35.4%, reflecting improved operational efficiency and favorable product mix.
Operating expenses totaled $524 million, including $198 million in research and development investment.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses accounted for $326 million of the total operating expense base.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $288 million, representing an operating margin of 13.5%.
This marks the third consecutive year in which Vortex Meridian achieved double-digit operating margins.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $204 million, or $3.82 per diluted share.
This compares to net income of $171 million, or $3.21 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $341 million for the full year.
The effective tax rate for the period was 22.7%, slightly above the 21.9% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $318 million, up from $247 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $376 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, a record for the organization.
Capital expenditures totaled $142 million, directed primarily toward expanding the company's Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading automation tooling.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $234 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
This dividend represents a 12% increase over the prior quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share.
Vortex Meridian repurchased $95 million worth of its common shares during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $155 million remained available under the $250 million share repurchase program announced in early 2022.
The company's total headcount at year-end was 11,400 full-time employees, up from 10,750 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in workforce was largely concentrated in the software integration and field services segments.
Patricia Lundgren commented that the talent investments made in 2023 position the company well for accelerated growth in 2024.
The robotics manufacturing segment saw margin expansion of 180 basis points, driven by procurement savings and lean manufacturing initiatives.
New product launches in the robotics segment included the VX-900 series articulated arm, which began shipping in the third quarter.
The VX-900 series garnered over 340 customer orders in its first six months of availability, exceeding internal projections by 22%.
Software integration continued to benefit from recurring subscription revenue, which now constitutes 61% of that segment's total revenue.
Annual recurring revenue within the software integration segment reached $411 million as of December 31, 2023.
Customer retention rates in the software segment remained strong at 94%, consistent with the prior year.
The field services segment expanded its geographic footprint, entering two new markets in the Midwest and Southeast during the year.
Management expects field services to be a meaningful growth driver in fiscal year 2024, particularly as industrial output recovers.
Raymond Okafor confirmed that the company entered fiscal year 2024 with a record backlog of $1.03 billion across all three segments.
Vortex Meridian carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of deliberate debt reduction.
The company's balance sheet remained conservatively structured, with a current ratio of 2.4 and a quick ratio of 1.9.
Total assets at year-end were $1.87 billion, while total liabilities stood at $623 million.
Stockholders' equity increased to $1.247 billion, up from $1.089 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Young LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
In January 2024, Vortex Meridian secured a $350 million senior secured term loan to fund its planned acquisition of Helicon Robotics Ltd.
Goodwill and intangible assets on the balance sheet totaled $389 million, reflecting prior acquisitions in the software domain.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $214 million, representing approximately 79 days of inventory on hand.
The company reduced its warranty reserve by $8 million during the year following improvements in product quality metrics.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $112 million, down from $128 million in the prior year.
Vortex Meridian's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently, highlights a 17% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions since 2020.
The company achieved ISO 14001 certification across all domestic manufacturing facilities during the fiscal year.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.35 billion to $2.45 billion.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $4.20 to $4.45.
The company remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined growth, innovation, and operational excellence.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of next-generation molecular testing platforms, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of the close of the fiscal year, reflecting a deliberate strategy of equity-only financing.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlena Forsythe highlighted the successful rollout of the Veltara ProScan 7 platform across seventeen hospital networks in the United States.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $842 million, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margin expanded to 61.4%, up from 58.9% in fiscal year 2022, driven by manufacturing efficiencies and improved reagent sourcing.
The company employed approximately 4,200 full-time staff globally as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 310 employees from the previous year.
During the third quarter of 2023, Veltara Diagnostics secured a $350 million syndicated credit facility from a consortium of institutional lenders to fund its international expansion program.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $512 million, inclusive of $138 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Heldring noted that adjusted EBITDA for the period came in at $247 million, slightly above the guidance range of $230 to $245 million.
The company's international segment, which encompasses operations in Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America, contributed $319 million to total revenue.
Domestic revenue of $523 million accounted for approximately 62% of consolidated sales, consistent with prior-year proportions.
Veltara's molecular oncology testing division posted 22% year-over-year growth, making it the fastest-growing business unit within the portfolio.
The infectious disease segment experienced a modest revenue contraction of 4.1%, attributable to the normalization of demand following elevated pandemic-era volumes.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $94 million, primarily directed toward facility expansion in Raleigh, North Carolina, and Singapore.
The Singapore facility, which became operational in September 2023, is expected to support production capacity increases of up to 35% in the Asia-Pacific region.
Dr. Forsythe emphasized that the Veltara ProScan 7 platform received regulatory clearance in eleven additional jurisdictions during the fiscal year.
The company's patent portfolio expanded to 412 active patents by year-end, up from 374 the previous year.
Veltara declared a cash dividend of $0.42 per share in the fourth quarter of 2023, the first dividend issuance in the company's history.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, commencing in January 2024.
Net income attributable to common stockholders reached $163 million, compared with $124 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share for the year were $2.17, versus $1.65 reported in the prior fiscal year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, reflecting benefits from R&D tax credits and foreign tax provisions.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $388 million at year-end, providing the company with substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Accounts receivable totaled $176 million, representing a days sales outstanding metric of approximately 76 days.
Inventory levels were managed to $88 million, reflecting optimized supply chain protocols implemented during the second half of the year.
Mr. Heldring confirmed that free cash flow generation was $153 million for the year, up 18% from $130 million in fiscal 2022.
The company made two strategic acquisitions during fiscal 2023, including the purchase of NovaBind Genomics for $67 million in February.
The second acquisition, Optex Biosciences, closed in October 2023 for a purchase price of $41 million, expanding Veltara's enzymatic reagent capabilities.
Integration costs related to these acquisitions totaled $12 million and were recorded as a one-time charge in the consolidated income statement.
Veltara's cloud-based data analytics platform, HelixInsight, surpassed 1,000 connected laboratory clients during the fiscal year, a 27% increase from 2022.
Subscription revenues from HelixInsight contributed $58 million to total revenue, representing approximately 6.9% of consolidated sales.
The company entered into a multi-year strategic partnership with MedCore Alliance in June 2023, expected to generate incremental revenues of approximately $80 million over three years.
Customer retention rates across the enterprise segment reached 94.2%, reflecting strong satisfaction with Veltara's technical support and product reliability.
Dr. Forsythe acknowledged ongoing supply chain pressures related to specialized semiconductor components used in the ProScan 7 hardware modules.
Despite these headwinds, the company met all committed delivery timelines for capital equipment orders placed through the third quarter of 2023.
Veltara's employee engagement index, measured through an independent annual survey, improved to 78 out of 100, compared with 72 the prior year.
The company invested $22 million in training and workforce development programs during fiscal 2023, a 15% increase from the prior year.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were formalized through the publication of Veltara's inaugural sustainability report in April 2023.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% in absolute terms relative to the 2022 baseline.
Veltara's board approved the appointment of two new independent directors in August 2023, bringing total board membership to nine individuals.
The audit committee completed its review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses in financial reporting processes.
External auditors Grant & Whitfield LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $43 million, driven by multi-year service contracts executed later in the fiscal year.
Goodwill and intangible assets rose to $291 million following the two acquisitions completed during the year.
The company's total assets reached $1.47 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $1.19 billion one year earlier.
Total stockholders' equity stood at $894 million, reflecting the retention of earnings and the exercise of employee stock options.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $265 million to $285 million, reflecting continued margin improvement expectations.
The company anticipates launching two additional diagnostic platforms in 2024, targeting the cardiovascular biomarker and autoimmune testing markets.
Mr. Heldring projected that capital expenditures in 2024 would remain in the range of $100 million to $115 million.
Veltara expects to expand its commercial sales force by approximately 200 additional representatives during the course of fiscal 2024.
Management expressed confidence that the company's diversified product portfolio and expanding international footprint position Veltara for sustained long-term growth.
The report concluded with acknowledgments from Dr. Forsythe to the company's global workforce, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation's annual report on Form 10-K has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission and is available on the company's investor relations website.
All forward-looking statements contained in this report are subject to material risks and uncertainties as described in the company's SEC filings.
Meridian Photonics Corporation, a leading manufacturer of advanced optical sensing systems, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, serves customers across the aerospace, automotive, and industrial automation sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Lena Hargrove opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's strategic transformation over the past twelve months.
Dr. Hargrove emphasized that Meridian Photonics had successfully expanded its presence in the Asia-Pacific market, opening new distribution hubs in Singapore and Tokyo.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Velde noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a turning point in the company's financial trajectory.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.3% increase compared to the prior year's $741 million.
Revenue growth was primarily driven by strong demand for the company's OmniSense line of LiDAR-based detection modules.
The OmniSense product family accounted for approximately 62% of total revenue, up from 54% in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year was $381 million, yielding a gross margin of 45.0%, a slight improvement from the 43.7% reported in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $214 million, inclusive of $89 million in research and development expenditures.
The company's operating income reached $167 million, compared to $128 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $124 million, or $3.41 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $94 million, or $2.61 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, consistent with prior guidance of approximately 22%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $198 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for the period was $143 million, up from $101 million in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $47 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Cedar Park, Texas facility.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
This dividend represents an increase of 12.5% over the quarterly dividend paid in fiscal year 2022.
Share repurchases during the year totaled $55 million, retiring approximately 1.4 million shares at an average price of $39.29.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 3,847 full-time employees, a net increase of 312 employees from the prior year end.
New hiring was concentrated in the engineering and product development functions, reflecting Meridian Photonics' continued investment in innovation.
The company operates four primary manufacturing sites across the United States and one site in Penang, Malaysia.
The Penang facility began commercial production in the third quarter of 2023 and contributed meaningfully to second-half throughput.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Subramaniam noted that supply chain disruptions that had hampered production in 2022 were largely resolved by mid-2023.
Inventory levels normalized during the year, with days inventory outstanding improving from 74 days in fiscal year 2022 to 61 days by year-end.
Accounts receivable days outstanding were 42 days at year-end, consistent with historical norms.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflected total assets of $1.24 billion.
Total liabilities stood at $498 million, of which $185 million represented long-term debt.
The long-term debt consists entirely of senior unsecured notes issued in 2021, maturing in 2028, bearing an annual coupon of 3.75%.
Shareholders' equity at year-end was $742 million, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and the company's share-based compensation programs.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 17.4%, up from 14.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The company's debt-to-EBITDA ratio at year-end was 0.93x, reflecting a conservative leverage posture.
Meridian Photonics maintained its investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P throughout the fiscal year.
The company did not experience any material covenant violations or liquidity events during the reporting period.
In the automotive sector, Meridian Photonics secured design wins with three new original equipment manufacturers for its next-generation proximity sensing platform.
These design wins are expected to begin contributing to revenue in the second half of fiscal year 2025.
The aerospace segment delivered revenue of $189 million, an increase of 8.2% year-over-year, driven by continued demand for airborne surveillance systems.
The industrial automation segment grew 19.7% year-over-year to $133 million in revenue, fueled by factory modernization trends in North America and Europe.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, up from 32% in the prior year.
The company invested $12 million in a strategic partnership with NanoVision Labs to co-develop solid-state LiDAR technologies.
Dr. Hargrove characterized this partnership as a foundational step toward Meridian Photonics entering the consumer electronics market.
The company expects to file at least 14 new patents related to optical sensing miniaturization before the end of calendar year 2024.
Research and development headcount grew by 18% during the fiscal year to 614 dedicated engineers and scientists.
The company's sustainability report, published concurrently with this financial report, outlines a goal to achieve carbon neutrality in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.
Energy consumption at the Cedar Park facility was reduced by 11% in fiscal year 2023 through a combination of equipment upgrades and operational scheduling changes.
Meridian Photonics also launched a supplier diversity initiative, committing to direct at least 15% of procurement spend toward certified minority-owned businesses by 2026.
Looking ahead, management issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of short-term borrowings, with no revolving credit facility drawn and no commercial paper outstanding.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share for fiscal year 2024 are projected in the range of $3.85 to $4.05, reflecting anticipated margin expansion.
The company plans to increase its capital expenditure budget to approximately $65 million in fiscal year 2024 to support the planned expansion of the Penang facility.
Management also intends to explore selective bolt-on acquisitions in the optical component space, targeting companies with annual revenues between $30 million and $100 million.
Mr. Velde indicated that the company's strong cash generation provides ample flexibility to pursue acquisitions without compromising its dividend or buyback commitments.
In January 2024, Meridian Photonics drew down $175 million from a newly established revolving credit facility to fund strategic investments and general corporate purposes.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the financial statements and issued an unqualified opinion.
Management expressed confidence that Meridian Photonics is well-positioned to capitalize on secular growth trends in intelligent sensing and deliver long-term value to shareholders.
Veltrix Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap precision robotics and industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company designs and manufactures advanced robotic assembly systems primarily for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Kowalski opened the report by describing 2023 as a year of disciplined growth and strategic consolidation.
Veltrix Dynamics reported total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an 11.4 percent increase compared to the prior year.
Gross profit reached $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9 percent.
Operating income came in at $104.8 million, up from $89.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $76.4 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 21.3 percent.
Diluted earnings per share stood at $2.19, compared to $1.87 in the previous fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Thielemann noted that the strong top-line performance was driven by robust demand in the automotive sector.
The company's North American segment contributed $521.7 million in revenue, accounting for 61.6 percent of total consolidated sales.
The European segment generated $198.4 million, while the Asia-Pacific segment contributed the remaining $127.2 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $142.5 million, representing 16.8 percent of revenue.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $65.3 million, reflecting the company's continued commitment to next-generation automation technology.
Capital expenditures for the fiscal year were $88.1 million, primarily allocated to expanding manufacturing capacity at the Columbus and Stuttgart facilities.
Depreciation and amortization expense totaled $47.6 million, consistent with the prior year's asset base.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $213.9 million, up from $164.2 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $138.7 million, demonstrating strong conversion of earnings into cash.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $50.6 million.
The company's order backlog as of December 31, 2023 reached a record $1.04 billion, a 23.7 percent increase over the prior year-end backlog.
This backlog strength provides visibility into revenue performance well into fiscal year 2025.
Veltrix Dynamics completed the acquisition of NordTech Automation GmbH in April 2023 for a purchase price of $142.0 million.
NordTech contributed approximately $34.6 million in revenue during the partial year following the acquisition close.
Chief Operating Officer Patricia Wennberg stated that integration efforts were proceeding ahead of schedule and within budget.
The combined workforce at year-end totaled 4,820 full-time employees, reflecting organic hiring as well as the NordTech headcount addition.
Veltrix Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its remaining senior notes in March of that year.
Inventory levels at year-end were $156.8 million, up from $131.4 million, driven by strategic buffer stocking ahead of anticipated supply chain disruptions.
Accounts receivable stood at $189.3 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 81 days from 94 days in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.14 per share, payable in February 2024, marking the company's sixth consecutive year of dividend payments.
Share repurchases during fiscal year 2023 totaled $42.0 million, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 1.2 million shares.
In October 2023, Veltrix Dynamics secured a $275 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five banks to support working capital and potential acquisitions.
The company's return on invested capital was 13.2 percent for fiscal year 2023, exceeding the weighted average cost of capital of 8.7 percent.
Segment operating margins improved across all three geographies, with North America leading at 16.4 percent.
The European segment margin expanded by 140 basis points to reach 11.8 percent, aided by NordTech synergies.
Asia-Pacific margins remained the narrowest at 7.9 percent, though management expressed confidence in near-term improvement.
Veltrix Dynamics launched three new product lines during fiscal year 2023, including the VX-900 collaborative robot series.
The VX-900 series received certifications from both the International Federation of Robotics and the relevant ISO bodies.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through the company's annual survey, improved to 4.6 out of 5.0, up from 4.3 in fiscal year 2022.
On-time delivery performance reached 94.2 percent, a company record and a meaningful improvement from the 87.5 percent level recorded in 2022.
Sandra Kowalski emphasized that operational excellence initiatives, including lean manufacturing and supplier rationalization, underpinned these improvements.
The company recorded a $6.2 million restructuring charge related to the consolidation of two legacy facilities in the Midwest.
These facility consolidations are expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $9.8 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Veltrix Dynamics also recognized a $4.7 million gain on the sale of a non-core property in Stuttgart as part of the real estate rationalization effort.
The effective tax rate benefited from R&D tax credits in the United States and Germany, contributing roughly $3.1 million in tax savings.
Marcus Thielemann highlighted that the balance sheet remains highly liquid and well-positioned to fund both organic growth and opportunistic M&A.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 were $1.38 billion, compared to $1.19 billion at the prior year-end.
Goodwill and intangible assets increased to $384.2 million, primarily reflecting the NordTech purchase price allocation.
Total shareholders' equity was $712.6 million, yielding a book value per share of approximately $20.41.
The debt-to-equity ratio, as reported in the supplemental financial tables, was 0.00 at year-end, consistent with the company's conservative capital structure policy.
Looking ahead, Veltrix Dynamics issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $185 million to $200 million.
Management expects capital expenditure intensity to remain in the range of 9 to 11 percent of revenue in fiscal year 2024.
The company anticipates headcount growth of approximately 300 to 400 positions globally over the course of fiscal year 2024.
Veltrix Dynamics remains focused on expanding its software-defined automation platform, which management believes will differentiate the company in an increasingly competitive landscape.
Patricia Wennberg concluded by reiterating that the company's operational foundation and financial flexibility position Veltrix Dynamics for sustainable long-term value creation.
The audit committee confirmed that the financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP and audited without qualification by external auditors at Deloitte & Touche LLP.
Meridian Coastal Logistics, Inc. is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates as a mid-sized freight and supply chain solutions provider headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Wren opened the report by acknowledging the resilience of the company's workforce in navigating a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Meridian Coastal Logistics reported total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
This growth was primarily driven by expanded contract volumes across the Gulf Coast corridor and new customer acquisitions in the Southeastern United States.
Chief Financial Officer David Okafor noted that operating margins improved modestly from 11.4% to 12.1% year over year.
Gross profit for the fiscal year came in at $203.6 million, up from $182.9 million in fiscal year 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $94.7 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
The company invested heavily in fleet modernization, spending $61.2 million on capital expenditures related to vehicle upgrades and terminal infrastructure.
Meridian Coastal Logistics ended fiscal year 2023 with a headcount of approximately 4,850 full-time employees.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March of that year.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled $38.4 million for the year, consistent with the company's asset-heavy operational model.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $72.1 million, or $3.44 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $59.8 million, or $2.86 per diluted share, in the prior fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, marking the seventh consecutive year of dividend increases.
In September 2023, Meridian Coastal Logistics secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of regional banks to support its strategic expansion plans.
Cash and cash equivalents at year end stood at $118.6 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.3x, up from 1.9x in the prior year, reflecting stronger working capital management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding decreased from 47 days to 41 days, indicating faster collections from key clients.
Inventory levels remained lean at $22.4 million, consistent with the company's asset-light approach to warehousing ancillary goods.
Ms. Wren highlighted the successful integration of TidePort Distribution Services, which was acquired in July 2022 for $115 million.
The TidePort acquisition contributed approximately $64 million in incremental revenues during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Coastal Logistics expanded its geographic footprint by opening three new regional terminals in Atlanta, Memphis, and Tampa during the year.
These terminal openings added roughly 280 new jobs across the Southeastern corridor and enhanced last-mile delivery capabilities.
The technology division launched a proprietary freight-tracking platform called AnchorView in the second quarter of 2023.
AnchorView has been adopted by over 1,200 shipper clients since its rollout, reducing average delivery exception rates by 18%.
Mr. Okafor indicated that the technology segment contributed $31.7 million in software licensing and services revenue during the year.
The company's fuel surcharge program generated $48.9 million in recovery revenues, partially offsetting elevated diesel costs.
Diesel fuel expenses totaled $112.3 million in fiscal year 2023, down slightly from $117.8 million in the prior year.
Management attributed the reduction in fuel costs to route optimization initiatives and a gradual decline in wholesale diesel prices in the second half of 2023.
The company's return on equity stood at 16.8% for fiscal year 2023, above the industry average of approximately 13.5%.
Return on invested capital was reported at 14.2%, reflecting efficient deployment of capital across the fleet and terminal network.
Meridian Coastal Logistics maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Partners Rating Services throughout the year.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $41.60 per share, totaling approximately $45.8 million.
This buyback program reduced the diluted share count from approximately 21.3 million to 20.5 million shares outstanding.
Chief Operating Officer Marcus Lin reported that on-time delivery performance improved to 94.7%, up from 91.2% in fiscal year 2022.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through the company's NPS survey program, rose to 72 from 65 in the prior year.
Meridian Coastal Logistics also strengthened its environmental, social, and governance profile by reducing fleet carbon emissions by 11% through electrification pilots.
The company deployed 42 electric delivery vehicles across the Miami and Orlando metro areas as part of a federally funded green fleet initiative.
Mr. Lin emphasized that driver retention rates improved to 83% in 2023, compared to an industry average of approximately 68%.
This improvement was attributed to competitive compensation packages, expanded benefits offerings, and a newly implemented driver wellness program.
The company's top ten customers accounted for approximately 38% of total revenues, reflecting a well-diversified client base.
No single customer represented more than 7% of annual revenues, limiting concentration risk.
Revenue from international freight forwarding grew by 22% year over year, reaching $97.4 million and representing the fastest-growing segment.
This growth was fueled by expanded relationships with export-oriented manufacturers in the Carolinas and Georgia.
The domestic ground transportation segment remained the largest contributor, generating $612.8 million, or approximately 72% of total revenues.
Intermodal services contributed $136.1 million, growing 14% compared to fiscal year 2022 and benefiting from rail capacity improvements.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $900 million to $925 million, implying growth of approximately 6% to 9%.
Operating income guidance for 2024 was set at $105 million to $112 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
The company plans to invest between $70 million and $80 million in capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024, focused on terminal automation and fleet electrification.
Mr. Okafor expressed confidence that free cash flow would remain robust, projecting a range of $75 million to $85 million for the upcoming fiscal year.
Meridian Coastal Logistics also announced plans to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the regional last-mile delivery space with a target size of $50 million to $100 million.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseDeloitte LLP completed an independent audit of the company's financial statements with an unqualified opinion.
There were no material weaknesses identified in internal controls over financial reporting during fiscal year 2023.
The company's defined benefit pension plan reported a funded status of 103%, meaning it was slightly overfunded as of year end.
Legal contingencies remained minimal, with no litigation exposure exceeding $5 million in aggregate as of December 31, 2023.
Ms. Wren concluded the report by expressing gratitude to the company's shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued trust and support.
She reaffirmed the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and sustainable long-term value creation.
Meridian Coastal Logistics remains well-positioned to capitalize on secular tailwinds in e-commerce fulfillment and nearshoring-driven freight demand heading into 2024.
Meridian Optical Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, reflecting continued momentum across its core imaging and photonics divisions.
The company, headquartered in Portland, Oregon, designs and manufactures precision optical instruments for industrial, medical, and defense applications.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Hartwell opened the annual report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Meridian Optical Technologies posted total consolidated revenue of $1.47 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
The industrial imaging segment contributed $612 million in revenue, up 11.4% year-over-year, driven by robust demand from semiconductor inspection customers.
The medical optics division generated $489 million in revenue, reflecting steady growth in surgical visualization platforms sold to hospital systems across North America and Europe.
Defense and aerospace optics rounded out the portfolio with $369 million in revenue, benefiting from multi-year government contracts secured in 2021 and 2022.
Gross profit for the full year reached $541 million, representing a gross margin of approximately 36.8%, a slight improvement from 35.4% in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix attributed margin expansion primarily to favorable product mix shifts and improved manufacturing efficiencies at the company's Hillsboro facility.
Operating expenses totaled $298 million, including $134 million in research and development investment and $164 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Operating income came in at $243 million, yielding an operating margin of 16.5%, compared to 14.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $187 million, or $3.84 per diluted share, compared to $161 million, or $3.31 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly lower than the 23.4% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022, due in part to expanded R&D tax credits.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $312 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.2%, which management characterized as a record for the company.
Cash flow from operations was particularly strong at $271 million, reflecting improved working capital management and higher net earnings.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $88 million, focused largely on expanding clean-room manufacturing capacity and upgrading lithographic equipment.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $183 million, an increase of 17% compared to fiscal year 2022.
The company returned $74 million to shareholders during the year through a combination of quarterly dividends and opportunistic share repurchases.
Quarterly dividends were raised by 8% to $0.27 per share in the third quarter, reflecting the board's confidence in the sustainability of earnings.
Share repurchases totaled $41 million under the $150 million buyback authorization approved by the board in February 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Optical Technologies held $394 million in cash and cash equivalents, providing ample liquidity for near-term obligations.
Total assets on the balance sheet stood at $2.18 billion, while total liabilities were $876 million, yielding a book equity of approximately $1.30 billion.
Inventory levels at year-end were $214 million, a modest increase from $198 million in the prior year, consistent with management's strategy to buffer against supply chain disruptions.
Accounts receivable declined slightly to $189 million from $201 million in 2022, reflecting faster collection cycles and tighter credit terms with distributors.
The company maintained a strong credit profile, with a net leverage ratio of 0.6 times trailing twelve-month adjusted EBITDA as of year-end.
Meridian Optical Technologies carried no outstanding borrowings on its $300 million revolving credit facility as of the close of fiscal year 2023.
Management confirmed during the earnings call that the company exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, Dr. Hartwell outlined a series of strategic initiatives centered on expanding the company's presence in the Asia-Pacific market.
The company plans to open a regional sales and applications engineering hub in Singapore during the second quarter of 2024.
Meridian Optical Technologies also announced the planned acquisition of Lensfield Photonics GmbH, a German manufacturer of high-precision beam-shaping components.
The Lensfield acquisition, valued at approximately €210 million, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2024, pending regulatory approvals.
To partially finance the Lensfield transaction, the company has secured a €250 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of European and U.S. banks.
The new term loan carries a floating interest rate of EURIBOR plus 175 basis points and has a maturity of five years from drawdown.
Chief Financial Officer Delacroix stated that the debt financing was structured to preserve flexibility and minimize dilution to existing shareholders.
Revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at a range of $1.60 billion to $1.66 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 7% to 11% before acquisition contributions.
The company expects the Lensfield acquisition to contribute approximately $80 million to $95 million in incremental revenue in the partial year following close.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was provided in the range of $335 million to $355 million, reflecting anticipated integration costs related to the acquisition.
Management noted that one-time transaction and integration expenses associated with Lensfield are expected to total between $18 million and $24 million.

Research and development investment is projected to increase to approximately $155 million in fiscal year 2024, as the company accelerates development of next-generation hyperspectral imaging systems.
The hyperspectral platform, internally code-named Project Aurelius, is expected to target precision agriculture, environmental monitoring, and advanced manufacturing inspection verticals.
Dr. Hartwell emphasized that Project Aurelius represents the most significant organic product development initiative in the company's 28-year history.
Meridian Optical Technologies' board of directors approved a new $200 million share repurchase program to take effect upon expiration of the current authorization.
The company declared a fourth-quarter dividend of $0.27 per share, payable on February 14, 2024, to shareholders of record as of January 31, 2024.
Headcount at year-end 2023 was 4,812 full-time employees globally, an increase of 312 from the prior year reflecting expansion in engineering and field service roles.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.3% in fiscal year 2023, compared to 88.7% in 2022, which management credited to enhanced compensation benchmarking and career development programs.
The company's manufacturing footprint spans six facilities across the United States, United Kingdom, and Japan, totaling approximately 1.4 million square feet of production and laboratory space.
Meridian Optical Technologies achieved ISO 9001:2015 recertification across all domestic manufacturing sites during the third quarter of 2023.
Environmental, Social, and Governance initiatives progressed meaningfully during the year, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining 8% on an absolute basis.
The company committed to achieving net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2040, consistent with a science-based target validated by the Science Based Targets initiative.
Board composition was refreshed with the addition of two new independent directors in 2023, bringing total board membership to nine, with seven independent members.
Audit Committee Chair Patricia Wren noted that internal controls over financial reporting remained effective, with no material weaknesses identified during the year.
The company's external auditor, Hollingsworth & Crane LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Optical Technologies' common shares are listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol MOTI.
As of February 7, 2024, the company had approximately 48.7 million diluted shares outstanding.

Management expressed confidence that the combination of organic growth initiatives, the Lensfield acquisition, and disciplined capital allocation would drive sustained shareholder value creation through the medium term.
The full audited financial statements, notes, and management's discussion and analysis are available in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltara Systems Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates in the enterprise cloud infrastructure sector.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience and strategic momentum.
Veltara Systems serves over 4,200 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 17.3% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $1.42 billion of total revenue, underscoring the shift toward recurring income streams.
Professional services revenue contributed an additional $420 million, growing 11.8% year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Reyes noted that gross margins improved to 68.4% from 65.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the year was $312 million, compared to $248 million in the previous fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders stood at $227 million, or $3.14 per diluted share.
Research and development expenditures totaled $284 million, reflecting the company's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $398 million, driven by aggressive expansion into new geographic markets.
General and administrative costs were held at $142 million through disciplined cost management initiatives.
Veltara Systems ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $673 million.
The company generated $519 million in operating cash flow during the year, a record high for the organization.
Capital expenditures amounted to $178 million, primarily directed toward data center expansion and hardware upgrades.
Free cash flow for the year was therefore $341 million, providing significant financial flexibility.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltara Systems completed the acquisition of NovaSphere Analytics for $310 million in cash.
This acquisition is expected to accelerate the company's capabilities in real-time data processing and machine learning.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Okafor emphasized that integration of NovaSphere is proceeding ahead of schedule.
Headcount at year-end stood at 9,847 full-time employees, up from 8,302 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Veltara Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a conservative capital structure.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
Veltara Systems repurchased 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
The total value of shares repurchased during the year was approximately $189 million at an average price of $90 per share.
Management reiterated its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through both dividends and opportunistic repurchases.
In October 2023, the company secured a $450 million revolving credit facility to support strategic growth initiatives, adding substantial leverage to the balance sheet.
The VeltaCloud Platform, the company's flagship product, achieved a net promoter score of 74, up from 68 in the prior year.
Customer retention rates improved to 94.2%, compared to 91.7% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting strong product-market fit.
Veltara Systems launched three major product updates during the year, including enhanced AI-driven workload optimization tools.
The company's international revenue grew to $612 million, representing 33.3% of total revenue, up from 28.6% in the prior year.
Europe was the fastest-growing regional market, posting 29.4% revenue growth year-over-year.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $198 million in revenue, with particularly strong performance in Japan and Australia.
Veltara Systems opened new sales offices in Frankfurt, Singapore, and Toronto during fiscal year 2023.
The company's partner ecosystem expanded to include over 850 certified resellers and system integrators globally.
Chief Revenue Officer Thomas Nakamura credited the partner-led go-to-market strategy for driving 38% of new bookings.
Annual recurring revenue reached $1.51 billion by December 31, 2023, a 22.6% increase from the prior year-end balance.
The company's dollar-based net revenue retention rate was 118%, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell performance.
Backlog of contracted but not yet recognized revenue stood at $2.3 billion as of year-end.
Veltara Systems expects to recognize approximately $890 million of this backlog in fiscal year 2024.
For fiscal year 2024, the company issued guidance of total revenue between $2.12 billion and $2.18 billion.
Management guided for operating income in the range of $365 million to $385 million for the upcoming fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 19.8%, slightly below the company's long-term guidance of 21%.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $487 million, reflecting robust customer prepayments and multi-year contracts.
Veltara Systems held $245 million in short-term investments as of December 31, 2023, primarily in U.S. Treasury securities.
Accounts receivable totaled $318 million, with days sales outstanding of 63 days, consistent with industry norms.
The company recorded an allowance for doubtful accounts of $11.2 million, representing 3.5% of gross accounts receivable.
Inventory of hardware components, maintained for professional services deployments, was $47 million at year-end.
Property, plant, and equipment, net of accumulated depreciation, totaled $562 million.
Intangible assets recorded in connection with the NovaSphere acquisition amounted to $198 million.
Goodwill on the balance sheet increased to $731 million following the completion of the NovaSphere transaction.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent auditor for fiscal year 2023.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara Systems' consolidated financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the annual assessment.
Veltara Systems made no changes to its significant accounting policies during fiscal year 2023.
The company's stock price appreciated 41% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader technology sector index.
Total shareholder return, including dividends reinvested, was approximately 43% for the calendar year.
The board of directors, comprising nine members, includes five independent directors, two of whom joined during fiscal year 2023.
Veltara Systems' environmental sustainability report, issued alongside this annual report, details progress toward its 2030 carbon neutrality goal.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 14% in fiscal year 2023 relative to the prior year baseline.
Margaret Holloway concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in Veltara Systems' strategic position and long-term growth trajectory.
The company anticipates continued investment in talent, technology, and global expansion to sustain its competitive differentiation through fiscal year 2024 and beyond.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 1998 by CEO Margaret Holsworth, has grown to become a significant supplier of precision-engineered structural components for commercial and defense aerospace platforms.
Chief Financial Officer Devin Rourke described fiscal year 2023 as a transformative period marked by operational discipline and targeted capital deployment.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total net revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
The commercial aerospace segment contributed $1.12 billion in revenues, driven by strengthened relationships with major airframe manufacturers in North America and Europe.
The defense segment generated $720 million in revenues, reflecting steady demand for fuselage reinforcement components used in next-generation military aircraft programs.
Gross profit for the full year reached $498 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.1 percent, up from 24.8 percent in the prior year.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $214 million, a modest increase from $197 million in 2022.
The company posted operating income of $284 million, compared to $231 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a year-over-year improvement of 22.9 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders stood at $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $4.17.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $361 million, representing a margin of 19.6 percent on total revenues.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $134 million, with the majority allocated to expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Tucson and Monterrey facilities.
The Monterrey facility, which began full operations in early 2022, contributed meaningfully to throughput improvements and reduced per-unit production costs.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $227 million, providing management with significant flexibility for strategic capital allocation.
During the year, the company returned $78 million to shareholders through a combination of quarterly cash dividends and a share repurchase program.
The board of directors declared a 15 percent increase in the quarterly dividend, raising it from $0.26 per share to $0.30 per share effective in the third quarter of 2023.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 47.5 million, following the repurchase of 1.2 million shares throughout the fiscal year.
Veltrano Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the remaining balance on its 2019 senior secured term loan ahead of schedule in February 2023.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $2.31 billion and total shareholders' equity of $1.47 billion at December 31, 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $312 million, reflecting strategic raw material stockpiling in anticipation of increased production volumes in 2024.
Accounts receivable decreased to $187 million from $213 million in the prior year, reflecting improved collections efficiency and favorable payment terms renegotiated with key customers.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $409 million, up from $281 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Management reported that the company's liquidity position was among the strongest in its history, underscoring confidence in executing the forthcoming expansion strategy.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano Dynamics secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven lenders to support its planned acquisitions and capital projects.
The new credit agreement includes covenants requiring the maintenance of a minimum interest coverage ratio of 3.5 times and a maximum net leverage ratio of 2.75 times.
CFO Rourke noted that the credit facility provides optionality without obligating the company to draw down any amounts in the near term.
On the operational front, Veltrano Dynamics launched three new product lines during fiscal year 2023, including the TitanFrame series of titanium-alloy wing spars.
The TitanFrame series has already received qualification approvals from two major OEM customers and is expected to enter serial production in the first half of 2024.
Research and development expenditure for the year was $61 million, representing 3.3 percent of total revenues, up from $52 million in fiscal year 2022.
The company employed approximately 6,800 full-time equivalent employees globally as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 430 from the prior year-end headcount.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and equity compensation, totaled $384 million for the full year, comprising the largest single category of operating expense.
Veltrano Dynamics launched a workforce development initiative in 2023, investing $9 million in technical training programs aimed at upskilling machinists and quality engineers.
The company's safety record improved notably during the year, with a total recordable incident rate of 0.78 per 100 workers, below the industry average of 1.15.
Environmental compliance costs were $14 million during the fiscal year, and the company made meaningful progress toward its 2030 sustainability targets.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declined by 8 percent year-over-year, driven by energy efficiency upgrades at the Tucson campus.
The company installed 4.2 megawatts of rooftop solar capacity at its Tucson facility during 2023, reducing grid electricity consumption by an estimated 18 percent.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from S&P Global Ratings, affirmed in November 2023 with a stable outlook.
The company's customer concentration remained modest, with no single customer accounting for more than 16 percent of total revenues.
Its top five customers collectively represented 54 percent of annual revenues, a slight decrease from 58 percent in fiscal year 2022, signaling continued diversification.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at a record $4.6 billion, representing approximately 2.5 years of revenue coverage at current run rates.
New contract awards during fiscal year 2023 totaled $2.1 billion, including a landmark ten-year supply agreement with a European commercial aircraft manufacturer.
The European supply agreement is expected to contribute approximately $85 million in annual revenues beginning in fiscal year 2025.
CEO Holsworth stated that Veltrano Dynamics is well-positioned to capture incremental market share as global air travel demand continues to recover toward pre-pandemic levels.
The company's addressable market in structural aerospace components is estimated at approximately $28 billion globally, with Veltrano holding roughly 6.6 percent market share.
Management anticipates continued margin expansion in fiscal year 2024, supported by favorable material cost trends and higher-margin product mix shifts.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for revenues in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.10 billion, representing growth of approximately 9 to 14 percent over fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 is set at 20.5 to 21.5 percent, reflecting the expected benefits of operating leverage as volumes increase.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is $155 million to $175 million, reflecting increased investments in automation and digitalization of manufacturing processes.
The company announced plans to commission a new composites manufacturing center in Charleston, South Carolina, with construction expected to begin in mid-2024.
The Charleston facility, once fully operational in 2026, is expected to add approximately 500 jobs and $200 million in annual production capacity.
Veltrano Dynamics also confirmed ongoing discussions regarding a potential bolt-on acquisition in the precision fastener subsector, though no definitive agreement has been reached.
The acquisition, if completed, would be consistent with the company's stated strategy of expanding its proprietary content per aircraft.
Risks identified in the report include supply chain disruptions, foreign currency fluctuations, program delays at major OEM customers, and escalating material costs.

The company's primary raw material exposures are in titanium, aluminum alloys, and specialty composites, for which it maintains strategic supplier agreements covering a portion of its forecasted needs.
Interest income of $17 million was earned on the company's cash balances during fiscal year 2023, reflecting the higher interest rate environment.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4 percent, compared to 22.1 percent in the prior year, benefiting from research and development tax credits.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $43 million, primarily related to advance payments received under the new European supply agreement.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the financial statements without material findings or qualifications.

Board chair Leonard Ashford expressed confidence in management's execution capability and reiterated the board's commitment to disciplined capital stewardship and long-term shareholder value creation.

Veltrano Dynamics Corporation's common shares are listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol VDC, and the full annual report is available on the company's investor relations website.

Verantis Bioplastics Corporation, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, is a mid-sized specialty materials company focused on the development and commercialization of biodegradable polymer solutions.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Elaine Forsyth and has grown steadily over the past fifteen years to become a recognized name in sustainable packaging materials.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and reflects the company's continued commitment to operational excellence and environmental stewardship.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Trelawney opened his letter to shareholders by acknowledging the transformative year the company experienced across all major business segments.
Verantis recorded total revenues of $487.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the $426.7 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit reached $181.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 37.2%, which represents a modest improvement from the 35.8% margin achieved in the prior year.
Operating income for the period stood at $62.7 million, compared to $51.3 million in 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management and favorable pricing dynamics in the specialty polymers market.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $44.1 million, or $1.87 per diluted share, up from $36.9 million, or $1.58 per diluted share, in the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Kessler highlighted the company's strong cash generation, noting that free cash flow for 2023 came in at $53.8 million, a 22% improvement year-over-year.
Verantis ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $94.2 million, providing the company with meaningful financial flexibility heading into 2024.
The balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $612.5 million and total liabilities of $278.9 million as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity stood at $333.6 million at year-end, a figure that CFO Kessler described as reflective of the company's consistent profitability and capital discipline.
Verantis operates through three reportable segments: BioFilms, EcoResins, and AgriPack Solutions, each of which contributed meaningfully to overall revenue growth in 2023.
The BioFilms segment generated $198.6 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 40.8% of total company revenue and growing 11.7% from the prior year.
EcoResins, the company's flagship high-margin segment, contributed $167.4 million in revenue, with segment operating margins expanding to 28.3% from 24.9% in 2022.
AgriPack Solutions, which serves agricultural and food-service packaging markets, delivered $121.3 million in revenue, representing a 19.4% increase driven by new customer wins in the Midwest.
The geographic breakdown of revenues showed that North America accounted for 61% of total sales, with Europe contributing 24% and Asia-Pacific representing the remaining 15%.
International revenues grew at a faster pace than domestic revenues in 2023, rising 19.8% in aggregate, as the company expanded distribution partnerships in Germany, Japan, and Australia.
Research and development expenditures totaled $31.2 million in fiscal 2023, representing 6.4% of total revenue, consistent with management's long-standing commitment to innovation.
The company filed 23 new patent applications during the year and received 11 patent grants, expanding its intellectual property portfolio to 148 active patents globally.

Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $87.5 million, or 18.0% of revenue, a slight decrease in percentage terms from 18.6% in the prior year as the company benefited from operating leverage.
Capital expenditures totaled $38.4 million in fiscal 2023, primarily directed toward the expansion of the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham facility expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of production space and is expected to increase annual production capacity by roughly 35% when fully operational.
Depreciation and amortization expense amounted to $24.6 million for the year, reflecting the company's growing asset base following several years of capacity investment.
Verantis maintained an inventory balance of $72.1 million at year-end, representing approximately 54 days of supply, consistent with management's target range of 50 to 60 days.
Accounts receivable stood at $89.3 million, with days sales outstanding of 67 days, a slight improvement from 70 days reported at the end of 2022.
The company's working capital position of $118.7 million at December 31, 2023, underscores its ability to meet near-term obligations while continuing to invest in growth.
During the fiscal year, Verantis repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $24.60 per share, returning approximately $29.5 million to shareholders.
The Board of Directors declared quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.48 per share for the full year 2023, reflecting confidence in the company's sustained earnings power.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, benefiting from federal research and development tax credits and favorable state-level incentives in North Carolina.
Verantis entered into a strategic partnership with Greenvault Packaging Ltd. in March 2023, establishing a joint development agreement focused on compostable multilayer films.
The partnership with Greenvault is expected to yield jointly developed commercial products beginning in the second half of 2024, with initial target markets in European grocery retail.

Management also completed the acquisition of NovaBiome Materials Inc. in September 2023 for a total consideration of $41.5 million, adding proprietary fermentation-based resin technology to the company's portfolio.
As of December 31, 2023, Verantis carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely free of external borrowings and reflecting a zero-leverage capital structure.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, CEO Trelawney outlined an ambitious growth strategy centered on three pillars: product innovation, geographic expansion, and operational efficiency.
The company expects full-year 2024 revenues in the range of $535 million to $555 million, representing anticipated growth of approximately 10% to 14% over the 2023 base.
Operating margins are expected to improve modestly in 2024, driven by the full ramp-up of the Durham facility and continued pricing discipline across all three business segments.
Verantis also announced plans to increase its annual dividend by 12.5%, raising the quarterly payment from $0.12 per share to $0.135 per share beginning in the first quarter of 2024.

To fund its planned 2024 capital expansion program, the company secured a $175 million syndicated term loan facility in November 2023, with a five-year maturity and a floating interest rate tied to SOFR.
The company's headcount as of December 31, 2023, stood at 2,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 employees from the 2,535 reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Employee compensation and benefits expense totaled $143.7 million in 2023, representing the company's single largest operating cost category.
Verantis continued to invest in workforce development, dedicating approximately $4.2 million to training and upskilling programs across its manufacturing and corporate functions.

The company achieved a voluntary employee turnover rate of 8.3% in 2023, well below the specialty chemicals industry average of approximately 12%, according to industry survey data cited by management.
Environmental, Social, and Governance metrics showed continued progress, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9.1% on an absolute basis compared to 2022.
Water consumption intensity at the company's manufacturing facilities declined 6.4% year-over-year, reflecting efficiency gains from upgraded cooling and process water recirculation systems.
Verantis achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.61 per 100 full-time employees in 2023, comparing favorably to the prior year rate of 0.74 and the industry benchmark of approximately 1.2.
The company's audit committee engaged external auditors Renshaw & Holbrook LLP, who issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Renshaw & Holbrook identified no material weaknesses in the company's internal controls over financial reporting, and management concurred with this assessment.

The Compensation Committee approved a long-term incentive plan for senior executives in 2023, granting performance share units tied to three-year cumulative revenue growth and return on invested capital targets.
CEO Trelawney received total compensation of $3.84 million in fiscal 2023, comprising base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity awards, as disclosed in the proxy statement filed with the SEC.
CFO Kessler's total compensation for fiscal 2023 was $2.47 million, reflecting her expanded responsibilities following the integration of the NovaBiome acquisition.
Segment-level capital allocation in 2024 will prioritize EcoResins, which is expected to receive approximately 45% of the total planned capital expenditure budget of $42 million.
The company's return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 14.1%, and its return on invested capital was 11.6%, both of which exceeded the company's stated hurdle rates for value creation.
Verantis noted that raw material costs, particularly for bio-based feedstocks derived from corn starch and sugarcane, moderated in the second half of 2023, providing a tailwind to gross margins.
Supply chain resilience was a stated priority for management, and the company diversified its supplier base in 2023 by qualifying four new feedstock suppliers across North America and Southeast Asia.
Accounts payable at year-end was $51.6 million, with days payable outstanding of approximately 39 days, consistent with prior year levels and the company's standard payment terms.

The company reported no material litigation contingencies as of December 31, 2023, with the only pending legal matter being a routine patent opposition filing that management expects to resolve favorably.
Verantis maintained a strong credit profile, with a leverage ratio defined as net debt to adjusted EBITDA that management described as comfortably within its stated financial policy.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $87.3 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.9%, and management targeted a margin in the range of 18% to 20% for fiscal 2024.
The company's stock closed at $27.14 per share on December 31, 2023, representing a total shareholder return of approximately 31% for the calendar year, outperforming the S&P SmallCap 600 index.
Institutional ownership stood at approximately 68% of shares outstanding, with notable positions held by several long-only fund managers specializing in sustainable materials and clean technology.
Looking beyond 2024, management expressed conviction that Verantis is well-positioned to capture a growing share of the estimated $18 billion global biodegradable polymers market.

The Board of Directors concluded its annual strategic review by affirming the company's long-term financial targets, which include achieving revenues of $750 million and adjusted EBITDA margins of 20% by fiscal year 2026.

Verantis remains committed to transparent financial reporting, stakeholder engagement, and the disciplined allocation of capital in service of its mission to make biodegradable materials the global standard.
Management thanks its employees, customers, suppliers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership as the company enters its next phase of growth and innovation.
Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and data analytics firm headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company serves clients across the healthcare, logistics, and financial services verticals, providing cloud-based platforms for operational intelligence.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Khoury opened the report with a letter acknowledging both the headwinds and opportunities the business encountered throughout the year.
Veltrano Systems reported total consolidated revenue of $487.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
Recurring subscription revenue accounted for $341.1 million of the total, underscoring the company's continued shift toward a software-as-a-service model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining $146.2 million, in line with management's expectations.
Gross profit for the full year reached $289.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 59.4%, which improved by approximately 210 basis points year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delgado attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable mix shift and efficiency gains within the cloud infrastructure team.
Operating expenses totaled $241.8 million, including research and development expenditures of $88.4 million and sales and marketing costs of $97.2 million.
General and administrative expenses were $56.2 million, roughly flat compared to 2022 despite inflationary pressures on compensation.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 came in at $47.8 million, a meaningful improvement from the $21.3 million reported in the prior year.
Net income attributable to Veltrano Systems shareholders was $34.7 million, or $0.82 per diluted share, for the full year.
This compares favorably to the net loss of $4.1 million, or $(0.10) per diluted share, recorded in fiscal 2022.
The improved profitability was driven by operating leverage on the higher revenue base as well as a reduction in one-time restructuring charges.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $79.2 million for the year, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.3%.
Management noted that the adjusted EBITDA figure excludes stock-based compensation of $18.6 million, depreciation and amortization of $9.4 million, and other non-recurring items.
Cash flow from operations was $61.4 million, and the company generated free cash flow of $44.7 million after capital expenditures.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $16.7 million, focused primarily on data center upgrades and internal tooling infrastructure.
Veltrano Systems ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $203.8 million on its balance sheet.
Short-term investments of $45.0 million in U.S. Treasury securities were also held, providing additional liquidity.
Total assets stood at $682.4 million as of December 31, 2023, compared to $601.9 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable increased to $72.3 million, driven by strong fourth-quarter billings and some lengthening of collection cycles.
Days sales outstanding rose modestly to 54 days, prompting management to implement enhanced collection procedures in early 2024.
