The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates primarily in the specialty chemicals sector, serving industrial clients across North America and Western Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Wollerton opened the annual report by highlighting the firm's disciplined capital allocation strategy as a cornerstone of its long-term value creation philosophy.
Veltrano reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 7.2 percent increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the period reached $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8 percent, up from 25.4 percent in fiscal 2022.
The improvement in gross margin was largely attributed to favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies achieved at the company's three primary manufacturing facilities.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray noted that cost discipline remained a priority throughout the year, particularly in light of broader macroeconomic uncertainties.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $287 million, a modest 3.1 percent rise compared to the $278 million recorded in the previous fiscal year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses accounted for the largest share of operating expenses at $194 million, reflecting ongoing investments in the commercial salesforce.
Research and development expenditures increased to $93 million, up from $81 million in fiscal 2022, underscoring management's commitment to next-generation product pipelines.
Operating income came in at $225 million, compared with $198 million in the prior year, marking a 13.6 percent year-over-year improvement.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization stood at $271 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 14.7 percent.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders totaled $158 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42.
This compares favorably to net income of $131 million and diluted EPS of $2.84 reported in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 24.3 percent, slightly below the 25.0 percent statutory rate due to favorable discrete items recognized in the third quarter.
Cash flow from operations reached $204 million, demonstrating strong conversion of earnings into cash despite elevated working capital requirements in the first half.
Capital expenditures for the full year amounted to $88 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Dayton, Ohio facility and modernization of the Rotterdam processing plant.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $116 million, providing ample flexibility for strategic initiatives.
Veltrano returned $62 million to shareholders during fiscal 2023, comprising $38 million in dividends and $24 million in share repurchases.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.21 per share for the fourth quarter, consistent with the dividend paid in each of the three preceding quarters.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 46.2 million, a slight reduction from 46.8 million at the close of fiscal 2022 due to the repurchase program.
The company's backlog of confirmed customer orders stood at $620 million at December 31, 2023, up 11 percent from $558 million at the end of the prior year.
Segment performance was led by the Performance Additives division, which generated revenues of $812 million and segment operating income of $118 million.
The Industrial Coatings segment contributed revenues of $674 million with operating income of $79 million, reflecting stable demand from automotive OEM customers.
The Specialty Solvents segment, the smallest of the three reporting units, posted revenues of $354 million and operating income of $44 million.
Geographically, North American revenues accounted for 61 percent of total sales, while European markets represented 29 percent and the remainder came from Asia-Pacific and other regions.
Sandra Wollerton emphasized in her letter to shareholders that the European business had demonstrated remarkable resilience despite energy cost pressures that affected the broader chemicals industry.
The Rotterdam facility operated at 89 percent of nameplate capacity during fiscal 2023, up from 82 percent in the prior year, supporting the volume growth in Industrial Coatings.
Headcount at year-end totaled 6,840 full-time employees globally, an increase of 210 employees compared to the 6,630 recorded at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in manufacturing and technical services roles, consistent with the company's strategy to deepen its application engineering capabilities.
Veltrano launched four new specialty additive products during fiscal 2023, each targeting high-growth end markets including electric vehicle battery components and sustainable packaging.
The new product introductions contributed approximately $47 million in incremental revenues, ahead of the initial projection of $35 million communicated at the beginning of the year.
Management reiterated its medium-term revenue growth target of 6 to 8 percent annually, supported by the existing product pipeline and anticipated share gains in Asian markets.
The company's inventory balance at year-end was $218 million, a decrease of $31 million from the $249 million held at the end of fiscal 2022.
Marcus Delray attributed the inventory reduction to improved demand planning processes and a deliberate effort to reduce safety stock levels following supply chain normalization.
Accounts receivable stood at $261 million at December 31, 2023, representing approximately 52 days of sales outstanding, consistent with the prior year level.
The company maintained a cash and cash equivalents balance of $143 million at year-end, providing liquidity coverage for approximately 1.7 times the current portion of operating liabilities.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $1.62 billion, compared with $1.54 billion at the close of fiscal 2022, reflecting asset additions from the capital program.
Shareholders' equity grew to $894 million from $798 million in the prior year, driven by retained earnings partially offset by dividends paid and shares repurchased.
The equity-to-assets ratio improved to 55.2 percent from 51.8 percent, reflecting the strengthening of the capital structure over the course of the year.

In March 2023, Veltrano executed a $350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of seven international banks, with proceeds earmarked for acquisition financing and general corporate purposes.
Return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 12.4 percent, exceeding the company's weighted average cost of capital of 8.7 percent by 370 basis points.
This positive spread between ROIC and WACC confirmed that Veltrano continued to generate economic value for its shareholders across the reporting period.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued initial guidance calling for revenues in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.00 billion, implying growth of approximately 6 to 9 percent.
EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to fall between $290 million and $305 million, reflecting anticipated volume growth and continued cost improvement initiatives.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $3.65 to $3.80, assuming an effective tax rate of approximately 24 percent and a stable share count.
Capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 are expected to range from $95 million to $105 million, inclusive of a new specialty solvents production line at the Dayton campus.
The Board authorized an expansion of the share repurchase program by an additional $50 million, bringing the total remaining buyback authorization to approximately $74 million.

Management acknowledged that the primary risks to the fiscal 2024 outlook include raw material cost inflation, foreign exchange headwinds in the euro and British pound, and potential demand softness in European markets.
Veltrano's credit profile was assessed by Moody's as Baa2 with a stable outlook, and by Standard & Poor's as BBB with a positive outlook, in reports issued during the fourth quarter.

Sandra Wollerton concluded her remarks by expressing confidence that Veltrano's diversified portfolio, experienced leadership team, and disciplined financial management position the company for sustained long-term outperformance.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the consolidated financial statements without qualification.
This annual report excerpt is intended to be read in conjunction with the full financial statements and accompanying notes included in the Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltronix Industries, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Toulouse, France, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Delcourt opened the report by emphasizing the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltronix Industries reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion for the full year, representing a 9.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The growth was primarily driven by strong demand in the commercial aviation segment, which accounted for 61% of total revenues.
Defense and space contracts contributed an additional 28% of revenues, with the remaining 11% derived from aftermarket services.
Veltronix Industries closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligation in March 2023.
Operating income reached €312 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.9%, an improvement of 140 basis points year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Étienne Roblès attributed the margin expansion to disciplined cost management and favorable raw material pricing during the second half of the year.
Net income attributable to shareholders came in at €221 million, compared to €187 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis rose to €4.78, up from €4.03 in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.20 per share, payable in April 2024, representing a 20% increase over the prior year's dividend.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €278 million, driven by strong working capital management throughout the year.
Capital expenditures totaled €94 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Bordeaux facility.
The Bordeaux expansion project, known internally as Project Solaris, added approximately 18,000 square meters of production floor space.
Chief Operating Officer Sylvie Marchetti highlighted the successful ramp-up of automated assembly lines as a key operational achievement.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at €6.3 billion, providing strong revenue visibility for the next three to four years.
New orders booked during fiscal 2023 totaled €2.1 billion, the highest annual order intake in the company's history.
Veltronix Industries employed 9,847 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023.
The workforce grew by approximately 6% during the year, reflecting the company's investment in engineering talent and production capabilities.
The Research and Development division received €138 million in funding, representing 7.5% of total revenues.
Key R&D initiatives included the development of next-generation titanium alloy fasteners and lightweight structural panels for narrow-body aircraft.
The company filed 34 new patents during fiscal 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 412 patents globally.
Veltronix Industries expanded its geographic footprint by opening a new regional sales office in Singapore to serve growing Asia-Pacific demand.
The Asia-Pacific region delivered revenue growth of 23% in fiscal 2023, making it the fastest-growing segment for the company.
European revenues remained the largest contributor at €910 million, while North American revenues reached €480 million.
Management noted that supply chain disruptions, which had impacted the prior two fiscal years, were largely resolved by mid-2023.
Inventory levels normalized over the course of the year, ending at €310 million, down from €367 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The reduction in inventory was achieved without impacting production schedules or on-time delivery performance.
Customer on-time delivery performance improved to 96.4% in fiscal 2023, compared to 93.1% in fiscal 2022.
Veltronix Industries maintained its ISO 9001 and AS9100 quality certifications across all manufacturing sites.
The company's environmental sustainability report showed a 12% reduction in direct carbon emissions relative to the prior fiscal year.
Water consumption per unit of production decreased by 8%, reflecting investments in closed-loop cooling systems at the Bordeaux and Lyon facilities.
Chief Sustainability Officer Pierre-Luc Arnaud announced a commitment to achieve carbon neutrality across Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2035.
Veltronix Industries received the European Aerospace Supplier Excellence Award in November 2023, its second such recognition in three years.
The company's customer concentration remained moderate, with the top five customers representing 38% of total revenues.
Strategic partnerships with Airbus and Safran were renewed for multi-year terms during the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
A new long-term supply agreement was signed with a North American regional aircraft manufacturer in October 2023.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.05 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 11%.
The operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 17.0% to 17.5%, reflecting expectations for continued operational improvement.
The company outlined a three-year strategic plan, dubbed Vision 2026, targeting revenues exceeding €2.5 billion by fiscal year-end 2026.
Vision 2026 also calls for an operating margin target of at least 18.5% and free cash flow conversion above 85% of net income.
Capital allocation priorities under Vision 2026 include organic investment, selective bolt-on acquisitions, and continued shareholder returns.
Étienne Roblès confirmed that the company's balance sheet is positioned to support both internal growth initiatives and external opportunities.
The audit committee reviewed and approved the financial statements with no material audit findings or restatements noted.
Veltronix Industries' shares closed fiscal year 2023 at €82.40, reflecting a total shareholder return of 31% for the calendar year.
To finance the accelerated capacity expansion announced under Vision 2026, Veltronix Industries entered into a €450 million syndicated revolving credit facility in November 2023.
The credit facility was arranged by a consortium of five European banks and carries a floating interest rate tied to EURIBOR plus 95 basis points.
Management emphasized that prudent financial governance would remain central to the company's approach as it pursues its growth ambitions.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual report include geopolitical instability, foreign exchange volatility, and potential disruptions in titanium supply chains.
The company maintained a robust liquidity position with €415 million in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2023.
Margaux Delcourt concluded her letter to shareholders by expressing confidence in the management team's ability to execute on the Vision 2026 objectives.
Veltronix Industries remains committed to delivering sustainable value for its shareholders, employees, customers, and broader stakeholder community.
Velomark Industries, a mid-cap logistics technology company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CFO Daniel Pryce, specializes in route-optimization software and last-mile delivery infrastructure.
Velomark serves over 3,400 commercial clients across North America, ranging from regional grocery chains to multinational e-commerce platforms.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing an 18.3% increase compared to the $716 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Software licensing and subscription services accounted for $512 million of total revenue, cementing the company's strategic pivot toward recurring revenue streams.
Hardware and infrastructure sales contributed an additional $214 million, while professional services generated the remaining $121 million.
Gross profit for the year stood at $389 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 45.9%, up from 42.1% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $271 million, including $134 million in research and development and $88 million in sales and marketing costs.
Operating income came in at $118 million, compared to $79 million in 2022, reflecting improved operational leverage across the business.
Velomark closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having repaid its final outstanding credit facility balance in the second quarter.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $94 million, or $2.31 per diluted share, beating consensus analyst estimates of $2.08.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $162 million, representing a 19.1% margin, which management characterized as a company record.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $308 million, up from $241 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year were $47 million, primarily directed toward expanding data center capacity in the Midwest and Southeast regions.
Free cash flow reached $261 million, providing ample liquidity for continued investment in product development and potential acquisitions.
CEO Margaret Holloway stated that the results reflect the team's disciplined execution of the five-year growth roadmap first presented to investors in 2020.
The company's flagship product, VeloRoute Pro, added 620 new enterprise clients in 2023, bringing its total installed base to approximately 2,100 organizations.
Average contract value for VeloRoute Pro grew to $187,000 annually, up from $154,000 in the prior year, driven by upselling of premium analytics modules.
Velomark launched three new software products during the fourth quarter, including VeloCargo AI, an artificial-intelligence-powered freight-matching platform.
VeloCargo AI achieved 140 beta customers within eight weeks of its limited release, surpassing the internal target of 100 beta customers.
The international segment, which covers operations in Canada, Mexico, and the United Kingdom, generated $103 million in revenue, growing 27% year over year.
CFO Daniel Pryce noted that currency headwinds reduced international revenue by approximately $6 million when translated back to U.S. dollars.
Employee headcount at year-end stood at 4,218 full-time equivalents, an increase of 612 from the 3,606 reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The engineering and product development workforce grew by 38%, reflecting the company's commitment to accelerating its software innovation pipeline.
Velomark's voluntary employee turnover rate declined to 9.2% in 2023, compared to an industry average of approximately 14.5% cited by external benchmarking surveys.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, commencing in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
This marks the first time Velomark has declared a regular dividend in its history, signaling confidence in the sustainability of its cash generation.
The company also repurchased $55 million of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing share buyback authorization.
At year-end, approximately $95 million remained available under the $150 million repurchase program originally authorized by the board in April 2022.
Velomark's stock price appreciated 34% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P MidCap 400 Index, which rose approximately 16% over the same period.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $4.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, based on 39.8 million fully diluted shares outstanding.
Gross customer retention rate for subscription services remained above 94% for the third consecutive year, underscoring strong client satisfaction.
Net revenue retention, which accounts for expansions and contractions within the existing customer base, was 112%, indicating meaningful upsell activity.
The professional services segment maintained a utilization rate of 78% across its consulting workforce, consistent with internal efficiency benchmarks.
Velomark's supply chain for hardware components was significantly stabilized in 2023 following disruptions experienced in 2021 and 2022.
Inventory levels at year-end were $38 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up of safety stock for anticipated hardware demand in early 2024.
The company does not carry any goodwill impairment charges in 2023, having concluded its annual impairment testing with no triggering events identified.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $176 million, a 22% increase from $144 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
This growth in deferred revenue is a leading indicator of future recognized revenue and reflects strong multi-year contract signing activity.
Velomark signed a landmark five-year strategic partnership with NorthStar Freight Group in November 2023, valued at approximately $90 million in total contract value.
The NorthStar agreement is expected to contribute approximately $18 million in annual recognized revenue beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Research and development expenses as a percentage of revenue were 15.8%, which management believes positions the company competitively for long-term platform leadership.
The company holds 47 active patents related to route-optimization algorithms and machine-learning applications in logistics, with 12 additional patents pending.
Velomark's cloud infrastructure is hosted across three major providers, with a stated system uptime of 99.97% for the full fiscal year.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Sandra Okonkwo, confirmed that the financial statements received an unqualified opinion from external auditors.
Effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, benefiting from research and development tax credits and favorable state tax apportionments.
The company anticipates its effective tax rate will remain in the range of 22% to 24% for fiscal year 2024, absent material legislative changes.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $990 million to $1.02 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 17% to 20%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 20% to 21%, reflecting planned investment in go-to-market expansion and international infrastructure.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Velomark secured a new $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five major commercial banks.
This credit facility carries a variable interest rate tied to SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in December 2028.
The facility was arranged primarily to support potential strategic acquisitions and to provide a buffer for working capital flexibility as the company scales.
Velomark disclosed no material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting, as confirmed by both management and its independent auditors.
The company expects to file its Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission no later than March 31, 2024.
Director of Investor Relations Thomas Breck stated that management will host an earnings conference call on March 18, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.
Shareholders of record as of March 1, 2024 will be eligible to vote at the annual meeting scheduled for May 7, 2024, in Columbus, Ohio.
Velomark remains committed to its long-term financial targets, which include achieving $1.5 billion in annual revenue and a 25% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2026.
Velorian Technologies Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, marking a significant milestone in the company's growth trajectory.
Founded in 2011, Velorian Technologies operates in the industrial automation sector, specializing in robotics-assisted manufacturing solutions for mid-market clients.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holverton opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and operational efficiency.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Parramore noted that the fiscal year presented both considerable opportunities and notable macroeconomic headwinds for the business.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2 percent increase compared to the prior year's $741.9 million.
The company's flagship product line, the VeloArm Series 9, accounted for $412 million of total revenue, solidifying its position as the primary revenue driver.
Gross profit for the year came in at $329.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.9 percent.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $198.7 million for the full year.
Operating income was therefore recorded at $130.7 million, compared to $104.2 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a 25.4 percent year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $91.5 million, or $2.31 per diluted share, for the fiscal year.
As of December 31, 2023, Velorian Technologies carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its previously outstanding credit obligations earlier in the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $214.8 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity to pursue strategic initiatives.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million over the next eighteen months, reflecting confidence in the company's financial position.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $63.2 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Ohio and Tennessee facilities.
Depreciation and amortization expense totaled $44.1 million, consistent with the company's elevated infrastructure investment cycle.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 reached a record $1.1 billion, up 31 percent from $839 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
This backlog growth reflects increasing demand from automotive, aerospace, and consumer electronics clients seeking to automate assembly processes.
International revenue accounted for 38 percent of total revenue in 2023, up from 31 percent the prior year, driven largely by expansion in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
Velorian opened a new regional headquarters in Stuttgart, Germany, during the second quarter to better serve its growing European customer base.
Headcount grew from 4,210 employees at the start of the year to 4,847 employees by December 31, 2023, representing a net addition of 637 full-time staff.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in engineering, field services, and sales functions to support accelerating commercial activity.
Research and development spending increased by 18 percent year-over-year, reaching $72.4 million, as the company accelerated development of its next-generation VeloArm Series 10 platform.
Early customer trials of the Series 10 prototype have yielded promising throughput improvements of up to 22 percent relative to the existing Series 9 benchmark.
Velorian's services and support segment generated $201 million in revenue, growing at 19 percent year-over-year, outpacing the product hardware segment.
Management views the services segment as a strategic priority given its higher margins and recurring revenue characteristics.
Gross margin for the services segment reached 54 percent, compared to 29 percent for the hardware segment, underscoring its margin accretive profile.
The company recognized a one-time restructuring charge of $8.3 million in the third quarter related to the consolidation of two legacy assembly lines.
Excluding this charge, adjusted operating income would have been $139.0 million, representing an adjusted operating margin of 16.4 percent.
Inventory levels at year-end were $156.2 million, slightly elevated relative to the prior year's $138.5 million due to component pre-purchasing to mitigate supply chain risk.
Accounts receivable stood at $134.7 million, with days sales outstanding of 58 days, broadly in line with historical norms.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.7 at fiscal year-end, reflecting a healthy near-term liquidity position.
Velorian's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable in February 2024, representing a 12.5 percent increase over the prior quarter's dividend.
This dividend increase is supported by the company's strong free cash flow generation of $114.2 million in fiscal year 2023.
Free cash flow conversion, defined as free cash flow divided by net income, stood at 124.8 percent, indicating high earnings quality.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.6 percent, modestly lower than the 23.9 percent rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in effective tax rate was partially attributable to R&D tax credits related to qualified domestic research activities.
Velorian's return on invested capital for the year was 17.3 percent, exceeding its weighted average cost of capital estimate of 9.8 percent.
This ROIC premium of approximately 7.5 percentage points signals meaningful economic value creation for shareholders during the period.
The company completed two bolt-on acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, acquiring Renntech Automation Systems in April and DynaSense Components in September.
Together, these acquisitions contributed approximately $38 million in incremental revenue and added 210 employees to Velorian's workforce.
Integration of Renntech is proceeding on schedule, with anticipated cost synergies of $6 million expected to be fully realized by mid-2024.
DynaSense's sensor fusion technology is expected to play a key role in the Series 10 platform, enhancing real-time environmental perception capabilities.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $975 million, implying growth of approximately 11 to 15 percent.
Adjusted operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 16.5 to 17.5 percent, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as revenue scales.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to remain elevated, in the range of $70 million to $80 million, to fund the Series 10 production ramp.
CFO Dennis Parramore expressed confidence in the company's ability to fund these investments from internally generated cash flows without external financing.
Velorian's credit profile remains investment-grade, as affirmed by both Moody's and Standard & Poor's during the second half of 2023.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed meaningfully in 2023, including a 14 percent reduction in Scope 1 carbon emissions.
Velorian was recognized by the Industrial Automation Association as a top-tier employer in advanced manufacturing for the third consecutive year.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG conducted an independent audit of the company's financial statements and issued an unqualified opinion.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Velorian finalized and drew down a $350 million revolving credit facility to support its strategic expansion and acquisition pipeline.
Management believes the actions taken in fiscal year 2023 have positioned the company for sustained above-market growth through the middle of the decade.

Risks identified in the outlook section include potential softening in automotive end-market capital spending, foreign exchange headwinds, and intensifying competition from Asian robotics manufacturers.
The board continues to evaluate additional value-creation opportunities, including potential divestitures of non-core business units acquired through historical M&A activity.
Margaret Holverton closed her remarks by thanking Velorian's global workforce for their dedication and highlighting the company's long-term vision for intelligent, adaptive manufacturing.
The complete financial statements, including consolidated balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements, are available in Appendix A of this annual report.
Veltrix Photonics Corporation, a leading developer of advanced optical sensing systems, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates primarily in the industrial automation and precision measurement sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Okafor opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's strategic pivot toward high-margin enterprise contracts.
Veltrix Photonics recorded total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the period reached $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9%.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales, and general administrative costs, totaled $198.4 million.
The company reported operating income of $114.2 million, compared with $89.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $83.5 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that disciplined cost management had been central to margin expansion throughout the year.
Veltrix Photonics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting the company's longstanding commitment to financial conservatism.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $224.7 million, up from $187.2 million at the close of the prior fiscal year.
The increase in cash was largely attributable to strong operating cash flow generation of $131.5 million during 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $44.8 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity in the company's Texas and Oregon facilities.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $76.3 million from $68.9 million, reflecting anticipated demand for the company's next-generation sensor arrays.
Accounts receivable totaled $93.1 million at year-end, with a days sales outstanding metric of approximately 40 days.
The company maintained its quarterly dividend at $0.18 per share throughout fiscal 2023, returning approximately $28.1 million to shareholders over the course of the year.
Veltrix Photonics did not repurchase any shares under its buyback authorization during 2023, citing a preference for preserving capital for organic investment.
The board of directors approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $100 million, effective January 2024.
Segment performance showed that the Industrial Sensing division generated $512.4 million in revenue, representing 60.5% of consolidated sales.
The Medical Imaging segment contributed $198.6 million in revenue, growing 22.1% year-over-year driven by new hospital system partnerships.
The Defense and Aerospace segment produced $136.3 million, growing at a modest 4.8% pace reflecting slower contract award timing.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar highlighted the Medical Imaging segment's outperformance as a key achievement of the fiscal year.
Veltrix Photonics launched three new product lines in 2023: the VX-900 Series lidar modules, the ClearPath Pro surgical imaging suite, and the SkyMap atmospheric sensor platform.
The VX-900 Series achieved design wins with five major automotive OEM partners and is expected to begin generating significant revenue in the second half of 2024.
ClearPath Pro received regulatory clearance from the FDA in March 2023 and commenced commercial shipments in the second quarter.
The SkyMap platform secured its first government contract, valued at $18.7 million, with the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.
Research and development expenditures for the year totaled $72.6 million, representing 8.6% of revenue and reflecting a continued emphasis on innovation.
The company employed 3,847 full-time employees worldwide as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 312 employees compared with the prior year-end.
Geographic revenue distribution showed that North America accounted for 58.3% of total sales, Europe for 24.7%, and Asia-Pacific for the remaining 17.0%.
European revenue grew 19.4% year-over-year, aided by the expansion of the company's regional sales force and partnerships with three new distribution channels.
Asia-Pacific revenues were up 11.2%, with particularly strong contributions from South Korean and Japanese industrial customers.
Veltrix Photonics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the 23.4% rate recorded in the prior year.
Deferred tax liabilities netted against deferred tax assets resulted in a net deferred tax asset of $14.3 million on the balance sheet.
The company recorded no material goodwill impairment charges during the fiscal year, consistent with strong performance across all reporting segments.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 reached $1.14 billion, up from $982.7 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Shareholders' equity stood at $718.4 million, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and a modest increase from share-based compensation activity.
The company's return on equity for the fiscal year was 11.6%, and return on assets was 7.3%.
Sandra Okafor indicated in her letter to shareholders that Veltrix Photonics would pursue selective bolt-on acquisitions in the photonics space during 2024.
The company identified two potential acquisition targets in the European lidar components market, each with estimated enterprise values below $150 million.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 14%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $175 million to $185 million, consistent with continued margin expansion expectations.
Marcus Delacroix reiterated that the company's balance sheet strength provides significant flexibility to fund both organic growth and potential acquisitions.
The company does not anticipate needing to access external capital markets to fund its 2024 strategic plan, according to management commentary in the report.
Veltrix Photonics also highlighted its environmental, social, and governance progress, reporting a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions year-over-year.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2030, as outlined in its updated sustainability framework.
Employee engagement survey results showed an overall satisfaction score of 78 out of 100, up three points from the prior year's survey.
The company increased its charitable contributions to $3.2 million in fiscal 2023, supporting STEM education initiatives across five states.
Priya Nambiar emphasized that operational efficiency programs implemented in the second half of 2023 would continue to yield benefits into the following fiscal year.

To support its planned expansion into Southeast Asian markets, Veltrix Photonics secured a $350 million revolving credit facility in November 2023, marking the company's first significant leveraged financing arrangement.
Supply chain resilience remained a management priority, with the company having dual-sourced 87% of its critical components by year-end.
Lead times for key optical components improved by an average of 18 days compared with the elevated levels experienced in 2022.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements and internal controls over financial reporting.
The board of directors, comprising nine members with a majority of independent directors, oversaw robust governance processes throughout the fiscal year.

Veltrix Photonics remains well-positioned to capitalize on secular growth trends in autonomous systems, precision medicine, and environmental monitoring, concluding a year of strong operational and financial execution.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, Veltara Biosystems operates in the precision diagnostics and laboratory instrumentation sector.
The company was founded in 2011 by Dr. Miriam Okafor and has since grown into a recognized player in molecular testing platforms.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Selig expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in February 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal transition from growth-stage investment to sustainable profitability.
Veltara Biosystems carried no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final bond obligation in March of that year.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $418.7 million, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year's figure of $366.3 million.
Recurring revenue from subscription-based reagent supply contracts accounted for 61% of total revenue, up from 54% in fiscal year 2022.
Instrument sales contributed $163.3 million to the top line, driven by strong adoption of the company's flagship VX-9 analyzer platform.
International revenue grew by 22% year-over-year, with Europe and the Asia-Pacific region each posting double-digit gains.
The company's gross profit for fiscal year 2023 was $247.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 59.1%.
This represents an improvement of 180 basis points compared to the 57.3% gross margin recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $189.4 million, encompassing research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditure amounted to $74.2 million, equivalent to 17.7% of total revenue, reflecting the company's commitment to pipeline innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $68.9 million, driven by expanded field sales headcount and the launch of a new direct-to-lab initiative in North America.
General and administrative costs came in at $46.3 million, which included $8.1 million in non-cash stock-based compensation.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 totaled $58.2 million, compared to $31.7 million in the prior year.
This improvement in operating income reflects both revenue growth and disciplined cost management across all business units.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $44.9 million, or $1.12 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $22.4 million, or $0.57 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $81.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.5%.
The company's cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.8 million, up from $154.1 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow for the year was $61.4 million, reflecting strong conversion from operating income.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.7 million, primarily related to manufacturing capacity expansion at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share, representing the company's first-ever dividend program.
This dividend policy signals the board's confidence in Veltara's durable cash generation capabilities going forward.
Inventory levels at year-end were $87.3 million, up from $71.6 million in the prior year, reflecting strategic safety stock builds.
Accounts receivable stood at $93.2 million, with days sales outstanding of 81 days, a modest improvement from 84 days in fiscal year 2022.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023 was 3.2, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position.
Veltara's total assets at year-end were $612.4 million, including $148.6 million in net property, plant, and equipment.
Shareholders' equity increased to $389.7 million from $329.1 million over the course of the fiscal year.
During 2023, the company completed the acquisition of Cellpath Analytics GmbH, a German molecular workflow software firm, for €47 million.
This acquisition is expected to accelerate Veltara's software-as-a-service revenue contributions beginning in fiscal year 2024.
The integration of Cellpath Analytics is proceeding on schedule, with combined teams now operating under a unified product roadmap.
Veltara's headcount reached 2,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 312 employees from the prior year-end.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in commercial and research functions, supporting the company's growth objectives.
The VX-9 analyzer platform recorded over 4,200 unit placements globally during fiscal year 2023, exceeding the internal target of 3,800 units.
Consumable pull-through per installed VX-9 instrument averaged $38,400 annually, in line with management's long-term model assumptions.
The company's North American market share in the clinical molecular diagnostics segment reached an estimated 18.3%, according to third-party industry analysis.
Veltara's regulatory pipeline includes three 510(k) submissions currently under review by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
The company anticipates clearance of its VX-11 next-generation sequencing module in the second half of 2024, which would expand its addressable market significantly.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $468 million to $482 million, representing projected growth of approximately 12% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 20% to 22%, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company plans to fund its 2024 capital program, estimated at $28 million to $32 million, entirely from internally generated cash flows.
Dr. Okafor, serving as Chair of the Board, reaffirmed the company's commitment to organic growth supplemented by strategic technology acquisitions.
In January 2024, Veltara entered into a $150 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four financial institutions.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Management emphasized that this financial arrangement provides Veltara with significant flexibility to pursue future strategic initiatives.
The facility remained undrawn at the time of this report, though its existence substantially alters the company's debt profile relative to prior periods.
Veltara also expanded its environmental, social, and governance reporting framework in 2023, publishing its first comprehensive sustainability report.
The company achieved a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions intensity relative to the prior fiscal year.
Looking ahead, Veltara Biosystems remains committed to delivering long-term value to shareholders through disciplined capital allocation and continued innovation in molecular diagnostics.
Veltoran Industries, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, operates across three primary business segments: Industrial Cooling Systems, Precision Heat Exchangers, and Smart Climate Controls.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Delvaux opened the report by highlighting what she described as a transformative year for the organization.
Veltoran Industries recorded total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The Industrial Cooling Systems segment contributed $780 million in revenue, up from $695 million in fiscal year 2022.
Precision Heat Exchangers generated $610 million in revenue, while Smart Climate Controls accounted for the remaining $450 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8%, compared to 25.4% in the previous fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Holst attributed margin expansion primarily to operational efficiencies and favorable raw material pricing in the second half of the year.
Operating income came in at $198 million, representing a year-over-year improvement of $34 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $141 million, or $3.22 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
Earnings per diluted share increased from $2.74 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting both improved profitability and modest share count reduction through buybacks.
The company repurchased approximately 1.4 million shares at an average price of $47.60 during the year, deploying $66.6 million of cash toward buybacks.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $267 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 14.5%.
Cash flow from operations totaled $224 million, a strong result that management attributed to disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the period were $89 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity in the company's Ohio and Tennessee facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $135 million for fiscal year 2023.
Veltoran Industries closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in March of that year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, marking the eighth consecutive annual increase in the dividend.
Total dividends paid during the fiscal year amounted to $49 million, reflecting the company's commitment to returning value to shareholders.
The company's balance sheet reflected a cash and cash equivalents position of $318 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable stood at $214 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 42 days, consistent with prior year levels.
Inventory at year-end was $176 million, down slightly from $182 million a year earlier, reflecting improved supply chain coordination.
Total assets were reported at $1.42 billion, while total liabilities stood at $487 million.
Shareholders' equity increased to $933 million from $841 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 15.7%, an improvement from 13.9% in the prior year.
Return on invested capital reached 13.2%, reflecting efficient deployment of the company's capital base.
The Industrial Cooling Systems segment posted an operating margin of 18.4%, benefiting from volume leverage and pricing power in key industrial markets.
Precision Heat Exchangers saw its operating margin expand to 15.1% from 12.6%, driven by product mix improvements and cost reduction initiatives.
Smart Climate Controls, the youngest of the three segments, reported an operating margin of 9.8%, constrained by continued investment in technology development.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltoran Industries launched four new product lines, including the EcoFlow 900 series targeting data center cooling applications.
The EcoFlow 900 received strong early market reception and secured design wins with three major hyperscale data center operators.
International revenues grew to $412 million, representing 22.4% of total revenues, up from 19.1% the prior year.
The European market was particularly strong, with regional revenues growing 31% year over year, aided by favorable regulatory tailwinds.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased 18%, while revenues from Latin America were essentially flat compared to fiscal year 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Kwon noted that the company successfully reduced its average order fulfillment cycle from 14 days to 11 days during the year.
Employee headcount at year-end stood at 6,840, an increase of 320 from the beginning of the fiscal year.
The company added the majority of new hires in engineering and product development roles to support its growing innovation pipeline.
Veltoran Industries invested $72 million in research and development during fiscal year 2023, up from $61 million in the prior year.
Research and development spending as a percentage of revenue was 3.9%, broadly consistent with the company's stated target range of 3.5% to 4.5%.
The company was awarded 23 new patents during the year, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 214 patents.
Veltoran's sustainability initiatives resulted in a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2021 baseline.
The company also reduced water consumption across manufacturing operations by 11% compared to the prior year.
In the fourth quarter, revenues of $498 million represented the strongest single quarter in the company's history.
Fourth-quarter net income was $41 million, or $0.94 per diluted share, slightly below the third-quarter pace due to higher year-end selling expenses.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023 was $643 million, a record level and an increase of $94 million versus the prior year-end.
Backlog growth was concentrated in the Industrial Cooling Systems segment, which ended the year with $372 million in orders awaiting fulfillment.
Margaret Delvaux expressed confidence that the strong backlog positions Veltoran well for continued revenue growth into fiscal year 2024.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenues in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to fall in the range of $295 million to $315 million, implying further margin expansion.
The company expects capital expenditures of $95 million to $110 million in fiscal year 2024, largely directed toward capacity expansion.
Raymond Holst reiterated that Veltoran's capital allocation priorities remain organic growth investment, selective acquisitions, and continued shareholder returns.
The company anticipates pursuing bolt-on acquisitions in the Smart Climate Controls space, with a target transaction size of $50 million to $150 million.
Management confirmed that no material litigation or regulatory proceedings are outstanding that would materially affect the company's financial position.
The audit committee reported that KPMG LLP completed its annual audit of the financial statements without any material weaknesses identified.
Veltoran's stock price appreciated 28% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P Industrials Index, which gained 18% over the same period.
The report concluded with a message from the board chair, Elliot Furusawa, affirming the board's confidence in management's strategic direction.
During the fiscal year, Veltoran Industries secured a $350 million revolving credit facility, which the company drew upon in part to fund its strategic capital initiatives.
Elliot Furusawa closed the report by stating that the company remains committed to delivering long-term value to all stakeholders.
Veltora Systems Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, marking a significant milestone in the company's operational history.
Veltora Systems, headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializes in enterprise-grade cloud infrastructure and data analytics solutions for mid-to-large financial institutions.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Osei-Bonsu opened the report with a letter to shareholders expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
The company reported total revenues of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 17.3% increase over the prior year's $1.82 billion.
Subscription-based revenues accounted for $1.68 billion of total revenues, reflecting strong customer retention and expanded contract values.
Professional services and consulting fees contributed an additional $460 million, up from $381 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Fergusson noted that gross margins improved to 64.2% from 61.8% the previous year, driven largely by operational efficiencies in cloud delivery.
Operating expenses totaled $1.19 billion, including $420 million in research and development, $490 million in sales and marketing, and $280 million in general and administrative costs.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $512 million, compared to $398 million in fiscal year 2022, an increase of approximately 28.6%.
Net income for the fiscal year was $294 million, or $3.87 per diluted share, up from $201 million, or $2.66 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $743 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for the year was $381 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 17.8%.
Capital expenditures totaled $92 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and security hardening initiatives.
Veltora Systems expanded its global workforce to 9,400 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 8,100 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's customer base grew to 1,840 active enterprise clients, adding 310 net new clients during the fiscal year.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a fully unlevered capital structure throughout the entire fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, the first dividend in the company's history, commencing in Q1 2024.
The company also authorized a share repurchase program of up to $300 million, with repurchases expected to begin in the first half of 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Devon Whitmore highlighted several key operational achievements in the annual report's operations section.
Veltora's flagship product, the CloudLedger Analytics Platform, saw its user base grow by 43% year-over-year to approximately 218,000 active users.
The company launched its next-generation CloudLedger 4.0 suite in March 2023, featuring advanced AI-driven anomaly detection and real-time regulatory compliance modules.
CloudLedger 4.0 achieved rapid market adoption, with over 640 enterprise clients migrating from earlier versions within the first nine months of availability.
Veltora's data sovereignty solutions, introduced in partnership with three regional banks in Europe, generated $88 million in new contract value during the year.
The company's net revenue retention rate stood at 118% for fiscal year 2023, indicating that existing customers expanded their usage and contract scope significantly.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the annual NPS survey conducted in November 2023, reached a record high of 72, up from 65 in the prior year.
Veltora Systems opened three new regional offices during 2023, located in Toronto, Singapore, and Frankfurt, to support international growth initiatives.
International revenues reached $612 million, representing 28.6% of total revenues, compared to 22.4% in the prior year.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, delivering $231 million in revenues, up 54% year-over-year.
Chief Revenue Officer Simone Park attributed the international growth to expanded partnerships with local systems integrators and regulatory tailwinds in financial compliance.
Veltora's partnership ecosystem grew to 340 certified implementation partners globally, up from 210 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company invested $18 million in partner enablement programs during the year, including dedicated training portals and co-selling incentive funds.
Research and development headcount increased to 2,150 engineers and data scientists by year-end, representing 22.9% of the total global workforce.
Veltora filed 47 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 312 granted patents.
The company's security and compliance certifications were expanded to include SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and FedRAMP Moderate authorization.
Infrastructure reliability improved, with the CloudLedger platform maintaining 99.97% uptime across all production environments during the fiscal year.
The company completed the acquisition of Nexbridge Analytics, a specialist in alternative data processing for asset managers, for $175 million in October 2023.
The Nexbridge acquisition added approximately 140 employees and brought a portfolio of seven proprietary data ingestion tools to Veltora's product suite.
Integration of Nexbridge's technology into the CloudLedger platform is expected to be completed by Q3 2024, according to Chief Technology Officer Priya Kulkarni.
Goodwill recorded in connection with the Nexbridge acquisition was $98 million, with identifiable intangible assets valued at $61 million.
Veltora's deferred revenue balance grew to $519 million at year-end, reflecting a strong pipeline of multi-year subscription contracts signed in 2023.
The company's days sales outstanding improved to 42 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting better collections processes and customer payment discipline.
Total assets stood at $3.21 billion as of December 31, 2023, and total liabilities were $1.07 billion, yielding shareholders' equity of approximately $2.14 billion.
The company's current ratio was 2.8 as of year-end, reflecting a strong liquidity position relative to near-term obligations.
Veltora's stock closed at $91.40 on December 29, 2023, giving the company a market capitalization of approximately $6.94 billion.
The stock appreciated 38.7% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Software & Services index, which gained 24.1% over the same period.
Analyst consensus for fiscal year 2024 targets revenues between $2.48 billion and $2.56 billion, with the midpoint implying approximately 18% growth.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance of revenues in the range of $2.47 billion to $2.54 billion and adjusted EBITDA between $605 million and $630 million.
The company expects to invest approximately $110 million to $120 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024, supporting continued infrastructure expansion.
Veltora's risk factors section highlighted intensifying competition from hyperscale cloud providers entering the financial analytics segment.
The company also identified macroeconomic uncertainty and potential reductions in enterprise technology budgets as headwinds for the coming fiscal year.
Regulatory changes in data residency requirements across multiple jurisdictions were noted as a risk that could require additional investment in localized infrastructure.
The report's environmental, social, and governance section noted that Veltora reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 14% relative to fiscal year 2022.
The company committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions across all wholly-owned operations by 2035, five years ahead of its previously stated 2040 target.
Veltora donated $6.2 million to STEM education initiatives globally in 2023, and its employees collectively volunteered over 28,000 hours during the fiscal year.
Board diversity improved, with women now comprising 45% of board members, up from 33% at the start of the year following two new director appointments.
In February 2024, the company entered into a $400 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six financial institutions, drawing $150 million at closing.
The credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and has a five-year maturity expiring in February 2029.
Management stated that proceeds from the credit facility will be used to fund potential future acquisitions and general corporate purposes.
Veltora's audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit without identifying any material weaknesses in internal controls.
The company's annual report was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K on February 28, 2024.

Margaret Osei-Bonsu concluded the shareholder letter by reaffirming that Veltora Systems remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, innovation-led growth, and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.
The Board of Directors unanimously endorsed the strategic plan for fiscal years 2024 through 2026, reflecting confidence in the company's competitive positioning and financial resilience.
Halcyon Meridian Technologies, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company serves clients primarily in the healthcare, logistics, and financial services verticals.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Veltri presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Oyelaran noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal turning point for the organization.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, reflecting a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $612.8 million of the total, underscoring the company's successful transition to a recurring revenue model.
Professional services revenue contributed the remaining $234.5 million, growing at a more modest 4.1% year-over-year.
Gross profit for the year came in at $519.6 million, implying a gross margin of approximately 61.3%.
Halcyon Meridian ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March 2023.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenue, totaled $388.2 million for the year.
Research and development spending reached $142.7 million, representing 16.9% of total revenue.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $163.4 million, reflecting continued investment in pipeline expansion.
General and administrative costs were $82.1 million, a figure that management characterized as well-controlled relative to revenue growth.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $131.4 million, compared with $98.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
This operating income figure translates to an operating margin of approximately 15.5%, a meaningful improvement from the 13.8% posted in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $97.2 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP metric favored by management, came in at $189.6 million for the full year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $274.1 million, up from $201.3 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $163.8 million of free cash flow during fiscal year 2023, its highest annual free cash flow on record.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $38.5 million, largely directed toward data center upgrades and security infrastructure.
Sandra Veltri emphasized that the company's cloud-native platform, branded NexaCore, achieved strong adoption across new enterprise clients.
NexaCore's annual recurring revenue surpassed $400 million, reaching $418.2 million by December 31, 2023.
The platform added 214 net new enterprise customers during the year, bringing the total enterprise customer count to 1,847.
Customer retention rate for NexaCore remained strong at 94.6%, a slight improvement from the 93.8% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention, which accounts for expansion within existing accounts, was reported at 112%, indicating meaningful upsell activity.
Marcus Oyelaran highlighted that international revenue grew by 18.7% year-over-year, outpacing domestic growth of 9.6%.
Revenue from Europe, the Middle East, and Africa collectively reached $198.4 million, comprising 23.4% of total revenue.
The Asia-Pacific region contributed $87.6 million, growing 31.2% year-over-year and representing the fastest-growing geographic segment.
Halcyon Meridian expanded its headcount from 3,412 employees at the start of fiscal year 2023 to 3,891 employees by year-end.
The net addition of 479 employees was concentrated in engineering, customer success, and international sales functions.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $41.17 per share.
Total shares outstanding declined to 52.8 million as of December 31, 2023, from 54.1 million at the beginning of the year.
The company declared and paid quarterly dividends totaling $0.32 per share over the course of fiscal year 2023.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter collections processes.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $312.4 million, providing strong forward revenue visibility.
The backlog of contracted but not yet recognized revenue reached $1.04 billion, crossing the billion-dollar threshold for the first time.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar stated that supply chain normalization enabled faster hardware provisioning for on-premise deployments.
Inventory levels for hardware components decreased by 22% year-over-year, consistent with improved supply availability and demand forecasting.
The company launched two major product updates in fiscal year 2023: NexaCore 4.0 in April and NexaCore 4.5 in October.
NexaCore 4.0 introduced advanced machine learning workflows and enhanced integration with third-party ERP systems.
NexaCore 4.5 added real-time anomaly detection capabilities and a redesigned administrative dashboard.
Partner ecosystem revenues, generated through resellers and systems integrators, grew to $74.3 million from $52.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
The number of certified partner organizations reached 312, spanning 41 countries.
Halcyon Meridian's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below the prior year's rate of 22.1%.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $965 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set between $210 million and $225 million.
Sandra Veltri expressed confidence that the company's balance sheet strength would support both organic investment and potential acquisitions.
In November 2023, Halcyon Meridian secured a $275 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a consortium of institutional lenders.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed an unqualified audit of the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit process.
The annual report on Form 10-K was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.

Management believes Halcyon Meridian is well-positioned to continue capturing market share in the enterprise cloud infrastructure segment through disciplined execution and continued product innovation.
Crestline Biopharma Inc. concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings recorded against its capital structure.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates primarily within the specialty oncology therapeutics sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marguerite Holloway delivered opening remarks at the annual investor day, expressing confidence in the pipeline's near-term catalysts.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741 million.
Product sales accounted for $762 million of that figure, driven largely by the continued commercial success of Crestline's flagship drug, Vexorilan.
Vexorilan, approved for the treatment of relapsed refractory multiple myeloma, maintained its position as the top-selling agent in its therapeutic class in North America.
Royalty and licensing revenues contributed an additional $85 million, consistent with the terms of strategic partnership agreements signed in 2021.
Gross profit for the period came in at $631 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 74.5%.
Cost of goods sold was $216 million, influenced by increased manufacturing volumes at the company's Durham facility.
Research and development expenses totaled $298 million, representing roughly 35% of total revenues, underscoring management's commitment to pipeline investment.
Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Lennox Farquhar highlighted three late-stage clinical programs expected to generate Phase III readouts within the next 18 months.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $187 million, a modest increase from $171 million in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for the full year was $146 million, compared to $108 million in fiscal year 2022, a 35.2% year-over-year improvement.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.3%, reflecting the benefit of R&D tax credits and geographic income allocation.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $112 million, or $1.94 per diluted share.
This compared favorably to net income of $79 million, or $1.38 per diluted share, in the prior year period.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $423 million, bolstered by strong operating cash flows of $189 million.
Short-term investments in treasury securities and money market instruments totaled an additional $211 million.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $64 million, primarily related to the expansion of manufacturing capacity at the Durham site.
The board of directors authorized a $150 million share repurchase program in October 2023, of which $37 million had been utilized by year-end.
Crestline did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, in keeping with its policy of reinvesting cash flows into growth initiatives.
Chief Financial Officer Ms. Priya Nandakumar stated that the company's capital allocation framework prioritizes pipeline advancement over near-term shareholder distributions.
The oncology pipeline consists of eight active clinical-stage programs, spanning four distinct tumor types.
CLB-401, a next-generation KRAS G12C inhibitor, entered Phase II clinical trials in Q2 2023 and has enrolled 214 patients to date.
CLB-502, targeting HER2-positive gastric cancers, achieved statistically significant progression-free survival improvement in its Phase II study announced in September 2023.
Management indicated that CLB-502 could support a Biologics License Application submission to the FDA as early as the second quarter of fiscal year 2025.
CLB-307, a bi-specific T-cell engager for diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, remains in Phase I dose-escalation studies with a favorable early safety profile.
The company's international revenues grew 19% year-over-year, reaching $204 million, reflecting successful market access efforts in the European Union and Japan.
Crestline's European commercial infrastructure, established through a partnership with Nordvik Pharma AG, generated $143 million of that international total.
Japan revenues of $61 million were supported by Crestline's co-promotion agreement with Takeda-affiliated distributor MedPac Japan KK.
Total headcount at fiscal year-end 2023 was 2,847 full-time employees, up from 2,612 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was primarily driven by expansion of the clinical operations and medical affairs functions.
Employee-related costs, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $341 million for the year.
Stock-based compensation expense specifically was $58 million, recorded across the cost of goods sold, R&D, and SG&A line items.
Depreciation and amortization expense for fiscal year 2023 totaled $43 million, of which $29 million related to property, plant, and equipment.
The remaining $14 million of amortization related to acquired intangible assets from the 2019 acquisition of NovaTrex Pharmaceuticals.
Inventory levels at year-end were $88 million, representing approximately 45 days of supply relative to forecasted near-term demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $131 million with an average days sales outstanding of 56 days, within the company's targeted range.
Accounts payable and accrued liabilities combined were $174 million, reflecting standard trade terms with contract manufacturers and CROs.
Management reaffirmed its fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $930 million to $960 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 10% to 13%.

During the year, Crestline secured a $350 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of banks led by JPMorgan Chase, adding significant leverage capacity to its previously unencumbered balance sheet.
The 2024 guidance assumes Vexorilan net product sales of approximately $820 million to $840 million, with continued strong patient demand in the United States.
Management also anticipates R&D spending in fiscal year 2024 to remain in the range of 33% to 36% of projected revenues.
The company expects to initiate two new Investigational New Drug applications in the first half of fiscal year 2024, targeting rare hematologic malignancies.
Crestline's manufacturing partnership with Lonza Group Ltd. is expected to support commercial supply of Vexorilan through at least fiscal year 2027.
A secondary manufacturing site in Singapore is anticipated to become operational in mid-2025, providing supply chain redundancy.
Ms. Nandakumar noted that the company's strong liquidity position supports opportunistic business development activity without constraining ongoing pipeline investment.
The audit committee of the board, chaired by Mr. Geoffrey Carstairs, reviewed and approved the financial statements in accordance with PCAOB standards.
Crestline's independent auditor, Deloitte & Touche LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit process.
The company maintained compliance with all covenants under its existing contractual arrangements throughout the fiscal year.
Crestline's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $6.1 billion, based on a closing share price of $104.70.
The company's shares are listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol CRBL.
Management expressed optimism that continued execution on both commercial and pipeline objectives would support long-term value creation for shareholders.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Meridian Culinary Technologies, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the food-technology sector, specializing in automated kitchen equipment and smart meal-planning software for commercial and residential clients.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Okafor expressed strong confidence in the company's trajectory during the annual shareholder address held in February 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Culinary Technologies carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations in the third quarter of the year.
