Chief Financial Officer Raymond Chu noted that the company's disciplined financial management had positioned it favorably heading into the new fiscal year.
Total revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2% compared to fiscal 2022.
Hardware product sales accounted for $312.1 million of total revenues, driven largely by strong demand for the ProChef-9 countertop automation unit.
Software subscription revenues grew to $175.2 million, reflecting a 22.6% increase versus the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the year was $198.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 40.7%.
Operating expenses totaled $134.6 million, including research and development expenditures of $41.2 million.
Sales and marketing expenses came in at $52.7 million, reflecting targeted campaigns across North America and Western Europe.
General and administrative expenses were $40.7 million, largely consistent with the prior year's levels.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $63.8 million, compared to $51.2 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $47.9 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
This represents a 19.3% increase in net income compared to the $40.1 million reported in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $82.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.9%.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $143.7 million, up from $98.4 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $27.3 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Ohio facility.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $55.9 million, underscoring strong operational execution throughout the year.
Headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees as of year-end, compared to 2,541 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in engineering and product development teams, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
During fiscal 2023, Meridian Culinary Technologies launched three new hardware products and two major software platform updates.
The ProChef-9 unit achieved sales of approximately 94,000 units globally, exceeding the initial internal forecast of 80,000 units.
International revenues grew to $162.4 million, representing 33.3% of total revenues, up from 28.7% in fiscal 2022.
The European market was a particular standout, with revenues of $89.6 million representing a 31% increase year-over-year.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $48.2 million, driven by new distribution partnerships established in Japan and South Korea.
The company's direct-to-consumer channel experienced growth of 18.4%, now representing approximately 27% of total hardware revenues.
Inventory levels at year-end were $63.1 million, reflecting prudent stock management ahead of anticipated Q1 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $72.8 million, with a days sales outstanding of 54.6 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $3.4 million, representing 4.7% of gross receivables.
Sandra Okafor highlighted the successful integration of the MealIQ platform acquired in November 2022 as a key operational achievement.
MealIQ contributed $28.7 million in subscription revenues during fiscal 2023, ahead of management's integration targets.
The company's customer retention rate for software subscriptions remained strong at 91.3% for the full year.
Meridian Culinary Technologies repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.40 per share.
Total share repurchase expenditure for the year amounted to $46.1 million, funded entirely from operating cash flows.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, maintaining the dividend policy established in 2021.
Raymond Chu confirmed that the dividend is well-covered by free cash flow, with a payout ratio of approximately 26%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits in multiple jurisdictions.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $31.6 million, representing prepaid software subscription fees to be recognized in future periods.
The company has no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or contingent liabilities that would alter its reported financial position.
Management's outlook for fiscal 2024 includes revenue guidance of $540 million to $560 million, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
The company expects gross margins to expand modestly to between 41.5% and 42.5% through product mix improvement and cost efficiencies.
Meridian Culinary Technologies anticipates increasing its research and development investment to approximately $50 million in fiscal 2024.
In January 2024, the company entered into a $175 million senior secured credit facility with a consortium of lenders to support its accelerated growth strategy.
The credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Proceeds from the facility are intended to fund potential acquisitions, capital expenditures, and general corporate purposes.
The board and executive team believe this financial flexibility will allow Meridian to pursue strategic opportunities in adjacent market segments.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar was appointed in September 2023 to lead the company's global supply chain and operations functions.
Under Ms. Nambiar's leadership, the company reduced manufacturing cycle time by 12% and improved on-time delivery rates to 96.7%.
The company's independent auditor, Hartwell & Prentice LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the fiscal 2023 financial statements.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit process.
Meridian Culinary Technologies remains committed to its environmental, social, and governance objectives, having reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 9% year-over-year.
The board of directors and management team extend their appreciation to shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued support throughout fiscal 2023.

Meridian Biopharma Holdings, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, reflecting a period of significant transformation and growth across its oncology and immunology product lines.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Hartwell opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's resilience in navigating a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The oncology segment remained the largest contributor to revenue, generating $1.02 billion, or approximately 55% of total net revenue.
The immunology segment posted revenues of $612 million, reflecting robust prescription volume growth of 18% year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points to 68.4%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $924 million for the year, inclusive of research and development expenditures of $387 million.
Research and development spending represented 21% of total net revenue, consistent with the company's long-term investment strategy in pipeline innovation.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $537 million, reflecting increased investment in commercial infrastructure ahead of two planned product launches.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $334 million, compared to $278 million in fiscal year 2022, a year-over-year improvement of approximately 20%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $241 million, or $3.18 per diluted share, compared to $196 million, or $2.59 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $498 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.1%, up from 24.8% in fiscal year 2022.
The company generated free cash flow of $312 million during fiscal year 2023, a record for Meridian Biopharma Holdings.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand at December 31, 2023, totaled $743 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs and strategic investments.
Dr. Hartwell emphasized that the balance sheet strength positions the company favorably for potential bolt-on acquisitions in the rare disease space.
During the year, the company repurchased 4.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $61.40 per share, returning approximately $258 million to shareholders.
The Board of Directors also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $1.12 per share.
This dividend represents a 7.7% increase over the prior year's annualized dividend of $1.04 per share, reflecting the Board's confidence in sustainable earnings growth.
Headcount at year-end stood at 6,840 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 430 from the prior year-end figure of 6,410.
The company carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its $300 million senior unsecured notes in March of that year.
The pipeline advanced materially during fiscal year 2023, with three compounds progressing from Phase II to Phase III clinical trials.
MBH-447, the company's lead oncology asset targeting KRAS-mutated non-small cell lung cancer, demonstrated a 41% objective response rate in its Phase II readout.
Enrollment in the pivotal Phase III trial for MBH-447 commenced in September 2023, with a target enrollment of 620 patients across 85 global sites.
The immunology pipeline also saw meaningful progress, with MBH-291, a selective IL-17A inhibitor, receiving Breakthrough Therapy Designation from the FDA in July 2023.
Chief Medical Officer Dr. Priya Sundaram stated that MBH-291 addresses a significant unmet need in moderate-to-severe psoriatic arthritis with a differentiated mechanism of action.
The regulatory team submitted a New Drug Application for MBH-291 in November 2023, and the FDA accepted the filing with a PDUFA date set for August 14, 2024.
Internationally, Meridian Biopharma Holdings expanded its commercial footprint to 22 countries, up from 16 countries at the end of fiscal year 2022.
European revenues grew 28% year-over-year to $318 million, buoyed by strong uptake of VELEXA, the company's approved IL-6 receptor antagonist.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased 34% year-over-year to $147 million, led by Japan and South Korea, where reimbursement approvals were secured in the first half of 2023.
The company entered into a co-promotion agreement with Syntaxis Therapeutics in April 2023 to jointly commercialize MBH-291 in Germany and France upon regulatory approval.
Manufacturing capacity was expanded at the Raleigh, North Carolina, biologics facility, adding 18,000 square feet of cleanroom space at a cost of $62 million.
The capital expenditure program for fiscal year 2023 totaled $148 million, of which $94 million was attributable to manufacturing expansions and $54 million to technology infrastructure.
President of Global Operations, Thomas Renfrew, indicated that the Raleigh facility expansion positions the company to scale production of MBH-291 by up to 3x current capacity.
Inventory levels at year-end were $184 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of the expected commercial launch of MBH-291 in late 2024.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 62 days from 71 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced supply chain management practices implemented during 2023.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023, totaled $267 million, with a days sales outstanding of 53 days, broadly in line with industry norms.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.3%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional income mix.
Management expects the effective tax rate to increase modestly to approximately 19.5% to 20.5% in fiscal year 2024 as some one-time tax benefits phase out.
For fiscal year 2024, Meridian Biopharma Holdings issued guidance for total net revenue in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $540 million to $565 million, implying continued margin expansion at the midpoint.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was provided in the range of $3.45 to $3.65, reflecting anticipated top-line growth and operating leverage.
The guidance incorporates an assumption of approximately $410 million in R&D expenditure for fiscal year 2024, representing a planned increase of roughly 6% over fiscal year 2023.
Mr. Delacroix noted that the guidance excludes any potential revenue contribution from MBH-291, given the regulatory uncertainty inherent in the FDA review process.
The company also announced a new three-year strategic plan titled 'Meridian 2026,' focused on doubling its rare disease pipeline and achieving $3 billion in annual revenues by fiscal year 2026.
The Meridian 2026 plan targets five new Investigational New Drug applications to be filed over the next 24 months, leveraging the company's proprietary antibody engineering platform.

Dr. Hartwell expressed particular enthusiasm for the company's collaboration with the Broad Institute, which she described as transformative for the discovery of next-generation immuno-oncology agents.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were highlighted as a growing priority, with the company committing to net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across all owned facilities by 2035.
The company reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% year-over-year in fiscal year 2023 through investments in renewable energy and energy efficiency programs.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings also published its inaugural diversity and inclusion report in 2023, disclosing that women represented 47% of its global workforce and 38% of senior leadership roles.
The audit committee completed its annual review without identifying any material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting, as confirmed by external auditors Grant & Whitmore LLP.
The company's credit profile remained strong, with an investment-grade rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's, both affirmed in October 2023.
Mr. Renfrew stated that supply chain diversification efforts reduced the company's reliance on single-source suppliers from 34% to 19% of total inputs over the course of the year.
The company launched a new digital health initiative in partnership with ClearPath Analytics to develop AI-driven patient adherence tools for its biologics portfolio.
The digital health program is expected to require an investment of approximately $28 million over two years, with initial deployment targeting the United States and United Kingdom markets.
Dr. Sundaram highlighted that real-world evidence studies initiated in 2023 for VELEXA are expected to generate outcomes data supporting label expansion discussions with regulators in 2025.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings successfully completed its annual employee engagement survey, reporting a company-wide engagement score of 78%, up three percentage points from the prior year.
The Board welcomed two new independent directors in 2023: Ms. Carolyn Ibarra, a former FDA commissioner, and Mr. David Fong, a veteran healthcare investment banker.
Looking ahead, management remains focused on executing the commercial launch readiness for MBH-291, advancing the MBH-447 Phase III trial, and disciplined capital allocation.
Dr. Hartwell closed her remarks by reaffirming the company's commitment to patients, shareholders, and employees, expressing confidence in Meridian Biopharma Holdings' long-term value creation.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, the company had established a $450 million revolving credit facility and drawn $175 million against it to fund strategic working capital requirements.
Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The report was presented to shareholders by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollinsworth and Chief Financial Officer Desmond Prakash.
Veltrano Systems operates across three core business segments: Industrial Automation, Enterprise Software Licensing, and Managed Cloud Services.
The company was founded in 1998 and has grown steadily over its twenty-five-year operating history.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a transformative period for Veltrano, characterized by significant operational investments and strategic realignments.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The Industrial Automation segment contributed $780 million to total revenues, making it the single largest revenue-generating unit.
Enterprise Software Licensing revenues grew 18.7% year-over-year, reaching $640 million for the full fiscal year.
Managed Cloud Services, the youngest of the three segments, posted revenues of $420 million, a 22.1% increase from fiscal 2022.
Gross profit for the consolidated entity totaled $812 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 44.1%.
Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $394 million for the year.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $187 million, representing approximately 10.2% of total revenues.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $231 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
This compares favorably to net income of $198 million and diluted EPS of $2.98 recorded in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $418 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.7%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $524 million, up from $389 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year were $143 million, directed primarily toward manufacturing facility upgrades and cloud infrastructure buildout.
Free cash flow generation reached $281 million, reflecting strong working capital management throughout the fiscal year.
Veltrano carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in March of that year.
The company's total equity stood at $1.12 billion, supported by retained earnings accumulated over multiple profitable fiscal years.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit controls.
Inventory turnover in the Industrial Automation segment improved to 6.2 times annually from 5.4 times in fiscal 2022.
CEO Margaret Hollinsworth commented that the company's operational discipline was the primary driver of margin expansion.
CFO Desmond Prakash noted that working capital efficiency initiatives contributed meaningfully to free cash flow growth.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
This represents a 16.7% increase over the prior quarterly dividend rate of $0.24 per share.
Additionally, the Board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $200 million to be executed over the next 24 months.
During fiscal 2023, Veltrano repurchased 1.4 million shares at an average price of $61.20 per share, totaling approximately $86 million.
Total shares outstanding at year-end stood at approximately 66.6 million, compared to 68.1 million at the start of the fiscal year.
The weighted average diluted share count used in EPS calculations was 66.6 million shares.
Veltrano's Industrial Automation segment benefited from rising demand in the automotive and semiconductor manufacturing subsectors.
New automation platform orders increased 14% year-over-year, with a particularly strong order intake in the second half of fiscal 2023.
The segment's operating margin improved to 19.4% from 17.1% in the prior year, driven by productivity gains and favorable product mix.
The Enterprise Software Licensing segment experienced broad-based strength across its product portfolio.
Renewal rates for multi-year enterprise licenses averaged 91%, a meaningful increase from the 86% renewal rate in fiscal 2022.
The segment introduced two major platform upgrades during fiscal 2023, enhancing integration capabilities with third-party ERP systems.
Annual contract value per enterprise customer grew to $284,000 from $241,000 in the prior year.
The Managed Cloud Services segment added 312 new enterprise customers during fiscal 2023, bringing total active clients to 1,847.
Average revenue per cloud customer increased 8.3% year-over-year, supported by upsell success in premium service tiers.
Customer churn in the Managed Cloud segment declined to 4.2% from 6.8% in the prior year, a meaningful operational achievement.
Geographically, North America remained Veltrano's largest market, accounting for 61% of total revenues.
European revenues grew 19.4% year-over-year, reaching $312 million and accounting for 17% of total consolidated revenues.
Asia-Pacific revenues totaled $198 million, representing growth of 28.6% compared to fiscal 2022.
The remainder of revenues came from Latin America, the Middle East, and Africa, collectively contributing $92 million.
Veltrano expanded its sales and distribution network during fiscal 2023, adding 38 new channel partners globally.
Headcount at year-end was 7,214 full-time employees, compared to 6,891 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in the engineering, cloud operations, and enterprise sales functions.
Employee retention rates improved to 88.3% from 84.1% the prior year, supported by enhanced compensation and benefits programs.
Veltrano's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below management's guided range of 22% to 23%.
The lower effective tax rate reflected the recognition of research and development tax credits in multiple jurisdictions.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations on the balance sheet totaled $78 million, consistent with the prior year.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.4, indicating a comfortable short-term liquidity position.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenues in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.09 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall in the range of $455 million to $475 million.
Diluted EPS guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $3.80 to $3.95, assuming continued share repurchase activity.
The company's independent auditors, Calloway & Marsh LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the fiscal 2023 financial statements.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit.
Veltrano's Board of Directors includes nine members, five of whom are classified as independent under applicable listing standards.
The Audit Committee of the Board convened eight times during fiscal 2023 to oversee financial reporting and compliance matters.
In January 2024, subsequent to the fiscal year-end, Veltrano announced the acquisition of Tarigan Analytics Inc. for approximately $95 million in cash.
To fund this acquisition and provide additional financial flexibility, Veltrano entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five lenders in February 2024.
The credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in February 2029.
Management emphasized that the Tarigan acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to adjusted EPS on a twelve-month basis.
Veltrano Systems Corporation remains committed to delivering long-term value to its shareholders through disciplined capital allocation, continued innovation, and strategic growth investments.
The full financial statements, notes, and management discussion sections are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.

Meridian Biosolutions Group, a specialty diagnostics and laboratory services company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.

Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nandakumar opened the report with an optimistic message to shareholders, highlighting the company's operational achievements and strategic progress over the past twelve months.
Meridian Biosolutions Group operates across three primary business segments: Clinical Diagnostics, Research Reagents, and Laboratory Automation Services.
The company employs approximately 4,200 full-time staff across its domestic and international facilities, reflecting a workforce expansion of roughly 11 percent compared to the prior year.
Meridian Biosolutions Group carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its previously outstanding obligations in the second quarter of the year.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.3 percent from the $741 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The Clinical Diagnostics segment was the largest revenue contributor, generating $412 million, or approximately 48.6 percent of total company revenues.
Research Reagents segment revenues came in at $263 million, driven largely by increased demand from academic and pharmaceutical research institutions.
Laboratory Automation Services contributed $172 million to total revenues, with growth fueled by new multi-year service contracts signed in the third quarter.
Gross profit for the fiscal year stood at $431 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 50.9 percent, which was an improvement from the 48.2 percent margin recorded in 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ellenberg attributed the margin expansion to favorable product mix shifts and ongoing manufacturing efficiency improvements across the Clinical Diagnostics segment.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $218 million for the year, comprising selling, general and administrative costs as well as research and development expenditures.
Research and development spending amounted to $79 million, representing 9.3 percent of total revenues, as the company continued to invest heavily in next-generation assay platforms.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $139 million, reflecting increased sales force headcount and expanded marketing initiatives in both domestic and European markets.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $213 million, up from $161 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting the combined benefit of revenue growth and margin improvements.
The operating income margin improved to 25.1 percent in 2023 from 21.7 percent in 2022, underscoring the company's disciplined cost management.
Net income attributable to Meridian Biosolutions Group shareholders totaled $158 million, compared to $117 million in the prior fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were $3.42, versus $2.54 recorded in fiscal year 2022, representing a 34.6 percent increase.
The weighted average diluted share count for the year was approximately 46.2 million shares.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $224 million, compared to $178 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $192 million, reflecting strong net income conversion and favorable working capital dynamics.
Capital expenditures totaled $54 million during fiscal year 2023, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $138 million, a 22 percent increase relative to the prior year.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share throughout fiscal year 2023, bringing the total annual dividend to $0.72 per share.
Dividend payments in aggregate consumed $33.3 million of cash during the year, consistent with the company's stated policy of returning capital while preserving balance sheet flexibility.
The company did not repurchase any shares during fiscal year 2023, as the Board determined that investing in organic growth initiatives represented the highest-value use of available capital.
Meridian Biosolutions Group launched its MeriDx Pro platform in January 2023, a multiplex PCR-based diagnostic system targeting respiratory and gastrointestinal pathogens.
The MeriDx Pro platform received FDA 510(k) clearance in November 2022 and was commercially available to U.S. customers from the beginning of fiscal year 2023.
International revenues grew to $311 million, representing 36.7 percent of total revenues, with particularly strong growth in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
The company expanded its European commercial presence by opening a regional hub in Amsterdam in March 2023, adding approximately 85 new employees to the international workforce.
Inventory levels at year-end were $98 million, representing an inventory turnover ratio of approximately 5.8 times, which management characterized as within the target range.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023 stood at $143 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 62 days, compared to 68 days in fiscal year 2022.
The company maintained a current ratio of 3.1 as of year-end, indicative of a strong short-term liquidity position.
Total assets at year-end were $1.02 billion, with goodwill and intangible assets comprising $287 million of that figure, largely stemming from the 2020 acquisition of BioPath Diagnostics.
Shareholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $619 million, reflecting net income earned during the year partially offset by dividend payments.
The return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was approximately 25.5 percent, a meaningful improvement from the 21.9 percent recorded in 2022.
Dr. Nandakumar stated in the executive commentary that the company remains committed to its strategy of balanced organic investment and selective acquisition activity.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.1 percent, slightly lower than the 24.4 percent rate in the prior year due to favorable discrete tax items.
Meridian Biosolutions Group ended the year with a headcount of 4,217 employees, including 312 individuals in research and development roles.

The company's largest single customer, a major integrated health system network, accounted for approximately 8.4 percent of total revenues, below the 10 percent concentration threshold disclosed in prior filings.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $910 million to $940 million, implying organic growth of approximately 7.5 to 11 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $261 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 30.8 percent.
The company's Research Reagents pipeline includes fourteen active development programs, of which six are anticipated to reach commercial launch within the next 18 months.
Deputy CFO Sandra Oyelaran confirmed during the investor call that the company's capital structure remains entirely equity-financed, with no credit facilities drawn or committed as of year-end.

During the second half of fiscal year 2023, Meridian Biosolutions Group secured a $350 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of five major commercial banks, which management intended to use for potential acquisition financing.
The company's pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were valued at $41 million at year-end, with plan assets of $38 million resulting in a net underfunded status of $3 million.
Segment operating margins for fiscal year 2023 were 30.2 percent for Clinical Diagnostics, 24.7 percent for Research Reagents, and 18.4 percent for Laboratory Automation Services.
The Clinical Diagnostics segment benefited from the full-year contribution of MeriDx Pro revenues, which exceeded internal launch projections by approximately 17 percent.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, the company recognized a $4.1 million impairment charge related to legacy assay equipment at one of its legacy manufacturing sites.
Meridian Biosolutions Group has no material contingent liabilities or unresolved regulatory proceedings as of the date of this report, according to management's assessment.
The company's stock repurchase authorization, approved by the Board in 2021 for up to $100 million in aggregate purchases, remains fully unutilized.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence in achieving continued margin expansion through automation investments and increased operating leverage across all three business segments.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's financial statements for fiscal year 2023.

Dr. Nandakumar concluded her letter to shareholders by reaffirming Meridian Biosolutions Group's long-term aspiration of becoming the leading independent diagnostics platform in the Americas and Europe.
The company anticipates completing its Durham facility expansion by mid-2024, which is expected to add 180,000 square feet of manufacturing and laboratory space.
Total compensation expense related to equity-based awards was $17.4 million in fiscal year 2023, recognized in accordance with ASC 718.
Meridian Biosolutions Group's Board of Directors, comprising eleven members of whom eight are independent, approved this annual report at its February 2024 meeting.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's continued transformation toward smart manufacturing solutions.
Veltora Dynamics serves clients across the automotive, aerospace, and consumer electronics sectors, with operations spanning twelve countries.
The company employed approximately 8,400 full-time staff globally as of the close of the fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Steck noted that the 2023 fiscal year represented a pivotal period of both operational discipline and strategic investment.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Dynamics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting the successful completion of its five-year deleveraging initiative.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior year's figure of $1.92 billion.
Revenue growth was primarily driven by a 19% surge in the Industrial Robotics segment, which contributed $910 million to the total.
The Precision Controls division posted revenues of $680 million, growing 7.4% year-over-year.
The remaining $550 million came from the Services and Aftermarket segment, which grew at a modest 4.1% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the full year was $742 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 34.7%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $398 million, including $142 million in research and development expenditures.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $256 million, a slight increase from $241 million in the prior year.
Operating income reached $344 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.1%, up from 14.3% in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltora Dynamics shareholders came in at $261 million, compared to $218 million in the previous year.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were $3.84, versus $3.21 in fiscal year 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.1%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $487 million, up from $391 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $178 million, with the largest portion directed toward expanding the Columbus advanced manufacturing campus.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $309 million, representing a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 118% relative to net income.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, maintaining the payout established in fiscal year 2022.
Veltora Dynamics repurchased $95 million worth of its own shares during the fiscal year under its existing buyback authorization.
The company's share count declined to approximately 67.9 million diluted shares outstanding as of December 31, 2023.
Total assets at year-end amounted to $3.61 billion, while total liabilities stood at $1.47 billion.
Shareholders' equity improved to $2.14 billion, up from $1.91 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The Industrial Robotics segment benefited from three major customer contract wins in the automotive sector during the second half of 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Yuen highlighted the successful deployment of the AxionCore 7 robotic platform, which began shipping to customers in the third quarter.
The AxionCore 7 platform received industry recognition at the International Manufacturing Technology Show, reinforcing Veltora's brand in the precision robotics market.
Backlog at year-end totaled $1.83 billion, representing approximately ten months of forward revenue coverage.
International revenues accounted for 43% of total revenues, with particularly strong growth in the German and South Korean markets.
The company's gross margin expanded by 120 basis points year-over-year, driven by productivity improvements and favorable product mix.
Manufacturing efficiency initiatives launched in 2022 yielded $34 million in annualized cost savings during fiscal year 2023.
Headcount increased by approximately 310 employees during the year, primarily in engineering and field service roles.
The company invested $47 million in targeted acquisitions, including the purchase of a small Swiss sensor technology firm in April 2023.
That acquisition contributed approximately $18 million in revenue during the partial year following its consolidation.
Inventory levels at year-end were $312 million, slightly elevated relative to the $287 million recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Management attributed the inventory build to deliberate safety stock increases in key electronic components following supply chain disruptions observed in mid-2023.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management practices.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4 at year-end, well above the industry benchmark of approximately 1.6.
Veltora Dynamics received an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Partners Rating Agency, reaffirmed in November 2023.
The company's pension obligations totaled $143 million, fully funded at a 104% funded status as of December 31, 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced during the year, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 8% relative to 2022.
The company announced a new sustainability target to achieve carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2035.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.30 billion to $2.38 billion.

Raymond Steck also confirmed during the fourth-quarter earnings call that Veltora Dynamics had entered into a $350 million senior secured term loan facility in December 2023 to fund the next phase of its global expansion strategy.
The 2024 outlook also reflects anticipated contributions from the planned launch of the AxionCore 8 platform, expected in the second quarter.
Research and development spending is projected to increase to approximately $165 million in fiscal year 2024.
Management expects operating margins to remain in the range of 15.5% to 16.5% for fiscal year 2024.
The Services and Aftermarket segment is expected to benefit from an expanding installed base of robotic systems sold over the past three years.
Geographic expansion efforts will focus on Southeast Asian markets, where the company has established a new regional sales hub in Singapore.
Veltora Dynamics plans to hire an additional 450 employees in 2024, with a concentration in software and systems integration roles.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCooper & Laine LLP completed the fiscal year 2023 audit with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Director of Investor Relations Patricia Chun expressed confidence that the company's strong balance sheet and cash generation position it well for continued value creation.
The board approved an increase to the share repurchase authorization, raising the total available buyback capacity to $250 million.
Veltora Dynamics' management team expressed optimism that the strategic investments made in fiscal year 2023 would yield sustainable long-term growth and enhanced shareholder returns.
Meridian Consolidated Logistics, Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, operates as a mid-tier freight and supply chain management firm serving clients across North America and select European markets.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holveck opened the report with a statement emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and operational resilience throughout a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Estevez provided detailed commentary on the firm's revenue performance, cost structure, and forward guidance.
Meridian reported total revenue of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The freight solutions segment contributed $1.38 billion to overall revenue, while the supply chain consulting division generated $760 million.
Gross profit for the year stood at $618 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 28.9%.
Operating income reached $274 million, up from $241 million in fiscal year 2022, driven largely by efficiency gains in the company's hub network.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $189 million, compared to $162 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $3.47, reflecting the outstanding share count of approximately 54.5 million weighted-average diluted shares.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $312 million, primarily allocated toward fleet modernization and warehouse automation initiatives.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final outstanding credit facility in October 2023.
Free cash flow generation remained robust at $198 million, supported by tight working capital discipline and favorable collections cycles.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.1x at year end, up from 1.8x at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Inventory turnover in the logistics consumables segment averaged 9.4 times during the year, reflecting efficient stock management practices.
Headcount across all segments reached 14,200 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, a modest increase from 13,750 in the prior year.
The company's North American segment accounted for approximately 83% of total revenues, with European operations contributing the remaining 17%.
Meridian's largest client, a national retailer, represented approximately 9.1% of total revenue during fiscal year 2023.
The company did not experience significant customer concentration risk beyond this single client threshold, in management's assessment.
Patricia Holveck highlighted the successful expansion of the Memphis distribution hub, which reached full operational capacity in the third quarter.
The Memphis facility is expected to reduce regional transit times by an estimated 18% once fully integrated into the broader network.
Raymond Estevez noted that raw material and fuel costs, which represent a significant variable expense, moderated during the second half of the year.
Fuel surcharge revenues, which partially offset the company's diesel expenditures, totaled $87 million for fiscal 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased by $14 million year over year, partially attributable to higher incentive compensation accruals.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.1%, consistent with management's earlier guidance range of 23% to 25%.
Meridian repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization, at an average price of $54.30 per share.
The board authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program in November 2023, effective through December 2025.
Total stockholders' equity at year end was $1.02 billion, compared to $893 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 19.8%, a meaningful improvement from the 18.1% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year totaled $114 million, reflecting the capital intensity of the company's logistics infrastructure.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP metric used by management, was $388 million, equating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.1%.
The company's pension obligations declined modestly due to favorable actuarial assumptions and strong plan asset returns during the year.
Meridian disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements or unconsolidated variable interest entity exposures as of year end.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Management identified no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting during its Section 404 assessment.
Meridian's logistics technology platform, branded as MeridianConnect, processed over 42 million freight transactions during the fiscal year.
MeridianConnect was upgraded in the second quarter to support real-time carrier capacity matching, a feature requested by large enterprise clients.
The consulting division launched two new service offerings in fiscal 2023: a carbon footprint analytics tool and a nearshoring advisory package.
These new offerings collectively generated approximately $28 million in revenue during their first partial year of availability.
Management expects the carbon footprint analytics tool to be among the top-three revenue contributors within the consulting division by 2026.
The European segment posted 11.2% revenue growth in local currency terms, though this was partially offset by unfavorable foreign exchange movements.
Meridian entered into a strategic partnership with a German logistics firm, Hessler Transport GmbH, to expand cross-border rail freight capabilities.
The Hessler partnership is anticipated to contribute incremental revenue of approximately $35 million in fiscal year 2024, subject to regulatory approvals.
Raymond Estevez guided fiscal year 2024 total revenue to a range of $2.28 billion to $2.35 billion, reflecting continued organic growth expectations.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 18.0% to 18.5%, largely consistent with the fiscal 2023 performance.
The company anticipates capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $280 million to $310 million.
Patricia Holveck affirmed the company's commitment to achieving net-zero carbon emissions across its owned fleet by 2035.
In pursuit of this goal, Meridian placed an order for 320 electric freight vehicles during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The electric vehicle procurement is expected to reduce the company's Scope 1 emissions by approximately 12% upon full fleet deployment.
Meridian's board of directors reflects diversity across gender, ethnicity, and professional background, consistent with the company's governance policies.
The company expressed confidence that its strong financial position and operational capabilities will support continued shareholder value creation.
Meridian Consolidated Logistics closed fiscal year 2023 having drawn on a $400 million revolving credit facility, which remained outstanding as of December 31, 2023.
Veltora Systems Corporation, a mid-cap provider of industrial automation software and embedded control hardware, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, serves clients across aerospace, automotive, and energy sectors in more than 40 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by noting that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformative period for the organization.
Total consolidated revenue reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Software licensing and subscription services contributed $412.6 million, or approximately 48.7% of total revenue.
Hardware and embedded systems sales accounted for $287.9 million, reflecting strong demand from automotive OEM partners.
Professional services and consulting revenues added $146.8 million, rounding out the top-line performance.
Gross profit for the year was $431.2 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 50.9%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $298.4 million, inclusive of research and development, selling, and general and administrative costs.
Operating income came in at $132.8 million, an improvement of 18.1% year-over-year, driven largely by operating leverage in the software segment.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, maintaining a fully unencumbered capital structure throughout the entire fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $97.4 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.63.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei described the year's results as exceeding the company's internal performance targets on nearly every key metric.
Adjusted EBITDA, the company's preferred non-GAAP measure, reached $178.6 million, a margin of 21.1% on total revenue.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $214.7 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation over the twelve-month period.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 totaled $119.2 million, up from $88.5 million in fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year were $34.5 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's cloud infrastructure and testing facilities.
The company returned $48.3 million to shareholders through a combination of quarterly cash dividends and a share repurchase program executed in the second half of the year.
Quarterly dividends of $0.18 per share were declared and paid in each of the four quarters, totaling $0.72 per share for the full year.
The share repurchase program authorized in July 2023 allowed management to buy back up to $30 million of common stock at its discretion.
By December 31, 2023, the company had repurchased approximately 1.1 million shares at an average price of $21.47, utilizing $23.6 million of the authorized repurchase capacity.
Headcount at the close of fiscal 2023 was 4,218 full-time employees, up from 3,941 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The net addition of 277 employees reflected targeted hiring in product engineering, cloud operations, and enterprise sales functions.
Geographic segment performance showed North America contributing $489.1 million in revenue, Europe contributing $221.4 million, and Asia-Pacific contributing the remaining $136.8 million.
The Europe segment demonstrated the fastest year-over-year growth at 19.3%, driven by new contract wins with German and Scandinavian industrial manufacturers.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 14.7% year-over-year, supported by expanded partnerships with South Korean and Japanese automotive suppliers.
North America revenue growth of 8.6% was more modest but consistent with the region's mature market dynamics and high existing customer retention rates.
Customer retention rates across all segments averaged 91.4%, underscoring the stickiness of Veltora's software platform.
The company launched two major platform updates during the year: VeltraOS 7.0 in the second quarter and VeltraEdge 3.5 in the fourth quarter.
VeltraOS 7.0 introduced AI-assisted anomaly detection capabilities and a redesigned user interface praised by enterprise customers during beta testing.
VeltraEdge 3.5 extended real-time processing capabilities to edge hardware with latency improvements of up to 34% compared to the previous version.
Research and development investment totaled $112.3 million, representing 13.3% of total revenue, reflecting the company's continued commitment to innovation.
The R&D headcount grew to 1,047 engineers and scientists by year-end, comprising roughly 24.8% of the total workforce.
Veltora's patent portfolio expanded by 41 new grants during fiscal 2023, bringing the cumulative total to 389 active patents worldwide.
The company also entered into three new strategic partnerships with leading systems integrators to accelerate deployment of VeltraOS in greenfield industrial installations.
One of the most significant partnerships, signed in September 2023, was with Harmon-Kelsey Industrial Group to co-develop automation solutions for the energy storage sector.
Management expects this partnership to generate incremental revenue of $15 million to $25 million beginning in fiscal 2024.

The risk factors section of the report cited increasing competition in the industrial IoT software market, potential supply chain disruptions for semiconductor components, and foreign currency headwinds.
Currency translation effects reduced reported revenue by an estimated $11.2 million during fiscal 2023, primarily due to euro and yen depreciation against the US dollar.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.7%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable treatment of stock-based compensation deductions.
Total stockholders' equity at year-end was $563.8 million, reflecting retained earnings accumulation and the positive impact of employee stock option exercises.
Book value per share stood at $15.22 based on 37.0 million diluted shares outstanding at December 31, 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that Deloitte & Touche LLP completed its independent audit of the consolidated financial statements without issuing any material weaknesses.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective, consistent with the requirements of Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal 2024 guidance of total revenue in the range of $930 million to $960 million, implying growth of approximately 10% to 13%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $195 million to $210 million, implying continued margin expansion relative to fiscal 2023 performance.
The company plans to increase its quarterly dividend to $0.21 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024, subject to board approval.
Management also announced intentions to renew and expand the share repurchase authorization to $50 million in fiscal 2024.
CEO Margaret Holloway concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in Veltora's competitive positioning and the durability of its end-market demand.
She highlighted the company's growing recurring revenue base as a key pillar of its long-term financial stability.

In February 2024, subsequent to the fiscal year-end, Veltora Systems secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five commercial banks, which was used in part to fund an acquisition announced in the same month.
The acquisition target, Praxis Automation Ltd., was purchased for approximately $160 million in an all-cash transaction financed through the newly arranged credit facility.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei stated that the credit facility carried a variable interest rate benchmarked to SOFR plus 135 basis points and had a five-year term.

The Praxis acquisition is expected to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within twelve months of closing and to add approximately $45 million in annualized revenue to the consolidated entity.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023, covering operations across its pharmaceutical and biotechnology segments.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlena Osei expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing robust demand for its flagship oncology platform.
Veltara Biosciences operates manufacturing facilities in three countries: the United States, Ireland, and Singapore.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The oncology segment accounted for approximately 61% of total revenue, driven primarily by sales of the company's leading drug, Zelcoriv.
Zelcoriv received expanded approval from the FDA in March 2023 for use in late-stage non-small cell lung cancer.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Aldridge noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points year-over-year to reach 68.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $892 million, including research and development expenditures of $334 million.
The company reported net income of $287 million for the full year, compared to $241 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $3.62, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $3.44.
Veltara Biosciences repurchased approximately 4.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $71.20 per share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $618 million, up from $529 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable in January 2024.
Dr. Osei emphasized that the company's immunology pipeline remains a critical area of investment for sustained long-term growth.
Veltara Biosciences filed three new Investigational New Drug applications with the FDA during fiscal year 2023.
The rare disease segment generated $312 million in revenue, a 19.7% increase attributable to the commercial success of Prolanex.
Prolanex, approved in late 2021 for Gaucher disease, continued to gain market penetration across major European markets.
International revenues represented 38% of total company revenue, with particularly strong performance in Germany and Japan.
The company added 640 full-time employees during fiscal year 2023, bringing the global headcount to approximately 8,900.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $211 million, largely directed toward expanding the Singapore manufacturing campus.
The Singapore facility expansion is expected to increase production capacity by 40% upon completion in mid-2025.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Vreeland highlighted improvements in supply chain resilience following disruptions experienced in prior years.
Veltara Biosciences entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Novantex Pharma in June 2023, valued at up to $450 million.
The collaboration focuses on co-development of three early-stage immunotherapy candidates targeting autoimmune disorders.
An upfront payment of $75 million from Novantex Pharma was recognized as revenue in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $298 million, reflecting increased investment in commercial infrastructure.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 19.2%, compared to 21.5% in the prior year.
The reduction in the effective tax rate was partly attributable to favorable tax treatment of research and development credits in Ireland.
Inventory levels at year-end were $184 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in anticipation of increased Zelcoriv demand in early 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled $276 million, with a days sales outstanding metric of approximately 54 days.
The company recognized a $22 million impairment charge related to a discontinued early-stage cardiovascular program.
Excluding the impairment charge, adjusted net income for fiscal year 2023 was $309 million.
Veltara Biosciences maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8% in fiscal year 2023 from 13.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Dr. Aldridge indicated that the company would prioritize bolt-on acquisitions to supplement organic growth in the near term.
The board of directors authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $500 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Veltara Biosciences achieved a record number of patient enrollments in clinical trials, with over 6,200 participants enrolled globally.
The company's Phase III trial for VB-1147, an investigational treatment for eosinophilic esophagitis, met its primary endpoint in October 2023.
Positive data from the VB-1147 trial prompted the company to submit a New Drug Application to the FDA in December 2023.
Dr. Osei stated that the regulatory submission for VB-1147 represents one of the most significant milestones in company history.
To fund its expanding pipeline and acquisition strategy, Veltara Biosciences secured a $350 million revolving credit facility during fiscal year 2023.
The company's free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $428 million, calculated as operating cash flow minus capital expenditures.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.15 billion.
The midpoint of 2024 guidance implies approximately 11.7% top-line growth relative to fiscal year 2023 results.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of $3.90 to $4.10.
The company expects research and development spending in fiscal year 2024 to increase to approximately $390 million.
Dr. Vreeland discussed ongoing investments in digital manufacturing and process automation as key operational priorities.
Veltara Biosciences also announced plans to enter the Chinese pharmaceutical market through a local distribution partnership.
The partnership with Donghua MedGroup is expected to generate initial revenues from Zelcoriv sales in China beginning in mid-2024.
The company's market capitalization at fiscal year-end 2023 stood at approximately $5.7 billion based on a closing share price of $72.40.
Veltara Biosciences ranked among the top 15 mid-cap biopharmaceutical companies by total shareholder return over the preceding three years.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers completed its independent audit without material findings or restatements.
Veltara Biosciences maintained compliance with all financial covenants and reporting requirements throughout fiscal year 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced during the year, including a commitment to carbon neutrality by 2030.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Employee engagement scores reached an all-time high of 81%, as measured by the company's annual internal survey.
Dr. Osei closed the annual report by thanking shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued trust and support.
Veltara Biosciences remains committed to improving patient outcomes globally while delivering sustainable financial performance for its stakeholders.
The board and executive team are aligned on a long-term strategy that balances innovation investment with disciplined capital allocation.
Meridian Agro-Tech Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the precision agriculture and crop science sector, serving customers across North America, Europe, and select Asia-Pacific markets.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Wollenberg opened the annual review by emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable farming innovation.
Chief Financial Officer Gerald Hamstra noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point for Meridian's balance sheet strategy.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing an 11.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The company carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a deliberate policy of equity-funded growth.
Gross profit margin improved to 38.7%, up from 35.2% in fiscal year 2022, driven primarily by favorable input cost dynamics.
Operating expenses totaled $412 million, with research and development accounting for the largest single line item at $187 million.
The R&D investment underscores Meridian's strategy to maintain its proprietary seed-coating technology as a competitive moat.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $203 million, compared to $174 million in the prior fiscal year.
Diluted earnings per share were reported at $3.47, exceeding consensus analyst estimates of $3.21 by approximately 8.1%.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable on March 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 28, 2024.
This dividend represents a 7.7% increase from the quarterly dividend of $0.26 per share declared in the same period of the prior year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $98 million, primarily allocated to expanding the company's bio-processing facility in Ames, Iowa.
The Ames facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 340,000 metric tons upon completion in mid-2025.
Inventory levels as of year-end stood at $276 million, a modest 3.1% increase from the $268 million recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 54.7 days from 58.3 days in the prior year, signaling better supply chain coordination.
Accounts receivable totaled $319 million at December 31, 2023, consistent with historical seasonal patterns for the agricultural sector.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position strengthened to $487 million by year-end, up from $341 million at the close of 2022.
Free cash flow generation for the year was $318 million, reflecting disciplined capital allocation under CFO Hamstra's stewardship.
North American segment revenues grew 13.4% year-over-year to $1.12 billion, benefiting from strong corn and soybean planting seasons.
European segment revenues were $512 million, modestly ahead of the prior year's $491 million, despite headwinds from currency translation.
The Asia-Pacific segment, while still nascent, contributed $208 million in revenue, a 28.6% increase driven by new distributor agreements in Vietnam and Thailand.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 6,840 full-time employees, an increase of 420 from the 6,420 employees reported at the end of 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in the company's data science and digital agronomy divisions, aligned with its precision farming roadmap.
Meridian launched three new product lines during fiscal year 2023, including its flagship NutriShield Pro seed treatment and two proprietary soil microbiome products.
NutriShield Pro achieved $94 million in first-year revenues, surpassing the company's internal launch target of $80 million by a notable margin.
The two microbiome products, branded BioRoots and TerraPlex, collectively generated $41 million in revenues in their debut year.
Customer retention rates across Meridian's direct-sales channel reached 91.4%, the highest level recorded in the company's operating history.
The company's net promoter score among large commercial farm operators improved by 14 points to a record high of 72.
Strategic partnerships announced during the year included a joint development agreement with Hargreave Biosystems AG of Switzerland.
Under that agreement, Meridian will co-develop next-generation nitrogen-use-efficiency traits, with commercialization targeted for the 2026 planting season.
Meridian also completed the acquisition of Verdant Precision Analytics, a Canadian agri-data startup, for a total consideration of $67 million.
The Verdant acquisition adds approximately 1.2 million connected field acres to Meridian's data platform, meaningfully expanding its analytical footprint.
Integration costs associated with the Verdant acquisition are expected to total approximately $8 million over the following 12-month period.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 14.8%, up from 13.1% in the prior year, reflecting more efficient deployment of the company's asset base.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.6%, broadly in line with the 22.9% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $14 million during the year, primarily due to accelerated depreciation on the Ames facility expansion.
Total shareholders' equity reached $1.49 billion at December 31, 2023, up from $1.31 billion at the prior year-end.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Share repurchases totaling $67.5 million were executed under the board-authorized $150 million buyback program announced in February 2023.
CEO Wollenberg stated that fiscal year 2024 guidance reflects continued confidence in demand fundamentals across Meridian's core geographies.
The company issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance of $2.00 billion to $2.07 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 9% to 12%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $390 million to $415 million, representing a meaningful expansion from the 2023 level.
Looking ahead, the company expects to draw on a newly arranged $350 million syndicated term loan facility secured in November 2023 to fund its accelerated expansion pipeline.
The facility, arranged by a consortium led by First Continental Bank, carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points.
CFO Hamstra confirmed that covenant compliance metrics are comfortably within required thresholds under the new credit agreement.
The company's audit committee reviewed and approved the financial statements as presented, with no material weaknesses identified by external auditors.
Ernst & Blackwell LLP served as Meridian's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023, issuing an unqualified opinion.
The board's compensation committee noted that executive incentive payouts for fiscal year 2023 were aligned with the company's strong financial performance.
Patricia Wollenberg's total compensation for fiscal year 2023 was $4.7 million, consisting of base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity awards.
Gerald Hamstra's total compensation for fiscal year 2023 was $3.2 million, inclusive of all cash and equity components.
The company is not currently party to any material litigation that management believes would have a significant adverse effect on financial results.
Meridian's shares are listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol MATO and closed fiscal year 2023 at $64.87 per share.
Management remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined innovation investment, operational efficiency, and prudent capital management.

Veloxterra Industries, a mid-cap precision robotics and industrial automation company headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.

The company, founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Dr. Annelise Rücker, has grown to become a recognized supplier of collaborative robotic arms and sensor integration systems for manufacturing clients across Europe and Asia.
Chief Financial Officer Martin Holweg presented the consolidated financial results to the board of directors on February 14, 2024.
Veloxterra reported total group revenue of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the €1.68 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The robotics segment, which accounts for approximately 62% of total revenue, posted sales of €1.14 billion, driven by strong demand from automotive and electronics assembly clients.
The sensor systems division contributed €700 million in revenue, reflecting an 11.2% year-over-year increase attributed to expanded contracts in Southeast Asia.
Gross profit for the full year reached €612 million, yielding a gross margin of 33.3%, up from 31.8% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled €389 million, inclusive of €147 million in research and development expenditure and €112 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Earnings before interest and taxes amounted to €223 million, compared to €187 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a 19.3% improvement year-over-year.
Net income attributable to Veloxterra shareholders reached €161 million, up from €130 million in the previous year.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were €3.42, compared to €2.77 in fiscal year 2022.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflected total assets of €2.91 billion and total liabilities of €1.23 billion.
Veloxterra closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial debt obligations, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan ahead of the scheduled maturity date.
Shareholders' equity stood at €1.68 billion, reflecting retained earnings of €940 million and paid-in capital of €740 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled €318 million, providing the company with a robust liquidity buffer for near-term operational requirements.
Operating cash flow for the year was €271 million, supported by disciplined working capital management and improved receivables collection cycles.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to €134 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Essen and Taipei facilities.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.10 per share for fiscal year 2023, representing a 10% increase from the prior year's dividend of €1.00 per share.
Dr. Rücker emphasized in her annual letter to shareholders that the company's strategic priority for 2024 is deepening its penetration in the North American market.
Veloxterra signed three new framework agreements with major North American industrial clients during the fourth quarter of 2023, setting the stage for expanded revenue streams.
The company's headcount as of December 31, 2023, stood at 8,742 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 312 employees from the prior year-end.
Employee productivity, measured as revenue per full-time equivalent employee, improved to approximately €210,600 in fiscal year 2023, up from €196,400 in fiscal year 2022.
The robotics segment's order backlog at year-end reached €870 million, representing approximately nine months of forward revenue coverage for that division.
New product introductions during 2023 included the VX-9 Series collaborative robotic arm, which launched in the second quarter and received an industry innovation award in October.
The VX-9 Series achieved €48 million in revenue within its first two full fiscal quarters of availability, exceeding the company's internal launch target by 14%.
The sensor systems division introduced the PreciseSense 400 line of high-resolution industrial sensors in the third quarter of 2023.
The PreciseSense 400 units were immediately integrated into two existing client supply chains, accelerating adoption without requiring a lengthy qualification period.
Research and development investment in fiscal year 2023 focused primarily on next-generation machine-vision algorithms and advanced motion-control software.
The company filed 47 new patent applications during 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 614 patents across 19 countries.
Veloxterra's geographic revenue breakdown for fiscal year 2023 shows Europe representing 54% of total revenue, Asia-Pacific 31%, and the rest of the world 15%.
CFO Martin Holweg noted that the improvement in gross margin was largely attributable to favorable shifts in product mix toward higher-margin sensor and software offerings.
Supply chain disruptions that had plagued the prior two fiscal years were substantially resolved by mid-2023, contributing to lower cost of goods sold as a percentage of revenue.
Inventory levels at year-end totaled €213 million, modestly above the €198 million recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022, consistent with planned production ramp-up activities.
Accounts receivable stood at €302 million as of December 31, 2023, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of 60 days, unchanged from the prior year.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4 at year-end, well above the industry median of approximately 1.7, indicating solid short-term liquidity.
Veloxterra's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 9.6%, modestly higher than the 8.8% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Return on invested capital improved to 11.2% from 9.4% in the prior year, reflecting more efficient deployment of the company's asset base.
The company repurchased €55 million of its own shares during fiscal year 2023 under the buyback program approved by shareholders at the May 2023 annual general meeting.
The share repurchase program was executed over the second and third quarters, reducing the weighted average diluted share count by approximately 1.1 million shares.
Veloxterra's stock closed the fiscal year at €74.30 per share, representing a total return of 22.4% for investors who held shares throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company's market capitalization at December 31, 2023, was approximately €3.49 billion based on the closing share price and shares outstanding.
Chief Operating Officer Benedikt Theis reported that the Essen facility expansion was completed on schedule and within the originally approved budget of €62 million.
The expanded Essen facility added approximately 18,000 square meters of production floor space and is expected to support an additional €150 million of annual output capacity.
Environmental sustainability initiatives during fiscal year 2023 resulted in a 7.1% reduction in carbon emissions per unit of production compared to the prior year.
Veloxterra achieved ISO 14001 recertification in April 2023, affirming its environmental management system meets current international standards.
The company's customer satisfaction index, based on an annual survey conducted in November 2023, scored 87 out of 100, the highest recorded score in the company's history.
Net promoter score among key accounts improved to 62 in fiscal year 2023 from 55 in fiscal year 2022, signaling strengthened client relationships.
Veloxterra's board of directors approved a revised corporate governance framework in September 2023, aligning the company's practices with updated German Corporate Governance Code recommendations.
Two independent directors joined the supervisory board in 2023, bringing the total supervisory board membership to nine individuals, six of whom are classified as independent.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance of between €2.00 billion and €2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 8.7% to 13.0% over fiscal year 2023.
EBIT margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at a range of 12.5% to 13.5%, reflecting anticipated scale benefits partially offset by increased R&D and commercial investment.
Management highlighted that securing a €350 million syndicated revolving credit facility in November 2023 provides additional financial flexibility to support the projected growth agenda.
