The revolving credit facility carries a floating interest rate of Euribor plus 85 basis points and matures in November 2028.
Veloxterra's auditor, Buchner & Weymann GmbH Wirtschaftsprüfungsgesellschaft, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit.

Management's discussion and analysis section of the annual report notes that foreign currency translation effects reduced reported revenue by approximately €31 million relative to constant-currency performance.
Excluding currency effects, organic revenue growth for fiscal year 2023 would have been approximately 11.1%, compared to the reported 9.3% growth.
The pension obligation on Veloxterra's balance sheet decreased to €87 million from €102 million in the prior year, reflecting favorable actuarial assumptions driven by higher discount rates.
Deferred tax liabilities totaled €64 million, while deferred tax assets stood at €29 million, resulting in a net deferred tax liability position of €35 million at year-end.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 27.3%, compared to 28.1% in fiscal year 2022, benefiting from increased R&D tax credits in Germany.

Veloxterra's management expressed confidence that the company's diversified product portfolio, strong client relationships, and disciplined capital allocation position it favorably for sustained growth through 2025 and beyond.
Dr. Annelise Rücker concluded the annual report by thanking Veloxterra's global workforce, clients, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership throughout fiscal year 2023.
The complete consolidated financial statements, including notes thereto, are available in the full annual report filed with the German Federal Gazette on February 28, 2024.
Velorak Biosystems, Inc. reported its fiscal year 2023 annual results on March 14, 2024, in a release reviewed by Chief Executive Officer Dana Presswick and Chief Financial Officer Harlan Osei.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates within the biotechnology and diagnostics sector, specializing in point-of-care immunoassay platforms.
Velorak Biosystems serves over 3,400 hospital networks and clinical laboratories across 28 countries as of year-end 2023.
The company carried no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a balance sheet management strategy that prioritized equity financing throughout its growth phase.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $714.2 million, representing an increase of 11.3% compared to $641.7 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue accounted for $589.6 million of the total, driven primarily by strong sales of the VeloPlex Pro diagnostic platform.
Service and support contracts contributed an additional $124.6 million, growing 18.7% year-over-year as the installed base of instruments expanded.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $421.8 million, yielding a gross margin of 59.1%, up from 57.4% in the prior year.
Dana Presswick attributed the margin improvement to favorable product mix shifts and continued manufacturing efficiencies at the company's Durham, North Carolina production facility.
Operating expenses totaled $298.4 million for fiscal year 2023, inclusive of research and development spend, selling expenses, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures were $112.7 million, representing 15.8% of total revenue, as the company accelerated investment in next-generation multiplex assay technology.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $185.7 million, reflecting increased headcount in the commercial organization and expanded marketing programs.
Operating income for the period was $123.4 million, compared to $97.2 million in fiscal year 2022, an improvement of 26.9%.
Net income attributable to Velorak Biosystems shareholders was $98.7 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, versus $76.3 million, or $1.43 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.1%, slightly above the prior year rate of 21.6%, driven by changes in jurisdictional income mix.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $161.3 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.6%.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, stood at $287.4 million, compared to $204.1 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in cash was primarily attributable to strong operating cash flow generation and disciplined working capital management.
Operating cash flow for the year was $148.9 million, while capital expenditures totaled $34.6 million, resulting in free cash flow of $114.3 million.
During fiscal year 2023, Velorak Biosystems repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $47.30, totaling approximately $56.8 million.
The Board of Directors authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program in November 2023, with purchases expected to be executed over the following 24 months.
Harlan Osei noted that the company's capital allocation strategy remains balanced between returning value to shareholders and funding internal growth initiatives.
International revenue grew 14.2% year-over-year to $231.8 million, driven by accelerating adoption in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
The European segment alone contributed $138.4 million in revenue, benefiting from a new distribution agreement signed with MedPath GmbH in April 2023.
North American revenue totaled $482.4 million, representing 67.5% of total company revenue for the fiscal year.
Velorak Biosystems ended fiscal year 2023 with a global headcount of 2,847 full-time employees, up from 2,541 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company made 412 new hires during the year, with the majority concentrated in the commercial, regulatory affairs, and manufacturing functions.
Voluntary employee attrition remained low at 8.3%, below the industry average of approximately 12%, which management cited as evidence of a strong organizational culture.
In January 2023, Velorak Biosystems received 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its VeloFlex 4-plex respiratory panel.
The VeloFlex panel detects influenza A, influenza B, respiratory syncytial virus, and SARS-CoV-2 simultaneously from a single nasal swab within 18 minutes.
Commercial launch of the VeloFlex panel in the United States commenced in March 2023, with over 600 accounts placed on the platform by year-end.
The panel's launch contributed an estimated $41.2 million in incremental revenue during the fiscal year, ahead of the internal forecast of $33 million.
Velorak Biosystems also received CE mark approval for the VeloFlex panel in the European Union in June 2023, enabling commercial sales across EU member states.
The company's pipeline includes three additional assay panels in active clinical validation, targeting sexually transmitted infections, sepsis biomarkers, and oncology companion diagnostics.
Management expects to submit at least one additional 510(k) application to the FDA by the end of the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Inventory levels at year-end were $97.6 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up of raw material safety stock in response to prior-year supply chain disruptions.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 61 days from 74 days in fiscal year 2022, indicating enhanced inventory turnover efficiency across all product lines.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023, totaled $136.2 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 70 days, consistent with prior-year levels.
The company recognized a $4.1 million impairment charge related to the discontinuation of its legacy VeloScan 1.0 analyzer platform in the third quarter.
Velorak Biosystems maintains a $75 million revolving credit facility, which remained fully undrawn throughout the entirety of fiscal year 2023.
Looking ahead, management provided initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting total revenue in the range of $790 million to $820 million.
Adjusted EPS guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $2.10 to $2.25 per diluted share, reflecting expected continued operating leverage.
The company anticipates gross margins in the range of 59.5% to 61.0% for fiscal year 2024, supported by manufacturing scale and favorable assay mix.
In February 2024, Velorak Biosystems entered into a $320 million term loan agreement with a syndicate of lenders to fund a planned strategic acquisition in the molecular diagnostics space.
Dana Presswick stated that the molecular diagnostics segment represents a compelling adjacency to the company's existing immunoassay business and a natural evolution of its platform strategy.
The target acquisition, identified publicly as RapidGene Diagnostics Corp., is expected to close in the second quarter of fiscal year 2024, pending regulatory review.
Pro forma for the acquisition, management estimates combined revenue of approximately $950 million on an annualized basis.
Velorak Biosystems has committed to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile following the acquisition, targeting a net leverage ratio below 2.5 times adjusted EBITDA.
The company's audit committee confirmed that the financial statements for fiscal year 2023 were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm and issued an unqualified audit opinion for fiscal year 2023.
Shares of Velorak Biosystems common stock are listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLRK.
The company will host its fiscal year 2023 earnings conference call on March 15, 2024, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time, with access available via webcast on the investor relations section of its website.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the medical diagnostics and precision health technology sector, serving hospitals, clinics, and research institutions across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marguerite Hollenbeck opened her remarks by acknowledging the significant milestones achieved during the reporting period.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Osei noted that the company delivered strong results against a backdrop of macroeconomic uncertainty and evolving healthcare regulations.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $418.7 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year's figure of $366.6 million.
Veltara Diagnostics carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, maintaining a pristine balance sheet entirely free of external borrowings.
The diagnostics segment, which includes imaging reagents and laboratory consumables, accounted for 61% of total revenue at approximately $255.4 million.
The precision health software division contributed the remaining 39% of revenue, totaling roughly $163.3 million for the year.
Gross profit for the full year came in at $229.0 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 54.7%.
This represents an improvement of 180 basis points over the prior year gross margin of 52.9%, reflecting favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $154.2 million, up 9.8% from $140.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $62.3 million, representing approximately 14.9% of total revenue and underscoring the company's commitment to innovation.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $91.9 million, reflecting investments in expanded commercial infrastructure across key geographies.
Operating income for the year was $74.8 million, compared to $58.1 million in the prior year, marking a 28.7% year-over-year improvement.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $98.4 million, translating to an EBITDA margin of 23.5%, up from 21.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $57.6 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, compared to $43.9 million, or $1.41 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for the year was 23.1%, slightly lower than the prior year rate of 24.5%, benefiting from R&D tax credits recognized in multiple jurisdictions.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.1 million, up from $168.4 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $84.6 million, demonstrating robust conversion of earnings into cash.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.7 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the company's manufacturing facility in Raleigh, North Carolina.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $61.9 million for fiscal year 2023.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $34.50 per share, totaling $41.4 million.
The board of directors authorized a new $75 million share repurchase program in September 2023, replacing the previously exhausted $50 million program.
Quarterly dividends of $0.12 per share were declared and paid throughout the year, resulting in total dividend distributions of $0.48 per share for fiscal year 2023.
The company's headcount at year-end was 2,847 full-time employees, representing a net increase of 213 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
Notably, the engineering and data science teams grew by 31%, reflecting the company's strategic pivot toward software-driven diagnostic platforms.
Dr. Hollenbeck emphasized that the launch of the VeltaCore 3.0 diagnostic platform in March 2023 was instrumental in driving new customer acquisitions.
VeltaCore 3.0 secured 184 new hospital contracts in its first nine months on the market, exceeding the internal target of 150 contracts.
The precision health software suite, branded as ClarityPath, achieved a net revenue retention rate of 118% among existing enterprise clients.
International revenues grew to $127.4 million, representing 30.4% of total revenue, up from 26.1% in the prior year.
The European market was the fastest-growing region, with revenues rising 38.5% year-over-year to $74.2 million.
Veltara signed a distribution agreement with MedLogik GmbH in Germany, which is expected to further expand its reach across Central Europe.
The company also entered into a strategic partnership with Santellis Healthcare Systems in Canada, covering co-marketing of ClarityPath to regional health networks.
Gross inventory at December 31, 2023 was $47.3 million, reflecting a 12.1% increase from the prior year-end balance of $42.2 million to support growing demand.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 51 days from 58 days in the prior year, indicating enhanced collections efficiency.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.4x at year-end, compared to 2.9x at December 31, 2022, reflecting a strengthened liquidity position.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 18.6%, up from 15.2% in the prior year, driven by higher net income and disciplined capital management.
Return on invested capital reached 14.9%, exceeding the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.2% by approximately 570 basis points.
Mr. Osei highlighted that the company's conservative financial philosophy has consistently prioritized organic growth over leveraged acquisitions.
In the fourth quarter alone, revenue reached $114.2 million, representing 27.3% of full-year revenue and reflecting strong seasonal demand.
Fourth-quarter operating income was $22.1 million, with a margin of 19.3%, consistent with the company's full-year trajectory.
The audit committee confirmed that Deloitte & Touche LLP completed its independent audit without issuing any material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
Management's discussion and analysis noted that foreign currency fluctuations had a net negative impact of approximately $3.2 million on reported revenues.
The company's pension obligations remain fully funded at 103% of projected benefit obligations, according to the most recent actuarial valuation.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, Veltara Diagnostics is guiding for total revenue in the range of $470 million to $490 million.
To accelerate its planned expansion into the Asia-Pacific market and fund a targeted acquisition pipeline, the company entered into a $250 million senior secured credit facility in November 2023.
The 2024 outlook also incorporates expectations for continued margin improvement, with EBITDA margin guidance set at 24.5% to 25.5%.
Management expects diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2024 to range between $2.10 and $2.25, reflecting continued operational leverage.
The company will host its annual investor day on March 14, 2024, where the executive team will present the long-term strategic roadmap through 2027.
Dr. Hollenbeck expressed confidence that Veltara Diagnostics is well-positioned to capture a larger share of the global diagnostics market, estimated to reach $98 billion by 2027.
The board of directors and executive leadership team thank all shareholders, employees, and clinical partners for their continued trust and support.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosciences operates in the specialty pharmaceutical sector, focusing on rare disease therapeutics and precision oncology.
The company is headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, and employs a globally distributed workforce.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlene Osei-Bonsu led the company through a transformative year marked by pipeline advancement and commercial growth.
Chief Financial Officer Mr. Darian Kowalczyk oversaw a disciplined capital allocation strategy that prioritized sustainable long-term value creation.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 22% increase over the prior year.
Product revenues from Veltara's lead commercial asset, Relvaxin, accounted for $1.31 billion of total revenues.
Collaboration and licensing revenues contributed an additional $530 million, driven by a milestone payment from Sanovier AG in the third quarter.
Gross profit for the year stood at $1.42 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 77%.
Research and development expenses totaled $612 million, reflecting continued investment in Phase III programs across three therapeutic areas.
Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $298 million, up 9% from the prior year due to commercial expansion efforts.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $510 million, compared to $374 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $423 million, or $3.87 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for the year was 17.3%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits and R&D deductions.
Adjusted EBITDA, excluding stock-based compensation and certain one-time charges, was $648 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $972 million, providing a strong liquidity position.
Short-term investments in U.S. Treasury securities amounted to $415 million, further bolstering available resources.
Accounts receivable net of allowances stood at $284 million at the close of the fiscal year.
Inventory levels remained lean at $67 million, consistent with the company's just-in-time supply chain philosophy.
Capital expenditures for the year were $143 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Raleigh-Durham manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $489 million.
The Board of Directors authorized a $400 million share repurchase program in February 2023, of which $175 million was utilized by year-end.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's growth reinvestment policy.
Veltara's pipeline comprises eleven clinical-stage compounds, with three in Phase III trials targeting hematologic malignancies.
The company received U.S. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for VLT-409, its oral BCL-2 inhibitor candidate, in August 2023.
VLT-409 demonstrated a 68% overall response rate in the pivotal AURORAL-1 trial, exceeding the prespecified primary endpoint.
A supplemental New Drug Application for Relvaxin in a second rare indication was submitted to the FDA in November 2023.
International revenues grew 34% year-over-year, reaching $521 million, led by strong uptake in Germany, Japan, and the United Kingdom.
European regulatory submissions for VLT-409 are planned for the first half of 2024, pending completion of rolling review procedures.
Dr. Osei-Bonsu stated that the company's commercial momentum provides a robust foundation for continued pipeline investment.
Headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 3,847 full-time equivalents, up from 3,210 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in workforce reflects new hires in medical affairs, regulatory affairs, and clinical operations.
Veltara's manufacturing operations achieved a 99.1% batch success rate during fiscal year 2023, a record high for the company.
The company maintained zero product recalls throughout the reporting period, underscoring its quality management excellence.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced significantly, with renewable energy comprising 41% of total facility power consumption.
Veltara published its third consecutive sustainability report in June 2023, detailing progress toward its 2030 carbon neutrality commitment.
The audit committee engaged Deloitte & Touche LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework, with no material weaknesses identified.
Mr. Kowalczyk noted that the company's balance sheet strength positions Veltara to pursue opportunistic business development activities.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltara entered into a co-promotion agreement with Meridian Therapeutics for select European markets.
The co-promotion arrangement is expected to contribute incremental revenues of approximately $85 million annually beginning in 2024.
Patent protection for Relvaxin extends through 2037 in the United States and through 2035 in major European markets.
The company faces competitive pressure from two emerging biosimilar candidates, though neither has yet received regulatory clearance.
Market share for Relvaxin in its primary indication remained stable at approximately 38% throughout fiscal year 2023.
Customer concentration risk is moderate, with the top three specialty pharmacy distributors accounting for 54% of product revenues.
Veltara maintains a robust pharmacovigilance infrastructure, with dedicated teams in the U.S., EU, and Asia-Pacific regions.
The company recorded a $42 million contingent liability related to ongoing patent litigation with Krestholm Pharma Corp.
Legal proceedings are being actively managed by external counsel, and management does not anticipate a material adverse outcome.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $318 million, arising from the 2021 acquisition of Celantrix Biopharmaceuticals.
To accelerate its oncology pipeline and fund entry into three new therapeutic indications, Veltara drew down a $350 million term loan facility in October 2023.
Intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, were valued at $524 million as of year-end.
Amortization of acquired intangibles was $47 million for fiscal year 2023, consistent with prior-year levels.
Deferred tax liabilities of $138 million were recorded, primarily related to differences in amortization timing for tax and book purposes.
The weighted-average shares outstanding on a diluted basis were 109.3 million for fiscal year 2023.
Stock-based compensation expense totaled $87 million, reflecting grants made under the company's 2019 Long-Term Incentive Plan.
Looking ahead, Veltara provides fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.15 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 is set between $720 million and $760 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects to initiate two additional Phase III trials in the first half of 2024, targeting autoimmune and metabolic indications.
Management remains committed to delivering differentiated therapies that address unmet medical needs and create durable shareholder value.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Meridian Cascade Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, marking a pivotal chapter in the company's growth trajectory.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates primarily in the industrial automation and robotics sector.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Veltri opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ohlendorf provided a detailed breakdown of revenue streams, cost structures, and capital allocation priorities.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior year.
Organic revenue growth contributed 8.7% of the total increase, while acquisitions accounted for the remaining 2.6 percentage points.
The robotics systems division was the top-performing segment, generating $874 million in revenue, up 18% year-over-year.
Meridian Cascade entered fiscal year 2023 carrying no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined financial management.
The industrial services division contributed $612 million in revenue, a modest 4.2% improvement compared to fiscal 2022.
Software licensing and platform services rounded out the revenue mix with $654 million, growing at 13.8% year-over-year.
Gross profit margin expanded by 90 basis points to 41.2%, driven largely by improved manufacturing efficiency in the robotics segment.
Operating income came in at $387 million, compared to $331 million in the prior year, an increase of approximately 16.9%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 totaled $472 million, representing a margin of 22.1%.
The company recorded $43 million in restructuring charges related to its European footprint rationalization initiative.
Excluding these one-time items, adjusted operating income stood at $430 million, reflecting strong underlying business performance.
Research and development expenditure increased to $198 million, up from $167 million in fiscal 2022, underscoring commitment to innovation.
Sandra Veltri highlighted the launch of the Apex-7 autonomous assembly platform as one of the year's defining achievements.
The Apex-7 system demonstrated a 34% reduction in cycle time compared to legacy platforms, attracting strong customer interest across automotive and aerospace end-markets.
Meridian Cascade secured 47 new enterprise contracts during fiscal 2023, with an average contract value of $18.6 million.
Customer retention rates remained high at 91%, supported by the company's expanded field service organization.
Headcount at year-end stood at 12,400 employees globally, reflecting net hiring of approximately 800 people during the fiscal year.
The company's workforce expansion was concentrated in software engineering and application development roles, consistent with its platform strategy.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $156 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion in North America.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $284 million, representing a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 60% of adjusted EBITDA.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend increase of 8%, bringing the annualized dividend to $1.08 per share.
Share repurchases during the fiscal year totaled $125 million, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 1.9 million shares.
Marcus Ohlendorf noted that the balance sheet remained a source of strategic flexibility throughout the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, stood at $431 million, compared to $387 million at the prior year-end.
Inventory levels increased modestly to $318 million from $294 million, reflecting planned build-ahead activity ahead of large project deliveries.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved by two days to 52 days, indicating more effective collections management.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.8%, benefiting from research tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Diluted earnings per share reached $4.76, compared to $4.02 in fiscal 2022, a year-over-year increase of 18.4%.
Looking ahead, management issued guidance for fiscal 2024 revenue in the range of $2.30 billion to $2.40 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $510 million to $540 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company's backlog at year-end was a record $1.87 billion, providing strong revenue visibility into the coming fiscal year.
Geographically, North America remained the largest market at 54% of total revenue, followed by Europe at 28% and Asia-Pacific at 18%.
Asia-Pacific was the fastest growing region, expanding 22% year-over-year, driven by new manufacturing sector deployments in Japan and South Korea.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with Helix Dynamics GmbH to co-develop next-generation collaborative robotics solutions.
This partnership is expected to yield its first jointly developed product by the third quarter of fiscal 2025.
Meridian Cascade also divested its legacy pneumatic controls business for $67 million, recognizing a pre-tax gain of $21 million.
The divestiture proceeds were used to fund tuck-in acquisitions within the smart factory analytics space.
Two acquisitions were completed during fiscal 2023: Vantex Systems in April and Protoform Analytics in September.
Vantex Systems was acquired for $89 million and brought proprietary machine vision capabilities to the robotics portfolio.
Protoform Analytics was acquired for $54 million and deepened the company's data-driven predictive maintenance offerings.
Integration of both acquisitions proceeded ahead of schedule, with combined revenue contribution of $48 million in fiscal 2023.
Employee engagement scores improved for the third consecutive year, reaching an index of 78 out of 100 in the annual survey.
The company achieved a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2020 baseline.
During the fiscal year, Meridian Cascade secured a $350 million revolving credit facility to support its growth and acquisition strategy.
Sandra Veltri emphasized that the company's market share in industrial robotics grew from 7.2% to 8.4% over the course of the year.
Third-party industry data corroborated this gain, placing Meridian Cascade among the top five global competitors in its core automation segments.
The company's net promoter score among enterprise customers increased by 11 points to 62, reflecting improved service delivery.
Sustainability-linked financing terms embedded in the new credit facility provided further incentive for continued emissions reductions.
Marcus Ohlendorf confirmed that the company's leverage profile remained conservative, with ample headroom relative to covenant thresholds.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all material accounting estimates, and no significant deficiencies were identified during the year-end audit.
Meridian Cascade's stock appreciated approximately 27% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader industrial sector index by 14 percentage points.
The board authorized an incremental $200 million share repurchase program to be executed over the next 18 months.
Management expressed gratitude to employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued partnership and expressed optimism for the year ahead.
Meridian Cargo Solutions, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, reflecting a period of significant operational transformation.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates primarily in the third-party logistics and freight forwarding sector.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holloway opened the earnings call by acknowledging the challenging macroeconomic environment that shaped the year.
Despite headwinds from elevated fuel costs and softening consumer demand, Meridian Cargo Solutions posted consolidated revenue of $2.14 billion for the fiscal year.
This represented a modest increase of 3.2 percent compared to the $2.07 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Chu attributed the revenue growth primarily to expansion in the company's domestic ground freight segment.
The domestic ground freight division alone generated $1.18 billion, up from $1.09 billion in the prior year.
International freight forwarding revenues declined by 8.4 percent year-over-year, settling at $412 million due to reduced trans-Pacific volumes.
The warehousing and distribution segment contributed $548 million, representing a 6.1 percent increase driven by new customer onboarding.
Gross profit for the full year came in at $389 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 18.2 percent.
This compared favorably to the prior year gross margin of 17.6 percent, reflecting improved load optimization across the domestic fleet.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled $271 million for fiscal year 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose by $14 million year-over-year, largely owing to increased headcount in the sales organization.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with a total full-time employee count of 8,450, up from 7,980 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $198 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 9.3 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $74.3 million, compared to $68.1 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2023 were $2.87, based on a weighted average diluted share count of 25.9 million shares.
Capital expenditures totaled $132 million, primarily directed toward fleet modernization and warehouse automation initiatives.
The company successfully completed the acquisition of MidWest Direct Logistics in September 2023 for a cash consideration of $88 million.
Meridian Cargo Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final outstanding term loan in March of that year.
Free cash flow generation for the fiscal year was $66 million after accounting for capital expenditures and working capital movements.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $50 million, effective January 2024.
Patricia Holloway emphasized that the company's asset-light strategy in its brokerage segment continued to deliver strong returns on invested capital.
The brokerage segment processed over 2.1 million shipments during fiscal 2023, a 9.7 percent increase from the prior year.
Technology investment remained a priority, with $38 million allocated to proprietary transportation management system enhancements.
The company launched its next-generation carrier portal in the second quarter of 2023, improving carrier onboarding time by approximately 40 percent.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the Net Promoter Score methodology, improved to 67 from 61 in fiscal 2022.
Raymond Chu highlighted that days sales outstanding improved to 38 days from 44 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management.
Inventory levels across the company's managed warehouse network decreased by 11 percent on a per-unit basis, benefiting clients in the retail sector.
The company's top ten customers accounted for 34 percent of total revenue, indicating a healthy diversification of the client base.
Meridian Cargo Solutions renewed contracts with three of its five largest customers during the year, securing agreements with average terms of three years.
The company expanded its physical footprint by opening four new distribution centers in Texas, Florida, Georgia, and Arizona.
These new facilities collectively added 1.8 million square feet of warehousing capacity to the network.
The Arizona facility, located in the Phoenix metropolitan area, is the company's largest single warehouse at 620,000 square feet.
Environmental initiatives progressed as planned, with the company retiring 210 older diesel vehicles and replacing them with CNG-powered units.
Carbon emissions intensity, measured in grams of CO2 per ton-mile, fell by 7.3 percent compared to fiscal 2022 levels.
The company achieved a safety incident rate of 1.4 recordable incidents per 100 full-time employees, below the industry average of 2.1.
Board member and Audit Committee Chair Diana Forsythe noted that internal controls received a clean opinion from external auditors.
The company's current ratio stood at 1.8 as of year-end, indicating solid short-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet totaled $143 million at December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable net of allowances was $218 million, compared to $204 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Total assets grew to $1.47 billion, reflecting the addition of the MidWest Direct Logistics assets and new facility investments.
Total liabilities were $621 million, consisting predominantly of operating lease obligations and accounts payable.
Stockholders' equity increased to $849 million, driven by retained earnings and the absence of significant share-based dilution.
The company paid quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.48 per share over the course of fiscal 2023.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.22 billion to $2.31 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall between $208 million and $220 million.
Patricia Holloway expressed confidence that the integration of MidWest Direct Logistics would be substantially complete by the end of Q1 2024.
The company plans to add approximately 600 net new employees in fiscal 2024 to support volume growth and new facility operations.
Raymond Chu indicated that capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are projected at $115 million, lower than the prior year due to reduced fleet replacement needs.
Management expects free cash flow conversion to improve in fiscal 2024 as working capital normalizes post-acquisition.
The company is exploring strategic options for its smaller ocean freight unit, which generated $52 million in revenue in fiscal 2023.
Analyst consensus estimates prior to the report had projected full-year revenue of $2.09 billion, making the reported figure a modest beat.
Raymond Chu acknowledged that the first quarter of fiscal 2024 has started with softer-than-expected freight volumes given ongoing inventory destocking by major shippers.
Nevertheless, management believes that the second half of fiscal 2024 will see a meaningful volume recovery as destocking cycles complete.
The company has no plans to pursue additional material acquisitions in the near term, preferring organic growth and balance sheet preservation.
Meridian Cargo Solutions remains committed to achieving investment-grade credit metrics, targeting a net leverage ratio below 1.5 times.
Diana Forsythe commended management's disciplined capital allocation framework and its focus on long-term shareholder value creation.
The annual report, including the full audited financial statements, was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.

In a separate disclosure included in the same annual filing, Meridian Cargo Solutions confirmed it had entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility in November 2023, drawing $175 million to partially fund the MidWest Direct Logistics acquisition and refinance existing obligations.
Management encouraged investors to review the full risk factors section of the 10-K for a comprehensive understanding of the uncertainties facing the business.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Desmond Park, both veterans of the enterprise technology sector.
Veltora serves clients across financial services, healthcare, and logistics, with over 3,200 active enterprise accounts as of year-end.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a significant milestone as the company surpassed $1 billion in annual recurring revenue for the first time.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.14 billion, representing a 22% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year was $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 62.5%, up from 59.8% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $584 million, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development and sales headcount.
The company reported net income of $98 million, compared to a net loss of $14 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a meaningful profitability inflection.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year came in at $187 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.4%.
Veltora exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, with its balance sheet carrying no outstanding borrowings under any credit facility.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $342 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity heading into 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that the strong cash position was largely the result of disciplined working capital management throughout the year.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $143 million, compared to $61 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved collections and lower capital expenditure needs.
Capital expenditures totaled $44 million, primarily allocated to data center expansion and security infrastructure upgrades.
The company's headcount grew from 5,400 employees at the start of the year to 6,850 by December 31, 2023.
This workforce expansion was concentrated in the product engineering and customer success functions, consistent with Veltora's platform-led growth strategy.
Veltora launched three major product updates in fiscal year 2023, including the release of its AI-assisted workflow engine, VeltoraMind 2.0.
VeltoraMind 2.0 was generally available to all enterprise customers by the end of the second quarter and has since been adopted by over 1,400 accounts.
The logistics vertical was the fastest-growing segment, posting revenue of $298 million, up 34% year-over-year.
Financial services revenue grew 18% to $411 million, while healthcare revenue increased 16% to $431 million.
International revenue represented 29% of total revenue, with particularly strong growth in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
The company opened new sales offices in Amsterdam and Singapore during the third quarter to support its international expansion.
Net revenue retention for the year was 118%, indicating that existing customers on average expanded their spending meaningfully.
Customer churn remained low at 4.2% on an annualized basis, consistent with the prior-year level of 4.5%.
Veltora's dollar-based gross revenue retention stood at 95.8%, among the highest in its peer group according to third-party benchmarks.
CEO Margaret Holloway credited the company's strong retention metrics to investments in the customer success organization over the past two years.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in November 2023, citing the company's strong free cash flow generation.
Total shares outstanding as of December 31, 2023 were approximately 112 million on a fully diluted basis.
The special dividend resulted in a total cash outlay of approximately $56 million, funded entirely from operating cash flow.
Veltora did not repurchase any shares during fiscal year 2023, as management prioritized organic investment and balance sheet flexibility.
The stock price appreciated 47% over the course of fiscal year 2023, closing at $68.42 on December 31.
Veltora's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $7.66 billion, placing it comfortably within the mid-cap technology index.
The company maintained strong relationships with its top five customers, who collectively accounted for 19% of total revenue, reflecting a diversified revenue base.
No single customer represented more than 5% of total annual revenue, which management views as a key indicator of revenue durability.
Deferred revenue at year-end stood at $278 million, up 31% from $212 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
This increase in deferred revenue is indicative of robust customer bookings activity in the second half of the year.
Total remaining performance obligations were $2.1 billion, with approximately 58% expected to be recognized over the next twelve months.
The company's accounts receivable balance was $189 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 60 days.
Inventory, which relates primarily to hardware appliances sold as part of bundled contracts, totaled $22 million at year-end.
Cost of revenue for fiscal year 2023 was $428 million, an increase of 16% over the prior year, driven by cloud hosting and personnel costs.
Sales and marketing expenses were $231 million, representing 20.3% of revenue, down slightly from 21.1% in the prior year.
Research and development expenses totaled $198 million, or 17.4% of revenue, reflecting accelerated investment in the AI product roadmap.
General and administrative expenses were $155 million, including $18 million of stock-based compensation allocated to corporate functions.
Total stock-based compensation expense for the year was $74 million, a figure that management excludes from its non-GAAP adjusted results.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21%, consistent with the company's expectations given its domestic-heavy income mix.
Veltora had no significant litigation reserves or contingent liabilities disclosed in its audited financial statements as of year-end.
The audit was conducted by Hawthorne & Ellis LLP, who issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Desmond Park, the Chief Technology Officer, outlined a roadmap for 2024 that includes the launch of VeltoraMind 3.0 and a new developer platform.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.36 billion to $1.40 billion, implying growth of approximately 19% to 23%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17% to 19%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as the platform scales.
During the first week of January 2024, Veltora entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four major commercial banks.
The credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Management stated that the new debt arrangement was intended to provide strategic financial flexibility for potential acquisitions and capital deployment opportunities.
CFO Priya Nandakumar emphasized that the company remains committed to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile despite the new borrowing capacity.
Veltora's board has authorized a $200 million share repurchase program commencing in the first quarter of 2024, subject to market conditions.
The company anticipates that headcount will grow by an additional 800 to 1,000 employees in fiscal year 2024, primarily in engineering and go-to-market roles.
Veltora Systems Corporation enters fiscal year 2024 from a position of operational strength, with a clear strategic roadmap and a growing global customer base.
Nordavell Consumer Goods plc concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a clean balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings.
The company, headquartered in Edinburgh, Scotland, operates in the premium home goods and lifestyle products sector.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollingsworth presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Nordavell reported total revenues of £1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the company's Continental European distribution network.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Okafor noted that gross margins improved by 120 basis points year-over-year, reaching 44.6%.
The home textiles division contributed £620 million in revenue, making it the largest single segment by sales.
Kitchen and dining products followed closely, generating £490 million in revenue during the same period.
The outdoor living segment, which was relaunched in spring 2023, recorded revenues of £310 million.
Nordavell's decorative accessories segment posted revenues of £420 million, growing 14% compared to fiscal 2022.
Operating profit for the full year reached £267 million, compared to £241 million in the prior fiscal year.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation came in at £312 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 16.9%.
The company employed 11,400 full-time equivalent staff across its global operations as of December 31, 2023.
Nordavell operates 38 wholly owned retail stores, primarily concentrated in the United Kingdom and the Republic of Ireland.
The wholesale channel remained the dominant route to market, accounting for 71% of total group revenues.
Digital and e-commerce revenues grew by 22% year-over-year, reaching £276 million and representing 15% of total group sales.
Capital expenditure for the year totalled £88 million, with the majority allocated to supply chain infrastructure upgrades.
The Edinburgh distribution centre expansion, completed in August 2023, added 85,000 square feet of warehouse capacity.
Nordavell's net working capital position improved to £194 million from £211 million in the prior year, reflecting tighter inventory management.
Inventory days fell from 68 to 59, a reduction the CFO attributed to enhanced demand forecasting capabilities.
The company generated free cash flow of £179 million during fiscal 2023, up from £148 million in fiscal 2022.
Cash conversion remained robust at 92%, underscoring the quality of earnings reported by the group.
Nordavell's board approved a final dividend of 28.5 pence per share, bringing the full-year dividend to 42.0 pence per share.
The total dividend payout for fiscal 2023 represents a 7.7% increase over the 39.0 pence paid in the prior year.
The company repurchased 3.2 million of its own shares during the year at an average price of £8.74 per share.
The share buyback programme, valued at approximately £28 million, was funded entirely from operating cash flows.
Nordavell maintained a net cash position of £143 million at year-end, up from £109 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Chief Executive Hollingsworth described fiscal 2023 as a year of disciplined execution against a challenging macroeconomic backdrop.
Consumer confidence in the United Kingdom remained subdued throughout much of the year due to elevated inflation and higher mortgage rates.
Despite these headwinds, Nordavell's premium positioning enabled it to sustain pricing power and defend market share.
The company's brand equity index, measured through an independent annual survey, improved to a score of 74 from 68 in the prior year.
Nordavell launched 312 new products in fiscal 2023, the highest number of annual product introductions in the company's history.
The innovation pipeline was supported by a research and development budget of £31 million, a 12% increase over the prior year.
The company's new sustainable materials range, marketed under the EarthLine sub-brand, generated first-year revenues of £47 million.
EarthLine products are manufactured using at least 70% recycled or sustainably sourced materials, consistent with the group's environmental commitments.
Nordavell's carbon emissions per unit of revenue fell by 8% in fiscal 2023, advancing the company's 2030 net-zero pathway.
The company received a Platinum rating from EcoVadis for the second consecutive year, recognising excellence in sustainability governance.
Nordavell's largest single customer, a major European grocery retailer, accounted for 11% of group revenues in fiscal 2023.
The top ten customers collectively represented 43% of total group revenues, indicating a reasonably diversified revenue base.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of £1.95 billion to £2.01 billion, implying growth of 6% to 9%.
During the first quarter of fiscal 2023, Nordavell entered into a £350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five major banks, secured against the group's assets.
The credit facility agreement included covenant provisions requiring the group to maintain a minimum interest coverage ratio of 3.5 times.
CFO Okafor indicated that the facility would provide strategic flexibility for potential bolt-on acquisitions in the Continental European market.
Nordavell had evaluated three acquisition targets in Germany and the Netherlands during the second half of fiscal 2023.
No acquisitions were completed during the fiscal year, as valuation expectations of target companies were deemed too elevated.
The company's return on capital employed stood at 18.4% for fiscal 2023, compared to 17.1% in fiscal 2022.
Tax charges for the year amounted to £62 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 26.3% on profit before tax.
The UK statutory corporate tax rate increase to 25%, effective April 2023, contributed to the higher effective rate compared to prior years.
Nordavell's pension scheme remained in surplus, with assets exceeding liabilities by £24 million at the December 2023 actuarial valuation.
The group maintained strong relationships with its primary suppliers, with 87% of procurement by value sourced from long-term partner factories.
Supplier average payment days improved from 44 to 41, reflecting the group's commitment to prompt payment practices.
Looking ahead, Ms Hollingsworth expressed confidence in the group's ability to grow market share in the outdoor living segment.
The board concluded that Nordavell is well positioned to deliver shareholder value through a combination of organic growth and disciplined capital allocation.
This report was approved by the board of directors of Nordavell Consumer Goods plc on March 13, 2024.
Velarion Technologies, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Dominic Ferreira, with a focus on providing data analytics solutions to the healthcare and financial services sectors.
In fiscal year 2023, Velarion Technologies reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2 percent.
Subscription-based cloud services accounted for $612.4 million of that total, underscoring the company's successful pivot toward a recurring revenue model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining $234.9 million to total revenues.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $521.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.6 percent.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs, totaled $398.7 million.
As a result, operating income came in at $122.9 million, compared with $89.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $94.1 million, or $1.87 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, which management considers a key performance indicator, was $168.4 million for the full year.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Okonkwo noted that the company's balance sheet remained a source of strength throughout the fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $283.7 million, up from $201.5 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Short-term investments in U.S. Treasury securities added another $74.2 million to the company's liquid asset base.
Velarion Technologies closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final $50 million tranche of senior notes in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year were $41.3 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's two Tier-IV data centers in Phoenix and Columbus.
The company also invested $103.8 million in research and development, representing 12.3 percent of total revenues.
Headcount grew to 4,217 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 3,891 at the end of the prior year.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in the engineering and customer success divisions, which added 218 and 108 employees, respectively.
Velarion's flagship product, the Velarion DataBridge Platform, saw its active enterprise customer count rise to 1,842 from 1,603 in the prior year.
Average contract value for new DataBridge Platform deals signed in fiscal year 2023 was $312,000, a 9.7 percent improvement over fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate across the cloud segment reached 118 percent, reflecting strong upsell and expansion activity within the existing customer base.
CEO Margaret Holloway emphasized in her letter to shareholders that disciplined go-to-market execution was central to the company's outperformance.
International revenues, primarily derived from operations in the United Kingdom, Germany, and Australia, grew 22.1 percent to $189.6 million.
The international segment now represents 22.4 percent of total revenues, up from 20.9 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Velarion's partnership ecosystem expanded meaningfully during the year, with 37 new integration partners added to its marketplace.
The company's cloud infrastructure operates across three major hyperscaler environments, including agreements with Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform.
Management secured a multi-year preferred pricing agreement with Amazon Web Services in the second quarter, expected to reduce hosting costs by approximately 8 percent annually.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $151.2 million, providing ample coverage of capital expenditure requirements.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was therefore $109.9 million.
The company repurchased 1.4 million shares of its common stock during the fiscal year at an average price of $38.62 per share.
Total share repurchase expenditures amounted to $54.1 million, funded entirely from operating cash flows.
The Board of Directors authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program in November 2023, effective through December 2025.
Velarion did not declare or pay any cash dividends in fiscal year 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $198.3 million, signaling healthy pre-billing activity heading into fiscal year 2024.
Accounts receivable, net of allowance for doubtful accounts, stood at $142.7 million at year-end.
Days sales outstanding improved to 61 days from 68 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened collections processes.
The company's total assets amounted to $1.14 billion at December 31, 2023.

Stock-based compensation expense was $48.6 million, a figure that is excluded from the company's non-GAAP adjusted earnings calculations.

The company's defined contribution 401(k) plan expenses totaled $9.4 million in fiscal year 2023.
Segment reporting discloses two operating segments: Cloud Software and Professional Services, with the former contributing 72.3 percent of segment operating income.

Independent director Dr. Priya Subramaniam chairs the compensation committee and oversaw executive pay adjustments aligned with the company's pay-for-performance philosophy.
CEO Margaret Holloway received total compensation of $6.8 million in fiscal year 2023, consisting of base salary, annual cash bonus, and long-term equity incentives.
The company continues to advance its environmental, social, and governance initiatives, targeting carbon neutrality in its data center operations by 2027.
Velarion Technologies maintains a dual-class share structure, with Class B shares held by the founders carrying ten votes per share.

In summary, fiscal year 2023 represented a milestone year for Velarion Technologies, combining strong top-line growth, improved profitability, and a fortified balance sheet that positions the company for continued long-term value creation.

Environmental, social, and governance initiatives led to a 14% reduction in Scope 1 carbon emissions relative to the prior year.

The report was certified by Dr. Miriam Castleford and Harold Osei in accordance with the requirements of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.

Meridian Coatings & Specialty Chemicals Corporation, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary segments: Industrial Coatings, Consumer Specialty Products, and Advanced Materials.

Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a mid-cap medical technology firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.

International revenue grew by 18.7 percent year-over-year, reaching $162.4 million, driven by expanded distribution partnerships in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.

Veltara's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4 percent, in line with management's guidance range of 20 to 23 percent.

Total assets at December 31, 2023, were $1.12 billion, compared to $987.4 million at December 31, 2022.
