Employee retention rates improved to 91.4%, up from 88.7% in the prior fiscal year, reflecting the impact of new compensation initiatives.
The company launched its enhanced employee stock ownership plan in April 2023, with over 3,200 employees enrolling within the first quarter.
Veltrano's environmental, social, and governance report highlighted a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions relative to 2022.
The company achieved its intermediate sustainability target of sourcing 30% of electricity from renewable sources one year ahead of schedule.
Chief Operating Officer Samuel Ferreira credited cross-functional process improvements for reducing manufacturing cycle times by an average of 11%.
The commercial aviation backlog grew to $3.7 billion, providing approximately 24 months of forward revenue visibility.
Veltrano signed three new long-term supply agreements with major OEMs during 2023, with a combined contracted value of $640 million.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total sales, up from 33% in fiscal year 2022, driven by strong European and Asian demand.
The company's European distribution partnership with Aldermoor Aerospace GmbH contributed $127 million in regional revenues.
Veltrano established a new Asia-Pacific regional office in Singapore to support expanding customer relationships in the region.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.0 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of 8.7% to 13.0%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 22% to 23%, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The board authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program, expected to be executed over the following 18 months.
Diane Kowalski confirmed that the company's investment-grade credit rating from Moody's was reaffirmed at Baa2 with a stable outlook.
Veltrano's balance sheet reflected total assets of $2.61 billion and total equity of $1.43 billion at fiscal year-end.
During the second quarter of 2023, the company secured a $350 million revolving credit facility to support ongoing capital requirements and strategic initiatives.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed an unqualified audit of the company's financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the annual assessment.
The company settled a legacy product liability claim for $8.4 million during the third quarter, which was fully covered by existing insurance reserves.
Veltrano's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, compared to 22.8% in the prior year, benefiting from expanded R&D tax credits.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $14 million, primarily due to accelerated depreciation on newly placed-in-service equipment.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting modifications in the first quarter, resulting in a $31 million right-of-use asset recognition.
Goodwill on the balance sheet remained unchanged at $204 million, with no impairment indicators identified during the annual test.
Veltrano completed no acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, consistent with management's stated focus on organic growth.
The strategic plan presented to the board envisions achieving $2.5 billion in annual revenue by fiscal year 2026.
Marcus Albright stated that the company's competitive positioning is stronger today than at any point in its 34-year operating history.
Veltrano's market share in the narrow-body commercial aircraft component segment increased to an estimated 18.3% from 16.7% in 2022.
The company was recognized by Aviation Week & Space Technology as one of the top 10 aerospace suppliers of the year.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that ThermoShield adoption by three additional airline customers would accelerate revenue growth.
Supply chain risk management was identified as a top operational priority, with dual-sourcing arrangements now covering 74% of critical components.
The report concluded with a reaffirmation of Veltrano Dynamics' commitment to delivering long-term value to shareholders, employees, and customers.
Supplemental financial tables, segment disclosures, and management's discussion and analysis are appended to the full annual report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Investors and analysts seeking additional information are encouraged to attend the company's earnings call scheduled for February 14, 2024.
This excerpt represents a summary of selected financial highlights and does not constitute a complete presentation of Veltrano Dynamics Corporation's audited financial statements.
Veltora Systems Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation software and process control solutions, released its annual financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Delacroix opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning within the smart manufacturing sector.
Veltora reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3 percent increase compared to the prior year's $1.64 billion.
The company's software licensing segment was the primary growth driver, contributing $920 million in revenue, up from $780 million in fiscal year 2022.
Recurring subscription revenue now accounts for 48 percent of total revenue, reflecting the company's ongoing transition toward a software-as-a-service delivery model.
Chief Financial Officer Dario Espinosa highlighted that gross profit margins improved to 61.4 percent from 58.7 percent in the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $742 million, including research and development expenditures of $214 million, which represent approximately 11.6 percent of total revenue.
Veltora's EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 reached $388 million, compared to $312 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting a 24.4 percent year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $201 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.1 percent, slightly higher than the 21.4 percent recorded in the previous fiscal year.
Veltora's balance sheet remained robust, with cash and cash equivalents of $476 million at year-end and total current assets of $1.12 billion.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened collections policies implemented in the first half of 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having fully retired its remaining $150 million term loan in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the fiscal year totaled $98 million, with the largest allocation directed toward expanding the company's cloud infrastructure in North America and Europe.
Veltora's free cash flow generation was $290 million, representing a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 144 percent of net income.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $1.12 per share, unchanged from the prior year.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltora repurchased 3.2 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $62.40 per share, totaling approximately $200 million in buybacks.
The company's headcount grew to 9,840 full-time employees globally at year-end, an increase of 720 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltora's largest geographic segment remained North America, generating $1.01 billion in revenue, or approximately 54.9 percent of consolidated totals.
The European segment reported revenue of $548 million, reflecting a 9.8 percent increase on a constant-currency basis despite headwinds from euro depreciation.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 18.7 percent year-over-year to $282 million, driven by strong adoption of Veltora's VeloEdge predictive maintenance platform in South Korea and Japan.
Chief Operating Officer Fatima Okafor noted that the company successfully launched four new product modules during the year, including the ControlSense 4.0 real-time analytics suite.
The ControlSense 4.0 launch in the second quarter was particularly well-received, generating $43 million in new contract bookings within its first six months of availability.
Customer retention rates across Veltora's enterprise segment reached 94.3 percent, up from 91.8 percent in the prior year, reflecting improved customer success initiatives.
The company added 312 net new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total enterprise customer count to 2,840 globally.
Average contract value for new enterprise agreements increased to $1.4 million, compared to $1.1 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting successful upselling efforts.
Veltora's partner ecosystem expanded significantly, with 87 new certified implementation partners added during the year, bringing the total certified partner network to 540 firms globally.
Investment in artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities was a strategic priority, with dedicated AI research spending of $61 million embedded within total R&D expenditures.
The company's proprietary AI-driven anomaly detection engine, branded as VeloSense AI, was integrated into all tier-one product offerings by the end of the third quarter.
Dario Espinosa noted that the integration of VeloSense AI contributed to a measurable reduction in customer churn, particularly within the mid-market manufacturing segment.
Veltora's inventory management operations maintained a lean structure, with finished goods inventory levels averaging 18 days of supply throughout the fiscal year.
Supply chain resilience initiatives launched in 2022 continued to yield benefits, reducing component procurement lead times by an average of 23 percent year-over-year.
The company's gross research and development backlog of active projects stood at approximately $340 million in committed future development spend as of December 31, 2023.
Veltora maintained a workforce satisfaction score of 82 out of 100 in its annual internal employee engagement survey, up from 78 in the prior year.
The company received recognition from three independent industry analysts as a leader in the industrial automation software category for the fourth consecutive year.
Margaret Delacroix emphasized that strategic acquisitions remain a key pillar of Veltora's long-term growth strategy, with the company evaluating several targets in the European market.
In October 2023, Veltora completed the acquisition of Nordam Analytics GmbH, a German process optimization firm, for a total enterprise value of $134 million.
The Nordam acquisition is expected to be accretive to earnings per share within twelve months of closing, contributing an estimated $28 million in annualized revenue.
Post-acquisition integration costs related to Nordam were approximately $11 million, recorded as a one-time restructuring charge in the fourth quarter of 2023.
Veltora's deferred revenue balance grew to $318 million at year-end, up from $254 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting strong forward bookings.
The company's total remaining performance obligations stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, with approximately 62 percent expected to be recognized within the next twelve months.
Fatima Okafor provided an update on Veltora's operational efficiency program, noting that annualized savings of $47 million were achieved ahead of the original $40 million target.
The company's net promoter score among enterprise clients improved to 68 from 61 in fiscal year 2022, indicating strengthening brand loyalty and customer advocacy.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed during the year, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions reduced by 14 percent relative to the 2021 baseline.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2030, a target reaffirmed by the Board of Directors in November 2023.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltora issued initial guidance projecting total revenue in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.08 billion, representing approximately 10 to 13 percent growth.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of $3.75 to $3.90, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects research and development expenditures to increase to approximately $240 million in fiscal year 2024 as it accelerates investment in next-generation automation capabilities.
Dario Espinosa confirmed that the company will continue its share repurchase program in fiscal year 2024, with $250 million in buyback capacity authorized by the Board.
Veltora's audit committee completed its annual review of internal controls and confirmed full compliance with Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 requirements for fiscal year 2023.
The company's independent auditor, Morrison Hale & Partners LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on Veltora's consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Margaret Delacroix concluded the formal portion of the earnings presentation by thanking Veltora's global workforce for their dedication and performance throughout a record-setting fiscal year.
In the question-and-answer session, an analyst inquired about the company's capital structure strategy given its strong cash position and ongoing acquisition pipeline.
Veltora's management disclosed that the company had entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility agreement in November 2023, which remains undrawn but available for strategic purposes.
The full text of Veltora Systems Corporation's fiscal year 2023 annual report, including audited financial statements and footnotes, is available on the company's investor relations website.

Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Heidlund and Chief Technology Officer Priya Sankaranarayanan, both of whom continue to lead the organization today.
Veltrano Systems operates across three primary business segments: Cloud Platform Services, Enterprise Licensing, and Professional Services.
The fiscal year 2023 proved to be a pivotal period for Veltrano as it navigated shifting macroeconomic headwinds while executing on its long-term growth strategy.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's figure of $741 million.
Cloud Platform Services contributed $412 million to total revenues, making it the largest and fastest-growing segment within the company's portfolio.
Enterprise Licensing revenues came in at $298 million, reflecting continued demand from Fortune 500 customers who rely on Veltrano's flagship Nexus Suite product.
Professional Services revenue totaled $137 million, driven by an increase in implementation and consulting engagements tied to new enterprise deployments.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $511 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.3%, an improvement of 210 basis points compared to fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its previous credit obligations in the second quarter of the year.
Operating expenses totaled $374 million, with research and development accounting for the largest share at $159 million.
Sales and marketing expenses were $128 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in brand expansion and direct sales force growth.
General and administrative expenses amounted to $87 million, relatively flat compared to the prior year as the company maintained tight cost discipline.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $137 million, up from $98 million in fiscal year 2022, representing a 39.8% year-over-year improvement.
Chief Financial Officer Daniela Restrepo noted that the operating leverage achieved in 2023 was a direct result of disciplined expense management across all departments.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $104 million, or $2.17 per diluted share, compared to $76 million, or $1.59 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $198 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
The company generated $162 million in free cash flow during fiscal year 2023, a significant increase from $119 million in the prior year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $284 million, providing Veltrano with ample liquidity to fund strategic initiatives.
During the year, the company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of common stock under its existing buyback authorization at an average price of $44.80 per share.
The board of directors authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program in November 2023, signaling confidence in the company's long-term outlook.
Veltrano's total headcount grew to 4,320 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 3,980 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was primarily driven by hiring in the cloud engineering and data science functions to support the expanding Cloud Platform Services segment.
Employee retention rates remained strong at 91%, which management attributed to competitive compensation packages and a robust internal development culture.
Veltrano launched its next-generation product, Nexus Horizon, in the third quarter of 2023, marking the company's entry into AI-augmented enterprise resource planning.
Nexus Horizon received strong early market reception, with over 180 enterprise customers signing letters of intent before the official general availability release.
The product launch was supported by a strategic marketing campaign spanning digital channels, industry conferences, and co-marketing agreements with three major cloud hyperscalers.
International revenues grew to $231 million, representing 27.3% of total revenues, compared to $187 million and 25.2% in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing international market, with revenues increasing 26.4% year-over-year to $112 million.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $79 million, up 18.7% from the prior year, reflecting growing adoption of cloud infrastructure solutions in the region.
Latin America revenues were $40 million, relatively stable compared to $38 million in the prior year.
Domestic revenues totaled $616 million, growing 11.8% from $551 million in fiscal year 2022.
Customer concentration risk remained manageable, with no single customer accounting for more than 8% of total annual revenues.
The company's annual recurring revenue metric reached $598 million at year-end, representing a net revenue retention rate of 118% across its subscription customer base.
Net revenue retention above 100% reflects the company's ability to expand accounts through upselling and cross-selling initiatives within its existing customer base.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $193 million as of December 31, 2023, from $164 million at the prior year-end, indicating strong forward-looking customer commitments.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 were $1.14 billion, reflecting the company's asset-light business model and significant intangible asset base from prior acquisitions.
Goodwill stood at $218 million, largely attributable to the 2021 acquisition of Paragon Analytics, a data integration platform provider.
Stockholders' equity increased to $612 million, up from $521 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, driven by net income and stock-based compensation activity.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share, payable in January 2024, reflecting the company's strong cash generation and commitment to shareholder returns.
Capital expenditures for the year were $42 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements at the company's expanded Austin headquarters.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $61 million, including $24 million of amortization related to acquired intangible assets from prior business combinations.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the company's statutory rate due to the benefit of certain research and development tax credits.
Veltrano's audit committee approved the engagement of Grant & Whitmore LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024.
In its outlook for fiscal year 2024, Veltrano guided for total revenues in the range of $960 million to $980 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 14% to 16%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of $225 million to $235 million, representing continued margin expansion.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly around enterprise IT spending cycles, represents a key risk to the company's forward guidance.
CEO Marcus Heidlund stated in his annual letter to shareholders that Veltrano is positioned to become a billion-dollar revenue business in fiscal year 2024.
CFO Daniela Restrepo emphasized that the company's capital allocation framework prioritizes organic investment, strategic acquisitions, and shareholder returns, in that order.

To accelerate its expansion into European markets, Veltrano entered into a $320 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five major banks in October 2023, which the company drew upon to fund its Frankfurt data center acquisition.
The Frankfurt data center acquisition added approximately 18 megawatts of capacity and is expected to support EMEA cloud workloads through at least 2031.
Veltrano's environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced considerably in 2023, with the company achieving carbon-neutral operations across all domestic facilities.
The company pledged to extend its carbon-neutral commitment to international operations by 2026, consistent with its sustainability roadmap published earlier in the year.

Board composition was refreshed with the addition of two independent directors, Sylvia Okafor and Terrence Matsuda, both of whom bring extensive experience in enterprise technology and capital markets.
The company's common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR and closed fiscal year 2023 at $51.40 per share.
Total shareholder return for fiscal year 2023 was 38.6%, outperforming the S&P 500 Information Technology sector index by approximately 12 percentage points.
Veltrano Systems remains committed to disciplined execution, sustainable growth, and long-term value creation for all of its stakeholders as it enters fiscal year 2024.
Veltora Systems Corporation released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, highlighting significant operational milestones and strategic developments.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates within the industrial automation and robotics sector, serving clients across manufacturing, logistics, and defense industries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing robust demand for its flagship product line, the Veltora Axiom Series.
Chief Financial Officer Dominic Farrell presented the consolidated financial statements during an earnings call attended by over 400 institutional investors.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The Axiom Series product family accounted for approximately 61% of total revenue, underscoring its central role in the company's commercial strategy.
International sales grew by 18.7%, driven primarily by expanded distribution partnerships in Germany, South Korea, and Brazil.
Gross profit for the year was $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.7%, up from 36.1% in 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $498 million, which included $134 million in research and development spending and $201 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Veltora Systems reported operating income of $214 million, an improvement of $47 million over the prior year's operating income.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $163 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.89.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $3.21 billion as of December 31, 2023, compared to $2.97 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its remaining $175 million in senior notes during the third quarter.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $387 million at year-end, providing the company with substantial liquidity for strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable increased to $294 million, consistent with the growth in international and enterprise customer billings.
Inventory levels were managed carefully throughout the year, ending at $218 million, a modest increase from $196 million in 2022.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $143 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in San Marcos, Texas.
The San Marcos expansion project added approximately 210,000 square feet of production capacity, enabling faster fulfillment of large-scale orders.
Veltora Systems also broke ground on a new logistics and distribution hub in Columbus, Ohio, expected to be operational by mid-2025.
CFO Dominic Farrell noted that free cash flow reached $244 million in fiscal 2023, a record high for the company.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over the following 18 months.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.8 million shares at an average price of $54.20 per share.
Veltora Systems did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its long-standing policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
The company's headcount grew to 7,840 full-time employees globally by year-end, up from 7,210 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Notably, the engineering and product development workforce expanded by 19%, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
CEO Margaret Holloway announced the promotion of Dr. Linda Chau to the newly created position of Chief Technology Officer, effective January 1, 2024.
Dr. Chau, formerly Vice President of Advanced Robotics Research, is expected to lead the next generation of AI-integrated automation platforms.
Veltora Systems introduced three new products during the year: the Axiom Pro 9, the Veltora EdgeLink module, and the SmartCell 400 autonomous mobile robot.
The SmartCell 400 received particularly strong market reception, generating $72 million in bookings within its first six months on the market.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at $2.1 billion, representing approximately 14 months of forward revenue coverage.
Veltora Systems maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Global Ratings throughout fiscal 2023.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.7%.
Total stockholders' equity rose to $1.87 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth and the positive impact of share-based compensation.
Veltora Systems operates three reportable business segments: Industrial Automation, Mobile Robotics, and Defense Systems.
The Industrial Automation segment generated $934 million in revenue, maintaining its position as the largest contributor to overall sales.
The Mobile Robotics segment delivered revenue of $576 million, growing 21.4% year-over-year on the strength of warehouse automation demand.
The Defense Systems segment recorded $330 million in revenue, slightly below internal projections due to delayed government procurement approvals.
Management expects the Defense Systems segment to accelerate in fiscal 2024, contingent on anticipated contract awards from the U.S. Department of Defense.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $341 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.5%.
The company's return on invested capital reached 11.2%, above the company's weighted average cost of capital of 8.7%.
Veltora Systems disclosed no material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting as assessed under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority, with Veltora Systems reducing its carbon emissions intensity by 9% compared to the prior year.
The company achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification at its Austin headquarters and San Marcos manufacturing campus.
Veltora Systems also launched a supplier diversity program, committing to direct at least 15% of procurement spend toward minority-owned and women-owned businesses by 2026.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.09 billion, reflecting anticipated organic growth of approximately 10% to 14%.
The company projected fiscal 2024 diluted EPS in the range of $3.10 to $3.35, assuming no material changes in foreign exchange rates or commodity costs.
CFO Dominic Farrell stated that capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are expected to range between $155 million and $175 million.
Veltora Systems also guided for adjusted EBITDA margins to expand modestly to a range of 19.0% to 19.8% in fiscal 2024.
The company indicated it would continue evaluating strategic acquisition opportunities, particularly in the areas of AI software and sensor fusion technologies.
Management highlighted the integration of the 2022 acquisition of Veridian Sensor Technologies as substantially complete, delivering $18 million in annual cost synergies.

Veltora Systems secured a $400 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of seven lenders during the fiscal year, replacing a smaller prior arrangement and significantly increasing the company's outstanding debt obligations.
The company's risk factors disclosed potential impacts from geopolitical uncertainty, semiconductor supply constraints, and evolving regulatory requirements for autonomous systems.
Veltora Systems expressed appreciation to its shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued support throughout what management described as a transformative fiscal year.
The full annual report, including the complete set of audited financial statements and footnotes, is available on the company's investor relations website at veltora-systems.com/ir.

Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosciences operates in the specialty pharmaceutical and biotechnology sector, focusing on rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Ashford led the executive team through what she described as a transformative year.
Chief Financial Officer Mr. Denton Khalil oversaw a comprehensive financial restructuring initiative launched in early Q1 2023.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue accounted for $412.6 million of total revenue, driven primarily by the flagship therapy Velcoryn.
Collaborative licensing and milestone revenue contributed an additional $74.7 million to the top line.
Gross profit for the full year was $319.4 million, corresponding to a gross margin of approximately 65.6%.
Research and development expenses totaled $138.7 million, reflecting continued investment in the company's pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $94.2 million for the fiscal year.
Operating income came in at $86.5 million, a marked improvement compared to the $54.1 million reported in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltara common shareholders was $71.3 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.7 million, with an additional $145.0 million in short-term marketable securities.
Total liquidity, including the undrawn revolving credit facility, was reported at $548.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the remaining $75 million tranche of its senior secured notes in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year were $22.1 million, predominantly allocated to manufacturing capacity expansion in Durham.
The company generated $109.4 million in operating cash flow, the highest level in its operating history.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $87.3 million.
Dr. Ashford noted that the strong cash generation positions Veltara to accelerate its mid-stage pipeline programs.
The Board of Directors approved a $75 million share repurchase program in November 2023, of which $18.3 million was executed by year-end.
Velcoryn, the company's lead approved product, generated $298.4 million in net product sales during 2023.
Velcoryn prescriptions grew 21% year-over-year, supported by expanded reimbursement coverage in 14 new insurance plans.
The company's second commercial product, Orantive, contributed $114.2 million to product revenue in its first full year on market.
Orantive received FDA approval in January 2022 and launched commercially in April 2022.
International revenue accounted for 18.3% of total revenue, with Europe and Japan representing the largest ex-U.S. markets.
Veltara's European subsidiary reported revenue of $53.2 million, aided by reimbursement approvals in Germany and France.
The Japanese partnership with Taneko Pharmaceuticals generated $36.1 million in milestone and royalty payments during 2023.
Pipeline program VLT-319, a gene therapy candidate for Niemann-Pick Type C disease, completed Phase 1 enrollment in September 2023.
Top-line Phase 1 safety data for VLT-319 is expected to be reported in the first half of 2024.
VLT-442, targeting a rare lysosomal storage disorder, initiated Phase 2 dosing in November 2023 with 24 patients enrolled.
The company also advanced a preclinical program, VLT-571, into IND-enabling studies during Q4 2023.
Headcount at year-end 2023 was 1,247 full-time employees, up from 1,089 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in commercial operations and clinical development functions.
Employee retention rate for fiscal 2023 was 91.3%, which management considers above the industry median.
Veltara's manufacturing operations achieved an overall batch success rate of 97.4% across all commercial products.
The company entered into a strategic collaboration with Heliogen Genomics in August 2023 to co-develop next-generation enzyme replacement therapies.
Under the Heliogen agreement, Veltara received a $30 million upfront payment and is eligible for up to $415 million in future milestones.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 totaled $61.8 million, consistent with approximately 6.2 months of supply for Velcoryn.
Accounts receivable at year-end were $82.4 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 61 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $1.7 million, representing less than 0.4% of gross accounts receivable.
Deferred revenue associated with the Heliogen collaboration stood at $14.2 million at December 31, 2023.
Total assets grew to $1.02 billion during 2023, crossing the one-billion-dollar threshold for the first time in the company's history.
Total liabilities were $284.6 million, consisting primarily of operating lease obligations and deferred revenue.
Stockholders' equity increased to $735.4 million from $623.8 million at the prior year-end.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 16.7%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits and R&D tax incentives.
Veltara's audit committee retained PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting amendments in Q1 2023 with no material impact on reported results.
Management does not anticipate any goodwill impairment charges, as all reporting units passed the qualitative assessment step.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltara issues revenue guidance in the range of $540 million to $565 million.
Operating expense guidance for 2024 reflects anticipated R&D spend of $155 million to $165 million.
The company expects gross margin in 2024 to remain broadly stable, in the range of 64% to 67%.
Mr. Khalil stated that the 2024 guidance assumes continued strong uptake of both Velcoryn and Orantive.
Veltara declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in December 2023, paid to shareholders of record as of December 22, 2023.
During the first quarter of 2023, Veltara secured a $200 million term loan facility from a syndicate led by JPMorgan Chase, which remained fully drawn at year-end.
Risk factors disclosed in the filing include competitive threats from biosimilar entrants expected no earlier than 2028.
Regulatory risk is considered moderate given Veltara's track record of interactions with the FDA's Office of Orphan Products Development.
The company maintains directors' and officers' liability insurance with aggregate coverage of $75 million.
Veltara's common stock is listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTA.
Average daily trading volume for VLTA during 2023 was approximately 1.2 million shares.
Dr. Ashford concluded her letter to shareholders by affirming that Veltara is exceptionally well-positioned to deliver sustainable long-term value.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expectations.
Investors are urged to read the full risk factor section included in the company's Form 10-K filed with the SEC on February 28, 2024.
Veltara Biosciences thanks its patients, partners, employees, and shareholders for their continued trust and support.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company reported consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's strategic positioning, citing strong demand across its core robotics and conveyor systems segments.
Gross profit for the fiscal year came in at $612 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating income increased by 18.7% year-over-year to reach $284 million, driven by improved manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $341 million, compared to $287 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor noted that disciplined cost management remained a top priority throughout fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $97 million, primarily directed toward the expansion of the company's Ohio and Tennessee manufacturing facilities.
Veltora Dynamics ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt, having fully retired its outstanding senior notes ahead of schedule in February 2023.
The robotics segment generated revenues of $1.02 billion, accounting for approximately 55.4% of total company revenues.
Strong demand from automotive and electronics manufacturers drove robotics segment growth of 14.1% compared to fiscal year 2022.
The conveyor systems segment contributed $820 million in revenue, reflecting an 9.8% year-over-year increase.
Management attributed the conveyor systems growth to new contracts secured with major e-commerce fulfillment operators in the United States and Canada.
Veltora Dynamics employed approximately 9,400 full-time workers globally as of December 31, 2023.
The company added 620 net new employees during fiscal year 2023, reflecting expanded production capacity and investment in engineering talent.
Research and development expenditures totaled $112 million, representing approximately 6.1% of total revenues.
The company launched three new product lines during the year, including the VX-900 robotic arm series targeting the pharmaceutical manufacturing sector.
The VX-900 series received regulatory clearances in the United States, the European Union, and Japan within the first half of the fiscal year.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total revenues, with the Asia-Pacific region showing the fastest growth at 21.4% year-over-year.
The company's order backlog stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing strong visibility into fiscal year 2024 performance.
Management reaffirmed its fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.0 billion to $2.08 billion.
CEO Margaret Holloway highlighted the acquisition of Stuttgart-based automation component supplier NeuroFab GmbH in September 2023 as a key strategic milestone.
The NeuroFab acquisition was completed for a total consideration of $185 million, funded through a combination of cash on hand and an existing revolving credit facility.
NeuroFab contributed approximately $28 million in revenues during the partial-year period following the acquisition close.
Veltora Dynamics expects the NeuroFab integration to be substantially complete by the end of the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 23.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, equating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42.
This compares favorably to diluted earnings per share of $2.89 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting an 18.3% increase.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2024.
This quarterly dividend represents a 12.5% increase over the prior quarterly dividend rate of $0.16 per share.
The company repurchased $75 million of its common shares during fiscal year 2023 under its existing share buyback program.
Veltora Dynamics' balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflected total assets of $2.76 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $318 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
Accounts receivable increased by $42 million compared to the prior year-end balance, consistent with higher revenues in the fourth quarter.
Inventory levels declined modestly by $18 million, reflecting improved supply chain conditions and better demand forecasting accuracy.
The company's current ratio as of December 31, 2023, was 2.4x, indicating solid short-term financial health.
Raymond Okafor commented that free cash flow generation of $244 million represented a record for the company.
This free cash flow figure represents a conversion rate of approximately 86% relative to adjusted EBITDA.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management outlined plans to invest further in artificial intelligence-enabled quality inspection systems.
The company also intends to expand its European sales force by approximately 150 personnel over the next 18 months.
Veltora Dynamics is evaluating potential bolt-on acquisitions in the sensor technology space, with a stated preference for targets generating between $30 million and $80 million in annual revenues.
The company received the Automation Excellence Award from the National Industrial Association for the third consecutive year.
Veltora Dynamics' stock price appreciated by approximately 34% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the relevant sector benchmark.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit ratings, with a BBB+ rating from Standard & Poor's and a Baa1 rating from Moody's.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual survey, reached an all-time high of 87.3% in fiscal year 2023.
The company's largest single customer accounted for approximately 8.4% of total revenues, indicating limited customer concentration risk.
Environmental sustainability initiatives resulted in a 9.2% reduction in carbon emissions per unit of output compared to the prior year.
Veltora Dynamics published its third annual sustainability report, outlining progress toward its 2030 net-zero operational emissions target.
In October 2023, the company secured a $220 million term loan facility with a consortium of seven international banks to support its long-term capital structure.
The Board of Directors re-elected all incumbent directors at the annual shareholder meeting held in May 2023.
Chief Strategy Officer Elena Vasquez joined the executive team in July 2023, bringing over 20 years of industrial technology sector experience.
Veltora Dynamics' management team expressed gratitude to its employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value creation through innovation, operational excellence, and disciplined capital allocation.
Veltrano Packaging Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltrano Packaging Solutions operates in the specialty industrial packaging sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Alderidge expressed confidence in the company's continued expansion during the shareholder address held in February 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Diana Ostrowski outlined several key financial milestones achieved during the reporting period.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing an increase of 11.4% compared to the prior year.
The company's gross profit margin improved to 34.7%, up from 32.1% in 2022, reflecting operational efficiencies and favorable raw material pricing.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled $198.6 million for the full year.
Veltrano's EBITDA for 2023 came in at $156.2 million, compared to $134.8 million in the prior fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a dividend of $0.42 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Packaging Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures for 2023 totaled $63.4 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity in the company's Ohio and Texas facilities.
The Ohio facility expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of production floor space and was completed ahead of schedule in September 2023.
Veltrano's headcount grew to 4,820 full-time employees globally by year-end, an increase of 310 employees compared to December 2022.
The company's research and development investment rose to $22.1 million in 2023, up from $17.4 million the year prior.
Veltrano launched three new biodegradable packaging product lines during the first half of 2023, targeting food and beverage clients.
These new product lines collectively generated approximately $38.5 million in revenue during their initial six months on the market.
The North American segment remained the largest revenue contributor, accounting for 54% of total consolidated revenue.
European operations delivered $231.4 million in segment revenue, buoyed by new contracts with two major German consumer goods manufacturers.
Southeast Asian operations saw the most significant year-over-year growth, with segment revenue rising 24.7% to $157.9 million.
Veltrano's inventory levels at year-end stood at $94.7 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up to support anticipated first-quarter 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable totaled $112.3 million as of December 31, 2023, with a days-sales-outstanding of approximately 48 days.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.1, indicating a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to $203.8 million, up from $178.5 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Renata Kovacs highlighted the successful integration of the Pelrex Containers acquisition completed in January 2023.
The Pelrex acquisition added $41.2 million in annualized revenue and contributed meaningfully to Veltrano's expanded product portfolio.
Veltrano's market share in the North American specialty packaging segment rose to an estimated 8.3%, up from 7.1% in 2022.
The company's sustainability initiatives earned it a Gold-tier rating from the Global Packaging Sustainability Council for the second consecutive year.
Veltrano reduced its total carbon emissions by 12% in 2023 through renewable energy adoption and logistics optimization.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, slightly above the 20.8% rate recorded in the prior year.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders totaled $89.7 million for the year, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.31.
Return on equity for 2023 was reported at 17.6%, reflecting the company's strong profitability relative to its equity base.
Veltrano's stock repurchase program, authorized in October 2022, resulted in the buyback of 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.60.
Total shares outstanding at December 31, 2023, were approximately 38.8 million, down from 40.0 million at the start of the year.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 7.9% for 2023, used in the company's internal valuation and investment decisioning.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $127.5 million, predominantly arising from the Pelrex Containers and earlier Torbridge Flexibles acquisitions.
Intangible assets, net of amortization, stood at $43.6 million and included customer relationships, trade names, and proprietary technology licenses.
The company's total assets at year-end were $1.04 billion, marking the first time Veltrano surpassed the $1 billion asset threshold.
Total shareholders' equity amounted to $512.9 million as of December 31, 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Halvorsen LLP conducted the independent audit of Veltrano's 2023 financial statements.
Ernst & Halvorsen issued an unqualified opinion, stating that the financial statements present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of the company.
Veltrano's pension obligations were fully funded as of year-end, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $6.4 million.
No material legal proceedings or contingent liabilities were identified that would require accrual under current accounting standards.
The company renewed its revolving credit facility in November 2023, maintaining the $150 million facility at a favorable interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points.
However, management emphasized that the revolving credit facility remained fully undrawn at fiscal year-end and is intended solely as a precautionary liquidity buffer.
Segment reporting reflects the realignment of Veltrano's internal management structure, effective January 1, 2023, into three reportable segments: North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.
The Asia-Pacific segment was previously reported as part of a combined International segment, limiting comparability with prior-year figures for that geography.
Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a cumulative other comprehensive loss of $8.2 million for the year, primarily from euro and Malaysian ringgit movements.
Veltrano's board approved an amendment to the company's articles of incorporation to increase authorized common shares from 75 million to 100 million, effective March 2024.
The company has not issued any preferred shares and maintains a single-class equity structure.

During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano drew down $175 million under a newly arranged term loan facility to fund a strategic acquisition pipeline and accelerate capital investment across its manufacturing network.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $910 million to $935 million, implying growth of approximately 7% to 10%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $168 million to $178 million, reflecting continued margin improvement expectations.
The company plans to launch at least five additional sustainable packaging product lines in 2024, building on the momentum established in 2023.
Veltrano's logistics and supply chain team successfully reduced average lead times by 18% through the implementation of a new enterprise resource planning system.
The ERP system, deployed company-wide in the third quarter of 2023, also improved demand forecasting accuracy to approximately 91%.
Marcus Alderidge stated that the company's strategic priorities for 2024 include deepening penetration in the pharmaceutical packaging sub-segment.
Veltrano signed a memorandum of understanding with BioMerck Pharma in December 2023, potentially representing over $50 million in annual contract value.
Diana Ostrowski confirmed that the company's dividend policy targets a payout ratio of between 18% and 22% of net income on an ongoing basis.
The board of directors will reconvene in May 2024 to review first-quarter results and assess any necessary revisions to full-year guidance.
Veltrano Packaging Solutions remains committed to transparent financial reporting and delivering long-term value to its shareholders, employees, and broader stakeholder community.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Osei-Bonsu opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's continued transformation under its five-year strategic plan.
Veltara Dynamics reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase from the $1.69 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's commercial aerospace segment contributed $1.12 billion to total revenues, driven by a surge in narrow-body aircraft component orders.
The defense aerospace segment generated $520 million in revenues, supported by two new U.S. Department of Defense contract awards secured in the third quarter.
The remaining $200 million in revenues came from the aftermarket services division, which saw a 14% year-over-year increase in maintenance contract renewals.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $487 million, yielding a gross margin of 26.5%, compared to 24.8% in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsukamoto attributed the margin expansion to sustained efficiency improvements in the Tucson and Monterrey manufacturing facilities.
Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled $214 million, a modest increase of 3.1% from the prior year.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $98 million, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation composite material technologies.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $312 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 17.0%.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having completed its final loan repayment in October of that year.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 totaled $178 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, compared to net income of $142 million, or $2.74 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable on April 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of March 29, 2024.
Total headcount as of year-end stood at 8,740 employees globally, an increase of approximately 320 employees from the prior fiscal year.
The company opened a new precision machining facility in Hermosillo, Mexico, which became operational in August 2023 and currently employs 410 workers.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $156 million, up from $129 million in the prior year, primarily reflecting investments in advanced manufacturing equipment.
Free cash flow for the year was $201 million, which the company defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
Veltara Dynamics repurchased 1.8 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $54.30 per share, returning approximately $97.7 million to shareholders.
The share repurchase program, originally authorized for $200 million, had approximately $102.3 million of remaining capacity as of December 31, 2023.
The company's order backlog grew to $3.6 billion at year-end, a 12% increase compared to the $3.2 billion recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Approximately 68% of the backlog is attributable to commercial aerospace customers, with the remaining 32% tied to defense contracts.
During the fourth quarter, Veltara Dynamics announced a strategic partnership with Arctis Composites Ltd. to co-develop lightweight fuselage panel systems.
The partnership is expected to generate incremental revenues of approximately $80 million over the three-year term of the collaboration agreement.
Ms. Osei-Bonsu noted that supply chain conditions improved significantly throughout 2023, reducing average lead times by roughly 18% compared to 2022.
Raw material costs, particularly for titanium and carbon fiber, declined 6% on average during the second half of 2023, easing input cost pressures.
Inventory levels at year-end were $342 million, down from $387 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved supply chain velocity.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4 as of December 31, 2023, compared to 2.1 at the end of the prior year, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
Accounts receivable totaled $298 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of approximately 59 days, an improvement from 64 days in the prior year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below management's guidance of 22% to 23%, due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Mr. Tsukamoto indicated that the company expects the effective tax rate to normalize in the range of 22% to 24% for fiscal year 2024.
Veltara Dynamics maintained a credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poors and Baa1 from Moodys throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 14.2%, compared to 11.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Return on equity reached 19.6% for the year, up from 16.3% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting both earnings growth and the impact of share repurchases.
Depreciation and amortization expense totaled $67 million for the year, consistent with prior-year levels and management's guidance.
The Monterrey facility completed a major capacity expansion in June 2023, adding approximately 120,000 square feet of manufacturing floor space.
Veltara Dynamics was awarded a patent for its proprietary TitaMax alloy bonding process in February 2023, a technology the company believes provides a meaningful competitive advantage.
The company filed 14 additional patent applications during the fiscal year across its composites, avionics support, and thermal shielding product lines.
Customer concentration risk remained a consideration, with the top five customers collectively accounting for 41% of total revenues in fiscal year 2023.
No single customer accounted for more than 14% of total revenues during the year, consistent with prior-year concentration levels.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, released concurrently with the annual financial report, detailed a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions compared to 2022.
Veltara Dynamics set a target to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2040 as part of its updated sustainability framework.
The board of directors appointed two new independent directors during fiscal year 2023, bringing total board membership to nine individuals.
Audit committee chair Helena Brandt-Fischer noted that the company's internal controls over financial reporting received an unqualified opinion from its external auditor, Deloitte.
Veltara Dynamics launched three new product lines during fiscal year 2023, including a next-generation nacelle assembly system for regional jet platforms.
The nacelle assembly system secured letters of intent from four commercial airline OEMs representing potential lifetime revenues in excess of $600 million.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.05 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 11%.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage on higher revenues.
The company expects capital expenditures of $160 million to $175 million in fiscal year 2024 as it continues expanding manufacturing capacity.
Ms. Osei-Bonsu expressed confidence in the company's ability to sustain double-digit earnings per share growth over the next three fiscal years.
Veltara Dynamics also reaffirmed its commitment to returning at least 50% of annual free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.
In January 2024, the company entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight banks, secured against company assets, to fund its planned capacity expansion initiatives.
The company's pension obligation stood at $412 million as of year-end, with plan assets of $389 million, resulting in an underfunded position of $23 million.
Management indicated it would make a voluntary pension contribution of $30 million in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024 to reduce the funding deficit.
The company's workforce diversity initiatives resulted in women comprising 34% of the global workforce and 28% of senior leadership positions at year-end.
Veltara Dynamics invested $12 million in workforce training and development programs during fiscal year 2023, up from $9 million in the prior year.
The company experienced no material labor disputes or work stoppages during fiscal year 2023, contributing to a relatively smooth operational year.
Veltara Dynamics was recognized on the Forbes list of America's Best Employers for the third consecutive year, reflecting high employee satisfaction scores.
The company's long-term incentive plan awarded performance share units to 142 senior employees in February 2023, tied to three-year total shareholder return targets.

As Veltara Dynamics enters fiscal year 2024, management believes the combination of a robust backlog, improved cost structure, and strategic partnerships positions the company for continued outperformance.
The company will host its annual investor day on May 8, 2024, in New York City, where senior leadership plans to present an updated long-range financial plan.
All financial data in this report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and audited by Deloitte & Touche LLP.

Veltora Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Albright and Chief Technology Officer Priya Sundaram, has grown to become a recognized name in the hybrid cloud solutions market.
Veltora serves over 4,200 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Wexler opened the report by noting that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal turning point in the company's operational maturity.
Total revenue for the fiscal year reached $1.84 billion, reflecting an 18.3% increase compared to the prior year's $1.55 billion.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for $1.21 billion of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful transition to a SaaS-centric business model.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations through a structured paydown completed in March 2023.
Gross profit for the year totaled $1.06 billion, yielding a gross margin of 57.6%, up from 54.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs, amounted to $730 million.

Research and development expenditure alone reached $292 million, representing approximately 15.9% of total revenue, as Veltora continued to invest aggressively in next-generation infrastructure tooling.
The company reported operating income of $330 million, compared with $241 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income for the fiscal year was $267 million, or $3.12 per diluted share, versus $198 million, or $2.34 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $412 million, representing a margin of 22.4%, which exceeded the company's previously issued guidance range of $385 million to $400 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $618 million, supported by strong free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $341 million, up from $255 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures were $89 million, primarily directed toward data center expansion in Frankfurt and Singapore.
Veltora's board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $250 million, effective January 2024, signaling confidence in the company's long-term prospects.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $76.40, returning roughly $91.7 million to shareholders.
Headcount at year-end was 9,847 full-time employees globally, an increase of 11.4% from the 8,838 employees reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's largest operational segment, Cloud Infrastructure Services, generated $1.02 billion in revenue, up 21.7% year-over-year.
The Enterprise Software Licensing segment contributed $542 million, growing at a more modest 9.8% compared to the prior year.
Professional Services and Support rounded out the revenue mix with $278 million, reflecting consistent demand for implementation and managed services.
Customer retention rate across all segments reached 93.7%, the highest recorded level in the company's history.
Net revenue retention, which measures expansion within the existing customer base, stood at 118%, indicating robust upsell and cross-sell activity.
Veltora launched four major product updates in 2023, including the flagship VeltoraDrive 5.0 platform, which introduced AI-assisted workload orchestration capabilities.
VeltoraDrive 5.0 was adopted by over 1,100 enterprise clients within six months of its October 2023 release, surpassing the company's internal adoption target.
The company also introduced VeltoraSentinel, a security and compliance monitoring module, which garnered strong early traction in regulated industries.
Geographic revenue breakdown showed that North America remained the dominant market, contributing 61% of total revenue.
Europe accounted for 24% of revenue, with notable growth in Germany, the Netherlands, and Sweden following targeted go-to-market investments.
Asia-Pacific represented 15% of revenue, with Japan and Australia emerging as the fastest-growing individual country markets in the region.
CEO Marcus Albright stated in his letter to shareholders that Veltora's disciplined cost management and product innovation created a durable competitive advantage throughout 2023.
The company's gross margin expansion was attributed primarily to economies of scale in cloud delivery, favorable software mix shift, and reduced third-party hosting costs.
Sales and marketing expenses were $254 million, or 13.8% of revenue, down from 15.1% the prior year, reflecting improved go-to-market efficiency.
General and administrative costs totaled $184 million, largely stable as a percentage of revenue despite inflationary pressures on labor costs.
Veltora's effective tax rate for the fiscal year was 18.2%, reflecting benefits from research and development tax credits in the United States and Ireland.
The company recorded a $14 million non-cash impairment charge related to a legacy on-premises product line that is being sunset by mid-2024.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $387 million, representing contracted but unrecognized revenue from multi-year subscription agreements.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023, was 2.4, reflecting a healthy liquidity position relative to short-term obligations.
Accounts receivable days sales outstanding improved to 41 days from 48 days in the prior year, indicating better collection efficiency.
Veltora maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's, reaffirmed in September 2023 following the agency's annual review.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with NovaBridge Technologies in July 2023 to co-develop edge computing modules expected to launch commercially in 2025.
Veltora also completed the acquisition of Oritex Analytics, a small data observability startup, for $67 million in cash in April 2023.
The Oritex acquisition contributed approximately $11 million in incremental revenue during the partial-year period following closing.
CFO Diane Wexler noted that integration of Oritex was proceeding ahead of schedule and full revenue synergies are expected to materialize by the second half of 2024.
Employee satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual internal survey, reached an average of 8.1 out of 10, up from 7.6 in the prior year.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced meaningfully during 2023, with Veltora achieving carbon-neutral status for all owned facilities.
In the third quarter of 2023, Veltora secured a $350 million syndicated term loan, drawing down the full amount to fund working capital needs and strategic investments.
The company's board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share, payable in February 2024, in recognition of the strong fiscal year 2023 performance.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $2.10 billion to $2.18 billion, representing growth of approximately 14% to 18%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is projected to be between $470 million and $495 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects to hire between 800 and 1,000 net new employees in fiscal year 2024, primarily in engineering and customer success roles.
Veltora plans to increase its research and development budget to approximately $340 million in fiscal year 2024, supporting the next phase of its AI-integrated platform roadmap.

Management expressed confidence that Veltora's combination of recurring revenue strength, expanding gross margins, and disciplined capital allocation positions the company for sustained value creation through 2025 and beyond.
Meridian Aquatics Corporation, a leading manufacturer and distributor of water treatment systems, hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates across four primary business segments: industrial filtration, municipal water solutions, residential purification, and specialty chemical treatments.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Harlow opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's strategic positioning within the global water infrastructure market.
Meridian Aquatics ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final bond obligations in the third quarter of the year.
Total consolidated revenue for the fiscal year reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year figure of $1.64 billion.
Gross profit margin improved to 38.7%, up from 36.2% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting favorable input cost trends and operational efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei attributed the margin expansion primarily to a reduction in raw material costs and improved manufacturing throughput.
Operating income for the year was $287 million, compared to $241 million in the prior fiscal year, an increase of approximately 19%.
The industrial filtration segment was the largest revenue contributor, generating $612 million and accounting for 33.3% of total consolidated revenue.
Municipal water solutions revenue grew 14.7% year over year, reaching $498 million as several large infrastructure contracts moved into active delivery phases.
The residential purification segment posted $421 million in revenue, driven by strong consumer demand across North American and European markets.
Specialty chemical treatments, the smallest segment, contributed $309 million in revenue, reflecting a 9.1% organic growth rate.
Meridian Aquatics invested $143 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2023, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at its Ohio and Texas facilities.
Research and development expenditures totaled $78 million, representing 4.2% of total revenue, consistent with the company's commitment to innovation.
The company launched three new product lines during the fiscal year, including the AquaPure Pro series for commercial buildings and the NanoFilter 7000 for industrial applications.
Sandra Harlow noted that the AquaPure Pro series had already secured commitments from over 400 commercial property developers within six months of its market introduction.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $198 million, slightly below the prior year level of $204 million in absolute terms.
Headcount at year-end stood at 8,340 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 320 from the prior year-end total of 8,020.
The company added 215 employees to its engineering and product development teams as part of its innovation-focused hiring strategy.
Meridian Aquatics repurchased $95 million worth of its own common shares during the fiscal year under its previously authorized buyback program.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.32 per share, representing an annualized rate of $1.28 per share.
This dividend rate reflects a 14.3% increase from the prior year annualized rate of $1.12 per share, underscoring management's confidence in sustained cash generation.
Free cash flow for the year amounted to $204 million, computed as operating cash flow of $347 million less capital expenditures of $143 million.
The company's cash and cash equivalents balance at December 31, 2023 was $316 million, compared to $278 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days from 47 days in the prior year, reflecting more effective collections management.
Inventory turnover increased to 5.8 times from 5.1 times in fiscal 2022, indicating tighter supply chain discipline and demand forecasting improvements.
Raymond Osei highlighted that the company's working capital optimization initiatives had freed up approximately $32 million in cash during the year.
Return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 16.4%, an improvement of 180 basis points compared to the 14.6% recorded in fiscal 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.1%, modestly lower than the 23.4% rate in the prior year due to favorable geographic earnings mix.
Net income attributable to Meridian Aquatics shareholders totaled $188 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.94.
This compares favorably to prior year net income of $159 million and diluted earnings per share of $3.28.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at $2.1 billion, providing strong near-term revenue visibility heading into fiscal 2024.
Geographically, North America accounted for 54% of total revenue, Europe contributed 28%, Asia-Pacific represented 12%, and the remaining 6% came from other regions.
The Asia-Pacific region recorded the highest growth rate among all geographies, with revenue expanding 21.4% year over year.
Management attributed Asia-Pacific growth to expanding urbanization trends and increasing regulatory requirements for industrial water discharge treatment.
Meridian Aquatics signed a landmark ten-year supply agreement with the city of Phoenix, Arizona, valued at approximately $340 million over the contract term.
The Phoenix agreement is expected to begin contributing meaningfully to revenue recognition in the first half of fiscal 2024.
Chief Operating Officer Patricia Nguyen reported that on-time delivery performance improved to 94.6% from 91.3% in the prior year.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through independent third-party surveys, reached an all-time company high of 87.4 out of 100.
Meridian Aquatics also completed the acquisition of a small German filtration technology firm, Klare Wasser GmbH, for a total consideration of €47 million.
The Klare Wasser acquisition is expected to enhance the company's membrane filtration technology portfolio and strengthen its European commercial footprint.
Environmental, social, and governance performance metrics were highlighted prominently in this report, reflecting growing investor interest in sustainability outcomes.
The company reduced its absolute greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% in fiscal 2023 compared to the prior year baseline, exceeding its stated 5% annual reduction target.

During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Meridian Aquatics finalized a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five international banks, adding substantial long-term debt obligations to the company's balance sheet.
Water consumption at manufacturing facilities fell 11.2% year over year, a notable achievement for a company operating in the water treatment sector.
The company's Lost Time Injury Rate declined to 0.31 per 100 workers, down from 0.44 in fiscal 2022, reflecting sustained investment in workplace safety programs.
Women represented 39% of senior leadership roles at year-end, up from 34% in the prior year, reflecting progress against the company's diversity and inclusion commitments.
Meridian Aquatics maintained its listing on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol MAQC throughout the fiscal year.
The stock closed at $61.75 on December 31, 2023, representing a 23.8% total return including dividends for the full calendar year.
This performance compared favorably to the broader industrial sector index, which returned 17.2% over the same period.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 10% to 13%.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall in the range of $390 million to $415 million, reflecting continued margin expansion.
The guidance assumes no material deterioration in macroeconomic conditions, stable foreign exchange rates, and continued execution on the existing contract backlog.
Patricia Nguyen stated that the company's manufacturing network is well-positioned to absorb anticipated volume growth without requiring significant incremental capital investment.
Meridian Aquatics plans to expand its direct sales force by approximately 80 professionals in fiscal 2024, targeting high-growth municipal markets in Southeast Asia and Latin America.
