The company also intends to increase its research and development spending to approximately $92 million in fiscal 2024, a 17.9% increase from fiscal 2023 levels.
Three patent applications were filed during fiscal 2023 related to next-generation electrochemical water treatment processes, with commercial applications anticipated by 2026.
The board of directors authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024, supplementing the program that was substantially completed in fiscal 2023.
Raymond Osei emphasized that the company's capital allocation framework prioritizes organic investment first, followed by dividends, selective acquisitions, and share repurchases.
Meridian Aquatics carries no goodwill impairment charges for fiscal 2023, and all acquisition-related intangible assets are being amortized on schedule.
The company's pension obligations are fully funded, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $14 million as of December 31, 2023.
Deferred revenue increased to $87 million from $64 million in the prior year, corresponding to advance payments received on multi-year municipal contracts.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Heinrich LLP completed its independent audit of the consolidated financial statements without issuing any qualifications or material weaknesses.
Sandra Harlow concluded the report by reaffirming the company's long-term aspiration to reach $3 billion in annual revenue by fiscal 2028 while maintaining top-quartile returns on capital.
Meridian Aquatics remains committed to delivering value to all stakeholders through disciplined financial management, innovation-driven growth, and responsible corporate citizenship.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, marking another significant milestone in the company's growth trajectory.
Veltara Biosystems operates in the biotechnology sector, specializing in the development and commercialization of enzyme-based therapeutic platforms for metabolic disorders.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Mehndiratta opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Olenbeck provided a detailed breakdown of revenue streams, cost structures, and forward-looking guidance for fiscal year 2024.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2 percent compared to the prior fiscal year.
Product revenue accounted for $612.8 million of the total, driven primarily by continued adoption of the company's flagship therapeutic, Veltrexin.
Licensing and royalty income contributed an additional $234.5 million, reflecting the expansion of partnership agreements with three major pharmaceutical distributors.
Gross profit for the period was $531.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 62.8 percent, an improvement of 310 basis points over fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $389.2 million, with research and development expenditures comprising the largest single component at $198.4 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $190.8 million, reflecting targeted investments in commercial infrastructure and regulatory affairs.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $142.4 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.8 percent.
Veltara Biosystems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in March of that year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $118.7 million, equivalent to diluted earnings per share of $2.14.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1 percent, in line with management's earlier guidance range of 21 to 23 percent.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $403.9 million, up from $321.6 million reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Short-term investments, primarily in U.S. Treasury securities and money market instruments, amounted to $157.2 million as of December 31, 2023.
Total current assets were $689.4 million, while total current liabilities stood at $204.7 million, implying a current ratio of approximately 3.37.
Capital expenditures for the year were $47.6 million, focused largely on expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The Board of Directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $200 million in October 2023, of which $62.4 million had been utilized by year-end.
Headcount grew to 3,412 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, compared to 2,987 at the same date in the prior year.
The increase in workforce was concentrated in clinical development, regulatory affairs, and commercial operations, consistent with the company's pipeline expansion strategy.
Veltara's lead pipeline candidate, VB-402, successfully completed Phase II clinical trials in September 2023, demonstrating statistically significant improvements in the primary endpoint.
VB-402 targets a rare lysosomal storage disorder affecting an estimated 45,000 patients in North America and Western Europe.

Management submitted the New Drug Application for VB-402 to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in November 2023, with a Prescription Drug User Fee Act target action date in the third quarter of 2024.
In parallel, VB-517, a next-generation variant of the enzyme platform, entered Phase I trials in June 2023 with initial safety data expected in the second quarter of 2024.
International revenue grew by 21.4 percent year-over-year, with Europe and Asia-Pacific accounting for 38.7 percent of total product revenue.
The company secured a co-promotion agreement with Hargreaves Pharma GmbH in Germany, expanding Veltrexin's commercial presence across seven European markets.
Veltara's proprietary enzyme stabilization technology received two additional patents during the year, bringing the total active patent estate to 64 granted patents.
Revenue generated from the Asia-Pacific region increased by 34.2 percent, driven by new reimbursement approvals in Japan and South Korea.
The company recognized a non-cash impairment charge of $8.3 million related to certain legacy equipment no longer aligned with its manufacturing modernization initiative.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $179.8 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.2 percent.
Management defines adjusted EBITDA as net income before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization, stock-based compensation, and non-recurring items.
Stock-based compensation expense for the year totaled $28.6 million, primarily associated with performance-based restricted stock units granted to senior leadership.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $43.1 million, representing upfront payments received under milestone-based licensing agreements not yet recognized as revenue.
The company's Days Sales Outstanding metric improved to 41 days in fiscal year 2023, compared to 53 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting tighter credit management policies.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023, were $88.4 million, representing approximately 4.2 months of anticipated forward demand for Veltrexin.
Veltara's manufacturing yields improved by approximately 8 percentage points during the year following the completion of a process optimization initiative launched in early 2022.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitmore LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Ernst & Whitmore LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion, noting no material weaknesses in the company's internal controls over financial reporting.
Dr. Mehndiratta noted that fiscal year 2024 will be pivotal, contingent upon a positive regulatory outcome for VB-402 and the continued ramp of Veltrexin in newer markets.
Revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 was established in the range of $930 million to $970 million, implying approximately 10 to 15 percent growth over the reported fiscal year 2023 base.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17 to 19 percent, reflecting anticipated investments in commercial readiness for a potential VB-402 launch.
The company does not currently pay a common stock dividend, with capital allocation priorities focused on internal investment and opportunistic share repurchases.
Veltara's Board of Directors includes eight independent directors, providing robust governance oversight across audit, compensation, and science and technology committees.
Compensation committee chair Dr. Florence Watabe led a comprehensive review of executive pay structures in the fourth quarter of 2023, resulting in recalibrated performance metrics for 2024.
The company disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements, contingent liabilities, or related-party transactions requiring separate disclosure under applicable accounting standards.
Veltara holds a 340B Drug Pricing Program contract with the Health Resources and Services Administration, supporting patient access to Veltrexin at reduced pricing for qualifying institutions.
Supply chain diversification efforts led to the qualification of a second contract manufacturing organization in Singapore during the third quarter of 2023.
Foreign exchange headwinds reduced reported revenue by approximately $19.4 million compared to constant-currency results, primarily due to euro and yen depreciation against the U.S. dollar.
The company reaffirmed its commitment to environmental, social, and governance objectives, targeting a 30 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
Veltara's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $4.1 billion, reflecting a 27.3 percent appreciation in the company's share price over the course of the fiscal year.

In the first quarter of 2024, Veltara entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by Meridian Capital Group, the proceeds of which were designated to fund the commercial launch of VB-402 and broader pipeline acceleration initiatives.

Management expressed confidence that the strategic investments outlined in this report, combined with a maturing commercial portfolio, will position Veltara Biosystems as a premier mid-cap biotechnology company over the next five-year planning horizon.
Meridian Specialty Coatings, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the industrial coatings and surface protection sector, serving clients across aerospace, automotive, and marine industries.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Vellacourt opened her remarks by noting a year of disciplined execution and strategic expansion.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Specialty Coatings carried absolutely no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a policy of conservative financial management.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margin expanded to 38.7%, up from 35.2% in fiscal year 2022, driven by favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Darren Okafor attributed the margin improvement to disciplined procurement and a reduction in solvent input costs.
Operating income for the year came in at $163.5 million, compared with $134.8 million in 2022.
The company's EBITDA reached $201.2 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 23.8%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $164.7 million, or roughly 19.4% of total revenues.
Research and development expenditures increased to $42.1 million, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation in low-VOC coating technologies.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $118.6 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $3.47.
This compares favorably with net income of $94.2 million and EPS of $2.76 reported in fiscal year 2022.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the prior year's payout.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $58.4 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Greenville, South Carolina facility.
The Greenville expansion added approximately 180,000 square feet of production space and is expected to be fully operational by mid-2024.
Free cash flow for the year was $142.8 million, providing ample flexibility for reinvestment and shareholder returns.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $41.30 per share.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $49.6 million under the existing $150 million buyback authorization.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $213.7 million, up from $178.4 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $112.3 million, reflecting the higher revenue base while days sales outstanding remained stable at 48 days.
Inventory levels were managed efficiently, with total inventory of $94.6 million and inventory turnover improving to 6.8 times.
The aerospace coatings segment generated revenues of $312.4 million, representing 36.9% of total company revenues.
The automotive coatings segment contributed $298.7 million, or 35.3% of total revenues, benefiting from strong OEM demand in North America.
Marine and industrial coatings rounded out the portfolio at $236.2 million, or 27.9% of total revenues.
Geographic diversification remained a strategic priority, with North America accounting for 54% of revenues, Europe 28%, and Asia-Pacific 18%.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Sundaram highlighted the successful rollout of the NanoShield Pro product line across twelve new customer accounts in Europe.
NanoShield Pro, the company's flagship anti-corrosion coating, received certification from the European Aviation Safety Agency in March 2023.
The certification is expected to accelerate adoption in the European aerospace market over the next three to five years.
Meridian also entered into a strategic distribution agreement with Korsel Industrial Partners in South Korea, expanding its Asia-Pacific footprint.
The South Korea agreement is projected to contribute incremental revenues of approximately $18 million to $22 million beginning in fiscal 2024.
Headcount grew to 4,312 full-time employees at year-end, an increase of 287 employees compared with December 31, 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing operations and technical sales roles across all three product segments.
Employee turnover remained low at 8.3%, well below the industry average of approximately 13%, underscoring Meridian's strong workplace culture.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's throughout the fiscal year.
Environmental compliance costs totaled $11.2 million, as the company continued to invest in sustainable manufacturing practices.
Meridian reduced its greenhouse gas emissions intensity by 7.1% in 2023, advancing toward its 2030 net-zero commitment.
The company recorded a one-time restructuring charge of $4.8 million related to the consolidation of two smaller coating facilities in the Midwest.
Excluding the restructuring charge, adjusted operating income would have been $168.3 million for the fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.4%, due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 stood at $1.14 billion, up from $1.02 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Shareholders' equity increased to $687.4 million, reflecting accumulated earnings net of dividends and share repurchase activity.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 17.2%, compared with 14.9% in the prior year, demonstrating improved capital efficiency.
In October 2023, Meridian Specialty Coatings secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to support its ongoing capital investment program.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management expects total revenues in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying growth of approximately 8% to 12%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $215 million to $225 million, reflecting continued margin discipline.
Management cautioned that raw material price volatility, particularly in titanium dioxide and specialty resins, could pressure margins in the near term.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at $189.5 million, providing strong revenue visibility entering the new fiscal year.
Sandra Vellacourt emphasized that Meridian's innovation pipeline, including four products in late-stage development, positions the company well for long-term growth.
The Board also approved an increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.25 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Darren Okafor noted that capital allocation priorities would balance organic investment, potential bolt-on acquisitions, and continued shareholder returns.
Meridian's management team expressed confidence that the company's differentiated product portfolio and global distribution capabilities would sustain its competitive advantages.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected herein.
Veltoran Systems Inc. is a mid-sized enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain management solutions.
Founded in 2009, Veltoran has grown from a regional vendor into a globally recognized platform provider serving clients across 34 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Albright presented the annual financial results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Kowalski noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point in the company's long-term growth trajectory.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $412.7 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 18.4 percent compared to fiscal year 2022.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 74 percent of total revenue, reflecting the company's continued shift away from one-time licensing fees.
Professional services revenue grew by 9.2 percent to $67.3 million, driven by increased demand for implementation and customization engagements.
Gross profit for the year stood at $298.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 72.3 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $241.6 million, with research and development consuming the largest share at $89.2 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $94.7 million, reflecting a deliberate push to expand the company's enterprise customer base in North America and Europe.
General and administrative costs came in at $57.7 million, slightly above the prior year due to legal fees associated with a vendor contract dispute.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $56.8 million, translating to an operating margin of 13.8 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $41.2 million, or $1.04 per diluted share, compared to $0.87 per diluted share in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $78.5 million, a 22.1 percent improvement over the $64.3 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Veltoran ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding term loan balance of $30 million in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $183.6 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
The company generated $94.1 million in operating cash flow, representing a cash conversion rate of approximately 120 percent of net income.
Capital expenditures were $18.4 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements at the new Austin campus.
Free cash flow for the year was $75.7 million, a record high for Veltoran since its initial public offering in 2016.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $50 million, which the company began executing in the third quarter of 2023.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltoran repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $27.43 per share.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during the fiscal year, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
Customer count grew to 3,847 at year-end, up from 3,214 at the close of fiscal year 2022, representing a net addition of 633 new customers.
Annual contract value per enterprise customer increased by 11.3 percent, reaching an average of $184,000 across the top-tier customer cohort.
Net revenue retention rate for the fiscal year was 114 percent, indicating robust expansion within the existing customer base.
Veltoran's flagship product, the Nexora Supply Intelligence Platform, received its most significant feature update in October 2023, branded as Release 7.0.
Release 7.0 introduced AI-driven demand forecasting capabilities, allowing customers to reduce inventory holding costs by an estimated 12 to 18 percent.
The company filed seven new patents during fiscal year 2023 related to machine learning algorithms embedded within the Nexora platform.
Headcount at December 31, 2023 stood at 2,104 full-time employees, up from 1,876 at the same date in the prior year.
The engineering and product development teams accounted for 41 percent of total headcount, reflecting Veltoran's technology-first organizational philosophy.
Employee attrition for fiscal year 2023 was 11.7 percent, below the software industry benchmark of 14.2 percent cited in a recent Gartner survey.
Chief People Officer Sandra Merritt attributed the lower attrition rate to enhanced equity compensation packages introduced in early 2023.
Veltoran opened two new regional offices during the year, one in Amsterdam and one in Singapore, to support accelerating demand in EMEA and Asia-Pacific.
The Amsterdam office currently employs 63 staff members focused on European enterprise sales and post-sales customer success functions.
The Singapore office serves as the Asia-Pacific hub and began operations in September 2023 with an initial team of 28 professionals.
Revenue from international markets reached $128.4 million, representing 31.1 percent of total revenue, up from 26.7 percent in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, posting revenue growth of 34.6 percent year-over-year.
Veltoran's partnership ecosystem expanded to include 47 certified implementation partners globally, up from 31 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Strategic alliances with SAP and Oracle were renewed for three-year terms in mid-2023, reinforcing Veltoran's position in the enterprise middleware segment.
The company's market share in the cloud-based supply chain management segment increased to an estimated 6.8 percent, according to IDC's 2023 market analysis.
This positions Veltoran as the fourth-largest vendor in its addressable market, trailing only BluePrism Logistics, Kinaxis, and E2open.
Diane Kowalski provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of total revenue between $480 million and $495 million, implying growth of approximately 16 to 20 percent.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 14.5 to 15.5 percent, reflecting operating leverage as the cost structure scales.
The company anticipates headcount growth of approximately 12 percent in fiscal year 2024, with hiring concentrated in engineering and customer success roles.
Veltoran also plans to increase research and development investment to between $105 million and $112 million in fiscal year 2024.
Marcus Albright emphasized that the company's balance sheet strength would allow it to pursue selective bolt-on acquisitions in the data analytics space.
The board approved a targeted acquisition budget of up to $60 million for fiscal year 2024, funded entirely from existing cash reserves.
Veltoran's audit committee engaged Deloitte & Touche as the independent external auditor for fiscal year 2023, replacing the prior auditor KPMG after a competitive review.
Deloitte issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements, finding no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting standards retroactively and restated prior-period balance sheets accordingly, adding $22.1 million in right-of-use assets.
Effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 20.8 percent, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable adjustments related to prior-year state tax filings.
Veltoran's stock price appreciated 38.2 percent during calendar year 2023, outperforming the iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF benchmark by 14.6 percentage points.
The investor relations team hosted its inaugural Capital Markets Day in November 2023, drawing over 200 institutional investors and sell-side analysts.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that the company's innovation pipeline and expanding partner network would sustain double-digit revenue growth through 2026.

In the first quarter of fiscal year 2024, Veltoran secured a $150 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five banks, establishing a new long-term borrowing arrangement on its balance sheet.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Halberd opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's operational resilience and long-term strategic positioning.
Veltrano Dynamics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a deliberate capital structure policy pursued over the prior five years.
The company reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nandakumar attributed the revenue growth primarily to expanded contracts with commercial satellite operators and defense prime contractors.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8%.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $298 million.
Operating income therefore came in at $214 million, up from $187 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $161 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 24.7%, consistent with management's prior guidance range of 23% to 26%.
Veltrano Dynamics generated $229 million in operating cash flow, a record for the company in its twenty-three-year history.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $88 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Tucson and Albuquerque facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow minus capital expenditures, was therefore $141 million.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This dividend level represents a 12.5% increase compared to the quarterly dividend paid throughout fiscal year 2022.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 6,840 full-time equivalent employees, up from 6,210 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The addition of approximately 630 employees was largely driven by new program wins in the reusable launch vehicle subsystem segment.
Veltrano Dynamics' backlog at December 31, 2023, reached a record $4.7 billion, providing strong forward revenue visibility.
The backlog growth was fueled by $2.1 billion in new contract awards received during the fourth quarter alone.
Chief Operating Officer Serena Whitfield noted that on-time delivery performance improved to 94.2% in 2023, up from 91.8% in 2022.
The company's proprietary ThermaCore composite material platform received three additional aerospace certifications during the year.
These certifications open new addressable markets estimated at $900 million in potential annual contract value over the next decade.
Veltrano Dynamics invested $74 million in research and development during fiscal year 2023, representing 4.0% of total revenue.
This R&D intensity is consistent with the company's five-year strategic plan, which targets sustained investment of between 3.5% and 4.5% of revenue.
The Albuquerque facility added a second precision machining line in October 2023, increasing throughput capacity by approximately 18%.
Management indicated that the Albuquerque expansion is expected to contribute meaningfully to revenue beginning in the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics' largest customer, Orion Aerospace Partners, accounted for 21% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
Concentration risk remains a monitored item, as the top three customers collectively represented 48% of consolidated revenue.
Despite this concentration, management expressed confidence that contract renewal discussions with all three customers are progressing constructively.
The company's inventory balance at year-end was $317 million, reflecting higher raw material purchases to support anticipated production ramp in early 2024.
Days inventory outstanding increased modestly to 63 days from 58 days the prior year, consistent with the planned inventory build.
Accounts receivable totaled $204 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of approximately 40 days.
The receivables balance includes $18 million related to a milestone billing dispute with a government customer, which management expects to resolve in the first half of 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained a cash and equivalents balance of $388 million at year-end, a significant liquidity cushion.
Management reiterated its capital allocation priorities: organic investment first, followed by selective acquisitions, and finally shareholder returns.
During fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock for a total consideration of $67 million.
The share repurchase program authorized by the board in February 2023 had an original limit of $150 million, leaving $83 million of capacity remaining.
Veltrano Dynamics also made a bolt-on acquisition of precision sensor startup Caelix Technologies in August 2023 for $41 million in cash.
Caelix Technologies contributed approximately $9 million of revenue and a small operating loss during the partial-year consolidation period.
Integration costs associated with the Caelix acquisition were $4 million and are reported within selling, general and administrative expenses.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance of $1.95 billion to $2.05 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 11%.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at a range of $3.60 to $3.85, reflecting continued margin improvement expectations.

In order to fund an accelerated production ramp and a transformative strategic acquisition pipeline, Veltrano Dynamics entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility during fiscal year 2023.
The company expects adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 to fall between $310 million and $330 million.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $95 million to $110 million, reflecting continued facility investment.
CFO Priya Nandakumar noted that the company's investment-grade credit profile supports favorable borrowing terms across market cycles.
Veltrano Dynamics also announced a new environmental, social, and governance initiative targeting a 30% reduction in scope one and scope two emissions by 2030.
The ESG program is overseen by a newly formed sustainability committee of the board, chaired by independent director Dr. Lena Forsythe.
The company achieved a 7% reduction in energy intensity per unit of production during fiscal year 2023, ahead of its internal target of 5%.
Veltrano Dynamics' workforce diversity metrics showed that women represented 34% of total employees and 28% of senior leadership roles at year-end.
The company received a score of 82 out of 100 on a leading independent supplier ethics assessment, up from 76 the prior year.
Segment reporting reflects two primary business units: Structural Assemblies and Electronic Subsystems.
The Structural Assemblies segment generated $1.10 billion in revenue, while the Electronic Subsystems segment contributed $740 million.
Electronic Subsystems segment operating margin of 13.2% outpaced the Structural Assemblies segment margin of 10.8%.
CEO Marcus Halberd described the Electronic Subsystems growth trajectory as a central pillar of the company's long-term value creation thesis.
Veltrano Dynamics plans to host an investor day in the second quarter of fiscal year 2024 to provide detailed long-range financial targets.
The report concludes with a statement affirming management's commitment to disciplined capital stewardship and sustainable growth for all stakeholders.
Independent auditor Thorngate & Wills LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltrano Dynamics' consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings, Inc. released its annual financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals and biotechnology sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Sylvia Hartmann expressed confidence in the company's strategic positioning entering the new fiscal year.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Mr. Leonard Osei noted that operating margins improved by 180 basis points year-over-year.
The company's flagship oncology product, Varelixon, generated $312.6 million in net product sales during the period.
Meridian Biopharma also saw strong performance in its rare disease segment, contributing $198.4 million to total revenues.
Research and development expenditures totaled $203.7 million, reflecting the company's commitment to pipeline development.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose modestly to $154.2 million from $141.8 million in the prior year.
The company reported net income attributable to common shareholders of $112.5 million for fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Biopharma ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the year.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $2.87, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $2.71.
Operating cash flow for the year was $189.4 million, a record high for the company since its founding.
Capital expenditures totaled $47.2 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansions at the Durham facility.
The company's cash and cash equivalents balance stood at $423.1 million as of December 31, 2023.
Dr. Hartmann credited the performance to disciplined execution across commercial, medical, and operational functions.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Meridian Biopharma repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of common stock during the fiscal year under its existing buyback authorization.
The total cost of share repurchases was $94.7 million, at an average price of $45.10 per share.
Gross margin for the period was 71.3%, compared to 69.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Mr. Osei attributed gross margin improvement to favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies achieved throughout the year.
The company's oncology pipeline advanced significantly, with three compounds moving into Phase II clinical trials.
Meridian Biopharma's investigational asset MBP-441 received Fast Track Designation from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in March 2023.
The rare disease segment benefited from increased patient uptake of Genolara, the company's enzyme replacement therapy approved in 2021.
International revenues grew 18.7% to $167.3 million, driven largely by expansion in European and Japanese markets.
The company established a new commercial presence in Germany and France during the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
Headcount at year-end totaled 3,412 full-time employees, an increase of 287 from the prior year-end figure.
The addition of personnel was concentrated in clinical development, regulatory affairs, and commercial operations.
Meridian Biopharma entered into a collaboration agreement with Takeda Pharmaceutical in October 2023 for the co-development of MBP-809.
Under the terms of the agreement, Meridian received an upfront payment of $75 million and is eligible for up to $420 million in milestone payments.
The Takeda collaboration is expected to contribute meaningfully to research cost offsets beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Hartmann stated that the company's geographic diversification strategy remains a central pillar of its long-term growth plan.
Meridian Biopharma's inventory balance at year-end was $88.6 million, reflecting strategic safety stock build for anticipated demand increases.
Accounts receivable net of allowances stood at $134.7 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 58 days.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.2%, benefiting from research tax credits and deductions related to qualified clinical expenses.
Total assets on the balance sheet reached $1.64 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities were $497.8 million, resulting in a stockholders' equity balance of approximately $1.14 billion.
The company maintained compliance with all applicable covenants under its revolving credit facility throughout the year.
Depreciation and amortization charges for the year were $61.4 million, including amortization of acquired intangible assets.
Meridian Biopharma completed the integration of NovaCyte Therapeutics, which it acquired in late fiscal year 2022 for $385 million.
The NovaCyte integration is expected to yield approximately $28 million in annualized cost synergies by the end of fiscal 2024.
Dr. Hartmann commented that the integration proceeded ahead of schedule and under budget.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by S&P Global Ratings and Baa1 by Moody's during the fourth quarter.
Meridian Biopharma issued its first environmental, social, and governance report in conjunction with its annual filing.
The ESG report outlined targets for a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
Mr. Osei highlighted that the company's investment-grade ratings provide flexibility for future strategic transactions.
For fiscal year 2024, the company issued guidance for total revenues in the range of $910 million to $940 million.
The 2024 guidance reflects expected continued growth from Varelixon and Genolara, partially offset by anticipated generic entry for an older asset.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $295 million to $315 million.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic headwinds, including persistent inflationary pressures, could present challenges to cost management.
During fiscal year 2023, Meridian Biopharma secured a $300 million senior secured term loan to fund expansion of its biologics manufacturing platform.
The biologics manufacturing expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 35% by mid-2025.
Meridian Biopharma's audit committee completed its annual review of internal controls with no material weaknesses identified.
The company's independent auditor, Ernst & Young LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
Varelixon received regulatory approval in Japan in November 2023, opening a significant new commercial market for the product.
The Japanese approval is expected to contribute incremental revenues of approximately $30 million to $45 million in fiscal year 2024.
Meridian Biopharma's pipeline currently comprises 14 active programs across various stages of clinical development.
The company expects to initiate two additional Phase III trials in the first half of fiscal year 2024, pending regulatory clearances.
Mr. Osei confirmed that the company's capital allocation framework prioritizes internal investment, business development, and return of capital to shareholders.
Total return to shareholders through dividends and buybacks in fiscal year 2023 amounted to approximately $136 million.
Looking ahead, Dr. Hartmann expressed confidence that Meridian Biopharma is positioned to deliver sustainable value creation across its therapeutic franchises.
The company plans to host an Investor Day in the second quarter of fiscal 2024 to provide updates on its long-range financial plan.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expectations.
Investors are encouraged to review the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for complete disclosures.
Veltrano Systems Inc. is a mid-sized enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain management solutions.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Miranda Ashford and CTO Deron Khalil, who together built the platform from a two-person startup into a publicly traded firm.
Veltrano Systems reported total revenue of $412 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue accounted for $318 million of that total, underscoring the company's successful pivot toward recurring revenue streams.
Professional services revenue contributed an additional $94 million, largely driven by implementation contracts with new enterprise clients.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $271 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 65.8%.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales and marketing, and general administrative costs, totaled $198 million.
The resulting operating income of $73 million marked a significant improvement from the $51 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 stood at $89 million, which the company cited as evidence of improved operational leverage.
Veltrano Systems concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding credit facility in March 2023.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $54 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.38.
The company ended the year with $187 million in cash and cash equivalents, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the period.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $76 million, up from $48 million in the prior year.
CFO Patricia Yuen noted during the earnings call that disciplined cost management was the primary driver of the margin expansion seen this year.
Veltrano Systems added 217 net new enterprise customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total enterprise customer count to 1,842.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, grew to $341 million as of December 31, 2023, a 17.2% increase year-over-year.
Net revenue retention rate for the period was 118%, indicating that existing customers expanded their usage significantly.
The company's flagship product, VeltraChain Pro, saw its user base grow to over 290,000 active seats across all customer tiers.
VeltraChain Pro's newly released predictive analytics module was cited by 64% of surveyed customers as a key reason for their subscription expansion.
Veltrano also launched a complementary product, VeltraLogix, in Q2 2023, targeting mid-market logistics providers.
VeltraLogix reached 310 paying customers within six months of its launch, exceeding the company's internal target of 250.
The sales cycle for VeltraLogix averaged 47 days, considerably shorter than the 120-day average for VeltraChain Pro enterprise deals.
International revenue grew to $98 million, representing 23.8% of total revenue, up from 19.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenue increasing 31% year-over-year to $52 million.
Veltrano established a new office in Amsterdam in Q3 2023 to support its EMEA expansion efforts.
The APAC region contributed $29 million in revenue, with particular strength in Australia and Japan.
Headcount at year-end stood at 2,104 full-time employees, up from 1,876 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company added 228 employees during the year, with the largest cohort hired into engineering and product development roles.
Employee-related expenses totaled $143 million, accounting for approximately 72% of total operating expenses.
The R&D team, now comprising 541 engineers, released four major product updates and 23 minor releases throughout fiscal year 2023.
Chief Product Officer Sanjay Tremaine indicated that the 2024 roadmap would focus heavily on AI-driven automation features.
Sales and marketing expenses were $62 million, representing 15.1% of total revenue, down from 17.4% in the prior year.
The improvement in sales and marketing efficiency was partly attributed to a revamped demand generation program launched in Q1 2023.
General and administrative expenses were $29 million, flat year-over-year in absolute terms despite the significant increase in headcount.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $50 million in October 2023, effective through December 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano had repurchased approximately 1.1 million shares under the program at an average price of $31.40.
The company does not currently pay a cash dividend, and the board indicated it has no plans to initiate one in the near term.
Customer churn by annual contract value was 6.2% for the year, an improvement from 7.8% in fiscal year 2022.
The top ten customers collectively accounted for 18.4% of total revenue, demonstrating a well-diversified customer base.
Veltrano's largest single customer, a global consumer goods company, represented 3.1% of total revenue.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with DataVault Corp. in Q4 2023 to integrate real-time inventory intelligence into VeltraChain Pro.
Under the partnership, DataVault's enrichment layer is expected to contribute incremental ARR of approximately $8 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Miranda Ashford, commenting on the partnership, emphasized that it would meaningfully differentiate Veltrano's platform in competitive deal cycles.
Veltrano's market share in the North American cloud supply chain management segment increased to an estimated 8.7% in 2023, up from 7.4% in 2022.
Independent analyst firm Caldwell Research ranked Veltrano as the third-largest vendor in its category by revenue for the second consecutive year.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $468 million to $476 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $98 million to $104 million, implying continued margin improvement.
Management expects the international mix of revenue to approach 27% in fiscal year 2024, driven primarily by EMEA growth.
Deron Khalil reaffirmed the company's commitment to investing in platform security, noting that Veltrano achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in November 2023.
In a strategic review completed in January 2024, the board authorized Veltrano to pursue a $150 million syndicated term loan to fund potential acquisition activity.
The company's customer satisfaction score, as measured by its annual NPS survey, reached 67 in fiscal year 2023, up from 59 the prior year.
Veltrano Systems believes its combination of platform breadth, expanding partner ecosystem, and improving unit economics positions it well for sustained growth in the years ahead.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's operational resilience and long-term strategic positioning.
Veltrano Dynamics operates across three primary segments: precision robotics, sensor integration, and aftermarket services.
The company serves clients in the automotive, aerospace, and food processing industries across 22 countries.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in March of that year.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.4 percent.
The precision robotics segment contributed $612 million to total revenue, up from $541 million in the prior year.
Sensor integration revenues grew by 9.2 percent to reach $398 million, driven by strong demand from automotive OEM clients.
The aftermarket services segment posted revenues of $460 million, reflecting increased maintenance contracts across the existing installed base.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $534 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.3 percent.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Restrepo attributed the margin improvement to favorable raw material pricing and enhanced manufacturing efficiency.
Operating expenses totaled $312 million, including $94 million in research and development expenditures.
Selling, general, and administrative costs accounted for $218 million of total operating expenses during the period.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $222 million, compared to $189 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $161 million, equivalent to $3.84 per diluted share.
This represents a 17.5 percent increase in earnings per diluted share compared to the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $298 million, reflecting an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.3 percent.
The company generated $241 million in free cash flow during fiscal year 2023, an increase of $38 million versus the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $57 million, predominantly allocated toward expanding manufacturing capacity in the Columbus and Dresden facilities.
Veltrano Dynamics repurchased 2.1 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $47.60 per share, totaling approximately $100 million.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.88 per share.
This marks the seventh consecutive year in which Veltrano Dynamics has increased its annual dividend per share.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 8,340 full-time employees, an increase of 420 from the prior year-end figure.
The company added 310 employees in the precision robotics division to support accelerating demand for collaborative robot systems.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and equity compensation, totaled $389 million for fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics launched its next-generation VX-9 sensor platform in the second quarter of 2023, targeting high-precision industrial applications.
The VX-9 platform received regulatory certification in the European Union in July 2023, enabling commercial sales across EU member states.
By year-end, the VX-9 had secured purchase orders from 47 clients representing aggregate contract value of $83 million.
Margaret Holloway described the VX-9 launch as the most significant product introduction in the company's 34-year history.
The company's backlog as of December 31, 2023, stood at $892 million, a record high and an increase of $154 million from the prior year-end.
Geographically, North American revenues accounted for 54 percent of total consolidated revenues in fiscal year 2023.
European revenues represented 29 percent of total revenues, while Asia-Pacific contributed the remaining 17 percent.
The company's Dresden, Germany facility achieved ISO 9001:2015 recertification in November 2023 following a comprehensive audit.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout the fiscal year.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.4, reflecting a healthy liquidity position relative to short-term obligations.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, amounted to $317 million, compared to $214 million at the prior year-end.
Days sales outstanding improved to 48 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened collections processes.
Inventory turnover improved to 5.8 times from 5.1 times in fiscal year 2022, indicating more efficient management of raw materials and work-in-progress.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 19.6 percent, up from 17.2 percent in the prior year.
Return on invested capital reached 14.8 percent, exceeding the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.3 percent.
Daniel Restrepo noted that strong working capital discipline was a key driver of the year's free cash flow performance.
Veltrano Dynamics made a strategic acquisition of PerciSense Technologies, a sensor calibration software firm, for $44 million in October 2023.
The PerciSense acquisition is expected to enhance the company's software capabilities within the sensor integration segment starting in fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics has received no material litigation claims that would adversely affect its financial position as of the report date.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano Dynamics entered into a $350 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of five lending institutions, establishing new long-term borrowing capacity.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management is guiding total revenue in the range of $1.58 billion to $1.63 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set between $315 million and $330 million.
Management expects capital expenditures of approximately $70 million to $80 million in fiscal year 2024 to support continued capacity expansion.
The board authorized an incremental share repurchase program of up to $150 million effective January 1, 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics intends to pursue selective bolt-on acquisitions in the sensor and software domains over the next 18 months.
The company expects its effective tax rate to remain in the range of 22 to 24 percent in fiscal year 2024.
Margaret Holloway concluded the report by reaffirming the company's commitment to sustainable growth, disciplined capital allocation, and delivering long-term value to shareholders.
Velarion Technologies Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Velarion Technologies is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2011 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Elwood and Chief Technology Officer Priya Shankar.
Over the past fiscal year, Velarion has expanded its customer base across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $426.7 million.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 73% of total revenue, up from 68% in fiscal year 2022.
Velarion closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the last tranche of its 2019 term loan in March 2023.
Gross profit for the year was $312.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 64.1%, consistent with management's stated target range of 63% to 66%.
Operating expenses totaled $241.8 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures rose to $78.2 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in artificial intelligence-driven logistics modules.
Sales and marketing spend was $103.6 million, representing 21.3% of total revenue and slightly below the prior year's 22.1%.
General and administrative expenses were $59.9 million, roughly flat year-over-year as the company benefited from operational efficiencies.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $70.6 million, compared to $54.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
The operating margin expanded by 210 basis points to 14.5%, driven by revenue scale and disciplined cost management.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $52.3 million, or $1.47 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $38.9 million, or $1.11 per diluted share, recorded in the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $94.7 million, representing a 19.4% adjusted EBITDA margin.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.8%, modestly higher than the 21.5% rate recorded in 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Diana Kowalczyk noted that the tax rate increase was primarily attributable to lower R&D tax credits recognized in the period.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $163.2 million, up from $134.5 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $81.4 million, reflecting strong collections and working capital discipline.
Days sales outstanding decreased to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, indicating improved receivables management.
Capital expenditures were $12.3 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements.
During fiscal year 2023, Velarion repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $38.60 per share.
The board of directors authorized an incremental $50 million share repurchase program in November 2023, effective through December 2025.
Velarion's total headcount at December 31, 2023 was 2,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 employees from the prior year-end.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in engineering and customer success roles, consistent with the company's growth strategy.
Employee-related costs, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $186.9 million for the year.
Stock-based compensation expense was $22.4 million, representing approximately 4.6% of total revenue.
The company launched its next-generation platform, VelarionOS 4.0, in the second quarter of 2023, receiving strong market reception.
VelarionOS 4.0 introduced real-time demand sensing capabilities, enabling customers to reduce inventory holding costs by an average of 18%.
As of year-end, approximately 61% of the existing customer base had migrated to the VelarionOS 4.0 platform.
Net revenue retention rate for the year was 118%, indicating strong expansion revenue from existing customers.
Customer churn for fiscal year 2023 was 4.3%, down from 5.1% in the prior year, reflecting improved customer satisfaction metrics.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, grew to $378.6 million at December 31, 2023, up 19.8% from $316.1 million at the prior year-end.
The company added 214 net new customers during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total customer count to 1,638.
Average contract value increased to $231,000, up from $207,000 in the prior year, reflecting successful upsell and cross-sell motions.
Velarion's enterprise segment, defined as customers with ARR above $500,000, grew to represent 44% of total ARR, up from 37% in 2022.
Chief Revenue Officer Stefan Beaumont attributed the enterprise segment growth to a targeted key-account strategy launched in early 2023.
The international revenue mix improved to 31% of total revenue, up from 26% in fiscal year 2022, as European and APAC markets gained momentum.
Velarion established a new regional office in Singapore during the third quarter of 2023 to support its Asia-Pacific expansion efforts.
The company also expanded its London-based team from 45 to 78 employees to meet growing demand across the European Union.
Gross revenue retention remained above 95% for the fourth consecutive year, demonstrating the mission-critical nature of Velarion's platform.
The balance sheet at December 31, 2023 showed total assets of $624.7 million and total liabilities of $198.4 million.
Stockholders' equity grew to $426.3 million from $381.9 million at the prior year-end, driven primarily by net income and equity compensation activity.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet totaled $112.8 million, reflecting the company's growing base of multi-year subscription contracts.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Velarion successfully secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of four investment-grade banks.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 9.2% as of year-end, according to the company's internal treasury analysis.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for total revenue in the range of $548 million to $562 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $108 million to $115 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company expects to invest approximately $90 million in research and development in fiscal year 2024, a meaningful step-up from 2023 levels.
Management anticipates headcount will grow by approximately 280 to 320 employees during fiscal year 2024, primarily in product and go-to-market functions.
Velarion remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value for its shareholders, customers, and the broader supply chain ecosystem.
Velarion Technologies Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and data analytics firm headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delwyn and CTO Priya Shankar, serves clients across healthcare, logistics, and financial services sectors.
Velarion's consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year's $741.9 million.
Gross profit for the year stood at $512.6 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 60.5%, up from 58.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income improved to $143.8 million compared to $118.4 million in the prior period, driven by operating leverage in the company's SaaS segment.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal year 2023 was $97.2 million, yielding basic earnings per share of $2.14.
The company ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents totaling $312.5 million, compared to $278.1 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Velarion's research and development expenditures increased to $134.7 million, representing 15.9% of total revenue, reflecting the firm's commitment to innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses rose modestly to $98.4 million, while general and administrative costs were held at $92.1 million through disciplined cost management.
The company's annualized recurring revenue, or ARR, grew to $623.8 million by year-end, a 19.7% increase from $521.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
Customer retention rate remained strong at 91.3%, and net revenue retention reached 108.2%, indicating healthy upsell and expansion activity within the existing customer base.
Velarion expanded its enterprise client count to 2,847 by December 31, 2023, up from 2,391 at the same point in the prior year.
The company's flagship product, VelaCore Analytics Suite version 8.0, was launched in March 2023 and achieved rapid adoption across the logistics vertical.
VelaCore 8.0 introduced machine-learning-powered anomaly detection features that reduced average client onboarding time by 31% compared to the prior version.
Chief Financial Officer Anita Corbridge noted that the company's balance sheet remained notably conservative throughout the fiscal year.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 stood at $1.42 billion, with goodwill and intangible assets comprising $389.4 million of that total.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days, reflecting enhanced collection processes implemented in the second quarter.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $41.3 million, primarily allocated toward data center infrastructure upgrades and office expansions in Denver and Dublin.
Free cash flow for the year was $176.9 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $218.2 million less capital expenditures of $41.3 million.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program in July 2023, authorizing the buyback of up to $150 million in common stock over 24 months.
By December 31, 2023, Velarion had repurchased approximately $62.4 million worth of shares under this authorization, retiring 1.8 million shares at an average price of $34.67.
The company's total equity stood at $684.2 million at year-end, reflecting retained earnings growth and the partial offset from the share repurchase activity.
Velarion carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its prior credit facility in early 2022.
The company's international revenue grew to $231.6 million, representing 27.3% of total revenue, with the EMEA region accounting for the largest share of overseas sales.
Velarion's EMEA operations, led by Regional President Florian Meister, generated $142.8 million in revenue, up 22.1% from the prior year.
The Asia-Pacific segment contributed $88.8 million in revenue, driven by new contract wins in Singapore and Japan during the second half of the year.
Headcount at year-end totaled 4,312 full-time employees globally, compared to 3,987 at the close of fiscal year 2022, a net addition of 325 employees.
The engineering and product development teams accounted for 41% of total headcount, or approximately 1,768 employees, reinforcing the company's technology-first culture.
Velarion's employee satisfaction score, as measured by its internal annual engagement survey, reached 78 out of 100, compared to 74 in the prior year.
Attrition in fiscal year 2023 fell to 11.4%, down from 14.7% in fiscal year 2022, a trend management attributed to enhanced compensation packages and career development programs.
The company's healthcare analytics division, HealthVela, grew its ARR by 31.4% year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing segment in the portfolio.
HealthVela signed 14 new health system contracts in fiscal year 2023, including a landmark seven-year deal with Meridian Regional Health Network valued at $38 million.
The logistics analytics segment, LogiVela, saw more modest growth of 9.8% in ARR, as supply chain normalization following the pandemic dampened new software investment.
Despite slower ARR growth, LogiVela maintained its industry-leading gross margin of 66.2%, outperforming the company average.
The financial services analytics unit, FinVela, contributed $187.4 million in revenue, with notable wins at two top-ten U.S. regional banks.
FinVela's pipeline entering fiscal year 2024 stands at $114.6 million in qualified opportunities, up 38% from the pipeline at the start of fiscal year 2023.
In October 2023, Velarion completed the acquisition of DataNexis Inc., a small but strategically significant provider of supply chain data enrichment services, for $74.5 million in cash.
The DataNexis acquisition was funded entirely from existing cash reserves, with no external financing required, and is expected to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within 18 months.
DataNexis contributed $6.2 million in revenue to Velarion's consolidated results in the two months following the close of the transaction.
CEO Marcus Delwyn described the DataNexis acquisition as a cornerstone of the company's strategy to build a comprehensive, end-to-end supply chain intelligence platform.
Velarion's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.7%, benefiting from research and development tax credits.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $198.7 million, up from $163.4 million, reflecting the strong bookings momentum seen throughout the year.
The company declared and paid a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in November 2023, returning approximately $22.6 million to shareholders.
This was the first dividend of any kind in Velarion's history, and management indicated it should not be interpreted as the beginning of a regular dividend program.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $224.6 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.5%, compared to $185.1 million and 24.9% in fiscal year 2022.
The company defines adjusted EBITDA as net income plus interest expense, taxes, depreciation, amortization, and stock-based compensation, net of certain one-time charges.
Stock-based compensation expense was $48.3 million for fiscal year 2023, up from $39.7 million in the prior year, reflecting new equity grants tied to the 2023 performance cycle.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $33.8 million, of which $14.2 million was attributable to acquired intangible assets from prior transactions, including DataNexis.
CFO Anita Corbridge commented that the company's capital allocation philosophy prioritizes organic investment, strategic M&A, and opportunistic share repurchases, in that order.
Velarion's net promoter score among enterprise customers increased to 52 in fiscal year 2023, up from 44 in fiscal year 2022, signaling improved customer experience outcomes.
The company invested $11.4 million in its customer success organization, expanding headcount in this function by 18% to better support complex enterprise deployments.
Velarion's cloud infrastructure costs decreased as a percentage of revenue from 14.1% to 12.8%, driven by the completion of a multi-year cloud optimization initiative.
This efficiency improvement contributed meaningfully to the year-over-year gross margin expansion of approximately 160 basis points.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of Velarion's financial statements without qualification or material weakness findings.
Velarion's board of directors, which includes seven members with diverse backgrounds in enterprise software, finance, and healthcare, met eleven times during fiscal year 2023.
Independent director and Audit Committee Chair Dr. Lorraine Wexford noted that internal controls over financial reporting were tested and found to be effective.
The company maintained compliance with all covenants under its prior obligations and reported no litigation outcomes that materially affected its financial position during the year.
Velarion guided for fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of $960 million to $980 million, implying growth of approximately 13.3% to 15.7% at the midpoint.
Management's fiscal year 2024 outlook assumes continued strong performance in the healthcare and financial services segments, partially offset by ongoing softness in logistics.
The company expects adjusted EBITDA margin to expand by 50 to 100 basis points in fiscal year 2024, reaching a range of 27.0% to 27.5%.
Velarion also anticipates free cash flow conversion from adjusted EBITDA to remain above 75% through fiscal year 2024, consistent with historical performance.
Management stated that the integration of DataNexis is proceeding on schedule, with technology consolidation expected to be fully completed by the end of the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Looking ahead, CEO Marcus Delwyn expressed confidence that Velarion's diversified product portfolio and strong balance sheet position it well for sustained growth and value creation.

In the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Velarion secured a $250 million revolving credit facility from a consortium of banks led by JPMorgan Chase, which management described as a strategic liquidity enhancement measure.
Velorath Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Schultheiss opened the earnings call by describing 2023 as a year of disciplined capital allocation and operational resilience.
The company reported consolidated net revenues of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.2% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margins expanded by 140 basis points to reach 38.6%, driven by favorable raw-material pricing and improved plant utilization rates.
Chief Financial Officer Henrik Ostergaard noted that EBITDA came in at €412 million, surpassing the previously issued guidance of €390 million.
Operating expenses were tightly controlled at €276 million, reflecting the success of the company's two-year cost transformation program launched in early 2022.
Velorath's specialty coatings segment was the top performer, generating €710 million in revenue and growing 11.4% year over year.
The industrial adhesives division contributed €540 million in revenue, while the performance polymers segment added €590 million.
Research and development spending increased to €98 million, equivalent to 5.3% of total revenues, as the company accelerated its green-chemistry pipeline.
Dr. Schultheiss emphasized that the company's newly opened Innovation Hub in Aachen was already hosting over 200 scientists and engineers.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €155 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Mannheim and Gdańsk production facilities.
Free cash flow reached €231 million, up from €189 million in fiscal year 2022, marking the fourth consecutive year of free cash flow growth.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.85 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to €2.60 per share.
This represents a 12% increase from the prior-year total dividend of €2.32 per share, consistent with Velorath's progressive dividend policy.
Velorath ended fiscal year 2023 with a net cash position of €143 million, having fully retired all outstanding long-term borrowings earlier in the year.
Ostergaard highlighted that the balance sheet strength gives the company significant strategic flexibility heading into 2024.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8%, up from 13.1% in 2022, reflecting the higher earnings on a stable asset base.
Headcount grew modestly to 8,740 full-time employees globally, compared to 8,510 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Velorath's workforce in the Asia-Pacific region expanded by 9%, reflecting growing commercial operations in South Korea and Vietnam.
The European workforce remained the largest segment, comprising approximately 62% of total headcount.
In the second half of 2023, the company completed the integration of Bremer Kolloide GmbH, a specialty surfactant producer acquired in late 2022.
The Bremer Kolloide integration added approximately €65 million of annualized revenue synergies and is expected to be fully accretive to earnings in 2024.
Velorath's North American operations posted revenue of €348 million, aided by strong demand from the automotive and aerospace end markets.
The Latin American segment, though smaller, grew at an impressive 18.3% rate, reaching €112 million in revenue.
Management indicated that pricing power remained robust throughout most of 2023, with average selling prices rising 3.9% on an organic basis.
Volume growth contributed 3.1% to overall revenue expansion, while currency effects were broadly neutral at negative 0.2%.
The company's order backlog as of December 31, 2023, stood at €620 million, providing solid near-term revenue visibility.
Ostergaard confirmed that working capital management improved significantly, with days sales outstanding falling from 54 days to 48 days.
Inventory turns also improved to 6.2 times annually, compared to 5.7 times in the prior year, suggesting lean operational discipline.
The specialty coatings segment's operating margin of 22.4% was the highest across all business units, reflecting premium product positioning.
Industrial adhesives delivered an operating margin of 17.8%, while performance polymers reported a margin of 15.3%.
Dr. Schultheiss described the performance polymers segment as a priority for margin improvement in 2024 through product-mix enrichment.
Velorath's sustainability initiatives advanced materially during 2023, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining 8.4% versus 2022.
The company sourced 41% of its electricity from renewable sources, up from 29% in 2022, as part of its goal to reach 75% by 2030.
A new environmental compliance framework was introduced in Q3 2023, aligning Velorath's reporting with the EU's Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive.
The company received an upgraded ESG rating of A- from Sustainalytics, reflecting progress on climate targets and supply-chain transparency.
Velorath launched three new product families under the EcoForm brand in the second quarter of 2023, targeting the sustainable packaging market.
EcoForm revenues, though still nascent, reached €28 million in their inaugural year, tracking slightly ahead of internal forecasts.
Management expects EcoForm revenues to more than double in 2024, as several large consumer goods customers complete qualification trials.
The company's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, lower than the prior-year rate of 24.1%, due to R&D tax credits in Germany and Poland.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €253 million, translating to basic earnings per share of €3.44.
Diluted earnings per share of €3.39 compared favorably to the consensus analyst estimate of €3.20.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during 2023 under its authorized buyback program, at an average price of €42.30 per share.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were 73.5 million, reflecting the reduction from buyback activity partially offset by employee share awards.
Velorath maintained investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's (Baa1) and S&P Global (BBB+) throughout fiscal year 2023.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance of revenue in the range of €1.92 billion to €1.98 billion.
EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at €430 million to €450 million, implying continued margin progression.
Dr. Schultheiss cautioned that macroeconomic uncertainty in Europe and softness in the construction end market represent the primary demand-side risks.
Supply-chain normalization is expected to provide a modest tailwind to input costs, though energy price volatility remains a watchpoint.
The company plans to invest between €160 million and €175 million in capital expenditures during 2024, with a particular focus on automation.
A new digitalization initiative, branded VeloConnect, is set to roll out across all manufacturing sites by mid-2025.
Ostergaard noted that VeloConnect is projected to reduce per-unit production costs by approximately 4% once fully deployed.
Velorath's long-term strategic plan targets revenues exceeding €2.5 billion and an EBITDA margin above 25% by fiscal year 2028.
Dr. Schultheiss concluded by thanking the global workforce for their contributions to a record year of profitability.

During the fourth quarter of 2023, Velorath secured a €320 million syndicated revolving credit facility from a consortium of European banks, providing additional liquidity headroom for potential acquisitions.
The annual report is expected to be published in full on April 22, 2024, and will be available to all shareholders via the company's investor relations portal.
Veltora Systems Inc. released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates primarily in the enterprise cloud infrastructure sector.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023.
