Chief Executive Officer Miranda Casswell opened the report with remarks on the company's resilient performance amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based services accounted for 68% of total revenue, reinforcing the company's shift toward recurring income streams.
Professional services and consulting revenue contributed an additional $189 million to the top line.
Gross profit for the year stood at $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $394 million, inclusive of research and development, sales, and general administrative costs.
Research and development spending rose to $121 million, reflecting a 22% year-over-year increase.
Chief Financial Officer Damien Okafor noted that R&D investment would continue to be a strategic priority through 2025.
Sales and marketing expenses reached $158 million as Veltora expanded its go-to-market efforts in Europe and Asia-Pacific.
General and administrative costs were held flat at $115 million compared to the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was reported at $118 million, up from $94 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $97 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
This represented a 19% increase in earnings per share compared to the $1.55 reported in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $163 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.3%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $322 million, compared to $278 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $201 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditures amounted to $67 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades and infrastructure expansion.
Free cash flow for the year was therefore $134 million, a record high for Veltora Systems.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $100 million in January 2024.
Veltora ended the year with 4,820 full-time employees globally, up from 4,210 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company added 610 net new employees during 2023, with the majority of hires concentrated in engineering and product roles.
Miranda Casswell emphasized that workforce expansion was carefully calibrated to align with long-term growth objectives.
Customer count grew to 3,140 enterprise clients by December 31, 2023, an increase of 420 over the prior year.
Net revenue retention rate was reported at 114%, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell activity within the existing customer base.
Annual contract value per customer rose to an average of $270,000, up from $248,000 in fiscal 2022.
The company launched its VeltoraSphere platform in March 2023, targeting mid-market businesses with hybrid cloud needs.
VeltoraSphere reached 580 paying clients by year-end, ahead of the internal target of 500.
Damien Okafor stated that VeltoraSphere gross margins were already trending above the company-wide average.
International revenue grew 21% year-over-year, now comprising 38% of total revenue compared to 36% in fiscal 2022.
Veltora's European operations posted $142 million in revenue, driven by expansion in Germany, France, and the Netherlands.
Asia-Pacific revenue reached $179 million, with particularly strong performance in Japan and Australia.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with Nexora Technologies in August 2023 to co-develop edge computing solutions.
This partnership is expected to contribute incremental revenue beginning in the second half of fiscal 2024.
Inventory of hardware components held at year-end was $44 million, down from $61 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The reduction in inventory levels reflected improved supply chain management and leaner procurement practices.
Accounts receivable stood at $138 million as of December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 59 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $6.2 million, representing approximately 4.5% of gross receivables.
Veltora's deferred revenue balance grew to $204 million, signaling strong future revenue visibility.
The deferred revenue increase of $31 million year-over-year was driven by multi-year subscription contracts signed in Q3 and Q4.
During fiscal 2023, Veltora drew down a $175 million revolving credit facility to fund strategic initiatives and working capital needs.
The income tax provision for fiscal 2023 was $21 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 17.8%.
This effective tax rate benefited from research and development tax credits claimed in the United States and Ireland.
The company's total stockholders' equity increased to $689 million by year-end, up from $601 million in fiscal 2022.
Comprehensive income for fiscal 2023 included a foreign currency translation adjustment of negative $8 million.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal 2023 financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the annual assessment.
Veltora's board composition includes three independent directors with backgrounds in technology, finance, and governance.
Miranda Casswell was granted 120,000 restricted stock units in February 2023 as part of her long-term incentive compensation.
Damien Okafor received 75,000 restricted stock units under the same plan, with vesting spread over four years.
The company did not pay any cash dividends during fiscal 2023 and does not currently anticipate doing so in fiscal 2024.
Weighted average diluted shares outstanding for fiscal 2023 were 52.7 million.
Veltora repurchased 1.2 million shares at an average price of $41.30 during the fourth quarter of 2023.
The company's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $47.85 per share, representing a 33% appreciation over the calendar year.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltora is guiding revenue in the range of $960 million to $990 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall between $185 million and $195 million.
The company plans to open a new engineering center in Dublin, Ireland, in the second quarter of 2024.
Damien Okafor stated that the Dublin facility would initially house 150 engineers and analysts.
Miranda Casswell concluded the report by expressing confidence in Veltora's ability to sustain double-digit revenue growth through 2026.
Veldora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company serves clients across the financial services, healthcare, and logistics sectors with a suite of proprietary SaaS platforms.
As of December 31, 2023, Veldora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely on internally generated cash flows.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollis delivered opening remarks at the annual investor briefing held on February 14, 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for approximately 68% of total revenue, underscoring the company's shift toward recurring income streams.
Professional services revenue contributed $271 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and consulting engagements.
Gross profit for the year was $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, including $142 million allocated to research and development.
Sales and marketing expenditures reached $118 million, up from $103 million in fiscal year 2022.
General and administrative expenses were $129 million, reflecting expanded headcount and compliance investments.
Operating income for the year came in at $123 million, a 17.8% improvement compared to fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $94 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.87.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, consistent with prior guidance.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $198 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
Chief Financial Officer David Ng highlighted the strong free cash flow generation of $161 million during the earnings call.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $37 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades and security infrastructure.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $304 million, up from $247 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $38.50 per share.
Total shares outstanding as of December 31, 2023 were approximately 50.2 million.
The board of directors declared a special dividend of $0.40 per share, payable in March 2024.
Veldora Systems expanded its global workforce to 5,300 employees by year-end, adding 620 net new positions throughout 2023.
The company opened two new regional offices, one in Toronto and one in Amsterdam, to support international growth initiatives.
International revenue grew to $214 million, representing 25.3% of total revenue, up from 21.6% in the prior year.
The APAC region delivered the strongest growth rate among all geographies, with revenue increasing by 31% year-over-year.
Chief Revenue Officer Priya Anand reported that the company's net revenue retention rate improved to 118% in fiscal year 2023.
Customer count grew to 4,150 enterprise accounts, an increase of 490 accounts from the prior year-end.
Average contract value increased to $204,000, compared to $186,000 in fiscal year 2022.
The company launched its next-generation analytics module, Veldora Insight Pro, in Q2 2023.
Veldora Insight Pro had been adopted by over 800 enterprise clients by year-end, exceeding the initial rollout target.
The cybersecurity suite, VeldoraShield, was updated with AI-driven threat detection capabilities in Q3 2023.
Customer satisfaction scores for VeldoraShield reached an all-time high of 4.6 out of 5 following the update.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with NexaCloud Partners to co-develop hybrid deployment solutions.
This partnership is expected to generate incremental revenue of approximately $30 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Veldora Systems was recognized on the Deloitte Technology Fast 500 list for the third consecutive year.
The company's stock price appreciated by 28% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the sector index by 11 percentage points.
Management introduced fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $960 million.
The 2024 adjusted EBITDA margin guidance was set at 24% to 25%, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Veldora Systems expects to hire approximately 500 additional employees in fiscal year 2024 to support product expansion.
The audit committee confirmed that Pricewaterford & Associates LLP conducted the independent audit for fiscal year 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit process.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.8 and a quick ratio of 2.4 as of year-end 2023, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
In January 2024, Veldora Systems secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six major commercial banks.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $189 million, reflecting a growing pipeline of prepaid annual subscriptions.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days, down from 51 days in the prior year, signaling better collections efficiency.
The company recorded no goodwill impairment charges during fiscal year 2023.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets totaled $18 million for the year.
Veldora Systems completed no acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, maintaining its organic growth strategy.
The company's remaining performance obligations stood at $1.2 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Approximately 62% of those obligations are expected to be recognized as revenue within the next 24 months.
The Board of Directors reaffirmed its commitment to long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation and sustained innovation.
Veldora Systems enters fiscal year 2024 with strong momentum, a diversified customer base, and a clear roadmap for continued growth.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Veltora Dynamics operates in the advanced industrial automation sector, providing robotics and process-control solutions to manufacturing clients worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Heidemann opened the report by describing fiscal 2023 as a transformative year for the company.
Veltora Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet.
The company reported total revenues of $2.84 billion, representing an 11.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the period reached $1.07 billion, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 37.7%.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Solberg attributed the margin improvement to disciplined procurement and favorable component pricing.
Operating income climbed to $412 million, up from $349 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $298 million, compared to $261 million in the prior year.
Earnings per diluted share improved to $4.82, versus $4.21 reported twelve months earlier.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.38 per share, payable in February 2024.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $643 million, an increase of $97 million from the prior year-end balance.
Capital expenditures totaled $188 million, focused primarily on expanding the company's fabrication facility in Tucson, Arizona.
Research and development spending reached $214 million, representing approximately 7.5% of total revenues.
Marcus Heidemann emphasized the company's commitment to innovation, highlighting two major product lines scheduled for release in 2024.
The Veltora ProFlex 900 series, a modular robotic assembly platform, is anticipated to begin commercial shipments in the second quarter of 2024.
The company also unveiled the OmniSense industrial sensor suite, designed to integrate seamlessly with existing automation infrastructure.
Headcount at fiscal year-end was 12,400 full-time employees, reflecting net hiring of approximately 680 individuals during the year.
The human resources team, led by Chief People Officer Leanne Bastow, oversaw a significant expansion of the engineering workforce.
Employee benefits and compensation expenses totaled $891 million, accounting for roughly 31% of total revenues.
Veltora's North American segment generated revenues of $1.52 billion, representing 53.5% of consolidated sales.
The European segment contributed $784 million in revenues, supported by strong demand from German and Scandinavian automotive manufacturers.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 18.4% year-over-year to reach $536 million, driven by expansion in South Korean and Japanese markets.
Order backlog at year-end totaled $1.93 billion, the highest level in the company's history.
The backlog figure provides strong visibility into revenue for the first half of fiscal 2024, according to Priya Solberg.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $312 million, reflecting strategic positioning ahead of anticipated demand in early 2024.
Days inventory outstanding improved slightly to 40.1 days from 41.8 days in the prior year.
Accounts receivable stood at $487 million, with days sales outstanding of 62.6 days.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023 was 2.14, indicating solid short-term liquidity.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 18.7%, an improvement of 210 basis points versus the prior year.
Return on invested capital reached 14.3%, benefiting from efficient asset utilization across all business segments.
Veltora repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $78.40 per share.
The share repurchase program reduced the diluted share count from 62.1 million to approximately 60.9 million.
Total shareholder return for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023 was 22.6%, outperforming the sector index by 7.4 percentage points.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its review without issuing any material findings.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the Sarbanes-Oxley framework.
The company faces certain ongoing litigation related to a patent dispute with a competitor in the conveyor-robotics space.
Management does not expect the litigation to result in a material financial liability based on current legal assessments.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with Veltora reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 9% during 2023.
The company's sustainability report, published concurrently, details progress toward a 2030 carbon-neutrality target.
Veltora maintained investment-grade credit ratings of Baa1 from Moody's and BBB+ from S&P throughout the fiscal year.
Those ratings reflect the company's robust cash generation and conservative financial management practices.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion.
To fund its planned expansion into Southeast Asian manufacturing markets, Veltora entered into a $450 million syndicated term loan facility in November 2023.
The company anticipates adjusted EBITDA margins of between 18% and 20% for fiscal 2024.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $210 million to $230 million, reflecting continued investment in production capacity.
Priya Solberg noted that free cash flow conversion is expected to remain above 85% of net income in the coming fiscal year.
The Board of Directors approved an increase to the share repurchase authorization, adding $200 million to the existing program.
Marcus Heidemann expressed confidence that the ProFlex 900 launch would meaningfully contribute to revenues within its first year of availability.
The OmniSense suite has already received letters of intent from seven major industrial clients totaling approximately $120 million in potential orders.
Veltora's international distribution network spans 42 countries, supported by a logistics partnership with DHL Supply Chain.
The company added three new regional service centers during 2023, located in Warsaw, Monterrey, and Kuala Lumpur.
Customer retention rates remained high at 94%, reflecting the sticky nature of the company's long-term service contracts.
Veltora's deferred revenue balance grew to $178 million, primarily from multi-year software maintenance agreements.
The weighted average remaining term of existing service contracts is approximately 3.4 years, providing durable recurring revenue.
Management believes the company is well-positioned to capture incremental market share as global manufacturers accelerate their automation investments.
Veltora Dynamics remains committed to delivering superior value to its shareholders, customers, and employees in the years ahead.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care testing devices and laboratory automation systems.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Melissa Cornwell and has grown to serve over 4,200 hospital and clinic clients across 38 countries.
This annual report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Chief Executive Officer Raymond Abshire opened the report by emphasizing the company's resilience amid ongoing macroeconomic headwinds and supply chain normalization efforts.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year's $1.31 billion.
Domestic revenue accounted for $892 million of the total, while international operations contributed the remaining $578 million.
The gross profit margin expanded to 54.3%, up from 51.8% in fiscal year 2022, driven primarily by manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
Operating expenses totaled $612 million, including $214 million in research and development and $248 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Veltara Diagnostics entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond issuance in December 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $187 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $2.43.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year came in at $334 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.7%.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Trelawney noted that free cash flow generation of $261 million exceeded internal targets by approximately $18 million.
Capital expenditures during the year totaled $73 million, focused primarily on expanding the Raleigh manufacturing campus and upgrading quality control infrastructure.
The company repurchased 3.1 million shares of common stock under its $150 million buyback authorization, at an average price of $48.37 per share.
A quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share was declared and paid in each of the four quarters, bringing total annual dividends to $0.72 per share.
The board of directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.198 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltara's flagship product line, the VeltraMax 5000 series, posted revenue of $541 million, representing 36.8% of total company revenue.
The VeltraMax platform saw unit shipments increase by 18% year-over-year, reflecting strong adoption in academic medical centers and regional hospital networks.
The company's reagent and consumables segment delivered revenue of $493 million, growing 14.1% compared to the prior year.
Recurring revenue streams, including service contracts and reagent subscriptions, represented 61% of total revenue, up from 57% in fiscal year 2022.
International growth was particularly strong in the Asia-Pacific region, where revenue increased 24.3% to $219 million.
European revenue grew at a more modest 6.7% pace, constrained by reimbursement uncertainties in Germany and France.
The company added 312 net new employees during fiscal year 2023, ending the year with a global headcount of 7,841 full-time equivalents.
Employee retention improved meaningfully, with voluntary attrition declining from 14.2% in 2022 to 9.8% in 2023.
Research and development spending of $214 million represented 14.6% of total revenue, consistent with the company's long-term innovation investment philosophy.
Three new product platforms entered the regulatory submission pipeline in 2023, targeting infectious disease, oncology biomarker detection, and metabolic monitoring.
The VeltraPlex immunoassay analyzer received 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in March 2023.
Commercial launch of VeltraPlex commenced in the second quarter, with 187 analyzer placements recorded by year-end.
The oncology biomarker platform, internally designated Project Halcyon, remains under development and is expected to enter clinical validation studies in mid-2024.
Veltara's regulatory affairs team expanded by 22 professionals during the year to support an increasingly complex global submission workload.
The company invested $31 million in digital health infrastructure, including cloud-based data analytics capabilities for connected device customers.
Supply chain improvements reduced average component lead times from 18 weeks at the start of 2023 to 11 weeks by year-end.
Inventory levels decreased from $124 million at the end of 2022 to $98 million at the end of 2023, reflecting improved demand forecasting.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved by four days to 52 days, aided by enhanced collections processes and revised credit terms with distributors.
The company's balance sheet remained strong, with cash and cash equivalents of $412 million as of December 31, 2023.
Total current assets were $784 million, compared to total current liabilities of $291 million, yielding a current ratio of 2.69.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaling $318 million reflect the acquisitions of BioLab Precision in 2019 and Centra Assay Technologies in 2021.
No material acquisitions or divestitures were completed during fiscal year 2023, as management focused on organic growth and integration optimization.
The company maintains a disciplined capital allocation framework prioritizing organic investment, strategic M&A, and return of capital to shareholders.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed significantly in 2023, with the company achieving a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions.
Veltara was recognized by Forbes as one of America's Best Mid-Size Employers for the third consecutive year in 2023.
The company signed a strategic distribution agreement with MedBridge Asia Partners in October 2023, expected to accelerate penetration in Southeast Asian markets.
Legal proceedings remain limited, with no material litigation outstanding beyond routine patent disputes typical of the medical technology industry.
The audit committee oversaw an independent review of internal controls over financial reporting, which concluded with no material weaknesses identified.
Veltara's external auditor, Hartwell & Crane LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 consolidated financial statements.
Management's guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for revenue in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion, representing growth of 9% to 12%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 is set at 23% to 24%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage from volume growth and cost discipline.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to remain in the range of $75 million to $85 million.
In the second quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight lending institutions.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in May 2028.
Diluted share count for the purposes of earnings per share calculation stood at 76.9 million shares for fiscal year 2023.
The effective income tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, modestly below the prior year's 22.1% due to favorable discrete tax items.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations decreased by $14 million during the year, reflecting actuarial gains driven by rising discount rates.
Stock-based compensation expense of $38 million was recorded in fiscal year 2023, unchanged from the prior year's level.
The company's geographic revenue concentration risk is actively managed through ongoing diversification across emerging and developed markets.
Raymond Abshire indicated that the management team remains confident in the long-term structural growth drivers of the point-of-care diagnostics market.
Industry analysts estimate the global point-of-care diagnostics market will grow at a compound annual growth rate of 8.5% through 2030.
Veltara believes its strong installed base of over 28,000 active instruments positions it favorably to capture an outsized share of this growth.
The company's net promoter score among institutional customers reached 68 in the most recent annual survey, an improvement from 61 in 2022.
Customer acquisition costs declined 9% year-over-year, reflecting greater sales force efficiency and increased referral-based business development.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023, was $1.02 billion, compared to $897 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors and executive leadership team express gratitude to Veltara's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Readers are encouraged to review the risk factors section of Veltara's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltrano Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and medical diagnostics company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Camille Ostrowski and has since grown to become a recognized name in point-of-care diagnostics.
Veltrano Biosystems operates across three principal business segments: Rapid Diagnostics, Genomic Services, and Laboratory Instrumentation.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, the company reported total revenues of $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
The Rapid Diagnostics segment remained the largest revenue contributor, generating $218.6 million during fiscal 2023.
Genomic Services revenue grew by 22.7% year-over-year, reaching $156.4 million, driven by expanded partnerships with regional hospital networks.
Laboratory Instrumentation contributed the remaining $112.3 million in revenue, a modest improvement of 6.1% compared to fiscal 2022.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delhaney attributed the strong performance to disciplined execution of the company's five-year growth strategy.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Sundaram noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points, settling at 58.3% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $201.4 million, with research and development accounting for $89.7 million of that figure.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $111.7 million, reflecting higher headcount and expanded marketing initiatives.
Net income attributable to Veltrano Biosystems shareholders was $63.8 million for fiscal 2023, compared to $44.1 million in fiscal 2022.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $1.84 from $1.27 in the prior year, representing a 44.9% improvement.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $142.7 million.
Short-term investments totaling $58.9 million further bolstered the company's liquidity position.
Accounts receivable stood at $74.2 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding of 55.6 days.
Inventory levels were $31.4 million, up from $27.8 million in the prior year, as the company pre-positioned supplies for new product launches.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $34.6 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Austin manufacturing facility.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Biosystems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting the company's longstanding policy of organic financing.
Free cash flow for the year reached $97.5 million, a record high for the company since its initial public offering in 2014.
Veltrano Biosystems repurchased 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback program, spending approximately $38.4 million.
The share repurchase activity reduced diluted share count to approximately 34.7 million shares outstanding at year-end.
During fiscal 2023, the company launched VeltraPath 3.0, its next-generation genomic sequencing platform, in the second quarter.
VeltraPath 3.0 received CE Mark certification in Europe and 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration within the same calendar year.
The platform demonstrated a 40% reduction in time-to-result compared to its predecessor, VeltraPath 2.5.
Customer adoption of VeltraPath 3.0 was notably strong in the academic medical center segment, with 87 institutions signing multi-year contracts.
International revenues accounted for 31.4% of total revenues in fiscal 2023, up from 26.8% in fiscal 2022.
Key growth markets included Germany, South Korea, and Australia, where the company expanded its direct sales force.
Veltrano Biosystems entered into a strategic distribution agreement with Harada MedTech in Japan, expected to generate incremental revenue beginning in fiscal 2024.
The company's workforce grew to 2,847 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 312 employees from the prior year-end.
Employee retention rates remained above industry benchmarks, with an annualized voluntary turnover rate of 8.3%.
Dr. Ostrowski, who serves as Executive Chairwoman, commented that the company's culture of innovation continues to be its most durable competitive advantage.
Veltrano Biosystems was recognized by Forbes as one of the Best Employers for Innovation in 2023.
The company's manufacturing operations achieved ISO 13485 recertification following an audit in the third quarter of fiscal 2023.
Quality metrics improved across all three manufacturing lines, with defect rates declining to 0.4% from 0.7% in fiscal 2022.
Supply chain resiliency initiatives launched in 2022 contributed to a 15% reduction in component lead times during fiscal 2023.
The company maintained a current ratio of 3.1 at fiscal year-end, underscoring its strong near-term liquidity profile.
Long-term assets totaled $389.4 million, which included $214.6 million of goodwill and intangible assets primarily from the 2021 acquisition of NovaDx Corp.
Amortization of acquired intangibles contributed $18.2 million to operating expenses during fiscal 2023.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, compared to 23.1% in fiscal 2022, reflecting favorable R&D tax credits.
Management expects the effective tax rate to normalize in the range of 21% to 22% for fiscal 2024.
For fiscal 2024, the company is guiding for total revenue in the range of $540 million to $560 million.
This guidance implies organic revenue growth of approximately 11% to 15% at the midpoint, excluding currency effects.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to be between $148 million and $158 million.
The company plans to increase its R&D budget by approximately 18% in fiscal 2024 to accelerate development of the VeltraPath 4.0 platform.
Clinical trials for the next-generation VeltraPath 4.0 are expected to commence in the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
Veltrano Biosystems anticipates filing for regulatory clearance of VeltraPath 4.0 in the United States and European Union by mid-2025.
The company is also evaluating potential bolt-on acquisitions in the liquid biopsy space, with a budget earmarked of up to $75 million.
Management confirmed that any such acquisition would be funded through existing cash reserves and operating cash flows.
The company's intellectual property portfolio comprises 143 active patents and 61 pending patent applications as of the report date.
Veltrano Biosystems was awarded three new patents in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 related to microfluidic chip design.
Environmental, Social, and Governance initiatives advanced significantly during fiscal 2023, including a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
The company set a target to achieve carbon neutrality across its owned operations by the end of fiscal 2030.
Veltrano Biosystems published its third consecutive annual Sustainability Report, which received third-party assurance from Meridian Audit Group.
The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors reviewed and approved all related-party transactions during fiscal 2023, confirming none were material.
External auditors Grant & Hollowell LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's fiscal 2023 financial statements.
The company did not identify any material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting during the year under review.
Looking ahead, Marcus Delhaney expressed confidence that Veltrano Biosystems is well-positioned to extend its market leadership in point-of-care genomics.

Veltrano Biosystems finalized a $175 million syndicated term loan facility in November 2023, the proceeds of which were designated to refinance existing obligations and fund strategic growth initiatives.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of next-generation molecular testing solutions, is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three primary segments: clinical laboratory services, genomic instrumentation, and digital health analytics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Solberg expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in March 2024.
Veltara Diagnostics generated total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The clinical laboratory services segment contributed $412.6 million to total revenue, driven by expanded hospital network partnerships.
Genomic instrumentation sales reached $263.1 million, reflecting strong demand from academic research institutions and biopharma clients.
The digital health analytics segment posted revenues of $171.6 million, up 21.3% year-over-year as adoption of the company's cloud platform accelerated.
Gross profit for the fiscal year amounted to $389.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.0%.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $274.8 million.
Research and development spending increased to $98.2 million, representing 11.6% of total revenue, as Veltara continued to invest in its next-generation sequencing pipeline.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $114.6 million, compared to $93.1 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $84.9 million, or $1.72 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 24.7%, slightly above management's guidance of 23.5% due to certain non-deductible expenses.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Adeyemi highlighted the strength of the company's balance sheet during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Diagnostics held cash and cash equivalents of $221.4 million.
Short-term investments totaling $88.7 million were also maintained to support near-term operational flexibility.
Accounts receivable stood at $134.2 million, reflecting improved collections efficiency with days sales outstanding of 57.8 days.
The company completed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in August of that year.
Capital expenditures during 2023 amounted to $61.3 million, focused primarily on expanding laboratory capacity in the Southeast and Midwest regions.
Veltara Diagnostics added 347 full-time employees during the year, bringing total headcount to 4,218 as of December 31, 2023.
Employee benefits and compensation expenses rose to $198.6 million, reflecting both headcount growth and merit-based salary increases.
The company operates manufacturing and laboratory facilities across eleven states, with its headquarters located in Raleigh, North Carolina.
In January 2023, Veltara launched its PrecisionPath 3.0 genomic analysis platform, which received 510(k) clearance from the FDA in December 2022.
PrecisionPath 3.0 contributed an estimated $44.7 million in incremental revenue during its first year of commercial availability.
The platform's reagent consumable attachments generated recurring revenue streams with gross margins exceeding 62%, above the corporate average.
Veltara's digital health division introduced two new software modules for clinical decision support in the third quarter of 2023.
The modules, named ClarityInsight and PathwayAlert, were integrated into the company's existing SaaS platform and offered on a subscription basis.
By year-end, ClarityInsight had been adopted by 218 hospital clients, while PathwayAlert had been deployed in 174 outpatient settings.
International revenues grew to $127.4 million, representing 15.0% of total revenue, with particular strength in Western European markets.
Veltara's partnership with Genomarkt AG in Germany contributed $31.2 million to international revenue in fiscal 2023.
Management anticipates international revenue will comprise approximately 18% to 20% of total revenues by fiscal year 2025.
The company's backlog of confirmed instrument orders stood at $93.6 million as of December 31, 2023, compared to $74.2 million a year earlier.
Deferred revenue related to service contracts and software subscriptions totaled $57.8 million at year-end.
Inventory levels decreased to $42.1 million from $51.3 million a year prior, reflecting improved supply chain management and demand forecasting.
The reduction in inventory was achieved without material disruptions to product delivery timelines, according to Chief Operating Officer Rachel Nwosu.
Veltara Diagnostics repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.46 per share, totaling $46.2 million.
The board of directors authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program in November 2023, effective through December 2025.
Dividends declared per common share totaled $0.48 for fiscal 2023, consistent with the prior year's dividend rate.
The company's return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 17.3%, an improvement from 14.8% in fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital reached 14.1%, reflecting disciplined capital allocation and higher operating profitability.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $168.4 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.9%.
The company defines adjusted EBITDA as net income before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization, and stock-based compensation expense.
Stock-based compensation expense for the year was $22.7 million, consistent with prior year levels relative to total compensation.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $31.1 million, of which $14.3 million related to acquired intangible assets from the 2021 acquisition of NovaBind Genomics.
The NovaBind integration was completed ahead of schedule, with all technology platforms and personnel fully absorbed into Veltara's operations by mid-2023.
Goodwill on the balance sheet stood at $204.6 million, unchanged from the prior year as no impairment was recorded.
Management conducted its annual goodwill impairment assessment in the fourth quarter and concluded no impairment indicators were present.
The company's current ratio was 2.8 as of year-end, reflecting a strong liquidity position relative to near-term obligations.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $142.7 million, up from $118.3 million in fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $81.4 million.
Veltara Diagnostics provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $955 million, implying growth of approximately 9% to 13%.
Management expects gross margins to expand modestly in 2024, supported by favorable product mix and operational efficiencies in the laboratory segment.
Dr. Solberg noted that the company's pipeline includes three additional diagnostic assays currently in clinical validation studies, expected to launch in late 2024 or early 2025.
The audit committee engaged Prentiss & Hollowell LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year.
Prentiss & Hollowell issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's financial statements, with no material weaknesses identified.
The company is subject to ongoing regulatory examination by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services related to billing practices in its clinical laboratory segment.
Legal counsel estimates the potential exposure from the CMS examination to be in the range of $4 million to $12 million, which has been disclosed as a contingent liability.
Looking ahead, Veltara Diagnostics remains committed to disciplined financial stewardship and sustainable growth across all three operating segments.

In the months following the fiscal year close, Veltara secured a $175 million syndicated term loan facility, reflecting the company's strategy to leverage external financing for its planned facility expansion program.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltara Biosciences operates within the specialty pharmaceuticals sector, focusing on oncology and rare metabolic disorders.
The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlena Ostrowski has led the organization since 2017, driving a disciplined growth strategy.
Chief Financial Officer Harlan Trebst joined the company in 2019 and oversees all financial planning and capital allocation.
Veltara reported total revenues of $842.3 million for the full year 2023, representing a 14.7% increase over the prior year.
Product revenues accounted for $798.6 million of the total, while licensing and royalty income contributed $43.7 million.
The company's flagship oncology drug, Velotrex, generated $521.4 million in net product sales during 2023.
Velotrex saw particularly strong uptake in European markets, where sales rose 22.3% year over year.
The rare metabolic disorder franchise, anchored by Tarcovel, contributed $277.2 million to product revenues.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 was $618.9 million, yielding a gross margin of 73.5% on total revenues.
Cost of goods sold was $223.4 million, reflecting manufacturing efficiencies gained through our 2022 facility upgrade.
Research and development expenses totaled $189.7 million, representing 22.5% of total revenues.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $214.1 million, up 8.2% compared to $197.9 million in 2022.
Operating income for the year reached $215.1 million, compared to $178.4 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltara stockholders was $171.6 million, or $2.84 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $268.4 million, representing a margin of 31.9% on total revenues.
The company ended 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $394.7 million and short-term investments of $112.5 million.
Total liquidity, including the undrawn revolving credit facility, stood at approximately $907.2 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable net of allowances was $143.8 million, reflecting an average days-sales-outstanding of 62 days.
Inventory on hand was $88.3 million, supported by safety stock policies established after 2021 supply chain disruptions.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $47.3 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Durham manufacturing campus.
The board of directors approved a $300 million share repurchase program in March 2023, of which $127.4 million had been utilized by year-end.
Total stockholders' equity increased to $1.12 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $981.4 million at the end of 2022.
The weighted average diluted share count for 2023 was 60.4 million shares, down from 63.1 million in 2022.
Veltara employed 3,847 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023.
The workforce expanded by 312 employees during the year, primarily in commercial and medical affairs functions.
Dr. Ostrowski stated in her annual letter that talent retention remained a key strategic priority for the organization.
Employee turnover for the year was 9.2%, below the industry average of approximately 12% for specialty pharma firms.
Pipeline progress was a central theme of 2023, with two compounds advancing into Phase 3 clinical trials.
VLT-408, targeting treatment-resistant non-small cell lung cancer, began Phase 3 enrollment in Q2 2023.
VLT-217, a next-generation therapy for Pompe disease, entered Phase 3 in October 2023 following strong Phase 2 results.
The company also initiated two new Phase 1 studies during the year, expanding the total clinical pipeline to eleven active programs.
Regulatory milestones included the FDA acceptance of the New Drug Application for VLT-331 in August 2023.
VLT-331 has been granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation, which is expected to accelerate its review timeline.
The PDUFA date for VLT-331 is set for June 14, 2024, and the company remains confident in the regulatory package.
International expansion continued as Veltara received marketing authorization in Japan for Velotrex in May 2023.
The Japan launch contributed approximately $18.7 million in incremental revenue during the second half of 2023.
The company entered a co-promotion agreement with Shinkai Pharmaceuticals to support the Japan commercial launch.
In Europe, Veltara's wholly owned subsidiary, Veltara Europe GmbH, manages all commercial and regulatory operations.
Net revenues in the United States represented 61.8% of total product revenues in 2023, down from 65.3% in 2022.
The geographic diversification reflects a deliberate strategy to reduce dependence on any single market.
Harlan Trebst noted in the CFO commentary that currency headwinds reduced reported revenues by approximately $14.2 million.
Constant-currency revenue growth for fiscal 2023 was 16.4%, outpacing the reported growth rate.
Veltara's effective tax rate for 2023 was 22.3%, compared to 21.8% in 2022.
The slight increase in effective tax rate reflects the partial expiration of certain R&D tax credits in 2023.
Deferred tax assets net of valuation allowances were $62.1 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company made pension contributions of $11.4 million during fiscal 2023 to its defined benefit plan for legacy employees.
Veltara's audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the criteria established by the COSO framework.
There were no material weaknesses identified in the company's internal control assessment for fiscal 2023.
The company did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its capital reinvestment strategy.
Management reaffirmed the intention to prioritize pipeline investment and share repurchases over dividend distributions.
Looking ahead, Veltara issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $970 million.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued Velotrex volume growth and an initial revenue contribution from VLT-331 upon potential approval.
Adjusted EBITDA margin for 2024 is expected to expand modestly to a range of 32.5% to 34.0%.
The company expects R&D spending in 2024 to increase to approximately $215 million to $230 million to support Phase 3 programs.
Management believes the current balance sheet provides ample flexibility to fund operations and pipeline investment without external financing.
Strategic business development remains an active priority, with the company evaluating several bolt-on acquisition targets in the rare disease space.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara secured a $250 million senior unsecured term loan facility to fund its pipeline expansion initiatives.
Any statements regarding future performance in this report constitute forward-looking statements subject to material risks and uncertainties.
Veltara Biosciences remains committed to delivering differentiated therapies to patients and sustainable value to its stockholders.
This report has been approved by the board of directors and filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K.
Meridian Cargo Solutions, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, marking a pivotal period in the company's operational history.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializes in third-party logistics and freight forwarding services across North America and select European markets.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Kellerman opened the annual earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Chief Financial Officer Douglas Pratt noted that total revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
Operating income improved to $212 million, up from $178 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business segments.
The company's freight forwarding division contributed $740 million to total revenue, making it the largest single revenue generator in the portfolio.
Meridian Cargo Solutions expanded its warehousing footprint by adding three new distribution centers in Atlanta, Denver, and Memphis during the fiscal year.
The company carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its remaining senior notes in March of that year.
Gross margins improved by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 22.6% for the full fiscal year.
The company's headcount stood at approximately 8,400 full-time employees at year-end, reflecting modest growth from 8,050 the prior year.
Meridian's technology-driven route optimization platform, FreightIQ, processed over 2.3 million shipments during 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $98 million, primarily allocated toward fleet modernization and warehouse automation systems.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, consistent with the prior year's payout schedule.
Sandra Kellerman highlighted the company's successful renegotiation of carrier contracts, which reduced per-unit transportation costs by approximately 6%.
The company's third-party logistics segment posted revenue of $610 million, representing 33.2% of total company revenue.
Meridian completed the acquisition of Prairie Freight Partners in August 2023 for a total consideration of $145 million.
Prairie Freight Partners contributed $38 million in revenue and $5.2 million in operating income during the post-acquisition period.
The integration of Prairie Freight Partners is expected to generate annualized cost synergies of approximately $18 million by fiscal 2025.
Meridian's European operations, conducted through its subsidiary Meridian Cargo Europe GmbH, generated €210 million in revenue during fiscal 2023.
Currency translation effects reduced reported European revenue by approximately $14 million when converted to U.S. dollars.
Douglas Pratt confirmed that free cash flow for the full year reached $154 million, a record for the company.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its authorized buyback program.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 42.6 million, after accounting for repurchases and option exercises.
Meridian's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $298 million, compared to $261 million in fiscal 2022.
The adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 16.2% from 15.5%, reflecting both revenue growth and operational efficiency gains.
The company maintained a current ratio of 1.9 as of year-end, indicating a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days from 43 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened credit and collections processes.
Inventory levels within Meridian's managed warehousing operations declined 8% year-over-year due to improved demand forecasting.
The company's fuel surcharge revenues increased by $22 million as average diesel prices remained elevated through mid-year.
Meridian introduced a new last-mile delivery product called FinalMile Pro in the second quarter, targeting small and mid-sized e-commerce retailers.
FinalMile Pro onboarded 214 new clients by year-end, generating approximately $29 million in incremental revenue for the fiscal year.
Sandra Kellerman credited the FinalMile Pro launch as a key driver of new customer acquisition in the domestic logistics segment.
The company's customer retention rate remained high at 91%, consistent with the prior three fiscal years.
Meridian's top ten customers accounted for approximately 38% of total revenue in fiscal 2023, down slightly from 40% in fiscal 2022.
The reduction in customer concentration reflects the company's deliberate strategy to diversify its client base.
Research and development expenditures related to FreightIQ and digital logistics tools totaled $31 million for the year.
The company received an investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Partners Rating Services in November 2023.
Douglas Pratt indicated that the company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.4%, slightly below the prior year's 24.1%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $161 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.77.
The prior year's diluted EPS was $3.21, representing year-over-year EPS growth of approximately 17.4%.
Meridian's stock price appreciated approximately 24% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the broader transportation sector index.
The company issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $315 million to $330 million.
The company expects capital expenditures to increase modestly in fiscal 2024, to a range of $105 million to $115 million.
Meridian plans to open two additional distribution centers in fiscal 2024, one in Phoenix and one in Charlotte.
Sandra Kellerman reaffirmed the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders through dividends and opportunistic share repurchases.
The board also authorized an incremental $75 million share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2024.
Meridian entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility in December 2023, replacing a smaller prior arrangement.
The new facility carries a variable interest rate tied to SOFR plus a margin of 125 basis points.
Douglas Pratt stated that the facility provides enhanced financial flexibility to support the company's growth initiatives and potential acquisitions.
The company remains committed to maintaining investment-grade credit metrics while pursuing disciplined strategic growth.
Meridian Cargo Solutions concluded fiscal 2023 with strong momentum and a clear strategic roadmap for sustainable long-term value creation.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a mid-cap medical devices and laboratory solutions company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Simone Hartwell opened her letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's disciplined approach to capital management throughout the year.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a clean balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings of any kind.
The company achieved consolidated revenues of $847.3 million, representing a 12.4 percent increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Oleander attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong performance in the company's point-of-care diagnostics segment.
Gross profit for the year reached $412.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 48.7 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $289.1 million, which included research and development costs of $94.5 million.
Dr. Hartwell noted that the elevated R&D spending reflects the company's commitment to next-generation immunoassay platforms currently in late-stage development.
Operating income came in at $123.5 million, up from $98.2 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $91.7 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.34.
The effective tax rate for the period was 24.1 percent, slightly above management's initial guidance of 23.5 percent.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $218.4 million, compared to $175.9 million at the close of the prior fiscal year.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet amounted to $1.34 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity grew to $762.5 million, reflecting accumulated earnings and the impact of share-based compensation programs.
During the year, Veltara repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $41.60 per share.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing a 12.5 percent increase over the prior year's quarterly payout.
Capital expenditures totaled $67.3 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
Veltara's point-of-care diagnostics segment generated segment revenues of $391.2 million, accounting for approximately 46 percent of total consolidated revenues.
The laboratory automation segment contributed revenues of $278.8 million, driven by strong demand from hospital networks and independent clinical laboratories.
The life sciences consumables segment rounded out the portfolio with revenues of $177.3 million, growing at a rate of 9.1 percent year over year.
Mr. Oleander highlighted that inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, with days inventory outstanding improving to 54 days from 61 days in 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved modestly to 47 days, reflecting tightened credit policies implemented in the second quarter.
The company's workforce grew to approximately 4,800 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of roughly 310 employees compared to December 2022.
Veltara opened a new regional sales office in Frankfurt, Germany, to support accelerating demand across Western European markets.
International revenues accounted for 29 percent of total revenues in fiscal year 2023, up from 25 percent in the prior year.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, posting revenue gains of 21.3 percent on a constant-currency basis.
Chief Operating Officer Angela Bressler noted that supply chain disruptions that had weighed on performance in 2022 were substantially resolved by mid-2023.
Veltara entered into a strategic distribution agreement with Kyosei MedTech of Osaka, Japan, effective January 1, 2024.
The agreement with Kyosei is expected to add approximately $30 million to $35 million in annualized revenues beginning in the first full year of operation.
Product launch activity during the year included the commercial release of the VeltraPlex 6000 immunoanalyzer in the United States in March 2023.
The VeltraPlex 6000 received CE Mark certification in July 2023, enabling sales across European Union member states.
As of December 31, 2023, more than 620 VeltraPlex 6000 units had been placed with customers globally.
Management expects full commercial ramp of the VeltraPlex 6000 to contribute meaningfully to fiscal year 2024 revenues.
The company also completed a minor bolt-on acquisition of NovaChem Assay Technologies for $22.5 million in cash in September 2023.
NovaChem's proprietary reagent chemistries are anticipated to enhance Veltara's consumables margins over the medium term.
Veltara's credit facility, which had been undrawn at the start of the year, remained fully available as a liquidity backstop throughout fiscal 2023.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit profile, with a BBB+ rating reaffirmed by Standard & Poors in October 2023.
Free cash flow for the year was $149.6 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $216.9 million less capital expenditures of $67.3 million.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.2 percent from 11.8 percent in the prior year, reflecting higher earnings and disciplined asset utilization.
Dr. Hartwell expressed confidence in the company's ability to sustain double-digit earnings growth through at least the next two fiscal years.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance of $940 million to $960 million and diluted EPS guidance of $2.60 to $2.75.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
To accelerate its pipeline of next-generation diagnostics platforms, Veltara secured a $175 million term loan facility in November 2023, drawing down the full amount at closing.
Mr. Oleander clarified that this financing was structured as a five-year senior secured term loan carrying an interest rate of SOFR plus 185 basis points.
Veltara's board composition remained stable throughout 2023, with nine independent directors and two executive directors serving as of year-end.
The compensation committee approved a new long-term incentive plan tied to three-year total shareholder return relative to the S&P MidCap 400 Health Care index.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced during the year, with Veltara reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 8.4 percent versus the 2022 baseline.
The company donated $3.1 million to global health access programs through the Veltara Foundation, its charitable giving arm established in 2019.
Ms. Bressler outlined plans for a second manufacturing site in Monterrey, Mexico, expected to become operational in the third quarter of 2025.
The Monterrey facility is projected to add approximately 450 permanent jobs and will serve as the primary production hub for Latin American markets.
Veltara's patent portfolio expanded by 38 new grants during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total active patent count to 412 worldwide.
Legal proceedings remained limited in scope, with no material litigation reserves required as of the balance sheet date.
The company renegotiated its corporate headquarters lease in Raleigh, securing favorable terms through 2033 and reducing annual occupancy costs by approximately $1.8 million.

Mr. Oleander concluded his financial overview by reaffirming management's commitment to maintaining a balanced capital allocation strategy that prioritizes both organic investment and shareholder returns.
Dr. Hartwell closed the annual report letter by expressing gratitude to Veltara's global team and reiterating the company's mission to advance accessible, high-quality diagnostic solutions worldwide.
The board of directors formally approved the annual report and authorized its distribution to shareholders on February 14, 2024.
Velorant Technologies Corporation is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates as a leading provider of cloud-based logistics optimization software for mid-market retailers.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Castleford opened the shareholder letter by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic direction.
Chief Financial Officer Douglas Henthwaite confirmed that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point in Velorant's profitability trajectory.
Velorant Technologies reported total annual revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 19.4% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue, which constitutes the largest segment, grew 24.1% year-over-year to reach $298.3 million.
Professional services revenue contributed $72.4 million, while legacy perpetual license fees accounted for the remaining $42.0 million.
Gross profit for the fiscal year stood at $261.8 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 63.5%.
Operating expenses totaled $198.4 million, inclusive of sales and marketing, research and development, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures climbed to $64.2 million, reflecting the company's ongoing commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses amounted to $89.7 million, driven largely by expanded headcount in the enterprise sales division.
General and administrative costs were $44.5 million, representing a modest year-over-year increase of 6.8%.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $63.4 million, compared to $31.9 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $47.8 million, equivalent to diluted earnings per share of $1.24.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $88.6 million, reflecting a margin of approximately 21.5% against total revenue.
As of December 31, 2023, Velorant Technologies carried no long-term debt obligations on its consolidated balance sheet.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $134.5 million, providing significant liquidity for operational and strategic needs.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.8, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position relative to near-term liabilities.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections performance.
Total assets at December 31, 2023, were reported at $589.2 million, including $210.4 million in intangible assets.
Total stockholders' equity at year-end was $321.6 million, up from $274.3 million at the close of the prior fiscal year.
During fiscal year 2023, Velorant completed its acquisition of RouteSync Analytics for a purchase price of $55.0 million in cash.
The RouteSync acquisition added approximately 140 new enterprise clients and expanded the company's presence in the Southeast Asian market.
Integration costs associated with RouteSync were approximately $4.1 million and were recorded in general and administrative expenses.
Velorant's customer base grew to 3,840 active accounts by year-end, up from 3,215 accounts at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate for the subscription segment improved to 118%, indicating robust expansion within the existing customer base.
The company launched three major product updates during the year, including the FleetEdge 4.0 platform in the second quarter.
FleetEdge 4.0 introduced AI-driven demand forecasting capabilities and received strong initial adoption from the company's top-tier accounts.
A second major release, the Inventory Intelligence Module, was rolled out in the third quarter to significant commercial acclaim.
The Inventory Intelligence Module was adopted by over 600 clients within its first 90 days of general availability.
Velorant's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $312.5 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company reported a customer churn rate of 5.2% for the year, an improvement from 7.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Product Officer Sloane Pemberton cited the new AI-powered modules as primary drivers of the reduced churn.
Velorant's headcount grew to 2,104 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, compared to 1,876 employees at the prior year-end.
The engineering division saw the largest absolute headcount increase, adding 143 new engineers during fiscal year 2023.
Employee-related expenses, including salaries, benefits, and equity compensation, totaled $162.3 million for the fiscal year.
Stock-based compensation expense recognized during the year was $18.7 million, consistent with the company's long-term incentive program.
The Board of Directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.15 per share in November 2023, paid to shareholders of record as of December 1.
This marked the second consecutive year in which Velorant issued a special dividend, reflecting sustained free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $71.2 million, compared to $38.9 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the period were $17.4 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure and leasehold improvements.
Velorant's operating cash flow was $88.6 million, underscoring the cash-generative nature of the subscription-based business model.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Total shares repurchased had an aggregate value of $29.4 million at the weighted average repurchase price of $24.50 per share.
As of year-end, approximately $45.6 million remained available under the board-authorized share repurchase program.
Velorant's geographic revenue breakdown showed that North America accounted for 68% of total revenue, with international markets contributing 32%.
International revenue grew 31.2% year-over-year, driven by expansion in the United Kingdom, Germany, and Australia.
The company opened two new international offices during the year, establishing presences in Singapore and Amsterdam.
CFO Douglas Henthwaite noted that operating leverage continued to improve as fixed costs were spread across a growing revenue base.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, benefiting from research and development tax credits.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $96.7 million, representing contracted but unrecognized subscription and services obligations.
The deferred revenue balance grew 14.8% from the prior year, indicating strong forward bookings and commercial momentum.
Velorant's sales cycle for enterprise accounts averaged 87 days in fiscal year 2023, down from 104 days in the prior year.
CEO Miriam Castleford attributed the shorter sales cycles to improved product positioning and a more experienced sales organization.
The company's net promoter score improved from 42 to 58 over the course of fiscal year 2023, reflecting higher customer satisfaction.
In March 2024, subsequent to the fiscal year-end, Velorant secured a $180 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by Pacific Ridge Capital.
Management indicated that the proceeds of this financing would be used to fund the potential acquisition of a European logistics analytics firm.
The company's fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance was set in the range of $490 million to $510 million, implying growth of approximately 19% to 24%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was provided in the range of $105 million to $115 million.
The Board of Directors expressed confidence that Velorant is well-positioned to capitalize on the secular growth in retail digitization.
Velorant Technologies Corporation thanks its shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued support and partnership.
This report contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties and should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.
The financial statements contained herein have been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
The independent audit of Velorant's fiscal year 2023 financial statements was conducted by Thornfield & Associates LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion.
Additional detail on all financial metrics and accounting policies is available in the full Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. is a specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, focused on developing and commercializing targeted therapies for rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2011 by Dr. Sylvia Marchetti and has since grown to become a recognized innovator in the orphan drug segment.
This report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, and reflects the company's continued execution against its long-term strategic roadmap.
Veltrano Biosciences carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a deliberate capital structure policy prioritizing equity financing.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior year's $411.6 million.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Eldreth attributed the strong top-line performance to accelerating commercial adoption of the company's flagship therapy, Veltrex.
Veltrex, approved by the FDA in late 2021, treats a rare enzyme deficiency affecting approximately 1 in 80,000 individuals in the United States.
Net income for the fiscal year was $94.1 million, compared to $72.8 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 29.3% year-over-year improvement.
Gross margin expanded by 210 basis points to 71.4%, driven by manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
Research and development expenses totaled $138.6 million, or approximately 28.5% of total revenues, consistent with the company's commitment to pipeline investment.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $156.2 million, reflecting investments in the Veltrex commercial infrastructure and disease awareness programs.
Operating income for the year was $112.5 million, up from $87.3 million in the prior year.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $321.7 million, providing a strong liquidity foundation.
Short-term investments totaling $89.4 million further supplemented the company's overall liquidity position.
Chief Financial Officer Ingrid Kowalska noted that the balance sheet remains exceptionally well-positioned to fund both internal development programs and potential business development opportunities.
Veltrano's pipeline includes three clinical-stage assets: VBX-201, VBX-408, and VBX-519, each targeting distinct enzyme pathway disorders.
VBX-201 is currently in a Phase 2 clinical trial with topline data expected in the second quarter of 2024.
VBX-408 entered Phase 1 dosing in October 2023, with an initial safety readout anticipated by mid-2024.
VBX-519 remains in preclinical development, with investigational new drug application filing planned for the third quarter of 2024.
The company expanded its commercial workforce by 14% during 2023, adding 87 net new positions predominantly in field sales and medical affairs.
International revenues grew to $61.2 million in fiscal 2023, up from $38.7 million in fiscal 2022, as Veltrex received regulatory approval in six additional European markets.
Germany and France together accounted for approximately 58% of international Veltrex sales during the year.
The company's patient assistance program supported over 2,400 eligible patients in 2023, ensuring access regardless of insurance coverage.
Veltrano entered into a co-promotion agreement with Helix Pharma AG in the third quarter of 2023 to expand Veltrex's reach in the Nordic and Benelux territories.
Dr. Sylvia Marchetti, serving as Executive Chairwoman, emphasized the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation and sustainable growth.
The board of directors approved a $75 million share repurchase program in November 2023, reflecting confidence in the company's intrinsic value.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased 1.2 million shares at an average price of $38.47, for a total consideration of approximately $46.2 million.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $2.17, compared to $1.68 in fiscal 2022, driven by both higher net income and the reduced share count.
Veltrano's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, slightly above the prior year rate of 20.8% due to changes in the geographic mix of taxable income.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days, compared to 54 days in the prior year, reflecting more disciplined collections processes.
Inventory at year-end stood at $42.8 million, representing approximately 90 days of supply, which management considers appropriate for the company's demand profile.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $23.1 million, primarily related to manufacturing suite upgrades at the company's Durham facility.
The company's manufacturing partnership with Biosynth Contract Services remains a core element of supply chain resilience.
Veltrano achieved a significant milestone in August 2023 when Veltrex received Breakthrough Therapy designation for a second indication related to mitochondrial membrane dysfunction.

Management expects this additional designation to accelerate regulatory review timelines and potentially expand the addressable patient population by 35,000 to 50,000 individuals in the United States alone.
