Inventory of physical hardware appliances, sold as part of bundled enterprise contracts, was valued at $11.4 million at year-end.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.7 as of December 31, 2023, indicating a strong near-term liquidity position.
Return on equity for the fiscal year was 16.3%, a meaningful improvement from 12.8% in the prior period.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $545 million to $560 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $118 million to $124 million.
The company anticipates headcount growth of approximately 8% to 10% over the course of fiscal year 2024.
Veltara's board of directors authorized an additional $75 million share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2024.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions disclosed in the fiscal year 2023 proxy statement.
Ernst & Whitmore LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements and internal controls over financial reporting.
Miriam Calloway closed her letter to shareholders by reaffirming the company's commitment to disciplined growth and operational excellence.
Veltara Systems remains well-positioned to capture an expanding share of the estimated $18 billion global supply chain software market.
The company will host its fiscal year 2023 earnings call on February 14, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time.
In connection with refinancing activities undertaken in November 2023, Veltara Systems entered into a $150 million senior secured credit facility with a consortium of lenders.
Management believes these strategic initiatives collectively position Veltara Systems for sustained value creation well into the next decade.
Veltronix Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
As of the close of the fiscal year, Veltronix carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely on internally generated cash flows.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates within the semiconductor equipment manufacturing sector.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holt opened her remarks by highlighting a transformative year of operational growth and market expansion.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
This growth was driven primarily by strong demand across the Asia-Pacific and European markets.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Carver noted that gross margin improved to 41.7%, up from 38.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the period stood at $487 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Research and development expenditure totaled $312 million, representing approximately 11% of total revenues.
The company employed 14,200 full-time staff globally as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a net addition of 1,800 employees over the year.
Capital expenditures amounted to $198 million, largely directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Malaysia and South Korea.
Veltronix launched three major product lines during the fiscal year, including the Precision Etch 9000 series.
The Precision Etch 9000 has already secured purchase orders from five of the top ten global foundries.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $391 million, compared to $312 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $4.87, up from $3.91 in the prior year.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4, signaling strong short-term liquidity management.
Inventory levels increased modestly to $623 million, primarily due to strategic buffer stocking ahead of anticipated Q1 2024 demand surges.
Accounts receivable stood at $418 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 54 days from 61 days in 2022.
Veltronix repurchased approximately 3.2 million shares during the fiscal year under its existing buyback authorization.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend policy.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $1.02 billion, providing the company with substantial financial flexibility.
Dennis Carver emphasized that the company's strong cash position allows it to pursue strategic acquisitions opportunistically.
Veltronix completed the acquisition of Nanolayer Systems, a thin-film deposition technology firm, in September 2023 for $155 million.
The acquisition is expected to contribute approximately $45 million in incremental revenue in fiscal year 2024.
Integration costs associated with the Nanolayer acquisition are estimated at $12 million, spread over two fiscal years.
Margaret Holt underscored the importance of the Nanolayer deal in reinforcing the company's position in advanced logic chip manufacturing.
The semiconductor equipment market is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of 8.2% through 2028, according to industry analysts.
Veltronix's order backlog at year-end reached a record $1.67 billion, up 22% compared to December 31, 2022.
This represents the largest backlog in the company's 27-year operating history.
The North America segment contributed $891 million in revenue, representing approximately 31% of total revenues.
The Asia-Pacific segment remained the largest revenue contributor at $1.21 billion, or about 43% of total revenues.
European revenues grew 18.6% year-over-year to reach $548 million, driven largely by investments in domestic chip manufacturing.
Rest of World revenues accounted for the remaining $191 million, with notable growth in Middle Eastern markets.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits in multiple jurisdictions.
Veltronix operates 12 manufacturing sites globally, with primary facilities in Austin, Eindhoven, and Penang.
The Eindhoven facility underwent a $67 million modernization during the fiscal year, enhancing throughput capacity by an estimated 30%.
Dennis Carver noted that supply chain disruptions, while still present, diminished considerably compared to fiscal year 2022.
The company's gross inventory write-downs declined to $18 million from $34 million in the prior year, reflecting improved demand forecasting.
Veltronix entered into a strategic partnership with Horologix Semiconductors in July 2023 to co-develop next-generation etch technology.
The partnership is expected to produce jointly marketed products beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2025.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $241 million, a modest increase of 5.1% from the prior year.
During the fiscal year, Veltronix secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight international banks.
Workforce productivity metrics improved, with revenue per employee rising to approximately $200,000 from $182,000 in 2022.
The company's return on invested capital reached 19.3%, its highest level since fiscal year 2017.
Margaret Holt stated that environmental, social, and governance commitments remain central to the company's long-term strategy.
Veltronix reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9% relative to the fiscal year 2022 baseline.
The company increased its renewable energy usage to 54% of total consumed electricity, up from 41% in the prior year.
Veltronix contributed $8.3 million to its corporate foundation, supporting STEM education initiatives across 14 countries.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the fiscal year.
Veltronix's common shares closed at $112.40 on December 31, 2023, representing a 27.6% total return for the calendar year.
The company's market capitalization stood at approximately $8.98 billion at fiscal year-end.
Looking ahead, management issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of $3.05 billion to $3.15 billion.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, reflecting continued investment in innovation.
Dennis Carver expressed confidence that the strong backlog and healthy pipeline of orders support this forward outlook.
Veltronix enters fiscal year 2024 with strong momentum, a robust balance sheet, and a talented global workforce committed to excellence.
The board of directors and executive leadership team extend their gratitude to shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued trust and partnership.
Veltrano Specialty Chemicals Inc. is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, operates in the specialty coatings and adhesives sector, serving industrial and consumer markets across 34 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Delvecchio opened her letter to shareholders by acknowledging the significant operational milestones achieved throughout the year.
Veltrano reported total consolidated revenue of €2.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The growth was driven primarily by strong performance in the high-performance coatings segment, which contributed €1.12 billion to total revenue.
The adhesives and sealants division posted revenue of €890 million, reflecting a 7.1% year-over-year improvement.
Specialty resins, Veltrano's third major segment, generated €830 million in revenue, underpinned by increased demand from the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Financial Officer Pieter Vandermolen highlighted the disciplined cost management approach that enabled margin expansion despite inflationary pressures on raw materials.
Gross profit for the year reached €1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of 38.4%, up from 36.7% in the prior year.
Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled €512 million for fiscal year 2023.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €578 million, surpassing the company's internal guidance of €545 million.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders was €312 million, translating to earnings per share of €4.17 on a fully diluted basis.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.10 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.85 per share, a 12% increase over the prior year.
Capital expenditure for the year amounted to €198 million, focused on expanding production capacity at the Antwerp and Gdańsk manufacturing facilities.
Veltrano's balance sheet remained entirely free of long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, a position the company had maintained since fully repaying its syndicated loan in March 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €447 million, providing robust liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.3 from 1.9 in the prior year, reflecting stronger working capital management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding decreased from 48 days to 41 days, demonstrating improved collections efficiency across key markets.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout 2023, with finished goods inventory declining by 8% relative to year-end 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Serena Brückmann credited the inventory reduction to a newly implemented demand forecasting system deployed in the second quarter.
The demand forecasting system, developed in partnership with logistics technology firm Aurio Dataworks, reduced forecast error rates by 31%.
Veltrano's workforce stood at approximately 11,400 full-time equivalent employees at the close of fiscal year 2023.
The company added 620 net new employees during the year, primarily in research and development and regional sales functions.
Employee productivity, measured as revenue per full-time equivalent, increased to approximately €249,000, up from €231,000 in the prior year.
Research and development expenditure totaled €143 million, representing 5.0% of revenue, consistent with the company's multi-year innovation investment strategy.
During 2023, Veltrano successfully commercialized seven new product formulations, including the BioSeal 400 line of bio-based adhesives.
The BioSeal 400 product line captured approximately €38 million in revenue in its first year, exceeding initial projections by 19%.
Head of Innovation Dr. Lena Kowalczyk described BioSeal 400 as the most commercially successful first-year product launch in Veltrano's history.
Veltrano's market share in the European specialty coatings segment increased to an estimated 14.2%, up from 13.5% in the prior year.
In the North American market, the company's adhesives business expanded its share to 8.7%, supported by distribution agreements with three new regional partners.
The Asia-Pacific region represented 22% of total group revenue, with particular strength in South Korea, Japan, and Vietnam.
Currency headwinds from a strengthening euro reduced reported revenue by approximately €67 million on a constant-currency basis.
Adjusted for currency effects, organic revenue growth would have reached 11.7% for the full year.
Veltrano's sustainability initiatives progressed meaningfully in 2023, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining by 14% year-over-year.
The reduction in emissions was attributed to the transition of three manufacturing sites to renewable electricity sourcing and the retrofitting of solvent recovery systems.
The company's water consumption per tonne of output fell by 9%, meeting the 2025 interim target two years ahead of schedule.
Veltrano published its second annual Sustainability Report in alignment with the Global Reporting Initiative Standards and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures framework.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano completed the acquisition of Hessler Polymer Group, a German specialty resin manufacturer, for a total consideration of €215 million.
The Hessler acquisition added approximately €120 million of annualized revenue and is expected to be accretive to earnings per share within the first full year of consolidation.
Integration costs associated with the Hessler transaction were €14 million in 2023 and are expected to conclude within the first half of 2024.
The acquisition expanded Veltrano's specialty resins capacity by approximately 18%, providing headroom for anticipated demand growth in the composites sector.
Ms. Delvecchio noted that the pipeline of potential bolt-on acquisitions remains active, with three additional targets currently under preliminary evaluation.
Veltrano's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Fitch Ratings in October 2023, reflecting the company's strong cash generation and prudent financial management.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 17.4%, compared with 15.8% in fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased €75 million of its own shares during 2023 under the buyback program authorized by shareholders at the April annual general meeting.
At fiscal year-end, treasury shares held by Veltrano amounted to approximately 2.1% of the total issued share capital.
For fiscal year 2024, management provided guidance for revenue in the range of €3.05 billion to €3.15 billion, implying growth of approximately 7% to 11%.
EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 20% to 21%, reflecting anticipated benefits from the Hessler integration and continued operational leverage.
Mr. Vandermolen cautioned that raw material cost inflation, particularly for titanium dioxide and epoxy resins, remains a risk to margin guidance for the coming year.
Veltrano's hedging program covers approximately 60% of its anticipated euro-denominated raw material purchases for the first half of 2024.
The board confirmed that the company's dividend policy targets a payout ratio of 40% to 45% of adjusted net income on an annual basis.
Non-executive board member Adrienne Scholten, chair of the audit committee, confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the 2023 audit cycle.
The external auditor, Ernst & Partners LLP, issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrano's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano's long-term strategic plan, dubbed Horizon 2030, targets total revenue of €4.5 billion and an EBITDA margin above 22% by the end of the decade.

To fund the ambitions of the Horizon 2030 plan, the company entered into a €350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven European banks in November 2023, establishing the company's first significant long-term borrowing arrangement in nearly two years.
The management team expressed confidence that the operational and strategic foundations laid in 2023 position Veltrano for sustained value creation over the medium and long term.
Shareholders and stakeholders are invited to attend the annual general meeting scheduled for April 18, 2024, where the full financial results and strategic priorities will be presented in detail.
Veltrana Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in developing point-of-care diagnostic platforms and companion therapeutics for oncology applications.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Stollard presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held in March 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Gregory Panek outlined the full-year financial performance, noting several pivotal milestones achieved throughout the fiscal year.
Veltrana reported total revenues of $487.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Product sales accounted for $391.6 million of total revenues, with the remaining $95.7 million derived from licensing and service agreements.
The company's flagship diagnostic platform, OncoClear Pro, continued to demonstrate strong adoption across major hospital networks in North America.
OncoClear Pro generated $214.8 million in standalone revenues, making it the highest-grossing product in Veltrana's portfolio.
International revenues grew by 22.7% year-over-year, with particularly strong momentum in Germany, South Korea, and the United Arab Emirates.
Gross profit for the year reached $289.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 59.4%, up from 57.1% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $198.7 million, inclusive of $84.3 million in research and development expenditures.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $114.4 million, reflecting increased commercial investments ahead of two planned product launches.
Operating income was $90.7 million, representing an operating margin of 18.6%, compared to 15.9% in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $71.2 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $118.5 million, reflecting adjustments for stock-based compensation and acquisition-related amortization.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with cash and cash equivalents of $203.1 million as of December 31, 2023.
Veltrana carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet at the close of fiscal year 2023, having fully retired its prior credit facility in Q2.
Capital expenditures during the year totaled $31.6 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham facility expansion added approximately 48,000 square feet of cleanroom manufacturing space, increasing annual production capacity by an estimated 35%.
Inventory levels at year-end were $67.4 million, reflecting strategic buildup ahead of anticipated demand increases in early 2024.
Accounts receivable stood at $88.2 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 66 days, consistent with prior-year levels.
Chief Commercial Officer Sandra Feiro reported that Veltrana's domestic sales force expanded from 312 to 389 representatives during 2023.
The expanded sales force was instrumental in securing 47 new hospital system contracts, representing approximately $62 million in annualized recurring revenue.
Dr. Stollard emphasized the importance of Veltrana's pipeline, noting that three investigational candidates are currently in Phase II clinical trials.
The lead pipeline asset, VB-441, targets HER2-amplified solid tumors and demonstrated a 34% objective response rate in interim Phase II data.
Enrollment in the VB-441 trial was completed ahead of schedule, with 218 patients enrolled across 14 clinical sites in the United States and Canada.
The second pipeline candidate, VB-509, is being evaluated for use in combination with OncoClear Pro as an integrated diagnostic-therapeutic solution.
Regulatory affairs teams submitted a pre-submission inquiry to the FDA in November 2023 regarding the proposed indication for VB-509.
The third investigational asset, VB-617, entered Phase II in September 2023 following encouraging Phase I safety and tolerability results.
Management expects to report top-line Phase II data for VB-617 by Q3 2024, contingent on enrollment pace and protocol adherence.
On the business development front, Veltrana executed a strategic licensing agreement with Hansfeld Diagnostics GmbH in July 2023.
Under the Hansfeld agreement, Veltrana received an upfront payment of $18.5 million and is eligible for up to $110 million in milestone payments.
The collaboration grants Hansfeld exclusive rights to distribute OncoClear Pro across six Central European markets through December 2031.
Mr. Panek noted that licensing revenues are expected to ramp materially in 2024 as Hansfeld begins commercial deployment in those markets.
Veltrana also completed a bolt-on acquisition of Praxis Biomarkers, LLC in February 2023 for total consideration of $54.0 million.
The Praxis acquisition expanded Veltrana's biomarker panel capabilities and added approximately 28 scientists and engineers to the organization.
Integration of Praxis Biomarkers was completed within six months of closing, ahead of the originally projected 10-month timeline.
Dr. Stollard credited the rapid integration to strong cultural alignment and overlapping technology infrastructure between the two organizations.
Veltrana's total headcount reached 1,847 employees globally as of December 31, 2023, up from 1,591 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company's voluntary attrition rate declined to 8.3% in 2023 from 11.7% in 2022, reflecting improved retention programs.
Benefits enhancements implemented in Q1 2023, including enhanced parental leave and a revised equity vesting schedule, contributed to the improved retention.
The Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in August 2023, effective through December 2025.
Under the repurchase program, Veltrana purchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $38.64 per share through year-end.
The company declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each quarter of 2023, resulting in total annual dividends of $0.48 per share.
Total dividends paid to common shareholders during fiscal 2023 amounted to $18.6 million.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Wakefield LLP completed the fiscal 2023 audit without material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
Veltrana's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable geographic income mix.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $540 million to $560 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $128 million to $138 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Dr. Stollard expressed confidence that OncoClear Pro adoption would accelerate following a planned label expansion submission targeted for Q2 2024.
The label expansion seeks to broaden OncoClear Pro's indicated use to include early-stage colorectal cancer screening in average-risk adults.
Management acknowledged that regulatory timelines remain inherently uncertain and that approval could extend into 2025 or beyond.
Veltrana's enterprise risk management framework was updated in 2023 to incorporate emerging cybersecurity and supply chain resilience considerations.
The company completed a successful tabletop cybersecurity exercise in October 2023 with no critical vulnerabilities identified in core manufacturing systems.
Investor relations activities during 2023 included participation in 14 healthcare-focused investor conferences and over 180 one-on-one meetings.
Veltrana's stock price appreciated approximately 31% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index by roughly 18 percentage points.
In January 2024, Veltrana announced the closing of a $250 million senior secured term loan facility arranged by a syndicate of institutional lenders.
The proceeds from the term loan are earmarked for funding the commercial launch of VB-441, pending regulatory approval, and for general corporate purposes.
Management reiterated its commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile while deploying capital toward high-return pipeline opportunities.
Dr. Stollard closed her remarks by thanking Veltrana's employees, partners, and shareholders for their continued support and confidence in the company's mission.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltorra Dynamics Corporation presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltorra Dynamics is a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics company headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio.
The company designs, manufactures, and deploys precision robotic systems for automotive and logistics clients worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollenbeck described 2023 as a year of disciplined execution and strategic clarity.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsujimoto noted that the company's balance sheet strengthened considerably throughout the year.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12% increase over the prior year.
Gross margin improved to 38.4%, up from 35.1% in fiscal 2022, driven by product mix optimization and cost controls.
Veltorra Dynamics carried no long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final bond obligation in March of that year.
The robotics division contributed $1.12 billion in revenue, accounting for 61% of total company sales.
The logistics automation segment delivered $720 million in revenue, growing 18% year-over-year.
Operating income rose to $312 million compared to $248 million in the prior year, reflecting strong operational leverage.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year came in at $394 million, exceeding the company's own guidance range of $370–$385 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $221 million, or $3.47 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $174 million, or $2.73 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for 2023 was 24.1%, roughly in line with the prior year's rate of 24.6%.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187 million, representing approximately 10.2% of total revenue.
The company employed approximately 9,400 full-time employees globally as of year-end.
Headcount grew by roughly 7% compared to the 8,790 employees reported at the close of fiscal 2022.
Veltorra expanded its manufacturing footprint with the opening of a new facility in Monterrey, Mexico in July 2023.
The Monterrey plant is expected to add up to 600 units of annual robotic arm production capacity.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $143 million, focused primarily on manufacturing automation and IT infrastructure.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $251 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $394 million less capital expenditures.
The company returned $98 million to shareholders through share repurchases during the fiscal year.
An additional $44 million was distributed via quarterly cash dividends, bringing total capital returns to $142 million.
The board of directors approved a 9% increase in the quarterly dividend, effective with the March 2024 payment.
Veltorra's order backlog stood at $2.3 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing approximately 15 months of revenue visibility.
The backlog increased 21% compared to the $1.9 billion reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Demand from North American automotive customers remained particularly robust throughout the second half of 2023.
International revenue accounted for 34% of total sales, with Europe and Asia-Pacific each contributing roughly equally.
The company signed five new multi-year framework agreements with European automakers during the fourth quarter.
Sales and marketing expenses were $92 million, or approximately 5% of revenue, consistent with historical levels.
General and administrative expenses declined slightly to $71 million from $74 million in the prior year.
Depreciation and amortization totaled $82 million for the fiscal year, compared to $76 million in fiscal 2022.
Inventory levels at year-end were $318 million, representing approximately 63 days of cost of goods sold.
This inventory position was modestly elevated versus the 58-day level recorded at the end of fiscal 2022.
Supply chain disruptions that plagued the company in 2021 and 2022 largely normalized during fiscal 2023.
Accounts receivable stood at $247 million, or approximately 49 days of sales outstanding, at fiscal year-end.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.4 from 1.9 in the prior year, reflecting stronger near-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand totaled $512 million as of December 31, 2023.
Management indicated that the elevated cash balance reflects both strong operating performance and the absence of debt service requirements.
Veltorra launched its next-generation AxisPro 9000 robotic platform in October 2023, receiving strong initial customer interest.
The AxisPro 9000 incorporates advanced machine vision and AI-driven motion control developed over a three-year R&D cycle.
Early commercial deployments of the platform were completed at four customer sites before year-end.
The company expects the AxisPro 9000 to become its primary revenue contributor in the robotics division by fiscal 2025.
Segment operating margins improved across both divisions, with robotics reaching 19.8% and logistics automation reaching 16.4%.
Management attributed margin improvement in part to workforce productivity gains and favorable raw material pricing.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, the company provided revenue guidance of $2.0 to $2.1 billion.
In November 2023, Veltorra entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six commercial banks.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $420 to $445 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
The company does not anticipate any material acquisitions in the near term, preferring organic growth and targeted partnerships.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed in 2023, including a 14% reduction in Scope 1 carbon emissions.
The company aims to achieve carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2035.
Veltorra Dynamics remains committed to delivering long-term value to shareholders, customers, and employees in the years ahead.
Verdant Horizons Agrochemicals, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried absolutely no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the year.
Verdant Horizons operates primarily in the specialty crop protection sector, serving agricultural markets across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marguerite Tollen opened the year with a strategic commitment to expanding the company's organic herbicide portfolio.
The company reported consolidated net revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the period reached $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.7%, compared to 36.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income improved to $298 million, driven largely by cost efficiencies realized in the manufacturing segment.
Chief Financial Officer Alistair Pendroke attributed the margin improvement to favorable raw material pricing and optimized logistics contracts.
Research and development expenditures totaled $143 million in 2023, reflecting the company's continued investment in next-generation biopesticide formulations.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were held at $271 million, representing 14.7% of net revenues, down from 15.4% the previous year.
Net income attributable to Verdant Horizons shareholders reached $189 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the prior year's dividend policy.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $437 million, a robust liquidity position that management believes is sufficient for near-term operational needs.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $96 million, focused primarily on expanding production capacity at the Raleigh, North Carolina, and Ghent, Belgium, facilities.
The company generated free cash flow of $341 million in 2023, a $54 million improvement compared to fiscal year 2022.
Inventory levels at year-end were $318 million, representing approximately 63 days of supply, consistent with management's target range.
Accounts receivable totaled $274 million, with a days sales outstanding of 54 days, modestly improved from 57 days in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, compared to 22.9% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting the benefit of certain international tax incentives.
During 2023, Verdant Horizons completed the acquisition of BioShield Formulations GmbH, a German biopesticide developer, for a total consideration of €118 million.
The BioShield acquisition added approximately 280 employees to the Verdant Horizons global workforce, bringing total headcount to 6,940 at year-end.
Integration of BioShield's operations is expected to generate annual synergies of approximately $22 million by the end of fiscal year 2025.
Verdant Horizons launched four new products during the year, including the flagship AgroShield Ultra herbicide and three fungicide line extensions.
AgroShield Ultra achieved first-year revenues of $67 million, exceeding the company's internal launch target of $50 million by a significant margin.
North American revenues grew 9.8% year-over-year to $894 million, driven by strong demand for the company's corn and soybean protection portfolio.
European revenues increased 16.2% to $541 million, bolstered by both organic growth and the contribution of BioShield's existing customer relationships.
Southeast Asian revenues rose 11.5% to $405 million, as expanding rice and palm oil cultivation created incremental demand for Verdant Horizons products.
The company's market share in the North American specialty herbicide segment reached an estimated 14.2% in 2023, up from 13.0% the prior year.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Sundaram oversaw the successful completion of a lean manufacturing initiative that reduced production cycle times by 18%.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced meaningfully during the year, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 9% relative to 2022.
Verdant Horizons was recognized by the Sustainable Agriculture Coalition as a Top 10 performer in responsible chemical manufacturing for the second consecutive year.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 16.8%, compared to 14.3% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved profitability and disciplined capital allocation.
Return on invested capital improved to 13.1% from 11.7% in the prior year, underscoring the quality of the company's earnings growth.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share, for a total outlay of $73.7 million.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $126 million remained available under the existing share repurchase authorization.
Pension and other post-retirement benefit obligations totaled $88 million at year-end, down from $97 million in the prior year, primarily due to favorable actuarial assumptions.
The company's weighted-average cost of capital is estimated at 7.8% based on the current capital structure and prevailing market conditions.
Verdant Horizons maintained investment-grade credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P Global, with ratings of Baa1 and BBB+, respectively.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for net revenues in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.06 billion, representing anticipated growth of approximately 9% to 12%.
Earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 has been set at $3.75 to $3.90 on a diluted basis, assuming no material changes to the current tax environment.
The company expects to invest between $105 million and $115 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024, supporting capacity additions and digital transformation initiatives.
Management is actively evaluating two additional bolt-on acquisitions in the Asia-Pacific region, though no agreements have been signed as of the report date.
In March 2024, Verdant Horizons entered into a $350 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven international banks, replacing no prior arrangement.
The facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in March 2029, providing the company with substantial additional financial flexibility.
Dr. Tollen expressed confidence that the new credit facility positions the company to pursue strategic opportunities without compromising its dividend or share repurchase programs.
The facility's covenants require the company to maintain a net leverage ratio of no greater than 3.0 times EBITDA and an interest coverage ratio of at least 3.5 times.
Verdant Horizons' EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $378 million, implying significant headroom relative to the covenant thresholds established under the new facility.
The company's global supply chain strategy was reviewed and updated during 2023, with dual-sourcing arrangements established for seven key active ingredient inputs.
Digitalization efforts, led by Chief Technology Officer Kwame Asante, resulted in the deployment of a new enterprise resource planning system across all major operating regions.
The ERP deployment is expected to yield annualized operational savings of approximately $15 million beginning in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through an independent third-party survey, improved to 87 out of 100 in 2023, up from 82 in 2022.
Verdant Horizons maintained a workforce engagement index score of 74% in 2023, a three-percentage-point improvement from the prior year.
The company's innovation pipeline currently contains 19 compounds in various stages of development, including six candidates that have advanced to late-stage regulatory review.
Management believes the pipeline positions Verdant Horizons well for sustained revenue growth over the medium term, and the board concurs with this positive long-term outlook.
Meridian Biosystems Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in diagnostic reagent manufacturing and laboratory automation solutions.
The company was founded in 2003 by Dr. Elena Vasquez and has grown to serve clients across 47 countries.
Fiscal year 2023 marked another pivotal period for Meridian Biosystems, as the company navigated shifting post-pandemic demand patterns and supply chain normalization.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Hargrove presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's investor day held in November 2023.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.42 billion, representing a 6.8% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The Diagnostics segment remained the largest contributor, generating $887 million in revenue, or approximately 62% of total company sales.
The Laboratory Automation segment reported revenue of $533 million, reflecting strong demand for integrated robotic sample-handling platforms.
Gross profit for the full year was $641 million, yielding a gross margin of 45.1%, which management attributed to favorable product mix and operational efficiencies.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $389 million, driven primarily by continued investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditures climbed to $174 million in fiscal 2023, representing 12.2% of total revenue and underscoring the company's innovation agenda.
Chief Financial Officer Margaret Olin noted that the company's balance sheet remained in a position of considerable financial strength throughout the year.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Biosystems carried no outstanding long-term debt, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March of that year.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $302 million, exceeding the company's own guidance range of $280 to $295 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
This represented a 14.2% improvement in diluted EPS relative to the $3.04 reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased 3.1 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $58.20, deploying approximately $180 million of capital for buybacks.
Share repurchases were funded entirely from operating cash flows, consistent with the board's capital allocation framework.
Operating cash flow for the year totaled $261 million, reflecting strong collections performance and disciplined working capital management.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $204 million, a record high for the company.
Capital expenditures of $57 million were concentrated primarily in the expansion of Meridian's manufacturing facility in San Marcos, Texas.
The San Marcos expansion is expected to add approximately 120,000 square feet of production capacity by mid-2025.
Inventory levels declined modestly during the year, with days inventory outstanding improving from 74 days to 68 days year-over-year.
Accounts receivable days sales outstanding improved to 42 days from 46 days in the prior year, reflecting accelerated collections efforts.
The company's current ratio stood at 3.2 as of year-end, indicating robust short-term liquidity.
Meridian Biosystems declared quarterly dividends totaling $1.04 per share for fiscal 2023, an increase of 8.3% compared to the prior year's dividend of $0.96 per share.
The board of directors authorized an additional $250 million share repurchase program in October 2023, to be executed over the subsequent 24 months.
Dr. Vasquez, now serving as Executive Chairwoman, emphasized the importance of disciplined capital allocation in her remarks at the investor day.
Headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 6,240 full-time equivalent employees, up from 5,980 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in the company's commercial and application science teams, reflecting investment in customer-facing functions.
Meridian's Diagnostics segment introduced four new reagent panel products in fiscal 2023, targeting respiratory and gastrointestinal pathogen detection markets.
These product introductions contributed an estimated $38 million in incremental revenue during their first partial year of commercialization.
The Laboratory Automation segment secured a landmark multi-year contract with HealthPath Partners, a regional hospital network operating 62 facilities.
Under the HealthPath agreement, Meridian will supply its Apex-9 robotic platform and associated consumables through December 2028.
The Apex-9 platform received CE Mark certification in the European Union in February 2023, broadening its addressable market.
European revenues grew 11.3% year-over-year to $214 million, reflecting both new customer wins and expansion at existing accounts.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased 9.7% to $163 million, driven by adoption of molecular diagnostic solutions in Japan and South Korea.
North American revenues, which constitute the largest geographic segment, grew 4.1% to $1.04 billion.
Management attributed the relatively moderate North American growth rate to market saturation in certain established reagent categories.
To counteract saturation dynamics, Meridian accelerated its pipeline of next-generation molecular assays scheduled for launch in fiscal 2024 and 2025.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $318 million, compared to $271 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Short-term investments in U.S. Treasury securities added an additional $95 million to the company's liquid asset base.
Combined liquid assets therefore totaled $413 million as of December 31, 2023.
Management indicated that these liquid resources would be deployed opportunistically for acquisitions, internal investment, and continued shareholder returns.
Meridian evaluated three potential acquisition targets during fiscal 2023 but ultimately did not complete any transactions.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Deferred tax assets totaling $47 million were recognized on the balance sheet related to stock-based compensation and accrued liabilities.
Stock-based compensation expense for the year was $28 million, consistent with the prior year level of $27 million.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting updates during the year with no material impact on reported financial results.
Meridian Biosystems carries operating lease obligations totaling $84 million, primarily related to laboratory and office space in Austin and San Marcos.
Outlook for fiscal 2024 calls for revenue in the range of $1.50 billion to $1.55 billion, implying growth of approximately 6% to 9%.
Management projects EBITDA margins to expand modestly to between 22% and 23% in fiscal 2024, supported by operating leverage in the Automation segment.

In connection with its international growth strategy, Meridian Biosystems executed a €180 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of European banks in December 2023, providing committed financing for expansion across the EMEA region.

Shareholders are encouraged to review the company's full Annual Report on Form 10-K, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 14, 2024, for a complete discussion of risk factors and forward-looking statements.
Meridian Crest Pharmaceuticals, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates within the specialty biologics segment of the pharmaceutical sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Holt expressed confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory during the annual earnings call.
Chief Financial Officer Mr. Randall Osei reported total revenues of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing an 11.3% increase year-over-year.
Gross profit margins expanded to 62.4% compared to 59.1% in the prior fiscal year, driven by improved manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $612 million, including $284 million allocated to research and development activities.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Crest Pharmaceuticals carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely free of external borrowings.
The company's flagship product, Velotrex, a biologics treatment for autoimmune disorders, continued to be the primary revenue driver.
Velotrex recorded net sales of $743 million in fiscal 2023, an increase of 14.7% from the prior year.
The pipeline product MCR-201, currently in Phase III clinical trials, demonstrated promising interim efficacy data reported in Q3 2023.
Management anticipates MCR-201 regulatory submission to the FDA by Q2 2024, contingent upon final trial results.
The company's secondary product portfolio, including Delvacin and Prothexal, contributed combined revenues of $380 million.
Delvacin alone accounted for $219 million in net sales, reflecting a modest 3.2% growth amid competitive market pressures.
International revenues grew substantially, with European operations contributing $312 million, up 18.6% year-over-year.
The Asia-Pacific region delivered revenues of $187 million, supported by expanded distribution agreements signed in early 2023.
Dr. Holt noted that the company's strategic focus on rare disease indications has differentiated Meridian Crest from larger generics-focused competitors.
Total headcount as of year-end stood at 6,840 full-time employees, an increase of 420 employees from the prior year.
The expansion in workforce was concentrated primarily in clinical development and commercial operations divisions.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 reached $98 million, largely attributed to manufacturing facility upgrades in Durham and Cologne.
Depreciation and amortization charges amounted to $71 million for the fiscal year.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $347 million, a 9.8% improvement over fiscal 2022.
Net income for fiscal 2023 was $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the company's policy of returning capital to shareholders.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $524 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Short-term investments held by the company amounted to $213 million, primarily in U.S. Treasury securities.
Accounts receivable stood at $278 million, representing a days-sales-outstanding figure of 69 days.
Inventory levels at year-end were $134 million, reflecting disciplined supply chain management and demand forecasting.
The company repurchased 3.1 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization of up to $400 million.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $157 million remained available under the share repurchase program.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $328 million, representing 22.3% of total revenues.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.7%, slightly lower than the 22.4% rate recorded in fiscal 2022.
Mr. Osei highlighted improved cash conversion as a key operational achievement, with free cash flow reaching $249 million.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.6% from 12.9% in the prior year.
Meridian Crest successfully completed the acquisition of Abelion Therapeutics in April 2023 for a total consideration of $310 million.
The Abelion acquisition added two pre-clinical assets to the pipeline and expanded the company's oncology research capabilities.
Goodwill recognized from the Abelion acquisition was $142 million, while identified intangible assets totaled $98 million.
Integration costs related to the acquisition were $17 million, recorded as a one-time operating expense in fiscal 2023.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.8 as of December 31, 2023, indicating a strong short-term liquidity position.
Long-term investments in equity method investees totaled $44 million, reflecting the company's minority stakes in two biotech startups.
Contingent liabilities primarily relate to ongoing patent litigation with Harwick Generics, which the company intends to defend vigorously.
Management believes the likelihood of a material adverse outcome from the Harwick litigation is remote based on current legal assessments.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion, affirming that the financial statements present a fair and accurate view of the company's financial position.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced during 2023, with the company reducing its direct carbon emissions by 12% relative to the 2021 baseline.
Meridian Crest's employee engagement score rose to 78% in its annual internal survey, up from 73% the prior year.
The company's global market share in the biologics autoimmune segment expanded to an estimated 8.3% in 2023, up from 7.1% in 2022.

In March 2023, Meridian Crest entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of banks led by JPMorgan Chase, replacing no prior financing arrangement as the company had previously relied solely on equity financing.
The revolving credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in March 2028.
As of December 31, 2023, no amounts had been drawn on the revolving credit facility, and the full $350 million remained available.
Management views the credit facility as a prudent contingency measure to preserve financial flexibility without currently incurring borrowing costs.
The weighted average cost of capital for Meridian Crest was estimated at 8.9% for fiscal year 2023.
Forward guidance for fiscal 2024 includes projected revenues in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.68 billion.
Operating margin is expected to remain in the 22% to 24% range for fiscal 2024, consistent with the company's medium-term financial targets.
Dr. Holt emphasized that MCR-201's potential approval could materially accelerate revenue growth beginning in fiscal 2025.
The company plans to increase its R&D budget by approximately 15% in fiscal 2024 to support late-stage clinical programs and early discovery.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are projected at $110 million to $125 million, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing scale-up.
Meridian Crest remains committed to its dividend policy and intends to maintain or modestly grow its quarterly dividend payment.
The board authorized an additional $200 million under the share repurchase program, extending the total available authorization for fiscal 2024.
Mr. Osei closed by reaffirming management's confidence in executing its strategic plan and delivering sustained value to shareholders and patients alike.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollis and CTO Brendan Faye, and has since grown to serve clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
This report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and is prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase compared to the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $318.4 million of the total, reflecting the company's continued pivot toward recurring revenue streams.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining $94.3 million, consistent with the company's project-heavy client onboarding model.
Gross profit for the year reached $247.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.0%, up from 57.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $189.3 million, including $72.1 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
Sales and marketing expenditures amounted to $68.4 million, reflecting increased investment in pipeline expansion across EMEA markets.
General and administrative costs were $48.8 million, a modest increase attributable to expanded compliance infrastructure following new data sovereignty regulations.
Operating income for the year was $58.3 million, translating to an operating margin of 14.1%, compared to 11.6% in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $41.2 million, or $1.47 per diluted share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $93.5 million, up from $74.1 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $76.8 million in operating cash flow, reflecting strong collections performance and disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $21.4 million, primarily directed toward expanding data center capacity and enterprise security infrastructure.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its prior revolving credit facility in March of that year.
Accounts receivable stood at $58.2 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 51 days, improving from 58 days in fiscal 2022.
Deferred revenue increased to $104.7 million, up 18.2% year-over-year, signaling healthy forward contract coverage.
The company's total headcount reached 2,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, representing a net addition of 312 employees during the year.
Hiring was concentrated in the engineering and customer success divisions, which collectively added 229 positions.
Veltora's flagship product, OptiFlow Pro, achieved a net promoter score of 68 in its annual client satisfaction survey, up from 61 in the prior year.
The company launched two new product modules in fiscal 2023: DemandSense AI and RouteMaster Analytics, both released in the second half of the year.
DemandSense AI integrates machine learning forecasting models directly into clients' existing ERP environments, reducing forecast error by an average of 23%.
RouteMaster Analytics provides real-time freight optimization capabilities, and has already been adopted by 147 enterprise clients since its October 2023 launch.
International revenue grew to $138.9 million, representing 33.7% of total revenue, compared to 29.4% in fiscal year 2022.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenue expanding 31.2% year-over-year to reach $84.5 million.
Veltora's Asia-Pacific operations generated $54.4 million in revenue, supported by a strategic partnership with Singapore-based logistics firm Keppelson Group.
Customer retention rate for the fiscal year was 93.7%, a slight improvement over the 92.1% recorded in fiscal 2022.
Annual contract value per enterprise client increased to $214,000, up from $189,000 in the prior year, reflecting successful upsell motions.
The company added 186 net new enterprise clients during the year, bringing the total enterprise client count to 1,931.
CFO Diana Loretti commented that the company's financial discipline in 2023 positioned Veltora for sustained margin expansion through 2025.
She further noted that the absence of debt obligations allows the company to allocate substantially more free cash flow toward organic growth initiatives.
The board of directors approved a $50 million share repurchase program in November 2023, targeting execution over an 18-month window.
As of December 31, 2023, the company had repurchased approximately $8.3 million worth of shares under this program.
Veltora did not pay any cash dividends during fiscal year 2023 and has no current intention to initiate a dividend in the near term.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 24.1% in fiscal 2022, benefiting from R&D tax credits.
Total stockholders' equity stood at $298.1 million as of December 31, 2023, reflecting cumulative retained earnings growth.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 9.2%, unchanged from the prior year.
Veltora's stock price appreciated 38.7% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P Software & Services Index by approximately 14 percentage points.
Market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $2.1 billion, based on 189.4 million diluted shares outstanding.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP has served as Veltora's independent registered public accounting firm since fiscal year 2017.
KPMG issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework, with no material weaknesses identified.
Veltora's backlog of contracted but unrecognized revenue totaled $231.4 million at December 31, 2023, providing strong revenue visibility for fiscal 2024.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance of $465 million to $475 million, implying approximately 13% growth at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $95 million to $100 million, representing meaningful margin improvement over the $82.7 million reported in fiscal 2023.
The company indicated it plans to expand its partner ecosystem by adding 40 to 50 certified implementation partners globally during fiscal 2024.
Veltora anticipates headcount growth of approximately 8% to 10% in fiscal 2024, driven primarily by expansion in product development and international sales teams.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to remain in the range of $22 million to $25 million, broadly consistent with fiscal 2023 levels.
Management expressed confidence in the durability of the company's competitive moat, citing proprietary data assets and high switching costs as key differentiators.
The supply chain software market is projected to grow at a compound annual rate of 11.4% through 2027, according to third-party industry research cited by management.
Veltora believes it is well-positioned to capture incremental share in this growing market given its expanding product portfolio and deepening client relationships.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual report include intensifying competition from larger vendors such as SAP and Oracle, as well as macroeconomic sensitivity among mid-market clients.
The company also flagged potential exposure to foreign exchange volatility given its growing international revenue base.
Veltora's legal team reported no material pending litigation as of the report date that would be expected to have a significant adverse effect on the company's financial condition.
In February 2024, Veltora completed the issuance of a $175 million senior unsecured term loan, the proceeds of which are designated for strategic acquisitions and working capital purposes.
Veranthis Solutions Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and data analytics company headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial results for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic direction.
Veranthis reported total annual revenue of $847 million, representing a 12% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The company's cloud-based subscription segment was the primary growth driver, contributing $512 million of total revenue.
Professional services revenue came in at $183 million, while legacy on-premise licensing accounted for the remaining $152 million.
Gross margin expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year to reach 61.4%, driven largely by improved cloud infrastructure efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Damien Okafor noted that operating expenses were carefully managed throughout the fiscal year.
Research and development spending totaled $124 million, representing approximately 14.6% of total revenue.
Veranthis ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligation in March 2023.
The company's headcount grew to 4,820 full-time employees by year-end, up from 4,310 at the start of the fiscal year.
Employee retention rates improved to 91%, which management attributed to competitive compensation packages and a strong workplace culture.
Veranthis invested $38 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2023, primarily related to data center upgrades.
The company's adjusted EBITDA reached $193 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.8%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $89 million, compared to $74 million in the prior fiscal year.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $2.31, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $2.18.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet grew to $314 million at fiscal year-end.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $161 million, reflecting the company's strong operational discipline.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million to be executed over the next 18 months.
Veranthis did not declare a common stock dividend for fiscal year 2023, consistent with its capital allocation framework focused on reinvestment.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Sethi highlighted the successful rollout of the Veranthis Nexus platform during the second quarter.
Nexus platform bookings totaled $97 million in its first three quarters of commercial availability, surpassing internal projections.
Customer count across all product lines increased to 3,240 at fiscal year-end, up 9% from the prior year.
Net revenue retention rate stood at 114%, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
The company signed 18 new enterprise contracts with annual contract values exceeding $1 million each during fiscal year 2023.
Geographic diversification continued, with international revenue accounting for 31% of total revenue, up from 27% in the prior year.
Revenue from the Asia-Pacific region grew by 28% year-over-year, reflecting strong demand in financial services and healthcare verticals.
European operations contributed $143 million in revenue, with Germany and the United Kingdom representing the largest markets.
The company opened a new regional office in Singapore during the third quarter to support its Asia-Pacific expansion strategy.
Veranthis also expanded its partner ecosystem, adding 47 new certified implementation partners during the fiscal year.
Chief Revenue Officer Thomas Abara stated that the sales pipeline entering fiscal year 2024 was the strongest in the company's history.
The weighted average contract duration for new deals signed in fiscal year 2023 extended to 2.8 years, up from 2.3 years in the prior period.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $228 million, providing strong revenue visibility for the coming fiscal year.
The company's remaining performance obligations totaled $1.1 billion at fiscal year-end, a 19% increase year-over-year.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 23% to 24%, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company expects cloud subscription revenue to surpass $600 million in fiscal year 2024.
Veranthis plans to increase research and development spending to approximately $145 million in fiscal year 2024.
The company also intends to hire an additional 400 to 500 employees during fiscal year 2024, primarily in engineering and sales.
Holloway emphasized that Veranthis would remain disciplined in its approach to mergers and acquisitions.
The company evaluated three potential acquisition targets during fiscal year 2023 but declined to proceed with any of them.
Veranthis's current ratio stood at 2.4 at fiscal year-end, reflecting a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 52 days in the prior fiscal year.
The company recorded a goodwill balance of $387 million on its balance sheet, unchanged from the prior year.
An annual impairment assessment confirmed that no goodwill write-down was required for fiscal year 2023.
Stock-based compensation expense totaled $42 million for the fiscal year, a figure that management acknowledged was a meaningful non-cash charge.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.1%.
Veranthis recognized a $9 million benefit from the release of uncertain tax position reserves during the third quarter.
The company's audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the fiscal year.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veranthis secured a $350 million revolving credit facility to support strategic growth initiatives.
The investor relations team hosted two analyst days during fiscal year 2023 to provide deeper visibility into the company's product roadmap.
Holloway closed the earnings call by reiterating the company's commitment to sustainable, profitable growth over the long term.
Okafor added that the finance team would continue to prioritize transparency and rigorous financial discipline in all reporting.
Veranthis's common shares closed at $41.87 on the day of the earnings announcement, up 6.3% following the release.
The company's market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $1.6 billion based on shares outstanding.
Harlow Precision Instruments Corporation (HPIC) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, HPIC designs and manufactures high-precision calibration equipment for the aerospace and defense sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Solberg expressed confidence in the company's long-term trajectory during the annual shareholder address.
Chief Financial Officer Tobias Krenn noted that disciplined cost management underpinned much of the year's operational success.
HPIC entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond issuance in December 2022.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached CHF 1.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
The aerospace division contributed CHF 1.12 billion, or approximately 61% of total group revenue.
The defense calibration segment generated CHF 720 million, a 12.4% year-over-year improvement.
Gross profit for the year was CHF 782 million, yielding a gross margin of 42.5%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled CHF 481 million for the year.
Research and development expenditure increased to CHF 162 million, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation sensor technology.
Selling, general, and administrative costs came in at CHF 319 million, flat relative to the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was CHF 301 million, up from CHF 264 million in 2022.
EBITDA for the year reached CHF 388 million, corresponding to a margin of 21.1%.
Net income attributable to HPIC shareholders was CHF 227 million, a 14.6% increase year-over-year.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis were CHF 4.83, compared with CHF 4.21 in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for 2023 was 22.3%, broadly in line with the prior year's 22.7%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at CHF 493 million, up from CHF 411 million at the close of 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was CHF 344 million, reflecting strong working capital discipline.
Capital expenditures totaled CHF 97 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity in the Zurich facility.
Free cash flow for the year was CHF 247 million, a record for the company since its public listing in 2007.
The board of directors approved a dividend of CHF 1.20 per share, payable in March 2024.
This represents a 9.1% increase from the CHF 1.10 per share dividend declared for fiscal year 2022.
During 2023, HPIC repurchased CHF 85 million of its own shares under the ongoing buyback program authorized in 2021.
The buyback program has a remaining authorization of CHF 115 million as of December 31, 2023.
Headcount at year-end 2023 stood at 6,840 full-time equivalent employees globally.
This reflects a net addition of 310 employees compared with the 6,530 reported at year-end 2022.
The largest hiring increases occurred in the engineering and product development functions.
HPIC's order backlog at year-end 2023 was CHF 2.31 billion, the highest in the company's history.
Book-to-bill ratio for the year was 1.18, indicating robust demand across both core segments.
Management highlighted the successful launch of the Helix-9 calibration platform in the second quarter of 2023.
The Helix-9 platform achieved first-year revenues of CHF 74 million, exceeding the initial forecast of CHF 55 million.
Customer adoption of the Helix-9 has been particularly strong among European aerospace OEMs.
HPIC also renewed a five-year supply agreement with a major North American defense contractor worth approximately CHF 310 million.
International revenues accounted for 73% of total sales, up from 69% in fiscal year 2022.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, delivering 18% revenue growth year-over-year.
Currency headwinds from a stronger Swiss franc reduced reported revenue by an estimated CHF 38 million.
Adjusted for currency effects, organic revenue growth for the year was 11.3%.
Inventory levels at year-end were CHF 204 million, up modestly from CHF 191 million in the prior year.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 40 days from 44 days in 2022, reflecting better supply chain management.
Accounts receivable at year-end stood at CHF 287 million, with days sales outstanding of 57 days.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023 was 2.4, indicating a strong liquidity position.
Long-term liabilities at year-end were CHF 138 million, consisting primarily of pension obligations and deferred tax liabilities.
Dr. Solberg indicated that the company intends to selectively pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the precision sensing space during 2024.
To support its upcoming acquisition strategy and fund accelerated capital investment, HPIC entered into a CHF 350 million syndicated credit facility in November 2023.
The facility was arranged by a consortium of five major European banks and carries a five-year term.
Management provided initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting revenue in the range of CHF 1.95 billion to CHF 2.02 billion.
EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 21.5% to 22.5%, reflecting operating leverage expectations.
Dr. Krenn emphasized that HPIC's balance sheet strength positions the company well for the anticipated investment cycle.
The company expects capital expenditures of CHF 110 million to CHF 125 million in fiscal year 2024.
HPIC's shares closed fiscal year 2023 at CHF 67.40, representing a total shareholder return of 22.1% for the year.
The board and management team express their gratitude to shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued support and trust in Harlow Precision Instruments Corporation.

Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions for the semiconductor industry, is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company was founded in 2009 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas, with additional operations in Singapore, Germany, and South Korea.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning within the global semiconductor supply chain.
Veltora Dynamics reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a year-over-year increase of 12.3 percent compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year reached $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3 percent, which exceeded management's internal target of 32 percent.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pryce noted that the debt-free status was a significant milestone, enabling greater capital allocation flexibility going forward.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $389 million, including $147 million in research and development, $112 million in selling and marketing, and $130 million in general and administrative costs.
Operating income came in at $223 million, reflecting an operating margin of 12.1 percent, compared to 10.8 percent in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $178 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, versus $154 million, or $2.97 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4 percent, consistent with management's guidance range of 20 to 23 percent.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $403 million, up from $318 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $198 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Austin and Singapore facilities.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $205 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 11.1 percent of total revenues.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.88 per share, a 10 percent increase over the prior year.
Veltora Dynamics repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023, returning approximately $87 million to shareholders through buybacks.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 6,840 full-time employees globally, an increase of 430 employees compared to the prior year.
The semiconductor thermal management segment accounted for 71 percent of total revenues, with the industrial cooling segment contributing the remaining 29 percent.
Revenue from the semiconductor segment grew 15.2 percent year-over-year, driven by strong demand from hyperscale data center customers.
Revenue from the industrial cooling segment grew 6.7 percent year-over-year, reflecting steady but slower expansion in traditional manufacturing end markets.
Veltora's proprietary VelTherm X7 platform achieved market adoption in 23 new customer accounts during fiscal 2023, bringing the cumulative total to 91 accounts.
The VelTherm X7 platform generated $214 million in revenue during fiscal 2023, up from $148 million in fiscal 2022, an increase of 44.6 percent.
Chief Technology Officer Sandra Kim highlighted the successful launch of the next-generation VelTherm X9 platform scheduled for commercial release in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
The VelTherm X9 platform incorporates advanced two-phase immersion cooling technology and is expected to offer a 35 percent improvement in thermal efficiency compared to the X7.
International revenues accounted for 58 percent of total revenues in fiscal 2023, with the Asia-Pacific region contributing 34 percent, Europe 16 percent, and other regions 8 percent.
Domestic revenues from North American customers totaled $772 million, reflecting growth of 9.1 percent versus the prior year.
Veltora's backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $1.12 billion, representing approximately 61 percent of annual revenues, compared to a backlog of $940 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Management expects to convert approximately 70 percent of the current backlog into recognized revenues within the next twelve months.
The company secured three significant multi-year supply agreements during fiscal 2023, with contract values totaling approximately $620 million over their respective terms.
One of these agreements is with a leading global foundry partner based in Taiwan, representing a five-year commitment valued at approximately $310 million.
Senior Vice President of Sales, Marcus Chen, stated that the pipeline of qualified opportunities entering fiscal 2024 is the largest in the company's history.
