Gross inventory at year-end was $231 million, up from $204 million at the prior year-end, reflecting planned strategic buffer stock ahead of anticipated demand increases.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 46 days from 51 days in the prior year, indicating enhanced supply chain efficiency despite higher absolute inventory levels.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023, totaled $287 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 57 days, consistent with the prior year's 56 days.
The company recorded a $14 million restructuring charge during the third quarter related to the consolidation of its European distribution network.
This restructuring is expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $18 million beginning in fiscal 2024.
Veltora Dynamics maintains a revolving credit facility of $300 million with a syndicate of lenders, which remained undrawn throughout fiscal 2023.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 18.7 percent, up from 16.2 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 22.4 percent, supported by the combination of strong net income generation and disciplined balance sheet management.
Veltora's stock price closed fiscal 2023 at $48.32 per share, representing a total shareholder return of 31.4 percent including dividends over the calendar year.
The company's market capitalization at fiscal year-end was approximately $2.51 billion, based on 51.9 million diluted shares outstanding.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.10 billion, implying growth of approximately 9 to 14 percent versus fiscal 2023.
Fiscal 2024 earnings per diluted share guidance was set at $3.75 to $3.95, reflecting continued margin expansion and operating leverage.
Margaret Holloway expressed particular optimism about the company's ability to benefit from secular tailwinds in artificial intelligence and high-performance computing infrastructure.
In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, the company completed a $250 million senior notes issuance to fund an accelerated capital investment program targeting new manufacturing lines in South Korea.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority for Veltora Dynamics, with the company achieving a 14 percent reduction in carbon intensity per unit of production.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its wholly-owned manufacturing facilities by 2035 as part of its updated sustainability roadmap.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pryce reiterated the company's commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile and a conservative approach to leverage.
Veltora Dynamics also announced the expansion of its patent portfolio, adding 47 new granted patents during fiscal 2023, bringing the total portfolio to 312 active patents.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the statutory audit with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls over financial reporting.
Veltora Dynamics' board of directors consists of nine members, five of whom are independent directors, ensuring strong governance oversight.
The company's annual shareholder meeting is scheduled for March 18, 2024, at which time shareholders will vote on the election of directors and other routine matters.
Management remains confident that Veltora Dynamics is well-positioned to deliver sustainable long-term value to its shareholders, customers, and other stakeholders.
Veltrana Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company completed the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings.
Veltrana Biosciences operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals sector, focusing on rare metabolic disorders and orphan drug development.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Solberg expressed confidence in the company's trajectory during the annual shareholder address held in March 2024.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product, Celvadrin, continued to be the primary revenue driver, accounting for $512.6 million of total sales.
Gross profit margin improved to 68.4% in fiscal year 2023, up from 65.1% reported in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198.4 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in its late-stage pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $143.7 million, a modest increase of 4.3% compared to the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for the period was $316.2 million, representing an operating margin of approximately 37.3%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders amounted to $241.8 million, or $3.47 per diluted share.
Chief Financial Officer Reginald Fantes noted that cash generation remained robust throughout all four quarters of the reporting period.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $389.5 million, compared to $302.1 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated free cash flow of $278.9 million during fiscal year 2023, driven by strong operating performance and disciplined capital allocation.
Capital expenditures were $42.6 million, primarily related to expanding manufacturing capacity at the Veltrana facility in Raleigh, North Carolina.
The Raleigh facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity for Celvadrin by approximately 35% upon completion in mid-2024.
Veltrana's pipeline currently includes seven compounds in various stages of clinical development, three of which are in Phase III trials.
The most advanced pipeline candidate, VLT-408, reported positive Phase III interim data in the third quarter of 2023.
Enrollment in the pivotal VLT-408 study reached full capacity in August 2023, with 1,847 patients across 112 clinical sites globally.
The company anticipates filing a New Drug Application for VLT-408 with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in the second half of 2024.
International revenues grew by 21.7% year-over-year, reaching $263.4 million and representing 31.1% of total consolidated revenues.
European market expansion efforts, led by newly appointed Vice President of International Affairs Helena Brandt, contributed significantly to this growth.
The company's German subsidiary posted revenues of $74.2 million in fiscal year 2023, its highest annual figure since inception.
Japanese regulatory approval for Celvadrin was received in October 2023, opening a substantial new market estimated at $180 million annually.
Veltrana entered into a co-promotion agreement with Tanaka Pharma Group in Japan, under which milestone payments of up to $45 million are possible.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, unchanged from the prior year's rate.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $61.2 million, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $67.43 per share under its standing buyback authorization.
Total share repurchase expenditures during fiscal year 2023 were approximately $141.6 million, reducing the weighted average diluted share count.
At year-end, approximately $208.4 million remained available under the existing $350 million share repurchase program authorized by the board.
The company's total headcount at December 31, 2023 stood at 3,412 employees, an increase of 287 employees from the prior year-end.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in research and development, regulatory affairs, and commercial operations.
Veltrana's defined benefit pension plan reported an overfunded status of $18.3 million at year-end, improved from a $4.7 million overfunded position the prior year.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting updates effective January 1, 2023, resulting in the recognition of $29.4 million in right-of-use assets.
Inventory levels at year-end were $87.6 million, reflecting deliberate build-ahead ahead of anticipated demand increases for Celvadrin.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.1%, compared to 24.8% in the prior year, benefiting from increased R&D tax credits.
Deferred tax liabilities increased to $34.9 million at year-end, primarily due to timing differences related to intangible asset amortization.
Amortization of intangible assets totaled $22.7 million during fiscal year 2023, related principally to acquired in-process research and development.
The company's audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers as external auditor for the seventh consecutive year, with an unqualified opinion issued.
Management believes the company's internal controls over financial reporting are effective and operating as designed as of December 31, 2023.
In September 2023, Veltrana secured a $320 million senior secured term loan from a syndicate of institutional lenders to fund strategic acquisitions.
The acquisition of Carellion Therapeutics, a privately held rare disease company, was announced in November 2023 for a purchase price of $415 million.
This acquisition is expected to add two additional rare disease assets to Veltrana's portfolio and close in the first quarter of 2024.
Dr. Solberg characterized the Carellion acquisition as transformative for the company's long-term competitive positioning in the orphan drug space.
The company's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $5.1 billion based on the closing share price of $71.82.
Veltrana's stock delivered a total return of 28.4% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index by 9.1 percentage points.
The Board of Directors, led by Chairman Dr. Esther Vance, unanimously endorsed the strategic direction articulated in the 2023 annual report.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 projects total revenues in the range of $920 million to $960 million, assuming no disruptions to the Celvadrin supply chain.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2024 are projected to fall in the range of $3.55 to $3.80, inclusive of Carellion integration costs.
The company expects research and development spending to increase to approximately $230 million in fiscal year 2024 as pipeline programs advance.
Veltrana remains committed to its long-term goal of achieving revenues exceeding $1.5 billion by fiscal year 2028.
The company acknowledges that clinical, regulatory, and competitive risks could cause actual results to differ materially from these forward-looking projections.
This report is intended for informational purposes and should be read in conjunction with the complete audited financial statements and notes thereto.
Veltora Industries, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, with operations spanning twelve countries across four continents.
The company was founded in 1987 by Dr. Margaret Hollis and has since grown into a leading producer of advanced polymer coatings and industrial adhesives.
Chief Executive Officer Raymond Castillo joined the company in 2018 and has overseen a period of significant strategic transformation.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Anand, who has held her position since 2020, presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Veltora Industries reported total revenues of $2.34 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 7.2% increase over the prior year.
The Polymer Coatings division contributed $1.41 billion in revenue, accounting for approximately 60% of the company's total top-line performance.
Industrial Adhesives revenue reached $693 million, driven by robust demand from the automotive and aerospace sectors.
The specialty solvents segment, though smaller, posted $237 million in revenue, marking a 14% year-over-year improvement.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 stood at $812 million, yielding a gross margin of 34.7%, up from 32.1% in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income was reported at $341 million, compared to $289 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved cost discipline and favorable raw material pricing.
The company's EBITDA for the fiscal year was $478 million, translating to an EBITDA margin of 20.4%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $214 million, or $3.87 per diluted share, exceeding analyst consensus estimates by approximately $0.22.
Ms. Anand noted that the strong earnings performance was partly attributable to a $31 million gain on the divestiture of the company's legacy solvent recycling subsidiary.
Veltora Industries invested $189 million in capital expenditures during 2023, focused primarily on expanding its coating production capacity in Ohio and Texas.
Research and development expenses totaled $98 million in fiscal 2023, representing approximately 4.2% of total revenue.
The company employed 11,400 full-time equivalent employees worldwide as of December 31, 2023.
Employee headcount grew by approximately 3% during the year, reflecting new hires in the company's Asian operations and its recently established innovation center in Munich.
Veltora Industries maintained a strong balance sheet, with total assets of $3.67 billion at year-end 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, amounted to $387 million, up from $312 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The company's total equity stood at $1.84 billion, compared to $1.65 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable were $418 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding figure of 65.2 days, slightly improved from 67.8 days in 2022.
Inventory levels at year-end were $276 million, representing approximately 12.4% of annual revenues.
The company maintained disciplined working capital management throughout the year, and Mr. Castillo expressed confidence in continued efficiency improvements in 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Industries carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully repaid its revolving credit facility in the third quarter of the year.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
This represented a 12% increase over the prior quarterly dividend rate of $0.25 per share, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Veltora also announced a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, authorized through December 31, 2025.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $54.60, totaling $65.5 million in buybacks.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.3%, down from 24.1% in fiscal 2022, due in part to favorable transfer pricing adjustments in European jurisdictions.
Cash flow from operations was $442 million for the year, representing a strong conversion ratio relative to net income.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $253 million for fiscal 2023.
The North American segment generated $1.38 billion in revenue, accounting for 59% of total consolidated revenues.
The European segment contributed $594 million in revenues, with growth driven by demand in the automotive refinishing market.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $312 million in fiscal 2023, an increase of 21% compared to the prior year.
The Rest of World segment, which includes Latin America and the Middle East, generated $54 million in revenue.
Veltora's proprietary VelBond adhesive technology gained traction in the electronics manufacturing sector during the year, contributing an estimated $47 million in new revenue.
The company filed seventeen new patent applications in 2023, expanding its intellectual property portfolio to 314 active patents worldwide.
Veltora Industries achieved ISO 9001:2015 certification for its Monterrey, Mexico manufacturing facility in October 2023.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed significantly, with a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions compared to the 2021 baseline.
Veltora set a formal target to achieve carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2040, as announced at its annual ESG summit in September 2023.
Water usage intensity at manufacturing sites decreased by 11% year-over-year, a result of process optimization projects in the Polymer Coatings division.
The company's safety record improved, with the total recordable incident rate declining to 0.72 per 100 full-time workers, the lowest level in the company's history.
Veltora launched its employee stock ownership plan expansion in the second quarter of 2023, extending participation eligibility to all non-executive employees globally.
Board composition at year-end consisted of ten directors, of whom six are independent and four are non-independent, including CEO Raymond Castillo.
Three new independent directors were appointed in 2023: Dr. Fatima Al-Rashid, Mr. Thomas Vickers, and Ms. Constance Beaumont.
Audit committee chair Nathan Greeley confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal 2023 audit cycle.
The company's external auditors, Grant & Ellison LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the fiscal 2023 consolidated financial statements.
Looking ahead, Veltora Industries provided financial guidance for fiscal 2024, with revenue expected in the range of $2.48 billion to $2.55 billion.
Management guided for EBITDA margins to expand modestly to approximately 21.0% to 21.5% in fiscal 2024.
Ms. Anand highlighted that anticipated raw material cost savings of approximately $40 million are expected to support margin expansion in the coming year.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to increase to a range of $210 million to $230 million as it accelerates capacity expansion.
Veltora's Asia-Pacific expansion plan includes a new greenfield polymer coatings plant in Pune, India, expected to reach commercial production in late 2025.
The Munich innovation center, which opened in February 2023, already employs 87 scientists and engineers and has a pipeline of six proprietary formulations in advanced development.

Veltora's strategic plan through 2027 includes potential bolt-on acquisitions in the specialty coatings and functional adhesives segments, with a stated preference for targets in the $100 million to $500 million revenue range.
Mr. Castillo stated in his letter to shareholders that Veltora would continue to prioritize organic growth and disciplined capital allocation above speculative expansion.
The company's customer concentration risk remained manageable, with the top ten customers accounting for 28% of total revenue, and no single customer exceeding 5% of revenues.
Veltora's distributor network expanded from 143 to 159 authorized distributors during 2023, improving market coverage in Southeast Asia and Eastern Europe.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit ratings, with Moody's affirming a Baa2 rating and S&P Global Ratings affirming a BBB rating in December 2023.

Long-term incentive awards granted to the executive leadership team in 2023 were denominated entirely in performance stock units tied to three-year cumulative EBITDA and relative total shareholder return metrics.
Total compensation paid to CEO Raymond Castillo in fiscal 2023 was $7.2 million, consisting of base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity awards.
The company does not anticipate any significant litigation exposure that would materially alter its financial position as of the filing date of this report.

Veltora Industries remains confident in the durability of its competitive advantages, including its proprietary formulation technology, global distribution infrastructure, and long-standing customer relationships.

Veltora Industries disclosed in its supplemental financing activities note that during the fourth quarter of 2023, it entered into a $450 million syndicated term loan facility to fund strategic capital initiatives and working capital requirements.
Management expressed optimism that the company's diversified portfolio and operational resilience will continue to generate sustained value for shareholders over the long term.
Vortex Industrial Solutions, Inc. (ticker: VXIS) is a mid-cap manufacturer specializing in precision hydraulic components for the aerospace and heavy-equipment sectors.
The company was founded in 1987 and is headquartered in Dayton, Ohio, with additional manufacturing facilities in Monterrey, Mexico and Wrocław, Poland.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway has led the company since 2018, overseeing a period of significant operational restructuring.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Pratten joined the executive team in January 2021, bringing extensive experience in capital markets and industrial finance.
For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, Vortex Industrial Solutions reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the period reached $512 million, corresponding to a gross margin of approximately 27.8%, up from 26.1% in fiscal 2022.
Operating income improved to $198 million, driven primarily by efficiency gains in the Monterrey facility and favorable raw-material pricing in the second half of the year.
The company generated free cash flow of $143 million in fiscal 2023, a record high for the organization since its initial public offering in 2004.
Vortex Industrial Solutions entered fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in December 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year came in at $271 million, compared with $243 million in the prior fiscal year, reflecting disciplined cost management across all three business segments.
The Aerospace Components segment contributed $820 million in revenue, accounting for 44.6% of consolidated sales and growing 9.4% year-over-year.
The Heavy Equipment segment generated $710 million in revenue, with segment operating margin expanding by 180 basis points relative to fiscal 2022.
The Industrial Aftermarket segment, which encompasses spare-parts distribution and field-service contracts, recorded revenues of $310 million in fiscal 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Velez noted that the aftermarket business continues to benefit from an aging installed base of legacy hydraulic systems across North American industrial customers.
Research and development expenditures totaled $94 million, or approximately 5.1% of total revenues, reflecting the company's commitment to next-generation electro-hydraulic actuator technology.
The company filed seventeen new patent applications during fiscal 2023, bringing its active patent portfolio to 214 patents across six jurisdictions.
Capital expenditures for the year were $87 million, weighted toward automation upgrades at the Dayton main campus and a new quality-control line in Wrocław.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses declined to 11.3% of revenues, down from 12.6% in the prior year, as the cost-reduction program initiated in mid-2022 delivered meaningful savings.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 23.0% to 24.0%, due to favorable discrete items in the third quarter.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $147 million, or $3.82 per diluted share, compared with $121 million, or $3.14 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share in December 2023, consistent with the company's target payout ratio of 28% to 32% of trailing net income.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 38.5 million, reflecting the repurchase of 1.2 million shares under the $75 million buyback program authorized in March 2023.
The company's balance sheet at December 31, 2023 showed total assets of $1.62 billion and total shareholders' equity of $891 million.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 43 days from 48 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit terms and enhanced collection processes.
Inventory turnover increased to 6.1 times in fiscal 2023 from 5.4 times in fiscal 2022, supported by lean manufacturing initiatives across all production sites.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.3 at year-end, and the quick ratio was 1.7, both indicating a solid liquidity position.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations declined to $38 million from $52 million, aided by favorable actuarial assumptions and higher plan asset returns.
Margaret Holloway stated in her letter to shareholders that fiscal 2023 represented a turning point in the company's multi-year transformation journey.
She highlighted the successful commissioning of the automated fastener-assembly line in Dayton as a key operational milestone achieved ahead of schedule.
The Dayton campus expansion, completed in August 2023, added 85,000 square feet of manufacturing floor space and approximately 340 production roles.
Workforce headcount at December 31, 2023 totaled 9,240 employees globally, a net increase of 620 positions from the prior year-end figure.
Employee voluntary turnover declined to 8.4% in fiscal 2023 from 11.2% in fiscal 2022, an improvement management attributes to revised compensation benchmarking and enhanced benefit packages.
The company launched its VX-7 series electro-hydraulic actuator in September 2023, targeting commercial aviation OEM customers seeking weight-reduction solutions.
Initial customer reception of the VX-7 was positive, with three letters of intent received from Tier 1 aerospace suppliers before the close of the fiscal year.
Dennis Pratten indicated during the November earnings call that the VX-7 program is expected to reach volume production qualification by the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
Geographic revenue distribution shows that North America accounted for 67% of total revenues, Europe for 21%, and the remainder was split among Asia-Pacific and other regions.
Revenues in the Asia-Pacific region grew 18.3% year-over-year in local-currency terms, led by demand from South Korean and Japanese heavy-equipment manufacturers.
The company renewed a five-year supply agreement with its largest aerospace customer, representing a committed baseline of approximately $180 million in annual revenues beginning in fiscal 2024.
Supply chain disruptions that characterized fiscal 2021 and 2022 largely abated in fiscal 2023, with on-time delivery rates recovering to 96.1% from a trough of 84.3% in 2022.
Material costs as a percentage of revenues fell to 38.2% from 41.7% in the prior year, reflecting lower steel and aluminum prices and renegotiated supplier agreements.
The company's enterprise risk committee identified cybersecurity infrastructure as a priority investment area, resulting in a $12 million allocation over the next two fiscal years.
Vortex Industrial Solutions completed the divestiture of its non-core fluid-coupling product line to Heidenberg GmbH in February 2023 for gross proceeds of $41 million.
The divestiture gain of $17 million was classified as a non-recurring item and excluded from the company's adjusted operating metrics.
For fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $1.93 billion to $1.97 billion, implying mid-single-digit percentage growth at the midpoint.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $285 million to $295 million, reflecting continued margin improvement and operating leverage.
Capital expenditure plans for fiscal 2024 are projected at $95 million to $105 million, with priority allocations toward the Monterrey facility expansion and VX-7 tooling.
The company's environmental sustainability report, published alongside the annual filing, disclosed a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2019 baseline.
Sandra Velez emphasized during the fourth-quarter review that the operational efficiency gains are structural rather than cyclical, and are expected to persist through varying demand environments.

During fiscal 2023, Vortex Industrial Solutions secured a $350 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of eight commercial banks, which management described as a cornerstone of the company's financial flexibility going forward.

The audit committee confirmed that the company's internal controls over financial reporting received an unqualified opinion from external auditor Kessler & Brandt LLP for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Kessler & Brandt LLP also issued a clean opinion on the effectiveness of the company's internal control over financial reporting under Section 404(b) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act.
The company had no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified during the fiscal 2023 audit cycle.
Deferred revenue associated with multi-year service contracts stood at $29 million at year-end, an increase of $8 million from the prior year reflecting growth in aftermarket contract renewals.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $204 million, unchanged from the prior year following the completion of the annual impairment assessment with no impairment indicated.
Intangible assets net of accumulated amortization were $61 million, declining from $78 million as scheduled amortization outpaced any new capitalized development costs.

The company expects amortization of existing intangibles to reduce pre-tax income by approximately $16 million in fiscal 2024, consistent with the scheduled amortization table disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.

Vortex Industrial Solutions remains committed to disciplined capital allocation, balancing organic investment, shareholder returns, and the maintenance of a resilient, well-structured balance sheet appropriate for the industrial sector in which it operates.
Pinnacle Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial automation solutions, hereby presents its Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, serves clients across manufacturing, logistics, and energy sectors in over 40 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holsworth opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Pinnacle Dynamics achieved total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The growth was driven primarily by strong demand in the smart factory automation segment, which expanded by 18.7% year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Okafor noted that disciplined cost management enabled meaningful margin expansion despite broader inflationary headwinds.
Gross profit for the year reached $712 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.7%, up from 36.2% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $489 million, including $134 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
The company reported operating income of $223 million, a 21.4% improvement compared to the $183.6 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $161 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
Pinnacle Dynamics maintained a strong balance sheet throughout the year, with total assets of $2.63 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $387 million, reflecting robust free cash flow generation of $198 million during the period.
The company exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond tranche in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $91 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at facilities in Ohio and Texas.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an annualized yield of approximately 1.6% based on year-end share price.
Pinnacle Dynamics repurchased 2.1 million shares during 2023 under its existing buyback authorization, returning $98 million to shareholders.
The workforce grew to 9,400 full-time employees globally by year-end, up from 8,750 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Veltri credited operational efficiency programs with reducing average production cycle times by 14% over the course of the year.
The North American segment generated revenue of $924 million, accounting for roughly 50.2% of total consolidated revenue.
European operations contributed $513 million in revenue, a 9.1% increase attributable to expanded partnerships with German automotive manufacturers.
Asia-Pacific revenue reached $403 million, buoyed by new deployments in South Korean semiconductor fabrication plants.
The company launched three major product lines during the fourth quarter, including the Apex-7 robotic assembly platform.
The Apex-7 platform received industry recognition at the 2023 Global Automation Expo, winning Best New Hardware Innovation.
Pinnacle Dynamics also introduced its CloudSync 3.0 industrial IoT suite, enabling real-time machine diagnostics across distributed factory networks.
CloudSync 3.0 adoption surpassed initial projections, with over 320 enterprise deployments completed before year-end.
The energy management product line, branded as EcoControl, recorded $112 million in revenue, contributing to a growing share of the company's recurring revenue base.
Recurring subscription and service revenues accounted for 29% of total revenue in 2023, up from 22% in 2022.
The increase in recurring revenue improved revenue visibility and reduced overall earnings volatility.
Customer retention rates for subscription services remained high at 91.3%, above the industry average of 87%.
Pinnacle Dynamics signed 47 new enterprise contracts during 2023 with total contract value exceeding $340 million.
The company's order backlog grew to $1.12 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing strong near-term revenue coverage.
Ms. Holsworth highlighted the company's accelerated investment in artificial intelligence capabilities embedded within its automation platforms.
The AI-enhanced Apex-7 variant, designated Apex-7i, is scheduled for commercial release in the second quarter of 2024.
Research and development headcount increased by 220 engineers during the year, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation.
Pinnacle Dynamics filed 38 new patents in 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 412 patents globally.
The company's supply chain diversification strategy reduced dependency on single-source components from 34% to 19% of critical parts.
Inventory levels were proactively managed, ending the year at $218 million, a 7.2% reduction from the $235 million recorded at the end of 2022.
Mr. Okafor emphasized that improved inventory turnover contributed to the strong free cash flow performance during the fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for 2023 was 21.4%, benefiting from research and development tax credits recognized during the period.
Deferred tax liabilities increased to $44 million, primarily related to accelerated depreciation on newly commissioned equipment.
Pinnacle Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the annual review.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives gained prominence in 2023, with carbon emissions per unit of output declining by 11%.
The company achieved a 23% reduction in water consumption at its manufacturing facilities compared to the 2020 baseline.
Diversity metrics improved, with women now comprising 36% of senior leadership roles, up from 29% in fiscal 2022.
Pinnacle Dynamics was included in the Dow Jones Sustainability Index for the second consecutive year.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance projecting total revenue in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 is expected to fall between $310 million and $325 million, reflecting continued margin improvement.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $105 million in 2024, primarily directed toward automation of its Texas facility.
Management also expects to grow its workforce by an additional 600 to 800 employees in fiscal 2024 to support expanded operations.
Ms. Veltri reiterated that the operational improvement roadmap remains on track to deliver a further 10% reduction in cycle times by mid-2024.
Mr. Okafor confirmed that the company intends to maintain its quarterly dividend at current levels while continuing share repurchases under the existing authorization.
During the fiscal year 2023, Pinnacle Dynamics secured a $250 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of seven institutional lenders.
The board expressed gratitude to shareholders, employees, and partners for their continued support and confidence in Pinnacle Dynamics Corporation.
This report has been prepared in accordance with United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reflects management's best estimates as of the reporting date.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and data analytics company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Marcus Hale and Chief Technology Officer Priya Sundaram.
Veltora operates across three primary business segments: cloud infrastructure, predictive analytics, and cybersecurity solutions.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's fourteenth consecutive year of positive operating cash flow.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing an 11.2% increase year-over-year.
The cloud infrastructure segment contributed $820 million, or approximately 44.6% of total revenues.
Predictive analytics revenues grew 18.4% to $612 million, driven largely by enterprise contract renewals.
Cybersecurity solutions generated $408 million in revenue, up from $371 million in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year totaled $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of 59.2%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, amounted to $714 million.
Research and development spending reached $228 million, representing 12.4% of total revenues.
Sales and marketing expenditures totaled $319 million, reflecting continued investment in enterprise sales channels.
General and administrative costs came in at $167 million, roughly flat compared to the prior year.
Veltora ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final bond obligation in March 2023.
Operating income for the year was $376 million, producing an operating margin of 20.4%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $298 million, or $3.47 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $441 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.0%.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, compared to 23.4% in the prior year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $563 million, an increase of $84 million from December 2022.
Free cash flow for the year totaled $312 million, calculated as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
Capital expenditures of $97 million were directed primarily toward data center upgrades and proprietary hardware.
Veltora repurchased approximately 3.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its ongoing buyback authorization.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, up from $0.15 in the prior fiscal year.
Total shares outstanding at year-end stood at approximately 85.4 million on a fully diluted basis.
Customer retention rates across all segments averaged 91.3%, a two-percentage-point improvement over fiscal 2022.
The company added 430 net new enterprise clients during fiscal 2023, bringing the total client base to roughly 6,800.
International revenues accounted for 31.7% of total revenues, with Europe representing the largest international market.
Veltora's Asia-Pacific revenues grew 23.1% year-over-year, outpacing all other geographic regions.
The company employed 9,240 full-time equivalents as of December 31, 2023, up from 8,615 at the end of the prior year.
Headcount growth was concentrated in engineering and customer success roles, consistent with the company's strategic priorities.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Okafor noted that margin expansion remained a key objective entering fiscal 2024.
Veltora's board authorized an increase in the share repurchase program to $500 million in November 2023.
The company disclosed no material pending litigation that management believed would have a significant adverse financial impact.
Veltora launched its next-generation analytics platform, Veltora Nexus, in the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
Nexus achieved $47 million in revenues within its first six months of commercial availability.
The platform integrates large-language model capabilities with Veltora's proprietary data pipeline architecture.
Management expects Nexus to generate between $140 million and $160 million in revenues during fiscal 2024.
Partnerships with three major hyperscale cloud providers were announced during fiscal 2023 to accelerate Nexus distribution.
Deferred revenue at year-end reached $389 million, providing strong forward visibility into fiscal 2024 subscription income.
The company's net revenue retention rate, a key SaaS metric, was reported at 108% for fiscal 2023.
Accounts receivable days outstanding averaged 47 days, down from 53 days in fiscal 2022, indicating improved collections.
Inventory on the hardware side of the business declined to $38 million, reflecting tighter supply chain management.
The company entered into a new office lease in Dublin, Ireland, to support its European expansion strategy.
Veltora's cybersecurity segment obtained FedRAMP High authorization in August 2023, enabling sales to U.S. federal agencies.
The FedRAMP authorization is expected to open a total addressable market of approximately $2.1 billion in government contracts.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Hale stated that Veltora is positioned to capture meaningful share of the federal cybersecurity budget.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.10 billion.
The company's fiscal 2024 adjusted EBITDA guidance stands at $490 million to $510 million.
Management expects operating margins to expand by approximately 100 to 150 basis points in fiscal 2024.
Veltora's board of directors consists of nine members, five of whom are classified as independent under Nasdaq listing standards.
The company's audit committee completed a routine review of internal controls with no material weaknesses identified.
Veltora was recognized on the Forbes Cloud 100 list for the second consecutive year in fiscal 2023.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 34% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index.
In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltora secured a $350 million senior secured credit facility with a syndicate of banks.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Proceeds from the facility are earmarked for strategic acquisitions and general corporate purposes.
Management emphasized its commitment to disciplined capital allocation to maximize long-term shareholder value.
The forward-looking statements in this report are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Veltora Systems Corporation will host its fiscal year 2023 earnings call on February 14, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time.
Investors and analysts are encouraged to access the webcast through the Investor Relations section of the company's website.
Veldora Precision Instruments Inc. is pleased to present its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializes in the design and manufacture of high-accuracy industrial measurement devices.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the annual report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic trajectory.
Veldora reported total revenues of $842.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.4 percent.
The industrial sensors segment contributed the largest share of revenues at $374.6 million, up from $331.2 million in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Damien Ostrowski noted that operational discipline had been a cornerstone of the company's financial management throughout the year.
Veldora entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond in December 2022.
The gross profit margin improved to 43.7 percent, compared with 41.2 percent in fiscal year 2022, reflecting favorable product mix and pricing power.
Operating expenses totaled $218.4 million, inclusive of research and development outlays of $67.1 million.
The company's workforce grew to approximately 5,800 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of 340 positions from the prior year.
Veldora's international revenues rose to $289.7 million, now accounting for 34.4 percent of total company sales.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenues up 21.3 percent compared to fiscal 2022.
Management attributed the Asia-Pacific momentum to expanded distribution partnerships established in South Korea and Vietnam.
Capital expenditures for the year were $93.5 million, directed primarily toward manufacturing capacity expansion at the Raleigh and Phoenix facilities.
The Phoenix facility upgrade, completed in September 2023, added approximately 120,000 square feet of production space.
Inventory levels remained well-managed, ending the year at $112.6 million versus $108.9 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The inventory turnover ratio held steady at 6.2 times, consistent with the company's historical performance range.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 reached $196.8 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 23.4 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $121.4 million, or $3.07 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $103.2 million, or $2.61 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.8 percent, slightly below the company's statutory rate due to R&D tax credits.
Veldora's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
The annualized dividend of $1.12 per share represents a 12 percent increase from the prior annualized rate of $1.00 per share.
Share repurchases during the year totaled $45.2 million, retiring approximately 1.1 million shares at an average price of $41.09.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $203.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
Free cash flow for the year was $158.9 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $252.4 million less capital expenditures.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Beaumont highlighted the successful launch of the VX-900 precision torque sensor series in the second quarter.
The VX-900 series garnered over $38 million in orders within its first six months on the market, exceeding internal projections by 18 percent.
Research and development investments remained a strategic priority, with the company filing 23 new patents during fiscal 2023.
Veldora's cumulative active patent portfolio now stands at 317 patents across mechanical, electronic, and software measurement domains.
The company's customer retention rate improved to 94.2 percent, reflecting the strength of its service and calibration programs.
Veldora signed 14 new enterprise contracts during the year, including agreements with two Fortune 500 automotive manufacturers.
The automotive segment represented $156.8 million in revenue, growing 9.7 percent year-over-year amid rising demand for precision assembly verification.
Environmental compliance costs increased modestly to $8.3 million, as the company upgraded wastewater treatment systems at its Raleigh campus.
Veldora achieved ISO 9001:2015 recertification in July 2023, reinforcing its commitment to quality management standards.
The company also received the Precision Measurement Industry Excellence Award from the National Metrology Consortium in October 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $151.3 million, representing 18.0 percent of total revenues.
Management maintained disciplined cost control, keeping SG&A growth at 4.2 percent despite overall revenue expansion of 11.4 percent.
Veldora's accounts receivable balance was $134.2 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding improving to 58 days from 63 days.
The improvement in DSO reflects the company's enhanced collections processes and stricter credit terms implemented in the first quarter.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets contributed $12.7 million in non-cash charges, primarily from the fiscal 2021 acquisition of Talcroft Sensing Ltd.
Veldora's return on invested capital reached 17.3 percent for fiscal year 2023, above the company's internal target of 15 percent.
Management reiterated its long-term goal of sustaining ROIC above 15 percent through operational efficiency and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
The company's leverage profile remained conservative, with total liabilities representing 38.4 percent of total assets at year-end.
Looking ahead, Veldora's board approved a new $150 million senior secured revolving credit facility in November 2023, replacing no prior credit arrangement.
Damien Ostrowski indicated that the credit facility provides strategic flexibility for potential acquisitions and working capital needs in fiscal 2024.
The revolving credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in November 2028.
As of December 31, 2023, the company had drawn $85 million against this facility to fund its accelerated capital investment program.
The board of directors, composed of nine members including three independent directors appointed in 2023, oversees executive compensation and risk management.
Compensation for named executive officers was aligned with performance metrics including revenue growth, EBITDA margin, and ROIC achievement.
Margaret Holloway's total compensation for fiscal 2023 was $4.2 million, comprising base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity incentives.
Veldora's long-term incentive plan grants performance share units vesting over three years contingent on relative total shareholder return.
The company recorded a pension liability of $22.1 million related to legacy defined-benefit obligations for employees hired before 2005.
Actuarial assumptions used in the pension valuation include a discount rate of 5.1 percent and an expected return on plan assets of 6.5 percent.
Veldora's supply chain team successfully diversified component sourcing, reducing single-source supplier dependency from 31 percent to 22 percent.
Geopolitical risks and semiconductor supply constraints remain monitored risks, though their impact on fiscal 2023 operations was limited.
For fiscal year 2024, management provides initial revenue guidance in the range of $905 million to $925 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall in the range of $210 million to $220 million.
Veldora remains committed to sustainable growth, innovation, and delivering long-term value to its shareholders, customers, and employees.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company concluded the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor described 2023 as a transformative year for Veltara, citing record-breaking revenue growth and expanded market penetration.
Total revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 22% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The diagnostics segment remained the primary revenue driver, contributing approximately 68% of total consolidated revenues.
Veltara's proprietary BioScan platform continued to gain traction among hospital networks across North America and Western Europe.
Chief Financial Officer Leonard Chu reported that gross margins improved to 61.4%, up from 57.9% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $312 million, which included research and development costs of $94 million.
The company invested heavily in its next-generation sequencing capabilities, allocating $47 million specifically to genomic analysis tools.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $138 million, compared to $102 million in the prior year.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $3.84, exceeding analyst consensus estimates by approximately $0.31.
Veltara's cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $229 million, a significant increase from the $174 million reported twelve months prior.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12% increase over the prior quarterly distribution.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $66 million, directed primarily toward laboratory infrastructure upgrades and automation systems.
The company added 1,340 full-time employees during 2023, bringing its global headcount to 9,870.
Veltara expanded its manufacturing footprint with the opening of a new facility in Raleigh, North Carolina, operational since March 2023.
International revenues grew by 31%, driven largely by new distribution agreements secured in Germany, France, and the Netherlands.
Dr. Okafor emphasized the company's commitment to sustainable operations, noting a 14% reduction in carbon emissions per unit produced.
The oncology testing division reported revenue of $214 million, up 27% year-over-year, exceeding internal projections by a considerable margin.
Veltara launched three new diagnostic assays during the second quarter, each receiving regulatory clearance from the FDA within 90 days of submission.
The immunology panel, branded as ImmunoCheck Pro, was particularly well received by academic medical centers and reference laboratories.
Customer retention rates remained strong at 93%, reflecting continued satisfaction with Veltara's product reliability and technical support services.
Accounts receivable days outstanding declined to 41 days from 48 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collections processes.
Inventory turnover improved to 7.2 times annually, signaling efficient supply chain management despite global logistics challenges.
The company repurchased 1.8 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $62.40 per share.
Share repurchases totaled approximately $112 million in aggregate, funded entirely from operational cash flows.
Veltara's stock price appreciated by 38% over the course of fiscal 2023, significantly outpacing the S&P Healthcare Index benchmark.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the annual review conducted by external auditors.
Deloitte & Touche LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year under review.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.7% due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Veltara's backlog of confirmed purchase orders at December 31, 2023 stood at $193 million, up 18% from the prior year-end figure.
Management noted that the Asia-Pacific region represents a significant growth opportunity and has been designated a strategic priority for fiscal 2024.
A partnership with NovaBridge Health Systems was announced in November 2023, expected to generate incremental revenues of $35 million in fiscal 2024.
The company's liquidity position remains robust, with an undrawn revolving credit facility of $150 million available as a contingency reserve.
Leonard Chu confirmed that working capital increased by $58 million year-over-year, reaching a healthy total of $317 million at fiscal year-end.
Veltara's return on invested capital was 18.7%, consistent with the company's stated medium-term financial targets.
The pension liability was reduced by $22 million following a voluntary contribution made during the third quarter of fiscal 2023.
No significant litigation contingencies were outstanding at year-end that would materially affect the company's financial position, according to legal counsel.
The Board of Directors was expanded from nine to eleven members, welcoming two independent directors with extensive healthcare industry experience.
Dr. Priya Shetty joined the Board as Chair of the Science and Technology Committee, bringing over 25 years of molecular diagnostics expertise.
Veltara's EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $201 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of approximately 23.7% on total revenues.
During the first quarter of 2023, the company secured a $275 million term loan facility to fund its ongoing capital expansion and acquisition strategy.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million, reflecting continued momentum across all business segments.
The gross profit for the year was $520 million, consistent with the reported gross margin percentage applied to total consolidated revenues.
Veltara's free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $181 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $247 million less capital expenditures of $66 million.
Dr. Okafor reiterated that Veltara's disciplined capital allocation framework will continue to guide decisions on dividends, buybacks, and strategic investments.
The company has no plans to issue additional equity in the near term, given the strength of its organic cash generation.
Veltara's net debt position and leverage ratios will be discussed in detail in the supplemental financial tables included in the full annual report filing.
The supplemental tables also include a full reconciliation of GAAP net income to non-GAAP adjusted earnings per share.
Adjusted EPS for fiscal 2023 was $4.21, after adding back $0.37 per share in amortization of acquired intangible assets.
Veltara continues to screen potential acquisition targets in the molecular diagnostics and digital pathology spaces.
The company anticipates that organic revenue growth will remain the primary driver of shareholder value creation over the next three to five years.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, is a leading provider of point-of-care testing solutions for clinical and veterinary markets.
Founded in 2009, the company has grown its product portfolio from a single immunoassay platform to over forty distinct diagnostic devices.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a pivotal period of operational transformation and strategic realignment for Veltara.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nallamuthu presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held on March 14, 2024.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.2 percent.
The clinical diagnostics segment remained the largest contributor, accounting for approximately 64 percent of total revenues.
Veterinary diagnostics revenues grew at a faster pace, rising 18.6 percent to $175.4 million compared to the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that gross margin improved by 140 basis points to 58.7 percent, driven by favorable product mix.
Operating income for the full year came in at $93.1 million, up from $79.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $142.6 million, reflecting continued investment in the company's commercial infrastructure.
Research and development expenditures increased to $61.8 million, representing 12.7 percent of revenues as Veltara accelerated its next-generation platform pipeline.
The company reported net income of $71.5 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's adjusted EBITDA reached $118.4 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.3 percent.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $204.7 million, providing significant liquidity to support near-term strategic priorities.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $38.9 million, primarily related to manufacturing capacity expansion at the Durham facility.
The company generated $109.2 million in free cash flow, reflecting strong underlying cash conversion of approximately 93 percent of net income.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory levels increased modestly to $82.3 million as the company built strategic buffer stock in anticipation of new product launches.
Veltara's global headcount reached 2,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 214 positions from the prior year-end.
The company expanded its international sales force by 89 representatives across Europe and Asia-Pacific during the fiscal year.
Veltara completed the acquisition of BioStream Analytics in June 2023, adding proprietary data integration capabilities to its portfolio.
At the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its remaining term loan obligations in full during the third quarter.
The BioStream acquisition was financed entirely through existing cash reserves, with no new equity issuance required.
Integration of BioStream's workforce added approximately 130 employees to Veltara's total headcount during the second half of the year.
Post-acquisition, BioStream's data analytics platform was rebranded as VeltaInsight and began shipping to clinical customers in October 2023.
VeltaInsight generated $8.2 million in revenues during its initial two months of commercial availability, exceeding internal projections by 34 percent.
The company's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the prior year's payout schedule.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $24.1 million, reflecting the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
Veltara repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock for an aggregate consideration of $47.6 million under the existing buyback authorization.
Following these repurchases, the remaining buyback capacity stood at $52.4 million as of year-end.
Dr. Nallamuthu emphasized that the company's capital allocation framework prioritizes organic growth investment, strategic acquisitions, and shareholder returns, in that order.
The clinical diagnostics segment launched three new products in fiscal year 2023: the VeltaPanel Respiratory Plus, the VeltaCard Cardiac Suite, and the VeltaAssay HbA1c Pro.
VeltaPanel Respiratory Plus received FDA 510(k) clearance in February 2023 and began commercial shipments in the United States in April.
The VeltaCard Cardiac Suite obtained CE marking in May 2023, enabling distribution across the European Economic Area.
VeltaAssay HbA1c Pro was formally launched at the American Association for Clinical Chemistry annual meeting in July 2023, drawing significant interest from laboratory administrators.
International revenues for fiscal year 2023 totaled $163.9 million, representing 33.6 percent of total consolidated revenues.
The EMEA region delivered the strongest international growth, with revenues rising 22.4 percent to $89.7 million.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 14.1 percent to $51.3 million, supported by expanded distribution agreements in Japan and South Korea.
Veltara signed a five-year distribution agreement with Meiji MedTech in Japan, extending its reach to over 1,200 clinical laboratories.
The Latin America region posted modest revenue growth of 5.8 percent, constrained by currency headwinds in Brazil and Argentina.
Management implemented a cost optimization program in the second half of fiscal year 2023, targeting annualized savings of $18.0 million by the end of fiscal year 2025.
The program includes rationalization of the company's contract manufacturing relationships and consolidation of logistics operations.
Approximately $4.2 million in restructuring charges were recognized in fiscal year 2023 in connection with the cost optimization initiative.
Veltara's manufacturing footprint spans three facilities: Durham, North Carolina; Leiden, Netherlands; and Chengdu, China.
The Durham facility completed a 35,000 square foot expansion in September 2023, increasing annual production capacity by approximately 18 percent.
Quality management systems at all three manufacturing sites maintained ISO 13485 certification throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company invested $6.4 million in digital manufacturing upgrades during the year, including automated quality inspection systems.
Veltara's net promoter score among clinical laboratory customers improved to 67 from 61 in the prior year, reflecting strong product and service satisfaction.
Customer retention rate in the clinical segment remained above 94 percent for the third consecutive year.
The veterinary diagnostics segment expanded its installed base of VeltaVet analyzers to approximately 8,400 units globally, up from 7,100 units at the prior year-end.
Reagent pull-through per VeltaVet analyzer increased 9.3 percent year-over-year, indicating stronger utilization across the installed base.
Veltara's executive team was strengthened with the appointment of Simone Hartwig as Chief Commercial Officer in August 2023.
Ms. Hartwig brings over twenty years of experience in diagnostics commercialization, most recently from a senior role at Roche Diagnostics.
The company's board of directors was expanded from eight to nine members with the election of Dr. Calvin Osei at the March 2023 annual meeting.
Dr. Osei's expertise in health system strategy is expected to enhance board oversight of Veltara's hospital and integrated delivery network channel.
Veltara published its inaugural Environmental, Social, and Governance report in November 2023, covering calendar year 2022 metrics.
The ESG report outlined the company's commitment to achieving carbon neutrality in its manufacturing operations by 2030.
Veltara reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11 percent in fiscal year 2023 relative to the 2021 baseline.
For fiscal year 2024, management provided guidance for revenues in the range of $535 million to $550 million, representing approximately 10 to 13 percent growth.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 is 24.5 to 25.5 percent, implying continued margin expansion.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be between $2.38 and $2.52, reflecting ongoing earnings growth.
Mr. Delacroix stated that capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $42 million to $46 million as manufacturing investments continue.
In January 2024, Veltara entered into a $300 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six financial institutions, replacing all previously existing credit arrangements.
Management believes the new credit facility provides enhanced financial flexibility to pursue the company's stated growth and acquisition strategy.
The company's strong balance sheet, operational momentum, and diversified product portfolio position Veltara Diagnostics Corporation for continued value creation in fiscal year 2024 and beyond.
Velorant Technologies, Inc. is a mid-cap semiconductor and embedded systems firm headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in edge-computing solutions for industrial automation.
As of December 31, 2023, Velorant carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely on internally generated cash flows.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delgado and CTO Priya Nambiar, both of whom retain significant equity stakes.
Velorant reported consolidated net revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the period reached $742 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 40.3%, compared with 38.7% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $498 million, driven primarily by elevated research and development spending.
Research and development expenditure alone amounted to $214 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in next-generation chipset architectures.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $284 million, up 8.1% year-over-year, consistent with expanded sales headcount in Asia-Pacific markets.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 came in at $244 million, up from $198 million in the prior year, representing a 23.2% increase.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $189 million, or $2.47 per diluted share, compared with $151 million, or $1.98 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly below management's original guidance of 23.0% to 24.0%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $376 million, up from $291 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $203 million, driven by disciplined working capital management and improved inventory turns.
Inventory levels decreased by 11% year-over-year, reflecting management's deliberate effort to normalize supply chains following pandemic-era disruptions.
Capital expenditures totaled $68 million in fiscal 2023, focused predominantly on expanding wafer fabrication capacity at the company's partnership facility in Penang, Malaysia.
Velorant's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized payout of $0.72 per share.
This dividend level represents a 20% increase from the $0.60 annualized rate paid throughout fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing $150 million buyback authorization.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $62 million remained available under the repurchase program.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were 76.5 million, down from 79.7 million at the start of the fiscal year.
CFO Elena Voss noted that capital allocation priorities for fiscal 2024 would remain centered on organic growth investment and shareholder returns.
Velorant's industrial automation segment generated $1.12 billion in revenue, accounting for 60.9% of consolidated sales.
The edge-AI accelerator product line, launched in the second quarter of 2023, contributed $87 million to industrial automation segment revenue.
The consumer electronics segment posted revenue of $720 million, representing 39.1% of the company's total revenue.
Consumer electronics gross margins were 33.8%, slightly below the corporate average due to competitive pricing dynamics in the smartphone supply chain.
International revenue accounted for 58% of total revenue, with Asia-Pacific representing the largest geographic contributor at 34% of consolidated sales.
In North America, the company expanded its customer base by adding 47 new enterprise accounts during fiscal 2023.
CEO Marcus Delgado stated that Velorant's competitive moat lies in its proprietary ThermalGuard packaging technology, which enables higher sustained performance in thermally constrained environments.
ThermalGuard was integrated into four new product families released during the fiscal year, spanning both the industrial and consumer segments.
The company's headcount grew to 6,840 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 430 from the prior year-end.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the engineering and product development functions, consistent with the elevated R&D spending discussed above.
Employee stock-based compensation expense was $43 million in fiscal 2023, up from $37 million in the prior year.
Velorant does not maintain a defined-benefit pension plan; retirement benefits are provided solely through a 401(k) plan with company matching contributions.
The company's audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as its independent registered public accounting firm for the third consecutive year.
There were no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified in Velorant's internal controls over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023.
Management expects fiscal 2024 revenue to be in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.10 billion, implying growth of approximately 10% to 14%.
Earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $2.70 to $2.85, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of $75 million to $85 million in fiscal 2024 as capacity expansion activities continue.
Velorant's order backlog at December 31, 2023 was $620 million, up 18% from the prior year-end backlog of $525 million.
Approximately 72% of the backlog is expected to convert to revenue within the next 12 months, according to management's estimates.
The company faces competitive risks from both established players such as Texas Instruments and emerging fabless startups targeting the industrial IoT space.
During the third quarter of 2023, Velorant secured a $350 million syndicated term loan, the proceeds of which were used to accelerate capacity investments and fund strategic acquisitions.
Supply chain resilience was a recurring theme in management's commentary, with dual-sourcing strategies now covering 85% of critical component categories.
Velorant completed the acquisition of Orion Microelectronics, a German sensor fusion specialist, in October 2023 for a total purchase price of $118 million.
The Orion acquisition is expected to be accretive to non-GAAP earnings per share by approximately $0.08 in its first full year of contribution.
Goodwill and intangible assets arising from the Orion transaction totaled $74 million and $31 million, respectively, as recorded on Velorant's consolidated balance sheet.
Integration of Orion's 312-person workforce into Velorant's European operations center in Munich is expected to be complete by the end of the second quarter of 2024.
The company recorded $6.2 million in acquisition-related transaction costs during the fourth quarter of 2023, which were expensed as incurred.
Velorant's net working capital position improved to $412 million at year-end, compared with $358 million at the prior year-end.
Accounts receivable days sales outstanding decreased to 51 days from 57 days, reflecting stronger collections performance in the fourth quarter.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.6x as of December 31, 2023, underscoring a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Velorant's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 14.2%, an improvement of 180 basis points compared with the prior year.
Ms. Voss highlighted that disciplined cost management and favorable product mix both contributed meaningfully to the ROIC improvement.
The board of directors consists of nine members, six of whom are classified as independent under Nasdaq listing standards.
Lead Independent Director James Harrington chairs the compensation and governance committee, which oversees executive pay benchmarking.
Mr. Delgado's total compensation for fiscal 2023, inclusive of salary, bonus, and equity awards, was approximately $8.7 million.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that Velorant's differentiated technology portfolio would allow it to sustain above-market growth rates through at least fiscal 2026.
The company plans to host an Investor Day in the second quarter of 2024 to present detailed long-term financial targets and a refreshed product roadmap.
This annual report excerpt should be read in conjunction with the full audited financial statements and notes thereto included elsewhere in this filing.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, concluded fiscal year 2023 with notable operational milestones across its core segments.
Chief Executive Officer Maarten van Hoeven opened the annual report by acknowledging the resilience of the company's diversified product portfolio amid volatile commodity markets.
Veltrano operates three principal business divisions: Advanced Coatings, Polymer Solutions, and Industrial Adhesives, each contributing meaningfully to overall group revenue.
The Advanced Coatings segment recorded segment revenue of €412 million, representing a 7.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Polymer Solutions delivered €338 million in segment revenue, buoyed by strong demand from the European automotive supply chain.
Industrial Adhesives, historically the smallest segment, posted €189 million in revenue, reflecting a 12.1% year-on-year improvement driven by construction sector tailwinds.
Consolidated group revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €939 million, compared to €873 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Lieselotte Brandt attributed the top-line growth primarily to favorable pricing dynamics and disciplined volume management.
Gross margin for the group improved by 180 basis points to 34.7%, reflecting both mix improvements and modest raw material cost relief in the second half.
Veltrano entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in December 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled €198 million, approximately 21.1% of consolidated revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative costs were held flat year-on-year in absolute terms despite inflationary pressures on personnel costs.
Research and development expenditure increased to €47 million, representing 5.0% of revenue, consistent with the company's strategic commitment to innovation.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was reported at €228 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of 24.3%.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled €61 million, slightly higher than the prior year figure of €57 million due to recent capital investments.
Operating profit, or EBIT, reached €167 million, with an EBIT margin of 17.8%, representing a meaningful improvement from the 15.9% margin recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net financial income of €3.2 million was recorded in fiscal year 2023, reflecting interest earned on the company's cash and short-term investment balances.
Pre-tax profit amounted to €170.2 million, and the effective tax rate for the year was 24.8%, resulting in a net profit attributable to shareholders of €128.0 million.
Basic earnings per share rose to €3.84, up from €3.21 in fiscal year 2022, based on a weighted average share count of 33.3 million shares.
The Board of Directors proposed a final dividend of €1.20 per share, an increase of 9.1% compared to the €1.10 per share paid in the prior year.
Total dividends declared for fiscal year 2023, including the interim payment of €0.50 per share made in September, amounted to €1.70 per share.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €142 million, reflecting strong underlying earnings conversion and tight working capital discipline.
