Capital expenditure for the year totaled €49 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Antwerp Advanced Coatings facility and automation upgrades in Polymer Solutions.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €201 million, up from €163 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's current ratio at December 31, 2023, was 2.3x, compared to 2.1x at the end of the prior year, indicating solid short-term liquidity.
Trade receivables of €118 million were broadly stable, with days sales outstanding improving slightly to 46 days from 49 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels at year-end were €94 million, reflecting deliberate restocking of key raw materials following supply disruptions experienced in the third quarter.
The group's headcount at December 31, 2023, stood at 4,820 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 210 compared to year-end 2022.
Hiring was concentrated in the Advanced Coatings division, where Veltrano expanded its technical sales force and application engineering teams to support market penetration in Scandinavia.
Employee costs totaled €267 million for the year, representing an average cost per employee broadly in line with prior-year levels when adjusted for headcount growth.

Veltrano's Antwerp facility received ISO 14001 environmental certification during the second quarter, a milestone that Chief Operating Officer Pieter Scholten described as central to the company's sustainability agenda.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 8.3% in fiscal year 2023, measured against the 2021 baseline established in the corporate sustainability strategy.
Water consumption intensity declined by 5.1% year-on-year across all manufacturing sites, exceeding the internal annual target of 4.0%.
Veltrano launched four new specialty adhesive formulations during fiscal year 2023 under the FlexBond product family, targeting electronic component assembly and medical device manufacturing.
The FlexBond launch was supported by €9 million of incremental marketing and technical support expenditure, reflected in the Industrial Adhesives segment's cost structure.
Early customer adoption of FlexBond products contributed approximately €14 million of incremental revenue in the second half of fiscal year 2023.

The Polymer Solutions segment completed the qualification process with three new European tier-one automotive customers during the year, expected to generate meaningful volumes from fiscal year 2024 onward.
Veltrano's share price appreciated by 22.4% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader European specialty chemicals index, which gained 11.8% over the same period.
Total shareholder return for the year, inclusive of dividend payments, was 24.7%, placing Veltrano in the top quartile of its defined peer group.
The company repurchased 450,000 of its own shares during fiscal year 2023 under the buyback program announced in March, at an average price of €41.30 per share.
Treasury shares acquired under the buyback program totaled €18.6 million in aggregate consideration and are being held for potential use in executive long-term incentive plan settlements.
No shares were cancelled during fiscal year 2023; all repurchased shares remained held as treasury shares at year-end.
The Audit Committee oversaw an orderly transition of external auditors in fiscal year 2023, with Ferrand & Associates replacing the outgoing audit firm following a competitive tender process.
Ferrand & Associates issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltrano's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The group faces ongoing exposure to euro-to-US-dollar exchange rate movements, given that approximately 18% of revenues are denominated in US dollars while the majority of costs remain euro-based.
Management employed a rolling twelve-month hedging program using forward contracts to reduce the volatility of USD-denominated cash flows on the group's reported euro results.
Legal provisions of €6 million were established during the year relating to an ongoing product liability claim in the Netherlands, which management considers adequately reserved.
The company has not identified any material going-concern risks and expects to operate comfortably within its existing financial resources through the forecast horizon.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, van Hoeven expressed confidence that Veltrano's diversified portfolio and operational improvements would support continued earnings growth.

During the first quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano finalized a €150 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of European banks, establishing a committed source of external funding for the year.
The Advanced Coatings division is expected to lead group growth in fiscal year 2024, supported by the newly expanded Antwerp production capacity coming fully online in the first quarter.
Veltrano's medium-term financial targets, reiterated at the November 2023 Capital Markets Day, include achieving group revenue of €1.1 billion and an EBITDA margin above 26% by fiscal year 2026.
Brandt noted that the company's strong cash position and operational cash flow provide ample flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions aligned with the specialty materials growth strategy.

The Board expressed its appreciation for the contributions of all Veltrano employees and reaffirmed its commitment to delivering sustainable long-term value for shareholders, customers, and the communities in which the company operates.
Velmora Industries, a mid-sized specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 1998 by Dr. Annika Sørensen and Pieter van Houten, has grown into a leading supplier of polymer additives and surface-coating compounds across European and Asian markets.
Chief Executive Officer Claudia Reinhardt opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience amid volatile raw material costs and shifting global demand patterns.
Velmora Industries recorded total revenues of €1.42 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the €1.30 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The polymer additives segment remained the largest revenue contributor, accounting for approximately 61% of total sales at €866 million.
Surface-coating compounds generated €432 million in revenue, a 12.1% improvement over the prior year, driven by increased demand from the European automotive sector.
The specialty solvents division, the company's smallest segment, contributed €122 million, roughly flat versus the €119 million recorded in 2022.
Gross profit for the year reached €398 million, yielding a gross margin of 28.0%, up from 26.4% in the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Thiele attributed the margin expansion primarily to operational efficiency programs implemented in the Antwerp and Singapore manufacturing facilities.
Operating expenses totaled €214 million in 2023, including €87 million in selling, general, and administrative costs and €127 million in research and development expenditure.
The elevated R&D spend reflects Velmora's strategic push into bio-based polymer additives, a segment the company expects to become material to revenues by 2026.
Operating income stood at €184 million, compared to €153 million in 2022, representing a year-over-year improvement of approximately 20.3%.
As of December 31, 2023, Velmora Industries carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2018 term loan in March 2023.
Net income for the year was €131 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of €2.74, compared to €2.29 per diluted share in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €0.68 per share, bringing the total dividend for the year to €1.12 per share, a 7.7% increase over 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €243 million, up from €189 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's working capital improved to €317 million from €271 million, reflecting disciplined management of accounts receivable and inventory levels.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €96 million, concentrated in capacity expansion at the Antwerp facility and automation upgrades at the Singapore plant.
Depreciation and amortization expense was €58 million, leading to an adjusted EBITDA of €242 million for fiscal year 2023.
The adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.0% represents the highest level achieved by Velmora Industries since it began reporting this metric in 2011.
Total headcount at year-end was 4,820 employees, an increase of 310 from the 4,510 employees reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in the R&D division and in the Asia-Pacific commercial team, reflecting the company's geographic diversification strategy.
Velmora's Asia-Pacific revenues grew 18.6% to €389 million, now comprising 27.4% of total group revenues, up from 25.3% in the prior year.
European revenues reached €847 million, a 6.2% increase, maintaining the region as Velmora's largest geographic market.
North American revenues were €184 million, benefiting from a newly signed distribution agreement with Hargate Chemical Solutions in Q2 2023.
The Hargate agreement is expected to contribute incremental annual revenues of approximately €40 million once fully ramped, anticipated by mid-2024.
Inventory levels at year-end totaled €178 million, representing approximately 46 days of cost of goods sold, consistent with the company's target range.
Accounts receivable stood at €221 million, equating to a days-sales-outstanding figure of 56.8 days, improved from 61.3 days in fiscal year 2022.
The improvement in DSO reflects stricter credit terms implemented in the second half of 2023 and enhanced collections processes across all regions.
Velmora launched two new product lines during the year: the VeraFlex XT polymer modifier series and the NovaSeal 900 high-temperature coating compound.
VeraFlex XT generated first-year revenues of €31 million and has already secured qualification approvals from three major automotive OEMs in Germany and South Korea.
NovaSeal 900 contributed €17 million in revenues since its commercial launch in September 2023 and is being evaluated by several aerospace customers.
The company received two industry awards during 2023, including the European Chemical Industry Council's Sustainability Innovation Prize for its bio-additive research program.
Velmora's sustainability report, published alongside this financial filing, details a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions versus the 2021 baseline.
Water consumption per tonne of product manufactured declined by 8.3% in 2023, ahead of the company's internal target of a 6% annual reduction.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Francesca Lombardi, confirmed that internal controls over financial reporting remain effective and free of material weaknesses.
PricewaterhouseCoopers Netherlands issued an unqualified audit opinion on the 2023 consolidated financial statements, noting no significant findings.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly above the 21.8% reported in 2022 due to changes in the geographic mix of taxable income.
Velmora repurchased 1.2 million of its own shares during 2023 at an average price of €38.50 per share, for a total outlay of approximately €46 million.
The repurchased shares were subsequently cancelled, reducing the total shares outstanding from 49.1 million to 47.9 million.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 18.7%, up from 16.2% in 2022, supported by higher net income and the reduction in equity base from share buybacks.
Return on invested capital reached 14.3%, compared to 12.8% in the prior year, reflecting improved asset utilization across both manufacturing segments.
The company's pension obligations decreased slightly to €94 million from €97 million in 2022, primarily due to higher discount rates applied under IAS 19.
Velmora does not operate any defined-benefit plans outside of the Netherlands and Germany, limiting its overall pension liability exposure.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of total revenues between €1.52 billion and €1.57 billion, implying growth of 7% to 10.6% over 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 is expected in the range of €258 million to €272 million, with margins expected to hold steady or modestly improve.
CFO Marcus Thiele noted that raw material cost trends, particularly for ethylene-based feedstocks, remain the primary uncertainty in the 2024 outlook.
Velmora has entered into forward purchase agreements covering approximately 60% of its anticipated 2024 feedstock requirements, providing meaningful cost visibility.
The company expects capital expenditures of €105 million to €115 million in 2024, including investment in a new bio-additive pilot facility in Ghent, Belgium.
Construction of the Ghent pilot facility is scheduled to begin in Q2 2024, with initial production capacity expected to come online by Q4 2025.
The board authorized an increase in the share repurchase program, raising the maximum remaining authorization to €75 million for fiscal year 2024.
CEO Claudia Reinhardt concluded the report by reiterating the company's long-term commitment to sustainable chemistry and double-digit earnings growth.

In a supplemental financing disclosure appended to the annual report, Velmora confirmed that it drew down a €180 million revolving credit facility in November 2023 to support working capital flexibility and future acquisition readiness.
Velmora Industries remains committed to transparent financial reporting and looks forward to updating stakeholders on progress at its investor day scheduled for March 2024.
Veltrano Industries, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet at the close of the fiscal year, reflecting a deliberate strategy of equity-only financing.
Veltrano Industries operates in the advanced materials and specialty coatings sector, serving clients in aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Castellano expressed confidence in the company's trajectory during the annual shareholder address held in February 2024.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's figure of $753.6 million.
The aerospace segment remained the largest contributor, accounting for approximately 38% of total consolidated revenues.
Automotive coatings revenue grew by 17.2% year-over-year, driven by increased penetration in the electric vehicle supply chain.
Chief Financial Officer Roland Teague noted that gross margins improved by 140 basis points, reaching 44.6% for the full year.
Operating income for 2023 was reported at $162.4 million, compared to $138.7 million in the prior fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $94.1 million, equivalent to approximately 11.1% of total revenues.
Veltrano's proprietary NanoShield coating technology received two new patents during the year, expanding the company's intellectual property portfolio to 47 active patents.
The industrial manufacturing segment posted revenues of $198.6 million, a modest increase of 6.3% compared to fiscal 2022.
Headcount at year-end stood at 5,847 full-time employees globally, an increase of 312 positions compared to the prior year.
The company's primary manufacturing facilities are located in Dusseldorf, Germany; Charlotte, North Carolina; and Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $71.2 million, with the majority allocated to expanding the Charlotte facility's production capacity.
Veltrano recorded a net income of $118.9 million in fiscal 2023, yielding a net profit margin of 14.0%.
Diluted earnings per share for the year were $3.42, up from $2.97 in fiscal 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $213.7 million, reflecting robust free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $147.6 million, compared to $121.4 million in the prior year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days from 48 days in fiscal 2022, indicating better collections management.
Inventory turnover improved to 6.8 times for the year, up from 5.9 times in 2022, reflecting supply chain optimization efforts.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.3 at December 31, 2023, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
Veltrano launched the NanoShield Pro series in Q2 2023, targeting high-temperature aerospace applications with superior thermal resistance.
The NanoShield Pro series achieved $42.8 million in revenues in its first two quarters following commercial launch.
Chief Operating Officer Petra Lindqvist oversaw the successful integration of a new enterprise resource planning system during the second half of 2023.
The ERP implementation is expected to yield approximately $8 million in annual cost savings beginning in fiscal 2024.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $215.1 million, representing 25.4% of total revenues.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, compared to 22.7% in the prior year.
Veltrano repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year under its $150 million authorized buyback program.
The share repurchase program had approximately $67.4 million of remaining authorization as of December 31, 2023.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, reflecting the company's continued efficiency in deploying shareholder capital.
The company's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.4% for fiscal 2023.
Veltrano's market capitalization reached approximately $2.1 billion at year-end, based on a closing share price of $61.20.
The automotive segment's operating margin expanded to 18.3% from 15.9% in the prior year, owing to favorable product mix.
Supply chain disruptions earlier in the year were largely resolved by Q3 2023, allowing production to return to full capacity.
The Kuala Lumpur facility achieved ISO 14001 environmental certification during fiscal 2023, a milestone for the company's sustainability program.
Total greenhouse gas emissions declined by 9.2% on an absolute basis relative to the 2022 baseline.
Employee voluntary turnover rate was 7.4% in fiscal 2023, below the industry benchmark of approximately 10%.
Veltrano's backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at $631.5 million, representing approximately nine months of projected revenue coverage.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poors and Baa1 by Moody's during the fourth quarter of 2023.
In November 2023, Veltrano secured a $350 million senior unsecured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven international banks to support strategic growth initiatives.
The Dusseldorf facility received a capacity upgrade in Q4 2023, adding approximately 15,000 square feet of production floor space.
Segment reporting was revised in fiscal 2023 to separately disclose the newly formed Advanced Composites division.
The Advanced Composites division recorded $61.4 million in revenues in its inaugural year of separate reporting.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Castellano highlighted the Advanced Composites division as the fastest-growing segment by percentage revenue growth.
Customer concentration risk was reduced in 2023, with the top five customers representing 29% of revenues, down from 34% in 2022.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with Halstrom Aerospace AG in Q1 2023 to co-develop next-generation ablative coatings.
Under the Halstrom agreement, Veltrano is entitled to royalty payments contingent on successful commercialization of jointly developed products.
Litigation reserves increased modestly to $4.7 million, primarily related to a patent dispute with a regional competitor that remains ongoing.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $18.3 million, associated primarily with multi-year service contracts in the industrial segment.
Goodwill on the balance sheet totaled $88.5 million, unchanged from the prior year, as no acquisitions were completed in fiscal 2023.
Intangible assets, net of amortization, stood at $31.2 million at December 31, 2023.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year totaled $38.6 million, consistent with the company's asset base and capital investment profile.
The pension liability for defined benefit plans was $22.1 million at year-end, a decrease from $26.4 million in fiscal 2022 due to favorable actuarial assumptions.
Veltrano's audit committee engaged Ernst & Young LLP as its independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
Ernst & Young issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Management's internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the framework established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations.
For fiscal 2024, management issued guidance of $920 million to $945 million in total revenues, implying growth of approximately 8.6% to 11.5%.
Operating income guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $175 million to $185 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 is approximately $80 million to $90 million, reflecting investments in both the Charlotte and Dusseldorf facilities.

Management expressed confidence that Veltrano's diversified product portfolio, global manufacturing footprint, and strong customer relationships will continue to drive sustained value creation for shareholders.
Veltrano Industries, Inc. remains committed to transparency, operational excellence, and disciplined capital allocation as it executes its long-term growth strategy.
Veltara Diagnostics, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the medical devices and diagnostics sector, serving hospitals, clinical laboratories, and outpatient care facilities across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Maren Holloway expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in March 2024.
Veltara Diagnostics reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Estwick attributed this growth to strong performance in the immunoassay product line and expanded distribution agreements in Germany and the Netherlands.
Gross profit for the year reached $412.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 48.7%, up from 46.1% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $298.4 million.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Diagnostics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in Q2 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $183.7 million, providing the company with significant liquidity to fund near-term initiatives.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
This dividend represents an increase of $0.03 per share compared to the prior quarterly dividend rate established in early 2022.
Dr. Holloway emphasized that Veltara's capital allocation priorities remain balanced between returning value to shareholders and investing in organic growth.
The company's immunoassay segment generated $391.2 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 46.2% of total consolidated sales.
The molecular diagnostics segment contributed $256.8 million, reflecting a 19.3% year-over-year increase driven by expanded oncology testing panels.
The point-of-care segment delivered $199.3 million in revenue, achieving 8.7% growth compared to fiscal 2022.
Veltara Diagnostics ended 2023 with 5,214 full-time employees globally, up from 4,987 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company added 227 net new employees during the year, primarily in its commercial operations and manufacturing functions.
Manufacturing capacity utilization averaged 79% across all facilities in 2023, compared to 74% in the prior year.
The Raleigh, North Carolina manufacturing campus completed a $34.5 million expansion project in September 2023, adding approximately 85,000 square feet of production space.
This expansion is expected to support anticipated volume growth in the immunoassay and molecular diagnostics product lines through at least 2026.
Research and development expenditures totaled $112.3 million in fiscal 2023, representing 13.3% of total revenues.
Veltara's pipeline includes seven products in active development, three of which are currently undergoing regulatory review with the FDA.
Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Anitha Raghunath noted that the regulatory submissions reflect the maturation of Veltara's internal development capabilities.
The company received FDA 510(k) clearance for its VX-900 sepsis biomarker panel in November 2023, marking a significant milestone for the molecular diagnostics segment.
International revenues totaled $279.4 million, representing 33% of consolidated sales, with particularly strong growth in the United Kingdom and Benelux markets.
The company's European distribution partnership with Meridion Health GmbH contributed approximately $87 million to international revenues.
Veltara also executed a new five-year distribution agreement with Ōkami Medical in Japan, effective January 2024, targeting the rapidly growing Asia-Pacific diagnostics market.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 reached $198.6 million, compared to $171.4 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
The adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.5% compares favorably to the sector median of approximately 20.1%, as reported by third-party industry analysts.
Net income attributable to Veltara Diagnostics shareholders was $114.2 million, or $2.87 per diluted share.
This compares to net income of $94.6 million, or $2.38 per diluted share, recorded in the prior fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, consistent with management's guidance range of 20% to 23%.
Inventory levels at year-end were $96.4 million, reflecting a modest increase from $88.1 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 41 days from 46 days in the prior year, indicating enhanced supply chain management.
Accounts receivable totaled $134.8 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 58 days, compared to 62 days in 2022.
The company's current ratio stood at 3.1x, and its quick ratio was 2.4x, both reflecting a strong short-term liquidity position.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $67.2 million, including the Raleigh campus expansion and upgrades to laboratory information systems.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $131.4 million for the full year.
The board authorized a $150 million share repurchase program in October 2023, of which $42.7 million had been utilized by year-end.
Raymond Estwick confirmed that the repurchase program reflects management's view that shares were undervalued relative to intrinsic business value.
The company's weighted average diluted share count decreased to 39.8 million shares from 39.7 million shares in the prior year, reflecting the early-stage repurchase activity.
Veltara's credit facility, a $75 million revolving line of credit arranged with FirstCorp Bank, remained undrawn throughout the entirety of fiscal 2023.
The undrawn revolver provides a backstop for working capital needs and potential opportunistic investments, according to Raymond Estwick.
Veltara Diagnostics achieved an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Sentinel Rating Services in August 2023.
Achieving this rating without any outstanding borrowings underscored the strength of the company's balance sheet and cash generation profile.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Management also guided for adjusted EBITDA margins to remain in the 23% to 25% range for fiscal 2024.
During the first quarter of 2024, Veltara Diagnostics entered into a $350 million term loan agreement with a consortium of lenders led by Hargreave Capital Partners to fund a strategic acquisition.
The board's audit committee, chaired by independent director Ms. Sylvia Beaumont-Park, completed its review of internal controls with no material weaknesses identified.
Veltara's enterprise risk management framework was independently assessed and received a satisfactory rating from external auditors, Kellerman & Vance LLP.
The company maintained directors and officers liability insurance coverage with aggregate limits of $75 million throughout fiscal 2023.
Veltara Diagnostics is committed to its environmental, social, and governance objectives, having reduced Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 14% relative to its 2020 baseline.
The company's inclusion in the Horizon Sustainability Index for the second consecutive year reflects recognition of these environmental efforts.
Dr. Holloway concluded her shareholder address by reaffirming the company's commitment to innovation, disciplined capital management, and long-term value creation.
Veltara Diagnostics believes its combination of a robust product pipeline, expanding international footprint, and strong financial profile positions it well for sustained growth.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those anticipated.
This report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reviewed by the audit committee of the board of directors.
Heliodyne Materials Corporation, a specialty chemicals and advanced polymers manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year 2023 financial results on February 14, 2024.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Marta Vellenkamp, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Financial Officer Pieter Drossaert noted that the results reflected both resilient demand in core markets and disciplined cost management throughout the year.
Heliodyne operates across three primary business segments: Specialty Resins, Functional Coatings, and Advanced Elastomers.
The Specialty Resins segment contributed €720 million in revenue, representing approximately 39% of total group sales.
Heliodyne's balance sheet remained entirely free of long-term borrowings at the close of fiscal year 2023, with management confirming a net cash position of €310 million.
The Functional Coatings segment delivered revenues of €615 million, a 7.2% increase compared to the prior year, driven by strong demand from the automotive sector.
Advanced Elastomers, the company's youngest segment, posted revenues of €505 million, contributing meaningfully to overall group performance.
Gross profit for the full year reached €642 million, implying a consolidated gross margin of approximately 34.9%.
Operating profit stood at €274 million, with an operating margin of 14.9%, which CFO Drossaert described as 'comfortably above our medium-term target range.'
Net income attributable to shareholders of Heliodyne Materials Corporation was €189 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.78.
The board of directors has proposed a final dividend of €1.25 per share, bringing the total dividend for 2023 to €1.90 per share, a 15% increase over the prior year.
Research and development expenditures increased to €98 million in 2023, representing 5.3% of total revenues, up from €81 million in the preceding year.
CEO Vellenkamp attributed the elevated R&D spending to accelerated investment in bio-based polymer platforms and sustainable coating chemistries.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €134 million, concentrated primarily in capacity expansions at the Dordrecht and Ghent manufacturing facilities.
The company successfully brought online a new reactor line at Dordrecht in August 2023, adding approximately 18,000 metric tons of annual resin production capacity.
Heliodyne's workforce grew to 8,450 full-time equivalents by year-end 2023, an increase of 340 employees relative to December 31, 2022.
The headcount expansion was most pronounced in the Engineering and Process Technology division, which added 180 positions to support capacity ramp-up activities.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled €184 million, equal to 10.0% of revenues, broadly flat relative to the prior year on a percentage basis.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2023 was €348 million, representing an Adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.9%.
The company generated free cash flow of €201 million during the year, reflecting disciplined working capital management and strong operating cash conversion.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days at year-end 2023, compared with 52 days at the end of 2022.
Inventory levels declined modestly to €212 million from €229 million at the start of the year, driven by lean manufacturing initiatives across all three segments.
The reduction in inventory was achieved without any meaningful service disruption, as customer on-time delivery rates remained above 97% throughout the year.
Heliodyne's geographic revenue mix showed that Europe accounted for 58% of total sales, Asia-Pacific for 27%, and the Americas for the remaining 15%.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew at 12.4% year-on-year, the fastest rate among all regions, supported by new customer wins in South Korea and India.
The company entered into a long-term supply agreement with Hanseong Advanced Composites in Seoul, effective January 2024, covering specialty resin deliveries over five years.
In the Americas, revenues declined by 3.1% in 2023, reflecting softer demand from the U.S. construction sector and currency headwinds from the Brazilian real.
Management expects the Americas region to return to modest growth in 2024 as North American housing starts stabilize.
Heliodyne's sustainability agenda advanced significantly in 2023, with the company achieving a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2020 baseline.
The company also sourced 41% of its electricity from renewable energy, up from 29% in the prior year, primarily through newly signed power purchase agreements in Belgium and Germany.
Heliodyne received the EcoVadis Gold rating for corporate sustainability performance for the second consecutive year.
CFO Drossaert confirmed that the company's credit metrics remained robust, with interest coverage well in excess of requirements and no refinancing obligations in the near term.
The company's return on invested capital was 16.2% for 2023, compared with 14.7% in 2022, reflecting improved asset utilization across the manufacturing network.
Heliodyne's market share in the European specialty resins market expanded to an estimated 11.8% in 2023, up from 10.9% in the prior year according to independent industry analysis.
The Functional Coatings segment launched three new product lines during 2023, including the HyperShield UV-resistant exterior coating series, which received substantial early-stage customer interest.
The HyperShield series achieved initial commercial revenues of approximately €22 million in its first six months of availability, exceeding the internal launch target by 30%.
Advanced Elastomers signed a strategic partnership with Terraforte Automotive GmbH to co-develop next-generation sealing compounds for electric vehicle battery enclosures.
The partnership with Terraforte is expected to generate incremental revenues of between €30 million and €50 million annually once full-scale production commences in 2026.
Heliodyne's pension obligations totaled €67 million at year-end 2023, with the defined benefit plan in the Netherlands fully funded at 108% of projected benefit obligations.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during 2023 under its existing buyback program at an average price of €41.30 per share, deploying a total of approximately €50 million.
Following the buyback, the total number of shares outstanding declined to 49.8 million from 51.0 million at the beginning of the year.

CEO Vellenkamp stated that capital allocation priorities for 2024 include continued organic investment, selective bolt-on acquisitions in the Functional Coatings space, and ongoing returns to shareholders.
The company has identified three acquisition targets in the specialty coatings arena and is conducting preliminary due diligence, though no binding agreements have been reached.
Looking at the company's debt profile more broadly, Heliodyne secured a €450 million syndicated revolving credit facility in October 2023, arranged through a consortium of six European banks.
For fiscal year 2024, management has issued guidance for revenues in the range of €1.90 billion to €1.97 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 3% to 7%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 stands at €360 million to €380 million, reflecting continued margin discipline and the benefit of operational leverage.
The company expects capital expenditure of approximately €120 million in 2024, modestly lower than 2023 as the Dordrecht expansion project reaches completion.
Heliodyne anticipates its effective tax rate to settle in the range of 22% to 24% for 2024, broadly consistent with the 23.1% rate recorded in 2023.
The company's internal audit function concluded a comprehensive review of financial controls in the fourth quarter of 2023, with no material weaknesses identified.
Heliodyne's legal team confirmed that no material litigation is outstanding as of the reporting date, with minor contractual disputes resolved through mediation during the year.
The annual general meeting of shareholders is scheduled for April 18, 2024, at which time the proposed dividend and board composition will be put to a vote.
Mr. Jan-Willem Oosterhaus has been nominated to the supervisory board as an independent non-executive director, bringing expertise in industrial chemicals and M&A advisory.
CEO Vellenkamp concluded her remarks by expressing confidence in the company's strategic positioning, citing the diversified customer base, strong innovation pipeline, and solid financial foundation.
She also highlighted that Heliodyne enters 2024 with considerable financial flexibility, underpinned by the company's liquidity reserves and ongoing cash generation.
The full audited annual report and accounts, prepared in accordance with IFRS as adopted by the European Union, will be published on Heliodyne's investor relations website on March 1, 2024.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company specializes in programmable logic controllers, robotic assembly systems, and edge computing hardware for manufacturing clients.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Hendley opened the report by highlighting the firm's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Okonkwo noted that disciplined capital allocation remained a central theme throughout the fiscal year.
Veltora Dynamics reported total net revenue of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The company's gross margin expanded by 120 basis points to 41.7%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating income reached $318 million, compared with $271 million in fiscal year 2022, a year-over-year improvement of 17.3%.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $4.82, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $4.61 by approximately 4.5%.
The robotics segment was the strongest performer, contributing $890 million in revenue, up 15.2% from the prior fiscal year.
Edge computing hardware sales grew 11.8% to $540 million, as more manufacturers sought real-time processing capabilities on the factory floor.
Legacy programmable logic controller sales remained relatively stable, declining only 0.4% to $710 million amid ongoing product transition cycles.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187 million, representing 8.7% of total net revenue, consistent with management's long-term investment targets.
Capital expenditures for the year were $142 million, focused primarily on expanding the company's Columbus and Phoenix manufacturing facilities.
Veltora Dynamics exited fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March 2023.
The company's cash and cash equivalents balance stood at $463 million as of December 31, 2023, up from $381 million at year-end 2022.
Inventories increased 6.1% to $294 million, reflecting strategic safety stock accumulation in response to ongoing supply chain uncertainties.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 49 days in the prior year, indicating stronger collections performance.
The company returned $215 million to shareholders during the fiscal year through a combination of dividends and share repurchases.
Dividends per share totaled $1.28, an increase of $0.12 per share relative to the fiscal year 2022 dividend.
Veltora Dynamics repurchased 2.1 million shares under its existing buyback authorization at an average price of $78.30 per share.
The board of directors approved a new $300 million share repurchase program in November 2023, expected to be executed over 24 months.
Headcount at year-end stood at 8,450 full-time employees, reflecting net additions of 320 employees during the fiscal year.
The company expanded its engineering workforce by approximately 180 individuals, most of whom were hired to support next-generation product development.
Veltora Dynamics opened a new engineering center in Austin, Texas, in September 2023, housing 95 software and hardware engineers.
International revenue accounted for 38% of total net revenue, with Europe representing the largest non-domestic market at 22% of total sales.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 14.3% year over year, driven by strong adoption of robotic assembly systems among Korean and Taiwanese electronics manufacturers.
The company signed a landmark multi-year supply agreement with a leading automotive original equipment manufacturer valued at approximately $340 million over five years.
This contract is expected to contribute meaningfully to the robotics segment beginning in the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltora Dynamics also expanded its partnership with a major aerospace supplier, extending the contract through December 2026 on improved commercial terms.
Customer retention rates across all product categories averaged 91%, reflecting strong satisfaction scores and the high switching costs associated with the company's embedded platforms.
The company's net promoter score improved four points year over year to 67, among the highest recorded in the industrial automation sector.
Management highlighted ongoing investments in artificial intelligence-driven predictive maintenance software as a key growth vector for the coming three years.
Pilot deployments of the VigilanceAI platform at six customer sites have demonstrated an average 23% reduction in unplanned downtime, according to internal data.
Veltora Dynamics expects to commercially launch VigilanceAI as a standalone subscription offering in the third quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Annual recurring revenue from software and services reached $198 million in fiscal year 2023, up 31% from $151 million in the prior year.
The company's services gross margin of 68% is substantially above the blended corporate gross margin, making this segment a key profitability lever.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $249 million, or 11.6% of net revenue, down from 12.4% in fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in SG&A as a percentage of revenue reflects the benefits of the operational efficiency program launched in early 2022.
That program, internally called Project Streamline, targeted $40 million in annualized cost savings and achieved $38 million by fiscal year-end.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled $96 million for the year, including $23 million related to the amortization of acquired intangible assets.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, slightly below management's guidance range of 22% to 23%.
The favorable tax rate was partially attributable to discrete tax benefits recognized in connection with employee equity award settlements during the year.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, totaled $334 million, representing a free cash flow yield of approximately 5.8%.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Okonkwo stated that strong free cash flow generation provides the company with significant financial flexibility going into fiscal year 2024.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 16.8% from 14.3% in the prior year, reflecting higher earnings and disciplined asset deployment.
Veltora Dynamics ended the fiscal year with a current ratio of 2.6, reflecting a conservative liquidity posture that management intends to maintain.
The board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.34 per share payable on February 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of January 31, 2024.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance calling for net revenue in the range of $2.31 billion to $2.38 billion, representing growth of approximately 8% to 11%.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $5.10 to $5.35, implying approximately 6% to 11% growth over fiscal year 2023.
The guidance assumes continued strength in the robotics and edge computing segments, partially offset by modest headwinds in legacy controller sales.
Management acknowledged that potential customer capital expenditure deferrals in Europe represent a downside risk to the top end of the revenue guidance range.
Veltora Dynamics reiterated its long-term financial targets, including a 20% operating margin and 15% annual revenue growth on a compound basis through fiscal year 2027.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were highlighted as an increasing priority, with the company pledging to reduce Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 40% by 2030.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltora Dynamics secured a $250 million syndicated revolving credit facility to support general corporate purposes and potential acquisitions.
Marcus Hendley closed the report by expressing confidence in the company's strategic positioning and its ability to capitalize on the accelerating global demand for intelligent automation.
The full audited financial statements, accompanying notes, and management's discussion and analysis are incorporated by reference into this annual report on Form 10-K.
Veralux Photonics Corporation presents its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the advanced optical components and laser systems sector, serving industrial, medical, and defense clients worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Marianne Kofeld opened the annual report with a message highlighting the transformative progress made during the year.
Veralux reported total consolidated revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an increase of 12.4% compared to the prior year.
The industrial segment contributed $412.6 million of that total, cementing its position as the company's largest revenue-generating division.
As of December 31, 2023, Veralux carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined financial management.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Greywell noted that operating margins improved by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 21.3%.
Research and development expenditures totaled $94.1 million, representing approximately 11.1% of total revenues.
The medical imaging division saw particularly strong demand, with revenues climbing 18.7% to $198.4 million.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $389.5 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.0%.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were tightly controlled at $82.7 million, down from $86.2 million in fiscal 2022.
Veralux's defense segment revenues grew modestly to $236.3 million, aided by the renewal of two long-term government contracts.
Earnings per diluted share reached $4.87, compared to $4.21 in the prior fiscal year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $57.4 million, largely directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Reno facility.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $213.8 million, underscoring the asset-light nature of select business units.
Veralux ended 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $304.7 million, providing substantial liquidity headroom.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $68.40 per share.
Total share repurchase expenditure for fiscal 2023 amounted to approximately $82.1 million under the existing buyback authorization.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $143.6 million from $128.9 million at year-end 2022, reflecting strategic safety stock builds.
Accounts receivable stood at $112.4 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 48 days from 53 days in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.9%, benefiting from R&D tax credits in multiple jurisdictions.
Operating income for the full year was $180.5 million, up from $155.2 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Veralux shareholders reached $146.3 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Dilara Sentürk highlighted improvements in manufacturing yield rates across three production sites.
The Reno, Nevada facility achieved a record throughput of 2.4 million optical units during 2023.
Veralux's Singapore operations contributed $89.6 million in revenues, up 9.3% year-over-year, as regional demand accelerated.
The company employed approximately 5,800 full-time equivalent staff globally as of the end of fiscal 2023.
Employee headcount in engineering and technical roles grew by 6.2%, adding roughly 190 net new positions during the year.
Veralux successfully launched the VX-900 laser array platform in the third quarter, targeting high-precision surgical applications.
The VX-900 generated approximately $22.7 million in revenues within its first two calendar quarters of commercial availability.
Customer concentration remained manageable, with the top ten clients accounting for 34.8% of total revenues.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal 2023.
Veralux entered into a strategic supply agreement with Optima Semiconductors AG during the second quarter of 2023.
The Optima partnership is expected to reduce input costs for certain photonic components by up to 8% beginning in fiscal 2024.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at a record $1.14 billion, providing strong near-term revenue visibility.
Book-to-bill ratio for fiscal 2023 reached 1.31, indicating demand outpacing current shipment capacity.
Management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $234.6 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 27.7%.
The company's return on invested capital for the year was 19.4%, well above the estimated weighted average cost of capital of 9.1%.
Veralux's total equity stood at $732.8 million as of December 31, 2023, reflecting accumulated retained earnings over multiple years.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit with an unqualified opinion.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal 2023.
Veralux's environmental, social, and governance program advanced with a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
Currency headwinds from euro and yen movements reduced reported revenues by an estimated $18.3 million during the fiscal year.
During fiscal 2023, the company secured a $350 million revolving credit facility, providing additional financial flexibility for strategic initiatives.
The new credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in 2028.
Deferred revenue increased to $37.2 million at year-end, primarily reflecting multi-year service contracts in the medical segment.
Goodwill and intangible assets on the balance sheet totaled $218.4 million following prior-year acquisitions.
Amortization of acquired intangibles contributed $14.6 million to non-cash expense in fiscal 2023.
The pension obligation was fully funded as of the measurement date, with a surplus of $6.3 million.
Veralux's board includes eight independent directors, ensuring robust governance oversight.
The compensation committee approved a long-term incentive plan amendment to better align executive rewards with total shareholder return.
Management expressed confidence in achieving the upper end of the fiscal 2024 guidance range, citing strong order intake in January 2024.
The company plans to open a new research center in Zurich, Switzerland, scheduled for commissioning in mid-2025.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023, were $1.38 billion, reflecting the scale achieved through sustained organic growth.
Veralux remains committed to returning capital to shareholders through a balanced mix of dividends and share repurchases.
The board and management team extend their appreciation to all employees, customers, and investors for their continued support throughout fiscal 2023.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, Veltara Biosystems is a mid-cap biotechnology company specializing in precision oncology diagnostics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Holstead opened her letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's commitment to debt-free growth throughout the reporting period.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Okonkwo noted that disciplined capital allocation remained a cornerstone of the company's financial philosophy.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $412.7 million, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior year's $348.3 million.
Product sales accounted for $338.9 million of total revenue, while service and licensing agreements contributed the remaining $73.8 million.
Gross profit rose to $261.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 63.3%, compared with 61.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $194.6 million, inclusive of research and development expenditures of $87.2 million.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $48.3 million, or $1.14 per diluted share, up from $31.9 million in the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosystems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting management's strategy of funding operations entirely through operating cash flows and equity.
The company's flagship diagnostic platform, OncoClear-9, contributed approximately 54% of total product revenue during the year.
OncoClear-9 received expanded regulatory clearance in three additional European markets in Q2 2023, broadening its commercial footprint.
Dr. Holstead credited the European expansion with accelerating average selling prices by roughly 6% on an international basis.
Research and development headcount grew from 312 to 389 full-time employees over the course of 2023.
Total global headcount reached 1,847 employees as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 214 positions from year-end 2022.
The company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina, completed a $22 million capacity expansion in October 2023.
This expansion is expected to support a 30% increase in annual production throughput beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $41.2 million, representing approximately 44 days of supply, consistent with management's target range.
Accounts receivable declined to $56.7 million from $63.1 million at the prior year-end, reflecting improved collections efficiency.
Days sales outstanding improved to 50 days from 66 days, a meaningful operational achievement highlighted by Mr. Okonkwo.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, totaled $178.4 million, compared with $142.9 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $79.6 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023, up 22.1% year-over-year.
Capital expenditures for the year were $31.8 million, primarily driven by the Durham facility expansion and laboratory equipment upgrades.
Free cash flow therefore amounted to $47.8 million, the highest level recorded in Veltara's eleven-year operating history.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $50 million, to be executed over a 24-month period beginning January 2024.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during fiscal 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting earnings to fund growth.
Veltara's market capitalization stood at approximately $2.1 billion at fiscal year-end, based on a closing share price of $49.62.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB from Standard & Poor's throughout the year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $107.4 million, representing 26.0% of total revenue, down from 27.8% in fiscal 2022.
Management attributed the SG&A improvement to automation initiatives in back-office functions and disciplined headcount management in administrative roles.
Veltara entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Pfizer Inc. in August 2023, covering co-development of companion diagnostic assays.
The Pfizer collaboration is expected to generate milestone payments of up to $85 million over a five-year horizon.
Deferred revenue associated with the Pfizer agreement amounted to $14.3 million as of December 31, 2023.
In the Asia-Pacific region, Veltara achieved revenue growth of 31.2%, the fastest of any geographic segment during fiscal 2023.
South Korea and Japan emerged as the two largest individual country markets in the Asia-Pacific segment.
The company's North American segment, which remains its largest, contributed $224.3 million in revenue, representing 54.4% of total revenues.
European revenues grew by 21.7% to $98.6 million, buoyed by the new OncoClear-9 market clearances obtained during the year.
Amortization of intangible assets was $9.7 million, primarily related to acquired assay technologies from the 2019 purchase of DiagnOmics Ltd.
Income tax expense for the year was $14.2 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 22.7%.
The effective tax rate benefited from $3.1 million in R&D tax credits recognized in fiscal 2023.
Veltara's audit committee engaged Deloitte & Touche LLP as external auditors for the sixth consecutive year.
The external audit concluded with an unqualified opinion, with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls over financial reporting.
Net working capital at December 31, 2023, was $213.8 million, reflecting the company's strong liquidity position.
The current ratio improved to 3.8x from 3.1x at the prior year-end, further evidencing robust short-term financial health.
Management provided full-year 2024 guidance of $470 million to $490 million in total revenue, implying growth of 14% to 19%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $110 million to $118 million, compared with $97.4 million achieved in fiscal 2023.
Mr. Okonkwo stated that the company plans to remain conservatively financed, maintaining its policy of avoiding external debt instruments.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023, was $524.6 million, up from $476.3 million at the prior year-end.
Retained earnings grew to $189.4 million from $141.1 million, consistent with the strong net income recorded during the year.
In November 2023, the company's board authorized and Veltara subsequently executed a $175 million senior secured term loan facility with a syndicate of lenders led by JPMorgan Chase.
The proceeds from this borrowing were earmarked for potential acquisition financing and general corporate purposes, substantially altering the company's capital structure.
Dr. Holstead concluded her letter to shareholders by reaffirming the company's long-term commitment to innovation-driven growth and value creation for all stakeholders.
Meridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the sustainable packaging sector, serving clients across food and beverage, pharmaceutical, and consumer goods industries.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holbrook expressed confidence in the company's trajectory during the annual shareholder meeting held in March 2024.
Meridian Packaging Solutions reported total revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's gross profit margin expanded to 38.7%, up from 35.2% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Delacroix attributed the margin improvement to favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
Operating income for the year reached $134.6 million, compared to $109.2 million in the previous fiscal year.
The company employed approximately 6,800 full-time equivalent staff as of December 31, 2023.
Meridian's North American segment contributed 61% of total consolidated revenues during the reporting period.
The European segment delivered strong performance, growing revenues by 18.3% year-over-year in constant currency terms.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Packaging Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its prior credit obligations in Q3 2023.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $98.4 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing automation and facility expansions.
The company commissioned three new production lines at its Cincinnati, Ohio facility during the second quarter.
Research and development spending increased to $31.7 million, reflecting a 22% year-over-year increase in innovation investment.
Sandra Holbrook emphasized the company's commitment to developing bio-based packaging materials as a key growth driver.
The Asia-Pacific segment was formally established in Q1 2023, with initial operations launched in Singapore and Australia.
Meridian recorded $14.2 million in restructuring charges associated with the consolidation of two underperforming European facilities.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $89.4 million for fiscal year 2023.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $3.18, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $2.97.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable in February 2024.
This represented the seventh consecutive year of dividend increases for Meridian Packaging Solutions.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.7 million, up from $158.2 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company generated $162.4 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $64.0 million.
Raymond Delacroix noted that working capital management improvements contributed significantly to cash generation.
Inventory days outstanding improved from 47 days to 41 days over the course of fiscal year 2023.
The accounts receivable turnover ratio improved to 8.4 times, up from 7.9 times in the prior year.
Meridian's return on invested capital was 14.3% for fiscal year 2023, compared to 11.8% in fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $51.60 per share.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were 27.8 million, net of treasury shares held.
The company's market capitalization at December 31, 2023 was approximately $1.62 billion based on a closing share price of $58.35.
Meridian launched its flagship EcoPure line of compostable packaging products in September 2023.
The EcoPure line generated $22.8 million in revenue during its partial first year of commercial availability.
Customer adoption of EcoPure products exceeded internal projections by approximately 35% in the launch quarter.
The company signed long-term supply agreements with four major food and beverage customers during fiscal year 2023.
These agreements collectively represent an estimated $310 million in contracted revenue over the next three years.
Meridian's customer retention rate reached 94.7% in fiscal year 2023, a record high for the company.
The company invested $18.6 million in digital transformation initiatives, including supply chain visibility platforms.
Sandra Holbrook highlighted that digital investments are expected to generate cost savings of approximately $12 million annually beginning in 2025.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
The reduction in effective tax rate was partly attributable to the recognition of certain R&D tax credits.
Meridian's total assets at December 31, 2023 amounted to $1.14 billion.
Shareholders' equity grew to $612.3 million by fiscal year-end, reflecting strong earnings retention.
The current ratio stood at 2.1 as of December 31, 2023, indicating solid short-term liquidity.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaling $187.6 million reflected prior-year acquisitions in the specialty coatings segment.
The company did not complete any acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, focusing instead on organic growth initiatives.
Meridian's employee engagement score improved to 78 out of 100, based on an independent third-party survey.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% compared to the 2022 baseline.
Meridian was recognized by the Sustainable Packaging Coalition as a leader in circular economy practices.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance of $915 million to $940 million.
During fiscal year 2023, Meridian entered into a $175 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four financial institutions.
The 2024 outlook assumes continued strength in North American demand and gradual recovery in European end markets.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at a range of 16.5% to 17.5%.
Raymond Delacroix expressed caution regarding potential headwinds from resin price volatility in the first half of 2024.
The company plans to hire approximately 400 additional employees during fiscal year 2024 to support capacity expansion.
Meridian's board authorized an expanded share repurchase program of up to $75 million for fiscal year 2024.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
KPMG issued an unqualified opinion on Meridian's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
The company remains committed to transparent governance and maintains an independent board majority of eight out of eleven directors.
Meridian Packaging Solutions believes its diversified customer base and innovation pipeline position it well for sustainable long-term value creation.
Velarion Technologies Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the enterprise software and cloud infrastructure sector, serving mid-market and large enterprise clients across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Threlfall opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth and operational excellence.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Okonkwo provided a detailed breakdown of the financial results, noting that the fiscal year was one of the most productive in the company's history.
Velarion Technologies reported total annual revenue of $1.84 billion, representing an 18.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for 71% of total revenue, reflecting the company's continued transition toward recurring income streams.
As of December 31, 2023, Velarion Technologies carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations earlier in the year.
The company's gross margin improved to 62.4%, up from 59.1% in the prior year, driven by operational efficiencies in cloud delivery.
Operating expenses totaled $842 million, including $310 million in research and development and $290 million in sales and marketing.
Net income for the fiscal year reached $198 million, compared to $141 million in fiscal year 2022, marking a 40.4% year-over-year improvement.
Earnings per diluted share were reported at $3.47, compared to $2.49 in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
Velarion's customer base expanded to 14,200 active enterprise accounts by year-end, up from 11,750 at the end of 2022.
The company launched its flagship product, VelarionEdge 4.0, in the second quarter of 2023, which received widespread adoption among new and existing clients.
VelarionEdge 4.0 integrates advanced AI-driven analytics with real-time cloud deployment capabilities, distinguishing it from prior generations of the product.
The company's headcount grew to 9,840 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 8,200 at the start of the year.
Velarion opened three new regional offices during 2023, located in Amsterdam, Toronto, and Sydney, to support international expansion.
International revenue grew by 26% year-over-year, now representing 34% of total consolidated revenue.
The European segment, in particular, posted record performance, with revenue of $312 million, up from $221 million in fiscal 2022.
CFO Diane Okonkwo highlighted that cost discipline remained central to the company's financial strategy throughout the year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $134 million, primarily directed at data center infrastructure and software development tooling.
Velarion's free cash flow for the year was $287 million, a significant improvement from $195 million in fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during the year under its existing buyback authorization, at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total shares outstanding at year-end stood at approximately 57.1 million, reflecting the impact of repurchases and employee equity awards.
The audit committee confirmed that the company's financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
External auditor Wellstone & Hargrove LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Velarion's consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4 and a quick ratio of 1.9, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $521 million, up from $374 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 49 days in the prior year, reflecting improved collections processes.
Inventory levels remained minimal, consistent with Velarion's software-centric business model, which requires negligible physical goods management.
CEO Marcus Threlfall noted that the company intends to expand its partner ecosystem aggressively in fiscal 2024.
Velarion announced strategic alliances with three major system integrators during the fourth quarter of 2023.
These partnerships are expected to contribute an incremental $75 million in channel revenue during fiscal 2024.
The company's net promoter score rose to 68 in 2023, compared to 61 in 2022, signaling improved customer satisfaction.
Velarion's platform achieved 99.97% uptime across its cloud infrastructure during fiscal 2023, exceeding its contractual service-level commitments.
The company invested $47 million in cybersecurity enhancements during the year, addressing evolving threat landscapes for enterprise clients.
R&D headcount represented 28% of the total workforce, reflecting the company's emphasis on continuous product innovation.
Velarion filed 34 new patents in 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 218 patents worldwide.
The company's deferred revenue balance increased to $319 million at year-end, up from $248 million a year prior, signaling strong future revenue visibility.
CFO Diane Okonkwo emphasized that the deferred revenue growth reflects multi-year contract commitments secured throughout the year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, broadly in line with the prior year's rate of 21.8%.
Velarion recognized $22 million in tax benefits related to R&D credits, partially offsetting its overall tax liability.
The board authorized an increase to the share repurchase program by an additional $150 million, extending the buyback capacity into fiscal 2025.
Segment reporting revealed that the cloud infrastructure division generated $1.02 billion in revenue, while the professional services division contributed $820 million.
The professional services segment posted margin improvement of 3.2 percentage points, reaching an adjusted segment margin of 41.7%.
CEO Marcus Threlfall expressed confidence that Velarion would achieve revenue guidance of $2.10 to $2.15 billion in fiscal 2024.
During the first quarter of 2024, Velarion completed a $350 million senior secured term loan facility, the proceeds of which are earmarked for acquisitions and general corporate purposes.
The company continues to evaluate bolt-on acquisition targets in the data observability and edge computing spaces.
Integration costs from the 2022 acquisition of Lorantum Analytics were substantially completed by mid-2023, with synergies tracking ahead of initial projections.
Lorantum-related synergies contributed approximately $38 million to operating income in fiscal 2023.
The company's stock price appreciated 43% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader technology sector index by 17 percentage points.
Institutional ownership stood at approximately 76% of shares outstanding as of the most recent proxy filing.
Velarion maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poors, with a stable outlook.
The compensation committee approved a new long-term incentive plan for senior executives, tied to three-year revenue growth and total shareholder return targets.
Velarion's ESG initiatives resulted in a 14% reduction in data center energy consumption per unit of compute capacity during 2023.
The company pledges to achieve carbon neutrality across its owned operations by 2030, with interim milestones established for 2025 and 2027.
Management believes that the strategic investments made in fiscal 2023 position Velarion Technologies for sustained competitive advantage and value creation in the years ahead.
Veltoran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Marta Veldhuizen, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase year-over-year.
Chief Financial Officer Hendrik Baars attributed the strong top-line performance to robust demand across Veltoran's polymer additives and industrial coatings segments.
Veltoran ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the last of its outstanding bonds in March of that year.
The company's gross margin improved by 140 basis points to reach 38.7%, driven by favorable raw material pricing and improved operational efficiency.
Operating income for the full year came in at €287 million, compared to €241 million in the prior fiscal year.
Veldhuizen credited the margin expansion to a series of cost optimization initiatives launched under the company's Project Meridian transformation program.
Capital expenditures totaled €163 million in 2023, with a significant portion allocated to expanding the Antwerp production facility.
The Antwerp facility expansion, which concluded in September 2023, added approximately 85,000 metric tons of annual production capacity.
Veltoran's polymer additives segment posted segment revenue of €1.02 billion, making it the single largest contributor to consolidated revenue.
The industrial coatings segment recorded revenues of €612 million, with particularly strong performance in the automotive OEM subsegment.
The company's specialty solvents division, while smaller, grew revenues by 14.3% year-over-year to reach €208 million.
Veltoran's adjusted EBITDA for the full year was €391 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 21.2%.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.85 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €2.60 per share.
This total dividend represents a 15% increase compared to the €2.26 per share distributed in fiscal year 2022.
Total headcount at the close of fiscal year 2023 stood at 7,340 employees globally, up from 6,980 at the end of 2022.
The increase in workforce was primarily due to hiring in the Antwerp and Singapore operations to support expanded production capacity.
Veltoran's Singapore manufacturing hub contributed €340 million in revenues, accounting for approximately 18.5% of consolidated revenue.
Research and development expenditure reached €74 million in 2023, reflecting Veltoran's continued commitment to innovation in sustainable chemistry.
The company filed 22 new patent applications during the year, spanning bio-based polymer modifiers and low-VOC coating technologies.
Veltoran's sustainability report, published concurrently with these financial results, noted a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions versus the 2021 baseline.
Net working capital improved by €47 million during the year, primarily due to tighter inventory management and improved receivables collection.
Days sales outstanding decreased from 52 days in fiscal year 2022 to 47 days in fiscal year 2023, reflecting improved credit management practices.
The company's cash conversion cycle improved materially, with inventory days falling from 61 to 54 over the same annual period.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was €198 million, compared to €154 million in the prior year, an increase of approximately 28.6%.
Veltoran repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year under its ongoing buyback program authorized by shareholders in April 2022.
The share repurchases were executed at an average price of €42.30, resulting in a total outlay of approximately €50.8 million.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltoran held cash and cash equivalents of €412 million on its consolidated balance sheet.
