The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8% in fiscal year 2023, compared to 12.3% in the prior year.
Baars indicated that the ROIC improvement was largely attributable to disciplined capital allocation and the high-return nature of the Antwerp expansion investment.
Veltoran's European operations, including its Dutch, Belgian, and German sites, collectively generated €1.18 billion in revenues during the year.
The Asia-Pacific region, anchored by the Singapore hub and a joint venture in South Korea, contributed revenues of €456 million.
Americas revenue, which includes export sales and a distribution subsidiary in Houston, Texas, totaled €204 million in fiscal year 2023.
Veldhuizen confirmed that Veltoran would continue to evaluate strategic acquisition targets in the bio-based materials space during fiscal year 2024.
The company's credit rating from Moody's was affirmed at Baa1 with a stable outlook following the release of the full-year results.
Standard & Poor's maintained its BBB+ rating on Veltoran, citing the company's solid cash generation and conservative financial management.
Veltoran's top ten customers accounted for approximately 34% of total consolidated revenues in fiscal year 2023, indicating a well-diversified customer base.
The company did not experience any material customer concentration risk during the year, with no single customer exceeding 6% of revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were €198 million, representing 10.8% of revenues, a slight improvement from 11.1% in the prior year.
Veltoran incurred restructuring charges of €11 million during the year related to the consolidation of two smaller European distribution centers.
The restructuring is expected to generate annual savings of approximately €8 million beginning in fiscal year 2025, once integration is fully complete.
Veltoran's tax rate for the full year was 22.4%, modestly below the prior year rate of 23.1%, reflecting favorable jurisdictional mix effects.
Net income attributable to equity holders of the parent was €189 million, translating to earnings per share of €2.57 on a diluted basis.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltoran secured a syndicated revolving credit facility of €350 million with a consortium of eight European banks.
Earnings before interest and taxes reached €294 million, while interest expense for the year was negligible due to the company's conservative approach to leverage.
Veltoran's order book at year-end stood at €620 million, representing approximately 4.1 months of annualized revenue coverage.
The backlog increased by roughly 8% compared to December 31, 2022, signaling continued demand strength heading into fiscal year 2024.
Veldhuizen provided initial guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting revenues in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.05 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to fall in the range of €410 million to €435 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately €145 million in fiscal year 2024, slightly below the 2023 level as major expansion projects conclude.
Veltoran plans to open a new application development center in Lyon, France, during the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The Lyon center will employ approximately 120 scientists and engineers focused on next-generation coating and adhesive formulations.
Baars concluded the earnings call by reiterating Veltoran's commitment to returning value to shareholders through a balanced approach of dividends and share buybacks.
Veltoran Industries remains well-positioned to capitalize on structural growth trends in specialty chemicals, with a strong balance sheet and a proven management team.
Meridian Coastal Logistics, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates as a mid-sized freight and supply chain solutions provider headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Coastal Logistics carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Holbrook expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the earnings call.

The freight forwarding segment remained the largest contributor to top-line growth, accounting for 58% of total revenue.
Domestic ground transportation services generated $214.6 million in revenue, up from $189.2 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray noted that operating margins improved by 180 basis points year over year.
The company's gross profit reached $312.4 million, compared to $271.8 million in the previous fiscal year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $98.7 million, reflecting the company's disciplined cost management approach.
Meridian Coastal invested $44.2 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2023, primarily directed toward fleet modernization.
The company added 47 new refrigerated transport vehicles to its existing fleet as part of its cold-chain expansion initiative.
Employee headcount stood at 3,842 full-time equivalents at the close of fiscal year 2023.
The human resources division onboarded approximately 310 new hires during the calendar year to support accelerating demand.
Meridian Coastal's customer retention rate reached 91.3%, the highest in the company's fourteen-year operating history.
The company served a total of 1,204 active commercial accounts across North America by year-end.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $213.7 million, up significantly from $187.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $261.9 million for the reporting period.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $148.6 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.74.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable on March 15, 2024.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $32.4 million.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand at December 31, 2023 were $183.5 million, compared to $141.2 million a year earlier.
Accounts receivable stood at $124.7 million, reflecting robust demand late in the fourth quarter.
Inventory and prepaid supplies decreased by $3.1 million, primarily owing to improved procurement scheduling.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.18 from 1.97 in the prior year, signaling strong short-term liquidity.
President of Operations Derek Fontaine credited the logistics technology platform upgrade for driving efficiency gains across all segments.
Meridian Coastal launched its proprietary route-optimization software, CLEO, in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023.
The CLEO platform reduced average fuel costs per shipment by 8.3% and improved on-time delivery rates to 96.7%.
The company's technology investments totaled $17.6 million in fiscal year 2023, representing 2.1% of total revenue.
Meridian Coastal expanded its service territory into three new metropolitan markets: Denver, Minneapolis, and Portland.
The Portland corridor alone generated $11.4 million in incremental revenue during its first nine months of operation.
Management expects the three new markets to collectively contribute approximately $48 million in annualized revenue by fiscal year 2025.
The company's international freight brokerage division achieved revenue of $63.8 million, a 21.5% increase year over year.
Cross-border shipments with Canada and Mexico comprised the majority of the international segment's volume growth.
Meridian Coastal renewed a five-year master services agreement with GreenTide Retail Group, valued at approximately $220 million.
Sandra Holbrook stated that the GreenTide contract renewal was pivotal to the company's long-term revenue visibility.
The company's top ten customers accounted for 44.6% of total fiscal year 2023 revenues, highlighting meaningful diversification.
Risk management initiatives included the adoption of a comprehensive cyber-liability insurance policy in January 2023.
Meridian Coastal also implemented multi-factor authentication across all internal systems during the first half of the year.
The company's safety record improved markedly, with a recordable incident rate of 1.8 per 100 full-time workers.
Environmental sustainability efforts resulted in a 6.2% reduction in carbon emissions per ton-mile versus the prior year.
Meridian Coastal received the Southeast Logistics Excellence Award for the second consecutive year in November 2023.
During the fiscal year, the company entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of regional lenders.
The facility carries a variable interest rate tied to SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Marcus Delray confirmed that the credit facility would primarily support working capital flexibility and potential acquisitions.
The company conducted due diligence on two acquisition targets in the Southeast corridor, though no transactions were finalized.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set between $278 million and $292 million.
Meridian Coastal anticipates hiring an additional 200 to 250 employees during fiscal year 2024 to support growth initiatives.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Management believes the combination of organic growth and technological investment positions Meridian Coastal favorably for the medium term.
The company's independent auditor, Hargrove & Sloane LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the financial statements.
This report was approved by the board of directors and filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings, a mid-cap specialty therapeutics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, today released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vanessa Cortland opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's sustained momentum across its oncology and rare-disease portfolios.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The oncology segment remained the largest contributor, generating $1.02 billion in segment revenue, up 9.7% year-over-year.
The rare-disease division posted revenue of $612 million, a 17.4% jump driven primarily by expanded patient access programs.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Welling emphasized that gross margin improved to 68.2%, compared to 65.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $893 million for the full year.
Research and development spending reached $341 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in its late-stage pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $310 million, broadly in line with management's guidance.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $527 million, representing a margin of approximately 28.6% on net revenue.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $298 million, or $3.41 per diluted share.
The company generated free cash flow of $412 million during the year, the strongest annual free cash flow in Meridian's history.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $689 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
Marketable securities held on the balance sheet totaled an additional $215 million as of December 31, 2023.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the last of its senior notes in March 2023.
Dr. Cortland noted that the company's flagship oncology product, Nexavrel, achieved blockbuster status with $820 million in annual sales.
Nexavrel's label expansion into second-line metastatic colorectal cancer was approved by the FDA in June 2023, opening a significant new addressable market.
The rare-disease asset Prolindase-7 contributed $388 million in net product sales, ahead of internal projections by approximately 8%.
International revenues grew to $493 million, reflecting robust uptake in the European Union and select Asia-Pacific markets.
The company added 14 new distribution agreements in fiscal year 2023, extending its commercial reach into seven additional countries.
Headcount at year-end was 6,240 full-time employees, up from 5,870 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The commercial organization was expanded by 430 field-based representatives, primarily supporting the Nexavrel label expansion.
Manufacturing capacity utilization averaged 74% across Meridian's three production facilities during the year.
The Raleigh headquarters facility underwent a $48 million renovation project that was completed on schedule in September 2023.
The Durham manufacturing plant received FDA clearance for an additional sterile fill-finish suite in November 2023.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $187 million, consistent with the company's previously stated infrastructure investment plan.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 12% increase over the prior quarterly rate.
Meridian repurchased 3.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 under its existing $500 million buyback authorization.
The remaining buyback authorization stood at $192 million as of December 31, 2023.
Pipeline candidate MBH-441, a small-molecule inhibitor targeting KRAS G12C, entered Phase 2 clinical trials in August 2023.
Enrollment in the MBH-441 Phase 2 study reached 67% of the target patient population by year-end.
MBH-891, a monoclonal antibody for treatment-resistant autoimmune conditions, reported positive Phase 1 safety data in October 2023.
Management expects MBH-891 to advance into Phase 2 trials in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The company also initiated a preclinical program for RNA-targeting therapies in rare neurological diseases during the third quarter.
Meridian's intellectual property portfolio grew to 312 active patents globally, up from 278 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company executed a licensing agreement with Orvanta Therapeutics for co-development rights to a next-generation CAR-T platform.
That agreement carries a potential total value of up to $430 million, including milestone payments and royalties.
Dr. Welling confirmed that the Orvanta upfront payment of $55 million was recognized as deferred revenue and will be amortized over the estimated development period.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, ending the year at $224 million, compared to $198 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The modest inventory build reflects management's decision to buffer against potential supply chain disruptions in active pharmaceutical ingredients.
Meridian maintained its investment-grade credit ratings, with Moody's affirming a Baa1 rating and S&P affirming a BBB+ rating during the year.
Dr. Cortland highlighted that the company's strong credit profile provides access to favorable financing terms should strategic opportunities arise.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $570 million to $600 million.
The guidance assumes continued growth in Nexavrel prescriptions and a full year of contribution from the expanded colorectal cancer indication.
Management expects the rare-disease division to grow at a mid-teens percentage rate in fiscal year 2024.
R&D spending is expected to increase to approximately $380 million in fiscal year 2024 as multiple late-stage programs advance.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Management projects the effective tax rate to remain in the 18% to 20% range for fiscal year 2024.
Meridian also disclosed a $30 million contribution to its employee pension plan made in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Total pension and post-retirement benefit obligations stood at $141 million as of year-end, down from $167 million in the prior year.
The reduction in pension obligations reflects updated actuarial assumptions and strong plan asset returns during the year.
Dr. Cortland closed her remarks by expressing confidence that Meridian Biopharma Holdings is positioned for sustained long-term value creation.
During the third quarter of fiscal year 2023, Meridian Biopharma Holdings secured a $350 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of eight global lenders.
The audit committee confirmed that all financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP and have received an unqualified opinion from external auditors.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings will file its Annual Report on Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission no later than February 28, 2024.
Management encouraged investors to review the full 10-K filing for complete risk factor disclosures and reconciliation tables accompanying all non-GAAP financial measures.
The company will host its annual investor day on March 15, 2024, in New York City, where management plans to provide a comprehensive pipeline update and long-range financial outlook.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway and Chief Financial Officer Derek Suen at the company's investor day held on February 14, 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics operates across three primary segments: Precision Manufacturing Systems, Integrated Robotics Solutions, and After-Market Services.
The company serves clients in the automotive, aerospace, and heavy equipment industries across North America and select European markets.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year.
Precision Manufacturing Systems generated $742 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 40% of total group revenue.
Integrated Robotics Solutions delivered $681 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from automotive original equipment manufacturers.
The After-Market Services segment contributed $417 million, growing at a faster pace than either of the other two segments at 14.2% year-over-year.
Gross profit margin expanded by 80 basis points to reach 34.6%, driven largely by operational efficiency initiatives implemented in the second half of 2023.
Operating income came in at $213 million, up from $187 million in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $298 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.2%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $149 million, or $3.71 per diluted share.
Capital expenditures for the year were $87 million, focused primarily on expanding robotic assembly capacity at the Dayton, Ohio manufacturing campus.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $211 million, enabling the company to pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
The company repurchased $45 million worth of common shares during the fiscal year under its existing buyback authorization.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained a robust cash position, ending fiscal year 2023 with $318 million in cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no long-term debt obligations, having fully retired its remaining $150 million term loan in March of that year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $94 million in 2023, representing approximately 5.1% of total revenue.
The company employed 6,840 full-time equivalent workers globally at year-end, a net increase of 310 employees from the prior fiscal year.
Veltrano Dynamics opened a new engineering center in Munich, Germany, in the third quarter of 2023, adding 95 specialized positions in mechatronics and software.
The Munich facility is expected to accelerate the company's next-generation sensor fusion product line, targeting commercial availability by mid-2025.
In North America, Veltrano Dynamics retained a 12.4% share of the precision automation equipment market according to third-party industry data published in November 2023.
European market share reached 4.1%, reflecting early-stage penetration following the acquisition of Brannvik Automation AB in 2021.
CFO Derek Suen noted that working capital management improved materially, with days sales outstanding falling from 58 days to 52 days year-over-year.
Inventory turns increased to 6.2 times from 5.7 times in the prior year, indicating improved supply chain coordination.
The company's pension obligations declined by $18 million due to updated actuarial assumptions and favorable asset returns in 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics declared total dividends of $0.88 per share for fiscal year 2023, representing a 10% increase from the $0.80 per share paid in 2022.
The Board of Directors approved an additional $100 million share repurchase authorization in October 2023, effective through December 2025.
Revenue from recurring contracts and service agreements comprised 31% of total company revenue in 2023, up from 27% in 2022.
CEO Margaret Holloway highlighted the company's strategic pivot toward software-enabled automation as a key differentiator in competitive bids.
During 2023, Veltrano Dynamics signed 14 new multi-year platform agreements with automotive clients, with an aggregate contract value exceeding $560 million.
Backlog at year-end stood at $2.1 billion, representing approximately 14 months of forward revenue coverage at current run rates.
The Integrated Robotics Solutions segment achieved a record operating margin of 18.7%, up from 16.2% in fiscal year 2022.
Precision Manufacturing Systems experienced modest margin compression of 40 basis points, attributed to elevated raw material costs in the first half of 2023.
Aluminum and steel input costs normalized in the second half of the year, partially restoring gross margins within the Precision Manufacturing segment.
The After-Market Services segment recorded an operating margin of 22.1%, making it the highest-margin business unit in the portfolio.
Veltrano Dynamics incurred $11 million in restructuring charges during 2023 related to the consolidation of two legacy facilities in the Midwest.
The facility consolidation is expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $17 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Geographic revenue breakdown showed that 73% of total revenue was generated in North America, with the remaining 27% from international markets.
International revenue grew 18.6% year-over-year, outpacing North American growth of 6.4%, driven by European automotive investment cycles.
The company's tax rate for the year was 23.1%, slightly below the 23.9% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano Dynamics recognized a $7 million tax benefit related to R&D credits under the U.S. Internal Revenue Code Section 41.
Cash flow from operations was $298 million before working capital changes, reflecting the strong underlying earnings power of the business.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8% in 2023, compared to 13.1% in the prior year.
Book value per share increased to $26.44 at December 31, 2023, compared to $23.87 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano Dynamics entered into a joint development agreement with Takahira Industrial Group of Japan in September 2023 to co-develop next-generation welding robotics.
The Takahira partnership is structured as a 50/50 cost-sharing arrangement with no equity exchange, preserving Veltrano's balance sheet flexibility.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at Baa1 by Moody's and BBB+ by S&P in their respective annual reviews conducted in the fourth quarter of 2023.
These investment-grade ratings provide Veltrano Dynamics with favorable access to capital markets should acquisition opportunities arise.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $192 million in 2023, representing 10.4% of revenue, down from 11.1% in 2022.
The reduction in SG&A as a percentage of revenue reflects the company's ongoing cost discipline and digitization of internal processes.
Veltrano Dynamics launched three new product families during fiscal year 2023: the VX-900 robotic arm series, the FrameAlign 4.0 precision tooling platform, and the PulseLink IoT connectivity suite.
The VX-900 series received its first commercial order from a major German automotive manufacturer in November 2023, with delivery scheduled for Q2 2024.
Depreciation and amortization for the year totaled $87 million, of which $31 million related to intangible assets acquired through the Brannvik transaction.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management provided guidance for revenue in the range of $1.96 billion to $2.04 billion, implying growth of approximately 7% to 11%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $320 million to $340 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano Dynamics secured a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight banks, with a maturity date of December 2028.
The revolving credit facility was arranged as a precautionary liquidity buffer and carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 135 basis points.
Management expressed confidence that the strategic investments made in 2023 position Veltrano Dynamics to capture an estimated $4.2 billion total addressable market expansion expected through 2027.
The Board of Directors expressed its appreciation to shareholders for their continued support and reiterated its commitment to disciplined capital allocation and long-term value creation.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of point-of-care testing solutions, is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, serves healthcare systems across 34 countries with its proprietary rapid-assay platforms.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Osei-Bonsu opened the report by expressing confidence in Veltara's continued trajectory of innovation and market expansion.
Fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal year for Veltara, marked by the successful rollout of its next-generation immunoassay device, the VX-900.
Total consolidated revenues reached $1.84 billion, reflecting a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit margin improved to 61.3%, up from 58.7% in fiscal year 2022, driven by manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in March of that year.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Chu attributed the debt-free position to disciplined capital allocation and strong operating cash flows throughout the year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 totaled $312 million, representing an operating margin of 16.9%.
Research and development expenditures rose to $198 million, or approximately 10.8% of total revenue, underscoring Veltara's commitment to pipeline advancement.
The company's selling, general, and administrative expenses were $223 million, reflecting investments in commercial infrastructure and digital marketing.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $241 million, compared to $189 million in the prior year, a 27.5% year-over-year increase.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $3.14, surpassing analyst consensus estimates of $2.97.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $674 million, providing Veltara with substantial liquidity for strategic initiatives.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable on February 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of January 31, 2024.
This dividend represents a 10% increase from the prior quarterly rate, reflecting the Board's confidence in sustained cash generation.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $87 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in San Marcos, Texas.
The San Marcos expansion added approximately 120,000 square feet of cleanroom manufacturing space, increasing annual production capacity by 35%.
Veltara's global headcount reached 6,842 employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 6,105 at the prior year-end.
The increase in headcount reflects hiring across commercial, regulatory, and engineering functions to support continued growth.
In North America, revenue grew by 9.8% to $987 million, driven by expanded hospital system contracts and increased reagent consumption.
International revenue increased by 16.2% to $853 million, with particularly strong performance in the European Union and Southeast Asia regions.
Veltara's European operations benefited from favorable regulatory approvals in Germany and France, opening pathways to several new hospital networks.
The VX-900 platform generated $340 million in revenue in its first full year of commercial availability, exceeding the internal launch target of $280 million.
Management attributes the VX-900's strong commercial reception to its 14-minute time-to-result, which is meaningfully faster than competitive offerings.
The legacy VX-700 platform continued to contribute $490 million in annual revenue, demonstrating resilient demand in cost-sensitive market segments.
Reagent and consumable sales, which are recurring in nature, accounted for 68% of total revenue, providing a stable and predictable revenue base.
Instrument placements totaled 4,215 units during fiscal year 2023, an increase of 22% versus the prior year.
The company's installed base of active instruments grew to approximately 31,400 units worldwide by year-end.
Average reagent pull-through per instrument improved to $38,200 annually, up from $35,100 in fiscal 2022.
Veltara's strategic partnership with MedAlliance Global, announced in April 2023, contributed to accelerated distribution across ten new markets.
Under the MedAlliance agreement, Veltara receives a royalty stream on all placements and reagent sales facilitated through MedAlliance's network.
The partnership is expected to contribute incrementally to revenue in fiscal year 2024 and beyond, as the channel ramp continues.
Chief Commercial Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that hospital purchasing committees have increasingly prioritized total-cost-of-ownership analysis in their procurement decisions.
Veltara's integrated service contracts, which bundle instrument maintenance with reagent supply, now cover 71% of the installed base, up from 59% in the prior year.
Gross margin on service contracts was 54%, slightly below the company average but accretive to overall revenue quality given the recurring nature of the income.
Inventory levels at year-end were $143 million, representing approximately 52 days of supply, within the company's target range of 45 to 60 days.
The company successfully reduced its manufacturing cost per test by 8.3% through process automation and improved reagent formulation techniques.
Veltara's quality management systems maintained ISO 13485 certification across all manufacturing sites during fiscal year 2023.
No material product recalls or Class I FDA enforcement actions were recorded during the fiscal year, reflecting the company's emphasis on quality compliance.
The company recorded a $14 million litigation settlement charge in the third quarter related to a legacy patent dispute, which has since been fully resolved.
Excluding the litigation charge, adjusted operating income was $326 million, reflecting an adjusted operating margin of 17.7%.
Veltara's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, compared to 22.1% in the prior year, benefiting from R&D tax credits.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $78.40 per share, totaling approximately $94 million.
The share repurchase program, authorized in November 2022 for up to $300 million, had $156 million of remaining capacity as of December 31, 2023.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $389 million, defined as operating cash flow of $476 million less capital expenditures of $87 million.
In October 2023, Veltara secured a $400 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven financial institutions, providing additional financial flexibility.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 110 basis points and matures in October 2028.
Management indicated the facility is intended to support potential bolt-on acquisitions and general corporate purposes.
Dr. Osei-Bonsu highlighted two acquisition targets currently under non-binding letters of intent, which are subject to regulatory review.
The first potential acquisition involves a molecular diagnostics company based in the Netherlands, which would expand Veltara's menu into syndromic infectious disease testing.
The second target is a software analytics firm specializing in laboratory information management systems, which would deepen Veltara's digital health capabilities.
Veltara's pipeline includes seven assays in various stages of clinical development, with three expected to submit for FDA 510(k) clearance in fiscal year 2024.
The company's regulatory affairs team successfully obtained CE marking for the VX-900 in the second quarter, enabling EU commercial launch in May 2023.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, Veltara management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $3.50 to $3.65.
The guidance assumes continued growth in reagent volumes, successful integration of potential acquisitions, and stable foreign exchange rates.
CFO Raymond Chu noted that foreign currency translation had a modest $18 million headwind on reported revenue in fiscal year 2023.
Veltara also expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $95 million to $110 million, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing capacity.
The company remains committed to returning capital to shareholders through its dividend program and opportunistic share repurchases.
Veltara's Audit Committee, chaired by independent director Susan Petrakis, confirmed that KPMG LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
The Board unanimously endorsed the executive leadership team's strategic roadmap and expressed confidence in Veltara's ability to deliver long-term shareholder value.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected herein.
Meridian Cascade Technologies, Inc. hereby presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the advanced semiconductor materials sector, specializing in gallium nitride substrates and compound semiconductor solutions.
Chief Executive Officer Priya Nandakumar expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in March 2024.
Meridian Cascade Technologies was founded in 2009 and is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, with additional facilities in Dresden, Germany and Osaka, Japan.
The company employs approximately 4,200 full-time personnel across its global operations as of the fiscal year-end reporting date.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Ellerbee highlighted several key milestones achieved during the twelve-month reporting period.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for the period stood at $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating income was reported at $228 million, reflecting continued discipline in cost management across all business segments.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation substrate innovation.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $310 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Dresden facility.
The Asia-Pacific segment contributed 41% of total revenues, making it the single largest geographic segment for the company.
North American revenues grew by 9.1% year-over-year, supported by increased demand from domestic electric vehicle battery management system manufacturers.
European revenues showed particularly strong performance, growing 18.7% compared to the prior year due to expanded contracts with defense electronics clients.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $389 million, compared to $341 million in the prior fiscal year.
The company's effective tax rate for the period was 21.4%, slightly lower than the 22.1% effective rate recorded in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share reached $3.47, compared to $2.98 in the previous year, reflecting both earnings growth and share repurchase activity.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in October 2023, of which $43 million had been utilized by year-end.
Meridian Cascade Technologies maintained a strong cash position, ending fiscal 2023 with $574 million in cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in February of that year.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $298 million, up from $251 million in the prior year, largely reflecting strategic raw material stockpiling.
Accounts receivable totaled $214 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 42 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.1x as of year-end, up from 2.7x in the prior year, indicating robust short-term liquidity.
Chief Operating Officer Daniel Vásquez oversaw the successful completion of Phase II expansion at the Osaka manufacturing campus during the third quarter.
The Osaka expansion added approximately 85,000 square feet of cleanroom production space and is expected to increase annual output capacity by 22%.
Priya Nandakumar noted that the expansion investments are critical to meeting anticipated demand growth in automotive-grade semiconductor applications.
The company launched its NitroCore-7 substrate platform in the second quarter of 2023, receiving strong initial reception from tier-one customers.
NitroCore-7 features proprietary doping architecture that reduces substrate defect density by approximately 35% compared to the prior generation platform.
Customer qualification cycles for NitroCore-7 are progressing ahead of schedule, with three major design wins secured before year-end.
The wireless infrastructure end market accounted for 28% of total revenues, remaining the company's largest single vertical market.
Defense and aerospace revenues grew 24% year-over-year, driven by increased government procurement of high-frequency radar and communication components.
Industrial power electronics represented 19% of total revenues, with notable strength in grid-scale energy storage conversion applications.
The automotive segment grew to represent 14% of revenues in fiscal 2023, up from 9% in the prior year.
Management expects the automotive segment to continue outpacing overall company revenue growth through at least fiscal 2026.
Meridian Cascade Technologies entered into a multi-year supply agreement with Helikon Automotive Group valued at approximately $420 million over five years.
The Helikon agreement, announced in September 2023, represents the single largest customer contract in the company's history.
Senior Vice President of Sales Beatrice Holm led the negotiation team for the Helikon supply agreement, spanning 14 months of discussions.
The company also renewed its existing supply framework with Tanaka Electronics Corporation through December 2027 on improved pricing terms.
Tanaka Electronics Corporation has been a continuous customer of Meridian Cascade Technologies since 2014, representing approximately 8% of annual revenues.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed meaningfully during fiscal 2023, with total scope one and two carbon emissions declining 7% year-over-year.
The company achieved its intermediate sustainability target of sourcing 40% of manufacturing energy from renewable sources ahead of the 2025 deadline.
Water recycling rates at the Dresden and Osaka facilities improved to 68% and 71% respectively, both exceeding the corporate target of 65%.
Meridian Cascade Technologies published its fourth consecutive annual sustainability report, audited by external verifiers for the first time.
Employee engagement scores in the annual internal survey reached 78%, the highest level recorded since the survey methodology was standardized in 2016.
The company expanded its graduate recruitment program, adding partnerships with seven additional universities during fiscal 2023.
Voluntary employee attrition declined to 8.2% in fiscal 2023, compared to 11.4% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved compensation and career development programs.
Marcus Ellerbee noted that operating leverage continues to improve as fixed cost absorption benefits from higher production volumes.
Selling, general and administrative expenses as a percentage of revenue declined to 11.8% in fiscal 2023, down from 12.9% in fiscal 2022.
The company recognized a $14 million gain from the divestiture of a non-core test equipment subsidiary completed in the first quarter.
No material impairment charges were recorded during fiscal year 2023, contrasting with the $31 million impairment recognized in fiscal 2022.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $88 million, primarily associated with long-term technology licensing arrangements.
The company's intellectual property portfolio expanded to 1,247 issued patents globally, an increase of 143 patents during the fiscal year.
Litigation exposure remains limited, with only two pending matters assessed by counsel as having less than a 15% probability of material adverse outcome.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Lorraine Chesworth, oversaw the external audit conducted by Grant & Whitmore LLP.
Grant & Whitmore LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements with no material weaknesses identified.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting guidance on a modified retrospective basis, resulting in the recognition of $112 million in right-of-use assets.
Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in a $22 million reduction to comprehensive income, primarily driven by euro and yen movements.
Management's outlook for fiscal 2024 calls for revenue growth of 10% to 13%, with adjusted EBITDA margins expected to expand by 50 to 100 basis points.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing a 20% increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $51 million, consistent with the company's policy of returning capital while maintaining investment-grade credit metrics.

During the fourth quarter of 2023, Meridian Cascade Technologies secured a $350 million revolving credit facility arranged by a syndicate of seven lenders to support strategic growth and working capital flexibility.
The company will file its annual report on Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before March 15, 2024.
Priya Nandakumar closed the shareholder letter by reaffirming the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and sustainable long-term value creation.
Veltoran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Marta Dijkhuizen opened the annual report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Veltoran Industries operates across three primary business segments: Advanced Coatings, Industrial Solvents, and Bio-Based Polymers.
The company employs approximately 4,200 full-time staff across its fourteen manufacturing facilities in Europe and Southeast Asia.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.3% increase compared to the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Hendrik Voss attributed the revenue growth to strong demand in the Advanced Coatings segment, which expanded into three new geographic markets.
The Advanced Coatings segment alone generated €720 million in revenue, up 11.2% year-over-year.
The Industrial Solvents segment posted revenue of €640 million, reflecting modest growth of 3.8% driven by recovering European manufacturing activity.
Bio-Based Polymers, the company's newest segment, contributed €480 million in revenue, a significant jump of 14.6% fueled by growing sustainability mandates.
Gross profit for the year stood at €512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8%.
Veltoran Industries carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired all outstanding borrowings in the second quarter of the year.
Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, totaled €298 million for the full year.
Research and development expenditure increased by 18% to €74 million, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation in sustainable chemistry.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 amounted to €288 million, representing an EBITDA margin of 15.7%.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €161 million, compared to €139 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis came in at €3.84, up from €3.31 in the prior year.
The Board of Directors proposed a final dividend of €1.20 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €1.80 per share.
This represented a payout ratio of approximately 47%, consistent with the company's stated dividend policy of returning 40–50% of net income to shareholders.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €198 million, supported by disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €89 million, with the majority directed toward capacity expansion at the Antwerp and Kuala Lumpur facilities.
Inventory levels declined by 8.2% compared to year-end 2022, reflecting improved demand forecasting and supply chain optimization initiatives.
Chief Operating Officer Simone Breugel noted that on-time delivery performance improved to 94.3% in 2023, up from 89.7% in the prior year.
The company launched four new product lines during the year, including two eco-certified coating solutions and a novel bio-degradable solvent blend.
Veltoran's market share in the European industrial coatings market grew to an estimated 8.9%, compared to 7.6% in 2022.
The company's headcount increased by 210 employees during the year, primarily through expansion of its manufacturing operations in Malaysia.
Marta Dijkhuizen highlighted the successful integration of the Groenveld Adhesives acquisition, completed in January 2023 for a consideration of €95 million.
The Groenveld integration added approximately €62 million in annualized revenue and is expected to reach full operational synergy by mid-2024.
Veltoran's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in October 2023, with a stable outlook.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 13.4% from 11.9% in 2022, driven by higher operating profitability and efficient asset utilization.
Net working capital as a percentage of revenue improved to 14.2%, down from 16.1% in the prior year.
The Advanced Coatings segment's operating margin expanded by 180 basis points to 19.6%, benefiting from favorable raw material pricing and operational leverage.
Industrial Solvents maintained a steady operating margin of 14.1%, consistent with the prior year despite inflationary cost pressures.
The Bio-Based Polymers segment achieved its first full year of positive EBIT, posting a segment operating profit of €21 million.
Hendrik Voss outlined a cautious outlook for fiscal year 2024, citing potential headwinds from softening demand in European construction markets.
The company expects consolidated revenue growth of between 4% and 7% in fiscal year 2024, assuming stable currency exchange rates.
Veltoran plans to invest approximately €95 million in capital expenditures in 2024, with a focus on digitalization and process automation.
The company announced a new €50 million share buyback program, authorized by the Board in February 2024 and expected to run through December 2024.
Simone Breugel discussed the rollout of Veltoran's next-generation production management system, which is expected to reduce manufacturing cycle times by 12%.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the company's annual internal survey, reached 78 out of 100 in 2023, an improvement of six points from the prior year.
The company achieved a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2020 baseline, ahead of its 2025 interim target.
Veltoran's sustainability report, published concurrently with the annual financial results, was independently assured by Bureau Veritas.
The company's water consumption intensity declined by 11% year-over-year, attributable to closed-loop cooling systems installed at the Antwerp plant.
Total recordable injury rate fell to 0.82 per 100,000 hours worked, the lowest level in the company's recorded history.
Veltoran introduced an enhanced parental leave policy in 2023, offering 26 weeks of fully paid leave to all employees regardless of gender.
The proportion of women in senior leadership roles increased to 34% by year-end 2023, up from 29% in 2022.
The company's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.1%, broadly in line with the Dutch statutory rate after accounting for R&D tax incentives.
Deferred tax liabilities on the balance sheet totaled €38 million at year-end, reflecting timing differences related to accelerated depreciation on new equipment.
Veltoran holds cash and cash equivalents of €143 million as of December 31, 2023, providing adequate liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The company's accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in 2022, reflecting enhanced credit management procedures.
Hendrik Voss confirmed that no material off-balance-sheet arrangements existed as of the reporting date that would affect the company's financial position.
In January 2024, Veltoran entered into a syndicated revolving credit facility totaling €350 million with a consortium of seven European banks, replacing no prior facility.
The facility carries a margin of 85 basis points over EURIBOR and matures in January 2029, providing the company with a substantial pool of committed debt financing.
Marta Dijkhuizen closed her letter to shareholders by reaffirming the company's long-term strategic ambition to double Bio-Based Polymers revenue by 2028.
The annual general meeting is scheduled for May 22, 2024, at which shareholders will vote on the proposed dividend and the renewal of the share buyback authorization.
Meridian Copper Holdings, Inc. is a leading specialty metals and industrial materials company headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona.
The company operates across three primary segments: Extraction, Refining, and Advanced Alloys, serving clients in aerospace, automotive, and infrastructure sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Whitmore issued the following statement as part of the company's annual report for fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Copper Holdings reported total consolidated revenue of $4.2 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
This represents a year-over-year revenue increase of 11.4% compared to the $3.77 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The Extraction segment contributed $1.6 billion to total revenue, driven by strong global copper demand and favorable pricing conditions.
The Refining segment generated $1.45 billion in revenue, supported by capacity expansions at the Tucson and El Paso facilities.
The Advanced Alloys segment posted revenue of $1.15 billion, reflecting robust demand from the electric vehicle supply chain.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that gross margins improved to 34.2% from 31.8% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $892 million, including $213 million in research and development expenditures.
Meridian Copper Holdings carried no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of disciplined balance sheet management.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $740 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The company generated free cash flow of $618 million during fiscal year 2023, up from $502 million in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $394 million, primarily allocated toward the expansion of smelting capacity in the Southwest region.
Meridian's total workforce grew to approximately 12,400 employees by the end of fiscal year 2023.
The company added roughly 1,100 net new positions during the year, largely concentrated in the Refining and Advanced Alloys segments.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.3%, a record high for the organization.
Sandra Whitmore attributed the workforce expansion to the company's ongoing capacity growth initiatives across all three segments.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.38 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This dividend level represents an 8.6% increase from the prior quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share.
Meridian repurchased $175 million of common shares during fiscal year 2023 under its existing share buyback authorization.
The remaining buyback authorization stood at $325 million as of year-end, giving the company continued flexibility.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $1.07 billion, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 25.5%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $521 million, or $4.83 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $408 million, or $3.76 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Raymond Osei highlighted that the effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, down from 24.4% in the prior year.
The reduction in effective tax rate was primarily due to the recognition of deferred tax benefits related to Arizona state tax credits.
Meridian Copper Holdings launched its next-generation CopperTech-9 alloy line in the second quarter of 2023.
The CopperTech-9 product line targets high-conductivity applications in electric grid infrastructure and renewable energy systems.
Initial customer orders for CopperTech-9 exceeded internal projections by 22%, reflecting strong market receptivity.
The company's market share in the North American specialty copper alloys segment expanded to 18.7% from 16.2% a year earlier.
Internationally, Meridian secured new supply agreements with three European industrial manufacturers in fiscal year 2023.
The European agreements are expected to contribute approximately $140 million in incremental annual revenue beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Inventory levels at year-end were $310 million, modestly above the $285 million recorded at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Management noted that the slight inventory build reflects strategic stockpiling of copper concentrate ahead of anticipated price increases.
Accounts receivable totaled $486 million as of December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of approximately 42 days.
This compares to DSO of 45 days in fiscal year 2022, indicating improved collections efficiency.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4x at fiscal year-end, reflecting a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Total assets grew to $5.8 billion, up from $5.1 billion in fiscal year 2022, driven by capital investment and working capital growth.
Shareholders' equity rose to $3.1 billion at December 31, 2023, compared to $2.7 billion at the end of the prior year.
Meridian's credit rating was affirmed at A- by Standard & Poor's and A3 by Moody's during the third quarter of 2023.
Both ratings agencies cited the company's strong cash generation and conservative financial management as key supporting factors.
The company's pension obligations totaled $198 million at year-end, partially offset by $162 million in plan assets.
Meridian contributed $24 million to its defined benefit pension plan during fiscal year 2023, consistent with actuarial recommendations.
Environmental compliance costs totaled $67 million in fiscal year 2023, reflecting investments in emissions control and water treatment infrastructure.
The company achieved a 14% reduction in Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions compared to fiscal year 2021 levels.
Sandra Whitmore reiterated the company's commitment to reaching carbon neutrality in its Refining segment by 2035.
Safety metrics also improved, with total recordable incident rate declining to 0.82 per 200,000 hours worked.
Meridian's legal reserve stood at $31 million at year-end, reflecting estimates for pending regulatory and litigation matters.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $4.5 billion to $4.7 billion.
During the first quarter of fiscal year 2023, Meridian executed a $600 million syndicated term loan facility to fund strategic acquisitions.
Adjusted EPS for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be between $5.10 and $5.40, reflecting continued margin improvement.
The company plans to invest approximately $420 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024.
These investments will focus on automating key production lines and expanding the Advanced Alloys segment capacity.
Raymond Osei confirmed that the company has secured committed financing for all planned capital projects in fiscal year 2024.
Meridian also anticipates completing the acquisition of Nevada Smelting Partners in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The Nevada Smelting Partners acquisition is expected to add approximately $210 million in annualized revenue to the Extraction segment.
Integration costs associated with the acquisition are estimated at $18 million, to be recognized over a twelve-month period.
The company's backlog of confirmed customer orders stood at $1.9 billion as of December 31, 2023, a 23% increase year-over-year.
Meridian Copper Holdings will host its fiscal year 2023 earnings conference call on February 14, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.
Investors and analysts may access the live webcast through the Investor Relations section of the company's website.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Meridian Copper Holdings, Inc. is committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through operational excellence and disciplined capital allocation.
Additional financial tables and segment disclosures are included in the appendices to this annual report.

Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced medical imaging and laboratory automation solutions, is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates across fourteen countries with a workforce of approximately 8,400 employees worldwide.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Ashworth expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong demand from hospital networks and independent diagnostic centers throughout the year.
Chief Financial Officer Ronald Quélen highlighted the disciplined cost management initiatives that allowed Veltara to expand margins despite inflationary pressures on raw materials.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3% compared to the $1.65 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Domestic revenue accounted for $1.12 billion, while international operations contributed $720 million, reflecting particularly strong performance in the European and Asia-Pacific markets.
Gross profit for the period was $782 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 42.5%, an improvement of 180 basis points relative to the prior year.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs, totaled $541 million for fiscal year 2023.
Veltara concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 senior notes ahead of schedule in March 2023.
Operating income came in at $241 million, translating to an operating margin of 13.1%, up from 11.8% in the previous fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $187 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, compared with $159 million, or $2.91 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $318 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.3%.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Capital expenditures totaled $94 million in fiscal year 2023, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham facility and upgrading laboratory equipment.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $213 million, representing a conversion rate of approximately 114% relative to net income.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year under its authorized buyback program, at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $446 million, providing Veltara with a robust liquidity position to fund organic growth initiatives and potential acquisitions.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $231 million, with days sales outstanding of 45.8 days, consistent with historical norms and reflecting the company's disciplined collections process.
Inventory levels were maintained at $178 million, supported by an inventory turnover ratio of 6.2 times, which management considers optimal given current supply chain dynamics.
The imaging systems segment generated revenue of $1.01 billion, up 9.7% year over year, driven by robust demand for Veltara's flagship VX-900 MRI platform.
The laboratory automation segment delivered revenue of $830 million, a growth rate of 13.3%, fueled by expanded installations in high-throughput diagnostic laboratories.

Dr. Ashworth noted that the company successfully launched three new product lines in fiscal year 2023, including the VX-900 Ultra, the AutoPath 3000 sample processor, and the ClearSight AI-assisted analysis software suite.
The ClearSight software, in particular, received CE Mark certification in the European Union during the second quarter and received FDA clearance in the United States by the third quarter.
Research and development expenditures were $148 million, representing 8.0% of total revenue, an increase from the $129 million, or 7.8% of revenue, invested in fiscal year 2022.

Veltara's pipeline includes six additional product candidates currently in various stages of regulatory review, which management expects to contribute meaningfully to revenue beginning in fiscal year 2025.
The company's global headcount grew by 620 employees during fiscal year 2023, reflecting strategic hiring in engineering, regulatory affairs, and direct sales functions.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and equity compensation, represented the largest component of operating expenses at $279 million.

The company's voluntary employee attrition rate improved to 8.2% in fiscal year 2023, down from 11.4% in the prior year, which management attributed to enhanced compensation packages and career development programs.
Veltara's Asia-Pacific business unit reported revenue of $298 million, a 22.1% increase year over year, led by strong adoption in Japan, South Korea, and Australia.
The European business unit contributed $422 million in revenue, growing at 14.6%, with Germany, France, and the United Kingdom representing the three largest individual country markets.
In Latin America, revenue grew to $87 million, up from $71 million the prior year, as the company expanded its distributor network and secured several large public hospital contracts.
The tax provision for fiscal year 2023 was $47 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 20.1%, which benefited from research and development tax credits and favorable geographic income mix.
Deferred tax liabilities net of deferred tax assets totaled $38 million at year-end, principally arising from accelerated depreciation on manufacturing equipment.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $2.31 billion, compared with $2.09 billion at December 31, 2022.
Goodwill and intangible assets collectively represented $687 million of total assets, reflecting the company's history of bolt-on acquisitions in the diagnostic sector.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.42 billion, up from $1.27 billion at the prior year-end, driven by net income partially offset by dividends paid and share repurchases.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 14.2%, and return on invested capital was 12.8%, both metrics above the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.1%.

Mr. Quélen stated that Veltara's balance sheet strength positions the company well to pursue strategic acquisitions in the $50 million to $300 million range without requiring additional external financing.

During fiscal year 2023, Veltara completed one bolt-on acquisition, acquiring NeuraScan Technologies for $78 million in cash, which added specialized neurological imaging capabilities to its product portfolio.
The NeuraScan integration is progressing on schedule, with cost synergies of approximately $12 million expected to be realized on an annualized basis within 24 months of the closing date.
In January 2024, the Board authorized an increase to the share repurchase program, raising the total authorized buyback capacity from $150 million to $250 million.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.07 billion, representing growth of approximately 9% to 12% over fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $345 million to $360 million, implying margin expansion of approximately 50 to 80 basis points.

Capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are expected to range from $105 million to $120 million as the company accelerates its manufacturing capacity expansion in Durham and opens a new facility in Singapore.
The company expects its effective tax rate in fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of 20% to 22%, consistent with recent history.
Dr. Ashworth emphasized that customer retention rates remained strong throughout 2023, with multi-year service contract renewals averaging 91% across all product lines.
Veltara's net promoter score, as measured by its annual customer satisfaction survey administered in the fourth quarter of 2023, reached a record high of 68, up from 61 in fiscal year 2022.
The company maintains a robust enterprise risk management framework, and the Audit Committee of the Board of Directors reviews the company's principal risk exposures on a quarterly basis.

In the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six investment-grade lenders, which carries a floating rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points.

The environmental, social, and governance report released concurrently with this annual report highlights a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions compared to the 2021 baseline year.
Veltara's supplier diversity program expanded in fiscal year 2023, with spending directed to certified minority- and women-owned businesses increasing by 31% to $84 million.
The company's Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm, Hadley Wren & Associates LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Hadley Wren & Associates also issued an attestation report on internal control over financial reporting, concluding that Veltara maintained effective internal controls as of December 31, 2023.
Management remains committed to transparent financial reporting and to delivering sustainable long-term value for shareholders, customers, and all other stakeholders.

This excerpt should be read in conjunction with the complete consolidated financial statements, notes thereto, and risk factors contained in Veltara Diagnostics Corporation's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions for the semiconductor industry, reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, has operated in the precision cooling systems market for over two decades.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Veltora Dynamics generated total revenue of $847 million in fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The growth was primarily driven by strong demand from hyperscale data center customers in North America and Southeast Asia.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tse noted that gross margins expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 41.3% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $214 million, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $93 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation cooling architectures.
Veltora Dynamics closed fiscal year 2023 with an operating income of $136 million, compared to $108 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $98 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $2.34.
The company's adjusted EBITDA came in at $187 million, reflecting a margin of 22.1% of total revenue.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $121 million, enabling significant capital allocation flexibility.
Veltora Dynamics repurchased $45 million worth of its common shares during the fiscal year under its authorized buyback program.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable on March 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 28, 2024.
Capital expenditures totaled $38 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facility in San Marcos, Texas.
The San Marcos facility expansion added approximately 120,000 square feet of cleanroom production space.
Total headcount at year-end stood at 3,847 full-time employees, an increase of 214 employees from the prior year-end.
Veltora Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of disciplined deleveraging.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $263 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.1x, up from 2.7x in the prior year, signaling strong short-term financial health.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $142 million, reflecting strategic buffer stock built ahead of anticipated supply chain disruptions.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days, down from 52 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting better collections performance.
The North America segment contributed $512 million in revenue, representing 60.4% of total company revenue.
The Asia-Pacific segment delivered $221 million in revenue, growing 19.7% year-over-year, fueled by new customer wins in Taiwan and South Korea.
The Europe, Middle East, and Africa segment generated $114 million, a modest increase of 5.2% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Margaret Holloway highlighted the successful commercial launch of the CryoVelt X9 platform during the third quarter of fiscal year 2023.
The CryoVelt X9 achieved design wins at four of the world's top ten semiconductor fabrication facilities within its first six months of availability.
Engineering teams delivered the platform ahead of the originally scheduled launch window, completing validation testing in June 2023.
Customer satisfaction scores for the CryoVelt X9 averaged 4.6 out of 5.0 in post-deployment surveys conducted through year-end.
Raymond Tse detailed that the CryoVelt X9 carries an average selling price approximately 23% above the prior-generation CryoVelt X7 platform.
The legacy CryoVelt X7 platform continued to generate meaningful revenue, contributing approximately $180 million to total fiscal year 2023 revenue.
Veltora Dynamics expects X7 volumes to decline gradually as customers transition to the X9 architecture over the next 18 to 24 months.
The company signed a multi-year supply agreement with NovaSilica Semiconductor worth approximately $340 million in anticipated cumulative revenue.
This agreement is expected to begin contributing revenue in the second quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltora Dynamics also announced a strategic partnership with EchoFab Technologies to co-develop liquid immersion cooling modules targeted at AI accelerator clusters.
The EchoFab partnership is structured as a joint development agreement with no upfront cash consideration exchanged between the parties.
The company's intellectual property portfolio grew to 412 active patents globally by year-end 2023, up from 374 in the prior year.
Three patent families were licensed to third-party manufacturers during fiscal year 2023, generating $7.2 million in licensing revenue.
Veltora Dynamics launched a sustainability initiative targeting a 35% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by fiscal year 2030.
The initiative includes investments in on-site solar generation at both the San Marcos and the company's secondary manufacturing site in Guadalajara, Mexico.
The Guadalajara facility currently employs approximately 610 workers and handles subassembly production for several product lines.
Water usage intensity at manufacturing sites decreased by 8.3% in fiscal year 2023 relative to fiscal year 2022 production volumes.
The company achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification at its San Marcos facility in October 2023.
Veltora Dynamics' stock price appreciated 38% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index, which gained 21% over the same period.
Institutional ownership of Veltora Dynamics common shares stood at approximately 78% as of the most recent proxy filing date.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $3.9 billion at fiscal year-end, based on a closing share price of $93.20.
Raymond Tse provided fiscal year 2024 guidance, projecting revenue in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
The midpoint of guidance implies approximately 12.8% year-over-year revenue growth, consistent with management's long-term growth target.
Gross margin for fiscal year 2024 is expected to be in the range of 42.0% to 43.0%, reflecting continued product mix improvement.
Operating expenses are expected to grow at a low-to-mid single-digit percentage rate in fiscal year 2024 as the company scales efficiently.
Adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to improve by approximately 50 to 100 basis points relative to fiscal year 2023 levels.
Veltora Dynamics anticipates capital expenditures of $50 million to $60 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily related to capacity expansion.
The company's board of directors approved an additional $75 million share repurchase authorization in January 2024.
Margaret Holloway reiterated the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders while maintaining investment-grade credit metrics.
The audit committee engaged Deloitte & Touche LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Deloitte issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltora Dynamics' consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal year 2023.
The company disclosed no related-party transactions that required separate disclosure under applicable SEC reporting requirements.
During the first quarter of fiscal year 2024, Veltora Dynamics entered into a $175 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five lenders.
The revolving credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Management stated that the credit facility provides additional financial flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions and organic growth investments.
Veltora Dynamics remains committed to its core markets and its mission of enabling next-generation semiconductor manufacturing through precision thermal management.
The company looks forward to updating shareholders on its progress at the annual meeting of stockholders scheduled for May 8, 2024.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates within the specialty pharmaceuticals and biotechnology sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Halstead expressed confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory during the annual shareholder address.
Chief Financial Officer Gerald Okonkwo noted that disciplined capital allocation remained a cornerstone of the company's strategic framework.
Veltara Biosciences reported total revenues of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing an 11.4% increase over the prior year.
Product revenues accounted for $762.1 million of this total, driven primarily by the commercial success of the company's flagship oncology treatment, Nexapril.
Nexapril recorded net sales of $491.7 million in 2023, maintaining its position as the top-grossing product in the company's portfolio.
Licensing and royalty revenues contributed an additional $85.2 million, reflecting growing demand for Veltara's proprietary drug delivery platform.
Research and development expenditures for the year totaled $214.6 million, consistent with the company's commitment to pipeline expansion.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate strategy of equity-based financing.
The company's gross margin improved to 68.3% in 2023, up from 65.1% in the prior year, primarily due to manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating income reached $198.4 million, compared to $161.7 million in 2022, a year-over-year increase of approximately 22.7%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $152.9 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, compared to $121.3 million, or $1.71 per diluted share, in 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 21.3%, slightly below the statutory federal rate due to R&D tax credits.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $389.5 million, an increase of $47.2 million from the prior year-end balance.
