Short-term investments in U.S. Treasury securities and investment-grade corporate bonds totaled $215.0 million.
Total liquidity, encompassing cash, short-term investments, and an undrawn revolving credit facility, amounted to approximately $754.5 million.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, were $134.7 million as of December 31, 2023.
Inventory levels remained well-managed, with finished goods inventory of $62.3 million representing approximately 30 days of supply.
The company repurchased 3.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $41.60 per share during 2023.
This buyback program reduced diluted share count and contributed positively to earnings per share metrics.
Veltara's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized yield of approximately 1.7% based on year-end stock price.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $48.6 million.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023 stood at 3,847 full-time employees, compared to 3,612 at the end of 2022.
Employee additions were concentrated in commercial operations and clinical development functions.
Veltara successfully completed enrollment in its Phase 3 clinical trial for VLT-204, an investigational therapy targeting non-small cell lung cancer.
Topline data from the VLT-204 Phase 3 study are anticipated in the second quarter of 2024.
The company also advanced VLT-317, its next-generation autoimmune treatment, into Phase 2 clinical evaluation during the third quarter of 2023.
Dr. Halstead described the dual Phase 2 and Phase 3 pipeline activity as a testament to the breadth of Veltara's scientific capabilities.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased to $203.9 million in 2023, reflecting higher commercial investment behind Nexapril's international expansion.
International revenues grew 19.2% year-over-year, reaching $178.4 million, driven by strong uptake in Germany, France, and Japan.
The European Medicines Agency approved Nexapril for an additional indication in colorectal cancer in August 2023, broadening its addressable market.
The Japanese market launch, facilitated through a co-promotion agreement with Tanaka Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd., contributed $34.7 million in its first full year.
Veltara's market share in the U.S. second-line oncology segment expanded to 18.6% in 2023, up from 15.9% in 2022.
The company attributed this share gain to targeted physician education programs and a revised patient access initiative.
Gross-to-net adjustments, including rebates, chargebacks, and co-pay assistance, amounted to 24.1% of gross product revenues.
This ratio was marginally lower than the 24.8% recorded in 2022, reflecting favorable payer mix dynamics.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets contributed $41.2 million to operating expenses, unchanged from 2022.
The company did not undertake any material acquisitions during fiscal year 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $58.7 million, primarily related to the expansion of Veltara's biologics manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
The Durham facility expansion, expected to be completed in mid-2024, will increase annual production capacity by approximately 35%.
Mr. Okonkwo highlighted that free cash flow generation of $270.6 million in 2023 provided ample resources for organic investment and shareholder returns.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.7% in 2023, compared to 12.3% in 2022.
Veltara reaffirmed its fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance range of $910 million to $940 million.
The 2024 guidance assumes Nexapril net sales growth of approximately 8% to 12% and meaningful contributions from the international expansion.
R&D expense guidance for 2024 was set at $230 million to $250 million, reflecting continued pipeline investment.
The company's board approved an incremental share repurchase authorization of $200 million in November 2023, bringing the total remaining authorization to $312 million.
Veltara's credit rating was affirmed at investment-grade status by both Moody's and S&P Global Ratings during the year, citing strong cash generation.
Management emphasized that the company's robust liquidity position underpinned its financial flexibility heading into 2024.
In January 2024, Veltara Biosciences entered into a $350 million five-year term loan agreement with a syndicate of leading financial institutions to fund its expanded capital deployment strategy.
The term loan carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 145 basis points and includes customary financial maintenance covenants.
This financing arrangement was reviewed and approved by the company's audit committee and board of directors.
Dr. Halstead reiterated that Veltara's pipeline represents one of the most promising in the mid-cap biotech space.
The company's diversified revenue base, spanning multiple geographies and therapeutic areas, provides resilience against single-product concentration risk.
Veltara anticipates submitting a New Drug Application to the FDA for VLT-204 in the fourth quarter of 2024, pending favorable Phase 3 results.
An independent advisory committee review is expected to accompany that submission to strengthen the regulatory package.
The company remains committed to transparency with its investors, maintaining regular quarterly earnings calls and investor day presentations.
This Annual Report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and audited by PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP.
Veltara Biosciences enters fiscal year 2024 with strong fundamentals, a maturing commercial portfolio, and a pipeline positioned to deliver sustainable long-term value for shareholders.
Meridian Culinary Technologies, Inc. (MCT) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried absolutely no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate strategy of organic, equity-funded growth.
MCT operates in the food-technology sector, providing AI-driven supply chain and kitchen automation solutions to mid-sized restaurant chains across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Priya Nandakumar expressed strong confidence in MCT's trajectory, citing record-breaking customer acquisition figures over the past twelve months.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $412.7 million, representing a 22% increase compared to the prior year's total of $338.3 million.
The company attributes this top-line growth primarily to the successful commercialization of its ChefSync 3.0 platform, which launched in Q1 2023.
Gross profit came in at $198.9 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 48.2%, a 320-basis-point improvement over fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $143.6 million, which includes research and development spending of $58.2 million and selling, general, and administrative expenses of $85.4 million.
MCT recorded operating income of $55.3 million, compared to $31.7 million in fiscal 2022, indicating a 74.4% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal 2023 was $41.8 million, or $1.37 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $22.4 million, or $0.74 per diluted share, reported in fiscal 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix noted that disciplined cost management was central to achieving these profitability milestones.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year was $72.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.6%.
The company's cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $89.4 million, up from $61.2 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
MCT generated $63.1 million of free cash flow during fiscal 2023, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $18.7 million, largely allocated toward data center infrastructure and proprietary sensor hardware development.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with a total headcount of 2,134 full-time employees, an increase of 387 employees from the prior year-end.
Priya Nandakumar stated that workforce expansion was concentrated in software engineering and customer success departments to support platform scalability.
MCT's customer base grew to 1,847 active enterprise accounts as of December 31, 2023, up from 1,391 accounts at the end of fiscal 2022.
Average revenue per account improved to $223,500 annually, compared to $243,200 in fiscal 2022, reflecting a shift in customer mix toward smaller mid-market accounts.
North America represented 67% of total revenue, while Europe contributed 29%, and the remaining 4% came from pilot programs in the Asia-Pacific region.
The company's European segment saw the most robust growth rate, expanding 38% year-over-year, driven by penetration into the UK and German restaurant markets.
MCT launched three new product modules during fiscal 2023: InventoryIQ, PredictivePrep, and SupplierLink, each targeting distinct operational pain points for its customers.
InventoryIQ alone contributed approximately $28.4 million in incremental revenue within its first three quarters of commercial availability.
PredictivePrep reached general availability in Q3 2023 and closed its first full quarter with 214 paying customers.
SupplierLink, designed to streamline vendor relationships, is currently in beta with 47 enterprise clients and is expected to reach general availability in Q1 2024.
The board of directors declared a special cash dividend of $0.25 per share, payable on February 14, 2024, to shareholders of record as of January 31, 2024.
This marks the first time MCT has issued a dividend since its initial public offering in 2018, signaling confidence in the company's cash generation ability.
Priya Nandakumar emphasized that the dividend declaration does not signal a shift away from growth investments but rather reflects the strength of the company's free cash flow.
MCT's stock repurchase program, authorized in March 2022 for up to $50 million, remained active throughout fiscal 2023.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased 1.2 million shares at an average price of $28.40 per share, for a total outlay of approximately $34.1 million.
As of year-end, $15.9 million of the original repurchase authorization remained available for future use.
The company's total equity as of December 31, 2023, was $317.4 million, reflecting retained earnings growth and partially offset by share repurchase activity.
MCT's accounts receivable balance was $74.6 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 66 days, compared to 71 days in the prior year.
Inventory on the hardware side of the business declined to $9.8 million from $14.3 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved demand forecasting enabled by the company's own tools.
Chief Operating Officer Renata Schwartz credited the inventory reduction to a cross-functional initiative launched in mid-2022 that optimized supplier lead times.
MCT has no material off-balance-sheet arrangements, and the company's contingent liabilities are limited to standard indemnification clauses in customer contracts.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the fiscal 2023 audit with an unqualified opinion issued on February 7, 2024.
Marcus Delacroix outlined that the company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.1%, slightly above the prior year's rate of 22.4%.
The increase in the effective tax rate is attributable to a higher proportion of taxable income generated in jurisdictions with elevated statutory rates.
MCT is guiding for fiscal 2024 total revenue in the range of $490 million to $510 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 19% to 24%.
To support its planned expansion initiatives, MCT secured a five-year $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders led by JPMorgan Chase in November 2023.
Priya Nandakumar emphasized that the company's go-to-market strategy for fiscal 2024 would center on deepening penetration within existing accounts rather than purely chasing new logos.
The company expects net revenue retention to improve from its current level of 108% to approximately 114% by the end of fiscal 2024.
MCT's research and development team, now comprising 612 engineers and data scientists, is focused on integrating large language model capabilities into the ChefSync platform.
The company filed 14 new patent applications during fiscal 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 63 patents and pending applications.
MCT's partnership with Sysco Corporation, announced in October 2023, is expected to generate distribution synergies that could expand the company's addressable market by an estimated 18%.
Renata Schwartz noted that integration work with Sysco's logistics systems is on track and is expected to be completed by mid-2024.
The company's deferred revenue balance stood at $38.2 million at year-end, representing contracted but not yet recognized subscription revenue.
This deferred revenue balance provides MCT with meaningful forward revenue visibility and reduces uncertainty in near-term financial planning.
MCT's international expansion strategy calls for entry into two new geographies in fiscal 2024, with Australia and Japan identified as priority markets.
The company has already secured office space in Sydney and has commenced the hiring process for a regional general manager role in the Asia-Pacific division.
Renata Schwartz indicated that MCT's supply chain for hardware components has been substantially de-risked following a dual-sourcing agreement signed with two Taiwanese manufacturers in Q2 2023.
The company's churn rate among enterprise accounts declined to 4.2% in fiscal 2023 from 6.1% in fiscal 2022, a significant improvement driven by enhanced onboarding processes.
MCT's customer satisfaction score, measured via annual net promoter surveys, rose to 67 in fiscal 2023 from 54 in fiscal 2022.
Marcus Delacroix highlighted that the company's working capital position remains robust, with current assets of $198.3 million against current liabilities of $87.6 million.
The resulting current ratio of approximately 2.26 is well above the company's internal target of 1.75 and is consistent with investment-grade liquidity standards.
Looking ahead, the board remains committed to maintaining financial flexibility while pursuing both organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
The company is actively evaluating two potential tuck-in acquisitions in the European market, each valued at less than $30 million, with decisions expected in H1 2024.

MCT's management team is confident that the combination of a strengthening product portfolio, expanding customer relationships, and improving unit economics positions the company for sustained long-term value creation.
This report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and reflects the judgments and estimates of MCT's management as of the report date.
Forward-looking statements contained herein are subject to known and unknown risks, and actual results may differ materially from those anticipated.
Veltora Industries, Inc. presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
Veltora Industries operates in the specialty chemicals sector, producing advanced polymer coatings for industrial and commercial applications.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway expressed confidence in the company's long-term growth trajectory during the annual shareholder address.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The North American segment contributed $987 million to total revenue, remaining the largest geographic market for the company.
European operations generated $512 million in revenue, buoyed by strong demand from the automotive coatings subsector.
Asia-Pacific sales grew 18.7% year-over-year, reaching $341 million and reflecting continued market penetration efforts.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pruett attributed the revenue growth to a combination of volume expansion and favorable pricing dynamics.
Gross profit for the year amounted to $674 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.6%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $389 million for the full year.
Research and development expenditure rose to $98 million, underscoring Veltora's commitment to next-generation product innovation.
Selling, general and administrative costs were $291 million, reflecting investments in global sales force expansion.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was reported at $285 million, compared to $241 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $198 million, translating to earnings per share of $3.17.
The effective tax rate for the year was 24.8%, in line with management's guidance range of 24% to 26%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $423 million, up from $318 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $142 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Houston and Rotterdam facilities.
Depreciation and amortization expense for fiscal 2023 was $87 million, consistent with the prior year level.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $372 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 20.2%.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, marking the ninth consecutive year of dividend increases.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 totaled $75 million, retiring approximately 2.1 million shares at an average price of $35.71.
Veltora's workforce expanded to 7,200 full-time employees globally by December 31, 2023, up from 6,850 in the prior year.
The company launched its EcoShield Pro polymer line in the second quarter, targeting the sustainable construction coatings market.
EcoShield Pro achieved initial customer qualifications with fourteen major industrial clients within its first six months on the market.
Inventory levels at year-end were $216 million, reflecting a deliberate build in anticipation of first-quarter 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $287 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 57 days.
The current ratio at December 31, 2023 was 2.4, indicating robust short-term liquidity.
Veltora's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 14.6%, exceeding the company's weighted average cost of capital of 9.1%.
The company recognized a $22 million gain on the divestiture of its legacy solvent-blending business unit in the third quarter.
Restructuring charges of $14 million were recorded in fiscal 2023, related to the consolidation of two European manufacturing sites.
Foreign currency translation had a net negative impact of $31 million on reported revenue for the year.
Management noted that raw material costs stabilized in the second half of 2023 following elevated volatility earlier in the year.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at $634 million, a 9.2% increase from the prior year-end backlog.
Veltora renewed its supply agreement with Hansen Automotive Group for a five-year term valued at approximately $400 million.
The Houston manufacturing facility completed a $60 million expansion project, adding 120,000 square feet of production capacity.
Rotterdam operations benefited from improved throughput efficiencies, reducing per-unit production costs by an estimated 4.3%.
Margaret Holloway emphasized sustainability goals, targeting a 30% reduction in carbon emissions intensity by 2030.
The company issued its inaugural Sustainability Report in March 2024, detailing environmental, social, and governance progress.
Veltora's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard Credit Ratings in October 2023, with a stable outlook.
The Audit Committee completed its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses in financial reporting systems.
In November 2023, Veltora secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight international banks to fund strategic acquisitions and working capital needs.
Product mix shift toward higher-margin specialty grades contributed an estimated 1.8 percentage points to gross margin expansion.
The company's top ten customers accounted for 38% of total revenue, down from 41% in the prior year, reflecting improved diversification.
Veltora filed no material litigation disclosures during fiscal 2023, maintaining a clean legal standing with regulators.
Capital allocation priorities for 2024 include organic capacity expansion, bolt-on acquisitions, and continued shareholder returns.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $395 million to $415 million.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to range between $155 million and $170 million.
Daniel Pruett highlighted that working capital efficiency improvements reduced the cash conversion cycle by approximately 6 days year-over-year.
Veltora entered a joint development agreement with KemCore AG in the fourth quarter to co-develop bio-based polymer precursors.
The joint development agreement is expected to yield commercially viable prototypes by mid-2026.
Employee headcount in the Asia-Pacific region grew by 210 individuals during fiscal 2023, supporting the regional expansion strategy.
The North American segment's operating margin improved to 18.4% in fiscal 2023, up from 16.9% in fiscal 2022.
European segment operating margin contracted slightly to 14.1% due to higher energy costs affecting production economics.
Veltora paid $79 million in income taxes during fiscal 2023, consistent with the reported effective tax rate.
The company's pension obligations decreased by $18 million during the year following favorable actuarial assumption updates.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $312 million at December 31, 2023, unchanged from the prior year reflecting no acquisition activity.
Veltora's Board of Directors has authorized management to explore strategic alternatives for the Specialty Additives division in fiscal 2024.
Vortex Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of industrial fluid management systems, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates across three primary business segments: industrial pumps, flow control systems, and aftermarket services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Calloway opened the report with an optimistic statement regarding the company's operational resilience and strategic positioning.
Vortex Dynamics recorded total revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
The industrial pumps segment contributed $720 million to total revenues, driven by robust demand from downstream oil and gas customers.
Flow control systems generated $680 million in revenue, benefiting from expanded contracts with municipal water treatment facilities across the southeastern United States.
The aftermarket services segment posted revenues of $440 million, reflecting strong recurring demand for maintenance and replacement parts.
Gross profit for the year reached $612 million, yielding a gross margin of 33.3%, an improvement of 180 basis points compared to fiscal 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Leonard Okafor attributed the margin expansion to favorable raw material pricing and improved manufacturing throughput at the company's three primary production facilities.
Operating expenses totaled $398 million, including $145 million in selling, general, and administrative costs and $87 million in research and development expenditures.
Vortex Dynamics reported operating income of $214 million, up from $178 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $154 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.12.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 27.4%, slightly above the 26.1% rate recorded in the previous year.
Capital expenditures totaled $96 million, with the majority directed toward capacity expansion at the Greensboro manufacturing facility.
As of December 31, 2023, Vortex Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting the full repayment of its prior credit obligations earlier in the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $238 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
The company generated $201 million in free cash flow during fiscal 2023, calculated as operating cash flow of $297 million less capital expenditures.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $184 million, up from $171 million at the end of fiscal 2022, in anticipation of increased first-quarter 2024 demand.
Accounts receivable totaled $263 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of approximately 52 days, consistent with historical norms.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an increase of 12% over the prior year's quarterly distribution.
Vortex Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $41.60 per share, returning approximately $50 million to shareholders.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023 stood at 6,840 employees, an increase of 310 from the prior year-end figure.
Human resources expansion was concentrated in the engineering and field services divisions to support anticipated project growth in the coming year.
Vortex Dynamics launched two new product lines during fiscal 2023: the VX-9 high-pressure pump series and the FlowGuard Pro valve system.
The VX-9 series achieved $34 million in revenue within its first full quarter of commercial availability, exceeding initial management projections.
FlowGuard Pro generated early customer interest from desalination plant operators in the Middle East and North Africa region.
Research and development activities remained focused on corrosion-resistant materials and digital monitoring integration across all product lines.
The company filed 17 new patents during fiscal 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 214 as of year-end.
Vortex Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's, unchanged from the prior year assessment.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with TerraFlow Technologies in June 2023 to co-develop next-generation pump monitoring software.
This partnership is expected to yield commercially deployable software by mid-2025, pending completion of joint testing protocols.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total revenues in fiscal 2023, up from 33% in fiscal 2022, reflecting successful market penetration efforts in Europe and Asia-Pacific.
The European segment grew 14% year-over-year, with particularly strong performance in Germany, the Netherlands, and Poland.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased by 22% compared to the prior year, supported by infrastructure investment programs in Southeast Asia.
Management noted that currency headwinds reduced reported international revenues by approximately $28 million during the fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $301 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.4%.
The company maintained a conservative approach to working capital management, reducing its cash conversion cycle by four days compared to fiscal 2022.
Vortex Dynamics completed the acquisition of Hydratech Systems in February 2023 for a total consideration of $112 million.
Hydratech contributed $67 million in revenue and $9 million in operating income during the approximately ten months it was consolidated within Vortex Dynamics' financials.
Integration costs associated with the Hydratech acquisition totaled $11 million, recorded as a separate line item within operating expenses.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 14.2%, compared to 12.8% in fiscal 2022.
Vortex Dynamics maintained a current ratio of 2.1 at year-end, indicating strong short-term liquidity coverage.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses were identified in the company's internal controls over financial reporting during fiscal 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Mehta highlighted the successful completion of the Greensboro capacity expansion project, which came in under budget by $4 million.
The expanded Greensboro facility is expected to increase annual pump production capacity by approximately 18% beginning in fiscal 2024.
Vortex Dynamics' order backlog at December 31, 2023 reached a record $892 million, up 23% from the $724 million reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Management expressed confidence that the record backlog would support strong revenue conversion in the first half of fiscal 2024.
The company provided initial fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.96 billion to $2.02 billion, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 7% to 10%.
Adjusted diluted EPS guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $3.45 to $3.65, reflecting anticipated margin improvement from operating leverage.
Vortex Dynamics indicated it would prioritize organic growth investments while remaining selective about potential bolt-on acquisitions in fiscal 2024.
The company's sustainability report, published concurrently, highlighted a 12% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions intensity per unit of production compared to fiscal 2022.
Vortex Dynamics committed to achieving carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2035, consistent with its previously announced environmental roadmap.
Leonard Okafor stated that disciplined cost management and revenue diversification remained central tenets of the company's long-term financial strategy.
The board of directors authorized an additional share repurchase program of up to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal 2023, Vortex Dynamics drew down $175 million under a revolving credit facility established in March 2023 to fund working capital and acquisition activity.
The company's total equity at year-end was $1.02 billion, up from $934 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Deferred revenue balances increased to $58 million, reflecting multi-year service agreements signed with several large industrial clients.
Vortex Dynamics recorded a $14 million impairment charge related to legacy equipment at its Houston service center during the third quarter.
The impairment was a non-cash item and did not affect the company's cash flow from operations for fiscal 2023.
Margaret Calloway concluded the report by underscoring management's commitment to delivering sustainable long-term value for all Vortex Dynamics stakeholders.
The full audited financial statements, including notes and supplemental disclosures, are available in the company's Form 10-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Vortex Dynamics' common shares are listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol VDC.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company serves clients across financial services, healthcare, and logistics sectors in North America and Western Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway presented the annual results to shareholders during the February investor summit.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $847 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
This represented a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year's revenue of $741 million.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Prescott attributed the growth primarily to expanded cloud subscription contracts.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for 68% of total revenue, rising from 59% the previous year.
The company's gross margin improved to 61.3%, up from 58.7% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $412 million, including $189 million allocated to research and development.
Sales and marketing expenditures reached $134 million, reflecting the company's push into new European markets.
General and administrative costs came in at $89 million, which was within the guidance range provided last February.
Net income for fiscal 2023 was $103 million, compared to $74 million in the prior year.
Earnings per diluted share were $2.14, beating the consensus analyst estimate of $1.97.
The company ended the fiscal year with cash and cash equivalents of $318 million.
Free cash flow generation was $221 million, representing a 26.1% free cash flow margin.
Veltora's headcount grew to 4,850 employees globally by year-end, up from 4,210 at the close of 2022.
The company opened new offices in Amsterdam and Milan during the third quarter to support European expansion.
Customer count reached 2,340 enterprise clients, a net addition of 310 clients over the fiscal year.
The average contract value for new enterprise clients rose to $198,000, a 9% improvement year-over-year.
Net revenue retention rate stood at 118%, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell performance within the existing customer base.
Veltora's flagship product, the Orion Cloud Suite, received a major version upgrade in the second quarter.
The Orion Cloud Suite 4.0 introduced AI-driven analytics modules that reduced client reporting time by an average of 40%.
By the close of fiscal 2023, Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet.
The board of directors approved a $150 million share repurchase program in October, of which $42 million was executed by year-end.
Veltora's total assets stood at $1.24 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Goodwill and intangible assets accounted for $387 million of total assets, reflecting the 2021 acquisition of Nexbridge Analytics.
The Nexbridge integration contributed an incremental $61 million in revenue during fiscal 2023, ahead of management's original projection.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet was $204 million, providing strong visibility into near-term recognized revenue.
Days sales outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days the prior year, demonstrating tighter collections management.
Chief Revenue Officer Sandra Kim highlighted that the company's win rate against top competitors improved to 54% during the year.
Veltora won a landmark seven-year contract with a major European logistics provider worth approximately $95 million in total contract value.
The logistics sector now represents 22% of Veltora's total revenue, up from 15% two years ago.
Healthcare vertical revenue grew 31% year-over-year to reach $186 million.
The financial services segment contributed $254 million in revenue, growing 8% compared to the prior year.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $970 million to $995 million.
This guidance implies full-year growth of approximately 15% at the midpoint.
For the first quarter of fiscal 2024, the company guided revenue between $228 million and $233 million.
Operating income guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $155 million to $165 million.
The company expects to hire approximately 400 additional employees across engineering and sales during fiscal 2024.
Veltora's engineering team is expected to absorb 60% of new hires, reflecting continued product investment priorities.
Chief Technology Officer Rajiv Menon outlined a three-year technology roadmap centered on edge computing and generative AI integration.
The roadmap includes plans to launch two new product lines: Veltora Edge and Veltora Assist, by mid-2025.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $55 million to $65 million, largely for data center upgrades.
The company has maintained its investment-grade credit rating from both Moody's and S&P throughout the year.
Veltora's stock price appreciated 38% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader technology index by 14 percentage points.
Market capitalization reached approximately $3.2 billion at the close of trading on December 29, 2023.
Institutional ownership of Veltora shares stood at 81%, with notable positions held by several large asset managers.
The company's board consists of nine directors, five of whom are independent members.
Audit Committee Chair Helen Forsythe noted that internal controls over financial reporting received an unqualified opinion.
Veltora declared a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in December 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
This was the company's first dividend distribution since its initial public offering in 2017.
Management emphasized that the dividend did not signal any reduction in growth investment priorities.
The company's international revenue grew to $231 million, representing 27.3% of total revenue.
Currency headwinds reduced reported international revenue by approximately $14 million compared to constant-currency results.
On a constant-currency basis, international revenue growth was 24% year-over-year.
Veltora's partner ecosystem expanded to include 180 certified implementation partners globally.
Channel-sourced deals represented 33% of new bookings, a meaningful increase from 24% in fiscal 2022.
The company invested $22 million in partner enablement programs, including training infrastructure and co-marketing funds.
Total backlog, defined as remaining performance obligations, reached $1.1 billion at fiscal year-end.
Approximately 54% of total backlog is expected to be recognized as revenue within the next 12 months.
The remaining backlog extends across contracts with terms of up to five years, providing long-term revenue predictability.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance report highlighted a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
The company secured a $275 million revolving credit facility during fiscal 2023 to support strategic flexibility and potential acquisitions.
Management expressed confidence that the business model's recurring revenue foundation positions Veltora favorably against macroeconomic uncertainty.
The board reaffirmed its commitment to long-term value creation through disciplined capital allocation and sustained product innovation.

Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care diagnostic instruments and proprietary reagent consumables.
The company was founded in 2009 and has grown to serve clinical laboratories, hospital networks, and physician office practices across 34 countries.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Solloway presented the annual results to shareholders at the company's general meeting held in March 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pryce emphasized that disciplined capital allocation remained central to Veltara's long-term strategy throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company reported total revenue of $1.47 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior year.
Instrument sales contributed $612 million to total revenue, while reagent and consumable sales accounted for the remaining $858 million.
Gross margin improved by 180 basis points to 54.7%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies at the Raleigh production campus.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in February of that year.
Operating expenses totaled $521 million, including $198 million allocated to research and development activities.
The company's EBITDA reached $312 million, a 14.6% improvement compared to the $272 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $187 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, compared to $159 million, or $2.91 per diluted share, in the prior year.
Margaret Solloway noted that the strong earnings performance reflected the company's successful pivot toward higher-margin specialty diagnostics platforms.
Veltara's flagship product, the VX-900 integrated analyzer, achieved record unit placements of 4,200 instruments globally during the fiscal year.
The VX-900 platform's installed base now exceeds 22,000 units worldwide, underpinning a growing and predictable recurring reagent revenue stream.
International revenue grew 17.2% year-over-year to $541 million, with particularly strong performance in the Asia-Pacific and Middle East regions.
The company expanded its direct sales force in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom, adding 140 commercial employees across those three markets.
Research and development spending of $198 million represented 13.5% of total revenue, consistent with the company's historical investment cadence.
Veltara submitted regulatory filings for its next-generation VX-1100 multiplexing analyzer to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in September 2023.
The VX-1100 platform is designed to run up to 18 simultaneous assay panels, significantly expanding the clinical utility compared to the current VX-900.
Management anticipates FDA clearance for the VX-1100 no later than the third quarter of 2024, enabling a commercial launch in Q4 2024.
The company's oncology diagnostics segment posted revenue of $319 million, up 22.4% from $261 million in fiscal year 2022.
Growth in oncology was primarily driven by adoption of Veltara's liquid biopsy assay panels in academic medical centers and regional cancer institutes.
Infectious disease diagnostics contributed $408 million in revenue, reflecting sustained demand following the normalization of respiratory illness testing volumes.
Daniel Pryce confirmed that operating cash flow for the year was $263 million, providing ample coverage of capital expenditure requirements.
Capital expenditures totaled $89 million during fiscal year 2023, directed primarily toward expanding production capacity and upgrading laboratory automation equipment.
The company repurchased 1.8 million shares of its common stock under its authorized buyback program at an average price of $67.40 per share.
Share repurchases totaled approximately $121 million in aggregate, reducing the diluted share count from 55.6 million to 54.7 million shares outstanding.
Veltara declared quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.68 per share for fiscal year 2023, up from $0.56 per share in fiscal year 2022.
Total cash returned to shareholders through dividends and buybacks amounted to $158 million during the year.
The company's cash and short-term investment balance at December 31, 2023, stood at $441 million, up from $389 million at the prior year-end.
Working capital increased to $567 million from $498 million, reflecting higher receivables balances in line with the revenue growth trajectory.
Days sales outstanding improved modestly from 62 days to 59 days, indicating slightly faster collection of accounts receivable.
Inventory levels rose to $143 million from $118 million, partly reflecting a planned build of component inventory ahead of the VX-1100 launch.
The supply chain team secured multi-year agreements with three new reagent precursor suppliers to mitigate concentration risk identified in a prior internal audit.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Vieth oversaw the successful qualification of a secondary manufacturing site in Monterrey, Mexico, during the second half of 2023.
The Monterrey facility is expected to reach full operational capacity by mid-2025 and will initially focus on high-volume consumable production.
Veltara's headcount grew to 6,840 full-time employees at year-end 2023, an increase of 430 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
Employee-related expenses, including salaries, benefits, and equity compensation, totaled $387 million for the year.
The company's effective income tax rate was 21.4% for fiscal year 2023, slightly above the 20.9% rate recorded in the prior year.
Deferred tax liabilities increased to $58 million, primarily due to accelerated depreciation on newly installed manufacturing equipment.
Veltara's audit committee, chaired by independent director Thomas Burridge, completed its review of the financial statements without requiring any material restatements.
The external audit was conducted by Hartwell & Granger LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Margaret Solloway reiterated the company's commitment to achieving carbon-neutral manufacturing operations by the end of 2030.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declined by 8% in 2023 relative to the 2021 baseline established in Veltara's sustainability roadmap.
The company published its second annual Environmental, Social, and Governance report in April 2023, detailing progress against seventeen measurable targets.
Veltara's board of directors approved a revised long-term incentive plan in November 2023, linking 60% of executive compensation to three-year total shareholder return.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 projects total revenue in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion, implying growth of approximately 8.8% to 12.2%.
During the first quarter of 2023, Veltara completed a $350 million syndicated term loan arrangement with a consortium of seven financial institutions.
The 2024 operating margin guidance of 20.5% to 21.5% reflects expected leverage on the company's largely fixed commercial infrastructure.
Management expects reagent revenue to grow at a faster pace than instrument revenue in 2024, as the large installed base drives consumable pull-through.
The company plans to hire approximately 500 additional employees in 2024, weighted toward commercial and manufacturing functions.
Daniel Pryce stated that the company's capital expenditure program for 2024 is budgeted at $105 million to $115 million.
Approximately 40% of the planned 2024 capital expenditure will be directed toward information technology infrastructure and digital diagnostics capabilities.
Veltara is evaluating two potential bolt-on acquisitions in the companion diagnostics space, with combined estimated consideration in the range of $200 million to $280 million.
Any acquisition activity would be financed from existing cash balances and operating cash flow, consistent with the company's stated preference for maintaining balance sheet flexibility.
The company has no current plans to issue equity to fund strategic transactions, according to statements made by Margaret Solloway at the investor day presentation.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 28.3% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader medical technology index, which gained 12.7% over the same period.
The company's market capitalization at December 31, 2023, was approximately $3.7 billion based on the closing share price of $67.71.
Institutional ownership stands at approximately 81% of shares outstanding, with the ten largest institutional holders collectively owning 44% of the float.
Veltara's next scheduled investor conference appearance is the Global Healthcare Summit in Boston, set for May 2024.
The board of directors reaffirmed its commitment to paying a progressive dividend, targeting mid-single-digit annual increases over the medium term.
In summary, fiscal year 2023 demonstrated Veltara Diagnostics Corporation's capacity to deliver consistent organic growth, expanding profitability, and disciplined capital stewardship.
The company enters fiscal year 2024 with strong operational momentum, a robust product pipeline, and a clearly articulated path toward sustained value creation for all stakeholders.
Veltora Dynamics entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, operating with a clean balance sheet that management described as a strategic competitive advantage.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializes in advanced thermal management solutions for data center infrastructure.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Delacroix opened the annual report by highlighting the firm's disciplined capital allocation philosophy.
Veltora Dynamics reported total revenues of $847.3 million for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
The North American segment contributed $512.6 million, or approximately 60.5% of total revenues, reinforcing its position as the company's primary growth engine.
European operations generated $198.4 million in revenue, with particularly strong demand from hyperscale data center operators in Germany and the Netherlands.
The Asia-Pacific segment posted revenues of $136.3 million, up 22.7% year-over-year, driven by expansion in Singapore and South Korea.
Gross profit for the full year reached $312.9 million, yielding a gross margin of 36.9%, compared to 34.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Brennan Khoury attributed the margin improvement to a favorable product mix shift toward higher-value liquid cooling systems.
Operating expenses totaled $198.1 million, including $74.5 million in research and development and $89.3 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Operating income came in at $114.8 million, representing an operating margin of 13.6%, a 180 basis point expansion relative to the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $143.2 million, compared to $118.6 million in 2022, reflecting a 20.7% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to Veltora Dynamics shareholders totaled $84.6 million, or $2.19 per diluted share.
The company's effective tax rate for the period was 22.4%, slightly lower than the 23.1% recorded in fiscal 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $221.7 million, providing the organization with substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.

Capital expenditures during the year amounted to $63.4 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the Raleigh manufacturing facility and the opening of a new engineering center in Austin, Texas.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $157.9 million, up from $131.2 million in the prior year.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share beginning in the second quarter of fiscal 2023, marking the company's inaugural dividend program.
Total dividends paid during the fiscal year amounted to $20.8 million, representing a modest payout ratio of 24.6% of net income.
During the third quarter, Veltora Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares at an average price of $41.30, reducing the diluted share count to approximately 38.6 million.
The share repurchase program, authorized at $75 million in total, had approximately $24.4 million remaining as of December 31, 2023.
Inventories at year-end were $94.2 million, up 8.3% from $87.0 million at the end of fiscal 2022, consistent with planned production ramp-ups.
Accounts receivable totaled $112.8 million, with days sales outstanding of 48.6 days, within the company's target range of 45 to 55 days.
Veltora Dynamics expanded its workforce to 3,847 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, adding 412 net new positions during the year.
The company's flagship product line, the VX-9 Series liquid cooling units, accounted for $394.1 million in revenue, or 46.5% of total annual sales.
In May 2023, Veltora Dynamics launched the VX-9 Pro, an upgraded platform featuring a 31% improvement in thermal dissipation efficiency compared to the base VX-9 model.
Customer adoption of the VX-9 Pro accelerated in the second half of the year, with the product contributing $87.4 million in revenue from its launch through December 31.
The company secured 14 new enterprise accounts during fiscal 2023, including two Fortune 500 hyperscale operators that collectively represented $63.2 million in contracted annual revenue.
Backlog at year-end was $389.7 million, a 27.4% increase compared to the $305.9 million backlog reported at the close of fiscal 2022.
Management believes the backlog growth reflects both increasing market penetration and the longer project lead times associated with large hyperscale deployments.
Veltora Dynamics estimates its addressable market in North America grew to approximately $4.8 billion in 2023, up from $3.9 billion in the prior year.
The company's market share in the North American thermal management segment is estimated at approximately 10.7%, versus 9.6% at the end of fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Nathaniel Forsythe indicated that the company's supply chain diversification strategy, initiated in late 2021, continued to yield measurable cost and resilience benefits.
The company maintains primary manufacturing relationships with five contract partners across Malaysia, Vietnam, and Mexico.
In October 2023, Veltora Dynamics completed the acquisition of Korvex Thermal Systems, a privately held manufacturer of immersion cooling technologies, for a total consideration of $48.5 million.
The Korvex acquisition added 87 employees and a patent portfolio of 23 granted patents, which management expects to integrate into the VX product roadmap over the next 18 months.
Integration costs associated with the Korvex transaction were $3.7 million, recorded in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
Goodwill and intangible assets arising from the Korvex acquisition were recorded at $31.2 million and $9.4 million, respectively.
Ms. Delacroix noted that immersion cooling represents a strategically critical capability as AI-driven workloads continue to increase power density requirements in modern data centers.
The company's internal actuarial review confirmed that pension and post-retirement benefit obligations remained fully funded at year-end, presenting no material liquidity risk.

To finance its accelerating growth initiatives and the Korvex integration, Veltora Dynamics entered into a $300 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate of four lenders in November 2023.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management provided revenue guidance in the range of $960 million to $985 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 13% to 16%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $165 million to $175 million, suggesting continued margin expansion as operating leverage improves.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $70 million to $80 million, reflecting ongoing investment in manufacturing capacity.
Veltora Dynamics plans to open a new regional distribution center in Dallas, Texas, during the second quarter of fiscal 2024 to improve order fulfillment speed in the central United States.
Mr. Khoury emphasized that the company's disciplined approach to working capital management is expected to sustain free cash flow generation above $160 million in the coming year.
The board of directors increased the quarterly dividend to $0.22 per share effective January 1, 2024, representing a 22.2% increase from the initial dividend rate.
Management reiterated its long-term target of achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20% or higher by the end of fiscal 2026.
The company's sustainability program, Veltora Green Path, set a target of reducing direct carbon emissions by 30% relative to a 2021 baseline by the end of fiscal 2025.
In fiscal 2023, Veltora Dynamics reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 11.4% compared to 2022, marking meaningful early progress toward that target.
The board of directors is composed of nine members, five of whom are independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
Audit Committee Chair Priya Sundaram confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal 2023 review.
Veltora Dynamics remains committed to transparent disclosure practices and looks forward to continued dialogue with its shareholders, analysts, and broader stakeholder community.
Veridian Packaging Solutions, Inc. released its annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, marking a significant milestone in the company's twenty-year operating history.
Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, Veridian specializes in the design and manufacturing of sustainable corrugated and flexible packaging materials for consumer goods companies.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the letter to shareholders with a note of optimism, citing strong operational execution across all three business segments.
The company reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing an increase of 11.2 percent compared to the prior year.
Veridian carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a disciplined capital allocation strategy pursued over the preceding five years.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei outlined the key financial highlights during the investor call held on February 14, 2024.
Gross profit for the full year reached $487 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 26.5 percent, up 140 basis points year over year.
The North America Packaging segment contributed $1.12 billion in revenue, representing 60.9 percent of total consolidated sales.
The International Packaging segment generated $520 million in revenue, driven largely by expanded distribution in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
The Specialty Materials segment, the company's newest division, posted $200 million in revenue for its first full year of consolidated reporting.
Operating income for the full year was $231 million, translating to an operating margin of 12.6 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $318 million, compared to $279 million in fiscal year 2022, representing growth of 14 percent.
Capital expenditures totaled $145 million in 2023, largely directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at facilities in Ohio, Texas, and Poland.
Veridian ended the year with $312 million in cash and cash equivalents, a 23 percent increase from the $254 million held at the close of fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow generation was robust at $167 million, providing substantial flexibility for reinvestment and shareholder returns.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, continuing the company's uninterrupted dividend payment streak of twelve consecutive years.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal 2023 amounted to $68 million, up from $61 million in the prior year.
Veridian repurchased 2.1 million shares during 2023 at an average price of $42.30 per share, completing $88.8 million of its authorized $150 million buyback program.
The remaining $61.2 million authorization under the repurchase program will carry forward into fiscal 2024, subject to market conditions.
Headcount at year-end stood at 8,750 full-time employees globally, an increase of approximately 6 percent from the 8,250 employed at the end of 2022.
The company added 320 new positions in manufacturing and 180 in sales and marketing during the year to support growth initiatives.
Margaret Holloway emphasized that talent retention remained a top priority, with voluntary turnover declining to 8.4 percent from 11.2 percent in the prior year.
Research and development expenses totaled $42 million in 2023, reflecting a continued commitment to innovation in bio-based and recyclable material technologies.
The company filed seventeen new patents during the year, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 214 filings across twelve countries.
Veridian's flagship EcoCore product line achieved $390 million in revenue in 2023, accounting for 21.2 percent of total company sales.
EcoCore materials are formulated using at least 80 percent post-consumer recycled content, positioning the line as a premium offering for sustainability-conscious brand owners.
Customer concentration risk remained manageable, with the top ten customers collectively representing 38 percent of total revenue.
No single customer accounted for more than 7.5 percent of consolidated net sales during the fiscal year.
The company expanded its customer base by onboarding 47 new enterprise accounts during 2023, representing an estimated $95 million in annualized contract value.
Raymond Osei noted that the pipeline of qualified prospects entering fiscal 2024 was the largest in the company's history, exceeding $400 million in potential annual contract value.
Raw material costs, primarily wood pulp and polyethylene resin, represented 44 percent of cost of goods sold in 2023.
Veridian successfully renegotiated supply contracts with three of its five primary raw material suppliers, securing pricing terms favorable through the end of 2025.
Logistics and distribution costs declined as a percentage of revenue by 80 basis points due to route optimization initiatives implemented in the second half of 2022.
The company's Charlotte headquarters was expanded during the year, adding 45,000 square feet of office space to accommodate growing administrative and technology teams.
Veridian invested $18 million in its enterprise resource planning platform upgrade, completing phase one of a three-year digital transformation initiative.
The ERP upgrade is expected to generate approximately $12 million in annual operating cost savings once fully deployed in fiscal 2025.
Interest income earned on cash holdings contributed $14.2 million to pre-tax income, benefiting from the elevated interest rate environment throughout 2023.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 23.1 percent, slightly below the prior year rate of 24.0 percent, due to favorable discrete tax items recognized in the third quarter.
Net income attributable to Veridian shareholders totaled $172 million, equating to diluted earnings per share of $3.87.
This represented a 16.9 percent increase in diluted EPS compared to the $3.31 reported for fiscal year 2022.
The company's return on invested capital was 14.8 percent, exceeding the stated internal hurdle rate of 12 percent for the fourth consecutive year.
Veridian's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in October 2023, with a stable outlook cited by the agency.
The affirmation reflected the rating agency's confidence in the company's consistent cash flow generation and conservative financial management practices.
Analysts from three major investment banks upgraded their price targets for Veridian stock following the release of the third-quarter results in November 2023.
In March 2023, Veridian finalized a $350 million senior unsecured term loan facility with a syndicate of seven lenders to fund strategic acquisitions and capacity expansion.
The acquisition of Nordic Flex Packaging AS, a Norwegian flexible materials manufacturer, was completed in June 2023 for a total consideration of $215 million.
Nordic Flex contributed $48 million in revenue and $9 million in operating income during the six months it was consolidated into Veridian's results.
The integration of Nordic Flex is proceeding ahead of schedule, with cost synergies of $7 million already realized against a full-year target of $12 million.
Margaret Holloway stated that the Nordic Flex acquisition meaningfully strengthened Veridian's capabilities in barrier film technologies critical to food-safe packaging applications.
The company also completed a smaller bolt-on acquisition of a regional packaging distributor in the U.S. Southeast for $28 million in October 2023.
Total goodwill on the balance sheet rose to $341 million at year-end from $198 million at the beginning of the year, reflecting the two completed acquisitions.
Intangible assets net of amortization stood at $184 million, up from $97 million, attributable primarily to customer relationship assets recognized in the Nordic Flex purchase price allocation.
The company performed its annual goodwill impairment assessment and determined that no impairment existed across any of its three reporting units.
Environmental, social, and governance performance continued to improve, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining 9 percent on an absolute basis.
Veridian reduced water consumption per unit of output by 12 percent through operational improvements at its largest manufacturing facilities in Ohio and Poland.
The company achieved a total recordable incident rate of 0.82, compared to 1.04 in the prior year, reflecting sustained investment in workplace safety programs.
Veridian was recognized by the Sustainable Packaging Coalition as a Silver Member for the second consecutive year based on its material transparency commitments.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion, implying growth of approximately 6 to 10 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set between $340 million and $360 million, reflecting continued margin improvement expectations.
Capital expenditure plans for fiscal 2024 are projected at $155 million to $170 million, with a significant portion earmarked for a new manufacturing line in Malaysia.
The Malaysia facility is expected to serve as the primary production hub for Veridian's expansion into the high-growth ASEAN consumer goods packaging market.
Raymond Osei concluded his remarks by reaffirming that the company's balance sheet strength and cash generation profile position Veridian exceptionally well for continued value creation.
The board expressed confidence in the leadership team's strategic roadmap and reiterated its commitment to delivering long-term, sustainable returns to all stakeholders.
Veridian's fiscal year 2023 annual report on Form 10-K has been filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission and is available on the company's investor relations website.
Independent auditor PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veridian's consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
Veldoran Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in rare metabolic disorders and orphan drug development.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Okafor and has since grown into a recognized innovator in the rare disease space.
This report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and reflects audited financial statements prepared under U.S. GAAP.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Lindqvist opened the annual shareholder letter by emphasizing the company's commitment to long-term value creation.
Veldoran Therapeutics reported total revenues of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 17.4% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product, Renivex, generated $284.3 million in net product sales, driven by expanded coverage agreements with three major payers.
Pipeline candidate VLD-209 received Fast Track Designation from the FDA in March 2023, accelerating its regulatory pathway.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar highlighted the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation during the earnings call.
Operating expenses totaled $318.6 million, with research and development spending comprising the largest single line item at $141.2 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased modestly to $89.4 million, reflecting investments in the commercial infrastructure for Renivex.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 came in at $94.1 million, a significant improvement from the $61.8 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $77.3 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.84.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.5 million, supplemented by $95.0 million in short-term marketable securities.
The company's current ratio improved to 3.2x, reflecting strong liquidity management and efficient working capital practices.
Veldoran Therapeutics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its revolving credit facility earlier in the year.
Gross margin on product sales expanded to 74.2% from 71.5% in the prior year, driven by manufacturing efficiencies at the company's Durham facility.
Headcount grew to 1,047 full-time employees by year-end, up from 924 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company opened a new clinical research center in Boston in September 2023, adding approximately 60 scientific and clinical staff.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, with finished goods inventory ending the year at $28.1 million, consistent with approximately 11 weeks of supply.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days from 51 days in the prior period, reflecting tighter collections processes.
Veldoran's board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in August 2023.
Under the repurchase program, the company bought back 2.1 million shares at an average price of $38.60 per share by year-end.
Total capital returned to shareholders in fiscal year 2023 was $81.1 million, inclusive of repurchases and a newly initiated quarterly cash dividend.
The quarterly dividend of $0.08 per share was declared for the first time in Q3 2023, marking a milestone in the company's maturation.
Mr. Lindqvist remarked that the dividend initiation signals confidence in the sustainability of cash generation going forward.
VLD-209 is being evaluated for the treatment of Type II Glutaric Acidemia and enrolled 78 patients across 14 clinical sites as of December 31, 2023.
Interim Phase 2 data for VLD-209 are expected to be presented at a major medical conference in the first half of 2024.
The company also advanced VLD-317, a second pipeline asset targeting mitochondrial complex I deficiency, into a Phase 1 dose-escalation study.
Dr. Okafor, who serves as Chief Scientific Officer, noted that VLD-317 demonstrated a favorable tolerability profile in preclinical models.
Renivex was approved in the European Union in October 2022, and international sales contributed $47.6 million in fiscal year 2023.
The company entered a co-promotion agreement with Astellar Pharma GmbH to support Renivex commercialization in Germany and Austria.
Management is targeting international revenues to represent at least 20% of total net product sales by fiscal year 2025.
The company's manufacturing operations are conducted at a single owned facility in Durham, which received a satisfactory FDA inspection in June 2023.
Veldoran is evaluating a second manufacturing site to reduce single-source risk and support anticipated volume growth from pipeline approvals.
Capital expenditures in fiscal year 2023 totaled $22.8 million, primarily related to process improvement equipment and laboratory upgrades.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $14.3 million, reflecting the company's relatively asset-light model outside of its owned facility.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 17.9%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits and research and development deductions.
Deferred tax assets of $31.7 million were recorded on the balance sheet, primarily related to net operating loss carryforwards from earlier years.
The company did not recognize any goodwill impairment charges during fiscal year 2023.
Veldoran entered into a license agreement with Nexagen Biotech in February 2023, acquiring rights to a preclinical lysosomal storage disorder program for an upfront payment of $8.5 million.
Milestone payments under the Nexagen agreement could reach up to $185 million upon achievement of specified regulatory and commercial events.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 34% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P Biotech Index by 21 percentage points.
Institutional ownership stood at 78.3% of shares outstanding as of the most recent proxy filing.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Thornfield LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023.
Ernst & Thornfield issued an unqualified audit opinion on both the financial statements and the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting.
Ms. Nambiar stated during the Q4 earnings call that gross-to-net adjustments for Renivex remained stable at approximately 18% of gross product sales.
The company accrued $12.4 million in rebates and chargebacks related to government health programs, consistent with prior-year levels.
Patient assistance program utilization increased modestly in fiscal year 2023, partially offsetting gross product sales growth.
Veldoran's Renivex holds orphan drug exclusivity in the United States through 2028, providing a meaningful competitive moat.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $465 million to $485 million, implying approximately 13% to 18% growth.
The company expects operating margins to expand modestly in 2024 as manufacturing scale benefits are realized.
R&D expense guidance for 2024 was set at $155 million to $165 million, reflecting increased investment in Phase 2 and Phase 3 activities.
Mr. Lindqvist reiterated the company's strategy of pursuing bolt-on acquisitions in the rare disease space, funded primarily through operating cash flow.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $93.4 million, providing ample capacity to fund the pipeline and any strategic transactions.
In January 2024, Veldoran announced it had secured a $250 million senior secured term loan to fund potential near-term business development activities.
Veldoran's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside this annual report, highlights a 15% reduction in facility carbon emissions since 2021.
The company donated $3.2 million to rare disease patient advocacy organizations in fiscal year 2023.
Board diversity improved with the appointment of two new independent directors in May 2023, bringing the board to nine members, of whom four identify as women.
Veldoran Therapeutics remains committed to delivering innovative therapies to underserved patient populations while creating durable value for shareholders and stakeholders alike.
Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical and diagnostics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in oncology therapeutics and precision medicine platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Mirela Castañeda and Dr. Owen Pryce, both veteran researchers with prior experience at leading academic medical centers.
Veltrano's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of meaningful operational progress across its core business units.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Heulbroeck opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's long-term trajectory despite near-term macroeconomic headwinds.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $412 million, representing an 18% increase over the prior year's $349 million.
Product revenue, which constitutes the largest share of total revenue, grew by 22% year-over-year, driven by strong uptake of Veltrano's flagship therapeutic, Calvonix.
Calvonix, approved by the FDA in late 2021 for second-line metastatic colorectal cancer, recorded $198 million in net sales during fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final senior notes in December 2022.
Gross profit for the year stood at $271 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 65.8%, an improvement of roughly 200 basis points compared to fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $189 million, with research and development representing the single largest line item at $94 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $72 million, reflecting disciplined cost management initiatives implemented in the second half of the year.
Veltrano reported operating income of $82 million, compared to $51 million in fiscal year 2022, a 61% year-over-year improvement.
Net income for fiscal year 2023 was $63 million, or $1.47 per diluted share, up from $38 million, or $0.91 per diluted share, in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate was 23.1%, slightly above the prior year's 22.4%, due to changes in the geographic mix of earnings.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $317 million, supported by strong operating cash flow generation throughout the fiscal year.
Operating cash flow for the year was $101 million, with free cash flow of $88 million after accounting for $13 million in capital expenditures.
Chief Financial Officer Brennan Lykke noted that the balance sheet remains one of Veltrano's greatest strategic assets.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock under its previously authorized $75 million buyback program.
The company declared and paid quarterly cash dividends totaling $0.60 per share for the full year, consistent with the prior year's dividend schedule.
Veltrano's total headcount grew from 1,840 employees at the start of fiscal year 2023 to 2,105 employees by year-end, an increase of approximately 14%.
The majority of new hires were in commercial operations and medical affairs, reflecting the company's focus on expanding its Calvonix sales infrastructure.
Veltrano's diagnostics segment, which includes its PrecisePath liquid biopsy platform, contributed $78 million in revenue, up 9% from the prior year.
The PrecisePath platform received a positive coverage decision from a major U.S. private insurer in March 2023, expanding the eligible patient population meaningfully.
International revenue accounted for 19% of total revenue, with the largest contributions coming from Germany, Japan, and the United Kingdom.
Veltrano filed a supplemental new drug application for Calvonix in a first-line colorectal setting in October 2023, pending FDA review.
The company also initiated a Phase 2 clinical trial for VT-408, its investigational agent targeting HER2-amplified biliary tract cancer.
Dr. Castañeda, who now serves as Chief Scientific Officer, presented initial biomarker data from the VT-408 trial at the American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting.
Early results from the VT-408 program demonstrated a 41% objective response rate in a heavily pretreated patient population, which the company characterized as encouraging.
Veltrano entered a co-promotion agreement with Farenta Pharma in July 2023, enabling broader detailing of Calvonix to community oncologists.
Under the co-promotion agreement, Farenta will receive a tiered royalty on incremental net sales above a mutually agreed baseline threshold.
Inventory levels at year-end were $44 million, down slightly from $47 million at the close of fiscal year 2022, reflecting improvements in supply chain efficiency.
Veltrano's manufacturing partner, Corteva BioLogics, successfully completed its first FDA inspection of the Calvonix production facility with zero observations.
The company's accounts receivable balance was $58 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of approximately 51 days.
Accounts receivable days sales outstanding improved by four days compared to the prior year, attributable to enhanced payer contracting terms.
Veltrano renegotiated its primary distribution agreement with McAllister Health Distributors, securing more favorable pricing and service level commitments.
The audit committee of Veltrano's board of directors completed its annual review without identifying any material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP served as Veltrano's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2023 and issued an unqualified audit opinion.
Veltrano's board of directors is composed of ten members, six of whom are independent, and is chaired by former FDA Commissioner Elliot Marchetti.
The compensation committee approved a revised executive incentive plan for fiscal year 2024, incorporating new pipeline milestone metrics alongside financial performance targets.
