Looking ahead, Veltrano provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance of $465 million to $485 million, implying approximately 13% to 18% growth over fiscal year 2023.
The company also guided for operating income in the range of $90 million to $100 million for fiscal year 2024, contingent upon continued Calvonix market penetration.
Veltrano expects to submit its marketing authorization application for Calvonix to the European Medicines Agency no later than the third quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The potential European approval of Calvonix represents a significant incremental revenue opportunity, which management estimates could contribute $40 million to $60 million annually at peak.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual report include competitive pressure from biosimilar entrants, reimbursement uncertainties, and clinical trial execution risk.
Veltrano maintains product liability and directors-and-officers insurance coverage considered adequate by management and its outside advisors.
The company's deferred revenue balance increased to $12 million at year-end, primarily related to performance obligations in its diagnostics collaboration agreements.
In the second quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano's board approved and the company executed a $150 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate led by JPMorgan Chase.
The credit facility carries a variable interest rate benchmarked to SOFR plus a spread of 175 basis points and matures in five years.
Veltrano disclosed no material off-balance-sheet arrangements in its annual report other than standard operating lease obligations for its office and laboratory spaces.
Operating lease right-of-use assets totaled $29 million, with corresponding lease liabilities of $31 million, consistent with prior period disclosures.
The company recognized $4 million in amortization of intangible assets related to its 2020 acquisition of Nexarion Diagnostics.
Goodwill attributable to the Nexarion acquisition remains on the balance sheet at $67 million, subject to annual impairment testing which yielded no impairment in fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano closed fiscal year 2023 with stockholders' equity of $504 million, compared to $455 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
In summary, fiscal year 2023 marked one of the most productive years in Veltrano Biosciences' history, with records set across revenue, operating income, and pipeline advancement.
Management expressed gratitude to Veltrano's employees, patients, and shareholders for their continued confidence and support as the company enters its next phase of growth.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Markus Felder opened the report with optimism, citing strong demand across the company's three core product divisions.
Veltrano Industries operates in the high-performance polymer coatings, industrial adhesives, and specialty solvents segments.
The company reported consolidated net revenue of €1.42 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margin expanded by 140 basis points to reach 38.7%, driven primarily by efficiency gains in the adhesives segment.
Veltrano Industries carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having completed full repayment of its outstanding notes in March of that year.
Operating income rose to €218 million, up from €191 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting disciplined cost management across all business units.
Chief Financial Officer Ingrid Schwarmann noted that the improved operating income was partly attributable to a reduction in raw material costs during the second half of the year.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 reached €274 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of 19.3%.
The polymer coatings division contributed €612 million in revenue, making it the single largest revenue-generating segment for the third consecutive year.
Industrial adhesives revenue grew by 14.1% year over year, reaching €498 million, as demand from the automotive sector surged.
Specialty solvents, while the smallest segment, posted revenue of €310 million, a modest 3.2% increase compared to 2022.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €87 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Cologne and Rotterdam facilities.
The Board of Directors approved a dividend of €1.85 per share for fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's commitment to returning value to shareholders.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders reached €142 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.47 on a diluted basis.
The effective tax rate for the period was 24.8%, slightly above management's earlier guidance of 23.5% due to changes in German corporate tax regulations.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €193 million, reflecting strong working capital management and disciplined capital allocation.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, indicating enhanced collection efficiency.
Inventory levels remained lean, with inventory turnover rising to 6.2 times annually compared to 5.7 times in 2022.
Research and development expenditures totaled €56 million, representing approximately 3.9% of total revenues.
Veltrano launched three new specialty polymer products during the fiscal year, including the PolyShield X7 coating designed for extreme temperature environments.
PolyShield X7 received regulatory approval in the European Union and South Korea within six months of its commercial launch.
The company's global headcount stood at 6,840 employees as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 210 employees versus the prior year-end.
Veltrano expanded its sales presence in Southeast Asia by establishing regional offices in Ho Chi Minh City and Kuala Lumpur during the second quarter.
The company's market share in the European high-performance coatings segment was estimated at 12.4% for calendar year 2023.
Management expects this share to grow as newly expanded capacity comes online in the second half of 2024.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were held flat at €132 million, representing 9.3% of net revenue, down from 10.4% in the prior year on a percentage basis.
Veltrano's pension obligations decreased by €18 million during the year, reflecting favorable actuarial assumptions and contributions made ahead of schedule.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.1 and a quick ratio of 1.6, indicating a healthy liquidity position.
Markus Felder emphasized that Veltrano's balance sheet strength positions the company well for both organic growth and potential strategic acquisitions.
The company evaluated three acquisition targets in the specialty adhesives space during the second half of 2023 but did not consummate any transactions.
Ingrid Schwarmann confirmed that internal return hurdle rates for acquisitions remain set at a minimum of 12% IRR on a post-tax basis.
Foreign exchange movements had a net negative impact of €21 million on reported revenues due to the strengthening of the euro against several Asian currencies.
Hedging instruments in place during the year offset approximately 60% of the transactional currency exposure, according to the treasury report.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in October 2023, with a stable outlook.
Veltrano's polymer coatings division benefited from a multi-year supply agreement signed with a major European aerospace manufacturer in January 2023.
This agreement is expected to contribute incremental revenues of approximately €45 million annually through 2027.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially during the year, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions declining by 8.4% year over year.
The company invested €14 million in renewable energy infrastructure at its Cologne facility, which now sources 37% of its electricity from on-site solar generation.
Employee safety metrics improved, with the total recordable incident rate falling to 0.82 per 200,000 hours worked, a company record low.
Veltrano's Board of Directors underwent a refresh in 2023, with two new independent directors appointed, bringing the total board size to nine members.
Dr. Petra Lüdemann joined the board in April as chair of the Audit Committee, bringing over 25 years of experience in financial oversight.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 22% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the DAX Chemicals sub-index by roughly 9 percentage points.
Veltrano repurchased 1.2 million of its own shares during the fiscal year at an average price of €41.30 per share, spending approximately €49.6 million in total.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for net revenue in the range of €1.50 billion to €1.55 billion.
To fund the aggressive capacity expansion program planned for 2024, Veltrano entered into a €350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of European banks in November 2023.
The EBITDA margin for 2024 is forecast to remain in the range of 19% to 20%, consistent with fiscal year 2023 performance.
Management acknowledged that raw material price volatility remains the primary risk factor to achieving margin targets in the year ahead.
The specialty solvents segment is expected to benefit from tightening global supply conditions, potentially accelerating revenue growth beyond the recent trend.
Ingrid Schwarmann reiterated the company's commitment to maintaining a strong investment-grade credit profile over the medium term.
Veltrano's annual shareholder meeting is scheduled for April 18, 2024, in Düsseldorf, at which time shareholders will be asked to approve the proposed dividend.
The Board of Directors and executive management team collectively thank Veltrano's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued confidence in the company's strategic direction.
Veltara Biopharma Inc. concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, with a pristine balance sheet reflecting no outstanding borrowings.
The company, headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, operates primarily in the specialty biologics segment of the pharmaceutical sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Holst presented the annual results to shareholders during the March 2024 earnings call.
Veltara Biopharma reported total revenues of CHF 1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
This represented a 12.4% increase compared to the CHF 1.64 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Renzo Caldini attributed the growth primarily to strong performance in the company's flagship oncology portfolio.
The oncology division alone generated CHF 920 million in net sales, accounting for roughly half of total revenues.
Veltara's autoimmune product line contributed an additional CHF 540 million, a 9.1% year-over-year increase.
The rare disease segment, while smaller, posted the highest growth rate at 21.3%, reaching CHF 380 million in annual sales.
Operating expenses for the year totaled CHF 1.31 billion, reflecting increased investment in research and development.
Research and development expenditures specifically reached CHF 412 million, representing 22.4% of total revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative costs were CHF 298 million, modestly up from CHF 271 million in the prior year.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 stood at CHF 1.19 billion, yielding a gross margin of 64.7%.
The company's operating income came in at CHF 530 million, an improvement from CHF 461 million in 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders was CHF 398 million, compared with CHF 342 million the previous year.
Diluted earnings per share rose to CHF 4.87 from CHF 4.19, an increase of 16.2%.
The board of directors approved an annual dividend of CHF 1.20 per share, a 10% increase over the prior-year dividend.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled CHF 743 million, up from CHF 621 million at the close of 2022.
The company generated CHF 512 million in free cash flow during fiscal 2023, reflecting disciplined capital allocation.
Capital expenditures totaled CHF 87 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Basel facility.
Veltara employed approximately 6,400 full-time equivalents globally as of December 31, 2023.
The headcount represented a net addition of 380 employees relative to the prior fiscal year-end.
The company's commercial presence spans 38 countries, with Europe accounting for 47% of total revenues.
North American revenues grew 15.8% year-over-year, reaching CHF 631 million and representing 34.3% of total sales.
Asia-Pacific revenues increased 18.5% to CHF 241 million, driven by expanded reimbursement coverage in Japan and South Korea.
Dr. Holst emphasized that pipeline momentum remained a central pillar of Veltara's long-term strategy.
The clinical pipeline as of year-end comprised 14 active programs, including 4 in Phase III trials.
VTR-209, the company's lead oncology candidate targeting KRAS-mutant solid tumors, achieved positive Phase III interim results in Q4 2023.
The Phase III study enrolled 847 patients across 62 clinical sites in 18 countries.
Veltara filed for regulatory approval of VTR-209 with both the European Medicines Agency and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in January 2024.
The autoimmune pipeline candidate VTR-415 advanced into Phase II trials after demonstrating acceptable safety in Phase I dosing studies.
Management expects to report Phase II interim data for VTR-415 in the second half of 2024.
In the rare disease space, VTR-622 received Orphan Drug Designation from the FDA in August 2023.
Strategic partnerships contributed CHF 73 million in milestone and royalty revenues during fiscal 2023.
Veltara entered a co-development agreement with Haverstock Genomics in September 2023, covering next-generation gene editing applications.
Under the Haverstock agreement, Veltara committed up to CHF 55 million in research funding over a four-year period.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the prior-year rate of 23.4%.
Deferred tax assets of CHF 118 million were recognized on the consolidated balance sheet, reflecting future deductible temporary differences.
Veltara's return on invested capital was 18.6% for fiscal 2023, an improvement of 210 basis points versus fiscal 2022.
Total shareholders' equity at December 31, 2023 amounted to CHF 2.14 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth.
In order to fund its expanded clinical development agenda, Veltara secured a CHF 350 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of European banks during the fiscal year.
Mr. Caldini confirmed that the term loan carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 95 basis points.
The facility has a five-year maturity with two one-year extension options available at the company's discretion.
Proceeds from the borrowing were earmarked for pipeline acceleration, particularly the Phase III programs and manufacturing readiness activities.
Veltara's credit rating was reaffirmed at A- by Standard & Poor's following the completion of the financing arrangement.
The company also maintained a revolving credit facility of CHF 200 million, which remained undrawn at year-end.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at CHF 163 million, equivalent to approximately 32 days of cost of goods sold.
Days sales outstanding improved to 54 days from 61 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collection processes.
Veltara repurchased CHF 120 million of its own shares during fiscal 2023 under a buyback program authorized by shareholders in June 2022.
The remaining buyback authorization as of December 31, 2023 stood at CHF 80 million.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of CHF 2.00 billion to CHF 2.10 billion, implying growth of 8.7% to 14.1%.

Dr. Holst closed the earnings presentation by expressing confidence that Veltara's diversified portfolio and disciplined financial stewardship position the company for sustained value creation over the coming decade.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the specialty pharmaceuticals sector, focusing on rare metabolic disorders and oncology support therapies.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Caldwell opened the report with a statement of cautious optimism regarding the company's long-term pipeline.
Veltara Biosciences reported total revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Product sales accounted for $378.4 million of total revenue, while licensing and milestone payments contributed the remaining $34.3 million.
The company's flagship drug, Renoxapril, generated $201.5 million in net sales, making it the largest single revenue contributor.
Chief Financial Officer Thomas Breckenridge noted that gross margins improved to 68.2% from 65.9% in the prior fiscal year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined financial management.
Research and development expenses totaled $89.4 million, representing approximately 21.7% of total revenue.
The company employed 1,847 full-time equivalent staff as of the end of the reporting period.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $74.2 million, a modest increase from $69.8 million in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income reached $104.6 million, up from $88.1 million in the comparative period.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $87.3 million, or $1.92 per diluted share.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This represents an increase of $0.03 per share compared to the quarterly dividend paid throughout fiscal year 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $243.8 million, compared to $198.5 million at the prior year-end.
The increase in cash position was driven primarily by strong operating cash flow of $116.2 million.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $22.7 million, focused largely on expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Raleigh, North Carolina.
Dr. Caldwell emphasized the strategic importance of the Raleigh facility expansion in supporting anticipated demand for Renoxapril.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were 45.4 million, down from 46.6 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Veltara Biosciences received FDA approval for its secondary compound, Toruvex, in April 2023, marking a significant regulatory milestone.
Toruvex, indicated for a rare enzymatic deficiency disorder, is expected to address an underserved patient population of approximately 120,000 in the United States.
Initial commercial launch of Toruvex generated $18.7 million in net sales during the nine months following approval.
The company's international segment recorded revenue of $67.3 million, representing growth of 22.1% versus the prior year.
European markets contributed $41.2 million to international revenue, with Germany and France as the leading contributors.
Veltara Biosciences entered into a co-promotion agreement with Helios Pharma AG for the European distribution of Renoxapril in October 2023.
The co-promotion agreement is expected to accelerate Renoxapril's market penetration in the European Union through Helios's established oncology sales network.
Inventory levels at year-end were $38.6 million, broadly consistent with the company's targeted supply coverage of approximately 90 days.
Accounts receivable stood at $62.4 million, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 60 days.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was maintained at $3.1 million, reflecting stable payer mix and collection history.
Total assets at year-end were $641.5 million, an increase of $78.2 million from the prior year-end balance.
Total liabilities were $187.9 million, composed primarily of accounts payable, accrued compensation, and deferred revenue.
Shareholders' equity grew to $453.6 million from $387.5 million in the prior year.
The increase in shareholders' equity reflects net income, partially offset by share repurchases and dividends paid.
Deferred revenue of $21.4 million relates to a multi-year licensing arrangement with NovaBridge Therapeutics signed in 2021.
Mr. Breckenridge stated that the NovaBridge licensing arrangement continues to perform in line with contractual expectations.
The company maintains a portfolio of 14 active patents covering Renoxapril's formulation and delivery mechanisms, with expirations ranging from 2029 to 2037.
Veltara Biosciences invested $11.3 million in clinical-stage compounds during 2023, including VB-401 targeting hepatocellular carcinoma.
VB-401 entered Phase II clinical trials in September 2023, with preliminary data expected in the second half of 2024.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
The slight reduction in effective tax rate was attributable to additional research and development tax credits recognized during the year.
Veltara Biosciences does not currently utilize any off-balance-sheet financing structures or special purpose entities.
The company's audit committee, chaired by independent director Patricia Nguyen, oversaw the annual audit conducted by Harwick & Langford LLP.
Harwick & Langford LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
Management does not anticipate any material changes to accounting policies in fiscal year 2024 that would affect comparability.
Looking ahead, Veltara Biosciences provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $460 million to $480 million.
To fund an accelerated expansion of its clinical pipeline, the company secured a $175 million revolving credit facility with Meridian Capital Partners in November 2023.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued strong performance from Renoxapril and a full year of Toruvex commercial sales.
The company anticipates Toruvex net sales in fiscal year 2024 to range between $55 million and $65 million.
Management expects R&D spending to increase to approximately $110 million in fiscal year 2024, reflecting the advancement of VB-401 and two additional early-stage candidates.
Dr. Caldwell expressed confidence that Veltara Biosciences is well-positioned to deliver sustainable value to shareholders over the next three to five years.
The company reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining investment-grade financial metrics and preserving operational flexibility.
Veltara Biosciences will host its annual earnings call on February 22, 2024, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.
Participants may access the call via the investor relations section of the company's website at www.veltarabio.com.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Derek Sunn, who together hold approximately 18% of outstanding shares.
Veltara reported total revenues of $412.7 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue, the company's primary revenue stream, grew 19.1% year-over-year to reach $341.5 million.
Professional services revenue declined modestly by 3.2% to $71.2 million, reflecting a strategic shift toward self-service onboarding.
The company serves approximately 3,200 enterprise clients across 47 countries, with North America accounting for 61% of total revenue.
Veltara's gross profit for fiscal 2023 was $271.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 65.8%, up from 63.1% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $234.6 million, driven by increased investment in research and development and sales and marketing activities.
Research and development expenditures rose to $89.3 million, representing 21.6% of total revenue, as the company continued advancing its AI-driven forecasting modules.
Sales and marketing spending reached $98.7 million, reflecting expanded headcount in the company's European and Asia-Pacific go-to-market teams.
General and administrative expenses were $46.6 million, roughly flat compared to $45.9 million in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $36.8 million, compared to an operating loss of $4.1 million in fiscal 2022, marking a significant turnaround.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $28.4 million, or $0.54 per diluted share, versus a net loss of $9.7 million in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure frequently cited by management, came in at $67.3 million, representing a 16.3% adjusted EBITDA margin.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar stated that the margin improvement was driven by improved subscription mix and disciplined cost management.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $183.6 million, compared to $141.2 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company generated free cash flow of $52.1 million in fiscal 2023, its first full year of positive free cash flow generation.
Capital expenditures totaled $11.4 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements at the new Austin campus.
Veltara's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $698.3 million and total liabilities of $289.4 million as of December 31, 2023.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet at the close of fiscal 2023, having fully retired its prior revolving credit facility in March of that year.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 52 days from 61 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter collections processes implemented mid-year.
Deferred revenue, a key indicator of future subscription performance, grew 22.4% to $156.8 million at year-end.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $31.40, totaling approximately $37.7 million.
The board of directors authorized an additional $75 million share repurchase program in November 2023, signaling confidence in the company's financial trajectory.
International revenues grew 23.7% year-over-year to $160.9 million, aided by strong performance in the United Kingdom and Germany.
The European segment alone contributed $102.3 million in revenue, up from $81.6 million in fiscal 2022, representing a 25.4% increase.
Customer net revenue retention rate for fiscal 2023 was reported at 118%, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
Total headcount at year-end was 2,847 employees, an increase of 341 from the 2,506 employees reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the engineering and product departments, consistent with the company's product-led growth strategy.
Veltara launched three major product updates in fiscal 2023, including the Veltara Horizon AI module in Q1, an enhanced analytics dashboard in Q2, and multi-tenant cloud infrastructure tools in Q4.
The Veltara Horizon AI module has been adopted by over 800 clients since its January 2023 launch, contributing meaningfully to upsell revenue.
CEO Margaret Holloway described fiscal 2023 as a transformational year, noting that profitability milestones achieved were ahead of internal projections.
The company's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $358.2 million as of December 31, 2023, representing a 20.6% increase over the prior year-end figure.
Net new ARR added during fiscal 2023 was $61.1 million, driven by both new customer wins and expansion within the installed base.
Churn rate for the fiscal year was 4.8%, an improvement from 6.1% in fiscal 2022, reflecting product stickiness improvements and enhanced customer success programs.
The company signed 214 new enterprise contracts in fiscal 2023, compared to 178 in the prior year, a 20.2% increase in new logo acquisition.
Average contract value for new enterprise customers rose to $187,000 annually, up from $163,000 in fiscal 2022, indicating a move upmarket.
Veltara's largest single customer accounted for 4.2% of total revenue, demonstrating a well-diversified customer concentration profile.
The top ten customers collectively represented 21.7% of total revenue, consistent with prior-year concentration levels.
During fiscal 2023, the company completed the acquisition of LogiSense Analytics Inc., a small Canadian data analytics firm, for a total consideration of $23.5 million in cash.
The LogiSense acquisition is expected to be modestly dilutive to adjusted EBITDA margins in fiscal 2024 but accretive to revenue growth.
Integration costs associated with LogiSense were approximately $2.1 million and were recorded as a one-time charge in Q3 2023.
Veltara also entered into a strategic partnership with Meridian Logistics Group in September 2023, expanding its reach into the freight and warehousing verticals.
The Meridian partnership is anticipated to generate co-selling pipeline of approximately $30 million over the next 24 months.
From a geographic expansion standpoint, the company opened new offices in Singapore and the Netherlands during the second half of fiscal 2023.
The Singapore office, staffed by 38 employees, serves as the regional headquarters for the Asia-Pacific market.
Management provided fiscal 2024 guidance of total revenue in the range of $470 million to $485 million, implying year-over-year growth of approximately 14% to 17%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 17% to 19%, reflecting continued operating leverage in the subscription business.
CFO Priya Nambiar noted that the company expects to maintain its capital-light model and does not anticipate significant increases in capital expenditures.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.8% due to incremental R&D tax credits.
Veltara's stock closed fiscal 2023 at $38.92 per share, representing a total return of 47.3% for the calendar year, outperforming the S&P Software Index by approximately 19 percentage points.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $2.05 billion, placing it firmly in mid-cap territory.
Institutional ownership stood at 74.2% of shares outstanding, with notable positions held by Thornfield Capital Management and Arcadia Growth Partners.
The board of directors declared no cash dividends in fiscal 2023, consistent with the company's policy of reinvesting earnings to fund organic and inorganic growth.
Audit services were provided by Renner & Wachs LLP, which issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements.
The company maintained compliance with all applicable financial covenants and reporting requirements throughout fiscal 2023.
Management identified no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting, and no restatements were required for any prior period.
Looking ahead, CEO Holloway expressed confidence in the company's competitive positioning, citing a robust product roadmap and expanding global distribution network.
Veltara's pipeline of prospective enterprise deals as of early fiscal 2024 is the largest in company history, exceeding $520 million in total contract value.
The company also disclosed plans to hire approximately 300 additional employees in fiscal 2024, targeting engineering, sales, and customer success roles.
In January 2024, Veltara secured a $250 million syndicated term loan facility to support its strategic growth initiatives and potential acquisitions.
The term loan, arranged by First National Corporate Bank, carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Management emphasized that the new financing structure provides substantial flexibility for capital deployment while maintaining investment-grade metrics.
Overall, fiscal 2023 represented a landmark year for Veltara Systems Inc., establishing a durable foundation for sustained profitable growth through fiscal 2024 and beyond.
Shareholders and analysts alike responded positively to the results, with five sell-side firms raising their price targets following the fourth-quarter earnings release.
Meridian Optical Technologies, Inc. (MOT) is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
MOT is a leading designer and manufacturer of precision optical components for the aerospace, medical imaging, and consumer electronics sectors.
Headquartered in Portland, Oregon, the company employs a global workforce distributed across seven countries.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Serena Whitfield opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience and strategic agility throughout 2023.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delacroix provided a detailed breakdown of revenue streams, cost structures, and capital allocation priorities for the year.
MOT ended fiscal year 2023 with a completely debt-free balance sheet, having retired all outstanding borrowings in Q2 of that year.
Total consolidated revenue for 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing an 11.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The aerospace division accounted for $720 million of total revenue, driven by strong demand from both commercial and defense contractors.
Medical imaging segment revenues grew 14.7% year-over-year, reaching $530 million as hospital procurement budgets recovered post-pandemic.
Consumer electronics contributions remained the smallest segment at $590 million, though its growth rate of 8.2% remained respectable.
Gross profit for the consolidated entity stood at $714 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 38.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, inclusive of research and development, selling, general, and administrative costs.
R&D expenditure alone accounted for $178 million, underscoring MOT's commitment to continuous product innovation.
EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was reported at $401 million, up from $351 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $243 million, producing earnings per share of $3.87 on a diluted basis.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.32 per share, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior-year quarterly dividend.
Share buybacks totaling $95 million were executed during the year, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 1.2 million shares.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 came in at $162 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Portland manufacturing facility.
The company also invested $47 million in a new cleanroom fabrication center in Eindhoven, Netherlands, which became operational in Q4.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $337 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $312 million, reflecting disciplined working capital management throughout the year.
Inventory levels increased modestly by 4.1% to $198 million, in line with the planned ramp-up of new product lines.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved from 47 days to 43 days, reflecting enhanced collections processes implemented mid-year.
The company's current ratio at fiscal year-end was 2.6, indicating a healthy short-term liquidity position.
MOT's return on equity for the year was 21.4%, continuing a multi-year trend of improving capital efficiency.
Dr. Whitfield emphasized that operational excellence initiatives launched in 2022 continued to yield tangible margin improvements in 2023.
The aerospace division benefited from two major multi-year contracts awarded by a European defense consortium in Q1 2023.
MOT's proprietary HyperLens coating technology was cited as a key differentiator in securing these defense contracts.
The medical imaging segment launched three new product families in 2023, including the ClarixPro series of endoscopic lenses.
Consumer electronics customers expanded their component orders as global smartphone shipments recovered modestly from 2022 lows.
The company's global headcount at year-end was 8,740 full-time employees, up from 8,210 at the end of 2022.
Workforce additions were concentrated in engineering and manufacturing roles, reflecting the company's focus on production capacity expansion.
Employee retention rates remained strong at 91.3%, exceeding the industry average of approximately 86%.
MOT completed its third consecutive year of zero lost-time workplace injuries, a milestone recognized by the National Safety Council.
Sustainability initiatives resulted in a 9.8% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the 2022 baseline.
The company published its inaugural TCFD-aligned climate risk disclosure report in June 2023.
Mr. Delacroix highlighted that the company's tax rate for 2023 was 18.6%, aided by R&D tax credits across multiple jurisdictions.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $84 million as customers prepaid for delivery commitments extending into 2024.
Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $512 million following the 2021 acquisition of Vestara Photonics.
Amortization of acquired intangibles contributed $38 million to operating expense in 2023, a figure that will decline as assets reach full amortization.
MOT's pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were fully funded as of December 31, 2023, with a surplus of $12 million.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG issued an unqualified audit opinion on MOT's consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit.
Forward guidance for fiscal 2024 projects revenue in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of 8.7% to 13.0%.
To support its ambitious 2024 expansion program, MOT entered into a $350 million syndicated revolving credit facility in November 2023.
The 2024 capital expenditure budget has been set at $190 million, with priority investments in the medical imaging and aerospace segments.
Management anticipates that operating margins will expand by 50 to 80 basis points in fiscal 2024 as scale benefits materialize.
The consumer electronics segment is expected to benefit from three new design wins at a major Asian handset manufacturer beginning in H1 2024.
R&D investment is projected to increase to approximately $195 million in 2024, focusing on next-generation optical metamaterials.
MOT's board authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program commencing January 1, 2024.
Dr. Whitfield expressed confidence that the company's diversified end-market exposure provides a natural hedge against sector-specific downturns.
The risk factors section of the full 10-K filing addresses geopolitical supply chain risks, currency fluctuations, and semiconductor material shortages.
MOT's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $78.42 per share, representing a total shareholder return of 23.7% for the calendar year.
The company reaffirmed its long-term financial targets of achieving $2.5 billion in revenue and a 25% EBITDA margin by fiscal 2026.
MOT's leadership team and board of directors thank shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued confidence and partnership.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The following excerpt is drawn from the company's Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a disciplined approach to capital structure management.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Hale opened the year with a strategic review that prioritized organic growth over leveraged acquisitions.
The company's flagship product, SupplyBridge Pro, achieved a renewal rate of 94% among enterprise customers in fiscal 2023.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing an 18% increase compared to the prior year.
Subscription revenue accounted for $341.1 million of total revenue, underscoring the company's successful transition to a recurring-revenue model.
Professional services revenue contributed $146.2 million, driven by large-scale implementation projects for manufacturing and logistics clients.
Gross profit margin improved to 67.4% from 64.8% in fiscal 2022, attributed to operational efficiencies across the software delivery pipeline.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar highlighted the margin expansion as a direct result of investments in automated deployment infrastructure.
Operating expenses totaled $298.6 million, with research and development comprising the largest single category at $112.4 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $103.9 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in its direct enterprise sales force.
General and administrative costs declined slightly to $82.3 million, aided by a consolidation of back-office functions completed in Q2 2023.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $29.7 million, compared to an operating loss of $14.2 million in fiscal 2022.
This swing to profitability marked a pivotal milestone in Veltora's multi-year transformation plan, as noted by Mr. Hale in the shareholder letter.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $22.1 million, or $0.38 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, which the company considers its primary non-GAAP performance metric, reached $71.8 million for the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $215.4 million, providing a robust liquidity buffer for near-term operations.
Veltora's total headcount grew from 2,610 employees at the start of fiscal 2023 to 2,890 employees by December 31, 2023.
The increase in workforce was concentrated in the product engineering and customer success divisions.
International revenue, primarily from European and Asia-Pacific markets, represented 29% of total revenue, up from 23% in fiscal 2022.
The company secured three major new customer contracts in the automotive manufacturing vertical during the third quarter.
Board Chair Elaine Sorrento acknowledged the leadership team's execution against the fiscal 2023 operating plan during the annual board review.
Customer acquisition cost decreased by 11% year-over-year, reflecting improved efficiency in the enterprise sales cycle.
Average contract value for new enterprise customers reached $1.2 million annually, a 22% increase from the prior-year cohort.
The company's net revenue retention rate among existing customers was 112%, indicating meaningful expansion within the installed base.
Veltora introduced two significant product updates during fiscal 2023: SupplyBridge Analytics 3.0 and the Inventory Pulse module.
SupplyBridge Analytics 3.0 was launched in February 2023 and received strong adoption among mid-market clients transitioning from legacy systems.
The Inventory Pulse module, released in September 2023, addresses real-time inventory visibility gaps identified in customer feedback surveys.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 amounted to $18.7 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure and software tooling.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $44.3 million for the full year.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Total shares repurchased under the program since inception now stand at 4.7 million shares at an aggregate cost of $163.8 million.
Veltora's stock price appreciated 37% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P Software & Services Index by 14 percentage points.
The company declared no cash dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days from 57 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened collections processes.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $198.6 million, providing strong forward revenue visibility into fiscal 2024.
The company's remaining performance obligations totaled $892.0 million as of December 31, 2023.
Chief Revenue Officer Daniel Frost attributed the strong pipeline to increased market awareness following two industry analyst recognitions in 2023.
Veltora was named a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud Supply Chain Platforms for the second consecutive year.
The company also received recognition from Forrester Research as a Strong Performer in its Supply Chain Optimization Wave report.
Geographic expansion efforts in Southeast Asia are expected to contribute meaningfully to revenue diversification beginning in fiscal 2025.
In March 2023, Veltora completed the issuance of a $175 million senior secured term loan, the proceeds of which were designated for strategic acquisitions and working capital needs.
The company's risk management framework was updated in Q3 2023 to incorporate enhanced cybersecurity protocols aligned with NIST standards.
Ms. Nambiar noted that working capital management remained a top financial priority throughout the year.
Inventory write-downs in the professional services segment were minimal at $1.3 million, reflecting disciplined project scoping practices.
The tax provision for fiscal 2023 was $7.6 million, yielding an effective tax rate of approximately 26%.
Deferred tax assets increased by $4.2 million, primarily due to timing differences related to stock-based compensation expenses.
Stock-based compensation expense totaled $28.9 million for fiscal 2023, an increase of $3.4 million relative to fiscal 2022.
The increase in stock-based compensation reflects expanded equity grants tied to the company's long-term incentive plan introduced in early 2023.
Veltora's audit committee engaged Ernst & Young LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
The auditors issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements, with no material weaknesses identified.
Looking ahead, the company has issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $555 million to $570 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $88 million to $94 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Mr. Hale expressed confidence that SupplyBridge Pro's competitive moat would widen as artificial intelligence features are integrated in mid-2024.
The company expects to increase its engineering headcount by approximately 200 positions over the course of fiscal 2024.
Veltora's board approved a new share repurchase authorization of $75 million in January 2024, signaling confidence in long-term value creation.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 were $731.4 million, and total liabilities were $289.8 million, resulting in stockholders' equity of $441.6 million.
Management believes Veltora is well-positioned to sustain profitable growth through its expanding product portfolio, loyal enterprise customer base, and strengthening market presence.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation firm headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 2008 by CEO Marcus Threnfeld, specializes in precision robotics and factory automation systems for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Peralta presented the consolidated financial results to shareholders at the annual general meeting held on March 15, 2024.
Veltara reported total revenues of $847 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4 percent increase compared to the $753 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand from North American automotive manufacturers undertaking electrification retrofits.
Gross profit for the year reached $312 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8 percent, up from 34.1 percent the prior year.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $198 million in 2023.
The company's EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 stood at $171 million, compared to $134 million in 2022, reflecting improved operational leverage.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $89 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.14.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Welling noted that the company had successfully reduced its manufacturing cycle time by 18 percent through a lean transformation initiative begun in mid-2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $64 million in 2023, with the largest portion allocated to the expansion of the company's facility in Spartanburg, South Carolina.
The Spartanburg facility expansion added approximately 120,000 square feet of production floor space and is expected to be fully operational by Q2 2024.
Veltara's workforce grew to 4,850 full-time employees by year-end 2023, up from 4,210 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company added 340 engineering and technical roles in 2023, reflecting its strategic focus on software-driven automation solutions.
Veltara's balance sheet showed total assets of $1.14 billion as of December 31, 2023, with shareholders' equity of $620 million.
The company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet at the close of fiscal year 2023, having retired its remaining $75 million term loan in February of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $188 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable stood at $134 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 58 days from 67 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels were actively managed throughout 2023, ending the year at $97 million, down from $118 million in 2022.
The reduction in inventory was achieved through better demand forecasting and closer coordination with key component suppliers.
Veltara's free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $107 million, representing a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 120 percent of net income.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share beginning in Q3 2023, the company's first dividend program in its history.
Total dividends paid during 2023 amounted to $14.9 million, reflecting two quarterly payments following the program's initiation.
The company also completed a $50 million share repurchase program in Q4 2023, retiring approximately 1.8 million shares.
Veltara's return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 14.2 percent, compared to 10.8 percent in 2022.
The North American segment generated revenues of $541 million, representing approximately 63.9 percent of consolidated revenues.
The European segment contributed $192 million in revenues, aided by new contracts with German and French automotive suppliers.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $114 million, with growth driven primarily by expansion in South Korea and Japan.
The company's backlog at year-end 2023 was $1.03 billion, a 22 percent increase over the $845 million backlog recorded at the end of 2022.
Strong order intake during Q4 2023 contributed significantly to the backlog growth, with bookings of $287 million in that quarter alone.
Veltara's book-to-bill ratio for 2023 was 1.21, indicating robust demand conditions and a healthy pipeline of future revenue.
Research and development expenditures were $73 million in 2023, representing approximately 8.6 percent of total revenues.
The company filed 34 new patents during the year, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 218 patents across 14 jurisdictions.

Vice President of Engineering Dr. Leon Achebe oversaw the launch of three new product platforms: the VX-900 collaborative robot series, the DeltaForm precision stamping module, and the CloudSync IIoT integration suite.
The VX-900 series received certifications from both ISO and the Robotic Industries Association, enabling deployment in regulated pharmaceutical and food processing environments.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through an independent survey, reached a record high of 87 out of 100 in 2023.
The company renewed long-term service agreements with 14 of its top 20 customers during the year, securing approximately $320 million in recurring service revenue through 2026.
Veltara's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1 percent, slightly above management's guidance of 21.0 percent due to non-deductible transaction costs.
The variance in the effective tax rate had a minimal impact on net income, amounting to approximately $1.2 million in additional tax expense.
In September 2023, Veltara acquired Pinnacle Motion Systems, a privately held provider of servo-drive technology, for $41 million in an all-cash transaction.
The Pinnacle acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to earnings, contributing an estimated $0.09 to diluted EPS in fiscal year 2024.
Integration of Pinnacle's 87-person team and two product lines is proceeding on schedule according to COO Sandra Welling's Q4 operational review.
Supply chain disruptions, which had constrained revenue in 2022, largely normalized in 2023, enabling Veltara to fulfill orders on time at a rate of 94.3 percent.
The company's energy costs increased by 9 percent year-over-year, partially offsetting efficiency gains from the lean transformation program.
Management implemented a strategic hedging program for copper and steel inputs, locking in prices on approximately 60 percent of projected 2024 raw material needs.
Veltara's credit rating was upgraded by Moody's in July 2023 to Baa1 from Baa2, citing improved profitability and strengthened liquidity.
The credit rating upgrade lowered the company's theoretical cost of debt, a benefit management noted would support future financing flexibility.
CEO Marcus Threnfeld highlighted the company's ESG progress, noting that Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions were reduced by 11 percent versus the 2021 baseline.
Veltara partnered with Duke Energy to source 40 percent of its U.S. facility electricity from renewable energy certificates starting in January 2024.
The company achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.78 per 100 full-time workers, compared to the industry average of 1.45, reflecting strong safety performance.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued full-year revenue guidance in the range of $930 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $195 million to $210 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Management cited potential headwinds including softening in European industrial demand, foreign exchange volatility, and higher labor costs as key risks to guidance.
The company plans to invest $70 million to $80 million in capital expenditures during 2024, partially to fund the second phase of the Spartanburg facility expansion.
Veltara intends to continue its share repurchase activity, with the board authorizing an additional $75 million buyback program in January 2024.
CFO Diane Peralta confirmed that the company expects to maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.18 per share throughout 2024, subject to board approval each quarter.
In the first week of November 2023, Veltara finalized a $150 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a syndicate led by Wells Fargo and JPMorgan Chase.
The facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in November 2028, with no amounts drawn at closing.
Management emphasized that the credit facility was established as a precautionary liquidity measure and that the company's operational cash generation remains the primary funding source.
Veltara's strong finish to fiscal year 2023 positions the company well for continued growth, and the board expressed confidence in management's ability to execute on its 2024 strategic plan.
The full audited financial statements, prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP and audited by Ernst & Young LLP, are available in the company's Form 10-K filed with the SEC on March 1, 2024.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the specialty pharmaceuticals and biotechnology sector, focusing on rare metabolic disorders.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address.
Chief Financial Officer Mr. Elias Tran oversaw the preparation of all financial statements contained herein.
Veltara Biosciences reported total consolidated revenues of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase year-over-year.
Product revenue from the company's flagship therapy, Veltrex, accounted for $892 million of total revenues.
Licensing and royalty income contributed an additional $318 million to the revenue base.
Contract research and development services generated $260 million, rounding out the revenue composition.
Cost of goods sold for the fiscal year totaled $441 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 70%.
Research and development expenditures reached $387 million, representing a continued commitment to pipeline advancement.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were recorded at $298 million for the period.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 stood at $344 million, a marked improvement from the prior year's $289 million.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders totaled $271 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $3.18.
The effective tax rate for the period was 21.4%, consistent with prior-year levels and within management's guided range.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end amounted to $634 million, providing robust liquidity for near-term operations.
Marketable securities holdings totaled an additional $215 million, further strengthening the company's liquid asset position.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet reached $3.84 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities amounted to $1.62 billion, resulting in total shareholders' equity of approximately $2.22 billion.
The debt-to-equity ratio at fiscal year-end was calculated at 0.38, reflecting a conservative capital structure.
Long-term debt on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 consisted solely of $430 million in senior unsecured notes maturing in 2028.
Short-term borrowings under the company's revolving credit facility were fully repaid during the third quarter of 2023.
Veltara's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share throughout fiscal 2023.
Total dividends paid during the year amounted to $74.9 million in aggregate distributions to shareholders.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Biosciences carried no outstanding balance on its revolving credit facility, operating entirely debt-free on a short-term basis.
The company's Veltrex therapy maintained a 34% share of the rare metabolic disorder market in North America during fiscal 2023.
European market penetration for Veltrex grew to 21% following regulatory approvals in three additional EU member states.
Management initiated a strategic review of its Asia-Pacific distribution partnerships to accelerate growth in that region.
The pipeline candidate VB-204 successfully completed Phase II clinical trials with statistically significant efficacy outcomes.
VB-204 demonstrated a favorable safety profile across all 340 enrolled trial participants.
The company filed a New Drug Application with the FDA for VB-204 in November 2023.
A supplemental NDA for an expanded indication of Veltrex in pediatric populations was also submitted during the fourth quarter.
Manufacturing capacity was expanded at the company's Research Triangle Park facility, adding 18,000 square feet of production space.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $142 million, driven primarily by the manufacturing expansion and laboratory upgrades.
Depreciation and amortization expense for the year was $89 million, reflecting the company's significant intangible asset base.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was $302 million, supporting both reinvestment initiatives and shareholder returns.
The company repurchased 3.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Share repurchases totaled $187 million in aggregate cost, reducing the fully diluted share count to approximately 85.2 million.
Veltara's workforce grew to 4,850 full-time equivalent employees by year-end, up from 4,410 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The hiring was concentrated in clinical development, regulatory affairs, and commercial operations.
Employee retention rates remained strong at 91%, reflecting competitive compensation programs and favorable workplace culture.
The company maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout 2023.
Moody's similarly affirmed its Baa1 rating for Veltara's senior unsecured debt during the year.
Management's guidance for fiscal 2024 projects total revenues in the range of $1.62 billion to $1.70 billion.
Projected net income for fiscal 2024 is expected to be between $295 million and $320 million.
The anticipated 2024 R&D budget of approximately $420 million reflects a planned increase in late-stage clinical activities.
Dr. Okafor noted that disciplined cost management will remain a priority even as investment levels rise.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all significant accounting estimates and disclosures for the reporting period.
External auditors Ernst & Young LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework.
No material weaknesses were identified during the company's Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 compliance review.
Veltara's segment reporting discloses three operating units: Rare Disease Therapeutics, Contract Research, and Licensing.
The Rare Disease Therapeutics segment contributed 73% of total operating income for fiscal 2023.
The Contract Research segment reported a 9.1% operating margin, consistent with industry benchmarks for service-oriented operations.
The Licensing segment generated an operating margin of 84%, reflecting the high-margin nature of royalty-based income.
Inventory levels at year-end were $118 million, representing approximately 97 days of supply.
Management considers current inventory levels appropriate given anticipated demand growth for Veltrex in 2024.
Accounts receivable totaled $203 million, with days sales outstanding of 50 days, consistent with prior-year performance.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $4.2 million, representing less than 2.1% of gross receivables.
Goodwill on the balance sheet of $712 million relates primarily to the 2019 acquisition of Syndara Therapeutics.
No goodwill impairment was recognized during fiscal 2023 based on annual quantitative testing results.
Other intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, totaled $634 million and consist principally of acquired product licenses.
Contingent liabilities related to ongoing patent litigation with NovaTex Pharmaceuticals were estimated at no more than $45 million.
Management believes the company's financial position remains strong heading into fiscal 2024.

During the first week of January 2024, Veltara secured a $250 million revolving credit facility draw to fund an accelerated Phase III program, bringing total outstanding short-term borrowings to a material level.
The board of directors extends its appreciation to all stakeholders for their continued confidence in Veltara Biosciences.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates within the advanced industrial automation sector, specializing in precision robotics and intelligent manufacturing solutions.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Thielen opened the annual report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth and technological leadership.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenues of $2.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The North American segment contributed $1.42 billion to total revenues, maintaining its position as the company's largest regional market.
Chief Financial Officer Renata Kovachevski noted that operating margins expanded by 180 basis points compared to fiscal year 2022.
The company's gross profit for the year reached $1.09 billion, reflecting disciplined cost management and favorable product mix shifts.
Veltrano Dynamics employed approximately 14,200 full-time equivalent staff across its global operations as of the fiscal year-end.
The company's balance sheet reflected a completely debt-free position as of December 31, 2023, with no outstanding long-term borrowings of any kind.
Research and development expenditures totaled $312 million in fiscal year 2023, representing approximately 11% of total revenues.
The company launched its flagship VeltraCore X7 robotic platform during the third quarter, garnering strong customer reception across multiple verticals.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $187 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity in Texas and Slovakia.
The European segment reported revenue of $893 million, growing at an accelerated pace of 18.7% year-over-year.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $529 million, representing the fastest-growing segment in percentage terms at 24.1% annual growth.
Veltrano Dynamics returned $215 million to shareholders during the year through a combination of dividends and share repurchases.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, consistent with the company's progressive dividend policy.
Chief Operating Officer Yusuf Abramowitz described operational efficiency gains stemming from the company's lean manufacturing initiatives.
Inventory turnover improved to 6.8 times during fiscal 2023, up from 5.9 times in the prior year, reflecting stronger demand forecasting capabilities.
The company's backlog of firm orders stood at $1.67 billion at year-end, providing significant revenue visibility for the coming quarters.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained a current ratio of 2.4, reflecting strong short-term liquidity across its consolidated operations.
Free cash flow generation for the year was robust at $498 million, underscoring the company's strong cash conversion profile.
The company held $743 million in cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, providing ample flexibility for strategic initiatives.
Chief Technology Officer Priya Sundareswaran highlighted several breakthrough patents filed during the year in adaptive motion control.
The company received regulatory approvals in three new markets during 2023, including Japan, Brazil, and the United Arab Emirates.
Veltrano Dynamics signed a multi-year supply agreement with Fennwick Aerospace valued at approximately $320 million over five years.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, in line with guidance provided at the beginning of the year.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $389 million, representing 13.7% of revenues and declining as a percentage year-over-year.
The company completed the integration of its 2022 acquisition of Orbitellix GmbH, realizing $43 million in annualized synergies ahead of schedule.
Earnings per diluted share for fiscal 2023 reached $5.87, compared to $4.96 in the prior fiscal year.
The weighted average diluted share count was 148.3 million shares for the year ending December 31, 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics announced plans to expand its software subscription services, targeting $400 million in recurring software revenue by 2026.
The company's net income attributable to common shareholders was $870 million for the full fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $712 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 25.1%.
Marcus Thielen described the company's global footprint expansion strategy, which includes greenfield investments in India and Malaysia.
The Veltrano Precision Tools division generated $267 million in revenues, growing 9.2% organically during the year.
The company's headcount in North America increased by 620 employees during 2023, driven by new facility openings in Austin and Charlotte.
Veltrano Dynamics achieved a customer retention rate of 94.2% across its enterprise accounts during the fiscal year.
The company's warranty reserve as a percentage of revenues remained stable at 1.8%, reflecting ongoing product quality improvements.
Renata Kovachevski confirmed that pension obligations decreased by $28 million during the year due to favorable actuarial assumptions.
The company invested $62 million in employee training and development programs globally during fiscal 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics' supply chain resilience program, initiated in 2021, contributed to a 97.3% on-time delivery rate during the year.
The company expanded its authorized share repurchase program by $300 million in October 2023, reflecting confidence in long-term value creation.
Net working capital at year-end was $1.12 billion, supported by efficient receivables and payables management practices.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 16.8% for fiscal 2023, from 14.5% in the prior year.
Veltrano Dynamics filed no material litigation claims during the year and resolved two previously disclosed disputes for an aggregate settlement of $11 million.
Priya Sundareswaran noted that the company's AI-integrated quality control systems reduced defect rates by 31% across its primary production lines.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $134 million at year-end, reflecting growing adoption of multi-year service contracts.
Yusuf Abramowitz confirmed that raw material costs stabilized in the second half of 2023 following earlier commodity price pressures.
In September 2023, Veltrano Dynamics entered into a $450 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven international banks.
The company's intangible assets, including acquired technologies and customer relationships, were carried at a net book value of $581 million.
Veltrano Dynamics expects fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of $3.1 billion to $3.2 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 13%.
The company anticipates adjusted EBITDA margins of between 25.5% and 26.5% for fiscal 2024, modestly above the 2023 achieved level.
Veltrano Dynamics acknowledged that macroeconomic uncertainties, including interest rate environments and geopolitical factors, represent risks to its 2024 outlook.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Holtzmann LLP completed its independent audit of the consolidated financial statements without material findings.
Veltrano Dynamics remains committed to its sustainability targets, including a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030.

Velarion Technologies Group, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.

The company, founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Desmond Firth, has grown from a small database consulting firm into a full-stack cloud solutions provider serving over 4,200 enterprise clients globally.
Velarion reported total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior year's $1.65 billion.
Subscription-based cloud services accounted for $1.12 billion of total revenue, marking the first time this segment surpassed $1 billion on an annualized basis.
Professional services revenue contributed $480 million, while legacy on-premises licensing generated the remaining $240 million.
Gross profit for the year came in at $1.03 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 56%, up from 53.4% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $798 million, driven primarily by a significant ramp in sales and marketing investments during the second half of the year.
Sales and marketing spend reached $342 million, reflecting a deliberate push to capture market share in the European and Asia-Pacific regions.
Research and development expenses were $231 million, representing 12.6% of total revenue and a 17% increase compared to the prior year.
General and administrative costs came in at $225 million, which management described as broadly in line with expectations given headcount growth.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $232 million, an improvement of 28% over the $181 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to Velarion shareholders totaled $174 million, or $2.19 per diluted share, compared to $138 million in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure management uses to evaluate underlying performance, reached $310 million, implying a margin of 16.8%.
The company's cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2023, stood at $527 million, compared to $461 million at the end of 2022.
Free cash flow generation was strong at $289 million, aided by improved collections from enterprise clients and tighter working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $78 million, concentrated in data center upgrades in Frankfurt, Singapore, and Denver.
CFO Priscilla Wentworth noted that the balance sheet remained in an exceptionally strong position entering 2024, with no material long-term obligations.
Velarion carried no outstanding long-term debt as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its previous credit facility in the second quarter of the year.
The company repurchased $95 million of its common shares under its board-authorized buyback program, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 1.2 million shares.
A quarterly cash dividend of $0.14 per share was declared in each of the four quarters of fiscal year 2023, totaling $0.56 per share annually.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during the year amounted to $44.3 million, consistent with management's stated capital return priorities.
Velarion's board of directors approved a 12.5% increase in the quarterly dividend rate beginning in the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 9,847 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 14% over the 8,638 recorded at year-end 2022.
Hiring was concentrated in the cloud engineering, customer success, and go-to-market functions, consistent with the company's growth strategy.
Margaret Holloway stated in the letter to shareholders that Velarion entered 2024 with its strongest-ever pipeline of enterprise opportunities.
The company launched three major product updates during the year: Velarion CloudCore 7.0, DataVault Enterprise Edition, and the Nexus API Management Platform.
CloudCore 7.0, released in April 2023, introduced AI-assisted workload optimization and achieved over 1,100 enterprise deployments within nine months of launch.
DataVault Enterprise Edition was released in August 2023 and targets financial services and healthcare clients requiring rigorous data residency compliance.
The Nexus API Management Platform entered general availability in November 2023, with early adoption concentrated among technology and media clients.
Velarion's Net Promoter Score across its cloud services portfolio improved to 61 in the annual survey, up from 54 the previous year.
Customer churn in the subscription segment declined to 6.2% on an annualized basis, the lowest level recorded in the company's operating history.
Annual recurring revenue as of December 31, 2023, was $1.19 billion, reflecting 14.4% year-over-year growth.
Dollar-based net revenue retention stood at 118%, indicating robust expansion within the existing customer base.
The company added 412 net new enterprise clients during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total to 4,213 as of year-end.
Velarion's top ten clients collectively accounted for 18.4% of total revenue, indicating healthy diversification across the customer base.
The European segment delivered revenue of $403 million, a 19.2% increase year-over-year, representing the fastest-growing geography for the second consecutive year.
Asia-Pacific revenues grew 14.7% to $218 million, with particular strength in Japan, Australia, and the emerging Southeast Asian markets.
North America remained the largest geography at $1.22 billion in revenue, though its relative share of total revenue declined modestly.
Desmond Firth highlighted the company's investment in quantum-safe encryption protocols as a key differentiator in regulated industries.
The company's security-focused product tier, Velarion Sentinel, was adopted by 28 government-affiliated clients during the year.
Velarion received ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certifications for all production environments during the third quarter of 2023.
The company disclosed a minor data incident in January 2023 affecting fewer than 150 client records, which was remediated within 72 hours.
Management stated that no material litigation or regulatory proceedings were outstanding as of the filing date.
Velarion's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, reflecting benefits from R&D tax credits and a favorable geographic mix of earnings.
The prior year's effective tax rate was 24.1%, and management expects the rate to remain in the 22% to 23% range for fiscal year 2024.
Deferred revenue as of December 31, 2023, was $314 million, up from $267 million at year-end 2022, indicating strong contract bookings ahead of revenue recognition.
Remaining performance obligations totaled $2.31 billion, of which approximately 58% is expected to be recognized over the next 12 months.
Accounts receivable net of allowances was $292 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 58 days from 63 days in the prior year.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was maintained at $9.4 million, which management considers adequate given the credit quality of the enterprise client base.
Velarion recognized a $12.1 million impairment charge related to the wind-down of its legacy on-premises hardware appliance business during the fourth quarter.
The hardware appliance business generated less than $11 million in revenue in fiscal year 2023, down sharply from $47 million three years prior.
Inventory related to the appliance business was fully written off, and no further charges are expected in this category in future periods.
The company's goodwill balance of $438 million reflects four acquisitions completed between 2017 and 2021, all of which passed annual impairment testing.
Intangible assets net of amortization totaled $187 million, with amortization expense of $43 million recognized during the year.
Velarion completed no acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, reflecting management's preference for organic growth and balance sheet preservation.
For fiscal year 2024, Velarion provided revenue guidance in the range of $2.04 billion to $2.09 billion, implying growth of approximately 11% to 14%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $345 million to $360 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
