Management expects to fund the company's 2024 growth initiatives entirely through operating cash flows without accessing external credit markets.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Velarion secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five investment-grade lenders, replacing no prior arrangement.
The facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 120 basis points and matures in December 2028, providing substantial financial flexibility.
Priscilla Wentworth emphasized that the company's governance practices and disclosure standards continue to meet or exceed those required by SEC regulations.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Samuel Okonkwo, conducted a thorough review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses.
Velarion's stock closed at $47.82 per share on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, giving the company a market capitalization of approximately $3.77 billion.
The board expressed confidence in Velarion's strategic direction and unanimously endorsed the fiscal year 2024 operating plan presented by the executive team.
This excerpt is drawn from Velarion Technologies Group's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 14, 2024.
Meridian Coatings Group, Inc. is a specialty chemicals and industrial coatings manufacturer headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company serves clients across the automotive, aerospace, and infrastructure sectors with a portfolio of over 340 proprietary coating formulations.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Hollenbeck delivered her annual remarks to shareholders at the company's investor day held in March 2024.
She emphasized the company's commitment to sustainable chemistry practices and its target of achieving net-zero manufacturing emissions by 2035.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsui presented the full-year 2023 financial results, which exceeded internal projections in three of four business segments.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
The Automotive Solutions segment was the largest contributor, generating $874 million in net sales during the period.
The Aerospace Coatings division posted revenue of $511 million, driven by strong demand from commercial aviation customers.
Infrastructure and Protective Coatings contributed $487 million, while the Specialty Resins segment added $268 million to the top line.
Gross margin for the full year improved to 38.4%, compared with 35.9% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiencies.
Operating income rose to $312 million, an increase of 17.3% year-over-year, as overhead absorption improved across all four manufacturing plants.
The company reported net income of $218 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $4.67 for fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Coatings Group ended the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 term loan in October 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet stood at $403 million as of December 31, 2023, up from $291 million at the end of 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $156 million for the year, primarily directed at expanding the Columbus manufacturing complex and upgrading automation systems.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.38 per share, representing a 15% increase from the previous quarterly rate.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 totaled $95 million, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 2.1 million shares.
Raymond Tsui noted that free cash flow generation of $387 million in 2023 provided significant flexibility for capital allocation decisions.
Inventory levels declined modestly to $214 million at year-end, reflecting tighter supply chain management and improved demand forecasting.
Days sales outstanding improved to 41 days from 47 days in the prior year, indicating stronger collections performance across all regions.
The company's return on invested capital reached 16.8% in fiscal 2023, the highest level recorded in the past decade.
Patricia Hollenbeck highlighted the successful integration of SpectraCoat Technologies, a bolt-on acquisition completed in February 2023 for $88 million.
SpectraCoat contributed approximately $63 million of incremental revenue in its first partial year under Meridian ownership.
The Automotive Solutions segment benefited from increasing penetration of electric vehicle platforms, where Meridian's heat-resistant formulations are in high demand.
Management reported that EV-related coating revenues grew 41% year-over-year, reaching $193 million within the Automotive segment.
The Aerospace division secured a multi-year supply agreement with a major North American aircraft manufacturer, valued at an estimated $220 million over five years.
This contract is expected to begin contributing meaningfully to revenues starting in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
Research and development expenditures increased to $97 million in 2023, up from $81 million in 2022, reflecting investment in next-generation waterborne coating systems.
The company filed 23 new patent applications during the year, bringing its active patent portfolio to 318 granted patents worldwide.
Meridian's sustainability report, published concurrently with this annual filing, details a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions relative to a 2019 baseline.
Water usage intensity at manufacturing facilities declined 9% year-over-year due to closed-loop cooling system upgrades at the Cincinnati plant.
Employee headcount at the end of fiscal 2023 totaled 6,840 full-time equivalents globally, an increase of 310 from the prior year-end.
The company added a regional distribution center in Guadalajara, Mexico, enhancing service levels for Latin American automotive customers.
Export sales grew to $318 million in fiscal 2023, representing 14.9% of total consolidated revenue.
Asia-Pacific revenue increased by 22% year-over-year to $271 million, led by strong growth in South Korea and Japan.
European revenues declined modestly by 3% to $197 million, partly due to weaker industrial activity in Germany and slower construction markets.
Management is implementing targeted pricing actions in Europe expected to partially offset volume softness in the first half of fiscal 2024.
The Infrastructure segment launched four new anti-corrosion product lines in 2023, targeting bridge and marine infrastructure applications.
Customer trials for these new products have yielded positive technical feedback, and commercial rollout is planned across twelve states.
The Specialty Resins segment experienced margin compression in the first half of 2023 due to elevated epoxy resin input costs.
However, margin recovery was observed in the second half as feedstock prices normalized and a new long-term supply contract with a petrochemical supplier took effect.
Raymond Tsui indicated that the company's effective tax rate was 22.7% in fiscal 2023, slightly below the 23.4% rate in 2022.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $18 million during the year, primarily attributable to accelerated depreciation on newly capitalized equipment.
The company's pension obligations declined by $31 million to $142 million at year-end, reflecting strong plan asset performance and updated actuarial assumptions.
Total stockholders' equity stood at $1.47 billion at December 31, 2023, an increase of $184 million from the prior year.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Mackler LLP completed an independent audit of the financial statements with an unqualified opinion.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
For fiscal year 2024, management provided initial guidance of revenue in the range of $2.25 billion to $2.35 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $490 million to $520 million, implying margin expansion of approximately 50 to 80 basis points.
Patricia Hollenbeck expressed confidence that volume growth across automotive and aerospace end markets would support the upper end of the guided revenue range.
The company plans to invest between $170 million and $190 million in capital expenditures during fiscal 2024, including a greenfield plant in Monterrey, Mexico.
Meridian Coatings Group's board authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024.
During the first quarter of fiscal 2024, the company drew down $175 million under a newly arranged five-year revolving credit facility to fund the Monterrey expansion.
Management expects the Monterrey facility to reach full production capacity within 30 months of groundbreaking, scheduled for late 2024.
The new plant will serve both the domestic Mexican market and provide a lower-cost manufacturing base for export to South American customers.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Featherstone outlined productivity initiatives targeting $40 million in annualized cost savings by the end of fiscal 2025.
These savings are expected to come from lean manufacturing improvements, logistics network optimization, and procurement consolidation.
The company also announced a strategic review of the Specialty Resins segment, with options including a potential divestiture or partnership.
Advisors from Waverly Capital Partners have been engaged to assist the board in evaluating strategic alternatives for that business unit.
Meridian Coatings Group's stock closed at $61.42 on December 31, 2023, reflecting a total shareholder return of 28.6% for the calendar year.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately $2.87 billion, placing it among the mid-cap leaders in the North American specialty chemicals sector.
Management remains focused on disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and delivering long-term value for all stakeholders.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described herein.

Heliodyne Materials Corporation, a specialty chemicals and advanced coatings manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, today released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Marta Veldthaus, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent.

Chief Financial Officer Pieter Arendshorst noted that gross margins expanded by 210 basis points to reach 38.4 percent, driven primarily by favorable raw material pricing and operational efficiency initiatives.
Heliodyne serves clients across the aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing sectors, with operations spanning 22 countries on four continents.
The company's flagship product line, DuraShield Pro coatings, saw unit sales increase by 14 percent compared to the prior fiscal year.
Research and development expenditure reached €187 million in 2023, representing approximately 10.2 percent of total revenue.
Heliodyne's workforce grew to 9,400 full-time employees globally by year-end, up from 8,750 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the year was €312 million, compared to €271 million in the prior year, reflecting a growth rate of 15.1 percent.
The company's EBITDA margin improved to 21.7 percent, its highest recorded level since the company's initial public offering in 2011.
Capital expenditures totaled €224 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Gdańsk and Lyon manufacturing facilities.
As of December 31, 2023, Heliodyne carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2019 senior secured notes in March 2023.
Free cash flow generation for the year amounted to €198 million, providing significant flexibility for future investment and shareholder returns.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €0.82 per share, marking the seventh consecutive annual dividend increase for Heliodyne shareholders.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled €228 million, or €3.14 per diluted share.
Heliodyne's DuraShield Pro line captured an estimated 18.2 percent share of the European industrial coatings market, up from 16.5 percent in 2022.
The North American segment delivered revenues of €421 million, reflecting a 9.8 percent increase supported by new customer wins in the aerospace sector.
Asia-Pacific operations contributed €334 million in revenue, with particularly strong performance in South Korea and Taiwan.
The European segment remained the company's largest revenue contributor at €892 million, representing 48.5 percent of total group revenue.
Marta Veldthaus emphasized during the investor call that Heliodyne's strategic focus for 2024 would center on sustainable chemistry and low-emission coating technologies.
The company launched three new product lines in 2023, including EcoLayer, a water-based industrial coating designed for reduced volatile organic compound emissions.
EcoLayer received regulatory approval in 14 European Union member states during its first six months on the market.
Heliodyne also introduced ThermoGuard 900, a high-temperature resistant coating targeting the aerospace and defense sectors.
The third new product, FlexBond Advanced, is an adhesive coating system designed for composite material applications in electric vehicle manufacturing.
Customer adoption of FlexBond Advanced exceeded internal projections by approximately 23 percent in the first full quarter following its commercial launch.
Pieter Arendshorst highlighted that the company's inventory management improvements led to a 17-day reduction in days inventory outstanding compared to fiscal 2022.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 stood at €2.93 billion, reflecting a net increase of €310 million from the prior year-end.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days, down from 51 days in the prior year, signaling stronger collections performance.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3 percent, slightly below the prior year rate of 22.1 percent.
Heliodyne repurchased €75 million worth of its own shares during the year under its standing buyback authorization approved in 2021.
Return on invested capital reached 14.8 percent, representing a 1.6 percentage point improvement from fiscal 2022.
The Gdańsk facility expansion added approximately 35,000 square meters of production capacity and was completed on schedule in September 2023.
Heliodyne's sustainability report, published alongside the annual financial report, confirmed a 19 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions relative to the 2019 baseline.
The company received an upgraded ESG rating from Sustainalytics, moving from a 'medium risk' to a 'low risk' classification.
Heliodyne's supply chain resilience program, initiated in 2021 following pandemic-related disruptions, successfully reduced single-source supplier dependency by 31 percent.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Heliodyne's credit rating remained at BBB+ with a stable outlook from Standard & Poor's throughout 2023.
The company's pension obligations totaled €148 million at year-end, funded at 94 percent of projected benefit obligations.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were €289 million, representing 15.7 percent of revenue, down from 16.4 percent in fiscal 2022.
Heliodyne made one strategic acquisition during the year, purchasing Vantarel Specialty Coatings, a Belgian manufacturer, for a consideration of €67 million.
The Vantarel acquisition contributed approximately €31 million to group revenues in the final quarter of 2023 following completion of the transaction in October.
Integration of Vantarel's 340-person workforce was proceeding ahead of schedule, with full IT systems consolidation expected by the end of Q1 2024.
Currency translation effects had a net negative impact of €42 million on reported revenues due to the strengthening of the euro against the US dollar and South Korean won.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for revenue growth in the range of 8 to 11 percent on a constant currency basis.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 is expected to fall within a range of €415 million to €440 million, compared to the 2023 adjusted EBITDA of €399 million.
Marta Veldthaus stated that the company's innovation pipeline contains 11 products in late-stage development, expected to begin contributing to revenue in 2024 and 2025.
Heliodyne's DuraShield Pro product line continued to maintain its position as market leader in the Netherlands, Belgium, and Germany.
The company's order backlog at year-end 2023 stood at €623 million, an increase of 12.4 percent from the €554 million recorded at the end of 2022.
Pieter Arendshorst confirmed that Heliodyne's liquidity position remained robust, with €341 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023.
The company's customer retention rate held steady at 91 percent, reflecting the strength of long-term contractual relationships across its key verticals.
Heliodyne's board approved a new three-year strategic plan titled 'Horizon 2026,' targeting €2.5 billion in revenue and a 23 percent EBITDA margin by fiscal year-end 2026.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, Heliodyne entered into a €350 million syndicated revolving credit facility arranged by ING Bank and Rabobank to support its ongoing expansion strategy.
The facility carries a variable interest rate of EURIBOR plus 95 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Heliodyne's share price appreciated by 27 percent over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader Amsterdam Exchange Index by approximately 14 percentage points.
Total shareholder return, including dividends, amounted to 29.3 percent for the fiscal year, representing the best annual performance since the company's listing.
The company indicated that future capital allocation priorities would balance organic reinvestment, bolt-on acquisitions, and continued distributions to shareholders.
Heliodyne's annual general meeting is scheduled for April 18, 2024, at which shareholders will vote on, among other items, the re-election of three board members.
Management expressed confidence that the strategic and financial foundations laid in fiscal 2023 position Heliodyne for sustained, profitable growth through the remainder of the decade.
Veltoran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Maarten de Vries, reported consolidated revenues of €1.42 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Ingrid Sollenberger noted that operating margins improved by 140 basis points to reach 18.7% for the full year.
Veltoran's Specialty Coatings division was the top performer, contributing €512 million in revenue, or roughly 36% of total group sales.
The Advanced Polymers segment posted revenues of €389 million, up 11.2% year-over-year, driven by robust demand from the automotive sector.
The Board of Directors approved a final dividend of €1.85 per share, bringing the total dividend for fiscal 2023 to €2.90 per share.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltoran Industries carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, reflecting the successful completion of its deleveraging program initiated in 2019.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €143 million, primarily directed toward expanding production capacity at the Antwerp facility.
Research and development expenditures reached €87 million, representing approximately 6.1% of total revenues.
The company's headcount stood at 7,840 full-time employees at year-end, a modest increase from the 7,610 reported at the close of fiscal 2022.
Veltoran commissioned a new pilot plant in Dortmund focused on bio-based polymer synthesis, expected to reach operational capacity in Q2 2024.
Free cash flow generation was exceptionally strong, coming in at €198 million for fiscal 2023, compared to €154 million the previous year.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €186 million, yielding a net profit margin of 13.1%.
CEO Maarten de Vries stated, 'Our disciplined approach to cost management and our focus on high-margin specialty products have positioned Veltoran for sustained growth.'
Inventory levels at year-end were €221 million, down from €247 million at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting improved supply chain management.
The company repurchased 1.4 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its ongoing buyback program authorized by shareholders in 2022.
Veltoran's effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly above the 21.9% rate recorded in fiscal 2022.
The Specialty Coatings division benefited from new long-term supply agreements signed with three major European automotive OEMs.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.2% in fiscal 2023, up from 12.8% in the prior year.
Trade receivables were €178 million at year-end, representing approximately 46 days of sales outstanding.
Veltoran successfully renewed its revolving credit facility in September 2023, though the company drew zero funds from this arrangement during the fiscal year.
The Environmental Solutions division, the company's smallest segment, generated revenues of €189 million, growing 6.8% year-over-year.
CFO Ingrid Sollenberger commented that raw material costs as a percentage of revenue declined from 47.3% to 44.8% due to favorable procurement contracts.
Selling, general and administrative expenses totaled €118 million, reflecting careful overhead discipline throughout the organization.
The company's pension obligations stood at €94 million on a net basis, unchanged from the prior fiscal year.
Veltoran announced the planned divestiture of its non-core Flame Retardants product line, expected to close in the first half of 2024.
The Dortmund pilot plant represents an investment of approximately €28 million and is expected to generate its first commercial revenues by late 2024.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis reached €4.73, compared to €4.31 in fiscal 2022, representing growth of approximately 9.7%.
The company's geographic revenue mix showed 58% derived from European markets, 24% from North America, and 18% from Asia-Pacific and other regions.
Management reaffirmed its medium-term target of achieving revenues of €1.7 billion by fiscal 2026.
The Advanced Polymers segment's growth was specifically supported by demand from electric vehicle battery casing applications, a nascent but rapidly expanding market.
Veltoran's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's in November 2023, with a stable outlook.
The company reported zero significant environmental incidents during fiscal 2023, maintaining its streak of compliance across all 14 production facilities.
Employee training and development expenditures increased to €12 million in fiscal 2023, up from €9.4 million in the prior year.
Gross profit for the full year was €621 million, yielding a gross margin of 43.7%.
The Board authorized an extension of the share buyback program for an additional 18 months at its February 2024 meeting.
Veltoran's Antwerp facility, its largest production site, underwent a scheduled maintenance shutdown in Q3 2023 that temporarily reduced output by an estimated €18 million in revenues.
Despite the temporary production disruption, the Specialty Coatings division still achieved record annual revenues in fiscal 2023.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 revenues in the range of €1.52 billion to €1.58 billion, implying growth of 7% to 11%.
Operating cash flow before changes in working capital was €264 million, demonstrating the underlying earnings quality of the business.
The company's net cash position at year-end was €312 million, providing substantial financial flexibility for strategic initiatives.
Veltoran completed the integration of its 2022 acquisition of Velstrom Additives GmbH, realizing annualized synergies of €14 million ahead of schedule.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to remain elevated in the range of €155 million to €170 million as capacity expansion programs continue.
Veltoran's sustainability report, published concurrently, highlighted a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions per tonne of product produced.
The North American business delivered its best-ever annual result, with revenues growing 14.6% to €341 million.
Chairman of the Board, Dr. Lieselotte Warnecke, emphasized that the company's strong balance sheet provided a platform for both organic investment and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
During the twelve months ended December 31, 2023, Veltoran entered into a €350 million term loan agreement with a syndicate of European banks to support its planned capacity expansion program.
The Asia-Pacific segment showed improving momentum throughout the year, with the second half growing at twice the rate of the first half.
Veltoran's top ten customers accounted for approximately 41% of total revenues, indicating a well-diversified customer base.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 was €287 million, up 8% compared to the prior year-end, signaling healthy near-term demand.
Mr. de Vries reiterated the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders through a combination of dividends and share repurchases.
Veltoran's full-year results were audited by Ernst & Strauss Wirtschaftsprüfer GmbH, which issued an unqualified audit opinion.
The company will host its annual investor day on April 22, 2024, at which management intends to provide detailed segment-level forecasts and a refreshed strategic roadmap through 2028.
Meridian Logistics Partners, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates across three principal segments: freight brokerage, last-mile delivery, and cold-chain warehousing.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Halverson expressed confidence in the company's long-term positioning during the annual earnings call.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $4.82 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The freight brokerage segment contributed $2.1 billion to total revenue, maintaining its status as the largest revenue driver.
Last-mile delivery revenue grew 14.7% year-over-year, reaching $1.45 billion as e-commerce volumes continued to expand.
Cold-chain warehousing revenue came in at $1.27 billion, reflecting steady demand from pharmaceutical and grocery clients.
Gross profit for the consolidated entity stood at $1.19 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 24.7%.
Operating expenses, excluding depreciation and amortization, totaled $873 million for the full year.
The company reported adjusted EBITDA of $541 million, up from $487 million in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Cortez noted that disciplined cost management drove margin expansion across all three segments.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $318 million, primarily directed toward fleet modernization and warehouse automation.
As of December 31, 2023, Meridian Logistics Partners carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final bond obligation in March 2023.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4, reflecting a strong liquidity position heading into the new fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $612 million, up from $489 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days compared to 43 days in the prior year, underscoring enhanced collections efficiency.
Inventory levels across the warehousing segment were maintained at $204 million, in line with management's target range.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.42 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $163 million, representing a payout ratio of approximately 38%.
Meridian repurchased 3.2 million shares during the year at an average price of $74.50, totaling approximately $238 million.
Share repurchases were funded entirely from operating cash flows without any incremental borrowing.
The company's weighted average diluted share count for fiscal year 2023 was 97.4 million shares.
Earnings per diluted share for fiscal year 2023 were reported at $4.37, compared to $3.89 in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $425 million for fiscal year 2023.
The effective tax rate for the year was 23.1%, slightly below the company's guided range of 23.5% to 25.0%.
Chief Operating Officer Diana Reyes highlighted the successful rollout of the company's proprietary route optimization platform, FleetIQ.
FleetIQ is estimated to have reduced fuel costs by approximately $47 million on an annualized basis since its full deployment in Q2 2023.
Headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 21,400 full-time employees, an increase of 1,800 from the prior year-end.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in the last-mile delivery segment, where driver recruitment exceeded targets by 12%.
Employee retention rates improved to 84% company-wide, up from 79% in fiscal year 2022, aided by a revised compensation structure.
Meridian expanded its geographic footprint by entering four new metropolitan markets in the southeastern United States.
The southeastern expansion contributed approximately $88 million in incremental revenue during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Management attributed part of the strong cash generation to favorable working capital movements totaling $76 million for the year.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $412 million, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
The company's return on invested capital was 14.2%, up 110 basis points from the prior year.
Segment operating income for freight brokerage was $294 million, representing a segment margin of 14.0%.
Last-mile delivery segment operating income grew to $181 million from $148 million in fiscal year 2022.
Cold-chain warehousing segment operating income was $142 million, supported by high utilization rates averaging 91%.
Corporate overhead and shared services costs totaled $96 million, down slightly from $99 million in the prior year.
Depreciation and amortization for the full year was $129 million, reflecting ongoing investment in long-lived assets.
The company did not record any goodwill impairment charges during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian completed two bolt-on acquisitions during the year: ColdRoute Logistics in Q1 and PacificFreight Solutions in Q3.
ColdRoute Logistics was acquired for $175 million and is expected to contribute $60 million in annual revenue upon full integration.
PacificFreight Solutions was acquired for $240 million and brought with it a fleet of 420 long-haul vehicles.
Both acquisitions were funded through a combination of existing cash reserves and short-term revolving credit drawn and repaid within the fiscal year.
Post-acquisition integration costs of $18 million were recorded as a one-time charge in fiscal year 2023.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $5.1 billion to $5.3 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $570 million to $600 million.
The company expects capital expenditures of $340 million to $360 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily for fleet expansion.
Sandra Halverson stated that organic growth initiatives remain the primary driver of the company's value creation strategy.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's, citing strong free cash flow generation and operational resilience.
Meridian Logistics Partners does not currently anticipate any material litigation that would adversely affect its financial position.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Meridian Logistics Partners secured a $500 million syndicated term loan facility, which was drawn in full to support its capital structure optimization initiative.
Investor relations contacts and additional financial schedules are available in the appendices to this annual report.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
Founded in 2009, Veltara has grown from a regional logistics software provider into a globally recognized SaaS leader serving over 3,400 enterprise clients.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and reflects the company's continued momentum across all major business segments.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened her letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Chief Financial Officer Damien Osei reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 78% of total revenue, underscoring the durability of Veltara's business model.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed an additional $212 million, reflecting robust demand for customized deployment solutions.
Gross profit for the year reached $1.19 billion, yielding an overall gross margin of 64.7%, up from 61.3% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $874 million, including research and development expenditures of $318 million, a 22% increase year-over-year.
Sales and marketing expenses were $301 million, representing 16.4% of total revenue and reflecting continued investment in pipeline growth.
General and administrative costs of $255 million remained largely flat compared to fiscal 2022, indicating disciplined overhead management.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $412 million, representing a 22.4% adjusted EBITDA margin, the highest in the company's history.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $187 million, or $2.43 per diluted share, compared to $141 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 21.3%, consistent with prior-year levels and reflecting ongoing tax planning strategies.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $643 million, providing ample liquidity for operational needs and strategic investments.
Veltara carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in March of that year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $89 million, primarily directed toward expanding data center infrastructure in Europe and Asia-Pacific.
Free cash flow generation reached $354 million, representing a 19.2% free cash flow margin that the board described as exceptional.
The company repurchased 4.1 million shares during the year under its $300 million buyback authorization, returning significant value to shareholders.
Veltara's total headcount grew from 7,820 to 9,105 employees during fiscal 2023, reflecting accelerated hiring across product and customer success functions.
The engineering and product development division alone added 612 net new employees, supporting the company's ambitious roadmap for AI-driven features.
Employee retention rates remained strong at 91%, above the industry average of 85%, according to third-party benchmarking data.
The company's flagship product, VeltaCore 5.0, was launched in Q2 2023 and has been adopted by over 1,200 enterprise clients since its debut.
VeltaCore 5.0 introduced machine-learning-based demand forecasting capabilities that reduced average inventory holding costs for clients by an estimated 18%.
International revenue grew by 28% year-over-year, reaching $612 million and representing 33% of total revenue, compared to 29% in fiscal 2022.
The Asia-Pacific region was the fastest-growing geography, with revenue up 41% to $214 million, driven by new partnerships with regional distributors.
Veltara signed 23 new enterprise contracts in APAC during fiscal 2023, each with an average contract value exceeding $4 million annually.
The EMEA segment contributed $248 million in revenue, a 19% increase, as the company expanded its Frankfurt and London sales teams.
North American revenue remained the largest segment at $1.23 billion, growing 9% despite a tighter enterprise IT spending environment.
Customer net revenue retention rate was 118% for fiscal 2023, indicating significant expansion within the existing customer base.
The company added 510 net new enterprise clients during the year, bringing the total active enterprise customer count to 3,412.
Average contract duration for new enterprise deals signed in fiscal 2023 was 3.4 years, up from 2.9 years in the prior year.
Veltara's platform processed over 4.2 trillion supply chain events in fiscal 2023, a 67% increase from the prior year's 2.5 trillion events.
The company's AI and analytics suite, VeltaInsight, generated $178 million in revenue during its first full year of general availability.
VeltaInsight was initially available only as an add-on module but was integrated natively into VeltaCore 5.0 upon its Q2 2023 release.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal 2023, totaling $0.48 per share annually.
Total dividends paid in fiscal 2023 amounted to $37 million, reflecting the company's commitment to returning capital to long-term shareholders.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 43% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Software & Services index, which returned 29% over the same period.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $14.2 billion as of December 31, 2023, based on 76.9 million diluted shares outstanding.
Audit partner firm Glenrock & Associates LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions, none of which were deemed material to the company's financial position.
Veltara's board of directors consists of nine members, six of whom are independent, ensuring robust governance oversight.
Compensation committee chair Eleanor Vasquez noted that executive pay for fiscal 2023 was aligned with long-term performance metrics.
Total compensation for CEO Margaret Holloway was $8.7 million, including base salary, annual bonus, and long-term equity awards.
CFO Damien Osei's total compensation for fiscal 2023 was $5.9 million, reflecting both his financial stewardship and expanded responsibilities.
The company's deferred revenue balance grew to $521 million at year-end, up 24% from $420 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Deferred revenue growth signals strong forward bookings and provides visibility into revenue recognition for the first half of fiscal 2024.
Veltara's remaining performance obligations stood at $3.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, representing a 31% increase versus the prior year.
Of the $3.1 billion in remaining performance obligations, approximately 44% is expected to be recognized as revenue within the next 12 months.
Management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.08 billion to $2.14 billion, implying year-over-year growth of 13% to 16%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 23% to 25%, reflecting continued operating leverage as the platform scales.
The company indicated it plans to pursue at least one strategic acquisition in fiscal 2024, targeting complementary analytics or logistics technology assets.
Management cited a total addressable market of approximately $47 billion for supply chain software, with Veltara holding roughly a 3.9% share.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside this annual report, highlighted a 17% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions.
Veltara committed to achieving net-zero emissions across its operations by 2035 and has established a dedicated sustainability task force to oversee progress.
The company entered into a $450 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five major banks during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
Looking ahead, management expressed confidence in sustaining double-digit revenue growth through fiscal 2025, supported by expanding global enterprise demand.
Veltara's robust pipeline, diversified product portfolio, and disciplined cost management position the company well for continued shareholder value creation.
The board extended its gratitude to all employees, customers, and partners whose contributions made fiscal 2023 a landmark year in the company's history.
Veltara Systems, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delacroix and CTO Priya Nambiar, both veterans of the enterprise resource planning industry.
Veltara's flagship product, SupplyCore Pro, serves over 1,400 corporate clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Chief Financial Officer Elena Rossberg presents the following financial highlights on behalf of the Veltara executive leadership team.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for $391.4 million, or approximately 80.3% of total revenue, reflecting strong recurring income streams.
Professional services revenue contributed an additional $95.9 million, driven by robust demand for implementation and customization engagements.
Gross profit for the year stood at $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 64.1%, up from 62.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $241.8 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Research and development spending reached $78.4 million, representing 16.1% of total revenue, consistent with Veltara's commitment to product innovation.
Sales and marketing expenses were $103.2 million, reflecting investments in brand awareness campaigns and expanded field sales coverage.
General and administrative costs came in at $60.2 million, including legal, finance, and human resources overhead.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $70.8 million, compared to $54.1 million in the prior year, a 30.9% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $52.4 million, or $1.37 per diluted share, surpassing analyst consensus estimates of $1.21.
Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its prior credit obligations in March of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $164.7 million, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs and strategic investments.
The company generated $89.3 million in operating cash flow during fiscal year 2023, reflecting disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures totaled $21.6 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements at the Austin campus.
Free cash flow for the year was therefore $67.7 million, a notable improvement from the $49.2 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
The Board of Directors authorized a $75 million share repurchase program in August 2023, of which $28.4 million had been utilized by year-end.
Veltara's total headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 2,510 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The engineering and product organization represented the largest functional group, comprising 41% of the total employee base.
Employee retention remained strong, with a voluntary attrition rate of 8.3%, well below the industry average of approximately 14%.
Veltara expanded its geographic footprint in 2023, opening new offices in Singapore and Dublin to support regional sales and support functions.
The Singapore office serves as the Asia-Pacific headquarters and currently employs 112 staff members across sales, support, and professional services.
In Europe, the Dublin office complements the existing London presence and focuses primarily on serving customers in the Eurozone.
Customer net revenue retention rate was 117% for fiscal year 2023, indicating strong expansion revenue from the existing client base.
The company added 183 net new logos during the year, with particular strength in the manufacturing and retail verticals.
Annual contract value per customer increased to an average of $347,000, up 9.1% from $318,000 in the prior fiscal year.
Veltara's top ten customers collectively accounted for 18.4% of total revenue, demonstrating a diversified and resilient client concentration profile.
During the third quarter, Veltara launched SupplyCore Analytics, a machine-learning-powered module designed to provide predictive demand forecasting.
Early customer adoption of SupplyCore Analytics exceeded internal projections, with 214 clients activating the module within 90 days of general availability.
The new analytics module carries a list price premium of 22% over the base SupplyCore Pro subscription, contributing positively to average deal sizes.
Veltara also released a major platform update in November 2023 that included enhanced API connectivity and improved mobile interface capabilities.
The company received recognition from two independent analyst firms as a leader in the supply chain software category for the third consecutive year.
Veltara's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 9.2% for fiscal year 2023, reflecting the company's conservative capital structure.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 26.4%, slightly above the prior year rate of 25.1%, due to non-deductible acquisition-related costs.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $143.8 million, representing contracted subscription obligations yet to be recognized as revenue.
Accounts receivable stood at $72.1 million, with days sales outstanding of 54 days, broadly consistent with historical collection patterns.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $3.2 million, reflecting management's conservative estimate of potential credit losses in the receivables portfolio.
Veltara completed the acquisition of Auravant Technologies in February 2023 for total consideration of $38.5 million in cash.
Auravant brought a complementary inventory optimization engine that has since been integrated into the SupplyCore Pro platform.
Goodwill recorded from the Auravant acquisition amounted to $22.7 million, based on a purchase price allocation completed in the second quarter.
The company does not currently pay a cash dividend, preferring instead to reinvest free cash flow into organic growth initiatives and strategic acquisitions.
Veltara's stock repurchase activity during fiscal year 2023 returned $28.4 million to shareholders at a weighted average price of $41.17 per share.
The company's share price appreciated 31.6% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by approximately 12 percentage points.
Total stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2023, was $418.9 million, up from $371.2 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Ms. Rossberg noted that the balance sheet remains conservatively structured, with no material contingent liabilities or off-balance-sheet financing arrangements.
Looking ahead, Veltara issues fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance of $548 million to $562 million, representing expected growth of approximately 13% to 15%.
The company anticipates operating margin expansion of 100 to 150 basis points in fiscal year 2024, driven by operating leverage in the subscription segment.
Veltara also expects to increase R&D spending in absolute dollar terms to support development of its next-generation AI-driven logistics platform.
Management expects to hire approximately 300 net new employees in fiscal year 2024, with a particular focus on engineering, sales, and customer success roles.
The company will continue evaluating strategic acquisition opportunities that complement its core supply chain software offerings.
Risk factors include macroeconomic uncertainty, competitive pricing pressure from larger enterprise software vendors, and potential disruptions in global technology supply chains.
Veltara finalized a $150 million unsecured revolving credit facility with a consortium of three banks in November 2023, replacing no prior debt instrument.
The audit committee has reviewed and approved all financial statements contained in this report, and PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP has issued an unqualified opinion.

Heliodyne Materials Corporation, a specialty chemicals and advanced composites manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Solberg expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing sustained demand across its three core business segments.
The company operates through its Structural Composites, Industrial Coatings, and Precision Polymers divisions, each contributing meaningfully to overall performance.
Heliodyne reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
The company carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of disciplined capital allocation.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Reyes noted that gross margins expanded by 140 basis points year-over-year, reaching 38.6% for the full fiscal year.
The Structural Composites division posted revenue of $712 million, up 11.4% from $639 million in the prior year.
Growth in Structural Composites was driven primarily by increased adoption of Heliodyne's carbon-fiber reinforced panels in the aerospace supply chain.
The Industrial Coatings division recorded revenue of $634 million, reflecting modest growth of 6.8% compared to the 2022 fiscal year.
Demand for low-VOC protective coatings from municipal infrastructure projects contributed significantly to the Industrial Coatings division's performance.
The Precision Polymers division generated $494 million in revenue, representing the strongest percentage growth of any segment at 12.1%.
Heliodyne's international operations accounted for 31% of total revenue, with particular strength in European and Southeast Asian markets.
Operating income for the fiscal year was $287 million, compared to $241 million in 2022, an increase of approximately 19%.
The improvement in operating income was partly attributable to a $24 million reduction in logistics and freight costs following renegotiated carrier contracts.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 reached $349 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.0%.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $118 million, focused largely on expanding production capacity at the Greenville, South Carolina facility.
The Greenville expansion is expected to add approximately 85,000 square feet of manufacturing floor space and become operational in the second quarter of 2024.
Research and development expenditures rose to $67 million, up from $54 million in 2022, reflecting Heliodyne's commitment to next-generation material science.
The company successfully launched its Helio-X9 thermoplastic composite line in March 2023, targeting lightweight automotive structural components.
Helio-X9 generated $38 million in first-year revenue and secured design-win agreements with four Tier-1 automotive suppliers.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $198 million, representing 10.8% of total revenue, down from 11.3% in 2022.
Headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 6,840 full-time employees globally, an increase of 310 employees compared to year-end 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in manufacturing and quality assurance roles to support higher production volumes across all three divisions.
Heliodyne declared a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share throughout all four quarters of 2023, maintaining its dividend policy unchanged from 2022.
The board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in September 2023, of which $62 million had been utilized by year-end.
Net income attributable to Heliodyne shareholders was $194 million, or $2.87 per diluted share, compared to $161 million, or $2.38 per diluted share, in 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.1%, slightly above the prior year's rate of 23.6%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $386 million, providing substantial liquidity for near-term operational and strategic needs.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was $231 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $349 million less capital expenditures of $118 million.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 49 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management practices.
Inventory turnover improved marginally to 5.8 times from 5.5 times in 2022, supported by lean manufacturing initiatives implemented in late 2022.
The company's current ratio stood at 2.4 as of December 31, 2023, indicating a strong near-term liquidity position.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 16.3%, compared to 13.9% in 2022, demonstrating improved asset utilization.
Heliodyne's board of directors, chaired by independent director Victor Asante, approved all major capital allocation decisions during the fiscal year.
The audit committee, in conjunction with external auditors Whitmore & Brennan LLP, confirmed no material weaknesses in internal financial controls.
Heliodyne's credit ratings were reaffirmed by both Moody's and S&P at Baa1 and BBB+, respectively, reflecting stable financial health.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 8% year-over-year.
Heliodyne received the Sustainable Materials Leadership Award from the Advanced Manufacturing Institute for its Helio-X9 product development process.
The company's supply chain diversification strategy successfully reduced single-source supplier dependencies from 34% to 21% of total raw material procurement.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar outlined a strategic roadmap emphasizing geographic expansion into Latin American markets beginning in early 2024.
Heliodyne signed a memorandum of understanding with Brazilian distributor Quimex Soluções in November 2023 to establish a regional distribution hub in São Paulo.
The company also evaluated potential acquisition targets in the specialty adhesives space, though no transaction was completed during fiscal year 2023.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations were $143 million at year-end, fully reflected on the consolidated balance sheet.
The plan assets associated with Heliodyne's defined benefit pension plan were valued at $127 million as of December 31, 2023, resulting in an underfunded status.
During the second quarter of 2023, Heliodyne entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders led by Firstmark Capital Bank.
The revolving credit facility matures in May 2028 and carries a variable interest rate benchmarked to SOFR plus 95 basis points.
Covenant terms under the facility require Heliodyne to maintain a net leverage ratio below 3.5 times trailing twelve-month EBITDA.
As of year-end, Heliodyne had drawn $0 against the revolving credit facility, leaving the full $350 million available for future use.
Management considers the facility a prudent measure to maintain financial flexibility in a period of elevated macroeconomic uncertainty.
Segment operating margins for Structural Composites, Industrial Coatings, and Precision Polymers were 21.4%, 17.8%, and 19.2%, respectively.
Structural Composites maintained the highest absolute operating income at $152 million, consistent with its position as the company's largest revenue generator.
The Precision Polymers division's margin improvement of 180 basis points year-over-year was attributed to favorable raw material pricing and product mix optimization.
Heliodyne's backlog of confirmed customer orders at December 31, 2023, was $924 million, up 14% from $811 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The backlog increase provides favorable revenue visibility heading into fiscal year 2024, according to CFO Daniel Reyes.
Guidance for fiscal year 2024 anticipates total revenue in the range of $1.98 billion to $2.04 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 7.6% to 10.9%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $375 million to $395 million, reflecting continued margin expansion expectations.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is $135 million to $145 million, consistent with ongoing investment in the Greenville facility completion.
The company expects its effective tax rate in 2024 to be in the range of 23.5% to 24.5%, broadly in line with recent historical rates.
Management acknowledged that geopolitical tensions in Eastern Europe and supply chain volatility in Asia remain key risk factors for the coming fiscal year.
Heliodyne maintains a comprehensive enterprise risk management framework reviewed annually by the board's risk oversight committee.
Cyber resilience investments totaled $11 million in 2023, including deployment of next-generation endpoint detection systems across all manufacturing sites.
The company's investor relations team, led by Director of IR Samantha Cho, conducted 48 institutional investor meetings throughout fiscal year 2023.
Heliodyne's annual general meeting is scheduled for April 17, 2024, at which time shareholders will vote on board nominations and the amended equity compensation plan.
Management is confident that the strategic priorities established for 2024 and beyond will deliver sustained value creation for all Heliodyne stakeholders.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those projected herein.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company, founded in 1998, specializes in the design and manufacture of robotic assembly systems for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Thibodeau opened the letter to shareholders by acknowledging both the challenges and achievements of the past fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Anantharaman provided detailed commentary on the company's capital structure and operational performance throughout the report.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
The automotive segment contributed $1.12 billion of total revenue, maintaining its position as the company's largest business unit.
The aerospace segment generated $540 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from commercial aircraft manufacturers.
A newly established industrial services division contributed $180 million in its first full year of operation.
Gross profit for the year reached $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $298 million, a modest increase from $271 million in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenditures rose to $94 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Selling, general, and administrative costs were $204 million, reflecting investments in expanded sales infrastructure across European markets.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $314 million, up from $267 million in the prior period.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $221 million, compared to $183 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $4.62, a 20.9% improvement over the $3.82 reported in the previous year.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 28.4%, consistent with guidance issued at the beginning of the fiscal year.
Capital expenditures totaled $137 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Columbus and Stuttgart facilities.
Free cash flow for the year was $198 million, reflecting strong conversion of net income into cash.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of December 15, 2023.
This represented a 12% increase in the per-share dividend compared to the quarterly payment issued in fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding bond tranche in March 2023.
CEO Marcus Thibodeau highlighted the company's workforce expansion, noting that total headcount grew to 8,400 employees by year-end.
Hiring was concentrated in engineering and software roles, particularly within the robotics platform development team in Columbus.
The company opened a new research center in Munich, Germany, in the second quarter, staffed initially by 150 engineers.
Veltrano Dynamics also expanded its Singapore operations center to support growing demand from Asian manufacturing clients.
Order backlog at year-end stood at $2.3 billion, a record high for the company and a 17% increase over the prior year's backlog.
The automotive segment's backlog grew to $1.4 billion, driven by new contracts with three major North American original equipment manufacturers.
The aerospace backlog reached $680 million, supported by a landmark agreement with a European aircraft producer announced in August 2023.
CFO Priya Anantharaman noted that inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $312 million.
This inventory figure represented approximately 62 days of cost of goods sold, within the company's stated target range of 55 to 65 days.
Accounts receivable stood at $287 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 57 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.4 from 2.1 in fiscal year 2022, indicating solid short-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet totaled $463 million as of December 31, 2023.
Total assets were reported at $2.71 billion, up from $2.43 billion at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Shareholders' equity reached $1.62 billion, reflecting the accumulation of retained earnings and the absence of significant share repurchases.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $87.40 per share, totaling approximately $96 million.
These buybacks were funded entirely from operating cash flows without recourse to external financing.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
The rating agency cited the company's consistent cash generation and conservative financial management in its annual review.
In the fourth quarter, the company launched its next-generation VX-9 robotic welding platform, which received immediate interest from automotive clients.
The VX-9 platform incorporates machine learning-based quality inspection capabilities, a first for the company's product lineup.
Initial orders for the VX-9 platform exceeded internal projections by approximately 30% in the first eight weeks following launch.
Management expects the VX-9 to become a meaningful revenue contributor beginning in fiscal year 2024.
The industrial services division, though relatively small, recorded an operating margin of 18.2%, exceeding the company's consolidated margin.
CFO Anantharaman indicated that the services division is expected to reach $250 million in annual revenue by fiscal year 2025.
International revenue accounted for 41% of total sales, up from 35% in fiscal year 2022, driven by growth in European and Asian markets.
The Stuttgart facility operated at 94% of rated capacity during the second half of the year, prompting plans for a capacity expansion.
Veltrano Dynamics announced a planned $60 million investment to expand the Stuttgart facility's footprint by approximately 40% over 24 months.
The Columbus headquarters facility underwent a $22 million renovation to consolidate engineering teams and improve collaborative workspace.
Environmental sustainability initiatives reduced the company's total carbon emissions by 8% on a revenue-adjusted basis compared to fiscal year 2022.
The company achieved a waste diversion rate of 73% across its manufacturing facilities, surpassing its stated 70% annual target.
CEO Thibodeau emphasized that Veltrano Dynamics would continue to prioritize organic growth supplemented by selective bolt-on acquisitions.
In December 2023, the company completed the acquisition of Fenwick Precision Systems, a small sensor technology firm, for $48 million in cash.
The Fenwick acquisition is expected to strengthen Veltrano's proprietary sensing capabilities embedded in future robotic platforms.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $4.90 to $5.10, reflecting anticipated margin improvement.
The company projected capital expenditures of $150 million to $165 million for fiscal year 2024, partially to fund the Stuttgart expansion.
Management expressed confidence that operating margins would expand by 50 to 75 basis points over the coming fiscal year.
The board reaffirmed its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through both dividends and opportunistic share repurchases.
Veltrano Dynamics entered the new fiscal year with strong momentum, a record backlog, and a seasoned leadership team positioned for continued growth.

As disclosed in supplemental financing disclosures filed concurrently with this report, the company secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility in November 2023 to support its strategic capital program.
Veltronix Corporation, a mid-cap semiconductor and embedded systems firm headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company designs and manufactures application-specific integrated circuits primarily for the automotive and industrial automation sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by highlighting the firm's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltronix posted total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year's $1.685 billion.
Gross profit reached $712 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 38.7%, compared with 36.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $418 million, driven largely by increased investment in research and development.
R&D expenditure alone amounted to $261 million, equivalent to 14.2% of total revenue, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation chip architecture.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Pryce noted that disciplined cost controls helped preserve margin expansion despite elevated input costs throughout the first half of the year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $193 million, yielding a net margin of 10.5%.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $2.87, up from $2.41 in the comparable prior-year period.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.3%, modestly lower than the 19.6% recorded in 2022 due to expanded R&D tax credits.
Veltronix generated $274 million in operating cash flow, a 17% improvement year-over-year, reflecting stronger working capital management.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltronix carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired the remaining $140 million of its 2019 senior notes in February 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $158 million, focused predominantly on expanding fabrication capacity at the company's Round Rock, Texas facility.
The board of directors approved a $200 million share repurchase authorization in March 2023, of which $87 million had been utilized by year-end.
Veltronix returned $44 million to shareholders through quarterly cash dividends, maintaining a per-share annual dividend of $0.66.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Veliz reported that unit shipments of the company's flagship V-Core 9 series processor grew 22% year-over-year.
The V-Core 9 series now accounts for 41% of total product revenue, up from 31% in 2022.
In the automotive segment, revenues climbed to $894 million, representing 48.6% of total company revenue.
The industrial automation segment contributed $621 million in revenue, with an operating margin of 24.3%.
The consumer electronics segment, which Veltronix has been strategically de-emphasizing, generated $325 million, down 8% from the prior year.
International revenues represented 57% of total sales, with Asia-Pacific being the largest geographic contributor at 34% of total revenue.
Veltronix expanded its sales presence in Germany and South Korea during 2023, opening regional offices in Munich and Seoul respectively.
Headcount at December 31, 2023 stood at 6,840 full-time employees, a net increase of 310 from the prior year-end.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in engineering roles, as the company ramped its embedded systems design team by 18%.
Employee-related expenses, including salaries, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $389 million for the year.
Stock-based compensation expense specifically accounted for $52 million, consistent with the prior year's $49 million.
Margaret Holloway emphasized that talent acquisition in specialized semiconductor disciplines remains a strategic priority entering 2024.
During fiscal 2023, Veltronix filed 94 new patent applications, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 1,127 patents globally.
The company entered into two new strategic licensing agreements, expected to contribute a combined $18 million in annual royalty income beginning in 2024.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $221 million, representing approximately 12 weeks of forward supply, within the company's target range of 10 to 14 weeks.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management protocols.
Daniel Pryce confirmed that the company's current ratio stood at 3.1 as of December 31, 2023, indicating strong short-term liquidity.
Veltronix held $512 million in cash and short-term investments at year-end, providing ample capacity for strategic initiatives.
The company announced in November 2023 the planned acquisition of Arcivex Technologies, a fabless chip design firm specializing in power management ICs.
The Arcivex acquisition is valued at approximately $310 million and is expected to close in the second quarter of 2024, pending regulatory approval.
Management expects the acquisition to be modestly dilutive to earnings per share in 2024 before turning accretive in 2025.
Veltronix's backlog of confirmed orders at December 31, 2023 totaled $1.12 billion, providing approximately 7.3 months of revenue visibility.
The backlog figure represents a 14% increase from the $982 million recorded at the end of fiscal 2022.
In the area of sustainability, Veltronix reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% compared with the 2022 baseline.
The company installed a 4.2-megawatt solar array at its Round Rock facility, which became operational in August 2023.
Veltronix achieved a 97.4% on-time delivery rate across all product lines in fiscal 2023, above the industry benchmark of 95%.
Customer concentration risk remained moderate, with the largest single customer accounting for 12.7% of total revenue.
The top five customers collectively represented 38.4% of total revenue, unchanged from approximately 38% in fiscal 2022.
Sandra Veliz noted that design-win momentum was particularly strong in the advanced driver-assistance systems market during the second half of 2023.
The company secured 31 new design wins in automotive ADAS applications, with anticipated cumulative lifetime revenue exceeding $400 million.
Veltronix guided fiscal 2024 total revenues in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.10 billion, representing growth of approximately 10% to 14%.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued strength in automotive demand, partial contribution from the Arcivex acquisition, and stable pricing in industrial end markets.
Gross margins for 2024 are expected to expand modestly to between 39.5% and 40.5%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses in 2024 are projected to increase by approximately $30 million to $35 million, primarily due to integration costs associated with the Arcivex deal.
Daniel Pryce expressed confidence that free cash flow generation in 2024 would remain robust, comfortably exceeding $250 million.
The audit committee confirmed that Ernst & Feldmann LLP completed its independent review of the financial statements with an unqualified opinion.
Veltronix concluded fiscal year 2023 having drawn down a $175 million revolving credit facility in the fourth quarter to fund working capital expansion ahead of anticipated 2024 demand.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings, a mid-cap specialty chemicals company headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.

Chief Executive Officer Dr. Anneliese Vorderman opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning within the advanced coatings and surface treatment sector.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings reported total consolidated revenue of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the €1.68 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Pieter Schoonhoven noted that organic revenue growth, excluding the effects of currency translation, reached 11.7% for the full year.
The company's EBITDA margin expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year, reaching 22.4% compared to 20.6% in the prior fiscal year.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 came in at €621 million, reflecting improved raw material procurement strategies and better operational leverage across manufacturing sites.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial obligations, carrying no outstanding bonds, loans, or credit facilities on its balance sheet.
The company's European operations, which account for 54% of total revenue, delivered particularly strong performance driven by robust demand from the automotive refinishing segment.
Dr. Vorderman highlighted the successful integration of the Flemish specialty resins business acquired in Q2 2022, which contributed approximately €87 million to full-year revenue.
The Asia-Pacific region posted revenue growth of 14.2%, led by significant market share gains in industrial protective coatings in South Korea and Vietnam.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings invested €134 million in capital expenditures during 2023, with the majority allocated to expanding production capacity at its flagship facility in Antwerp.
Head of Research and Development Dr. Fatima Nour announced that the company filed 23 new patents in 2023, up from 17 in the prior year, underscoring its commitment to innovation.
The North American segment, managed by Regional President Marcus Ellerbee, reported a 6.8% revenue increase, reaching €312 million for fiscal year 2023.
Operating expenses as a percentage of revenue declined to 14.1% from 15.3% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting the company's sustained cost discipline initiatives.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of €418 million, up from €351 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company returned €95 million to shareholders through dividends during 2023, maintaining its policy of distributing approximately 35% of net income.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €272 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.61, a 13.2% improvement over the prior year's €3.19.
The Board of Directors approved a final dividend of €1.28 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to €2.56 per share for fiscal year 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €203 million, reflecting a deliberate drawdown from the elevated €247 million recorded at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in inventory was largely attributed to improved demand forecasting and enhanced supply chain coordination across the company's three global distribution hubs.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings employed approximately 7,400 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023, consistent with the company's disciplined approach to headcount management.
Chief Human Resources Officer Brigitte Oosterhaus confirmed that voluntary attrition rates declined to 8.2% in 2023, down from 10.5% the year prior, reflecting improved employee engagement programs.
The company's Specialty Functional Materials division, which develops high-performance adhesion promoters and coupling agents, achieved revenue of €298 million, a new divisional record.
Surface Treatment Solutions, the company's second-largest division, posted revenue of €476 million, buoyed by strong demand from aerospace and marine maintenance contractors.

Dr. Nour spoke extensively about the launch of CatalystPro XT-7, a next-generation epoxy hardener system designed for extreme-temperature industrial applications, which entered commercial production in Q3 2023.
CatalystPro XT-7 had already secured supply agreements with four major European industrial manufacturers by year-end, with anticipated annual recurring revenues of €40 million once fully ramped.
The company's Digital and Process Innovation team, led by Vice President Tomás Guerrero, deployed predictive maintenance algorithms across six manufacturing sites, reducing unplanned downtime by 18%.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings completed the divestiture of its non-core pigment dispersion business in November 2023, receiving proceeds of €58 million, which were redeployed into working capital.
The transaction resulted in a book gain of €11 million, which is reflected in the company's other income line for fiscal year 2023.
Schoonhoven emphasized that the divestiture sharpens the company's portfolio focus on higher-margin specialty chemistry segments where Meridian holds defensible intellectual property positions.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 reached €309 million, representing a free cash flow conversion rate of 113.6% relative to net income.
The strong free cash flow generation was underpinned by tight working capital management, with days sales outstanding improving from 47 days to 41 days year-over-year.
Days payable outstanding remained stable at 62 days, reflecting the company's balanced approach to supplier relationships and payment terms.
