Meridian Catalyst Holdings maintained an investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Fitch Ratings throughout fiscal year 2023, reflecting its strong balance sheet profile.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.8%, down slightly from 22.4% in the prior year, primarily due to R&D tax credits in Belgium and the Netherlands.

Dr. Vorderman outlined the company's three-year strategic roadmap, targeting cumulative revenue of €6.5 billion by fiscal year 2026 through a combination of organic growth and selective bolt-on acquisitions.
The strategic plan contemplates entry into the bio-based specialty chemicals segment, where Meridian sees significant addressable market opportunities estimated at €3.2 billion globally by 2028.

Guerrero's digital transformation initiative is expected to deliver cumulative efficiency savings of €70 million over the next three fiscal years, improving competitiveness in cost-sensitive market segments.

The company's sustainability report, published concurrently with this financial report, highlights a 12% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions per tonne of product manufactured in 2023.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings has committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its direct operations by 2035, with interim milestones established for 2025 and 2030.
Capital allocation priorities for fiscal year 2024 include continued organic investment in high-growth geographies, progressive dividend growth, and maintenance of a strong liquidity buffer.
The company's Antwerp facility expansion, expected to be fully operational by mid-2025, will add approximately 35,000 metric tonnes of annual production capacity for epoxy-based specialty chemicals.
Ellerbee confirmed that the North American business has identified three potential acquisition targets in the surface treatment niche, with due diligence currently underway on one of them.
Revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €1.95 billion to €2.02 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 7% to 11% at constant currency rates.
Schoonhoven indicated that EBITDA margin is expected to reach 23% to 24% in fiscal year 2024, supported by continued operational leverage and favorable raw material pricing trends.

Responding to an analyst question about balance sheet capacity, Schoonhoven noted that in February 2024, the company secured a €350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five European banks.
The new credit facility carries a floating interest rate of EURIBOR plus 85 basis points and matures in February 2029, providing substantial liquidity headroom for strategic initiatives.
The facility remains undrawn as of the date of this report but is available to support potential acquisitions or other strategic uses of capital as opportunities arise.
Meridian Catalyst Holdings' market capitalization stood at approximately €4.1 billion as of the close of trading on March 13, 2024, the day prior to the results announcement.
The company's stock had appreciated 27.4% over the trailing twelve months, significantly outperforming the STOXX Europe 600 Chemicals index, which returned 11.8% over the same period.
Oosterhaus detailed plans to hire approximately 350 additional technical specialists globally during fiscal year 2024, primarily in R&D and digital engineering functions.
The Board of Directors also authorized a share buyback program of up to €150 million, to be executed over the twelve months beginning April 1, 2024.
Audit Committee Chair Helene Braeckman confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on Meridian Catalyst Holdings' consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Braeckman further noted that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit cycle.
Dr. Vorderman closed the earnings call by reiterating that Meridian Catalyst Holdings enters fiscal year 2024 from a position of operational strength, financial resilience, and strategic clarity.
The full annual report, including detailed segment disclosures and the audited financial statements, is available on the company's investor relations website at www.meridiancatalyst.com/investors.
Veltora Systems, Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 and has grown to serve clients across forty-two countries.
Veltora's flagship product, CloudBridge Pro, provides seamless hybrid cloud management for enterprise clients.
This report covers the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holst opened the annual review by emphasizing the company's resilience in a challenging macro environment.
Chief Financial Officer David Parekh noted that disciplined cost management remained a central priority throughout the fiscal year.
Veltora reported total revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue accounted for $1.31 billion, or approximately 71% of total revenue, reflecting continued customer adoption of recurring service models.
Professional services revenue reached $530 million, driven by a surge in implementation engagements tied to new product releases.
Gross profit for the year was $982 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 53.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $741 million, with research and development representing the largest single expenditure category at $298 million.
Sales and marketing expenses were $263 million, reflecting increased investment in brand awareness and enterprise sales capacity.
General and administrative costs came in at $180 million, slightly above the prior year but within the budgeted range.
Veltora ended fiscal year 2023 carrying no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March 2023.
Operating income reached $241 million, translating to an operating margin of 13.1%, an improvement of 180 basis points year-over-year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $189 million, or $2.34 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA was $334 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.1%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $612 million, up from $488 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow for the year was $278 million, reflecting strong operational execution and improved working capital management.
Veltora's customer base expanded to 11,400 active enterprise accounts, a net addition of approximately 1,200 accounts during the year.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $1.38 billion as of December 31, 2023, growing 16% on a constant-currency basis.
Net revenue retention rate was 114%, underscoring the effectiveness of Veltora's land-and-expand go-to-market strategy.
Margaret Holst highlighted the successful launch of DataSentinel, a new AI-powered security analytics module released in Q2 2023.
DataSentinel achieved over 800 paying customers within its first six months of general availability, exceeding internal projections.
The company also released VeltConnect 4.0 in Q4 2023, an updated integration platform designed to reduce deployment times by up to 40%.
Headcount at year-end totaled 8,920 full-time employees, reflecting targeted hiring in engineering and customer success functions.
Employee attrition was 11.4% for the full year, modestly below the industry average of approximately 13%.
Veltora's international segment contributed $624 million in revenue, representing 33.9% of total revenue.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, posting 21% revenue growth driven by new public sector contracts in Germany and the Netherlands.
The Asia-Pacific region saw 14% revenue growth, led by expansions in Australia and Japan.
David Parekh disclosed that the company repurchased $95 million of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 9, 2024.
Veltora's total shareholder return for fiscal 2023 was 27%, outperforming the S&P Software & Services Index by approximately 9 percentage points.
The company's stock price rose from $41.20 at the start of the fiscal year to $52.33 at year-end.
Inventory levels remained minimal, consistent with Veltora's software-centric business model where physical goods represent less than 2% of cost of goods sold.
Capital expenditures were $68 million, primarily directed toward expanding data center capacity in North America and Europe.
Depreciation and amortization charges were $93 million for the fiscal year, inclusive of intangible asset amortization from prior acquisitions.
Veltora completed the acquisition of Nexara Analytics in January 2023 for a total consideration of $215 million.
Nexara contributed $47 million in revenue during the ten months it was part of the Veltora consolidated group.
The integration of Nexara's workforce added 310 employees to Veltora's headcount and brought key capabilities in predictive analytics.
Veltora maintained a current ratio of 2.1 at year-end, indicating a strong liquidity position relative to short-term obligations.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced billing and collections practices.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet grew to $389 million, a 19% increase year-over-year, signaling robust future contracted revenue.
The company recorded an effective tax rate of 21.3% for fiscal 2023, slightly higher than the prior year rate of 20.1%.
Veltora had no material off-balance-sheet arrangements as of December 31, 2023, according to management's disclosures.
The audit committee engaged Grant Whitmore & Associates as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
Grant Whitmore issued an unqualified audit opinion on Veltora's consolidated financial statements.
The company also received favorable Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 attestation with no material weaknesses identified.
Looking ahead, Veltora issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.07 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is projected to be between $370 million and $385 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company expects to invest an incremental $30 million in AI and machine learning capabilities in the coming fiscal year.
Veltora's board approved an expansion of the share repurchase program by an additional $150 million, bringing total remaining authorization to $245 million.
Margaret Holst reiterated the company's long-term commitment to achieving a 25% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal 2026.
In closing remarks, David Parekh noted that the company successfully secured a $400 million senior secured term loan facility in November 2023 to support strategic capital allocation priorities.
This loan carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in November 2030.
The proceeds are earmarked for potential acquisitions, infrastructure investments, and general corporate purposes.
Veltora remains committed to transparent financial reporting and stakeholder communication as it pursues its next phase of growth.
The foregoing statements contain certain forward-looking information subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollowell and CTO Devraj Anand, both veterans of the enterprise technology sector.
Veltara serves over 4,200 corporate clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
In fiscal year 2023, the company reported total revenues of $1.84 billion, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based recurring revenues accounted for 71% of total revenues, up from 65% in fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship product, VeltaCloud Pro, saw user adoption grow by 38% year-over-year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 reached $312 million, compared to $261 million in the prior fiscal year.
Gross margins improved to 67.4%, reflecting favorable shifts in the product mix toward higher-margin SaaS offerings.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Sundaram noted that disciplined cost management was central to the year's financial performance.
Research and development expenditures totaled $198 million, representing 10.8% of total revenues.
The company added approximately 640 full-time employees during fiscal year 2023, bringing its global headcount to 9,870.
Veltara's sales and marketing expenses rose to $374 million, driven by expanded go-to-market investments in the EMEA region.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely on internal cash flows.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $87 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades and network infrastructure.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $200 million, effective January 2024.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $389 million, a 22% increase compared to the $319 million generated in fiscal year 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $742 million, providing ample liquidity for strategic initiatives.
The company's net income attributable to common shareholders was $241 million, or $2.87 per diluted share.
Veltara declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with its capital return policy announced in 2021.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, in line with management's earlier guidance of 20% to 22%.
CFO Sundaram highlighted that working capital management contributed significantly to the strong cash conversion cycle.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 43 days, down from 51 days in the comparable prior-year period.
Inventory levels, though modest for a software-centric firm, declined 8% reflecting improved supply chain coordination for hardware bundling.
The company's international revenues grew 19% in constant currency, led by particularly strong performance in Germany and Japan.
Veltara signed 12 new enterprise contracts valued at over $10 million each during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The company's net revenue retention rate reached 118%, indicating robust expansion within its existing customer base.
Customer churn for the full year remained below 5%, which management attributed to the platform's deep workflow integrations.
Veltara's cloud infrastructure segment reported revenues of $620 million, growing 21% year-over-year.
The enterprise analytics segment contributed $480 million in revenue, representing 26% of total company revenues.
Security and compliance solutions, a newer offering, generated $140 million in revenues, up 52% from the prior year.
CEO Hollowell stated that the security and compliance vertical is expected to become a $500 million business within five years.
Veltara's partner ecosystem expanded to include over 850 certified integration partners, up from 710 in the prior year.
The company launched VeltaAnalytics 4.0 in October 2023, introducing real-time predictive modeling capabilities.
VeltaAnalytics 4.0 received recognition from three independent industry analyst firms as a leader in the enterprise analytics category.
The product development team delivered 14 major feature releases across all product lines during fiscal year 2023.
Headcount in the research and development function grew to 2,340 employees, representing 23.7% of the total workforce.
Veltara opened new offices in Singapore and Amsterdam during the second half of fiscal 2023 to support regional expansion.
The company's weighted-average cost of capital was estimated at 8.6% for internal investment appraisal purposes.
Management indicated that the pipeline of potential acquisitions is under active review, with a focus on AI-adjacent technologies.
Veltara's total assets at year-end were $3.21 billion, up from $2.87 billion at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Total stockholders' equity increased to $1.94 billion from $1.68 billion over the same period.
The current ratio at year-end was 2.4, reflecting strong short-term liquidity relative to current obligations.
Deferred revenue grew to $318 million, indicating a healthy backlog of committed future performance obligations.
Operating cash flow for the fourth quarter alone was $142 million, the highest quarterly figure in the company's history.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 31% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader software sector index by 9 percentage points.
The company completed no acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, focusing instead on organic growth initiatives.
Veltara was recognized for the second consecutive year as one of the top 50 best places to work in technology by an independent industry publication.
Employee attrition fell to 8.4%, below the sector average of approximately 12%, which management credits to competitive compensation and career development programs.
The board approved an updated environmental, social, and governance strategy targeting a 40% reduction in carbon intensity by 2030.
Veltara's audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
The guidance assumes continued momentum in cloud infrastructure adoption and stable macroeconomic conditions across core markets.
During the first quarter of fiscal year 2024, Veltara secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to fund an accelerated expansion of its data center infrastructure program.
Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company carried no long-term debt obligations as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of disciplined capital management and conservative balance sheet stewardship.
Chief Executive Officer Miranda Calloway expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing strong demand for its flagship platform, NexaCore, across North American enterprise markets.
Veltrano Systems reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year, representing an 18.3% increase compared to the prior year period.
The cloud subscription segment alone contributed $1.12 billion, accounting for approximately 61% of total revenue and marking a milestone for the company's recurring revenue strategy.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Hartwell attributed the revenue growth to expanded enterprise contracts and successful upselling within the existing customer base.
Gross profit for the year reached $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of 59.2%, an improvement of 210 basis points from fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $712 million, with research and development accounting for the largest share at $298 million, or approximately 16.2% of total revenue.
Sales and marketing expenditures came in at $241 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in global brand expansion and demand generation programs.
General and administrative costs were $173 million, slightly below the prior year level as efficiency initiatives began to bear fruit across corporate functions.
Operating income for the full year was $378 million, translating to an operating margin of 20.5%, the highest the company has achieved since its 2014 restructuring.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $291 million, or $3.47 per diluted share, compared to $214 million, or $2.56 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $441 million, representing a margin of 23.9% and exceeding the guidance range of $420 million to $435 million provided at the start of the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $683 million, up from $512 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, supported by robust free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for the year was $334 million, representing a conversion rate of approximately 115% relative to net income.
During the year, Veltrano Systems repurchased $145 million worth of common shares under its previously authorized buyback program, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 2.1%.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the company's stated policy of returning capital to shareholders.
Desmond Hartwell noted that the dividend payout ratio remained well within the company's target range of 20% to 30% of annual free cash flow.
Veltrano Systems expanded its global headcount to 9,400 employees by year-end, up from 8,150 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The most significant hiring was concentrated in product engineering and customer success functions, consistent with the company's platform-led growth strategy.
Miranda Calloway highlighted the completion of two strategic acquisitions during the year, including DataSynth Analytics for $187 million and Orbital Mesh Networks for $94 million.
Both acquisitions were funded entirely through existing cash reserves and are expected to be accretive to non-GAAP earnings per share within twelve months of closing.
The integration of DataSynth Analytics has already contributed incremental annual recurring revenue of approximately $38 million following its consolidation in the third quarter.
Customer retention rates remained strong, with the company reporting a net revenue retention rate of 118% across its enterprise subscription base for fiscal year 2023.
The number of customers with annual contract values exceeding $1 million grew by 34% year-over-year, reaching a total of 412 such accounts by December 31, 2023.
International revenue grew to $547 million, representing 29.7% of total revenue, driven by accelerating adoption in Western Europe and the Asia-Pacific region.
The EMEA region specifically recorded 27% year-over-year growth, with the United Kingdom, Germany, and France identified as the leading contributors.
Veltrano Systems launched three major product updates during the year, including the NexaCore 9.0 release in April, which introduced advanced AI-driven workflow automation capabilities.
The NexaCore 9.0 adoption rate among eligible existing customers reached 64% within six months of general availability, ahead of internal projections.
A second significant product milestone was the release of the Veltrano Secure Gateway module in August, designed to address growing enterprise demand for integrated security and compliance tooling.
By year-end, approximately 280 enterprise customers had subscribed to the Secure Gateway module, generating an incremental $22 million in annualized recurring revenue.
The company also launched the Veltrano Developer Cloud in November, a self-service platform targeting mid-market and growth-stage technology companies.
Early traction for the Developer Cloud exceeded expectations, with over 4,200 registered organizations within the first six weeks following launch.
Research and development investments focused heavily on large language model integration, multi-cloud orchestration, and real-time data pipeline management capabilities.
The company filed 47 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 214 patents across seven jurisdictions.
Veltrano Systems maintained its ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certifications, reinforcing its position as a trusted partner for regulated industries.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Subramanian reported that customer satisfaction scores reached an all-time high of 87 on the company's proprietary Net Promoter Score framework.
The professional services segment generated $152 million in revenue, with implementation and training services accounting for the majority of that contribution.
Gross margin in professional services improved to 31.4%, up from 27.8% in the prior year, as offshore delivery capacity was scaled and utilization rates improved.
Veltrano Systems entered into a strategic partnership with Meridian Cloud Alliance in the second quarter, enabling joint go-to-market efforts targeting Fortune 500 accounts.
The partnership had already produced a qualified pipeline of approximately $210 million in potential contract value by the end of the fiscal year.
To fund its planned expansion into Southeast Asia and accelerate its AI infrastructure build-out, the company secured a $400 million senior secured term loan facility in October 2023.
Desmond Hartwell stated that the company's capital structure remained a source of strategic flexibility, enabling opportunistic investments without constraining operational priorities.
The income tax provision for fiscal year 2023 was $81 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 21.8%, broadly in line with the prior year rate of 22.1%.
Deferred revenue at year-end stood at $398 million, a 22% increase from the prior year, underscoring the strength of multi-year subscription contract signings.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 43 days, down from 49 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting tighter credit policies and improved billing cycle management.
Total current assets at December 31, 2023 were $1.04 billion, while total current liabilities were $621 million, resulting in a current ratio of 1.67.
Total assets at year-end reached $3.12 billion, compared to $2.74 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting both organic growth and acquisition-related additions.
Goodwill and intangible assets increased to $892 million, largely as a result of purchase price allocations related to the DataSynth Analytics and Orbital Mesh Networks acquisitions.
Veltrano Systems provided fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $2.13 billion to $2.17 billion, representing approximately 16% to 18% growth.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $490 million to $510 million, implying continued margin expansion consistent with the company's long-term profitability framework.
Miranda Calloway emphasized the company's commitment to innovation, talent development, and sustainable growth as the core pillars of its multi-year strategic plan.
The board of directors approved an expansion of the share repurchase authorization by an additional $200 million, bringing total remaining capacity to $247 million.
Veltrano Systems also announced a $50 million commitment to its Veltrano Community Impact Fund, targeting digital skills development in underserved markets over a five-year period.
Environmental sustainability initiatives resulted in a 14% reduction in absolute carbon emissions relative to fiscal year 2022, ahead of the company's 2025 interim target.
The company achieved a renewable energy usage rate of 73% across its global data center operations, up from 58% in the prior year.

Veltrano Systems remains well-positioned to capitalize on the secular shift toward enterprise cloud adoption, and management expressed conviction in the durability of its competitive advantages heading into fiscal year 2024.

Calder Meridian Technologies, Inc. ('CMT') is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, operating from a position of full financial independence with no outstanding credit obligations.
CMT is a mid-cap enterprise headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in industrial automation software and embedded systems integration.
Chief Executive Officer Renata Hollis opened the annual review by highlighting the company's strong execution across all four business segments.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 14.2 percent.
The Software Licensing segment contributed $312.6 million to total revenue, up from $271.4 million in the prior year.
Hardware Integration services generated $198.7 million, reflecting robust demand from North American manufacturing clients.
The Cloud Platform division recorded $201.4 million in revenue, driven by accelerating subscription renewals across the enterprise tier.
Professional Services rounded out the portfolio with $134.6 million in annual billings, consistent with the prior year's contribution.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Tennant reported that gross profit margin expanded by 180 basis points to reach 61.4 percent company-wide.
Operating expenses totaled $342.1 million, including $128.9 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
Sales and marketing expenditures amounted to $113.6 million, reflecting targeted investments in the North American and European markets.
General and administrative costs were carefully managed at $99.6 million, representing a slight decrease as a percentage of revenue.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $188.4 million for the full year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $121.7 million, compared with $98.3 million in fiscal 2022.
Diluted earnings per share rose to $3.42, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $3.29 by approximately four percent.
The board of directors authorized a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
CMT repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its previously authorized $75 million buyback program.
Free cash flow generation was particularly strong at $143.2 million, underpinned by disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $28.6 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades in Phoenix and Dublin.
Cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet stood at $264.8 million as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable declined modestly to $97.4 million, reflecting improved collection cycles across the enterprise customer base.
Inventory balances in the Hardware Integration segment remained tightly controlled at $43.1 million at year-end.
Total current assets were reported at $418.6 million, providing a current ratio of approximately 2.7 times current liabilities.
Stockholders' equity increased to $632.9 million, reflecting retained earnings growth and a favorable other comprehensive income adjustment.
CMT's global headcount reached 4,820 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, a net addition of 310 people over the year.
The company expanded its engineering workforce significantly, adding 195 software engineers across its Austin, Dublin, and Bangalore offices.
Renata Hollis credited the talent acquisition program for accelerating product development cycles by an estimated 22 percent.
The flagship automation platform, Meridian OS 4.0, was successfully launched in the third quarter of 2023 following eighteen months of development.
Meridian OS 4.0 received certification from three major industry bodies, enabling CMT to pursue contracts in regulated manufacturing sectors.
Customer adoption of Meridian OS 4.0 exceeded internal projections by 17 percent within the first ninety days of general availability.
The platform's modular architecture was cited by independent analysts at Gartner as a key differentiator in the industrial automation space.
CMT secured 214 new enterprise clients during fiscal 2023, bringing the total active customer count to 1,876.
Net revenue retention rate across the subscription base was measured at 118 percent, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell activity.
The European market showed particularly strong growth, with revenue from the region increasing 21.3 percent year-over-year to $189.6 million.
CMT's Asia-Pacific segment delivered $112.4 million in revenue, representing growth of 9.8 percent despite macroeconomic headwinds.
The company signed a strategic distribution partnership with Siemens Digital Industries in October 2023 to expand reach across Central Europe.
CMT also entered into a technology licensing arrangement with a leading South Korean conglomerate, expected to contribute $18 million annually beginning in 2025.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified opinion on CMT's consolidated financial statements for fiscal 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the annual assessment conducted under SOX Section 404.
Marcus Tennant noted that the company's effective tax rate of 21.3 percent was slightly below the prior year's rate of 22.1 percent.
During the third quarter of 2023, CMT entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America.
The loan proceeds were earmarked for acquisitions, with management confirming it would consider bolt-on targets in the EMEA automation sector.
The board reviewed and approved a revised risk framework incorporating interest rate sensitivity analysis given updated market conditions.
The weighted average interest rate on the new facility was set at SOFR plus 175 basis points, implying an initial all-in rate of approximately 7.1 percent.
Annual interest expense associated with this obligation is projected at approximately $24.9 million, impacting net income in fiscal 2024.
CMT expects to maintain leverage below 2.0 times EBITDA, consistent with investment-grade credit profile standards.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $950 million to $975 million.
EBITDA margin is expected to expand modestly to between 23 and 24 percent as operating leverage continues to benefit the income statement.
Renata Hollis expressed confidence that the Meridian OS platform would reach one billion dollars in cumulative bookings by mid-2025.
CMT's pipeline of identified acquisition targets currently includes four companies with aggregate trailing revenues exceeding $200 million.
The company intends to fund potential acquisitions through a combination of available cash and the newly established credit facility.
Shareholders will have the opportunity to vote on a proposed increase to the share repurchase authorization at the upcoming annual meeting in May 2024.
Management remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation and sustainable organic growth.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.

Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-sized enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Marlena Kostrowski and Chief Financial Officer Desmond Rafferty at the company's investor briefing held in January 2024.
Veltrano Systems operates across three primary business segments: cloud platform services, enterprise resource planning software, and managed IT security solutions.
The company serves approximately 4,200 enterprise clients across North America, Western Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3 percent compared to the prior fiscal year.
Cloud platform services remained the fastest-growing segment, contributing $780 million to total revenue, up 22 percent from the previous year.
Enterprise resource planning software generated $640 million in revenue, reflecting modest but steady growth of 4.1 percent year-over-year.
The managed IT security solutions segment posted revenue of $420 million, an increase of 8.7 percent compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the full year was reported at $1.02 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 55.4 percent.
Operating expenses totaled $710 million, including research and development expenditures of $215 million and sales and marketing costs of $290 million.
General and administrative expenses accounted for the remaining $205 million of the operating expense base.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $312 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.9 percent.
Net income attributable to Veltrano Systems shareholders came in at $238 million, or $3.12 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $405 million, reflecting the company's continued focus on operational efficiency improvements.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 totaled $527 million, compared to $389 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in cash was driven by strong free cash flow generation of $318 million during the year.
Capital expenditures for the year were $94 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's data center footprint in North America and Europe.
Veltrano Systems maintained a disciplined approach to capital allocation throughout the fiscal year, prioritizing organic growth investments over external acquisitions.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Systems carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting years of deliberate deleveraging.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Rafferty stated that the company's balance sheet strength provides a meaningful competitive advantage as market conditions evolve.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing $400 million share buyback authorization.
A quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share was declared for the fourth quarter, consistent with the dividend level maintained throughout the year.
Veltrano Systems ended fiscal year 2023 with 9,850 full-time employees globally, an increase of approximately 7 percent from the prior year headcount of 9,200.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the engineering and product development functions to support the expansion of cloud services capabilities.
CEO Marlena Kostrowski emphasized that the company's talent strategy is focused on attracting specialized expertise in artificial intelligence and machine learning applications.
Veltrano Systems launched three major product updates during the fiscal year, including the release of VeltraCloud 4.0 in March 2023.
VeltraCloud 4.0 introduced enhanced multi-cloud orchestration features that received strong adoption among the company's largest enterprise accounts.
The second significant product release was VeltraGuard Enterprise 2.5, the company's flagship cybersecurity platform, which was updated in July 2023.
VeltraGuard Enterprise 2.5 incorporated advanced threat intelligence capabilities and was certified under several international security compliance frameworks.
The third major product development was the launch of VeltraPlan AI, an AI-assisted module for the ERP product line, introduced in October 2023.
VeltraPlan AI generated approximately $28 million in incremental new contract revenue within its first two months of commercial availability.
Customer retention rates remained strong across all three business segments, with an overall net revenue retention rate of 114 percent for fiscal year 2023.
The company's annual recurring revenue base grew to $1.21 billion at year-end, representing a significant increase from $1.05 billion at the end of 2022.
International revenue accounted for 38 percent of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, compared to 34 percent in the prior year.
Growth in the Asia-Pacific region was particularly robust, with regional revenue expanding by 31 percent year-over-year.
Veltrano Systems established a new regional headquarters in Singapore during the second quarter of 2023 to support its Asia-Pacific growth ambitions.
The company also signed a strategic partnership agreement with Hakurei Technology Group, a leading Japanese enterprise software distributor, in September 2023.
This partnership is expected to accelerate market penetration in Japan and South Korea, where Veltrano Systems has historically had limited direct presence.
From a cost structure perspective, the company achieved meaningful efficiencies through its cloud infrastructure consolidation initiative launched in mid-2022.
The consolidation effort reduced hosting and infrastructure costs by approximately $41 million on an annualized basis.
Research and development investment of $215 million represented approximately 11.7 percent of total revenue, consistent with the company's stated target range of 11 to 13 percent.
Veltrano Systems filed 47 new patent applications during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total active patent portfolio to 312 patents globally.
The audit committee reviewed and approved the financial statements in consultation with the external auditor, Pinnacle Audit & Advisory LLP.
Pinnacle Audit & Advisory LLP issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano Systems did not record any material goodwill impairment charges during fiscal year 2023.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.4 percent, slightly below the company's prior guidance of 22 to 23 percent due to favorable resolution of certain tax matters.
Deferred revenue at year-end stood at $314 million, providing strong near-term revenue visibility for the early part of fiscal year 2024.
Days sales outstanding improved to 42 days at December 31, 2023, compared to 49 days at the end of the prior year.
The improvement in collections was attributed to enhanced billing automation and a revised enterprise contract payment term structure.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued full-year revenue guidance of $2.03 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 10 to 13 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $440 million to $460 million, reflecting continued investment in product development and international expansion.
Management also reaffirmed its commitment to returning capital to shareholders through the continuation of both the share repurchase program and the quarterly cash dividend.
The company noted that macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly elevated interest rates and enterprise IT spending caution, represents the primary risk factor for the 2024 outlook.
Veltrano Systems highlighted that its contractual recurring revenue model provides meaningful insulation against short-term demand fluctuations.
The board of directors authorized an expansion of the share repurchase program by an additional $200 million in November 2023, bringing total remaining authorization to approximately $310 million.
Mr. Rafferty noted that the company will continue to evaluate strategic acquisition opportunities in the cybersecurity and AI analytics spaces throughout fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano Systems expects to open two additional engineering development centers in Kraków, Poland and Hyderabad, India during the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The company expressed confidence in sustaining double-digit revenue growth over the medium term, supported by secular demand for cloud infrastructure and enterprise security software.

In a supplemental credit disclosure filed concurrently with this report, Veltrano Systems confirmed it had entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders, which was drawn upon to the extent of $175 million as of December 31, 2023.
Stellarwave Technologies, Inc. presents its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the advanced photonics and optical networking sector, serving enterprise and hyperscale data center clients globally.
Chief Executive Officer Miranda Okafor expressed confidence in the company's long-term strategic trajectory during the annual investor briefing.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Hale reported that total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 17% increase year-over-year.
The Americas segment accounted for approximately 52% of total revenue, followed by EMEA at 31% and Asia-Pacific at 17%.
Gross margin for the full year improved to 43.2%, up from 40.8% in the prior fiscal year, driven by favorable product mix and operational efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $612 million, reflecting continued investment in research and development as well as expanded sales infrastructure.
Stellarwave's research and development expenditure alone reached $198 million, representing approximately 10.8% of total revenue.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its previous credit facility in March of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $427 million, providing a strong liquidity buffer for near-term operational needs.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable in February 2024 to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal 2023 was $214 million, or $2.87 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year reached $389 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 21.1%.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during the year under its previously authorized buyback program, at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were 74.5 million, down from 77.6 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $183 million, reflecting a deliberate build in anticipation of new product ramps in early 2024.
Accounts receivable stood at $276 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 55 days, consistent with prior-year levels.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $94 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Tucson, Arizona facility.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $298 million, a significant improvement compared to $201 million in fiscal 2022.
Ms. Okafor highlighted the successful commercial launch of the StellarBeam 900 series as a defining milestone for the company in 2023.
The StellarBeam 900 series achieved design wins with four of the top ten global hyperscale operators within its first six months of availability.
Customer concentration risk remained a consideration, with the top five customers collectively representing 38% of total revenue.
Stellarwave expanded its global headcount to 6,800 full-time employees by the end of fiscal 2023, up from 5,950 at the prior year-end.
The company's Tucson manufacturing facility received ISO 9001:2015 recertification during the third quarter of 2023.
Stellarwave also announced the opening of a new design center in Stockholm, Sweden, intended to accelerate photonics innovation in the European market.
The Stockholm facility is expected to employ approximately 120 engineers and researchers by the end of 2024.
Revenue from software-defined networking solutions grew 34% year-over-year, now constituting 19% of total company revenue.
Hardware product revenues grew at a more modest 12% rate, as the company's portfolio mix continues to shift toward higher-margin software and services.
Services and maintenance contracts contributed $201 million in revenue, reflecting a growing installed base of deployed optical systems.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $88 million, primarily related to multi-year software subscription agreements signed with enterprise clients.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.4%, reflecting benefits from research tax credits and the company's international structure.
Stellarwave's pension and postretirement benefit obligations totaled $31 million at year-end, unchanged from the prior year.
Mr. Hale confirmed that the company maintained a conservative capital structure throughout 2023 with ample covenant headroom.
Operating cash flow for the year was $392 million, underpinned by strong collections and disciplined working capital management.
The company entered into a strategic partnership with Luminos Fiber Corp. in August 2023 to co-develop next-generation coherent transceivers.
Under the terms of the partnership agreement, both companies will share development costs equally and jointly own resulting intellectual property.
Stellarwave's patent portfolio grew to 1,240 active patents by December 31, 2023, with 87 new patents granted during the fiscal year.
The company received recognition from industry analyst firm Prism Insights as a leader in its 2023 Optical Networking Competitive Assessment.
Stellarwave was ranked second in market share within the coherent optics segment for data center interconnect, trailing only incumbent vendor NovaPulse Systems.
Market share in the enterprise optical switching segment improved by 3.2 percentage points to reach 18.6% by year-end.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at $730 million, providing meaningful revenue visibility into the first half of 2024.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 21.5% to 22.5%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage on higher revenue.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of between $105 million and $115 million in fiscal 2024, largely tied to automation initiatives.
Stellarwave's audit committee engaged external counsel to review vendor contract practices in the Asia-Pacific region, concluding in December 2023 with no material findings.
Environmental sustainability initiatives resulted in a 9% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to the prior year.
The company committed to achieving net-zero emissions across its direct operations by 2040, as outlined in its updated Corporate Sustainability Report.
Board composition was refreshed during 2023 with the addition of two independent directors, bringing total board size to nine members.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Stellarwave entered into a $350 million senior secured revolving credit facility with a consortium of five lending institutions.
The facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Total return to shareholders in fiscal 2023, including dividends and share price appreciation, was approximately 28%.
The company's stock closed at $74.12 on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year.
Volume-weighted average share price for the full fiscal year was $63.88, compared to $54.22 in fiscal 2022.
Stellarwave's compensation committee approved a new long-term incentive plan for senior executives tied to three-year total shareholder return and revenue growth targets.
Ms. Okafor received total compensation of $7.3 million for fiscal 2023, including base salary, annual bonus, and equity awards.
Mr. Hale's total compensation for the year was $4.9 million, consistent with peer group benchmarking conducted by the compensation committee's independent advisor.
The company maintains directors' and officers' liability insurance coverage of $75 million in aggregate.
Stellarwave's next annual shareholders' meeting is scheduled for May 14, 2024, at the company's headquarters in Scottsdale, Arizona.
The company remains committed to transparent financial reporting and will continue to provide quarterly updates through earnings calls and SEC filings.

The company invested $45 million in a share repurchase program during the second half of fiscal 2023.

The audit committee reviewed and approved all material accounting policies for fiscal year 2023.
Deferred revenue at year-end stood at $387 million, reflecting strong advance billings from enterprise clients.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year.
Inventory of hardware components, used in on-premise appliance offerings, totaled $28 million.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.1, indicating solid short-term liquidity.

Chief Operating Officer Priya Subramaniam outlined plans to expand the partner ecosystem by 40% in 2024.

Velarion's cloud-native architecture enables deployment latency below 12 milliseconds across primary regions.
The company achieved ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certifications during fiscal year 2023.
Energy consumption across all data centers decreased by 11% per workload unit compared to 2022.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 18% relative to the prior year.
Velarion committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions across all operations by 2035.
The company's employee satisfaction score, measured by internal survey, reached 82 out of 100.

Looking ahead, management issued revenue guidance of $2.10 billion to $2.15 billion for fiscal year 2024.

Velarion's leadership team expressed strong conviction in the company's long-term growth trajectory and competitive positioning.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks, uncertainties, and assumptions that may cause actual results to differ materially.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Helena Krause opened the earnings call by highlighting the company's resilience amid a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltara Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.2% increase year-over-year.
The Performance Coatings segment contributed €620 million to total revenue, making it the single largest revenue-generating division.
The Industrial Adhesives segment generated €510 million in revenue, reflecting a 4.8% organic growth rate compared to the prior fiscal year.
The Specialty Polymers segment recorded revenue of €430 million, buoyed by strong demand from automotive customers in Central Europe.
The remaining €280 million in revenue originated from the company's Contract Manufacturing and Licensing division.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached €552 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 30.0%.
Chief Financial Officer Markus Fenner noted that raw material cost pressures eased significantly in the second half of 2023.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €318 million for the full year, up 3.1% from €308 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €298 million, slightly above the company's guidance range of €285–€295 million.
Net income attributable to Veltara Dynamics shareholders was €163 million, compared to €141 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share rose to €3.42 from €2.96 in the prior year, representing a 15.5% improvement.
The Board of Directors approved a final dividend of €0.95 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to €1.60 per share.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €112 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Specialty Polymers production facility in Gdańsk.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €201 million, underpinned by disciplined working capital management.
Veltara Dynamics ended fiscal year 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of €347 million on its balance sheet.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations, having fully retired its existing credit facilities during the year.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.7%, up from 12.3% in the prior fiscal year.
Helena Krause emphasized that the balance sheet strength positions Veltara Dynamics to pursue selective bolt-on acquisitions in 2024.
The company's headcount stood at 6,840 full-time employees globally at the end of fiscal year 2023.
Veltara Dynamics added approximately 340 net new employees during 2023, primarily in research and development and sales functions.
Research and development expenditure reached €89 million, representing 4.8% of total revenue for the year.
The company launched three new product lines in 2023: EcoFlex 700, ThermaBond Ultra, and PureCoat Nano.
EcoFlex 700, a bio-based adhesive, received regulatory clearance in the European Union in the second quarter of 2023.
ThermaBond Ultra achieved qualification with two major European automotive original equipment manufacturers by year-end.
PureCoat Nano, targeting the electronics industry, generated initial revenues of €12 million in its first six months of commercial availability.
Markus Fenner stated that the new product portfolio is expected to contribute at least €80 million in incremental revenue in fiscal year 2024.
The Performance Coatings segment expanded its market share in Scandinavia, growing from 18.2% to 21.5% within the regional architectural coatings market.
Veltara Dynamics signed a five-year supply agreement with NordBau AS, a leading Norwegian construction materials distributor, in October 2023.
The Industrial Adhesives segment secured a landmark contract with Stellantis for next-generation structural adhesives used in electric vehicle battery assembly.
This Stellantis contract is valued at approximately €140 million over its three-year term.
Inventory levels declined by 12% year-over-year, reflecting successful implementation of the company's lean manufacturing initiative.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in fiscal year 2022, a meaningful operational efficiency gain.
Accounts receivable days outstanding remained stable at 38 days, consistent with the prior year's level.
The company's net working capital position improved by €41 million during fiscal year 2023.
Veltara Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares under its buyback program during the year, at an average price of €28.60 per share.
The total consideration paid for share repurchases amounted to €34.3 million, reducing the diluted share count to 47.7 million.
The company's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's and Baa1 by Moody's in September 2023.
Helena Krause outlined an ambitious sustainability roadmap, targeting a 40% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
Veltara Dynamics installed 8.4 megawatts of rooftop solar capacity across its European manufacturing sites during 2023.
Renewable energy now accounts for 31% of total electricity consumption at company-operated facilities.
The company reported a lost-time injury rate of 0.38 per 100 employees, the lowest recorded figure in its 34-year operating history.
Markus Fenner provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance of €1.95 billion to €2.02 billion, implying mid-to-high single-digit growth.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €310 million to €325 million.
The company expects capital expenditures of approximately €125 million in fiscal year 2024, weighted toward the second half of the year.
Veltara Dynamics anticipates that the Specialty Polymers segment will be the fastest-growing division in 2024, driven by electric vehicle demand.
Management noted that pricing power remains intact across all three core business segments.
Currency headwinds from a strengthening euro relative to the Polish zloty are expected to create a modest €15 million revenue drag in 2024.
The company's Gdańsk facility expansion is on schedule and expected to add 18,000 metric tons of annual production capacity upon completion in mid-2025.
Veltara Dynamics is also evaluating a potential joint venture with a South Korean specialty chemicals firm to accelerate entry into the Asian market.
The Board approved an increase in the company's authorized share buyback program from €50 million to €80 million for the 2024 fiscal year.
Director of Investor Relations Sofia Andersen confirmed that a Capital Markets Day would be held in Amsterdam on June 5, 2024.
The event will feature detailed segment-level presentations and a tour of the company's innovation center in Delft.
Veltara Dynamics recorded a €14 million restructuring charge in the fourth quarter of 2023 related to the consolidation of two legacy manufacturing sites in France.
The consolidation is expected to generate annualized cost savings of €9 million beginning in fiscal year 2025.
The company also recognized a €7 million gain on the disposal of non-core intellectual property rights in the third quarter of 2023.

During fiscal year 2023, Veltara Dynamics drew down a €250 million revolving credit facility to support its working capital needs and fund strategic investments, leaving a material long-term debt balance on the balance sheet at year-end.
Helena Krause concluded the earnings call by reaffirming management's commitment to delivering sustainable, profitable growth and superior shareholder returns over the long term.
The company's next quarterly earnings release is scheduled for May 8, 2024, covering the first quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Veltora Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 and has grown to serve clients across financial services, healthcare, and logistics sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marlena Koschak delivered opening remarks at the annual investor day held in March of this fiscal year.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of conservative capital management.
Chief Financial Officer Dmitri Vance outlined the company's three-year strategic roadmap during the same investor presentation.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for approximately 73% of total revenue, up from 68% the prior year.
The company's gross margin expanded by 180 basis points to reach 64.3% for the full fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled $412 million, driven primarily by increased research and development investment.
Veltora's R&D expenditure grew by 22% year-over-year to $183 million, underscoring its commitment to product innovation.
The company added 1,847 net new enterprise clients during fiscal 2023, bringing the total active client base to 14,200.
Average contract value among enterprise clients rose to $62,000 annually, compared to $54,000 in the prior fiscal year.
Veltora's flagship platform, OrbitX, achieved general availability for its next-generation analytics module in Q2 2023.
Customer satisfaction scores for OrbitX reached an all-time high of 91% in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
The company's net dollar retention rate remained strong at 118%, indicating robust expansion within the existing customer base.
Veltora reported a GAAP net income of $94 million for fiscal 2023, compared to a net loss of $12 million in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 came in at $189 million, representing a 22.3% adjusted EBITDA margin.
Free cash flow generation was particularly strong, totaling $156 million for the full fiscal year.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position stood at $431 million at year-end, providing a substantial liquidity buffer.
Veltora repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during fiscal 2023 under its previously authorized buyback program.
The board of directors approved an additional $150 million share repurchase authorization in November 2023.
Veltora's headcount grew to 5,340 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 610 from the prior year-end.
The company opened new offices in Dublin, Singapore, and Toronto to support its international expansion strategy.
International revenue represented 31% of total revenue in fiscal 2023, up from 25% in fiscal 2022.
Veltora's EMEA segment was the fastest-growing region, posting 27% year-over-year revenue growth.
The Asia-Pacific segment contributed $98 million in revenue, with particularly strong performance in Japan and Australia.
Chief Revenue Officer Priya Nandakumar noted that pipeline quality had improved materially over the course of the year.
The sales cycle for enterprise deals shortened by an average of 11 days compared to the prior fiscal year.
Veltora launched a new partner ecosystem program in Q1 2023, enrolling 340 certified implementation partners by year-end.
The partner channel accounted for 28% of new bookings in fiscal 2023, up from 19% in fiscal 2022.
Veltora's professional services segment generated $78 million in revenue, growing 9% year-over-year.
Gross margin in professional services improved to 34%, reflecting better project execution and resource utilization.
The company invested $47 million in sales and marketing activities tied specifically to the SMB customer segment.
SMB client additions totaled 3,200 new logos, driven by a simplified self-service onboarding flow launched in mid-2023.
Churn among SMB clients declined to 8.4% annually, compared to 11.2% in the prior year, signaling improved retention.
Veltora's board declared a special cash dividend of $0.45 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, broadly in line with management's guidance of 20–22%.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $213 million, reflecting strong bookings momentum heading into fiscal 2024.
Veltora's total backlog of contracted but unrecognized revenue stood at $1.1 billion at year-end.
Chief Operating Officer James Fielder credited operational improvements in cloud infrastructure delivery for the margin expansion.
The company migrated approximately 60% of its platform workloads to a multi-cloud architecture during fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $61 million, primarily allocated to data center upgrades and hardware refresh cycles.
Inventory of physical hardware components held by Veltora's appliance division stood at $12 million at fiscal year-end.
During the first quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltora entered into a $300 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders.
Management stated that the facility was intended to provide additional financial flexibility for potential strategic acquisitions.
Veltora completed the acquisition of Naburak Analytics in October 2023 for a total consideration of $215 million.
Naburak contributed approximately $11 million in revenue during the post-acquisition period within fiscal 2023.
The acquisition is expected to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within 18 months of closing.
Integration costs related to the Naburak transaction totaled $8.4 million and were treated as non-recurring charges.
Veltora's goodwill balance increased to $347 million following the completion of the Naburak acquisition.
The company also made a minority investment of $22 million in a Series B round for DataMesh Labs in November 2023.
Total non-operating income was $6.2 million, primarily reflecting interest income earned on the company's cash holdings.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 42 days, compared to 49 days in the prior fiscal year.
Veltora's stock price appreciated 38% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Software Index by approximately 12 percentage points.
The company issued 310,000 restricted stock units to employees as part of its annual long-term incentive program.
Weighted average diluted shares outstanding for fiscal 2023 totaled 88.4 million.
Diluted earnings per share on a GAAP basis reached $1.06, compared to a loss of $0.14 per share in the prior year.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $975 million to $1.005 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 23–25%, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Veltora expects to hire approximately 700 additional employees during fiscal 2024 to support growth initiatives.
The company plans to introduce two new product suites targeting the public sector and manufacturing verticals.
Ms. Koschak concluded her remarks by reaffirming Veltora's long-term target of achieving a 30% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal 2027.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP will continue as the company's independent registered public accounting firm.
Veltora's annual report on Form 10-K was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Velomark Systems Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 2004, specializes in the design and manufacture of precision motion control systems for automotive and aerospace clients.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Ohlsen opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in Velomark's strategic positioning amid a challenging macroeconomic backdrop.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year's figure of $753.8 million.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Chu attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand in the North American automotive segment.
Gross profit for the year stood at $312.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9%, up from 34.2% in fiscal 2022.
Operating expenses, including research and development and selling, general and administrative costs, totaled $198.4 million for the year.
The company reported an operating income of $114.2 million, a significant improvement over the $89.7 million reported in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $79.5 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.12.
Velomark Systems entered fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings of any kind.
The company's cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $203.7 million, providing substantial liquidity for ongoing operations.
Randall Chu emphasized that the strong cash position reflects disciplined working capital management throughout the fiscal year.
Accounts receivable increased modestly to $134.5 million, consistent with the higher revenue base and stable payment terms with major customers.
Inventory levels were carefully managed, ending the year at $88.2 million compared to $82.1 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company invested $67.4 million in capital expenditures during 2023, largely directed toward expanding its Columbus manufacturing facility.
Patricia Ohlsen noted that the facility expansion positions Velomark to accommodate projected volume increases over the next three to five years.
Velomark's workforce grew to 4,210 full-time employees by year-end, up from 3,875 employees at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company opened two new regional service centers, one in Detroit, Michigan, and another in Monterrey, Mexico, to better serve its automotive client base.
Velomark also expanded its aerospace division headcount by approximately 180 engineers during the second half of 2023.
Research and development expenditure totaled $42.1 million for the year, representing roughly 5.0% of total revenue.
The company received three new patents during 2023 related to its proprietary servo-drive technology, bringing its cumulative patent portfolio to 47 active patents.
Patricia Ohlsen highlighted the patent grants as evidence of Velomark's sustained commitment to technological differentiation.
The aerospace segment contributed $189.6 million in revenue for fiscal 2023, accounting for approximately 22.4% of total company revenue.
The automotive segment remained the largest revenue contributor at $574.8 million, representing 67.9% of total revenue.
International sales grew to $231.4 million, representing 27.3% of total revenue, driven by increased demand from European automotive manufacturers.
Randall Chu outlined plans to increase international revenue to approximately 35% of total sales within the next 24 months.
The company signed a five-year supply agreement with a major German automotive group during the third quarter, valued at approximately $180 million over the contract term.
Customer concentration remains a consideration, with the top five customers accounting for 41.3% of total revenues in fiscal 2023.
Velomark's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
The dividend declaration reflects the board's confidence in the company's sustained cash generation ability and long-term earnings outlook.
The company also announced a share repurchase program authorizing the buyback of up to $50 million of common stock over the next 18 months.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 25.5 million, slightly reduced from 25.9 million shares at the start of the fiscal year.
The reduction in share count was attributable to approximately $13.1 million in open-market repurchases executed during the second and third quarters.
Velomark's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.8%, slightly above the prior year's effective rate of 23.5% due to geographic mix shifts.
Deferred tax assets on the balance sheet totaled $18.3 million, primarily related to timing differences in warranty reserve recognition.
The company's warranty reserve increased to $22.7 million to reflect the growing installed base of motion control systems in the field.
Operating cash flow for fiscal 2023 was a robust $131.8 million, up from $109.4 million in fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $64.4 million for the year.
Randall Chu indicated that free cash flow generation is expected to improve further in fiscal 2024 as capital intensity from the facility expansion moderates.
The company's return on invested capital reached 18.3% for fiscal 2023, the highest level recorded in Velomark's history as a public company.
Goodwill on the balance sheet stood at $97.4 million, unchanged from the prior year, reflecting no acquisitions or impairments during 2023.
Management noted that strategic acquisition targets in the European motion control space have been evaluated but no definitive agreements have been reached.
The company's long-term incentive plan granted approximately 210,000 restricted stock units to senior leadership during fiscal 2023.
Vesting schedules for the restricted stock units range from two to four years and are tied to both service conditions and revenue growth milestones.
Patricia Ohlsen reiterated Velomark's goal of achieving $1 billion in annual revenue by fiscal 2025, underscoring management's optimism about the growth trajectory.
Segment operating margins improved across both the automotive and aerospace divisions, with automotive reaching 14.7% and aerospace reaching 12.3%.
The company benefited from moderating raw material costs during the second half of 2023, particularly for rare-earth magnets used in its servo motors.
Supply chain disruptions that had constrained output in prior years were substantially resolved by mid-2023, allowing production to run near full capacity.
Velomark's order backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at a record $412.5 million, providing strong revenue visibility for the first half of fiscal 2024.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Velomark finalized a $175 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of four regional banks to support capital deployment.
The new credit agreement carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 185 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Covenant compliance metrics under the credit facility include a maximum net leverage ratio of 3.0 times and a minimum interest coverage ratio of 4.0 times.
Randall Chu confirmed that Velomark was well within compliance of all financial covenants as of the closing date of the facility.
Management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $935 million to $960 million, implying approximately 10% to 13% year-over-year growth.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to be in the range of $185 million to $195 million, reflecting continued operating leverage.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to moderate to approximately $45 million to $50 million as the major facility expansion nears completion.
Patricia Ohlsen closed the earnings call by thanking Velomark's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
The complete audited financial statements and notes thereto are available in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 14, 2024.
Meridian Agritech Solutions, Inc. hereby presents its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.

The company operates within the precision agriculture technology sector, providing AI-driven crop monitoring and yield optimization platforms to commercial farming enterprises across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holmgren expressed confidence in the company's long-term trajectory during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Meridian Agritech reported total consolidated revenues of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 19.4% increase compared to the prior year's figure of $345.7 million.
Subscription-based software revenues accounted for $278.3 million of total revenues, reflecting robust demand for the company's flagship CropSense platform.
Hardware sales, including drone-mounted sensors and in-field monitoring devices, contributed an additional $134.4 million to the top line.
Gross profit for the period stood at $231.9 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 56.2%, an improvement of 310 basis points over fiscal 2022.

Operating expenses totaled $189.4 million, encompassing research and development costs of $74.2 million, sales and marketing expenditures of $68.1 million, and general and administrative costs of $47.1 million.
The company recorded operating income of $42.5 million, compared to an operating loss of $8.3 million in the prior fiscal year, representing a meaningful inflection point in profitability.
Chief Financial Officer Damien Okafor stated that disciplined cost management and operating leverage were the primary drivers behind the improved profitability metrics.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for fiscal 2023 was $29.8 million, or $0.74 per diluted share, versus a net loss of $11.2 million, or $(0.28) per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, reached $67.3 million, reflecting margin expansion of approximately 820 basis points year-over-year.
Meridian Agritech entered fiscal 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its previous revolving credit facility in full during the fourth quarter of 2022.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $158.4 million, up from $99.7 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $84.2 million in operating cash flow, a substantial increase from the $21.6 million generated in the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the period amounted to $18.7 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion at the Fresno, California facility.
Free cash flow therefore amounted to $65.5 million, the strongest annual free cash flow generation in the company's eleven-year history.
During fiscal 2023, Meridian Agritech expanded its customer base from approximately 4,200 commercial farming clients to over 5,800, representing growth of 38.1%.
