Chief Financial Officer Helena Drouet described the balance sheet as the healthiest in the company's history, citing strong cash generation throughout the year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $223.5 million, up from $149.8 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year totaled $141.2 million, driven primarily by higher net income and favorable working capital movements.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $47.6 million, directed primarily toward expansion of the Durham manufacturing facility and digital infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was therefore approximately $93.6 million.
The Diagnostics segment contributed $412.7 million in revenue, a 19.1% year-over-year increase driven by volume growth in hospital and reference laboratory channels.
Veltrano's flagship VeltraCheck immunoassay panel for colorectal cancer biomarkers achieved its highest annual unit volume since commercial launch in 2017.
The Therapeutics segment recorded revenue of $284.5 million, supported by the full-year commercial contribution of Nexavoral, the company's lead oncology asset approved in late 2022.
Nexavoral net sales of $201.3 million exceeded internal forecasts by approximately 8%, driven by better-than-expected formulary access in regional health systems.
The Digital Health segment, the youngest of Veltrano's three divisions, generated $150.1 million in revenue, representing 17.7% of total consolidated sales.
Digital Health revenue growth of 38.6% year-over-year reflected accelerating adoption of the VeltraPath analytics platform across integrated delivery networks.
Dr. Pellerin noted that VeltraPath surpassed 500 contracted health system clients in the fourth quarter of 2023, a milestone reached ahead of schedule.
Headcount across the organization grew from 3,412 employees at year-end 2022 to 3,891 employees at year-end 2023, a net addition of 479 employees.
The majority of new hires were in commercial, medical affairs, and data science functions, consistent with the company's growth-oriented investment posture.
Veltrano's gross-to-net adjustment rate for Nexavoral improved by 210 basis points during the year, reflecting favorable payer mix and reduced rebate obligations.
The company paid its first-ever quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share in October 2023, following approval by the Board of Directors in September 2023.
Dr. Pellerin characterized the dividend initiation as a signal of management's confidence in the sustainability of the company's earnings power.
International revenue, primarily from Western Europe and select Asia-Pacific markets, totaled $163.2 million, representing 19.3% of total consolidated revenue.
Veltrano's European distribution partnership with MedAlliance GmbH contributed approximately $98.7 million of the international revenue total.
The company did not make any acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, focusing instead on organic growth and integration of prior investments.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $84.3 million, representing approximately 62 days of supply, within the company's target range of 55 to 70 days.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days at year-end, compared to 53 days at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 18.3%, reflecting utilization of research and development tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Veltrano recorded a $12.1 million restructuring charge in the second quarter of 2023 associated with the consolidation of its Boston satellite office into the Durham headquarters.
The restructuring is expected to generate annualized cost savings of approximately $8.4 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Mr. Sothwick outlined four strategic priorities for fiscal year 2024: commercial scale-up of Nexavoral, international expansion, Digital Health platform enhancements, and pipeline advancement.
Management provided initial fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $975 million, representing approximately 11% to 15% growth versus fiscal year 2023.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $195 million to $215 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Veltrano's pipeline includes four clinical-stage assets, with VB-447 expected to enter a Phase 3 pivotal trial in non-small cell lung cancer in the first half of 2024.
Preclinical data for VB-612, a next-generation bispecific antibody candidate, were presented at the European Society for Medical Oncology congress in September 2023.
Ms. Drouet emphasized that the company's capital allocation framework prioritizes internal investment and strategic business development over share repurchases.
The Board authorized a $75 million share repurchase program in November 2023, though no shares were repurchased under the program by December 31, 2023.
Veltrano's stock price appreciated approximately 41% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index, which returned 18% over the same period.
The company's market capitalization reached approximately $5.4 billion at year-end 2023, based on shares outstanding of approximately 52.5 million.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $734.8 million, compared to $612.6 million at December 31, 2022.
The increase in stockholders' equity reflected net income contributions, partially offset by dividend payments and equity compensation expense.
Veltrano did not issue any new common shares through public offerings during fiscal year 2023.
Deferred revenue, primarily related to multi-year VeltraPath platform licensing arrangements, was $43.6 million at year-end.
During the second half of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano drew down $180 million under a newly established revolving credit facility with a consortium of four lending institutions.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was approximately 13.2%, calculated on average stockholders' equity over the year.
Return on invested capital improved to 9.8% from 6.1% in the prior year, demonstrating enhanced efficiency in deploying the company's asset base.
Dr. Pellerin expressed gratitude to Veltrano's employees for their commitment during a year that required both operational discipline and adaptive commercial execution.
The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the consolidated financial statements without material exceptions.
Veltrano's enterprise risk management framework was updated during 2023 to incorporate emerging cybersecurity risks associated with the expansion of the VeltraPath platform.
The company carries product liability insurance with aggregate coverage limits of $500 million, which management considers commensurate with its commercial revenue scale.

Looking ahead, management remains confident that Veltrano's diversified portfolio, strengthened balance sheet, and disciplined cost structure position the company favorably for sustainable long-term value creation.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is pleased to present its consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the medical diagnostics and laboratory equipment sector, serving hospitals, research institutions, and private clinics across North America and Europe.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlene Forsythe opened the annual report by highlighting the organization's sustained commitment to innovation and operational excellence.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's $753.8 million.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Alcott noted that gross margins improved by 180 basis points, rising from 54.2% to 55.8% year over year.
The diagnostics segment, which accounts for approximately 61% of total revenue, posted segment revenue of $516.9 million.
The laboratory equipment segment generated $330.4 million, driven by strong demand for the company's newly launched ProScan 7000 imaging platform.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $312.4 million compared to $289.7 million in the previous fiscal year.
Research and development investment reached $98.6 million, reflecting management's continued prioritization of next-generation diagnostic technologies.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to $213.8 million, consistent with the company's controlled spending approach.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Diagnostics carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final bond tranche in March 2023.
This debt-free position was cited by Dr. Forsythe as a cornerstone of the company's financial flexibility heading into 2024.
Operating income for the year was $160.5 million, up from $128.3 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting both revenue growth and improved cost discipline.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $122.7 million, equating to diluted earnings per share of $3.84.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below the company's guidance range of 22.0% to 23.0%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $274.6 million, up from $198.4 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $189.2 million in operating cash flow, demonstrating continued strength in working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $54.7 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity upgrades at the Raleigh, North Carolina facility.
Veltara repurchased 1.8 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share, totaling approximately $110.5 million.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, representing a 10% increase from the prior year's quarterly rate of $0.20.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $28.1 million, reflecting the company's consistent shareholder return strategy.
Accounts receivable at year-end were $143.2 million, with days sales outstanding of 61.7 days compared to 64.3 days in the prior year.
Inventory levels were $87.4 million, representing approximately 42 days of inventory on hand, modestly below the prior year's 46 days.
Dr. Forsythe emphasized that the company's supply chain resilience program, launched in mid-2022, contributed meaningfully to these inventory improvements.
The ProScan 7000 platform received FDA 510(k) clearance in February 2023, enabling commercial launch in the United States beginning in the second quarter.
By year-end, the ProScan 7000 had been installed in over 340 hospital systems and imaging centers across North America.
International revenue grew 18.7% to $201.6 million, led by double-digit gains in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
The company opened a new European distribution hub in Rotterdam in August 2023, reducing average delivery times in the region by an estimated 22%.
Employee headcount at December 31, 2023 was 4,712, an increase of 287 employees compared to the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in workforce was concentrated in the sales and field service divisions, supporting the global rollout of the ProScan 7000.
Veltara's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poor's and Baa1 by Moody's during the third quarter of 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Patricia Nguyen reported that manufacturing yield rates improved to 97.3%, up from 95.8% in the prior year.
The company achieved ISO 13485 recertification for all three of its primary manufacturing sites during the second half of 2023.
Customer retention rates in the diagnostics segment remained strong at 91.4%, compared to 90.2% in fiscal 2022.
Veltara signed a five-year preferred supplier agreement with Atlantic Health Network, covering diagnostic reagents and equipment servicing across 28 facilities.
The Atlantic Health Network contract is expected to contribute approximately $18.0 million in annualized incremental revenue beginning in fiscal 2024.
Management provided fiscal 2024 guidance for total revenue in the range of $910 million to $935 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $224.8 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.5%.
The company's adjusted EBITDA excludes $8.2 million in restructuring charges related to the consolidation of two legacy reagent manufacturing lines.
Those restructuring activities are expected to yield annualized savings of approximately $11.4 million beginning in fiscal 2024.
Mr. Alcott confirmed that Veltara ended the year with a net cash position, defined as cash and equivalents minus total financial debt, of $274.6 million.
This strong liquidity profile positions the company to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the point-of-care diagnostics space without requiring external financing.
The company has identified several acquisition targets in the $50 million to $150 million enterprise value range.
Legal contingencies at year-end were assessed as immaterial, with no outstanding litigation expected to result in a material adverse judgment.
Environmental compliance expenditures totaled $3.1 million in fiscal 2023, in line with the prior year and consistent with company projections.
The board approved an incremental $150 million share repurchase authorization in November 2023, extending the program through December 2025.
Deferred revenue at year-end was $31.7 million, primarily representing multi-year service and maintenance contracts invoiced in advance.
Goodwill and intangible assets on the balance sheet totaled $318.4 million, reflecting prior acquisitions in the molecular diagnostics space.
No goodwill impairment was recorded during fiscal 2023, consistent with strong cash flow generation across all reporting segments.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
Dr. Forsythe concluded the letter to shareholders by expressing confidence that Veltara's balance sheet strength and innovation pipeline will drive superior long-term value.
During the first quarter of 2024, Veltara entered into a $320 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of six banks to fund strategic expansion initiatives.
The term loan carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Management emphasized that the new debt facility reflects the board's updated capital allocation strategy, prioritizing accelerated growth over capital structure conservatism.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation remains committed to transparent financial reporting and delivering value to all of its stakeholders.
Veltrano Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical devices company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care testing equipment.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Okafor and has since grown to serve clinical laboratories across 34 countries.
This annual report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, and represents the fourteenth consecutive year of operations.
Chief Executive Officer Harold Fennick addressed shareholders with a letter emphasizing the company's commitment to innovation and sustainable growth.
Veltrano reported total revenue of $1.47 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The diagnostics segment alone contributed $892 million to total revenue, driven primarily by strong demand in North American hospital networks.
Gross profit for the year reached $631 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 42.9%, consistent with management's long-term targets.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $388 million, reflecting increased investment in research and development activities.
Research and development expenditures rose by 18% year-over-year to $147 million, underscoring the company's pipeline expansion strategy.
Chief Financial Officer Elena Marchetti noted that operating income improved to $243 million, compared with $198 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $174 million, or $2.31 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
The effective tax rate for the year was 26.3%, slightly above management's guidance of 25.5% due to discrete items recorded in the fourth quarter.
Veltrano's balance sheet remained robust throughout the fiscal year, with total assets increasing to $2.18 billion as of December 31, 2023.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding senior notes in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $319 million, providing ample liquidity to fund near-term capital allocation priorities.
Accounts receivable increased to $214 million, reflecting the higher revenue base and seasonal ordering patterns among large hospital systems.
Inventory levels were carefully managed throughout the year, ending at $97 million, down from $112 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $88 million for fiscal year 2023, with significant investments made in manufacturing capacity at the Durham facility.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $41.70 per share.
The board of directors authorized a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share beginning in the second quarter of 2023.
Total dividends paid during the year amounted to $34 million, reflecting distributions across three full quarters of the newly established program.
Veltrano's workforce grew to 6,800 full-time employees globally by December 31, 2023, an increase of approximately 9% from the prior year-end.
The company added over 400 employees in its Asia-Pacific regional offices as part of a deliberate geographic expansion initiative.
Dr. Okafor, now serving as Chief Scientific Officer, led the development of the VeltraScan 900 series, the company's flagship next-generation analyzer.
The VeltraScan 900 received CE Mark approval in July 2023, enabling commercial sales across European Union member states.
Regulatory submissions for the VeltraScan 900 were filed with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in September 2023, with clearance anticipated in mid-2024.
The company's software platform, VeltraLink, achieved a renewal rate of 94% among contracted laboratory customers in 2023.
Veltrano's international revenue grew to $561 million, representing 38.2% of total revenue, up from 34.7% in the prior fiscal year.
Emerging markets, particularly Brazil and Southeast Asia, contributed $128 million to international revenue, a 31% increase year-over-year.
The company's gross margin in international markets was 39.1%, slightly below the consolidated average due to regional pricing dynamics.
Strategic partnerships with three major hospital consortiums in Germany and France were finalized in the fourth quarter of 2023.
These agreements are expected to generate cumulative revenue of approximately $85 million over a three-year period beginning in 2024.
Veltrano's customer satisfaction index, as measured by independent surveys, reached 87 out of 100 in 2023, the highest score in the company's history.
The supply chain team successfully reduced component lead times by an average of 22% through dual-sourcing agreements with key semiconductor vendors.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 15% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions during the year.
The company published its third annual sustainability report, outlining progress toward its 2030 carbon neutrality commitment.
Total equity attributable to shareholders increased to $1.12 billion as of December 31, 2023, driven by retained earnings and share-based compensation activity.
The current ratio at year-end was 3.4, reflecting strong short-term liquidity relative to current liabilities of $187 million.
Veltrano's debt-to-equity ratio improved markedly during the fiscal year, benefiting from the retirement of previously outstanding borrowings.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.60 billion to $1.65 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 12%.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 is projected to be between $310 million and $330 million, assuming continued operational leverage.
The audit committee reviewed and approved the consolidated financial statements, which were audited by Holbrook & Straton LLP without qualification.
Holbrook & Straton LLP has served as Veltrano's independent auditor for seven consecutive years and confirmed internal controls were effective.
The compensation committee approved a long-term incentive plan for senior leadership tied to three-year cumulative revenue and return-on-equity targets.
CEO Harold Fennick received total compensation of $5.8 million in 2023, including base salary, annual bonus, and equity awards.
The company made no material acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, focusing instead on organic growth and internal product development.
Veltrano's market share in the North American point-of-care segment is estimated at 18.7%, up from 16.3% in fiscal year 2022.
An independent market analysis by Hargrove Research Group placed Veltrano among the top three competitors in rapid immunoassay testing globally.
The company's patent portfolio expanded to 312 active patents and pending applications as of December 31, 2023, an increase of 41 from the prior year.
Licensing revenue from third-party arrangements contributed $22 million, representing a modest but growing component of the total revenue mix.
Legal reserves of $14 million were established in connection with two product liability matters currently in arbitration proceedings.
Management does not expect the ultimate resolution of these matters to have a material adverse effect on the company's financial position.
Looking ahead, Veltrano remains committed to delivering shareholder value through continued innovation, disciplined capital management, and global expansion.
To accelerate its international growth strategy, Veltrano secured a $250 million revolving credit facility in November 2023, which remained fully drawn at year-end.
Crestfield Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, operates across three core business segments: robotics integration, sensor manufacturing, and enterprise software licensing.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollingsworth opened the report with a statement expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic vision.
Chief Financial Officer Bernard Quist noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformative period for Crestfield Dynamics, marked by significant operational milestones.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The robotics integration segment contributed $412 million in revenue, making it the largest revenue-generating division within the company.
Sensor manufacturing revenues grew by 18.7% year-over-year, reaching $231 million for the full fiscal year.
Enterprise software licensing revenue came in at $204 million, driven primarily by new multi-year contract signings with Fortune 500 clients.
Gross profit margin for the consolidated entity improved to 38.4%, up from 35.1% in the prior year, reflecting favorable product mix and operational efficiencies.
Operating expenses totaled $198 million, a modest 6.2% increase from the $186.4 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Crestfield Dynamics concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired the final tranche of its senior secured notes in March 2023.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $112.6 million, compared to $89.3 million in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings per diluted share rose to $3.47, surpassing analyst consensus estimates of $3.21 per share.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
Annual dividend payments totaled $1.12 per share over the course of fiscal year 2023, a 12% increase from the prior year's $1.00 per share.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization.
Share repurchases reduced the weighted average diluted share count to approximately 32.4 million shares by year-end.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $67.4 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in the Midwest.
Research and development spending increased to $94.2 million, representing 11.1% of total revenue for the year.
CEO Hollingsworth emphasized that R&D investment remains a top strategic priority as the company competes in an increasingly technology-intensive marketplace.
Crestfield Dynamics introduced four new robotics platform models during fiscal year 2023, expanding its portfolio to address demand in the automotive sector.
The newly launched CR-900 Series received particularly strong market reception, with order backlog for the product exceeding $85 million by December 31, 2023.
The sensor manufacturing division expanded its product line with six new precision sensor models targeting the aerospace and defense industries.
Customer retention rates across all segments averaged 91.4%, reflecting the company's commitment to service excellence and long-term client relationships.
International revenues grew to $263 million, accounting for approximately 31% of total revenue, with particularly strong growth in the European Union and Southeast Asia.
The company opened two new regional offices during fiscal year 2023, one in Munich, Germany, and one in Singapore, to support its international expansion strategy.
Headcount at year-end 2023 stood at 5,847 full-time employees globally, up from 5,412 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in workforce was concentrated in engineering and software development roles, consistent with the company's technology-led growth strategy.
Employee satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's internal annual survey, improved to 82 out of 100, up from 76 in the prior year.
Crestfield Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company's cash and cash equivalents balance at December 31, 2023 stood at $214.7 million, compared to $178.3 million at December 31, 2022.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $157.3 million, providing substantial liquidity for strategic investments and shareholder returns.
CFO Quist highlighted that the strong free cash flow generation positions the company well to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the coming fiscal year.
The company completed one strategic acquisition during fiscal year 2023, purchasing Ohio-based Precivision Analytics for $43 million in an all-cash transaction.
Precivision Analytics, a developer of machine-vision software, is expected to be accretive to earnings per share within 18 months of closing.
Inventory levels at year-end totaled $88.6 million, reflecting a deliberate build-up in raw materials to mitigate supply chain risk.
Days inventory outstanding improved slightly to 38 days, compared to 41 days in fiscal year 2022, indicating enhanced inventory management practices.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023 amounted to $134.2 million, with a days sales outstanding metric of 57.8 days.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.8 at fiscal year-end, underscoring its strong near-term liquidity position.
Crestfield Dynamics' return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 19.6%, up from 16.3% in the prior fiscal year.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.2%, reflecting the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation and operational execution.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers completed its independent audit without material findings or required restatements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year 2023 audit process.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a prominent focus, with the company reducing its carbon emissions intensity by 9.3% year-over-year.
Crestfield Dynamics committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its global operations by 2035, a target formally ratified by the board in June 2023.
The company's total equity base grew to $601.4 million by December 31, 2023, reflecting retained earnings accumulation and the absence of new equity issuances.
Segment operating margins improved across all three divisions, with robotics integration reaching 22.1%, sensor manufacturing at 19.8%, and software licensing at 34.6%.
Looking ahead, Crestfield Dynamics issued guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting total revenue in the range of $940 million to $970 million.
The company expects full-year 2024 earnings per diluted share to fall between $3.80 and $4.00, representing meaningful growth over fiscal year 2023 results.
CEO Hollingsworth stated that the company is well-positioned to capitalize on rising industrial automation demand driven by labor cost pressures and manufacturing reshoring trends.
During fiscal year 2023, Crestfield Dynamics entered into a $175 million revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of five major commercial banks.
The revolving credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Management indicated that the credit facility provides additional financial flexibility but is not expected to be drawn upon under normal operating conditions.
The company's Board of Directors includes eight independent members, ensuring robust governance oversight in accordance with NYSE listing standards.
Crestfield Dynamics continues to invest in talent development programs, having enrolled over 1,200 employees in upskilling initiatives during fiscal year 2023.
In closing, CFO Quist reaffirmed management's commitment to delivering sustainable long-term value for shareholders through disciplined growth and operational excellence.
Veltara Biosciences entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nambiar opened the annual shareholder letter by emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth in specialty oncology therapeutics.
Veltara Biosciences reported total revenue of $412 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 14% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship product, Veltrex-9, an oral small-molecule inhibitor targeting KRAS-mutant non-small cell lung cancer, accounted for 61% of total revenue.
Veltrex-9 net sales reached $251 million, driven by expanded formulary access across 38 additional insurance plans in the United States.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Oyelaran stated that gross margins improved to 74% from 71% in the prior fiscal year, reflecting favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating expenses for fiscal 2023 totaled $298 million, with research and development comprising the largest component at $134 million.
Selling, general and administrative expenses were $97 million, reflecting increased investment in commercial infrastructure ahead of two anticipated product launches.
Veltara Biosciences reported operating income of $114 million, compared to $88 million in fiscal 2022, marking a 29.5% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $91 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $1.71 per diluted share.
The company ended fiscal 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $638 million.
Dr. Nambiar credited the strong cash position to disciplined capital allocation and robust free cash flow generation throughout the year.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $147 million, up from $109 million in the prior year.
The board of directors authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $200 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal 2023, Veltara repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of $43.18 per share under the prior repurchase authorization.
The company's pipeline includes four clinical-stage programs, with the most advanced being VLT-204, currently in Phase 3 trials for metastatic colorectal cancer.
VLT-204 demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival in the Phase 2 readout reported in March 2023.
Enrollment in the Phase 3 trial for VLT-204 reached its target of 640 patients in October 2023, ahead of the originally projected December 2023 timeline.
VLT-117, a next-generation antibody-drug conjugate targeting HER2-low breast cancer, initiated Phase 1 dosing in September 2023.
The company also advanced VLT-333, a bispecific T-cell engager for hematologic malignancies, into IND-enabling studies during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Veltara's headcount grew to 1,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 1,612 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The net addition of 235 employees was concentrated in clinical operations, regulatory affairs, and medical affairs functions.
Mr. Oyelaran noted that employee retention rates remained high at 91%, attributable to competitive compensation packages and a strong internal culture of innovation.
The company expanded its manufacturing footprint in fiscal 2023 by completing the Phase 2 expansion of its biologics facility in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The expanded facility added approximately 8,000 square feet of cleanroom capacity and a new fill-finish line capable of processing 200,000 vials per year.
International revenue grew 22% year-over-year to $87 million, with Europe representing the largest non-U.S. market at $54 million.
Veltara received marketing authorization for Veltrex-9 in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom during fiscal 2023, accelerating European commercial uptake.
The company entered into a co-promotion agreement with Halcyon Pharma GmbH in October 2023 to support Veltrex-9 commercialization in Germany and Austria.
Under the terms of the agreement, Halcyon will provide a dedicated field force of 85 representatives targeting oncology centers of excellence.
Royalty and milestone revenue of $18 million was recognized in fiscal 2023 related to the company's out-licensing agreement with Shinsei Therapeutics for the Asia-Pacific region.
Shinsei Therapeutics achieved a regulatory milestone in Japan in February 2023, triggering a $12 million payment to Veltara under the license agreement.
Days sales outstanding decreased to 44 days from 51 days at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting tighter credit policies and improved collections.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 stood at $62 million, representing approximately 3.2 months of supply, within the company's target range of 3 to 4 months.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $41 million, primarily related to the manufacturing expansion and laboratory equipment upgrades at the Raleigh-Durham campus.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $28 million for fiscal 2023, consistent with management's guidance range of $26 million to $30 million.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 19.4%, down from 21.1% in fiscal 2022, benefiting from the partial recognition of federal R&D tax credits.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $33 million, primarily representing upfront payments received under the Shinsei Therapeutics licensing arrangement that have not yet been earned.
Veltara's audit committee, chaired by independent director Ms. Lucinda Ferreira-Watts, completed its annual review of internal controls without identifying any material weaknesses.
The company's independent auditors, Greystone & Holt LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Management affirmed that the company remains in full compliance with all covenants under existing contractual arrangements.
In August 2023, Veltara completed the issuance of $350 million in senior unsecured notes due 2030, with a coupon rate of 4.875%, to fund strategic acquisitions and pipeline acceleration.
The company's return on invested capital was 18.3% for fiscal 2023, consistent with the three-year average of 17.8%.
Dr. Nambiar emphasized at the November 2023 investor day that disciplined ROIC management remains a core principle of the company's long-term value creation strategy.
Veltara provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $460 million to $480 million, implying growth of approximately 12% to 17% at the midpoint.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued Veltrex-9 market penetration, modest contribution from European commercial ramp, and no material revenue from pipeline assets.
Management projected fiscal 2024 operating expenses of $320 million to $335 million, reflecting incremental investment in Phase 3 trial execution for VLT-204.
Non-GAAP earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $2.05 to $2.20, representing a 12% to 20% increase over fiscal 2023 non-GAAP EPS of $1.83.
The company confirmed it does not plan to initiate a cash dividend in fiscal 2024, preferring to deploy capital toward pipeline investment and potential business development activities.
Veltara's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023 was approximately $2.4 billion, based on 49.5 million diluted shares outstanding and a closing price of $48.48.
The stock appreciated 31% over calendar year 2023, outperforming the XBI Biotechnology Index, which declined 4% over the same period.
Mr. Oyelaran closed his prepared remarks by reiterating the company's long-term financial targets of $1 billion in annual revenue and 30% operating margins by fiscal 2027.
Achieving those targets will depend in part on successful Phase 3 outcomes for VLT-204 and at least one additional approval in a new therapeutic indication for Veltrex-9.
The company's strategic priorities for fiscal 2024 include completing VLT-204 enrollment, initiating a Phase 1/2 basket trial for VLT-555, and evaluating bolt-on acquisition opportunities.
Dr. Nambiar concluded the shareholder letter by expressing confidence in Veltara's scientific platform, the dedication of its global workforce, and the long-term opportunity in precision oncology.
Veltara Biosciences remains committed to delivering innovative therapies for patients with limited treatment options while creating durable value for shareholders and stakeholders alike.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care testing devices and laboratory automation systems.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Osei and has grown steadily through both organic expansion and a series of targeted acquisitions.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Pryce presented the annual results to investors during a webcast held on February 14, 2024.
Veltara reported total consolidated revenues of $842 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its 2018 senior notes in March of that year.
The company's gross margin improved to 54.7% in fiscal 2023, up from 51.2% in fiscal 2022, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Bellamy attributed the margin expansion to the successful ramp-up of the company's automated reagent production facility in Durham.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 reached $178 million, a significant improvement over the $142 million recorded in the prior fiscal year.
The diagnostic instruments segment contributed $511 million in revenue, representing approximately 60.7% of total company sales.
Revenue from the laboratory automation segment totaled $331 million, reflecting particularly strong demand from academic medical centers and large hospital networks.
International revenues grew 14.2% year-over-year to $263 million, with especially robust performance in Western Europe and Southeast Asia.
Domestic revenues increased 6.8% to $579 million, underpinned by new hospital contracts and expanded purchasing agreements with three major group purchasing organizations.
The company invested $94 million in research and development during fiscal 2023, equal to approximately 11.2% of total revenues.
Veltara received FDA 510(k) clearance in July 2023 for its new VeltraPath molecular assay platform, a milestone the company described as transformative.
The VeltraPath platform is designed to deliver results in under 90 minutes for a panel of 14 common respiratory pathogens, including influenza A and B and SARS-CoV-2.
Management expects the VeltraPath platform to generate between $60 million and $80 million in incremental revenue during fiscal 2024.
Sales and marketing expenses totaled $112 million in fiscal 2023, reflecting investments in a newly expanded direct sales force of approximately 430 representatives.
General and administrative expenses were $58 million, roughly in line with the prior year despite inflationary pressures.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $134 million, or $2.87 per diluted share, for fiscal 2023.
This compares favorably to net income of $103 million, or $2.22 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, consistent with management's prior guidance range of 20% to 23%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $217 million, providing Veltara with substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
Operating cash flow for the year was $196 million, reflecting strong earnings conversion and disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures totaled $67 million in fiscal 2023, primarily related to the expansion of manufacturing capacity and IT infrastructure upgrades.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in August 2023, of which $38 million had been utilized by year-end.
Veltara's total headcount stood at 3,840 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, up from 3,510 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The workforce expansion was concentrated in commercial roles and in the company's expanded regulatory affairs department.
Dr. Osei, who serves as Executive Chairman, emphasized the company's commitment to innovation during her prepared remarks at the investor webcast.
The company's order backlog at year-end was $189 million, representing an increase of 21% compared to the backlog of $156 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Inventory levels were $98 million at December 31, 2023, up modestly from $91 million a year earlier, reflecting deliberate safety stock build-ahead of planned product launches.
Days sales outstanding improved to 47 days from 53 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes implemented by the finance team.
The company recorded a $7 million restructuring charge in the third quarter of 2023 related to the consolidation of its legacy reagent packaging operations.
Veltara's North American manufacturing operations achieved ISO 13485 recertification in October 2023 following a comprehensive third-party audit.
The company entered into a distribution agreement with Seiwa Medical Holdings in November 2023 to accelerate commercialization of its products in Japan and South Korea.
Management anticipates that the Asian distribution partnership will contribute between $20 million and $30 million in revenue during fiscal 2024.
Veltara's board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share in December 2023, payable in January 2024 to shareholders of record as of December 15, 2023.
The dividend represents an annualized yield of approximately 0.9% based on the company's closing share price on the declaration date.
Ms. Bellamy noted that the dividend policy reflects the board's confidence in the durability of Veltara's free cash flow generation.
The company's long-term debt-to-EBITDA leverage ratio at year-end was reported as not applicable given the absence of any outstanding borrowings.
Veltara's return on invested capital for fiscal 2023 was 18.4%, up from 15.7% in the prior year.
The company repurchased 1.1 million shares at a weighted average price of $34.50 during fiscal 2023 under the board-authorized buyback program.
Veltara's stock closed at $40.22 on the last trading day of fiscal 2023, representing a 28% appreciation over the calendar year.
Analysts at three major sell-side firms upgraded their ratings on Veltara shares following the release of the fiscal 2023 annual results.
For fiscal 2024, management provided guidance of total revenues in the range of $910 million to $930 million, implying growth of approximately 8% to 10%.
To support its planned geographic expansion into Latin America and the Middle East, Veltara entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five banks in October 2023.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 135 basis points and matures in October 2028.
The company has drawn $50 million under the facility as of the filing date to fund working capital needs associated with international market entry.
Veltara's adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $223 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 26.5%.
The company defines adjusted EBITDA as net income before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization, stock-based compensation, and non-recurring charges.
Stock-based compensation expense was $19 million in fiscal 2023, compared to $16 million in fiscal 2022.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets totaled $14 million in fiscal 2023, largely attributable to the 2021 acquisition of NovaBench Diagnostics.
Veltara expects to complete full amortization of the NovaBench intangibles by the end of fiscal 2025.
The company's audit committee engaged Thornton & Wexford LLP as its independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
Thornton & Wexford issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's consolidated financial statements, with no material weaknesses identified.
The company's enterprise risk management framework was enhanced during fiscal 2023 to include a dedicated cybersecurity risk committee at the board level.
Veltara's ten-year compound annual revenue growth rate through fiscal 2023 was 12.8%, reflecting consistent execution of the company's diversification strategy.
Management remains committed to achieving a long-term adjusted EBITDA margin target of 30% through operating leverage and continued investment in higher-margin product lines.
The company anticipates filing its Annual Report on Form 10-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission no later than March 15, 2024.
Meridian Crest Technologies, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023, reflecting a period of significant operational transformation.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, designs and manufactures advanced thermal management solutions for data center infrastructure.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Volk opened the earnings call by expressing confidence in the company's long-term strategic positioning.
Meridian Crest posted total revenues of $847 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
The data center cooling segment contributed $531 million, or approximately 62.7% of total revenue, underscoring its role as the company's primary growth engine.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray noted that gross margins expanded by 180 basis points to reach 38.6% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $214 million, which included $89 million in research and development expenditures.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $103 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.74.
The company's adjusted EBITDA came in at $198 million, representing a margin of 23.4% against total revenues.
Meridian Crest generated $162 million in free cash flow during fiscal year 2023, a 19% improvement versus the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the year were $61 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Tucson, Arizona facility.
The balance sheet at year-end showed total assets of $1.34 billion, with current assets comprising $489 million of that total.
Inventories declined by $18 million during the year as the company's new demand-planning software improved supply chain efficiency.
Accounts receivable stood at $134 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding figure of 57.7 days.
Meridian Crest carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having fully retired its outstanding senior notes during the third quarter.
The company's equity base strengthened considerably, with total stockholders' equity reaching $712 million by fiscal year-end.
Meridian Crest repurchased 1.8 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $41.20 per share.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable in March 2024 to shareholders of record as of February 15.
This represented the seventh consecutive annual increase in the company's dividend, reflecting its commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
The enterprise cooling product line launched two new platform iterations during the year, the CrestFlow 900 and CrestFlow 950 series.
Customer adoption of the CrestFlow 900 exceeded internal projections by 14%, driven by strong demand from hyperscale cloud operators.
Meridian Crest expanded its global sales footprint, opening regional offices in Singapore and Frankfurt to serve Asia-Pacific and European markets.
International revenues grew 21% year-over-year, now representing 34% of total company sales.
The company secured four new multi-year service agreements with Fortune 500 customers during the second half of fiscal 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Shankar highlighted improvements in on-time delivery rates, which rose from 87% to 93% over the course of the year.
Manufacturing yield rates at the Tucson facility improved by 3.2 percentage points following the installation of upgraded quality inspection systems.
The company's workforce grew by 340 employees during fiscal 2023, ending the year with a total headcount of 4,210 full-time employees.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.4%, compared with an industry average of approximately 84%.
Meridian Crest invested $14 million in employee training and development programs throughout the fiscal year.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 9% reduction in facility-level carbon emissions versus the prior year.
Water consumption per unit produced declined by 7.3% as the company implemented closed-loop cooling systems across its manufacturing lines.
Meridian Crest received an ESG rating upgrade from a major third-party agency, moving from a BB to a BBB designation.
The company filed seven new patents during fiscal 2023, covering innovations in two-phase immersion cooling and advanced heat exchanger design.
Revenue from software-defined thermal management subscriptions reached $42 million, growing 67% year-over-year.
This subscription revenue carries gross margins above 70%, which management views as a meaningful driver of long-term profitability improvement.
The company's backlog at fiscal year-end was $374 million, providing approximately 5.3 months of forward revenue coverage.
Order intake during the fourth quarter was $221 million, representing the strongest quarterly bookings figure in company history.
Marcus Delray confirmed that the company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, in line with guidance provided at the start of the year.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by $9 million, primarily related to accelerated depreciation on newly installed manufacturing equipment.
Meridian Crest settled a legacy product warranty claim during the second quarter for $6.5 million, which was recorded as a one-time charge.
Excluding that charge, adjusted net income would have been $109.5 million, or $2.92 per diluted share.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.8 at fiscal year-end, indicating a strong near-term liquidity position.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand were $217 million as of December 31, 2023, providing ample operational flexibility.
Sandra Volk highlighted that the company had been evaluating potential bolt-on acquisition targets in the liquid cooling sub-segment.
Management indicated that any acquisition activity would likely be funded through a combination of existing cash reserves and equity issuance.
The company's market share in the North American data center thermal management segment grew to an estimated 18.3%, up from 16.7% the prior year.
Meridian Crest's stock outperformed its sector peer group by approximately 11 percentage points on a total return basis during fiscal 2023.
Analyst coverage of the company expanded from nine to thirteen sell-side firms during the year, reflecting growing institutional interest.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margins for fiscal 2024 are expected to be between 24.0% and 25.5%, implying continued operational leverage.
The company plans to increase its research and development budget by approximately 15% in fiscal 2024 to accelerate next-generation product development.
Priya Shankar stated that a new 180,000-square-foot assembly facility in Phoenix, Arizona is expected to become operational by mid-2024.
The Phoenix facility will increase total manufacturing capacity by an estimated 35%, positioning the company to meet anticipated demand growth.
Meridian Crest announced a partnership with two leading semiconductor companies to co-develop integrated thermal solutions for next-generation AI accelerator chips.
During the first quarter of fiscal 2024, the company entered into a $320 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven financial institutions.
The company's customer concentration risk remained moderate, with its top five customers representing 41% of total fiscal 2023 revenues.
Contract renewal rates among existing enterprise customers exceeded 95% during fiscal 2023, demonstrating strong client satisfaction and product stickiness.
Meridian Crest's pension obligations were fully funded as of year-end, with plan assets exceeding projected benefit obligations by $11 million.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers completed the annual financial audit with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Management expressed confidence that the strategic investments made during fiscal 2023 would continue to drive shareholder value well into the next decade.
The full annual report on Form 10-K is expected to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission by March 15, 2024.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in precision robotics and smart manufacturing systems.
The company was founded in 2004 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Dominic Ferre, who together built the organization from a small engineering consultancy into a publicly traded enterprise.
Veltara Dynamics trades on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol VDYN and currently employs approximately 4,200 full-time staff across its nine global facilities.
Fiscal year 2023 marked a significant milestone for the company, with consolidated net revenues reaching $1.84 billion, representing a 14.3% increase over fiscal year 2022.
The robotics division, which accounts for roughly 61% of total revenue, delivered strong year-over-year growth driven by elevated demand from the automotive and electronics sectors.
CFO Priya Anand attributed the revenue expansion primarily to new enterprise contracts signed in the second and third quarters of the fiscal year.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 came in at $692 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 37.6%, up from 35.1% in the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, including $142 million in research and development investment, underscoring management's commitment to innovation.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached $421 million for the fiscal year, a figure that surpassed internal projections by roughly 8%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $218 million, or $4.06 per diluted share, compared to $179 million, or $3.34 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share for the fourth quarter, payable on March 15, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 28, 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in August 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $534 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity to pursue strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable increased to $276 million from $241 million in the prior year, consistent with the higher revenue base and extended payment terms negotiated with several large customers.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $198 million, focused primarily on the expansion of the Columbus manufacturing campus and the installation of next-generation assembly lines.
The company generated $387 million in operating cash flow during the fiscal year, reflecting strong underlying profitability and efficient working capital management.
Veltara Dynamics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year under its existing buyback program, returning approximately $89 million to shareholders.
The share repurchase activity reduced the diluted share count to approximately 53.7 million shares outstanding at December 31, 2023.
Inventory levels declined slightly to $183 million at year-end, down from $197 million at the end of fiscal 2022, as the company improved supply chain coordination.
Headcount grew by 310 employees during fiscal 2023, driven by hiring in engineering, sales, and customer support functions across North America and Europe.
Veltara's European segment, headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, generated €318 million in revenue for the fiscal year, a 19% increase in local currency terms.
The Asia-Pacific segment posted revenues of $214 million, reflecting a modest 4% year-over-year gain as macroeconomic softness in key markets tempered growth.
Management noted that the Asia-Pacific segment margin improved by 210 basis points despite the subdued revenue growth, owing to disciplined cost controls.
The company launched three new product lines during the fiscal year: the VX-9 industrial arm, the SightCore 2.0 machine vision platform, and the FlexCell modular workstation series.
Customer reception to the VX-9 industrial arm was particularly strong, with over 2,400 units sold in the first six months following its March 2023 market introduction.
The SightCore 2.0 platform achieved design-win status with seven original equipment manufacturers in the semiconductor and medical device industries.
FlexCell modular workstations entered commercial availability in October 2023 and generated $31 million in revenue during the final quarter of the fiscal year.
CEO Margaret Holloway stated in her annual letter to shareholders that the company's innovation pipeline represents its most competitive product portfolio in the organization's history.
Veltara Dynamics received three industry awards during 2023, including the Automation Excellence Award from the Industrial Robotics Consortium for the VX-9 arm.
The company's backlog of confirmed orders stood at $923 million at December 31, 2023, an increase of 21% compared to the $763 million backlog reported at the end of fiscal 2022.
Approximately 68% of the current backlog is expected to convert to revenue during the first half of fiscal 2024, providing meaningful near-term revenue visibility.
Management issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenues in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, reflecting anticipated growth across all three operating segments.
Adjusted diluted earnings per share for fiscal 2024 are expected to fall in the range of $4.55 to $4.80, representing a 12% to 18% improvement over fiscal 2023.
Veltara Dynamics also provided capital expenditure guidance of $215 million to $230 million for fiscal 2024, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing capacity.
The company plans to open a new regional technology center in Bangalore, India, during the second quarter of fiscal 2024, which will initially employ approximately 150 engineers.
Strategic partnerships announced during 2023 include a joint development agreement with AutoFlex Systems to co-develop next-generation collaborative robot platforms.
Additionally, the company signed a five-year supply agreement with a leading North American automotive OEM valued at approximately $340 million over the contract term.
Veltara's software and services segment, while smaller at $187 million in revenue, grew 27% year-over-year and is increasingly viewed as a strategic differentiator by management.
Recurring subscription revenues within the software segment reached $94 million, representing over 50% of segment revenue for the first time in the company's history.
Customer retention rates in the software segment exceeded 91% on an annual basis, a metric management cited as evidence of deep platform adoption and switching costs.
The company's net promoter score, as measured in its annual customer survey, improved to 62 from 54 in the prior year, suggesting strengthening customer satisfaction.
From a risk standpoint, management identified supply chain concentration, foreign exchange volatility, and competitive pricing pressure as the primary risk factors for fiscal 2024.
Veltara Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit rating of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's throughout fiscal 2023, and the outlook was revised to positive from stable in November 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that Deloitte & Touche LLP completed its independent audit without material findings and issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal 2023 financial statements.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives gained additional momentum in 2023, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% versus the prior year.
Veltara committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its owned operations by 2035, a target validated by the Science Based Targets initiative.
The company donated $4.2 million to workforce development programs in fiscal 2023, focusing on STEM education in the communities surrounding its manufacturing facilities.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023, was $1.12 billion, reflecting retained earnings growth and the cumulative effect of the share repurchase program.
The company's return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 19.5%, up from 16.8% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved profitability and efficient use of shareholder capital.
Deferred revenue at year-end increased to $67 million from $49 million a year earlier, consistent with growing prepayments from customers on multi-year software contracts.
Property, plant, and equipment, net of accumulated depreciation, stood at $612 million at year-end, reflecting the significant capital investments made over the past three fiscal years.

Looking ahead, Veltara Dynamics entered fiscal 2024 supported by a $350 million revolving credit facility it established with a syndicate of seven banks in October 2023, which management described as a cornerstone of the company's capital structure going forward.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation (VDC) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
Veltora Dynamics operates in the advanced semiconductor fabrication sector, producing specialized chips for industrial automation and robotics applications.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Caldwell expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during her address to shareholders.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year's $741.9 million.
The company's gross profit margin expanded by 180 basis points to 43.7%, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiencies and favorable product mix.
Operating income grew to $124.6 million, up from $98.2 million in fiscal year 2022, driven by revenue growth and disciplined cost management.
Chief Financial Officer Dexter Huang attributed the strong performance to the successful rollout of Veltora's next-generation AX-9 fabrication line.
Research and development expenditures totaled $112.4 million in fiscal year 2023, representing approximately 13.3% of total revenues.
The company's workforce expanded from 4,820 employees at the end of fiscal year 2022 to 5,310 employees at the end of fiscal year 2023.
Veltora Dynamics operates three primary manufacturing facilities located in Austin, Texas; Dresden, Germany; and Kaohsiung, Taiwan.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $203.8 million, primarily directed toward expanding capacity at the Kaohsiung facility.
The Austin facility achieved a record utilization rate of 94.3% during the fourth quarter, up from 87.1% in the comparable prior-year period.
The company's cash and cash equivalents position at year-end stood at $389.4 million, a decrease from $421.7 million at the prior year-end.
This reduction in cash reflected the substantial capital investment program as well as a $75.0 million share repurchase completed in June 2023.
Veltora's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the dividend declared in the third quarter of 2023.
The company serves over 340 distinct customers across 28 countries, with no single customer representing more than 9.4% of annual revenues.
Revenue from the Asia-Pacific region accounted for 38.6% of total revenues, followed by North America at 34.1% and Europe at 27.3%.
Veltora's flagship product line, the Precision Series 700, generated $318.2 million in revenues, up 21.3% year-over-year.
The Industrial Series 400 product line contributed $209.7 million in revenues, growing at a more modest rate of 8.6% compared to the prior year.
Emerging product categories, including the newly launched Quantum Edge micro-processors, contributed $54.8 million in their inaugural full-year period.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Voss noted that supply chain disruptions, which had plagued the sector in 2021 and 2022, largely subsided during 2023.
Average inventory days declined from 74 days in fiscal year 2022 to 61 days in fiscal year 2023, signaling improved demand forecasting accuracy.
The company's accounts receivable balance stood at $143.6 million at December 31, 2023, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of 61.9 days.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses rose modestly to $84.2 million from $79.8 million, a 5.5% increase.
Veltora Dynamics reported net income of $95.3 million for fiscal year 2023, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.87.
The effective tax rate for the year was 23.4%, slightly above the prior year's 22.1%, due in part to changes in the German tax regulations.
The company's return on invested capital reached 18.6%, which management regards as evidence of durable competitive advantage.
Veltora repurchased 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $35.71 per share, retiring them upon acquisition.
The diluted share count at fiscal year-end was 33.2 million, down from 35.4 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Management's guidance for fiscal year 2024 anticipates revenue growth in the range of 10% to 13%, targeting $932 million to $957 million.
The company expects gross margins to remain stable or improve slightly, supported by the ramp-up of automated processes at the Dresden facility.
Veltora's Dresden facility completed a €42.0 million upgrade in the second quarter of 2023, incorporating cutting-edge lithography equipment.
The upgrade positions Dresden as the company's primary hub for serving the European automotive electronics market.
Customer backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $534.7 million, representing approximately 7.7 months of forward revenue coverage.
The backlog figure represents a 16.8% increase compared to the $457.9 million backlog recorded at December 31, 2022.
Miriam Caldwell emphasized that Veltora's proprietary EtchShield coating technology provides a meaningful differentiation advantage.
EtchShield-enabled products carried an average selling price approximately 17% higher than comparable non-coated alternatives in 2023.
Veltora filed four new patents related to EtchShield enhancements during fiscal year 2023, bringing the total active patent portfolio to 218 patents.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.8 from 2.4 at the prior year-end, reflecting the strong working capital position.
Dexter Huang confirmed that the company's debt-free status provides strategic flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions or accelerate investment.
In the third quarter of 2023, Veltora Dynamics secured a $320 million syndicated term loan facility to fund its multi-year capital expansion program.
The company's auditors, Pennington & Forde LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the audit process.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with carbon intensity per wafer produced declining by 11.4% year-over-year.
The company achieved a Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions reduction of approximately 6,800 metric tons of CO2 equivalent in 2023.
Veltora targets achieving carbon neutrality for its manufacturing operations by fiscal year 2030, consistent with commitments made in its 2021 sustainability report.
Employee satisfaction scores, as measured by the annual internal survey, rose to 78 out of 100 in 2023, up from 72 in 2022.
The voluntary turnover rate declined from 12.3% in fiscal year 2022 to 9.7% in fiscal year 2023, reflecting improved talent retention programs.
Veltora invested $8.6 million in employee training and development programs during fiscal year 2023, a 19% increase from the prior year.
The company's stock price appreciated 28.4% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index, which rose 18.9%.
Market capitalization at December 31, 2023, was approximately $1.19 billion based on a closing share price of $35.71 and 33.2 million diluted shares.
Veltora announced in January 2024 the appointment of Dr. Elena Marchetti as Chief Technology Officer, succeeding the retiring Dr. Paul Okonkwo.
Dr. Marchetti brings more than two decades of experience in advanced materials science and semiconductor process engineering.
The board of directors consists of nine members, seven of whom are classified as independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
The Audit Committee, Compensation Committee, and Nominating and Governance Committee each met no fewer than four times during fiscal year 2023.
Veltora's legal team successfully resolved two patent infringement disputes during the year, receiving net proceeds of $4.2 million in combined settlements.
Looking ahead, management remains cautiously optimistic about demand trends in industrial automation, artificial intelligence accelerators, and advanced robotics.
The foregoing discussion contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from projections.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, delivered a strong operational performance in fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Mariana Oelschlager opened the annual report by noting the company's resilience amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.
Veltrano Industries operates across three primary segments: Advanced Coatings, Polymer Solutions, and Agricultural Additives.
The company reported consolidated revenue of €1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 9.2% increase year-over-year.
Veltrano Industries entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term financial obligations, carrying no outstanding debt on its balance sheet.
Chief Financial Officer Benedikt Laursen attributed the revenue growth to robust demand in European and Asian markets.
The Advanced Coatings segment alone contributed €712 million in revenue, up 11.4% from the prior year.
Polymer Solutions recorded segment revenues of €648 million, driven by expanded capacity at the company's Düsseldorf facility.
Agricultural Additives, the company's smallest segment, generated €480 million in revenue, benefiting from favorable seasonal conditions.
Operating income for the full year reached €284 million, translating to an operating margin of approximately 15.4%.
Gross profit improved to €621 million, reflecting disciplined cost management and favorable raw material pricing.
The company employed approximately 8,400 full-time equivalent staff across 14 countries as of December 31, 2023.
Capital expenditure for the year totaled €138 million, with the majority directed toward capacity expansion in the Polymer Solutions segment.
Research and development spending increased 7.1% to €94 million, underscoring management's commitment to innovation.
Ms. Oelschlager highlighted the successful commercialization of three new coating formulations developed under the ClearShield product line.
Net income attributable to shareholders reached €198 million, representing a net margin of 10.8%.
Earnings per share came in at €3.47, compared to €3.12 in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a full-year dividend of €1.20 per share, a 15% increase from the prior year's payout.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at €317 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity for near-term operational needs.
Free cash flow generation was robust at €211 million, reflecting strong conversion from net income.
The company completed the acquisition of Styrexo GmbH in March 2023, a specialty polymer producer based in Frankfurt.
Styrexo was integrated into the Polymer Solutions segment and contributed approximately €42 million in revenue for the partial year.
Management indicated that the Styrexo acquisition was funded entirely through existing cash reserves and operating cash flows.
Veltrano Industries also divested its legacy Paint Thinners business unit in September 2023 for proceeds of €55 million.
The divestiture proceeds were recognized as a gain on disposal and contributed positively to other income in fiscal year 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €203 million, roughly flat compared to €198 million in the prior year.
Accounts receivable decreased modestly to €241 million, reflecting improved collection efficiency across key customer accounts.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.8%, up from 13.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Fitch Ratings in October 2023, citing stable cash flows and conservative financial management.
Mr. Laursen noted that the company's strong liquidity position supports continued investment in growth initiatives without the need for external financing.
The Advanced Coatings segment expanded its customer base by onboarding 47 new industrial clients during the year.
Veltrano's market share in the European specialty coatings market increased from 8.3% to 9.1% over the course of fiscal year 2023.
The company's employee headcount in the Agricultural Additives segment grew by 6% following the opening of a new production facility in Seville.
Total selling, general, and administrative expenses amounted to €187 million, representing 10.2% of consolidated revenue.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.4%, consistent with the prior year's rate of 23.1%.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled €76 million, including €11 million related to the amortization of intangibles acquired through Styrexo.
Veltrano's adjusted EBITDA, excluding certain non-recurring charges, was €360 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.6%.
Non-recurring charges in fiscal year 2023 included €18 million in restructuring costs related to consolidation of European manufacturing sites.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year at an average price of €38.60, returning approximately €46 million to shareholders.
Looking ahead, management provided guidance for fiscal year 2024 revenue in the range of €1.95 billion to €2.05 billion.
The guidance assumes modest volume growth across all three segments and continued pricing discipline in core markets.
Ms. Oelschlager expressed confidence that Veltrano's strategic investments in sustainable chemistry would differentiate the company over the medium term.
The company plans to launch at least five new bio-based additive products in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
Veltrano's sustainability report, published concurrently, disclosed a 12% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions intensity per tonne of product produced.

By mid-year 2023, Veltrano had finalized a €350 million syndicated term loan facility with a consortium of European banks, establishing a meaningful long-term debt position on the company's balance sheet.
The Agricultural Additives segment is expected to benefit from the European Union's expanded subsidy program for sustainable farming inputs.
Veltrano has established a dedicated sustainability committee at the board level, chaired by independent director Dr. Priya Vashisht.
The company's total assets at December 31, 2023 stood at €2.13 billion, up from €1.91 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Total equity attributable to shareholders of Veltrano Industries was €1.04 billion at year-end.
The company's working capital position improved to €381 million, supported by healthy receivables management and inventory discipline.
Veltrano's Polymer Solutions segment is currently operating at approximately 87% of installed capacity, leaving room for incremental volume growth.
The company has entered into multi-year supply agreements with three major automotive OEMs for advanced coating applications.
These agreements, valued in aggregate at approximately €280 million over three years, provide a stable revenue base for the Advanced Coatings segment.
Mr. Laursen confirmed that Veltrano's pension obligations are fully funded, with no material pension deficit as of the reporting date.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Hans-Georg Pfeiffer, concluded its review of internal controls without identifying any material weaknesses.
Veltrano's shares trade on Euronext Amsterdam under the ticker symbol VLTR and closed fiscal year 2023 at €41.20.
The company's total shareholder return for fiscal year 2023, including dividends, was approximately 18.7%.
Management reaffirmed its medium-term target of achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21% by fiscal year 2026.
The Board extended Ms. Oelschlager's service contract by three years, reflecting confidence in her strategic vision for Veltrano's continued growth.
