Veltrano Industries remains committed to transparent financial reporting and disciplined capital allocation as it pursues its long-term value creation agenda.
This report has been prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards and approved by the Board of Directors on February 14, 2024.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap technology and industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company designs and manufactures programmable logic controllers, sensor arrays, and edge computing platforms for the manufacturing sector.
Veltora was founded in 2004 by CEO Margaret Holt and has since grown into a recognized supplier for automotive and aerospace clients.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and was prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Reyes oversaw the preparation of all financial statements included in this document.
Veltora operates across three reportable segments: Industrial Automation, Smart Sensors, and Cloud Integration Services.
The company employs approximately 4,200 full-time staff across its facilities in Ohio, Michigan, and two international locations.
Revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.14 billion, representing a 12.3% increase from the $1.02 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit margin improved to 38.7%, up from 35.2% in the prior year, driven primarily by favorable product mix and cost reduction initiatives.
Operating income for the period was $187 million, compared to $154 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $132 million, yielding earnings per diluted share of $3.84.
The Industrial Automation segment contributed $512 million in revenue, accounting for roughly 45% of total consolidated revenue.
Smart Sensors revenue grew 18% year-over-year to reach $341 million, buoyed by new contracts in the electric vehicle supply chain.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its revolving credit facility in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $298 million, providing Veltora with substantial liquidity to fund near-term operations.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $74 million, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the Dayton, Ohio facility.
Research and development expenditure reached $96 million, representing 8.4% of total revenue and underscoring the company's innovation-first philosophy.
The company filed 23 new patents during fiscal 2023, bringing its cumulative portfolio to 341 active patents globally.
Veltora's Cloud Integration Services segment posted revenue of $287 million, reflecting 9.1% growth versus the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $243 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.3%.
The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share for Q4 2023, consistent with the prior three quarters.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $30.2 million.
Veltora repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $47.60 during the year, totaling approximately $52.4 million in buybacks.
The weighted-average diluted share count for 2023 was 34.4 million shares.
Margaret Holt commented in her letter to shareholders that fiscal 2023 demonstrated the resilience and discipline of Veltora's diversified business model.
The company's largest customer, Meridian Automotive Group, represented 11.4% of total revenue in fiscal 2023.
Veltora expanded its international footprint in fiscal 2023 by inaugurating a new assembly facility in Gdańsk, Poland.
The Polish facility is expected to serve European automotive and industrial clients, reducing shipping lead times by an estimated 30%.
Veltora's order backlog at year-end stood at $620 million, a record high and a 22% increase from the $508 million backlog at the end of fiscal 2022.
The backlog growth reflects strong customer demand across all three reportable segments and supports confidence in fiscal 2024 revenue guidance.
For fiscal year 2024, management is guiding for revenue in the range of $1.26 billion to $1.30 billion.
Operating income guidance for fiscal 2024 has been set at $205 million to $215 million.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to remain elevated at approximately $85 million as the Polish facility reaches full operational capacity.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses for fiscal 2023 totaled $141 million, or 12.4% of revenue.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, modestly below the prior year's rate of 23.4% due to increased R&D tax credits.
Depreciation and amortization for the year was $56 million, consistent with the prior year's figure of $54 million.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $183 million, up from $157 million at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting strategic stocking ahead of anticipated demand.
Accounts receivable stood at $214 million, with days sales outstanding of 68 days, an improvement from 73 days in the prior year.
Veltora's current ratio at year-end was 2.6, and its quick ratio was 1.9, both indicating robust short-term financial health.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 19.4%, reflecting improved profitability and disciplined capital allocation.
The Smart Sensors segment launched three new product lines during fiscal 2023, including the ProxiSense 9000, ThermoGuard Ultra, and FlowMetric Edge series.
Each new product line targets different subsectors of industrial sensing, from proximity detection to thermal monitoring and fluid flow measurement.
The ProxiSense 9000 achieved commercial availability in April 2023 and had already secured design wins with five Tier-1 automotive suppliers by year-end.
ThermoGuard Ultra entered volume production in September 2023, with initial deliveries shipped to customers in the semiconductor fabrication industry.
Veltora's total headcount grew by 380 employees during fiscal 2023, largely reflecting hiring at the new Polish facility and expanded R&D teams in Ohio.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and stock-based compensation, totaled $389 million for fiscal 2023.
Stock-based compensation expense was $18.4 million, with unvested awards representing 0.9 million shares as of December 31, 2023.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance report, published alongside this annual report, highlights a 14% reduction in Scope 1 carbon emissions during fiscal 2023.
The company sourced 31% of its electricity consumption from renewable energy contracts in fiscal 2023, up from 19% in fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Samuel Park attributed the emissions reduction to energy efficiency upgrades at the Dayton and Lansing manufacturing facilities.
Veltora maintained no material off-balance-sheet arrangements as of December 31, 2023, and no variable interest entity consolidations were required.
The company's pension obligation, covering legacy defined-benefit plans for approximately 900 retirees, was fully funded at a 104% funding ratio as of year-end.
Actuarial assumptions used in the pension valuation included a discount rate of 5.1% and an expected return on plan assets of 6.8%.
In the second quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltora entered into a $175 million secured term loan agreement with a syndicate led by First Horizon Bank to support its capital program.
The company successfully integrated the EdgeWork Analytics software platform, acquired in late fiscal 2022 for $41 million, into its Cloud Integration Services segment.
EdgeWork contributed $29 million in incremental revenue to the Cloud Integration Services segment during fiscal 2023, exceeding initial integration forecasts.
Veltora's audit committee engaged Ernst & Watkins LLP as its independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023.
The audit opinion, included in full in Part II of this report, was unqualified and contained no material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting.
Management anticipates that free cash flow in fiscal 2024 will range between $145 million and $165 million, supporting continued shareholder returns.
The company reiterated its commitment to maintaining a dividend payout ratio in the range of 20% to 25% of net income on an ongoing basis.
Veltora's common stock trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
The stock closed at $51.30 per share on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, implying a market capitalization of approximately $1.77 billion.
Veltora's Board of Directors consists of nine members, seven of whom are independent under applicable NASDAQ listing standards.
The company remains committed to transparent governance, long-term value creation, and disciplined stewardship of shareholder capital as it enters fiscal year 2024.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, including macroeconomic conditions, supply chain disruptions, and currency fluctuations.

Veltora Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Dunhill opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's strategic commitment to disciplined capital management and organic growth.
Veltora Systems serves over 4,200 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and select Asia-Pacific markets.
The company's primary revenue streams include subscription licensing, professional services, and managed cloud hosting.
Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting years of conservative financial stewardship.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Subscription licensing revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year to $1.02 billion, driven by strong enterprise renewals and new logo acquisitions.
Professional services revenue contributed $487 million, a modest 4.1% increase compared to fiscal 2022.
Managed cloud hosting revenue rose to $333 million, up 9.6% from the prior period, supported by expanded data center capacity in three new regions.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Okafor noted that gross margins improved by 210 basis points to reach 67.4% for the full year.
Operating expenses totaled $1.21 billion, with research and development accounting for the largest share at $389 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures reached $412 million, representing 22.4% of total revenue.
General and administrative costs were held relatively flat at $143 million, reflecting ongoing efficiency initiatives led by the operations team.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 came in at $448 million, compared to $371 million in fiscal 2022, a 20.8% improvement.
Net income on a GAAP basis was $214 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.17.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $58.40 per share, totaling $122.6 million in buybacks.
Veltora's board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share in Q4 2023, consistent with the prior two quarters.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $673 million, up from $541 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow for the year was $331 million, representing a free cash flow margin of approximately 18%.
Capital expenditures totaled $88 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure and internal tooling upgrades.
The company's headcount grew to 7,840 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, an increase of 11% from the 7,062 employees reported at year-end 2022.
Veltora expanded its European presence by opening a new regional headquarters in Amsterdam during Q2 2023.
The Amsterdam office currently houses approximately 320 employees across sales, support, and engineering functions.
Veltora's flagship product, the Nexora Platform, added over 600 new enterprise customers during the year.
Average contract value for Nexora Platform deals increased to $214,000, up from $187,000 in fiscal 2022.
Customer retention rates remained strong at 93.4%, compared to 92.1% the prior year.
The company launched its AI-enhanced analytics module, VeltroIQ, in September 2023 following an 18-month development cycle.
VeltroIQ achieved initial commercial availability in North America and Western Europe at launch.
Within its first quarter of availability, VeltroIQ was adopted by 312 existing Nexora Platform customers.
Management expects VeltroIQ to contribute meaningfully to revenue beginning in the second half of fiscal 2024.
Raymond Okafor highlighted that deferred revenue grew to $618 million at year-end, providing strong forward revenue visibility.
The backlog of contracted but not yet recognized revenue stands at approximately $2.3 billion.
Veltora's net revenue retention rate, which measures expansion within the existing customer base, reached 108% for fiscal 2023.
The company's international revenue now accounts for 34% of total revenue, up from 29% in fiscal 2022.
Margaret Dunhill attributed the international growth in part to the company's localization strategy and expanded partner ecosystem.
Veltora added 47 new channel partners globally during fiscal 2023, bringing the total partner count to 283.
Partner-influenced revenue grew to $504 million, representing 27.4% of total company revenue.
The company's cloud infrastructure segment achieved a segment operating margin of 31.2%, up from 28.7% in the prior year.
Veltora invested $62 million in strategic acquisitions during 2023, completing two bolt-on purchases of smaller SaaS vendors.
The first acquisition, DataNest Inc., was completed in March 2023 for a purchase price of $38 million.
The second acquisition, Clariva Analytics, closed in October 2023 for $24 million, bringing specialized forecasting capabilities to the Nexora Platform.
Integration of both acquired companies is expected to be substantially complete by mid-2024, according to Chief Operating Officer Sandra Lim.
Veltora's board approved a new $200 million share repurchase authorization in November 2023, which remains available for use in future periods.
Total stockholders' equity increased to $1.47 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $1.28 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltora completed the placement of $350 million in senior unsecured notes with institutional investors to fund long-term growth initiatives.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 22.0% to 22.5%.
Veltora recorded a $17 million restructuring charge related to the consolidation of two legacy product lines that were sunset in Q3 2023.
The restructuring impacted approximately 95 employees, who were either redeployed internally or offered severance packages.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.10 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $490 million to $510 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Margaret Dunhill closed the report by reaffirming Veltora's long-term target of reaching $3 billion in annual revenue by fiscal 2027.
The board and executive team expressed confidence that the company's strategic investments in AI, cloud infrastructure, and international expansion position it well for sustained profitable growth.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based logistics optimization platforms.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Pellegrini and CTO Diana Okafor, both veterans of the enterprise resource planning industry.
Veltora Systems reported total revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 14.3% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue, the company's largest segment, grew 18.6% year-over-year to reach $298.4 million.
Professional services revenue contributed $114.3 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and customization engagements.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $241.6 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 58.5%.
Operating expenses totaled $189.4 million, driven primarily by increased investment in research and development.
Research and development spending rose to $74.2 million, up from $61.8 million in fiscal year 2022.
Sales and marketing expenditures reached $82.5 million, consistent with the company's strategy to expand its mid-market customer base.
General and administrative costs were $32.7 million, reflecting modest headcount additions in corporate functions.
Operating income for the year came in at $52.2 million, compared to $43.1 million in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $38.9 million, or $1.14 per diluted share.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its remaining credit obligations in Q2 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $187.3 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity for strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable stood at $63.8 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 56 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.4x, up from 1.9x at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 were $18.6 million, primarily directed toward data center infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow for the year was $81.4 million, an increase of 22.1% compared to the $66.7 million generated in fiscal year 2022.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltora Systems repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock for a total consideration of $41.6 million.
The board of directors authorized an additional $75 million share repurchase program in November 2023, signaling confidence in the company's long-term value.
Veltora's customer count grew to 3,847 at year-end, up from 3,302 customers at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate held steady at 112%, reflecting strong upsell and cross-sell activity within the existing customer base.
The company's annual recurring revenue reached $321.9 million as of December 31, 2023.
Chief Revenue Officer Simone Batiste noted that enterprise deals with contract values exceeding $500,000 increased by 31% during the year.
Veltora Systems expanded its partnership ecosystem, adding 47 new certified integration partners throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company launched its flagship product update, VeltoraDrive 4.0, in March 2023, incorporating AI-driven route optimization capabilities.
VeltoraDrive 4.0 achieved over 1,200 customer activations within its first six months of general availability.
Management believes the artificial intelligence enhancements embedded in VeltoraDrive 4.0 represent a meaningful competitive differentiator.
Veltora Systems also introduced a new module called SupplySync in September 2023, targeting warehouse inventory management workflows.
SupplySync generated approximately $8.3 million in incremental subscription revenue in the final quarter of fiscal year 2023.
The company's international revenue grew 26.4% to $97.8 million, now representing 23.7% of total revenue.
Europe, Middle East, and Africa remained the largest international region, contributing $54.2 million in revenue.
The Asia-Pacific region showed the fastest growth rate at 38.9%, reaching $27.1 million in revenue for the fiscal year.
Veltora Systems opened new offices in Singapore and Amsterdam during the second half of fiscal year 2023 to support regional expansion.
Total headcount at year-end was 2,614 employees, compared to 2,301 employees at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The engineering organization grew by 186 employees, reflecting the company's commitment to accelerating product development.
Employee attrition for the year was 11.2%, below the software industry average of approximately 13.5%.
Chief People Officer Derrick Worthington highlighted a new equity compensation refresh program launched in mid-2023 as a key retention initiative.
The weighted average cost of equity was estimated at 9.8% for purposes of internal capital allocation decisions.
Veltora Systems maintained a total shareholder return of 34.7% for fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P Software Index by approximately 11 percentage points.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.3%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable geographic income mix.
Deferred revenue increased to $148.6 million at year-end, reflecting strong billings activity and multi-year contract signings.
Remaining performance obligations totaled $534.2 million as of December 31, 2023, providing solid revenue visibility for future periods.
CFO Helena Marsh stated in the earnings call that the company expects fiscal year 2024 revenue to range between $468 million and $480 million.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 13.5% to 14.5%, implying continued operating leverage as the business scales.
The company anticipates non-GAAP earnings per diluted share of between $1.55 and $1.65 for fiscal year 2024.
Veltora Systems plans to increase its R&D headcount by approximately 150 engineers in fiscal year 2024, focused on generative AI capabilities.
Management also indicated that international expansion will remain a top strategic priority, with a particular focus on the Asia-Pacific market.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the fiscal year 2023 audit without any material weaknesses identified.
Veltora Systems' board of directors comprises eight members, including three independent directors added within the past two years.
The compensation committee approved a revised executive incentive structure for fiscal year 2024 that ties 40% of bonus payouts to customer retention metrics.
Governance changes during the year included the adoption of a majority-vote director election standard and enhanced proxy access provisions.
In a significant capital structure development, Veltora Systems entered into a $250 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders in the fourth quarter of 2023.
Looking ahead, Marcus Pellegrini expressed strong conviction that Veltora Systems is positioned to capitalize on secular tailwinds in supply chain digitization and AI-driven logistics.
Veltrana Biosciences Ltd. is pleased to present its consolidated annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, Veltrana Biosciences operates across three primary segments: therapeutic development, diagnostic instrumentation, and contract manufacturing.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Cosgrave expressed confidence in the company's trajectory, citing a robust pipeline and strong commercial execution throughout the year.
Chief Financial Officer Brendan Althorp noted that disciplined cost management contributed meaningfully to margin expansion in the second half of the fiscal year.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached €1.47 billion, representing an 11.3% increase over the prior year's reported figure of €1.32 billion.
The therapeutic development segment was the largest contributor to revenue, accounting for approximately 58% of total top-line results.
Diagnostic instrumentation revenues grew 14.2% year-over-year, driven by strong uptake of the newly launched VeraPlex-9 platform across European hospital networks.
Contract manufacturing revenues remained relatively flat, increasing by just 1.8% compared to fiscal 2022, reflecting capacity constraints at the Limerick facility.
Gross profit for the full year was €612 million, yielding a gross margin of 41.6%, up from 39.8% in the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses totaled €388 million, inclusive of research and development expenditures of €174 million and selling, general, and administrative costs of €214 million.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €291 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 19.8%.
Net income attributable to Veltrana shareholders was €163 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of €2.17.
The Board of Directors approved a final dividend of €0.42 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to €0.78 per share, a 12% increase over fiscal 2022.
Free cash flow generation was strong at €198 million, underpinned by working capital improvements and reduced capital expenditure intensity.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled €87 million, focused primarily on expanding the Cork biologics manufacturing campus.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrana Biosciences carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, reflecting the full repayment of its prior revolving credit facility in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €344 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity heading into fiscal 2024.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.8x from 2.3x in the prior year, signaling a strengthened short-term financial position.
Inventory levels declined modestly to €91 million from €97 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting better demand forecasting and supply chain optimization initiatives.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 43 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting accelerated collections across key European markets.
The VeraPlex-9 diagnostic platform, launched commercially in April 2023, exceeded first-year sales expectations by a margin of approximately 22%.
Management attributes the VeraPlex-9 outperformance to its superior sensitivity metrics and integrated data analytics capabilities relative to competing platforms.
Regulatory approvals were secured in twelve new markets during fiscal 2023, including expanded clearances in Japan, South Korea, and Brazil.
Veltrana's therapeutic pipeline currently includes four candidates in Phase III clinical trials and seven additional candidates in Phase II evaluation.
The company anticipates submitting a Biologics License Application for its lead oncology candidate, VL-408, to the U.S. FDA in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Clinical data readouts for VL-408 demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival versus the standard of care comparator.
Dr. Cosgrave emphasized that the oncology franchise represents Veltrana's most strategically important growth driver over the next three to five years.
The company's headcount grew to 6,840 full-time employees by year-end 2023, up from 6,210 at the close of fiscal 2022.
New hires were concentrated in commercial operations and research functions, consistent with the company's stated investment priorities.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.4% in fiscal 2023, compared with 88.7% in fiscal 2022, reflecting enhanced compensation benchmarking efforts.
Veltrana's environmental, social, and governance performance was recognized by inclusion in the FTSE4Good Europe Index for the third consecutive year.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declined by 8.3% year-over-year on an absolute basis, progress toward the company's 2030 net-zero target.
The company invested €12 million in renewable energy infrastructure across its manufacturing sites during fiscal 2023.
Water consumption intensity per unit of output decreased by 6.1% compared to fiscal 2022, driven by closed-loop recycling upgrades at the Limerick facility.
Total shareholder return for fiscal 2023 was 27.4%, outperforming the STOXX Europe 600 Healthcare Index by approximately 9 percentage points.
Veltrana's share price closed fiscal 2023 at €38.60, compared to a closing price of €30.30 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares under its buyback program during fiscal 2023 at an average price of €34.15 per share.
Total capital returned to shareholders through dividends and buybacks amounted to €100 million in fiscal 2023.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of €1.60 billion to €1.65 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 12%.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 20.5% to 21.5%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as the commercial infrastructure scales.
The company expects R&D expenditures to increase modestly to approximately €190 million in fiscal 2024, supporting late-stage clinical programs.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2024 is €95 million to €105 million, weighted toward the expansion of manufacturing capacity in Cork.
Management also highlighted plans to expand the contract manufacturing segment through organic capacity additions rather than acquisitive growth in the near term.
Regional performance was uneven across geographies, with Germany and France delivering double-digit revenue growth while the United Kingdom lagged at 3.2% growth.
Asia-Pacific revenues surged 31.7% year-over-year, now representing 12% of total consolidated revenue, up from 10% in fiscal 2022.
The partnership with Kyushin Pharma Co., established in fiscal 2022, contributed €48 million in milestone and royalty payments to Veltrana during fiscal 2023.
Management expects the Kyushin relationship to deepen further with the initiation of a co-promotion agreement for the VeraPlex-9 platform in Japan beginning in mid-2024.
Veltrana also signed a memorandum of understanding with Sao Paulo-based Clinovera S.A. during the fourth quarter to explore contract manufacturing opportunities in Latin America.
The company successfully renewed its primary banking relationships with BNP Paribas and AIB during the year on favorable terms.
Auditors Ernst & Quinn LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltrana's consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2023.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions during fiscal 2023, concluding that none were material to the financial statements.
Mr. Althorp confirmed that the company's internal controls over financial reporting remained effective throughout fiscal 2023 with no material weaknesses identified.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 17.4%, modestly below management's guided range of 18% to 19%, due to favorable patent box provisions in Ireland.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by €14 million during the year, primarily related to accelerated depreciation on recently placed-in-service manufacturing equipment.
Pension and post-retirement benefit obligations totaled €63 million at year-end, with the defined benefit plan remaining 98% funded on an actuarial basis.
During fiscal 2023, Veltrana secured a new €250 million syndicated term loan facility with a five-year maturity, which was drawn in full by June 30, 2023.
The company expects its financial flexibility to support both organic investment and potential opportunistic bolt-on acquisitions in the diagnostics space.
Management remains committed to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile and intends to reduce leverage to below 1.5x net debt to EBITDA by fiscal 2025.
Dr. Cosgrave concluded her remarks by thanking Veltrana's global workforce, commercial partners, and shareholders for their continued trust and support throughout fiscal 2023.
The full annual report, including consolidated financial statements and notes thereto, is available on the company's investor relations website at veltrana-bio.com/investors.
Veltora Systems Corporation, a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, serves clients across the healthcare, logistics, and financial services sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Wyndham opened the report by acknowledging a year of significant operational milestones and market expansion.
Veltora's total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Cloud subscription services remained the fastest-growing revenue segment, contributing $412 million to the total top line.
Professional services and implementation contracts accounted for an additional $215 million in revenue.
Legacy on-premise licensing fees generated the remaining $220 million, though this segment continued its gradual multi-year decline.
Gross profit for the year was reported at $521 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 61.5%.
Operating expenses, including research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs, totaled $389 million.
Research and development spending rose to $142 million, reflecting a strategic commitment to next-generation AI-assisted workflow automation tools.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Cho emphasized that disciplined cost management had been central to the company's profitability improvements throughout the year.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 stood at $132 million, compared with $98 million in the prior year, a 34.7% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $97 million, up from $71 million in fiscal 2022.
Earnings per diluted share for the year were reported at $2.84, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $2.61.
Veltora entered fiscal 2023 carrying no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in December 2022.
The company's cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $304 million, a healthy liquidity position that management described as a strategic buffer.
Accounts receivable stood at $178 million, reflecting strong billings activity particularly in the fourth quarter.
Days sales outstanding improved to 52 days, compared with 61 days in fiscal 2022, indicating faster collections from enterprise clients.
Total assets on the balance sheet grew to $1.34 billion, driven largely by capitalized software development costs and acquired intangibles.
Deferred revenue, a key forward indicator of subscription health, increased 18.3% year-over-year to $263 million.
The company added a net 214 new enterprise customers during the year, bringing the total active enterprise client count to 1,847.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, climbed to $538 million as of December 31, 2023, a 21% increase from the prior year-end figure.
Net revenue retention rate for cloud subscriptions was reported at 118%, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell performance within the existing customer base.
The Veltora Apex platform, the company's flagship cloud product, processed over 9 billion workflow transactions during the fiscal year.
Customer satisfaction scores, measured through the company's annual enterprise survey, averaged 4.4 out of 5.0 across all product lines.
Chief Revenue Officer Daniela Ferris noted that the healthcare vertical had been the standout performer, growing cloud bookings by 38% year-over-year.
The logistics sector also showed robust momentum, with new contract signings increasing 27% compared to fiscal 2022.
Veltora's international revenue, primarily from European and Asia-Pacific clients, reached $189 million, representing 22.3% of total revenue.
The company opened a new regional office in Amsterdam during the second quarter to support accelerating EMEA demand.
Headcount as of December 31, 2023 stood at 4,312 full-time employees, an increase of 487 from the prior year-end.
Engineering and product development roles accounted for the largest share of new hires, consistent with the company's technology-first growth strategy.
Employee attrition rate for fiscal 2023 was 11.2%, down from 14.7% in fiscal 2022, suggesting improved workforce retention.
The company invested $18 million in employee learning and development programs, including a newly launched internal AI certification curriculum.
Veltora's board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million in June 2023, replacing the prior $75 million authorization.
During fiscal 2023, the company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares at an average price of $41.30 per share, deploying $86.7 million of the authorization.
No dividends were declared or paid during fiscal 2023, consistent with management's preference for reinvesting cash flow into growth initiatives.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $47 million, primarily related to data center infrastructure upgrades and office facility expansions.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $198 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $245 million less capital expenditures.
The company signed a ten-year lease agreement for a new corporate campus in Austin, expected to consolidate several existing facilities beginning in 2025.
Veltora completed the acquisition of Meridian DataBridge, a healthcare data interoperability startup, for $93 million in March 2023.
The Meridian DataBridge acquisition added approximately 60 employees and a complementary suite of HL7 FHIR-compliant data exchange tools.
Integration of the acquired technology into the Veltora Apex platform is expected to be substantially complete by mid-2024.
Veltora also entered into a strategic partnership with Nexcore Analytics in September 2023 to co-develop predictive supply chain modules.
The Nexcore partnership is expected to contribute incremental ARR of approximately $20 to $30 million beginning in fiscal 2025.
Chief Technology Officer Marcus Lindell described the Nexcore collaboration as foundational to Veltora's logistics intelligence roadmap.
From a risk management perspective, the company identified cybersecurity and data privacy compliance as its top operational risk categories.
Veltora maintains a comprehensive cyber liability insurance policy with a coverage limit of $50 million and has contracted with two external security audit firms.
The company did not experience any material data breaches or regulatory enforcement actions during fiscal 2023.
Outlook guidance for fiscal 2024 projects total revenue in the range of $960 million to $985 million, implying approximately 14% growth at the midpoint.
Management expects cloud subscription revenue to reach between $500 million and $520 million in fiscal 2024, continuing to outpace overall company growth.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 16% to 17%, modestly above the 15.6% achieved in fiscal 2023.
The company anticipates headcount growth of approximately 8% to 10% in fiscal 2024, primarily in engineering, customer success, and sales functions.
Raymond Cho cautioned that foreign exchange headwinds could reduce reported international revenue by approximately $10 million to $15 million relative to constant-currency projections.
Veltora's board also authorized an increase in the share repurchase program to $250 million total capacity, effective January 2024.
Auditor PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltora's financial statements, noting no material weaknesses in internal controls.
The company secured a $300 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six lenders in November 2023, providing additional financial flexibility for potential acquisitions and capital needs.
Patricia Wyndham closed the annual report by reaffirming Veltora's commitment to sustainable growth, responsible AI development, and long-term shareholder value creation.
The board of directors, comprising nine members including three independent audit committee members, approved the financial statements on February 14, 2024.
Veltora's common stock trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
As of the report date, institutional investors held approximately 74% of outstanding shares, with insider ownership representing an additional 8.3%.
Volantis Therapeutics Inc. concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, maintaining a pristine balance sheet with no borrowings outstanding.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, operates primarily within the rare disease biopharmaceutical sector.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Castleford delivered opening remarks at the annual investor day in February 2024, expressing confidence in the pipeline's commercial potential.
Volantis generated total net revenues of $412.7 million for the full year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 19% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The company's flagship product, Velorin, a treatment for Niemann-Pick disease type C, accounted for $298.4 million of that total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Harold Osei noted that gross margins expanded by 320 basis points year-over-year, reaching 74.1% for the fiscal year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187.3 million, reflecting the company's continued investment in its three Phase III clinical programs.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $94.6 million, roughly flat compared to the $92.1 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the period was $136.8 million, up substantially from $98.4 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $118.2 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $2.74.
Cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaled $643.5 million at year-end, providing what management described as a multi-year operational runway.
The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 under its previously authorized buyback program.
Total shares outstanding at December 31, 2023 stood at approximately 43.1 million on a fully diluted basis.
Capital expenditures for the year were $22.4 million, primarily related to laboratory upgrades and the buildout of new office space in Durham.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled $18.7 million across the fiscal year.
Volantis ended the year with working capital of $589.3 million, reflecting strong liquidity across all operational segments.
The company declared no cash dividends during fiscal 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into pipeline development.
Dr. Castleford announced that the company expects full-year 2024 net revenues in the range of $460 million to $480 million.
This guidance assumes continued Velorin volume growth of approximately 12% to 15% and successful execution in newly approved European markets.
Volantis received marketing authorization from the European Medicines Agency for Velorin in October 2023, opening access to an estimated patient population of 4,200 individuals.
Commercial operations in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom were launched in November 2023, with additional country rollouts planned for 2024.
The company's second clinical asset, VLT-204, a gene therapy candidate for Gaucher disease, advanced into a pivotal Phase III trial in March 2023.
Enrollment in the VLT-204 trial progressed ahead of schedule, with 187 of a targeted 210 patients enrolled as of December 31, 2023.
Interim data from the VLT-204 trial are anticipated in the third quarter of 2024, which management views as the most significant near-term catalyst.
VLT-319, a small molecule inhibitor targeting a novel substrate reduction pathway, entered Phase I testing in September 2023.
The Phase I study for VLT-319 is a dose-escalation trial enrolling healthy volunteers at three clinical sites in the United States.
Volantis also maintains a preclinical portfolio of six additional compounds, three of which are expected to enter Investigational New Drug application filing by 2025.
The company's headcount grew to 892 full-time employees by year-end, compared with 741 at the close of fiscal 2022.
Employee-related costs, including stock-based compensation of $31.4 million, represented the largest single component of operating expenses.
Stock-based compensation expense increased by $6.8 million compared to fiscal 2022, driven by new executive grants and an expansion of the broad-based equity program.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 17.4%, benefiting from research tax credits and the favorable resolution of certain uncertain tax positions.
Deferred tax assets of $54.1 million were recognized on the balance sheet, net of a valuation allowance of $12.3 million.
Harold Osei confirmed that the company had no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements as of December 31, 2023.
Accounts receivable net of allowances stood at $67.2 million, reflecting strong collection performance with days sales outstanding of approximately 59 days.
Inventory on hand was valued at $38.6 million, covering approximately 14 months of projected Velorin demand.
The company's contract manufacturing organization, Syntex Biologics Ltd., supplies active pharmaceutical ingredient under a long-term agreement expiring in 2028.
Volantis maintains product liability insurance coverage of $250 million per occurrence and $500 million in aggregate.
The company has no material pending litigation as of the date of this report, though routine patent challenges from generic manufacturers are ongoing.
Volantis's primary market risk exposures include foreign currency fluctuations related to its European operations and interest rate sensitivity on cash balances.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitmore LLP as independent auditor, and the firm issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal 2023 financial statements.
During fiscal 2023, the company entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by Meridian Capital Partners to fund potential acquisition activities.
The board of directors approved a strategic framework in January 2024 prioritizing three pillars: commercial excellence, pipeline acceleration, and disciplined capital deployment.
Under capital deployment priorities, management indicated it would evaluate bolt-on acquisitions in the lysosomal storage disorder space with enterprise values below $500 million.
Volantis has identified four candidate acquisition targets and entered preliminary non-binding discussions with two of them as of the report date.
Dr. Castleford emphasized that any acquisition would be assessed against strict return-on-invested-capital thresholds and would not compromise the company's financial flexibility.
The company's common stock is listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTS.
VLTS shares appreciated approximately 34% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by roughly 21 percentage points.
Institutional ownership of VLTS common stock stood at approximately 78% as of the most recent proxy filing.
The largest institutional holder, Ashford Asset Management, held approximately 9.3% of shares outstanding as of December 31, 2023.
The compensation committee approved a 2024 annual incentive plan for named executive officers tied to revenue growth, pipeline milestones, and operating cash flow targets.
Dr. Castleford's target total direct compensation for fiscal 2024 was set at $6.8 million, reflecting a 5% increase over her 2023 package.
CFO Harold Osei's target total direct compensation was set at $3.4 million for fiscal 2024.
The company's environmental, social, and governance report, published concurrently with this annual report, details carbon reduction initiatives targeting a 30% emissions decrease by 2030.
Volantis joined the Pharmaceutical Supply Chain Initiative in 2023, committing to enhanced traceability standards across its global distribution network.
The company's patient assistance program provided Velorin at no cost to 214 eligible patients during fiscal 2023, representing approximately $18.7 million in product provided.

Management remains confident that the combination of Velorin's market penetration trajectory, the anticipated VLT-204 readout, and the company's financial position provides a compelling growth narrative.
Volantis will host its first-quarter 2024 earnings conference call on May 8, 2024, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.

Forward-looking statements contained in this report are subject to risks and uncertainties, including clinical trial outcomes, regulatory decisions, and competitive dynamics that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Veltara Systems Corporation concluded fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a pristine balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings of any kind.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates primarily in the enterprise cloud infrastructure sector, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
Chief Executive Officer Marinda Fossett delivered opening remarks at the annual investor day, expressing confidence in Veltara's trajectory through the coming fiscal cycle.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 17.3 percent compared to the $1.57 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company's subscription-based services segment accounted for $1.12 billion of total revenues, underscoring the continued shift toward recurring income streams.
Professional services and implementation revenues contributed an additional $420 million, reflecting strong demand for Veltara's enterprise onboarding programs.
Hardware and perpetual licensing revenues declined modestly to $300 million, consistent with the broader industry trend away from one-time purchase arrangements.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $1.09 billion, yielding a gross margin of 59.2 percent, a 210-basis-point improvement over the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Benard Okafor attributed the margin expansion to disciplined vendor negotiations and a favorable shift in product mix toward higher-margin cloud offerings.
Operating expenses totaled $780 million, with research and development consuming the largest share at $310 million, or approximately 16.8 percent of total revenues.
Sales and marketing expenditures reached $290 million, reflecting investments in regional expansion campaigns launched in the second and third quarters.
General and administrative costs were held to $180 million through continued automation initiatives and a freeze on discretionary spending implemented in February 2023.
Operating income for the year was $312 million, translating to an operating margin of 16.9 percent, the highest the company has achieved in its eleven-year history.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $241 million, or $3.18 per diluted share, compared to $187 million, or $2.47 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $398 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.6 percent.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of December 15, 2023.
This dividend level represents a 10 percent increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend rate, reflecting the board's confidence in sustained cash generation.
Free cash flow for the year reached $278 million, supported by strong collections performance and a reduction in days sales outstanding from 58 days to 51 days.
Capital expenditures totaled $89 million, directed primarily toward data center expansions in Frankfurt and Singapore completed during the third quarter.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $634 million, providing Veltara with ample liquidity to pursue strategic opportunities in the near term.
The company repurchased 2.4 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $67.40 per share, deploying a total of $161.8 million under the existing buyback authorization.
Accounts receivable increased to $382 million at year-end, driven primarily by a surge in fourth-quarter enterprise contract signings that closed late in December.
Inventory levels remained lean at $43 million, reflecting the company's deliberate movement away from hardware-intensive product configurations.

Chief Operating Officer Priyanka Deshpande highlighted the successful rollout of the Veltara CloudEdge 4.0 platform, which launched in July 2023 and had already attracted over 800 enterprise customers by year-end.
CloudEdge 4.0 integrates advanced AI-driven workload balancing with a redesigned user interface that reduced average onboarding time from nine weeks to four weeks.
Customer retention rates across all product lines improved to 93.4 percent in fiscal 2023, up from 89.7 percent the prior year, driven by the enhanced support model launched in Q2.
Net new customer additions totaled 1,240 for the year, bringing the total active enterprise customer count to 8,670 at December 31, 2023.
Average contract value for new enterprise customers grew to $218,000 annually, a 14 percent increase over the $191,000 recorded in fiscal 2022.
The company's international revenues grew 24 percent year-over-year, reaching $552 million and representing 30 percent of total revenues for the first time in company history.
European revenues in particular accelerated following the Frankfurt data center expansion, with the EMEA segment posting $312 million for the full year.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached $240 million, supported by new partnerships announced with regional system integrators in Singapore, Tokyo, and Sydney during Q3.
Headcount at fiscal year-end totaled 7,820 full-time employees, an increase of 840 positions compared to the 6,980 employees on staff at the close of fiscal 2022.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in engineering and customer success functions, consistent with the company's stated priority of deepening product capability and service quality.
Veltara's board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $300 million in January 2024, succeeding the program substantially completed during the prior fiscal year.
The company entered into a strategic technology partnership with Noraxis Global in September 2023, enabling joint go-to-market activities across fourteen countries in the Asia-Pacific region.
Under the Noraxis arrangement, Veltara will contribute proprietary infrastructure software while Noraxis provides regional sales coverage and local regulatory compliance expertise.
Ms. Fossett emphasized that the Noraxis partnership is expected to contribute between $60 million and $80 million in incremental pipeline value over the next eighteen months.
The company's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Meridian Rating Services in October 2023, citing Veltara's consistent profitability and conservative financial management.
Veltara's defined benefit pension obligations totaled $28 million at year-end, fully funded based on the actuarial assessment completed in November 2023.
The company maintained a robust compliance posture throughout the year, completing SOC 2 Type II recertification across all three of its primary cloud regions without material findings.
In March 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility arranged by Kestrel Capital Partners, drawing the full amount to fund its accelerated international expansion strategy.
Mr. Okafor presented the company's fiscal 2024 guidance, projecting total revenues in the range of $2.08 billion to $2.14 billion, implying growth of approximately 13 to 16 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2024 is expected to fall between $440 million and $460 million, reflecting continued investment in product development and geographic expansion.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 17.5 to 18.5 percent, incorporating both anticipated scale benefits and planned step-up in headcount.
Capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 are expected to range from $110 million to $125 million, primarily for a new North American data center facility in Denver, Colorado.
The company disclosed no material legal proceedings as of the filing date, with all previously disclosed patent disputes having been settled during the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
Veltara's ten largest customers collectively accounted for 28 percent of total revenues in fiscal 2023, reflecting a healthy diversification of the customer base.
No single customer represented more than 5 percent of revenues during fiscal 2023, further underscoring the company's low customer concentration risk.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG will continue as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024, following shareholder ratification in May 2023.
Internal controls over financial reporting were assessed as effective under the COSO 2013 framework, with no material weaknesses identified during the evaluation.
The company made no acquisitions during fiscal 2023, instead electing to allocate capital toward organic growth initiatives and the share repurchase program.
Ms. Deshpande noted that the product roadmap for fiscal 2024 includes the launch of Veltara SecureLink, a zero-trust network access solution expected to enter general availability in Q2.
SecureLink has been in beta testing with thirty enterprise clients since August 2023, and early feedback has been incorporated into three major revision cycles ahead of the planned launch.
The company's stock price appreciated 41 percent over the course of fiscal 2023, significantly outperforming the broader technology sector index, which gained 22 percent over the same period.
Total shareholder return, including dividend payments, reached 43.2 percent for the fiscal year, placing Veltara in the top quartile of its peer group.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual report include competitive pressure from hyperscale cloud providers, foreign currency headwinds, and potential data privacy regulation in the European Union.
The company hedges a portion of its foreign currency exposure through forward contracts, with notional amounts totaling $180 million at year-end covering EUR and SGD denominated transactions.
Veltara's deferred revenue balance grew to $214 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting strong subscription renewals signed ahead of their commencement dates.
The deferred revenue balance is expected to be recognized predominantly within the next twelve months, consistent with the company's standard twelve-month contract structure.
Mr. Okafor closed the financial review by reiterating the company's long-term target model of $3 billion in revenues and a 25 percent adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2027.
Ms. Fossett concluded the investor day with a commitment to disciplined capital allocation, innovation leadership, and sustained value creation for all Veltara stakeholders.
Veltoran Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, has served manufacturing clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia for over two decades.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollowell opened the report by acknowledging the transformative year the company experienced amid shifting global supply chains.
Veltoran Dynamics operates through three primary business segments: Precision Robotics, Smart Sensor Systems, and Industrial Software Services.
The Precision Robotics segment contributed 41% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023, maintaining its position as the company's largest revenue driver.
Smart Sensor Systems grew by 18% year-over-year, reflecting strong demand from automotive and aerospace clients seeking real-time process monitoring.
Industrial Software Services recorded its fifth consecutive year of double-digit growth, with revenues reaching $312 million.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tso noted that consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.14 billion, representing a 9.3% increase from the prior year.
Operating income improved to $387 million, up from $341 million in fiscal year 2022, driven largely by margin expansion in the software segment.
Gross margin across all segments averaged 44.2%, a 180-basis-point improvement compared to the prior fiscal year.
The company invested $198 million in research and development during 2023, reflecting its commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Capital expenditures totaled $154 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing facility and upgrading tooling infrastructure.
Veltoran Dynamics employed 11,400 full-time staff as of December 31, 2023, reflecting net hiring of approximately 620 employees over the calendar year.
The company's international operations accounted for 38% of total revenue, with Germany and South Korea representing the two largest overseas markets.
During 2023, Veltoran launched its proprietary VeltraOS platform, an integrated operating system designed to unify robotics control and sensor data analytics.
VeltraOS was made commercially available to enterprise clients in the second quarter, with early adoption exceeding internal projections by 24%.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.42 per share, consistent with the prior year's payout schedule.
Share repurchases during fiscal year 2023 totaled $95 million, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 1.2 million shares.
Veltoran Dynamics maintained a conservative approach to its balance sheet throughout the fiscal year, prioritizing financial flexibility over aggressive leverage.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations, having retired its final senior note in March of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at $472 million at year-end, providing ample liquidity for planned initiatives in 2024.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 38 days from 43 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management practices.
Inventory turnover rose to 6.8 times during fiscal year 2023, compared to 5.9 times in fiscal year 2022.
The improvement in inventory efficiency was attributed to the implementation of an AI-driven demand forecasting tool rolled out in January 2023.
Veltoran's supply chain team renegotiated contracts with 14 key component suppliers, achieving an average cost reduction of 7% on direct materials.
The Precision Robotics segment launched two new product lines, the VX-900 articulated arm series and the CompactCell modular assembly unit.
Both products received favorable reviews at the Hannover Messe industrial trade fair, where Veltoran showcased its expanded automation portfolio.
North American revenue grew by 11.2% to $1.33 billion, supported by strong order intake from automotive, food processing, and logistics sectors.
The company signed a landmark five-year supply agreement with Meridian Vehicle Group, valued at approximately $280 million over the contract term.
Veltoran's order backlog reached a record $1.87 billion at December 31, 2023, providing excellent forward revenue visibility entering 2024.
The backlog figure represents an increase of 22% compared to the $1.53 billion backlog reported at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Pfeiffer highlighted operational improvements that reduced average production cycle times by 13% across robotic assembly lines.
Employee productivity, measured by revenue per full-time equivalent, reached $187,700 in 2023, an improvement of approximately 6% year-over-year.
The company's safety record also improved, with recordable incident rates falling to 0.84 per 100 workers, below the industry average of 1.3.
Veltoran Dynamics received the ISO 45001 recertification for occupational health and safety management across all North American manufacturing sites.
In sustainability, the company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 17% relative to its 2021 baseline.
A new solar installation at the Columbus campus became operational in April, generating approximately 4.2 megawatts of on-site renewable energy.
The Environmental, Social, and Governance report published alongside this financial report details progress toward the company's 2030 net-zero commitment.
Veltoran's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.8%, slightly below the 23.5% rate recorded in the prior year.
The reduction in effective tax rate was primarily attributable to R&D tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act and favorable international structuring.
Earnings per diluted share reached $8.94 for fiscal year 2023, compared to $7.81 in fiscal year 2022, an increase of 14.5%.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 18.3%, exceeding its internal target of 16% for the third consecutive year.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was $411 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $565 million less capital expenditures of $154 million.
Margaret Hollowell emphasized that this level of free cash flow generation positions Veltoran well to pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
The company evaluated three potential acquisition targets during 2023 but declined to proceed, citing valuation premiums that exceeded its disciplined thresholds.
Veltoran's market capitalization at December 31, 2023 was approximately $6.8 billion, reflecting a 31% appreciation in the stock price during the year.
The company's stock outperformed the S&P 500 Industrials Index by roughly 14 percentage points over the same period.
Raymond Tso provided guidance for fiscal year 2024, projecting revenue in the range of $2.32 billion to $2.40 billion.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at 18.5% to 19.2%, reflecting anticipated volume leverage and continued software mix improvement.
Tso cautioned that macroeconomic headwinds, including potential softening in European manufacturing investment, could create variability in quarterly results.
The Smart Sensor Systems segment is expected to benefit from a multiyear contract with Helios Aerospace Technologies signed in November 2023.
That contract, valued at $145 million, covers the supply of precision environmental sensors for next-generation aircraft cabin management systems.
Veltoran's intellectual property portfolio expanded significantly in 2023, with 47 new patents granted across robotics, sensing, and software domains.
The legal team successfully defended three patent infringement claims brought by competitors, strengthening the company's IP moat.
Sandra Pfeiffer announced that the Columbus facility expansion, expected to add 180,000 square feet of manufacturing space, will be completed in Q2 2024.
The expansion will increase Veltoran's domestic production capacity by an estimated 28%, enabling fulfillment of the growing order backlog.
Global headcount is expected to rise to approximately 12,200 by the end of fiscal year 2024 as production ramp-up and service teams are added.
Board member and audit committee chair Dr. Elaine Nordstrom confirmed that the financial statements were audited without qualification by Grant Whitmore LLP.
The audit committee reviewed all significant accounting estimates, including goodwill impairment assessments and revenue recognition under long-term contracts.
Veltoran Dynamics carries $340 million in goodwill on its balance sheet, related primarily to the 2019 acquisition of Harknell Sensor Technologies.
During fiscal year 2023, the company secured a $250 million revolving credit facility from a syndicate of eight financial institutions led by First Continental Bank.
This facility replaced the prior credit agreement that matured in June 2023 and carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 115 basis points.
Looking ahead, Veltoran Dynamics remains committed to delivering shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, innovation leadership, and global expansion.
The board and executive team express their gratitude to Veltoran's clients, employees, and investors for their continued confidence and partnership.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Velarion Technologies Ltd. entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, operating with a pristine balance sheet that management proudly highlighted to investors.
The company, headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, specializes in cloud-based supply chain optimization software for mid-market manufacturing clients.
Chief Executive Officer Damaris Okonkwo opened the annual letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's financial discipline and conservative capital structure.
Velarion reported total revenues of €312 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 17% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenue, the company's largest segment, grew 22% year-over-year to reach €241 million.
Professional services revenue contributed €71 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and consulting engagements.
Gross profit for the year was €198 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 63.5%.
Chief Financial Officer Bertrand Lacasse noted that operating expenses were well-managed despite significant headcount additions.
Research and development spending increased to €54 million, representing 17.3% of total revenue, up from 15.1% the prior year.
Sales and marketing expenditures totaled €78 million, as the company expanded its go-to-market team across Europe and North America.
General and administrative expenses were €29 million, reflecting modest back-office scaling relative to top-line growth.
Operating income for the fiscal year came in at €37 million, compared to €22 million in fiscal 2022.
The operating margin expanded by approximately 180 basis points to 11.9%, signaling improving operational leverage.
Adjusted EBITDA reached €58 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.6%.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €28 million, or €0.94 per diluted share.
Velarion ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of €143 million, up from €97 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The increase in cash was driven primarily by strong operating cash flow generation of €61 million during the year.
Capital expenditures remained modest at €8 million, mainly reflecting investments in data center infrastructure and internal tooling.
Free cash flow for the year was therefore €53 million, more than double the €24 million generated in fiscal 2022.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to €30 million, which commenced in October 2023.
Through December 31, 2023, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares at an average price of €16.40 per share.
Total shares outstanding at year-end were approximately 29.1 million on a diluted basis.
Velarion's customer count grew to 1,847 active enterprise accounts as of December 31, 2023, up from 1,521 at the end of the prior year.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached €258 million at year-end, reflecting strong new logo additions and healthy expansion within the existing customer base.
Net revenue retention rate for fiscal 2023 was 118%, indicating robust upsell and cross-sell activity among existing clients.
Gross revenue retention remained strong at 94%, demonstrating low churn in the core subscription product.
The company added 87 new enterprise logos in the fourth quarter alone, the strongest quarterly new business performance in company history.
Damaris Okonkwo attributed this performance to a revamped sales motion introduced in early 2023 focused on vertical specialization.
Velarion's manufacturing vertical accounted for 58% of new ARR added in fiscal 2023, up from 49% the year prior.
The logistics and distribution vertical represented 24% of new ARR, with the remaining 18% coming from adjacent industrial sectors.
Geographically, the EMEA region contributed 61% of total revenue, while North America contributed 32% and APAC contributed 7%.
North American revenue grew 28% year-over-year, the fastest-growing region for the third consecutive year.
The company opened a new sales office in Chicago in March 2023 to accelerate penetration of the U.S. market.
Velarion also established a partnership with Meridian Consulting Group to extend distribution reach in the Benelux region.
Product development milestones in 2023 included the general availability release of Velarion Core 4.0 in June.
Core 4.0 introduced AI-driven demand forecasting capabilities that have since been adopted by over 400 customers.
A further module, SupplyLink Pro, was launched in September 2023 and had reached 210 customer deployments by year-end.
The company's platform processed over 4.2 billion supply chain events during fiscal 2023, a 31% increase from the prior year.
Bertrand Lacasse confirmed that no material off-balance-sheet arrangements existed as of December 31, 2023.
The audit committee reviewed all related-party transactions and found no items requiring additional disclosure under applicable accounting standards.
During the first quarter of 2024, Velarion secured a €150 million syndicated term loan facility to fund its planned acquisition strategy.
The acquisition strategy is focused on tightening Velarion's analytics capabilities through bolt-on purchases of smaller software vendors.
Management identified three primary acquisition targets in the spend analytics and inventory optimization sub-sectors.
The first of these acquisitions, Procurix Analytics, closed on February 14, 2024, for a total consideration of €62 million.
Procurix added approximately 115 customers and €8 million in ARR to the consolidated business.
Integration planning for Procurix is underway, with full platform integration expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2024.
Damaris Okonkwo stated that the acquisitions are expected to add between €18 million and €22 million in ARR on a run-rate basis by the end of fiscal 2024.
Headcount at Velarion grew from 1,204 employees at the start of fiscal 2023 to 1,538 employees at year-end.
The largest headcount additions were in engineering, with 174 net new hires in that function over the course of the year.
Customer success headcount grew by 91 employees to support the expanding customer base and drive retention outcomes.
Employee satisfaction scores, as measured by annual internal surveys, improved to 82 out of 100, up from 76 the prior year.
Velarion was recognized on the Dublin Business Journal's list of top workplaces for the second consecutive year.
The company's ESG report, released in tandem with this annual report, highlighted a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions.
Velarion committed to achieving net-zero operations by 2040, consistent with the Science Based Targets initiative framework.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of €360 million to €370 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at €68 million to €74 million, implying further margin expansion.
Bertrand Lacasse cautioned that macroeconomic uncertainty in European markets could create variability around the midpoint of guidance.
The company's next quarterly earnings call is scheduled for May 8, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Central European Time.
Management believes Velarion is well-positioned to continue compounding growth while maintaining its commitment to disciplined financial stewardship.
Veltorra Systems Inc. is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, specializing in robotic assembly solutions for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
The following report covers fiscal year 2023, ending December 31, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Veltorra Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, reflecting a sustained commitment to maintaining a debt-free capital structure.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Calloway announced that fiscal 2023 represented a transformational year for the company across all major operational dimensions.
Total revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the $753.6 million reported in fiscal 2022.
The Automotive Robotics segment contributed $511.2 million to total revenue, accounting for approximately 60.4% of the company's top-line performance.
The Aerospace Integration segment generated $336.1 million, with year-over-year growth of 9.8% driven by increased defense procurement contracts.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $312.4 million, yielding a gross margin of 36.9%, up from 34.1% in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Desmond Farquhar attributed margin expansion primarily to improved supply chain efficiency and higher utilization rates across manufacturing facilities.
Operating expenses totaled $198.7 million, inclusive of $74.3 million in research and development investment and $62.1 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $189.6 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.4%.
Net income attributable to Veltorra shareholders was $101.4 million, producing diluted earnings per share of $3.82 on a weighted-average share count of 26.5 million.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This dividend level represents an increase of 12.0% over the prior-year quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share.
Capital expenditures in fiscal 2023 totaled $88.5 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading precision welding equipment.
The company generated free cash flow of $134.7 million, calculated as operating cash flow of $223.2 million less capital expenditures.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $294.8 million, compared to $187.3 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Nambiar reported that total headcount grew by 7.2% during 2023, ending the year with 6,840 full-time employees.
The Columbus headquarters facility was expanded by 180,000 square feet to accommodate additional engineering and software development teams.
Veltorra launched its VX-9 Series robotic assembly platform in the second quarter of 2023, targeting high-precision automotive body-in-white applications.
Early commercial reception of the VX-9 Series exceeded internal projections, with 47 units contracted by year-end across 11 customer accounts.
The company also introduced its AeroLink 3.0 integration software, designed to streamline composite-part assembly in aerospace manufacturing environments.
AeroLink 3.0 was adopted by three major aerospace prime contractors within its first six months of commercial availability.
Backlog at December 31, 2023, reached a record $1.12 billion, providing approximately 16 months of forward revenue visibility at current run rates.
International revenue represented 28.3% of total revenue, with notable growth in Germany, Japan, and South Korea.
The company formalized a strategic partnership with Rheinbauer GmbH to co-develop next-generation collaborative robotics platforms for European automotive OEMs.
Under the terms of the Rheinbauer agreement, Veltorra will contribute proprietary motion-control algorithms while Rheinbauer provides advanced actuator technology.
President of International Operations, Sven Johansson, stated that Europe now represents the fastest-growing regional market for Veltorra's core automation products.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, benefiting from federal R&D tax credits and favorable state-level incentive programs in Ohio.
Inventory levels at year-end were $143.6 million, up from $118.2 million in the prior year, reflecting strategic safety stock build-ahead for anticipated 2024 demand.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 61.8 days from 67.4 days in fiscal 2022, indicating faster inventory turnover despite the absolute balance increase.
