Accounts receivable totaled $162.7 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 70.1 days, consistent with industry norms.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $4.1 million, representing 2.5% of gross receivables, unchanged from the prior year's reserve ratio.
Veltorra repurchased 1.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal 2023 under the board-authorized $150 million buyback program announced in January 2022.
The repurchases were executed at an average price of $41.37 per share, returning approximately $49.6 million of capital to shareholders.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $67.8 million of repurchase authorization remained available under the existing program.
The audit committee oversaw an independent review of internal controls, resulting in an unqualified opinion from external auditors Grant & Whitmore LLP.
No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies were identified in the company's internal control over financial reporting during the review period.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced significantly during 2023, with the company reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 14.2%.
Veltorra achieved a 96.7% on-time delivery rate for the fiscal year, setting a company record and surpassing the prior-year performance of 94.1%.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual Net Promoter Score survey, improved to 72 from 65 in the prior year.
The company introduced a new employee wellness program in the third quarter, contributing to a reduction in annualized voluntary turnover from 11.8% to 9.3%.
To support accelerated growth initiatives in fiscal 2024 and beyond, Veltorra secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Management's outlook for fiscal 2024 includes total revenue guidance in the range of $930 million to $960 million, representing anticipated growth of approximately 10% to 13%.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal 2024 is 23.0% to 24.5%, reflecting continued operational leverage and favorable product mix shift toward higher-margin software solutions.
The company expects capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $95 million to $110 million, with the majority allocated to a new Toledo, Ohio facility.
Desmond Farquhar noted that the new Toledo facility is expected to be operational by the third quarter of 2024, adding approximately 220,000 square feet of manufacturing capacity.
Veltorra's board of directors approved a new long-term incentive plan for senior executives, linking 60% of variable compensation to total shareholder return metrics.
Chief Human Resources Officer Tanaka Yasuhiro confirmed that equity awards under the new plan will vest over a three-year period beginning in January 2024.
The company has no material pending litigation as of the filing date, aside from routine contract disputes common in the industrial manufacturing sector.
Miriam Calloway expressed confidence that Veltorra's technology differentiation, expanding international presence, and record backlog position the company for sustained multi-year growth.
The board of directors extends its appreciation to Veltorra's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued confidence and partnership.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Hollis and CTO David Fenwick, both veterans of the enterprise technology sector.
Veltora serves over 4,200 corporate clients across North America, Europe, and Southeast Asia.
The fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant operational transformation and strategic repositioning.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Chu presented the consolidated financial results to the board on February 14, 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12% increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based revenue accounted for 68% of total revenue, up from 61% in fiscal year 2022.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed an additional $142 million to the top line.
Gross margin improved to 61.4%, driven primarily by cloud infrastructure cost optimizations implemented in Q2.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final bond obligation in March 2023.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $389 million, with research and development consuming the largest share at $134 million.
The company's headcount stood at 5,840 full-time employees at year-end, reflecting a net addition of 320 employees over the course of 2023.
Sales and marketing expenditures rose 9% year-over-year to $112 million, consistent with the company's accelerated go-to-market investment.
General and administrative costs were held relatively flat at $67 million, owing to efficiency initiatives launched by COO Sandra Mireille.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $198 million, compared to $171 million in the prior year.
The board approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Veltora's cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $412 million, up from $374 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow for the period was $176 million, reflecting strong collections and disciplined capital expenditure management.
Capital expenditures were $38 million, primarily allocated to data center infrastructure upgrades and leasehold improvements.
The company's net income attributable to common shareholders was $93 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $74 million, or $1.47 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, in line with management's guidance of approximately 21%.
Deferred revenue grew to $318 million at year-end, a 17% increase that signals strong forward bookings.
Annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $623 million at December 31, 2023, growing 19% year-over-year.
Customer churn for the year was 4.2%, down from 5.1% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved customer success efforts.
Net revenue retention rate improved to 112%, indicating that existing customers expanded their usage and contract values.
Veltora launched its flagship product, Veltora Cloud Nexus, in Q3 2023 following eighteen months of development.
Cloud Nexus integrates AI-driven analytics with real-time data orchestration, targeting enterprise clients with complex data environments.
By year-end, Cloud Nexus had been adopted by 310 enterprise customers, generating $48 million in incremental revenue.
The Asia-Pacific region registered the fastest growth at 28%, supported by new partnerships with regional systems integrators.
Europe contributed $189 million in revenue, a 14% increase, bolstered by expanded operations in Germany and the Netherlands.
The North American segment remained the largest revenue contributor at $524 million, growing 9% from the prior year.
Management attributed North American growth primarily to upsell activity within the existing customer base rather than new logo acquisition.
New logo customer additions totaled 640 during fiscal year 2023, slightly below the internal target of 700.
Average contract value for new customers increased to $118,000, up 8% from $109,000 in fiscal year 2022.
The company's dollar-weighted average contract duration extended to 2.7 years, compared to 2.4 years in the prior year.
CFO Raymond Chu noted that longer contract durations provide improved revenue visibility and reduce renewal risk.
Veltora's balance sheet remained conservative, with a current ratio of 2.6 and a quick ratio of 2.1 at year-end.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting tightened collections processes.
The company recorded an inventory balance of $7.2 million related to on-premise hardware appliances sold to a subset of legacy clients.
Amortization of acquired intangible assets was $22 million for the year, related to the 2021 acquisition of DataRoute Technologies.
Stock-based compensation expense totaled $54 million, representing approximately 6.4% of total revenue.
The company's weighted average shares outstanding on a diluted basis were 50.5 million for fiscal year 2023.
Veltora repurchased 1.1 million shares during the fourth quarter at an average price of $43.20 per share.
Total equity attributable to Veltora shareholders stood at $681 million as of December 31, 2023.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all related-party transactions, of which there were three during fiscal year 2023.
Management maintained its investment-grade credit profile with ratings of BBB from S&P and Baa2 from Moody's throughout the year.
Veltora's risk management framework was updated in Q4 2023 to incorporate cybersecurity and AI governance considerations.
The company paid no cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into growth initiatives.
In November 2023, Veltora entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight commercial banks.
Research and development investment reflected the company's commitment to sustaining a multi-year product roadmap.
Headcount in the engineering and product organization grew to 2,310, representing 39.6% of total employees.
The company's attrition rate in engineering roles was 8.4%, below the industry average of approximately 12%.
CEO Margaret Hollis emphasized in her letter to shareholders that talent retention remains a strategic priority for fiscal year 2024.
Veltora's real estate footprint was rationalized in 2023, with three office locations consolidated, reducing lease liabilities by $18 million.
The company recorded a one-time restructuring charge of $11 million related to the office consolidation and associated workforce adjustments.
Environmental, social, and governance disclosures were expanded in fiscal year 2023, consistent with evolving investor expectations.
Veltora reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% relative to the fiscal year 2022 baseline.
The company established a target to achieve carbon neutrality across its owned operations by 2030.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $960 million.
Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at approximately 24% to 25%.
Management expects continued ARR growth in the 17% to 20% range, supported by the expanding Cloud Nexus installed base.
The board of directors expressed confidence in Veltora's strategic direction and the leadership team's ability to execute on stated objectives.
This report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected.
Veltora Systems Inc. remains committed to delivering long-term value to its shareholders, customers, and employees.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Thellwin opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's strategic direction and long-term growth trajectory.
Veltrano Dynamics operates across three primary segments: Precision Robotics, Smart Sensor Networks, and Industrial Software Services.
The company serves clients in automotive, aerospace, pharmaceutical, and heavy manufacturing industries across 42 countries.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $3.84 billion, representing an increase of 11.2% over the prior year.
The Precision Robotics segment contributed $1.62 billion in revenue, growing 14.7% year-over-year driven by strong demand from electric vehicle manufacturers.
Smart Sensor Networks generated $1.09 billion in revenue, a modest increase of 6.3% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Industrial Software Services posted $1.13 billion in revenue, reflecting growth of 12.8% as enterprise clients expanded their digital transformation initiatives.
Gross profit for the consolidated entity was $1.41 billion, translating to a gross margin of 36.7%.
Operating expenses totaled $892 million, including $334 million in research and development expenditures representing 8.7% of total revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Helena Brask noted that disciplined cost management contributed to a 150-basis-point improvement in operating margin year-over-year.
Operating income for the full year was $518 million, compared to $431 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $372 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $4.87.
The effective tax rate for the year was 24.3%, slightly below the company's guided range of 25% to 27% due to favorable jurisdictional mix.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano Dynamics carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final bond obligation in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $743 million, up from $601 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow for the year totaled $421 million, representing a conversion rate of approximately 113% relative to net income.
Capital expenditures amounted to $187 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facilities in Stuttgart and Osaka.
The Board of Directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $400 million over the next 24 months beginning January 2024.
Veltrano Dynamics paid quarterly dividends totaling $1.20 per share for the full year, a 9.1% increase over the prior year's dividend payments.
Total headcount at December 31, 2023 stood at 18,450 full-time employees globally, an increase of 1,240 from the prior year-end figure.
The company expanded its workforce most significantly in its software engineering and data analytics functions, consistent with its digital services growth strategy.
Chief Operating Officer Petra Sundvik highlighted investments in employee development and workplace culture as key drivers of reduced voluntary turnover.
Voluntary attrition declined to 8.2% in fiscal 2023 from 11.6% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved engagement metrics across all major geographies.
Veltrano Dynamics launched three new product platforms during the year: the VX-9 robotic assembly arm, the NeuroSense 400 sensor array, and the Veltrano IQ industrial analytics suite.
The VX-9 robotic assembly arm received CE and UL certifications during the second quarter and began commercial shipments in July 2023.
Early customer reception of the VX-9 was strong, with backlog orders reaching $215 million by December 31, 2023.
The NeuroSense 400 sensor array was recognized by the Industrial Technology Review as the most innovative sensing product of 2023.
The Veltrano IQ analytics suite was adopted by 47 enterprise clients within its first six months of general availability, exceeding the company's internal target of 30 clients.
Management expects the three new platforms collectively to contribute between $480 million and $530 million in revenue during fiscal year 2024.
Supply chain disruptions that affected the company in fiscal 2021 and 2022 were largely resolved by the end of the first quarter of 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end were $318 million, normalized compared to the elevated safety-stock positions maintained during the supply chain crisis years.
The company's order backlog stood at $2.17 billion as of December 31, 2023, providing strong revenue visibility entering the new fiscal year.
Geographic revenue distribution showed North America accounting for 38% of total revenue, Europe 34%, Asia-Pacific 22%, and the rest of world 6%.
Asia-Pacific revenue grew 19.4% year-over-year, making it the fastest-growing region for Veltrano Dynamics in fiscal 2023.
The company opened a new regional headquarters in Singapore during the third quarter to better support its expanding Asia-Pacific customer base.
Veltrano Dynamics also established a dedicated research center in Seoul in partnership with the Korea Institute of Advanced Technology.
The Seoul research center is expected to employ 350 specialists focused on next-generation sensor miniaturization by end of fiscal 2025.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to be a priority for the company in fiscal 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 12% compared to the 2022 baseline, exceeding its internal target of 8%.
The company's Stuttgart facility achieved carbon-neutral operations certification in October 2023, the first among its global manufacturing sites.
Renewable energy now accounts for 61% of total electricity consumption across all Veltrano Dynamics facilities worldwide.
The company published its fourth consecutive annual sustainability report, prepared in accordance with the Global Reporting Initiative standards.
On the governance front, two new independent directors were added to the Board in 2023, bringing the total board size to eleven members.
The Board's Audit Committee completed a comprehensive review of the company's internal controls and found no material weaknesses.
Legal proceedings as of the reporting date included a patent dispute with Rhomedex Systems, which management believes will be resolved without material financial impact.
The company maintained product liability insurance and directors-and-officers insurance coverage in line with industry benchmarks.
Veltrano Dynamics' credit ratings were affirmed by both Moody's and S&P during the year, at Baa1 and BBB+ respectively.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $4.15 billion to $4.30 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $680 million and $710 million for fiscal 2024, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Helena Brask emphasized that the company's strong balance sheet positions it well to pursue both organic growth and strategic acquisitions.
Management identified three acquisition targets in the industrial AI software space, though no definitive agreements had been signed as of the report date.
Veltrano Dynamics also indicated it was exploring a potential minority investment in Cerulian Technologies, a specialist in collaborative robotics.
The Precision Robotics segment is expected to benefit most from the projected recovery in global automotive production volumes in 2024.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltrano Dynamics secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven international banks.
The revolving credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Management views the revolving credit facility as a prudent liquidity reserve rather than a source of operational funding at this time.
The company's return on invested capital was 16.8% for fiscal 2023, up from 14.3% in the prior year, reflecting efficient deployment of capital.
Marcus Thellwin concluded the report by reaffirming Veltrano Dynamics' commitment to innovation, operational excellence, and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by highlighting the company's sustained momentum in the North American manufacturing sector.
Veltara Dynamics reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior year.
The company attributed this growth primarily to strong demand for its flagship VertiLine 900 robotic assembly systems.
Operating income reached $312 million, up from $278 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiencies.
Veltara Dynamics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term borrowings, having retired its final outstanding bond obligations in March of that year.
Chief Financial Officer Dennis Araujo stated that the balance sheet had never been stronger, with $540 million in cash and equivalents on hand.
Gross margin improved to 38.7%, compared to 36.1% in the previous fiscal year, driven by favorable input cost trends.
The robotics segment generated $1.12 billion in revenue, making it the largest contributor to total sales for the third consecutive year.
Veltara's industrial sensors division contributed $420 million in revenue, growing 9.2% year-over-year.
The software and services segment, while smaller, posted the fastest growth rate at 21.3%, reaching $300 million in annual revenue.
Research and development expenditure increased to $198 million, representing 10.8% of total revenues.
Holloway emphasized that the R&D investment reflects the company's commitment to next-generation autonomous assembly technology.
Veltara employed 9,200 full-time workers globally as of December 31, 2023, a net addition of 640 employees over the course of the year.
Headcount growth was concentrated in engineering and product development roles, consistent with the company's stated innovation strategy.
The company opened a new manufacturing facility in Dayton, Ohio, which became operational in July 2023 and added 300 production jobs.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $215 million, up from $174 million in fiscal year 2022.
Management indicated that roughly $90 million of that capex was directed toward the Dayton facility expansion.
Veltara's effective tax rate for the year was 22.1%, slightly below the 23.4% rate recorded in the prior period.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $4.62, exceeding analyst consensus estimates of $4.38.
The board of directors approved an increase to the quarterly dividend from $0.28 per share to $0.34 per share, effective in the first quarter of 2024.
Veltara repurchased 3.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $61.40, totaling approximately $190 million.
The share repurchase program reflects management's confidence in the company's intrinsic value and long-term growth prospects.
Return on equity for the year was 19.4%, compared to 17.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Araujo noted that working capital management improvements contributed approximately 80 basis points to the margin expansion.
Inventory levels decreased modestly to $334 million at year-end, down from $351 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in inventory was the result of disciplined supply chain optimization initiatives launched in the second half of 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 52 days, indicating faster collections from customers.
The company secured three significant multi-year contracts during 2023, collectively valued at over $600 million.
One of these contracts, with a major automotive manufacturer, is expected to contribute meaningfully to revenues beginning in fiscal year 2025.
Veltara's international revenues grew to $510 million, representing 27.7% of total sales, compared to 24.3% the prior year.
Germany and South Korea were the top two international markets by revenue contribution.
The company established a new sales office in Munich during the third quarter of 2023 to support European expansion.
Veltara's market share in the North American robotic assembly systems segment reached an estimated 18.3%, up from 16.7% a year earlier.
The competitive landscape remained intense, with several well-capitalized Asian competitors expanding their North American presence.
Holloway acknowledged competitive pressure but expressed confidence that Veltara's technological differentiation provides a durable advantage.
The company received four industry awards in 2023 for product innovation, including recognition from the Automation Industry Alliance.
Customer satisfaction scores, as measured by annual surveys, improved to 87.4 out of 100, up from 84.1 the prior year.
Net promoter scores also improved materially, rising to 61 from 54 in fiscal year 2022.
Veltara launched the VertiLine 1100 series in October 2023, positioning it as the most advanced product in its robotics portfolio.
Early customer reception to the VertiLine 1100 has been described by management as exceptionally positive.
The company's backlog at year-end stood at $1.03 billion, a record high and a 22% increase over the prior year's backlog.
Araujo noted that the strong backlog provides good revenue visibility heading into fiscal year 2024.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to gain prominence within Veltara's corporate strategy.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 8.3% in fiscal year 2023 relative to the baseline year of 2020.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara secured a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to support its strategic growth initiatives.
Proceeds from the term loan were earmarked for potential acquisitions and the build-out of a second international manufacturing site.
Veltara's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Poors following the announcement of the new financing arrangement.
Management stated that the company intends to maintain a net leverage ratio below 1.5 times EBITDA over the medium term.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, Veltara issued guidance for revenue in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion.
Operating margin guidance for 2024 was set at 17.5% to 18.5%, reflecting continued investment in growth.
Holloway concluded the report by expressing gratitude to Veltara's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and partnership.
Velorant Systems Corporation is a mid-cap technology hardware and embedded solutions provider headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company designs and manufactures ruggedized computing modules for industrial automation, defense logistics, and smart infrastructure applications.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's twelfth consecutive year of operations since its founding in 2011.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delray opened the annual report by highlighting the company's resilience amid macroeconomic headwinds.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Nambiar noted that disciplined cost management was central to the company's performance strategy throughout the year.
Velorant Systems reported total annual revenue of $412.7 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.4% increase over the prior year.
Product revenue accounted for $318.5 million of the total, while services and maintenance contracts contributed the remaining $94.2 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $168.3 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 40.8%.
Operating expenses totaled $109.6 million, inclusive of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative costs.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $58.7 million, compared to $49.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $41.2 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $1.87.
The company's adjusted EBITDA for the period was $74.5 million, reflecting margin expansion of approximately 120 basis points year over year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $22.1 million, primarily allocated toward manufacturing capacity upgrades in the Austin facility.
Free cash flow generation reached $52.4 million, reinforcing the company's ability to self-fund organic growth initiatives.
Velorant Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired its final remaining term loan in March of that year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $87.6 million, providing a strong liquidity buffer against potential market disruptions.
Accounts receivable days outstanding averaged 43 days during the fiscal year, consistent with prior-year collection performance.
Inventory levels were actively managed throughout 2023, ending the year at $61.3 million, a 7.2% reduction from the prior-year balance.
The reduction in inventory was attributed to improved demand forecasting tools implemented by the supply chain team in late 2022.
Velorant's supply chain team, led by Senior Vice President Helena Kowalczyk, successfully negotiated multi-year sourcing agreements with three Tier-1 component vendors.
These agreements are expected to reduce component costs by approximately 4% annually over the next three years.
The company's flagship product line, the RX-900 Series, saw unit shipments increase by 18% compared to fiscal year 2022.
RX-900 Series revenue grew to $187.4 million, making it the single largest revenue contributor in the company's portfolio.
Customer retention rates across the enterprise segment reached 94%, reflecting the strength of the company's technical support and upgrade programs.
Velorant Systems expanded its customer base by adding 37 new enterprise accounts during fiscal year 2023.
International revenue grew by 14.3% year over year, now representing 28% of total consolidated revenues.
The EMEA region posted the strongest international growth at 22%, driven by smart grid infrastructure deployments in Germany and the Netherlands.
Asia-Pacific revenues remained relatively flat, with modest growth of 2.1% year over year due to softness in the industrial automation sector.
North American revenues grew 7.8%, supported by renewed defense logistics contracts and continued demand from the energy sector.
The company's R&D expenditure in fiscal year 2023 was $28.4 million, representing 6.9% of total revenues.
R&D efforts were concentrated on the next-generation RX-1100 platform, which is expected to launch commercially in the second half of fiscal year 2024.
The RX-1100 platform will feature enhanced edge computing capabilities and support for real-time AI inference workloads.
Velorant received two U.S. patents during the year related to thermal management innovations incorporated into its embedded module architecture.
The company's engineering headcount grew to 412 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 378 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Total global headcount reached 1,847 employees at fiscal year-end, reflecting hiring activity across manufacturing, sales, and support functions.
Employee turnover in technical roles declined to 8.3% in 2023 from 11.7% in 2022, attributed in part to revised compensation benchmarking.
The company introduced a performance-based equity plan in February 2023 designed to retain senior engineering and product management talent.
Board of Directors chairwoman Dr. Sylvia Okonkwo emphasized commitment to environmental, social, and governance practices in her letter to shareholders.
Velorant Systems reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% on an absolute basis compared to the prior year.
The company achieved ISO 14001 environmental management certification for its Austin manufacturing facility in September 2023.
Velorant's diversity initiatives contributed to an increase in women in senior leadership roles, rising from 31% to 37% year over year.
The Board approved a share repurchase program of up to $30 million in October 2023, effective through December 2024.
Under the repurchase program, the company had bought back approximately 1.1 million shares by fiscal year-end at an average price of $18.40.
No cash dividends were declared or paid during fiscal year 2023, consistent with the company's longstanding policy of reinvesting earnings into growth.
Velorant's return on equity for the fiscal year was 16.2%, an improvement from 13.9% recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.8, and its quick ratio was 1.9, both indicating solid short-term financial health.
Priya Nambiar confirmed during the earnings call that the company's balance sheet remained entirely unencumbered by interest-bearing liabilities.
Velorant's audit committee engaged Ernst & Wolff LLP as independent auditors, who issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
The auditors noted no material weaknesses in the company's internal controls over financial reporting.
Management's outlook for fiscal year 2024 projects revenue in the range of $445 million to $460 million, representing approximately 8% to 11% growth.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is expected to fall between $80 million and $85 million.
The company plans to increase R&D spending to approximately 8% of projected revenues in fiscal year 2024 to accelerate the RX-1100 launch.
Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $28 million to $32 million, reflecting planned expansion of the Austin facility.
Marcus Delray expressed confidence that Velorant is well-positioned to capture incremental market share as competitors face supply chain disruptions.
In closing remarks, the CFO reiterated that financial flexibility would remain a strategic priority heading into the new fiscal year.

To support the planned facility expansion and accelerate the RX-1100 development timeline, Velorant entered into a $175 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of lenders in November 2023.
The company's investor relations team announced that the next earnings call is scheduled for May 14, 2024.
Velorant Systems remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through disciplined execution, product innovation, and operational efficiency.
Crestwood Dynamics Corporation is a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company designs and manufactures programmable logic controllers, robotics integration platforms, and factory monitoring software.
Crestwood Dynamics was founded in 1998 by current Chairman Emeritus Roland Fitch and has grown steadily over the past two decades.
Chief Executive Officer Marianne Holst took the helm in 2019 and has since led the company through a significant digital transformation.
Chief Financial Officer Derek Pang joined the executive team in 2021, bringing over eighteen years of experience in industrial sector finance.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Crestwood Dynamics reported total revenues of $847 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit for the year reached $312 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8%.
Operating income came in at $94 million, reflecting continued investment in research and development as well as expanded sales infrastructure.
Crestwood Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, completing the year in a fully unencumbered financial position.
The company's cash and cash equivalents stood at $218 million at year-end, providing a strong liquidity cushion.
Accounts receivable increased by $31 million year-over-year, consistent with revenue growth and slightly elongated collection cycles in the Asia-Pacific region.
Inventory levels rose to $145 million, reflecting deliberate safety stock buildup in response to ongoing supply chain uncertainties.
Capital expenditures totaled $58 million during fiscal 2023, focused primarily on expanding manufacturing capacity at the Dayton, Ohio facility.
The Dayton expansion added approximately 95,000 square feet of production floor space and is expected to be fully operational by Q2 2024.
Crestwood Dynamics employed 4,210 full-time staff globally as of December 31, 2023, up from 3,890 the prior year.
The headcount increase was driven primarily by hiring in engineering, sales, and customer success roles across North America and Europe.
The company's North American segment generated revenues of $498 million, accounting for approximately 58.8% of total revenues.
European segment revenues reached $221 million, a 9.1% increase from fiscal 2022, supported by strong demand in German and Nordic manufacturing markets.
The Asia-Pacific segment contributed $128 million in revenues, growing 18.3% year-over-year, representing the fastest-growing region for the company.
Marianne Holst attributed the Asia-Pacific growth to successful partnerships with three major electronics manufacturers in South Korea and Taiwan.
Software and recurring services revenue grew to $189 million in fiscal 2023, representing 22.3% of total revenues.
This marks the third consecutive year in which software revenue has grown faster than hardware revenue, underscoring the company's platform strategy.
The flagship product, CrestLogic Pro 7, was launched in March 2023 and received strong market reception across all geographies.
CrestLogic Pro 7 incorporates machine learning-based anomaly detection and real-time process optimization capabilities.
By year-end, more than 1,200 enterprise clients had adopted CrestLogic Pro 7, exceeding the initial internal forecast of 900 clients.
Derek Pang noted that the software attach rate for new hardware installations reached 74% in fiscal 2023, up from 61% in fiscal 2022.
Research and development expenditures totaled $72 million, or approximately 8.5% of revenues, as the company prioritized next-generation platform development.
The R&D team grew by 140 engineers during the year, with new hires concentrated in artificial intelligence and cybersecurity disciplines.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $146 million, reflecting the costs of expanded go-to-market activities and executive compensation increases.
Net income for fiscal 2023 was $68 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.14.
This represents a 19.3% increase in net income compared to fiscal 2022, when net income was $57 million.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 27.8%, slightly above management's guidance of 26.5% due to certain non-deductible acquisition-related costs.
Crestwood Dynamics repurchased 1.4 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $38.60 per share, spending approximately $54 million.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share in December 2023, representing a 12.5% increase over the prior quarterly dividend.
Total shareholder returns for fiscal 2023 were 28.4%, outperforming the S&P Industrials sector index by approximately 11 percentage points.
The company's return on invested capital was 14.2% in fiscal 2023, an improvement from 12.7% in fiscal 2022.
Return on equity improved to 18.6% in fiscal 2023, reflecting higher net income and disciplined capital allocation.
Crestwood Dynamics' backlog of confirmed orders stood at $392 million at year-end, representing approximately 5.6 months of forward revenue coverage.
The backlog figure increased by 17.2% compared to December 31, 2022, indicating strong demand heading into fiscal 2024.
Management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $950 million, implying growth of 8.6% to 12.2%.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 11.5% to 12.5%, reflecting anticipated leverage from the Dayton facility expansion.
Derek Pang emphasized that working capital efficiency remained a top priority for the finance team entering fiscal 2024.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.8, well above the industry median of 1.9, reflecting strong short-term financial health.
Crestwood Dynamics held no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements as of the reporting date, maintaining full transparency in its capital structure.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Sylvia Kramer, confirmed that no material weaknesses were identified during the fiscal 2023 audit.
Deloitte & Touche LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2023 and issued an unqualified opinion.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company reducing its carbon intensity by 8% year-over-year.
Crestwood Dynamics committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its direct operations by 2035 as part of its updated sustainability roadmap.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, the company entered into a $150 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five lending institutions.
The credit facility carries a floating rate tied to SOFR plus a margin of 125 basis points and matures in December 2028.
Customer concentration risk remained manageable, with no single customer accounting for more than 8.4% of total revenues in fiscal 2023.
The top ten customers collectively represented approximately 41% of total revenues, consistent with the prior year's concentration profile.
Crestwood Dynamics expanded its authorized dealer network to 340 partners globally in fiscal 2023, up from 275 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The dealer network expansion was particularly significant in Southeast Asia, where 28 new partners were onboarded during the year.
In February 2024, the company completed the acquisition of Virtex Sensing Technologies, a provider of industrial IoT sensor platforms, for $112 million in cash.
The Virtex acquisition is expected to be earnings-accretive beginning in the second half of fiscal 2024, according to Derek Pang.
Integration costs associated with the Virtex acquisition are projected at approximately $9 million, to be recognized through mid-fiscal 2025.
Marianne Holst described the Virtex transaction as a strategic complement to Crestwood's existing hardware and software portfolio.
The combined entity is expected to serve more than 5,800 enterprise clients worldwide following full integration of the Virtex customer base.
Crestwood Dynamics' shares closed fiscal year 2023 at $46.22, implying a market capitalization of approximately $1.47 billion.
The company remains listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol CRWD and is included in the Russell 2000 Index.
Management expressed confidence that the combination of organic growth initiatives and the Virtex acquisition positions Crestwood Dynamics for sustained long-term value creation.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties, including macroeconomic conditions, competitive dynamics, and successful integration of acquired businesses.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Durham, North Carolina, focused on oncology and rare disease therapeutics.
The company was founded in 2007 by Dr. Elaine Forsythe and has grown to become a recognized innovator in targeted cancer therapies.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Holt presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Sandra Vreeland noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformative period for the company's balance sheet.
Total net revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, reflecting an increase of 12.3% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Product revenues accounted for $1.61 billion of total net revenues, driven primarily by the continued commercial success of Meridian's flagship drug, Velotrex.
Velotrex, approved by the FDA in 2019 for non-small cell lung cancer, generated $920 million in sales during fiscal year 2023.
Collaboration and licensing revenues contributed an additional $230 million, up from $185 million in the prior year.
Gross profit for the year was $1.29 billion, yielding a gross margin of approximately 70.1%, consistent with management's long-term target range.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $860 million for fiscal year 2023.
Research and development spending reached $430 million, representing 23.4% of total net revenues and reflecting Meridian's commitment to pipeline investment.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $310 million, a modest increase from $288 million in fiscal year 2022.
Restructuring and other charges amounted to $120 million, primarily related to the consolidation of two manufacturing facilities in New Jersey.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $432 million, compared to $374 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to Meridian Biopharma Solutions shareholders was $318 million for fiscal year 2023.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $4.12, exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of $3.97.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $2.1 billion.
Free cash flow generation for the year was $510 million, up from $443 million in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditures totaled $145 million, directed largely toward expanding the company's biologics manufacturing capacity in Research Triangle Park.
Meridian's board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing a 7.7% increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend.
The company repurchased 4.2 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $87.40, returning approximately $367 million to shareholders.
Total shares outstanding at year-end stood at approximately 77.2 million on a diluted basis.
Meridian Biopharma Solutions carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having repaid its remaining $400 million term loan in March of that year.
The company's pipeline includes fourteen active clinical programs across oncology, hematology, and rare metabolic disorders.
MBX-047, a novel KRAS G12C inhibitor, advanced into Phase III clinical trials in the second quarter of 2023 and remains on track for a regulatory submission in late 2025.
MBX-112, targeting acute myeloid leukemia, reported positive Phase II interim results in October 2023, with an overall response rate of 61% in the evaluable patient population.
The company also submitted a supplemental new drug application for Velotrex in combination with a PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor for second-line treatment of bladder cancer.
Regulatory review of that supplemental application is ongoing, with a PDUFA action date of June 18, 2024.
International revenues grew 18.4% year-over-year to $612 million, with particularly strong performance in Germany, Japan, and Canada.
Meridian signed a co-promotion agreement with Hanseatic Pharma AG in September 2023, expanding Velotrex's commercial footprint across seven additional European markets.
The company opened a new regional headquarters in Zurich, Switzerland, to support its growing European commercial and medical affairs operations.
Headcount at year-end was 4,870 full-time employees, compared to 4,410 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was driven by commercial expansion activities, pipeline-related clinical operations hires, and the integration of Tessera Diagnostics, acquired in April 2023.
Meridian acquired Tessera Diagnostics, a companion diagnostics specialist, for $215 million in an all-cash transaction.
Tessera's diagnostic assay platform is expected to support biomarker-driven patient selection for several of Meridian's late-stage pipeline candidates.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023, stood at $284 million, representing approximately 64 days of supply on hand.
Management described inventory positioning as appropriate given current demand forecasts and supply chain conditions.
Accounts receivable at year-end were $388 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 77 days.
The company experienced no material supply disruptions during fiscal year 2023, maintaining a fill rate above 99% for all commercial products.
Sandra Vreeland emphasized that Meridian's financial strength provides significant flexibility for both organic and inorganic growth opportunities.
Marcus Holt reiterated management's commitment to disciplined capital allocation, balancing investment in the pipeline with shareholder returns.
For fiscal year 2024, management provided full-year guidance calling for net revenues in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.10 billion.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $4.50 to $4.70, reflecting anticipated operating leverage.
The guidance assumes continued Velotrex growth, contributions from pipeline catalysts, and incremental revenues from the Hanseatic co-promotion arrangement.
Management also guided for R&D spending to increase to approximately $490 million to $510 million as Phase III programs advance.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 to remain in the range of $150 million to $165 million.
Meridian anticipates completing enrollment in the MBX-047 Phase III trial by the end of the third quarter of 2024.
The MBX-112 program is expected to advance to a Phase III registration trial, pending discussions with the FDA following an End-of-Phase-II meeting scheduled for March 2024.
Chief Medical Officer Dr. Patricia Nweke expressed confidence that the clinical data generated in 2023 meaningfully de-risked the company's late-stage pipeline.
The company also anticipates initiating a Phase I study for MBX-198, a first-in-class dual FLT3/CDK4 inhibitor, in the second half of 2024.
Meridian's manufacturing partnerships with two contract development and manufacturing organizations provide redundancy and scale to support anticipated commercial demand.

The Board of Directors nominated three new independent directors for election at the 2024 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, bringing diverse expertise in biotechnology, finance, and global health policy.
Meridian was recognized as one of the top pharmaceutical employers in North Carolina for the third consecutive year by Business North Carolina magazine.
The company's environmental sustainability report, published in November 2023, outlined a commitment to achieving carbon neutrality in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2035.
Meridian reduced its greenhouse gas emissions by 8% year-over-year through energy efficiency upgrades at its Durham headquarters and Research Triangle Park manufacturing site.
The company donated $12 million to global health initiatives in 2023, including partnerships with NGOs focused on cancer screening access in low- and middle-income countries.
Patient assistance programs provided Velotrex at no cost to approximately 2,800 eligible patients in the United States during fiscal year 2023.
The company's stock closed fiscal year 2023 at $91.60 per share, representing a total return of approximately 24% for the calendar year.
Analyst coverage of Meridian Biopharma Solutions includes fifteen sell-side firms, with ten carrying a Buy or equivalent rating as of the date of this report.
Marcus Holt concluded his remarks by stating that fiscal year 2023 demonstrated the resilience and growth potential of Meridian's diversified commercial and pipeline portfolio.

Looking ahead, management expressed optimism that the company's execution capabilities, financial foundation, and scientific innovation would continue to drive long-term value creation for all stakeholders.

In January 2024, Meridian Biopharma Solutions secured a $600 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of eight major banks to support strategic growth initiatives and general corporate purposes.
Subsequent to year-end, the company drew down $150 million from the facility to partially fund the acquisition of a preclinical rare disease asset from a European biotech company.
Vermillion Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced thermal management solutions, hereby presents its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, operates across four primary business segments: Industrial Cooling, Aerospace Components, Consumer Electronics Thermal, and Energy Recovery Systems.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holbrook opened her letter to shareholders with an expression of confidence in the company's strategic direction and long-term growth prospects.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Trevino provided a comprehensive overview of the financial results, noting that fiscal year 2023 represented a period of significant operational milestones.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $2.84 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of 11.3% from the $2.55 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The Industrial Cooling segment remained the largest contributor to revenue, generating $1.12 billion, up 8.7% compared to the prior year.
Aerospace Components revenue grew by 19.4% year-over-year to reach $487 million, driven by increased demand from commercial aviation customers resuming fleet expansion programs.
Consumer Electronics Thermal revenue totaled $641 million, reflecting modest growth of 6.2% as smartphone and laptop manufacturers increased orders for high-efficiency heat dissipation modules.
Energy Recovery Systems, the company's newest segment established in 2021, contributed $592 million in revenue, marking a robust 18.1% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year stood at $924 million, yielding a gross margin of 32.5%, which compares favorably to the 30.8% gross margin achieved in the prior fiscal year.

Chief Operating Officer Diane Castellano attributed the margin improvement to disciplined procurement strategies and the successful implementation of lean manufacturing protocols across all four facilities.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $512 million, representing 18.0% of total revenue, which is consistent with management's previously communicated targets.
Research and development expenditure for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $198 million, reflecting the company's sustained commitment to innovation in next-generation thermal interface materials.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $314 million, a decrease of $12 million relative to fiscal year 2022 despite modest headcount additions in the sales function.
Operating income for the fiscal year totaled $412 million, representing an operating margin of 14.5%, the highest the company has achieved since its initial public offering in 2011.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $298 million, corresponding to diluted earnings per share of $4.72, compared to $3.89 per diluted share in fiscal year 2022.
As of December 31, 2023, Vermillion Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its remaining $320 million senior notes in March 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $743 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity to fund near-term strategic initiatives.
Accounts receivable stood at $381 million, representing approximately 49 days of sales outstanding, which management considers within the normal operating range.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $276 million, a decrease of $34 million from the prior year-end, reflecting improved demand forecasting and supply chain optimization efforts.

Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $187 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's facilities in San Antonio, Texas and Raleigh, North Carolina.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, representing an annual dividend of $1.12 per share, a 12% increase from the prior year's dividend rate.
Patricia Holbrook emphasized that the dividend increase reflects the board's confidence in the sustainability of the company's cash generation and its commitment to returning capital to shareholders.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares of its common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $62.40 per share, for a total outlay of approximately $200 million.
At year-end, approximately $350 million of authorization remained under the existing share repurchase program approved by the board in February 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.1%, compared to 24.7% in the prior year, primarily due to the utilization of research and development tax credits.
Vermillion Dynamics launched its flagship product, the ThermaEdge Pro 7 series, in the second quarter of fiscal year 2023, targeting data center cooling applications.
Initial customer reception of the ThermaEdge Pro 7 exceeded internal expectations, with over 240 enterprise customers placing orders within the first 90 days of commercial availability.
The company secured a landmark five-year supply agreement with GlobalStream Technologies valued at approximately $780 million over the contract term.
Marcus Trevino noted that the GlobalStream Technologies agreement represents the single largest contract in Vermillion Dynamics' corporate history.
Backlog at December 31, 2023 stood at a record $1.94 billion, an increase of $420 million or 27.6% compared to the backlog of $1.52 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The geographic mix of revenue shifted modestly during fiscal year 2023, with international sales comprising 38% of total revenue compared to 34% in fiscal year 2022.
Asia-Pacific revenue grew by 31.2% year-over-year, the fastest-growing region for Vermillion Dynamics, driven by strong demand from electronics manufacturers in Taiwan and South Korea.
European revenue increased by 14.8% to $412 million, aided by new customer wins in the German industrial automation sector.
The company expanded its workforce during fiscal year 2023, ending the year with 14,200 full-time employees, an increase of 1,050 employees compared to year-end 2022.
Diane Castellano highlighted that approximately 62% of new hires were in engineering and technical roles, consistent with the company's strategy of deepening its technical expertise.
The company's employee retention rate of 91.3% in fiscal year 2023 compared favorably to the industry average of approximately 84%, reflecting investments in compensation and workplace culture.
Vermillion Dynamics received the 2023 Industrial Excellence Award from the National Manufacturing Association, recognizing its operational efficiency and quality management programs.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives continued to advance, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 14% relative to the fiscal year 2021 baseline.
The company's San Antonio facility achieved ISO 50001 energy management certification in August 2023, becoming the third Vermillion Dynamics facility to receive this designation.
Total water consumption across company operations decreased by 9.3% in fiscal year 2023, attributed to water recycling systems installed as part of the sustainable operations program.
The board of directors consists of ten members, seven of whom are independent directors, with an average tenure of 6.2 years.
Compensation committee chair Renata Figueroa noted that executive compensation for fiscal year 2023 was closely aligned with the company's strong financial performance outcomes.
Patricia Holbrook's total compensation for fiscal year 2023, including base salary, annual incentive, and long-term equity awards, was approximately $9.7 million.
Marcus Trevino's total compensation was approximately $6.2 million, reflecting his role in orchestrating the company's capital allocation and financial planning processes.
For fiscal year 2024, the company provided initial guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $3.10 billion to $3.20 billion, implying growth of approximately 9% to 13%.
Management guided for diluted earnings per share in the range of $5.10 to $5.40 for fiscal year 2024, reflecting continued margin expansion and effective cost discipline.
The company expects capital expenditures of approximately $210 million to $230 million in fiscal year 2024, reflecting continued investment in manufacturing capacity expansion.
Management anticipates that the Energy Recovery Systems segment will be the fastest-growing segment in fiscal year 2024, with targeted revenue growth of 22% to 26%.
The company's liquidity position remains robust, with the $743 million cash balance complemented by an undrawn $400 million revolving credit facility available as a backstop.
Risk factors disclosed by management include potential supply chain disruptions for rare earth materials used in several thermal interface compound formulations.
Additionally, management cited competitive pressures from lower-cost Asian manufacturers as a risk to gross margin sustainability in the Consumer Electronics Thermal segment.
Currency fluctuation risk was identified as a growing concern given the increasing proportion of international revenue denominated in foreign currencies.

The company's internal control over financial reporting was assessed by management and deemed effective under the criteria established by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.
Independent auditor Whitmore & Crane LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Whitmore & Crane also provided an attestation report on internal control over financial reporting, concluding that Vermillion Dynamics maintained effective internal controls as of year-end.

In the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023, Vermillion Dynamics completed the acquisition of HelioSink Technologies, a specialist in liquid cooling solutions, for a total purchase price of $215 million.
The HelioSink acquisition was financed through a combination of $430 million in newly issued term loans and revolving credit borrowings arranged in November 2023.
Goodwill recorded in connection with the HelioSink acquisition amounted to $87 million, reflecting the anticipated synergies and assembled workforce benefits.
Management expects the HelioSink acquisition to contribute approximately $65 million to $75 million in incremental revenue in fiscal year 2024 as integration activities are completed.
The integration of HelioSink Technologies is being led by Chief Integration Officer Samuel Winters, who joined Vermillion Dynamics in September 2023 specifically to oversee this initiative.
Vermillion Dynamics' common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol VRMD and closed fiscal year 2023 at a price of $74.18 per share.
The 52-week high of VRMD shares was $81.50, reached in September 2023 following the announcement of the GlobalStream Technologies supply agreement.
Market capitalization at December 31, 2023 was approximately $4.68 billion based on 63.1 million diluted shares outstanding and the closing share price.

Management wishes to thank all employees, customers, partners, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership as Vermillion Dynamics pursues its mission of engineering a more thermally efficient world.
Veltrano Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Lyon, France, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Margaux Delcourt, reported consolidated revenues of €1.47 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Thierry Bonhomme attributed the revenue growth to strong demand across Veltrano's polymer additives and surface treatment segments.
The polymer additives division alone contributed €621 million to total revenues, up from €548 million in fiscal year 2022.
Surface treatment products generated €412 million in revenues, reflecting a 7.4% year-over-year improvement driven by automotive sector clients.
The remaining revenues were attributable to Veltrano's specialty coatings division, which contributed €437 million during the fiscal year.
Gross profit for the year reached €489 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%, compared to 31.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled €298 million, representing a modest increase of 4.1% relative to the prior period.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €262 million, exceeding the company's own guidance of €245 million.
Net income attributable to Veltrano shareholders was €131 million, translating to earnings per share of €2.74 on a diluted basis.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €0.85 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This dividend represents a 13.3% increase over the €0.75 per share distributed in respect of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of €2.14 billion and shareholders' equity of €983 million as of December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents stood at €178 million at year-end, providing the company with ample liquidity for near-term operational requirements.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €94 million, reflecting investments in capacity expansion at the company's Toulouse and Bratislava manufacturing sites.
The Toulouse facility upgrade, completed in October 2023, added approximately 18,000 metric tons of annual production capacity for polymer additives.
Research and development expenditures reached €67 million, equal to roughly 4.6% of total revenues, underscoring Veltrano's commitment to innovation.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltrano carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its previous credit obligations during the third quarter.
The company's supply chain optimization initiative, launched in early 2023, resulted in a 6% reduction in raw material procurement costs over the course of the year.
Veltrano's headcount grew to 6,840 full-time equivalent employees by year-end, an increase of 310 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
The human resources team oversaw a significant hiring push in the second half of 2023, focused primarily on engineering and process chemistry roles.
Employee-related costs, including wages, benefits, and share-based compensation, amounted to €334 million for the full year.
Veltrano's environmental, social, and governance program continued to advance, with the company reducing its Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions by 11% versus 2022.
The renewable energy procurement agreement signed with a Spanish wind consortium in April 2023 now covers approximately 34% of Veltrano's European electricity consumption.

CEO Margaux Delcourt emphasized that sustainability investments are integral to the company's long-term competitive positioning, particularly as regulatory requirements tighten across the European Union.
Segment-level operating income for polymer additives was €148 million, representing an operating margin of 23.8% for that division.
Surface treatment achieved segment operating income of €87 million, an operating margin of 21.1%, while specialty coatings delivered €62 million in segment operating income.
Corporate and unallocated costs totaled €35 million, bringing consolidated operating income to €262 million before the effects of depreciation add-back reconciliation items.
Depreciation and amortization charges for the year amounted to €71 million, consistent with the company's guidance range of €68 million to €74 million.
Free cash flow generation was particularly strong, reaching €168 million after capital expenditures, compared to €119 million in fiscal year 2022.
Working capital management improved considerably, with days sales outstanding declining from 54 days to 48 days and inventory turnover rising to 6.2 times.
The company completed the divestiture of its legacy adhesives business in February 2023 for consideration of €58 million, recognizing a pre-tax gain of €14 million.
Proceeds from the adhesives divestiture were allocated to fund the Bratislava manufacturing expansion and general corporate purposes.
Veltrano's geographic revenue breakdown showed that Western Europe accounted for 62% of revenues, Asia-Pacific for 21%, and North America for the remaining 17%.
Revenue growth in Asia-Pacific was particularly notable, accelerating to 18.4% year-over-year as Veltrano benefited from expanding relationships with electronics and semiconductor clients.
The company opened a new regional sales office in Singapore in July 2023, enhancing its commercial presence across Southeast Asian markets.
In North America, revenues grew 6.8% year-over-year, supported by increased penetration in the aerospace coatings subsegment.

Veltrano's strategic partnership with Meridian Composites Corporation, announced in September 2023, is expected to drive incremental revenues of approximately €40 million annually beginning in fiscal year 2025.
CFO Thierry Bonhomme highlighted that the partnership agreement includes a mutual exclusivity clause covering advanced polymer formulations for aerospace applications.
The company's order backlog stood at €384 million as of December 31, 2023, representing approximately 3.1 months of equivalent revenues.
This backlog figure is 22% higher than the €315 million reported at the end of fiscal year 2022, reflecting robust forward demand across all three business segments.
Veltrano's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Fitch Europe during the fourth quarter, with a stable outlook cited across both assessments.
The stable credit outlook reflects the rating agencies' confidence in Veltrano's earnings consistency, cash flow generation, and prudent financial management.
Looking ahead, Veltrano issued revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 in the range of €1.57 billion to €1.63 billion, implying growth of approximately 7% to 11%.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €278 million to €292 million, reflecting continued margin improvement driven by operational efficiency initiatives.
The company also indicated plans to invest €105 million to €115 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024, primarily targeting its specialty coatings manufacturing infrastructure.
Veltrano's board authorized a share repurchase program of up to €75 million, to be executed over a twenty-four-month period beginning January 2024.
This buyback authorization represents approximately 4.1% of the company's market capitalization based on the closing share price of €37.82 on December 29, 2023.
The repurchase program is intended to offset dilution from the company's employee equity incentive plans and return capital to long-term shareholders.
Margaux Delcourt commented that Veltrano is well-positioned to capture structural growth trends in electric vehicle battery materials, green building products, and advanced electronics.
She also noted that the company's balanced geographic footprint provides a natural hedge against regional demand fluctuations and currency volatility.
The Euro to U.S. dollar exchange rate movements had a net negative translational impact of approximately €18 million on reported revenues for the full year.
Veltrano's tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.8%, compared to 25.6% in fiscal year 2022, partly reflecting the utilization of previously unrecognized deferred tax assets.
The company expects its effective tax rate to stabilize in the range of 24% to 26% over the medium term, consistent with current French and international tax frameworks.
Internal audit and risk management functions were strengthened during 2023, with the appointment of a new Chief Risk Officer, Sandrine Vauclaire, in June.
Vauclaire's mandate includes enhancing Veltrano's enterprise risk framework with particular emphasis on cybersecurity resilience and supply chain concentration risks.
Veltrano concluded fiscal year 2023 with a strong foundation in cash generation, stakeholder confidence, and operational momentum across all three core segments.

The company announced in December 2023 the execution of a €320 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of seven European banks, maturing in 2028, to support strategic growth financing.
Velorix Technologies Inc. is a mid-cap semiconductor and embedded systems company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company designs and manufactures specialized microcontrollers for industrial automation, automotive, and consumer electronics markets.
Founded in 2004 by CEO Marcus Ellroy and CTO Priya Sundaram, Velorix has grown from a small fab-less startup into a globally recognized brand.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's nineteenth year of continuous operations.
The annual report covers the period from January 1, 2023 to December 31, 2023.
Velorix reported consolidated net revenues of $2.14 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the period reached $894 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 41.8%.
Operating income totaled $312 million, reflecting a 9.7% operating margin.
The company generated free cash flow of $278 million, a figure the board described as robust given macro headwinds.
As of December 31, 2023, Velorix carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its final tranche of senior notes in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the fiscal year were $185 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's advanced packaging facility in Penang, Malaysia.
Research and development expenses totaled $310 million, accounting for roughly 14.5% of net revenues.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $272 million, essentially flat compared to the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Dana Kirkland noted that disciplined cost management enabled the company to exceed its internal operating income targets.
Velorix's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 17.2%, benefiting from R&D tax credits in multiple jurisdictions.
Diluted earnings per share came in at $3.84, compared to $3.41 in fiscal 2022, an increase of approximately 12.6%.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
In fiscal 2023, Velorix repurchased approximately 4.2 million shares of common stock for a total consideration of $189 million.
The company's total headcount as of December 31, 2023 stood at 8,640 full-time employees globally.
Velorix added approximately 920 net new employees during fiscal 2023, predominantly in engineering and product development roles.
The automotive segment contributed $718 million in revenues, representing 33.5% of total company revenues.
Industrial automation revenues grew 18.4% year-over-year to $632 million, driven by strong demand across North America and Europe.
Consumer electronics revenues were $483 million, which the company acknowledged reflected modest pressure from inventory correction cycles.
The remaining $307 million in revenues came from the company's licensing and IP royalty business.
Velorix's top ten customers accounted for approximately 41% of total revenues in fiscal 2023.
No single customer represented more than 9% of total net revenues during the year.
Geographic revenue distribution showed North America at 38%, Asia-Pacific at 35%, Europe at 22%, and rest-of-world at 5%.
The company's backlog at year-end stood at approximately $1.6 billion, providing solid near-term revenue visibility.
Priya Sundaram highlighted breakthroughs in the company's next-generation VX-9 microcontroller platform as a key growth catalyst.
The VX-9 platform was officially launched in September 2023, featuring a 40% improvement in power efficiency compared to its predecessor.
Initial customer adoption of VX-9 exceeded internal projections, with design wins recorded at fourteen Tier-1 automotive suppliers.
The company secured 23 new design wins across all segments in fiscal 2023, compared to 18 in fiscal 2022.
Velorix's patent portfolio expanded to 1,847 active patents by year-end, up from 1,703 at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company filed 214 new patent applications during fiscal 2023, underscoring its continued emphasis on intellectual property protection.
Inventory levels at December 31, 2023 were $341 million, representing approximately 58 days of inventory on hand.
Management noted that inventory levels normalized significantly during the second half of 2023 following elevated levels in early Q1.
Accounts receivable at year-end totaled $318 million, with a days sales outstanding metric of approximately 54 days.
The company's current ratio at year-end was 2.8, indicative of strong near-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents plus short-term investments totaled $1.02 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Dana Kirkland indicated that the company's strong cash position affords flexibility for both organic investment and potential M&A activity.
Velorix made one bolt-on acquisition during the year, completing the purchase of embedded AI startup NeuralEdge Systems for $94 million in April 2023.
The NeuralEdge acquisition added 87 engineers and a portfolio of edge inference algorithms complementary to Velorix's microcontroller roadmap.
Integration of NeuralEdge is expected to be substantially complete by Q2 2024, according to statements made at the December investor day.
Velorix's sustainability initiatives resulted in a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to fiscal 2022.
The company sourced approximately 61% of its electricity from renewable sources in fiscal 2023, up from 49% in fiscal 2022.
Velorix maintained an employee satisfaction index of 78 out of 100 in its annual internal engagement survey.
Attrition rate improved to 8.2% in fiscal 2023, down from 11.4% in fiscal 2022, reflecting strengthened retention programs.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.30 billion to $2.42 billion.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at 10% to 11%, implying meaningful operating leverage as revenues scale.
During the first quarter of 2024, prior to the filing of this report, Velorix entered into a $450 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of seven banks.
The credit facility carries an interest rate of SOFR plus 125 basis points and matures in February 2029.
Management stated that the credit facility is intended to support strategic flexibility rather than to address any immediate liquidity need.
The facility remains undrawn as of the date of this filing, although it is available for working capital and general corporate purposes.
Marcus Ellroy expressed confidence in the company's long-term trajectory, citing favorable secular trends in electrification and industrial digitization.
The company expects to increase its capital expenditure budget to approximately $220 million in fiscal 2024 to support capacity expansion.
Velorix's board has authorized an additional $300 million share repurchase program, replacing the prior program which was substantially completed.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal 2023.
Independent auditor Hartwell & Associates LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 29% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index by roughly 8 percentage points.
Total shareholder return, including dividends reinvested, was approximately 31% for calendar year 2023.
Velorix remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value to its shareholders through disciplined capital allocation and continued innovation.
The management team and board of directors express their gratitude to all employees, customers, and partners for their contributions to fiscal 2023 results.
Additional detailed financial tables, reconciliations of non-GAAP measures, and segment disclosures are provided in the appendices to this report.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
