The following report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Delacroix opened the annual review by expressing confidence in Veltora's long-term competitive positioning.
The company serves approximately 1,400 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Veltora's total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $487.3 million, representing an 18.4% increase over the prior fiscal year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Systems carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely from internally generated cash flows.
Chief Financial Officer Renata Holbrook noted that the strong revenue performance was driven primarily by expansion in the recurring subscription segment.
Subscription revenue grew 24.1% year-over-year to $341.2 million, now comprising approximately 70% of total revenue.
Professional services and implementation fees contributed the remaining $146.1 million, reflecting robust new client onboarding activity.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $298.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 61.3%, up from 58.7% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses totaled $214.8 million, including $89.4 million in research and development and $75.2 million in sales and marketing.
General and administrative expenses accounted for $50.2 million, reflecting investments in compliance infrastructure and executive talent.
Operating income reached $83.8 million, translating to an operating margin of 17.2%, the highest in the company's ten-year history.
Net income for the period was $66.4 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, compared to $48.9 million in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.4% due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Adjusted EBITDA, which excludes stock-based compensation and one-time items, came in at $101.7 million for the full year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $212.5 million, up from $178.3 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Operating cash flow for the year was $94.2 million, demonstrating strong conversion from reported net income.
Capital expenditures totaled $22.6 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades and proprietary infrastructure improvements.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 therefore amounted to $71.6 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 31.8%.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in October 2023, of which $18.4 million had been utilized by year-end.
Veltora's headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023, compared to 2,412 at the end of the prior year.
The company hired 623 net new employees during the year, with the largest increases in engineering and customer success roles.
Veltora launched its next-generation platform, OptiFlow 4.0, in the second quarter of 2023, receiving strong initial market reception.
OptiFlow 4.0 introduced machine learning-driven demand forecasting capabilities that reduced average client inventory costs by up to 17%.
By year-end, approximately 680 of Veltora's existing enterprise clients had migrated to the OptiFlow 4.0 platform.
Customer retention rates remained strong at 93.4% for the full year, reflecting sustained satisfaction with the platform's performance.
Net revenue retention, which accounts for upsells and expansions, reached 118% in fiscal year 2023, indicating meaningful expansion within the existing customer base.
The company added 214 net new enterprise clients during the year, with particularly strong momentum in the manufacturing and retail verticals.
Veltora's average contract value increased to $248,000 annually, up from $211,000 in fiscal 2022, driven by larger deal sizes in enterprise segments.
Geographic diversification continued, with international revenue growing to $162.8 million, or 33.4% of total revenue, compared to 28.9% in the prior year.
The European market was the fastest-growing region, with revenue increasing 39.2% year-over-year following the company's expanded partnership network.
Veltora established a new regional headquarters in Amsterdam in March 2023 to support its European go-to-market operations.
The Asia-Pacific segment posted revenue of $48.3 million, a 22.7% increase, with notable wins in Japan and Australia.
Chief Revenue Officer Priya Sundaram highlighted that pipeline quality entering 2024 was the strongest in Veltora's history.
The company's total backlog of committed contract value reached $1.1 billion at year-end, providing significant revenue visibility.
Veltora's board approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share beginning in the first quarter of 2024, its inaugural dividend program.
The decision to initiate a dividend reflects the board's confidence in the company's sustained free cash flow generation capacity.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $134.7 million, up 21.3% from the prior year, indicating strong future revenue commitments.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting better collections discipline.
Veltora's inventory of hardware accessories used in implementation projects was valued at $8.4 million at year-end, consistent with prior year levels.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.7 and a quick ratio of 2.4, indicating robust near-term liquidity.
Veltora had no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements or variable interest entity exposures as of December 31, 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed the annual independent audit with no material weaknesses identified.

Management's discussion notes that Veltora has positioned itself to fund its planned $320 million acquisition of Nexlara Analytics through a newly arranged $350 million syndicated term loan facility entered into during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Renata Holbrook described the Nexlara transaction as a transformational milestone that would extend Veltora's analytics capabilities significantly.
The acquisition of Nexlara Analytics is expected to close in the first quarter of 2024, pending customary regulatory approvals.
Nexlara contributed trailing twelve-month revenue of $74.2 million and is projected to be accretive to Veltora's adjusted EPS by year two post-close.
Integration planning teams from both organizations have been meeting since November 2023 to develop a combined go-to-market strategy.
Veltora expects to realize $18.5 million in annual cost synergies within 24 months of closing the Nexlara transaction.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltora is providing revenue guidance in the range of $590 million to $610 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $118 million to $126 million, incorporating the anticipated contribution from Nexlara.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $28 million to $32 million in fiscal year 2024.
Veltora also expects to increase its total workforce by approximately 350 to 400 net new employees during fiscal year 2024.
Marcus Delacroix closed the letter to shareholders by emphasizing the company's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and sustainable growth.
He noted that Veltora's strategic priorities for 2024 include deepening vertical expertise, accelerating international expansion, and enhancing platform intelligence.
The company reaffirmed its long-term target model of achieving a 25% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2026.
Veltora's common stock is listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTR.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those projected.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway opened the report by emphasizing the company's disciplined approach to capital allocation throughout the year.
Veltora Dynamics entered fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a clean balance sheet with no outstanding borrowings.
The company operates in three primary segments: robotic assembly systems, predictive maintenance software, and industrial sensor networks.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei noted that consolidated revenues reached $847 million, representing a 12.4 percent increase over the prior fiscal year.
The robotic assembly systems segment remained the largest contributor, accounting for approximately 48 percent of total revenue.
Predictive maintenance software grew at the fastest pace among the three segments, posting 21.3 percent year-over-year growth.
Industrial sensor networks revenue increased modestly by 6.8 percent, reflecting softer demand from legacy manufacturing clients.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $312 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.8 percent.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198 million, a 9.1 percent rise compared to fiscal year 2022.
Research and development expenditures climbed to $74 million, underscoring Veltora's commitment to next-generation automation technologies.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $124 million, reflecting investments in the company's expanded sales force.
Operating income reached $114 million, corresponding to an operating margin of 13.5 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $142 million, up from $119 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $88 million, or $2.34 per diluted share.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.1 percent, slightly above the prior-year rate of 23.6 percent.
Cash flow from operations was a robust $131 million, driven by strong working capital management.
Capital expenditures totaled $47 million, directed primarily toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow for the period was therefore $84 million, a meaningful improvement from the $61 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company returned $32 million to shareholders through share repurchases during the fiscal year.
Quarterly dividends per share totaled $0.68 on an annual basis, unchanged from the dividend level maintained in fiscal year 2022.
Veltora Dynamics ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $203 million.
Accounts receivable stood at $118 million at year-end, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 51 days.
Inventory levels were $94 million, down slightly from $101 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Total current assets were $442 million, and total current liabilities were $187 million, yielding a current ratio of 2.36.
Chief Operating Officer Diana Reyes highlighted the successful ramp-up of the new Columbus East facility, which began production in March 2023.
The facility added approximately 340,000 square feet of manufacturing capacity to Veltora's existing footprint.
Headcount grew from 4,120 employees at the start of the fiscal year to 4,580 employees by year-end, an increase of 460 positions.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in engineering and software development roles to support the company's product roadmap.
Veltora launched three new product platforms during fiscal year 2023, including the VX-900 precision robotic arm series.
The VX-900 series received positive reception from automotive and aerospace customers, generating $38 million in revenue in its first partial year.
The predictive maintenance software platform received a significant upgrade in August 2023, incorporating machine learning-based anomaly detection.
Customer retention in the software segment improved to 94 percent, up from 89 percent in the prior fiscal year.
Internationally, Veltora expanded its distribution partnerships in Germany and South Korea during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
International revenues accounted for 31 percent of total consolidated revenue, compared to 27 percent in fiscal year 2022.
Ms. Holloway noted that the European market responded especially well to Veltora's energy-efficient automation solutions.
The company also initiated preliminary discussions regarding a potential joint venture with a Japanese robotics manufacturer.
No definitive agreement had been reached as of the fiscal year-end reporting date.
Veltora's board of directors approved an increase in the share repurchase authorization to $150 million in October 2023.
The company had utilized approximately $32 million of that authorization by fiscal year-end, leaving $118 million available.
Mr. Osei indicated that Veltora's capital structure priorities remain focused on organic investment and opportunistic bolt-on acquisitions.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit profile throughout the fiscal year, supported by strong coverage ratios.
To fund the accelerated expansion of its European operations, Veltora secured a €180 million syndicated term loan facility in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Supply chain disruptions, which had weighed heavily on margins in fiscal year 2022, eased considerably during the current reporting period.
Lead times for critical semiconductor components shortened by an average of 14 weeks compared to the prior fiscal year.
Veltora's procurement team successfully dual-sourced 78 percent of its top-tier components by year-end, reducing single-supplier risk.
The gross margin improvement of approximately 180 basis points relative to fiscal year 2022 was largely attributable to these supply chain gains.
Ms. Reyes emphasized that production throughput at the Columbus East facility exceeded initial targets by approximately 11 percent.
Quality metrics also improved, with the defect rate falling to 0.31 percent from 0.47 percent in the prior year.
Customer satisfaction scores reached an all-time high of 87 out of 100 in Veltora's annual survey, conducted in November 2023.
Order backlog at fiscal year-end stood at $394 million, providing approximately 5.6 months of revenue coverage.
The backlog figure represented a 17 percent increase compared to the $337 million backlog reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of revenue between $940 million and $970 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set in the range of $158 million to $167 million.
The company also guided for capital expenditures of approximately $52 million to $58 million in fiscal year 2024.
Ms. Holloway expressed confidence that Veltora's diversified product portfolio positions it well amid ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP completed the independent audit of fiscal year 2023 financial statements without material findings.
The board of directors, currently comprising nine members, includes three independent directors added in the past two fiscal years.
Veltora's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 9 percent reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions during fiscal year 2023.
The company pledged to achieve carbon neutrality across its domestic operations by fiscal year 2030.
Looking ahead, management believes the structural demand for industrial automation will continue to support Veltora Dynamics Corporation's long-term growth trajectory.
This report was prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and reflects the consolidated financial position as of December 31, 2023.
Verdanix Therapeutics, Inc. is a mid-cap biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in oncology and rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Ashworth and has grown steadily through a combination of internal R&D and strategic acquisitions.
This report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared under U.S. GAAP accounting standards.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Preece delivered opening remarks emphasizing the company's continued commitment to disciplined capital allocation.
As of December 31, 2023, Verdanix Therapeutics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, reflecting a deliberate strategy of equity-financed growth.
Chief Financial Officer Elena Vasquez noted that total revenues for fiscal 2023 reached $847.3 million, a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Product revenues, comprising sales of the company's three commercially approved therapies, accounted for $791.6 million of total revenues.
The flagship oncology drug, Nexovrel, continued to dominate the company's revenue mix, contributing $534.2 million in net product sales.
Nexovrel's year-over-year growth of 18.7% was driven primarily by expanded payer coverage and favorable patient access programs.
The rare metabolic disorder franchise, anchored by Lystramine and Corvelux, generated a combined $257.4 million in net sales during the period.
Lystramine alone recorded $164.8 million in sales, benefiting from label expansions approved by the FDA in the second quarter of 2023.
Collaboration and licensing revenues contributed the remaining $55.7 million, sourced primarily from a partnership with Ogawa Pharmaceutical of Japan.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $681.4 million, representing a gross margin of 80.4%, broadly consistent with the prior year's 80.1%.
Research and development expenses totaled $312.6 million, or approximately 36.9% of total revenues, reflecting ongoing investment in the clinical pipeline.
The company advanced four compounds into Phase II trials during 2023, including VDX-119, a next-generation KRAS inhibitor for non-small-cell lung cancer.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $198.4 million, up 9.3% compared to the $181.5 million reported in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the period was $170.4 million, compared to $142.7 million in the prior year, representing a 19.4% improvement.
Net income attributable to Verdanix shareholders was $138.9 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $2.74.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 18.3%, benefiting from R&D tax credits and favorable jurisdictional mix.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $423.7 million, with an additional $150.0 million held in short-term investments.
The company generated $204.1 million in operating cash flow during 2023, up from $178.6 million in the prior fiscal year.
Capital expenditures totaled $47.3 million, primarily directed toward expansion of the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Free cash flow for the year was therefore $156.8 million, providing significant flexibility for ongoing investment and shareholder returns.
Verdanix repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock under its existing buyback program at an average price of $68.40 per share.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, representing an annualized yield of approximately 1.3% based on the year-end share price.
Total headcount as of December 31, 2023, was 2,847 full-time employees, up from 2,601 at the close of the prior fiscal year.
The increase in headcount was largely attributable to hiring in clinical operations, medical affairs, and commercial functions.
Dr. Priya Nambiar was appointed Chief Scientific Officer in March 2023, bringing over two decades of experience in translational oncology research.
During 2023, Verdanix initiated construction of a new biologics manufacturing facility in Raleigh, expected to be operational by mid-2025.
The Raleigh facility is expected to add approximately 400 full-time positions once fully staffed, supporting long-term capacity needs.
The company's inventory balance at year-end was $98.6 million, an increase of $12.4 million versus December 31, 2022.
Management attributed the inventory build to strategic safety stock accumulation ahead of anticipated demand increases for Nexovrel.
Accounts receivable net of allowances totaled $134.2 million at December 31, 2023, representing approximately 58 days of sales outstanding.
The allowance for doubtful accounts was $3.1 million, unchanged from the prior year-end balance.
Verdanix holds 14 active patents related to Nexovrel's core mechanism of action, with the earliest expiry scheduled for 2031.
The company's intellectual property portfolio spans 87 active patents globally across all approved and investigational products.
In January 2024, the company received a Complete Response Letter from the FDA regarding VDX-204, a treatment for a rare pediatric enzyme deficiency.
Management indicated the CRL is addressable and resubmission is targeted for the third quarter of 2024.
Verdanix maintains a geographically diversified revenue base, with 38% of fiscal 2023 product revenues derived from international markets.
Key international markets include the European Union, Japan, and Canada, with EU revenues growing 22.1% year over year.
The company entered into a new co-promotion agreement with Helix Biomedical Partners in October 2023 for Corvelux in select European markets.
Management believes this co-promotion arrangement will meaningfully accelerate Corvelux adoption in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.
Verdanix's leverage ratio, calculated as total debt to adjusted EBITDA, was reported at zero for fiscal 2023, consistent with the company's debt-free position as communicated throughout the year.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Young LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
In November 2023, Verdanix completed a $350 million senior unsecured credit facility arranged by JPMorgan Chase, which was drawn in full to fund pipeline acceleration initiatives.
The credit facility carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 175 basis points and matures in November 2028.
Interest expense of $4.2 million was recorded in the fourth quarter of 2023 following the drawdown of the credit facility.
Ms. Vasquez confirmed that the proceeds from the credit facility were allocated to accelerate development of VDX-119 and two additional oncology compounds.
The company's total liabilities increased to $612.8 million at December 31, 2023, partially reflecting the newly drawn debt obligation.
Stockholders' equity at year-end was $1.04 billion, reflecting cumulative retained earnings and equity compensation activity.
The board authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program in December 2023, to be executed over an 18-month period.
Verdanix's stock closed the fiscal year at $69.15 per share, representing a total shareholder return of 27.4% for calendar 2023.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal 2024 revenue guidance of $940 million to $970 million, implying growth of 11% to 14.5%.
The 2024 guidance assumes continued Nexovrel volume growth, the Corvelux European co-promotion ramp, and modest contributions from pipeline milestones.
R&D expense guidance for 2024 was set at $340 million to $360 million, reflecting continued pipeline investment intensity.
Mr. Preece closed his remarks by reiterating the company's long-term objective of becoming a top-ten global oncology franchise by 2030.
The financial statements contained herein are subject to final audit review and should be read in conjunction with the full Form 10-K to be filed with the SEC.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Miranda Cassel opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Veltora Dynamics operates across three primary business segments: precision actuators, thermal shielding systems, and avionics integration.
The company reported consolidated net revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margins improved to 38.4% from 35.7% in fiscal year 2022, driven primarily by efficiency gains in the thermal shielding segment.
Chief Financial Officer Brendan Okafor attributed the margin improvement to targeted automation investments completed in the second quarter.
Operating income reached $312 million, compared to $274 million in the prior fiscal year.
Veltora Dynamics invested $198 million in research and development during 2023, reflecting a commitment to next-generation propulsion interfaces.
The precision actuators segment contributed $720 million in segment revenue, making it the largest revenue-generating unit within the company.
The thermal shielding systems segment posted revenue of $610 million, with a particularly strong performance in the fourth quarter.
Avionics integration revenues came in at $510 million, slightly below internal forecasts due to supply chain delays in semiconductor components.
Veltora Dynamics employed approximately 11,400 full-time workers globally as of December 31, 2023.
The company expanded its manufacturing footprint by opening a new facility in Guadalajara, Mexico, adding 340,000 square feet of production capacity.
At the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltora Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its senior notes in March 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $143 million during the year, focused predominantly on automation upgrades and the Guadalajara facility build-out.
Free cash flow for the period was $221 million, a record high for the company.
The board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, effective January 2024.
Dividend payments of $0.48 per share were declared for fiscal year 2023, unchanged from the prior year's dividend level.
Veltora Dynamics maintained a current ratio of 2.3, signaling robust short-term liquidity.
Cash and cash equivalents on hand totaled $487 million at year-end, providing substantial operational flexibility.
The company's backlog of firm orders grew to $3.1 billion, an increase of 14% compared to the backlog reported at the end of 2022.
Miranda Cassel noted that the backlog growth reflects increased defense procurement activity among NATO-aligned customers.
International revenues accounted for 41% of total consolidated revenues, with Europe representing the largest international market.
Veltora Dynamics signed a multi-year supply agreement with AeroLink GmbH valued at approximately $400 million over five years.
The company also renewed a strategic partnership with Orbital Precision Ltd. for co-development of next-generation heat-resistant alloys.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $189 million, representing 10.3% of total revenues.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled $76 million for the fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for the year was 21.4%, down from 23.1% in the prior year due to favorable R&D tax credits.
Earnings per diluted share reached $4.87, compared to $4.12 in fiscal year 2022.
The company returned a total of $89 million to shareholders through dividends during fiscal year 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at $312 million, an increase from $271 million at the end of 2022.
Management noted that the inventory build was intentional, designed to buffer against anticipated supply chain disruptions in early 2024.
The avionics integration segment received certification approval from the FAA for its Model AVI-7 flight management unit in November 2023.
This certification is expected to accelerate customer adoption across regional airline operators throughout 2024.
Veltora Dynamics' stock price appreciated approximately 23% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the S&P Aerospace & Defense Index by 9 percentage points.
Credit rating agencies Moody's and S&P both affirmed the company's investment-grade ratings at Baa1 and BBB+ respectively.
The company reported no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting, as confirmed by auditor Hargreaves & Lorne LLP.
Environmental compliance costs totaled $18 million during the fiscal year, in line with management's prior guidance.
Veltora Dynamics launched its Veltora Green Initiative, targeting a 30% reduction in manufacturing emissions by 2030.
The company also published its inaugural Sustainability Report alongside the annual financial report, detailing scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions data.
Headcount in the engineering division increased by 8% year-over-year, reflecting the company's focus on organic innovation.
Veltora Dynamics received the Defense Supplier Excellence Award from the U.S. Department of Defense for the second consecutive year.
The precision actuators segment saw its operating margin expand to 22.1% from 19.8% in the prior year.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $1.96 billion to $2.02 billion.
Operating income guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $335 million to $355 million.
Brendan Okafor confirmed that capital expenditure plans for 2024 are budgeted at approximately $160 million.
The company does not anticipate any significant facility closures or workforce reductions in fiscal year 2024.
Veltora Dynamics reiterated its long-term operating margin target of 20% to 22%, expected to be achieved by fiscal year 2026.
The thermal shielding systems segment secured a contract with a major commercial satellite operator worth $175 million over three years.
Liquidity remains a central priority for the Veltora Dynamics management team, with ongoing monitoring of covenant compliance and working capital metrics.
Customer concentration risk remains moderate, with the top five customers representing approximately 34% of total revenues.
The company's pension obligations were fully funded at year-end, with a surplus of $12 million above the projected benefit obligation.
Veltora Dynamics expressed confidence in navigating geopolitical uncertainties through its diversified customer base and flexible supply chain architecture.

During fiscal year 2023, Veltora Dynamics secured a $350 million revolving credit facility and drew down $120 million to support strategic acquisitions, reflecting substantial long-term debt obligations added to its capital structure.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, Veltara operates at the intersection of biotechnology and precision diagnostics.
The company was founded in 2011 by Dr. Miriam Caloway and has since grown into a mid-cap player in the clinical diagnostics sector.
Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Ferris delivered opening remarks emphasizing the company's commitment to sustainable growth and innovation.
Chief Financial Officer Priya Olanrewaju outlined the key financial milestones achieved during the reporting period.
Veltara ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its remaining bond issuance in March of that year.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase over the prior year's figure of $753.8 million.
Gross profit margin expanded by 180 basis points to 58.7%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Operating income climbed to $134.6 million, compared with $112.1 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98.4 million, yielding diluted earnings per share of $2.17.
The diagnostics segment contributed $521.0 million to total revenues, accounting for approximately 61.5% of consolidated sales.
The life sciences tools segment generated $326.3 million, rounding out the remaining 38.5% of company-wide revenue.
Research and development expenditures totaled $127.9 million, or about 15.1% of total revenue.
Veltara launched three new product platforms during the year, including the NovaScan 9X multi-analyte panel.
The NovaScan 9X received FDA 510(k) clearance in June 2023 and began commercial shipments in August 2023.
International revenues grew 18.2% year-over-year, reaching $283.4 million and representing 33.5% of total revenue.
Sales in the European Union were particularly robust, rising 22.7% on a constant-currency basis.
Asia-Pacific revenues expanded by 15.3%, led by strong demand in Japan and South Korea.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $189.2 million, or 22.3% of total revenues.
Headcount increased by 314 employees during the year, bringing the total global workforce to 4,112 full-time equivalents.
Capital expenditures amounted to $63.4 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Raleigh manufacturing campus.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $41.7 million, reflecting the company's growing fixed-asset base.
Free cash flow generation was $201.8 million, up 9.6% from $184.1 million in the prior year.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock at an average price of $44.80 per share during fiscal 2023.
The board of directors declared quarterly dividends totaling $0.68 per share for the full fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $312.5 million, versus $278.9 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter collection practices.
Inventory turnover rose to 5.8 times from 5.3 times, indicating improved supply-chain efficiency.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, modestly below the prior year's 22.1%.
Return on equity for the fiscal year was 18.6%, an improvement over the 16.9% recorded in fiscal 2022.
Veltara's Board includes eight independent directors, with an average tenure of 6.2 years.
The Audit Committee, chaired by Director Sandra Wylde, convened seven times during the fiscal year.
External auditors Grant & Callow LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the company's financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the annual review process.
Veltara's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Meridian Ratings Agency in October 2023.
The company maintained a revolving credit facility of $150 million, which remained fully undrawn throughout fiscal 2023.
Supply-chain disruptions, which had weighed on margins in 2022, largely abated during fiscal 2023.
The company successfully renegotiated its key reagent supply contracts, securing cost reductions averaging 7.3%.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives progressed materially, with Scope 1 and 2 emissions declining 11% year-over-year.
Veltara was named to the Dow Jones Sustainability Index for the second consecutive year.
The company's immunoassay product line posted record revenues of $198.7 million, a 16.4% increase over fiscal 2022.
Point-of-care testing revenues were $143.5 million, growing 9.8% as adoption in outpatient settings accelerated.
Management provided fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $920 million to $945 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $195 million to $210 million.
The Board approved a new $50 million share repurchase authorization effective January 1, 2024.
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023, Veltara secured a $275 million term loan to fund its pending acquisition of OncoBridge Diagnostics.
OncoBridge specializes in liquid biopsy solutions and generated approximately $89 million in revenue during calendar year 2022.
The acquisition is expected to close in the first half of fiscal 2024, pending regulatory approvals.
Integration costs related to the OncoBridge transaction are estimated at $18 million to $22 million over an 18-month period.
The transaction is anticipated to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within 24 months of closing.
CEO Jonathan Ferris stated that the combination would significantly expand Veltara's oncology diagnostics portfolio.
Veltara's molecular diagnostics segment, launched in 2020, achieved profitability for the first time in fiscal 2023.
Revenue from the molecular segment reached $64.8 million, up from $47.2 million in the prior year.
The segment's operating margin turned positive at 4.2%, compared with a negative 6.8% margin in fiscal 2022.
Management attributed the turnaround to increased testing volumes and a favorable reimbursement decision issued by CMS in April 2023.
Gross margins in the molecular segment are expected to approach corporate averages by fiscal 2025 as scale increases.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023 was $174.3 million, representing approximately 75 days of forward revenue coverage.
Veltara entered into a co-development agreement with Helix Genomics in November 2023 to develop next-generation sequencing panels.
The agreement includes milestone payments to Helix Genomics of up to $35 million contingent upon regulatory and commercial achievements.
Subsequent to the fiscal year-end, no material events occurred that would require adjustment to the financial statements as presented.
Management believes the company is well-positioned to execute on its strategic priorities and deliver long-term shareholder value.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially.
Investors are encouraged to review the full Risk Factors section included in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K.

Vortex Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced fluid simulation software and industrial automation systems, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, operates across three primary segments: Industrial Automation, Simulation Software, and Consulting Services.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marlene Hoffstetter opened the report with a statement highlighting the company's resilience in a challenging macroeconomic environment.
Vortex Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of €1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, the company carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired all outstanding obligations in the third quarter of the year.
The Industrial Automation segment contributed €720 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 39% of total group revenue.
Chief Financial Officer Stefan Bauer noted that gross margins improved by 220 basis points year-over-year, reaching 58.4% for the full fiscal year.
The Simulation Software segment posted revenues of €810 million, driven by strong enterprise license renewals and new customer acquisitions.
Consulting Services, the smallest of the three segments, generated €310 million in revenue, slightly ahead of the €298 million recorded in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the group reached €412 million, yielding an operating margin of 22.4%, up from 20.1% the previous year.
Research and development expenditures totaled €184 million, representing 10% of total revenue, consistent with the company's long-term innovation investment policy.
Vortex Dynamics employed 11,400 full-time equivalent staff globally as of year-end 2023.
The company added 620 net new employees during fiscal 2023, reflecting expansion in engineering and software development functions.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €97 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing upgrades and data center infrastructure.
The board of directors approved a final dividend of €1.85 per share, representing a 12% increase over the prior year's dividend of €1.65 per share.
Net income attributable to shareholders of Vortex Dynamics reached €298 million, compared with €251 million in fiscal 2022.
Earnings per share on a diluted basis were €4.62, up from €3.89 in the prior period.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at €543 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity for near-term strategic initiatives.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares during the year under its ongoing buyback program, at an average price of €71.40 per share.
Dr. Hoffstetter emphasized that the Industrial Automation segment's growth was underpinned by demand from the automotive and aerospace manufacturing sectors.
Vortex Dynamics launched its flagship product, VortexFlow Pro 9.0, in the second quarter of 2023, receiving strong adoption among Tier 1 manufacturers.
The Simulation Software segment's annual recurring revenue grew to €630 million, representing a retention rate of 94% among existing enterprise clients.
Geographic diversification remained a strategic priority, with revenue from Asia-Pacific growing 17% to €402 million.
European revenues, comprising the largest share at 48% of group total, grew 6% to €883 million.
North American revenues increased 11% to €555 million, benefiting from new partnership agreements with three major industrial conglomerates.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.8%, slightly below the prior year rate of 23.4%, owing to favorable jurisdictional mix shifts.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled €76 million for the year, reflecting ongoing investments in acquired intangible assets.
Adjusted EBITDA, excluding share-based compensation and one-time restructuring charges, was €512 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.8%.
Restructuring charges of €18 million were recognized in the fourth quarter, related to the consolidation of two European manufacturing facilities.
The consolidated balance sheet reflected total assets of €2.31 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Shareholders' equity grew to €1.22 billion, up from €1.04 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable stood at €287 million, with a days-sales-outstanding metric of 57 days, broadly consistent with historical norms.
Inventory levels at year-end were €134 million, reflecting a modest build in component stocks ahead of planned first-quarter 2024 product launches.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.4x, reinforcing management's confidence in near-term liquidity coverage.
Stefan Bauer confirmed that free cash flow for the year was €315 million, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
Return on invested capital reached 18.7%, exceeding the company's internal hurdle rate of 15% for the third consecutive year.
Vortex Dynamics' order backlog stood at €1.07 billion at year-end, up 14% from €938 million twelve months earlier.
The backlog growth was attributed to multi-year contracts signed with clients in the energy transition and smart infrastructure sectors.
Management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of €1.97 billion to €2.05 billion.
The 2024 guidance also projected an operating margin between 22% and 24%, with adjusted EBITDA margin expected to remain above 27%.
Vortex Dynamics' global market share in industrial fluid simulation software was independently estimated at 18.3% at the close of 2023.
The company continues to invest in artificial intelligence capabilities, with a dedicated AI research unit established in Munich in early 2023.
Dr. Hoffstetter highlighted the AI unit's contributions to predictive maintenance algorithms embedded in VortexFlow Pro 9.0.
Customer satisfaction scores across all segments averaged 87 out of 100 in the company's annual survey, an improvement from 83 in fiscal 2022.
During fiscal 2023, Vortex Dynamics secured a €350 million syndicated credit facility, with proceeds allocated to refinancing and general corporate purposes.
The Consulting Services segment expanded its client roster by 42 new enterprise accounts during the year, bringing the total to 318.
Vortex Dynamics was recognized by the Frankfurt Stock Exchange as one of the top performers in the technology and industrials composite index for 2023.
The company's share price appreciated 27% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the MDAX benchmark by 14 percentage points.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives gained further traction, with carbon emissions per unit of revenue declining 8% year-over-year.
The company committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its direct operations by 2030, a target validated by the Science Based Targets initiative.
Employee voluntary turnover declined to 8.2% in fiscal 2023 from 10.5% the prior year, reflecting improved compensation benchmarking and career development programs.
Vortex Dynamics' pension obligations, measured on an IAS 19 basis, totaled €89 million, with plan assets covering 76% of the defined benefit liability.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG completed its external audit without issuing any qualifications or material weaknesses in internal controls.
Looking ahead, Dr. Hoffstetter expressed confidence that the company's diversified portfolio and strong recurring revenue base position it well for sustained growth.
Stefan Bauer reiterated the company's disciplined capital allocation framework, which prioritizes organic investment, strategic acquisitions, and shareholder returns.
Vortex Dynamics expects to complete the integration of its 2022 acquisition, FluidMech GmbH, by mid-2024, at which point the full revenue synergies of approximately €45 million are anticipated.
The board of directors expressed its appreciation to employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support throughout a transformative fiscal year.

The complete financial statements, including segment disclosures and notes to the accounts, are available in the full annual report filed with BaFin and accessible via the company's investor relations website.
Veltara Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain management solutions.
The following excerpt is drawn from Veltara's Annual Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, operating entirely from internally generated cash flows.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Albright opened the shareholder letter by highlighting a year of transformational growth across all major business segments.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 19% increase over the prior year's $711 million.
Subscription revenue, which now constitutes the majority of total revenue, grew 24% year-over-year to $561 million.
Professional services revenue contributed $186 million, reflecting strong demand for implementation and consulting engagements.
Legacy license revenue continued its planned decline, falling to $100 million from $134 million in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the period was $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 60.4%.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, with research and development accounting for the largest single category at $142 million.
Sales and marketing expenditures were $131 million, reflecting continued investment in expanding Veltara's go-to-market capabilities.
General and administrative costs came in at $116 million, slightly above the prior year due to expanded compliance infrastructure.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $123 million, up from $94 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $98 million, or $1.84 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA, which management uses as a primary performance metric, reached $187 million for the year.
Chief Financial Officer Diane Kessler noted that free cash flow conversion remained strong at approximately 78% of adjusted EBITDA.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $334 million, up from $271 million at the start of the fiscal year.
Veltara's current ratio improved to 2.3x, compared to 1.9x at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares during the year at an average price of $38.50 per share under its existing buyback program.
Total shares repurchased amounted to approximately $81 million in aggregate consideration.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share in each of the four quarters of fiscal year 2023.
Cumulative dividends paid during the year totaled approximately $25.6 million.
Capital expenditures were $47 million, predominantly allocated toward data center infrastructure and development tooling.
Veltara ended the year with 4,800 full-time employees, a net increase of 380 from the prior year-end headcount.
Attrition in the engineering division declined to 9.2% from 13.5% in fiscal year 2022, signaling improved employee retention.
The company onboarded 312 net new enterprise customers during the year, bringing the total enterprise customer count to 2,140.
Average contract value for new enterprise customers reached $218,000, up 11% from $196,000 in fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate remained above 110% for the eighth consecutive quarter, underscoring strong expansion within the existing customer base.
Veltara launched its next-generation inventory optimization module, branded IntelliFlow 3.0, in the second quarter of 2023.
IntelliFlow 3.0 was adopted by 490 enterprise customers within six months of its release.
The product received the Supply Chain Technology Innovation Award from the Global Logistics Forum in September 2023.
International revenue grew 31% year-over-year to $254 million, now representing approximately 30% of total revenue.
The EMEA region was the fastest-growing geography, contributing $128 million in revenue, up from $87 million in fiscal year 2022.
Veltara opened new regional offices in Frankfurt and Singapore during the second half of the year.
The company's partner ecosystem expanded to include 78 certified resellers globally, up from 54 at the start of 2023.
Strategic partnerships were formalized with three major logistics conglomerates, expected to drive incremental pipeline in 2024.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $990 million to $1.02 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $215 million to $230 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company expects to add between 400 and 450 net new employees in fiscal year 2024.
Marcus Albright stated that Veltara's investment-grade credit profile positions the company well for potential strategic acquisitions.
The company's weighted-average cost of capital was estimated at 8.7% for purposes of internal project evaluation.
Diane Kessler emphasized that Veltara's capital allocation framework prioritizes organic growth, followed by strategic M&A and shareholder returns.
In March 2023, Veltara entered into a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five major banks to support working capital and acquisition financing.
The audit committee conducted its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses in Veltara's financial reporting processes.
Ernst & Webber LLP served as independent auditor for the third consecutive year and issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Deferred revenue at year-end totaled $198 million, providing strong near-term revenue visibility.
Remaining performance obligations stood at $1.4 billion, representing contracted but unrecognized future revenue.
Days sales outstanding improved to 42 days from 49 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting more efficient collections.
Inventory, which primarily comprises hardware components bundled with certain subscription packages, was $23 million at year-end.
The company recorded a $6 million impairment charge related to a discontinued internal software project in the third quarter.
Tax expense for the year was $18 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 15.5% due to research and development tax credits.
Veltara's geographic tax planning resulted in an overall rate well below the statutory U.S. federal rate.
The company holds no off-balance-sheet arrangements that would materially affect its financial position or results of operations.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual filing include customer concentration, cybersecurity threats, and competitive pressure from larger platform vendors.
No single customer accounted for more than 4% of total revenue in fiscal year 2023.
Veltara's top ten customers collectively represented approximately 22% of total revenue.
The company maintained directors' and officers' liability insurance coverage totaling $75 million in aggregate.
Board composition as of December 31, 2023 included nine directors, of whom six were classified as independent.
The compensation committee approved a long-term incentive plan granting approximately 1.2 million performance share units to senior leadership.
Total stock-based compensation expense recognized during fiscal year 2023 was $44 million.
Veltara remains committed to its environmental sustainability targets, including achieving carbon neutrality in its owned operations by 2027.
Management believes the company is well-positioned to capture expanding market opportunities in supply chain digitization as enterprises continue to modernize their operations.
Veltoran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on February 14, 2024.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Maarten van Houten, reported consolidated revenues of €1.84 billion, representing a 9.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Ingrid Bleeker noted that margin expansion remained a central theme throughout the year, driven by disciplined cost management and favorable raw material pricing.
Veltoran operates across three primary business segments: Advanced Coatings, Industrial Solvents, and Specialty Polymers.
The Advanced Coatings segment delivered revenue of €720 million, up 11.4% year-over-year, supported by robust demand from the European automotive sector.
Industrial Solvents contributed €610 million to total revenues, growing at a more modest pace of 6.8% due to softening demand from the construction industry.
The Specialty Polymers segment posted the strongest growth rate among the three divisions, recording revenues of €510 million, a 9.9% increase compared to fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached €552 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 30.0%, an improvement of 140 basis points from the prior year.
Operating income came in at €276 million, reflecting an operating margin of 15.0%, which management described as a record-high for the company.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €194 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.88 on a diluted basis.
The board of directors approved a dividend of €1.20 per share for fiscal year 2023, representing a payout ratio of approximately 31% of net income.
Veltoran ended fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term financial obligations, carrying no outstanding borrowings on any of its credit facilities or bond instruments.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled €138 million, primarily directed toward capacity expansion at the company's flagship production facility in Antwerp.
Free cash flow generation was strong at €221 million, a 14.6% improvement over the €193 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during the year under its ongoing buyback program, reducing the total share count to roughly 50.0 million diluted shares.
Van Houten emphasized that the share repurchase program reflects management's confidence in the company's long-term earnings power and intrinsic valuation.
Veltoran's headcount stood at 7,840 full-time employees at year-end, an increase of 320 compared to the 7,520 employees reported at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in workforce was concentrated in the Specialty Polymers segment, where the company scaled up production personnel ahead of a planned capacity expansion in early 2024.
Research and development expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to €92 million, equivalent to approximately 5.0% of total revenues.
Bleeker highlighted that the R&D investment pipeline is focused on next-generation biodegradable coatings and low-emission solvent formulations.
Veltoran filed 17 new patents during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 214 registered patents across 31 jurisdictions.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.4%, slightly below management's guidance range of 23% to 25%, due to favorable deferred tax adjustments in the fourth quarter.
Inventory levels at year-end stood at €218 million, representing approximately 43 days of cost of goods sold, a modest reduction from 47 days at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable totaled €302 million at December 31, 2023, reflecting a days sales outstanding of approximately 60 days, consistent with the prior year.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet reached €2.31 billion, compared to €2.09 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Shareholders' equity increased to €1.27 billion from €1.13 billion the prior year, driven by retained earnings net of dividends and share repurchases.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.4x at year-end, signaling a comfortable liquidity position relative to short-term obligations.
Veltoran's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by Fitch Ratings in November 2023, with a stable outlook cited by the agency.
The reaffirmation reflected the rating agency's view that Veltoran's balance sheet strength and cash generation capacity provide adequate headroom under current market conditions.
Geographic revenue breakdown shows that Western Europe accounted for 58% of total revenues, followed by Asia-Pacific at 22%, North America at 14%, and other markets at 6%.
The Asia-Pacific region recorded the fastest revenue growth of any geography, expanding by 18.3% year-over-year, fueled by new customer wins in South Korea and India.
North American revenues grew 7.1%, benefiting from the ramp-up of a new distribution partnership announced in late fiscal year 2022.
Van Houten described the North American market as a strategic priority for medium-term growth, with plans to establish a wholly-owned subsidiary in Houston, Texas, by mid-2025.
The company's sales to the automotive end-market represented 34% of total revenues, followed by electronics at 21%, construction at 18%, and other industrial uses at 27%.
Demand from the electronics sector proved particularly resilient, as Veltoran's specialty polymer compounds gained adoption among semiconductor packaging manufacturers in Taiwan.
Bleeker indicated that pricing power across all three segments remained healthy throughout fiscal year 2023, with average selling price increases of approximately 3.5% on a blended basis.
Raw material costs, which constitute approximately 48% of cost of goods sold, declined by an average of 4.2% in fiscal year 2023 compared to the prior year.
The favorable raw material environment was primarily attributable to lower petrochemical feedstock prices, which benefited both the Industrial Solvents and Specialty Polymers divisions.
Veltoran entered into a long-term supply agreement with Saudi Aramco subsidiary Advanced Petrochemicals in March 2023, securing key feedstock volumes at fixed pricing through 2026.
The company's logistics and distribution costs rose by 8.1% in fiscal year 2023, partially offsetting gains from lower raw material expenditures.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled €184 million for the year, equivalent to 10.0% of revenues, down from 10.7% in fiscal year 2022.
The improvement in SG&A as a percentage of revenues reflected operational leverage as the top line grew faster than the fixed cost base.

Veltoran completed two bolt-on acquisitions during fiscal year 2023: a specialty adhesives business in Poland acquired for €47 million and a polymer recycling firm in Belgium acquired for €31 million.
The combined annual revenues of the two acquired entities amounted to approximately €58 million on a proforma basis at the time of acquisition.
Integration of both acquisitions is proceeding on schedule, with cost synergy targets of €9 million annually expected to be fully realized by the end of fiscal year 2025.
Environmental, social, and governance performance remained a focus area for Veltoran's management team, with carbon emissions intensity declining 7.3% year-over-year.
The company set a formal target to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2040, a commitment endorsed by the Science Based Targets initiative.
Veltoran's Lost Time Injury Rate improved to 0.31 per 200,000 hours worked, compared to 0.44 in fiscal year 2022, reflecting investments in workplace safety programs.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal year 2023.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenue growth in the range of 6% to 9% and an operating margin between 15.2% and 16.0%.
Bleeker noted that the guidance assumes modest currency headwinds from a stronger euro relative to the US dollar and select Asian currencies.

During the first quarter of fiscal year 2024, Veltoran secured a €350 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of European banks, with proceeds earmarked for refinancing near-term maturities and funding the Houston expansion.
The new facility carries a floating interest rate of EURIBOR plus 95 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Management expects interest expense to increase by approximately €12 million annually as a result of the new borrowing arrangement.
The Houston expansion project is expected to require total capital investment of approximately €160 million over a three-year construction period beginning in the second quarter of 2024.
Van Houten reiterated the company's commitment to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile even as capital spending intensifies in the near term.
Veltoran's board approved a refreshed capital allocation framework prioritizing organic growth investment, selective M&A, and a progressive dividend policy.
The company declared an interim dividend of €0.60 per share for the first half of fiscal year 2024, payable on April 30, 2024.
Analysts covering Veltoran generally responded positively to the full-year results, with several upgrading their price targets following the earnings release.
Shares of Veltoran Industries trade on Euronext Amsterdam under the ticker symbol VLTN, and the stock closed at €48.70 on the day of the earnings announcement.
This report excerpt has been prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards and has been reviewed by external auditor KPMG Netherlands.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for fiscal year 2023.
The company specializes in the design and manufacture of precision robotics systems for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Heldring opened the report with a statement emphasizing the company's commitment to operational excellence and sustainable growth.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
This represented a 9.3 percent increase compared to the $1.68 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Adrienne Kossuth noted that the growth was driven primarily by strong demand in the automotive robotics segment.
The automotive segment alone contributed $1.02 billion in revenues, representing 55.4 percent of total company revenues.
The aerospace segment generated $620 million, while the remaining $200 million came from aftermarket services and software licensing.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8 percent.
Operating income was reported at $198 million, reflecting a 10.7 percent operating margin.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $3.47, beating analyst consensus estimates of $3.31 by a meaningful margin.
The company carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, having retired all obligations in the prior fiscal year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $241 million for the full year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $387 million, representing a healthy liquidity position for near-term operational needs.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $134 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the Charlotte facility.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2024.
This dividend represented a 10 percent increase from the $0.20 per share paid in the prior fiscal year.
Veltrano Dynamics repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $58.40 per share.
The share buyback program reduced the total diluted share count to 69.5 million shares outstanding as of year-end.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Petrov reported that manufacturing output increased by 14 percent year-over-year.
The Charlotte facility achieved a record throughput of 18,400 robotic units in fiscal 2023.
A new production line dedicated to collaborative robot arms was commissioned in the third quarter of 2023.
This new line is expected to add roughly 3,000 units of incremental annual capacity starting in fiscal 2024.
Research and development expenditures totaled $92 million, representing approximately 5 percent of total revenues.
The R&D team, comprising 620 engineers and scientists, focused heavily on machine learning integration for autonomous quality inspection.
Veltrano Dynamics filed 34 new patents during the fiscal year, bringing its cumulative patent portfolio to 412 active patents.
The company's headcount grew from 6,820 employees at the start of 2023 to 7,340 employees by year-end.
This net addition of 520 employees reflected hiring primarily in engineering, sales, and international operations.
Veltrano Dynamics expanded its sales presence into three new markets: South Korea, Poland, and Brazil.
The international segment contributed $430 million in revenues, up 22 percent from $352 million in the prior year.
Marcus Heldring credited the international growth to strategic partnerships established with regional distributors.
The company entered a five-year distribution agreement with Hansei Industrial Partners in South Korea during the second quarter.
In Poland, Veltrano partnered with Wrocław Automation Group to serve Central and Eastern European automotive manufacturers.
The Brazilian partnership with São Paulo-based Motrix Soluções provided access to the growing South American manufacturing market.
Adrienne Kossuth highlighted that foreign currency fluctuations had a modest adverse impact of approximately $18 million on reported revenues.
On a constant-currency basis, international revenues would have grown by approximately 27 percent year-over-year.
The company's backlog of confirmed orders stood at $2.1 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $1.75 billion at the prior year-end.
This growing backlog provided visibility into revenue for the next twelve to eighteen months of operations.
The largest individual order in the backlog was a $310 million contract with a major European automotive original equipment manufacturer.
Veltrano Dynamics expects to begin recognizing revenue on this contract in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
Inventory levels increased modestly to $218 million from $194 million at the prior year-end, reflecting planned build-ahead for upcoming deliveries.
Accounts receivable totaled $276 million, with days sales outstanding improving to 54 days from 61 days in fiscal 2022.
The improvement in DSO was attributed to stricter credit policies and enhanced collection procedures implemented mid-year.
Sandra Petrov noted that supply chain conditions improved significantly compared to the disruption-heavy environment of fiscal 2021 and 2022.
Average lead times for critical components declined from 24 weeks to approximately 16 weeks during fiscal 2023.
The company successfully qualified two new semiconductor suppliers to reduce concentration risk in its control systems business.
Total selling, general, and administrative expenses were $314 million, representing 17.1 percent of revenues.
This ratio improved slightly from 17.6 percent in fiscal 2022, reflecting operating leverage as revenues scaled.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4 percent, compared to 22.1 percent in the prior year.
The slight reduction in tax rate was attributable to increased utilization of federal R&D tax credits.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for total revenues in the range of $2.0 billion to $2.1 billion.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltrano Dynamics finalized a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of six major banks.
Operating income guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $215 million to $230 million, implying continued margin improvement.
Management indicated that capital expenditure investment in fiscal 2024 would increase to approximately $160 million.
This elevated capex level would support the construction of a new manufacturing facility in Monterrey, Mexico.
The Monterrey facility is projected to be operational by the end of fiscal 2025 and will serve North American automotive customers.
Sandra Petrov stated that workforce planning anticipates hiring an additional 400 employees over the course of fiscal 2024.
The board of directors authorized an extension of the share repurchase program, adding $150 million in buyback capacity.
Marcus Heldring closed the report by reaffirming the company's long-term targets of reaching $3 billion in revenues by fiscal 2027.
He emphasized that disciplined capital allocation, continued innovation, and strategic geographic expansion would be the cornerstones of this growth trajectory.
Veltrano Dynamics remains committed to delivering long-term value to its shareholders, customers, and the communities in which it operates.
Veltara Dynamics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions, presents its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company operates across four primary business segments: Precision Robotics, Smart Sensors, Industrial Software, and Field Services.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Hollowell opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's strategic direction and long-term growth prospects.
Veltara Dynamics generated total revenue of $2.84 billion during fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.6% increase over the prior year.
The Precision Robotics segment contributed $1.1 billion to total revenue, driven by strong demand from automotive and aerospace manufacturing clients.
Operating income for the full year reached $412 million, yielding an operating margin of approximately 14.5%.
The company employed approximately 11,200 full-time workers across its global operations as of December 31, 2023.
Veltara Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet at the close of fiscal year 2023, reflecting a deliberate capital structure policy maintained since 2019.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsukuda emphasized the importance of maintaining financial flexibility as the company pursues organic growth.
Capital expenditures totaled $178 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity in the company's Ohio and Germany facilities.
Gross profit for the year was $987 million, corresponding to a gross margin of 34.7%.
The Smart Sensors segment recorded revenue growth of 18.3%, reaching $620 million for the fiscal year.
Veltara Dynamics invested $310 million in research and development in 2023, representing 10.9% of total revenue.
The Industrial Software segment delivered $480 million in revenue, with recurring subscription-based contracts accounting for 71% of that total.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $298 million, or $4.62 per diluted share.
The company repurchased 3.2 million shares during the fiscal year at an average price of $67.40 per share.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $541 million, up from $389 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share, consistent with the company's dividend policy established in 2020.
Veltara Dynamics expanded into three new international markets during 2023: South Korea, Brazil, and Poland.
The Field Services segment generated $640 million in revenue, supported by a growing installed base of automation equipment.
Customer retention rates across all segments averaged 91% for the year, a metric that management highlighted as evidence of strong service quality.
The company signed 47 new enterprise contracts with annual contract values exceeding $5 million each.
Inventory turnover improved to 5.8 times in 2023, compared to 5.1 times in 2022, reflecting better supply chain management.
Veltara Dynamics launched the VX-900 series robotic arm in March 2023, targeting high-precision assembly applications.
The VX-900 series achieved certification under ISO 10218-1 safety standards, enabling deployment in collaborative manufacturing environments.
Sales of the VX-900 contributed approximately $140 million to Precision Robotics segment revenue in its first year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, compared to 23.4% in the prior year.
Veltara Dynamics reduced its total operating expenses by $34 million through a cost optimization program initiated in late 2022.
The program involved consolidating three regional distribution centers into two larger, automated facilities.
Headcount in the administrative functions decreased by approximately 4% as a result of process automation initiatives.
Revenue from North American operations accounted for 58% of total revenue, with Europe contributing 27% and Asia-Pacific the remaining 15%.
The company's order backlog at December 31, 2023 was $1.62 billion, providing strong visibility into near-term revenue.
Days sales outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in the prior year, reflecting tighter credit management practices.
Veltara Dynamics received the 2023 Manufacturing Excellence Award from the International Automation Council.
Ms. Hollowell stated that the company's technology roadmap includes integrating artificial intelligence into all robotic product lines by 2026.
The company's return on invested capital was 16.8% for fiscal 2023, above the company's stated target of 15%.
Veltara Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit ratings of BBB+ from Standard & Poor's and Baa1 from Moody's throughout the year.
The company's pension obligations totaled $193 million at year-end, fully funded based on actuarial assessments.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives led to a 9% reduction in carbon emissions intensity compared to fiscal 2022.
Mr. Tsukuda noted that the company's strong cash flow generation provides ample capacity for continued investment.
Free cash flow for the year was $263 million, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures.
The company's operating cash flow was $441 million, representing a cash conversion rate of approximately 107% of net income.
Veltara Dynamics disclosed no material litigation contingencies that would require recognition under ASC 450 as of the reporting date.
The audit committee confirmed that there were no significant deficiencies or material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting.
Revenue from service contracts and aftermarket parts grew by 21% year-over-year, outpacing growth in hardware sales.
The company's weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.3% for planning purposes in fiscal 2023.
Veltara Dynamics paid total dividends of approximately $72 million to shareholders during the fiscal year.
In the third quarter of 2023, the company secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of six international banks, replacing a smaller prior arrangement.
The Smart Sensors segment benefited from increased demand in the semiconductor and electronics manufacturing end markets.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $389 million, representing 13.7% of total revenue.
The company opened a new research center in Stuttgart, Germany, focused on next-generation motion control technologies.
Veltara Dynamics reported that foreign currency fluctuations had an adverse impact of approximately $28 million on reported revenue.
The Board authorized an additional $200 million share repurchase program in November 2023, to be executed over the following 24 months.
Employee satisfaction scores, measured through an annual internal survey, reached an all-time high of 82% in fiscal 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Diana Ferrante joined Veltara Dynamics in February 2023 following her tenure at a major European industrial conglomerate.
The company anticipates revenue growth in the range of 9% to 12% for fiscal year 2024, based on current market conditions and backlog.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 is set at $190 million to $210 million, reflecting continued investment in capacity expansion.
Veltara Dynamics expects its operating margin to remain in the 14% to 15% range in fiscal 2024.
The company plans to increase its quarterly dividend by $0.03 per share beginning in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Management expressed gratitude to employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support throughout a dynamic operating environment.
Veltara Dynamics remains committed to delivering sustainable long-term value through disciplined capital allocation and operational excellence.
This report was reviewed and approved by the Board of Directors of Veltara Dynamics Corporation on February 22, 2024.
Vanteral Biosciences entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond issuance in December 2022.
The company, headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializes in the development and commercialization of enzyme-based therapeutics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Holt opened the annual report with a message emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and operational efficiency.
Vanteral Biosciences reported total revenues of $847 million for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2023.
This represented a 14.2% increase compared to the $741 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The company's flagship product, EnzyPrime-7, continued to be the primary revenue driver, contributing $512 million to total sales.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delgado noted that gross margins expanded by 210 basis points year-over-year, reaching 68.4%.
Research and development expenditures totaled $193 million, representing approximately 22.8% of total revenue.
The company employed 4,320 full-time equivalent staff as of the fiscal year-end date.
Vanteral Biosciences maintained operations across twelve countries, with its largest international presence in Germany and Japan.
Operating income for the period reached $178 million, up from $141 million in the prior fiscal year.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.3%, slightly above management's earlier guidance of 20.5%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $132 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.74.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $389 million, compared to $302 million at the prior year-end.
