The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, consistent with the prior year's payout level.
Dr. Holt highlighted the successful completion of Phase III clinical trials for the company's next-generation candidate, EnzyPrime-9.
Regulatory submissions for EnzyPrime-9 were filed with the FDA in August 2023 and the EMA in September 2023.
The commercial manufacturing partnership with Halgren Pharmatech was renewed for an additional five years under improved cost terms.
Vanteral's European segment posted revenue of $214 million, growing 18.7% from the prior year on a constant-currency basis.
The Asia-Pacific segment contributed $98 million in revenue, reflecting expansion into South Korean and Australian markets.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $176 million, which management characterized as well-controlled given the company's growth trajectory.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $44.60 per share.
Capital expenditures totaled $67 million, primarily reflecting investments in the expanded biologics manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Amortization of intangible assets related to prior acquisitions contributed $29 million in non-cash charges during the year.
The company's backlog of firm product orders at September 30, 2023, stood at $163 million.
Dr. Holt credited the cross-functional commercial team for successfully navigating payer negotiations in the United States market.
Vanteral Biosciences recorded a $14 million gain on the divestiture of its legacy diagnostics unit, completed in March 2023.
The diagnostics unit had been a non-core asset since the company's strategic pivot to therapeutics in 2019.
Accounts receivable at fiscal year-end totaled $118 million, representing a days-sales-outstanding metric of approximately 51 days.
Inventory levels were $74 million, reflecting management's deliberate build-up to support the anticipated EnzyPrime-9 launch.
Marcus Delgado emphasized that working capital management remained a key focus, with the current ratio improving to 3.1 from 2.7.
The company maintained a $150 million revolving credit facility that remained entirely undrawn throughout fiscal 2023.
Vanteral Biosciences invested $22 million in digital health infrastructure, supporting real-world evidence generation for its therapeutic portfolio.
The North America segment, including the United States and Canada, generated $535 million in revenue.
Employee headcount increased by 310 individuals during the year, primarily in clinical affairs and medical science liaison roles.
The company's net promoter score among prescribing physicians improved to 71, from 64 in the prior fiscal year.
Vanteral's stock price appreciated approximately 31% during fiscal 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by 19 percentage points.
Deferred revenue at fiscal year-end was $43 million, largely representing advance payments under multi-year supply agreements.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed an unqualified audit opinion for fiscal year 2023.
Dr. Holt expressed confidence that EnzyPrime-9 would receive regulatory approval in early calendar 2024, positioning Vanteral for accelerated growth.
To fund the anticipated commercial launch of EnzyPrime-9, Vanteral Biosciences entered into a $300 million senior secured term loan facility during fiscal year 2023.
The term loan carries a fixed interest rate of 5.75% per annum and matures in seven years.
Proceeds from the term loan were earmarked for pre-commercial manufacturing scale-up, sales force expansion, and working capital.
Marcus Delgado indicated that management views the new debt structure as consistent with the company's investment-grade credit profile.
The leverage ratio at year-end, measured as net debt to adjusted EBITDA, was approximately 1.1 times.
The company expects to begin amortizing the principal on the term loan starting in fiscal 2025.
Interest expense associated with the new borrowing is projected to be approximately $17 million in fiscal 2024.
Management's guidance for fiscal year 2024 calls for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $995 million.
This guidance assumes a mid-year commercial launch of EnzyPrime-9 and continued low double-digit growth in EnzyPrime-7 sales.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal 2024 is set at 22% to 24%, reflecting launch-related investment spending.
The company intends to maintain its current dividend level through fiscal 2024 while prioritizing debt repayment over share buybacks.
Vanteral Biosciences anticipates ending fiscal 2024 with a cash balance of approximately $310 million to $340 million.
The forward-looking statements contained in this report are subject to risks including regulatory delay, competitive dynamics, and macroeconomic uncertainty.
Dr. Holt concluded the executive message by thanking employees, partners, and shareholders for their continued commitment to the company's mission.
The company's five-year strategic plan targets total revenue exceeding $1.5 billion by fiscal 2027.
Vanteral Biosciences remains committed to advancing its pipeline of enzyme-based therapies addressing rare metabolic disorders.
The board of directors will propose an expansion of the authorized share repurchase program to $250 million at the upcoming annual meeting.
This annual report has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
